publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Dynamic obstacle avoidance constitutes a critical component in enabling autonomous mobility and safe operation of robots. In complex and dynamically changing operational environments, robots must possess real-time obstacle detection and dynamic path planning capabilities. This study employs a spider parallel robot as the platform, integrating an RGB-D depth camera and an inertial measurement unit to construct an environmental perception system, thereby providing the robot with multimodal information such as three-dimensional visual data and attitude angles. Firstly, an enhanced object detection model, YOLOv4-M, is proposed based on YOLOv4. By incorporating an additional detection layer, introducing a spatial pyramid pooling structure, and optimizing anchor boxes using K-means clustering, the model improves the accuracy and robustness of multi-scale obstacle recognition. The model identifies and localizes obstacles within color images and employs depth map alignment techniques to determine obstacle dimensions and their spatial distances relative to the robot. Based on real-time robot attitude data and obstacle identification results, an improved artificial potential field-based obstacle avoidance decision-making model is developed. This model integrates a dead-zone distance mechanism and a dynamic steering strategy to effectively address local minima issues inherent in traditional potential field methods, thereby enabling dynamic walking path planning. The generated decision commands are transmitted to the robot's motion control unit, ultimately achieving autonomous obstacle avoidance in unknown environments. Both simulation and physical experiments validate that the proposed system effectively accomplishes autonomous obstacle avoidance tasks for the spider parallel robot. This research provides a technical foundation and reference for obstacle recognition and autonomous navigation in robots relying solely on visual and inertial sensors.

title: Human-in-the-Loop AI for Waste Segregation: Accuracy, Equity, and Pathways to Scale
authors: Vinod Patel, Margi Patel, Akash Bhasney, Anand Gadwal, Nitin Rathore, Ramesh Naik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Human-in-the-loop (HIL) artificial intelligence has provided a viable way to implement waste-segregation systems in urban settings with high levels of noise and high variability. In this study, a socio-technical model is developed and tested in order to establish a relationship between data and governance factors and the adoption of HIL-AI segregation at scale. We assessed 9 reflective constructs: Data Quality, HIL Effectiveness, Perceived Accuracy, Explainability, Fairness & Equity, Trust, Perceived Scalability, Economic Viability, and Adoption Intention, using five-point Likert scales, on a crosssectional survey of 276 stakeholders in waste operations. Measurement and structural relations were measured using partial least squares structural equation modeling (SmartPLS; Mode A). Its measurement model exhibited high reliability and convergent validity (outer loadings were mostly above. 80, AVE was above. 70), moderate discriminant validity, and acceptable fit (SRMR was about 0.5). Perceived accuracy structurality Data Quality and HIL Effectiveness were the drivers of perceived accuracy, Trust was dominated by Explainability, then Fairness and Equity and Accuracy. The strongest explained factor was Adoption Intention, which was described by Economic Viability and Perceived Scalability, and Trust was considered a significant mediator; the direct effect between Accuracy to Adoption was also low and positive. The model described moderate variation in important outcomes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R} 2=.36$</tex> in Accuracy;. 29 in Trust;. 46 in Adoption Intention). The results suggest that scale requires accuracy but not enough, which is the combination of qualities of governance, such as transparent reasoning, fair distribution of burden, and plausible unit economics that transform the technical performance into the organizational commitment. The research also offers an empirically supported framework and validated tool to those municipalities that wish to implement HIL-AI wastesegregation in accurate, fair, and scalable ways.

title: Fed-DCL: A Federated Dual-Continual Learning Framework for Adaptive Electric Power Load Forecasting Under Concept Drift
authors: Yueyang Gong, Bin Pan, Kai Lyu, Fenggang Sun, Peng Lan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: To address the non-stationarity and privacy protection challenges in electric power load forecasting, this paper proposes a Federated Dual-Continual Learning (Fed-DCL) framework. This method introduces drift detection and dual knowledge distillation mechanisms within the federated learning framework, enabling adaptive learning and knowledge retention for conceptual drift. Clients assess distribution change intensity via a drift detector and activate dual knowledge distillation mechanism during strong drift to fuse historical and global knowledge, effectively mitigating catastrophic forgetting. Servers employ a multi-factor adaptive weighted aggregation strategy while maintaining a global knowledge pool for cross-round knowledge sharing and accumulation. Experiments validate the framework across three scenarios-normal, abrupt, and gradual-using the Electricity Load Diagrams dataset. Results demonstrate that Fed-DCL outperforms existing methods in prediction accuracy, convergence stability, and adaptation speed, exhibiting superior robustness and adaptability particularly in abrupt and gradual mutation scenarios.

title: AI Agents in Healthcare: A Survey of Applications, Resources, and Challenges
authors: Sami Rashid, Md Fahim Shahriar, Sumaiya Karim Katha, Ezharuddin Jubaer, Akm Moshiur Rahman Mazumder, Ashraful Islam, Mohsena Ashraf, Asif Mahmud, M. Ashraful Amin
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have accelerated the emergence of agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems in healthcare, representing a paradigm shift from static models to autonomous entities capable of complex medical reasoning. This survey examines AI agents across six domains: clinical decision support, healthcare systems management, clinical consultation, medical education, medical imaging, and evaluation frameworks. We analyze over 30 systems including MDAgents, Agent Hospital, ColaCare, and AgentClinic, identifying key architectural patterns and performance characteristics. These systems demonstrate promising results across a range of clinical tasks, leveraging both multi-agent collaboration and specialized medical reasoning. Critical deployment barriers include data privacy, algorithmic bias, regulatory gaps, safety concerns in multi-agent architectures, explainability requirements, and integration challenges. We identify open research problems and highlight key challenges to advancing safe, reliable, and impartial healthcare agentic AI.

title: Performance Analysis of FAS-Assisted RSMA Communication System
authors: Supriya Kumari, Vimal Bhatia, Keshav Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Fluid antenna systems (FAS) have recently been recognized as a promising enabler for sixth-generation (6G) wireless communications, owing to their ability to dynamically adapt antenna positions within a confined region. Rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA), on the other hand, allows messages to be divided into common and private parts, which can significantly improve the user experience, especially in scenarios with diverse channel conditions. This advantage is particularly pronounced in FAS, where users can dynamically adjust their antennas for optimal performance. In this paper, we investigate the performance of a FAS-assisted RSMA communication system to utilize their synergistic benefits. We consider a scenario where a fixed base station (BS) transmits information to users equipped with a planar fluid antenna. We begin by deriving the joint cumulative distribution function (CDF) of the effective channel at the user by utilizing copula theory to model the correlation between the channel fading coefficients. To gain more insights, delay outage rate (DOR) is derived. Simulation results demonstrates that the integration of RSMA and FAS leads to improved overall system performance compared to conventional single-input single-output (SISO) RSMA systems. The analytical expressions derived are validated through simulation results.

title: Physics Informed TCN-eKAN for Aeroengine Rotor Anomaly Detection
authors: Mengyu Chen, Qin Meng, Zhengda Zhao, Junshen Chen, Xin Lu, Zechen Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: As the core rotating component of an aeroengine, the rotor system directly determines the engine's reliability and flight safety. Therefore, it is crucial to adopt effective anomaly detection methods to monitor the rotor's operating condition. Typically, the vibration signal of the rotor is used as the key source of information for anomaly detection. However, fault data of aeroengine rotors under real operating conditions are extremely scarce, making it difficult to adequately train intelligent diagnostic models and thereby limiting the engineering application of rotor anomaly detection technology. In recent years, some studies have attempted to reduce the dependence on measured data by training models with a combination of simulated and measured data. However, significant differences in the information distribution between simulated and measured data lead to a substantial decline in the diagnostic accuracy of intelligent models. To address this issue, this paper proposes a Physics Informed Temporal Convolutional Network with efficient Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (PITCN-eKAN) for anomaly detection of aeroengine rotor systems. The proposed model obtains the theoretical response output of the actual data by constructing a mechanism simulation model, which is adopted as the physical constraint in the data-driven model training process. A network framework guided by physical information is constructed to improve the fitting accuracy of the deep learning model with the theoretical output, thus achieving high-precision anomaly detection. The PITCN-eKAN model provides a new concept for the engineering application of intelligent diagnostic algorithms for aeroengine rotors.

title: Sustainable Flexible Wearable Antenna Using Rubber Substrate for Wireless Body Area Networks
authors: Wan Mirza Liyana Wan Mazri, Sarah Yasmin Mohamad, Aiman Hakimi Rahimi, Norun Farihah Abdul Malek, Md. Rafiqul Islam, Mohamed Hadi Habaebi, Huda Adibah Mohd Ramli
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: The growing demand for flexible antennas in the telecommunications industry has driven the need for compact designs with improved performance. Microstrip antennas are particularly attractive due to their low profile, ease of fabrication, and adaptability. A key challenge in enhancing flexible antennas lies in selecting a suitable substrate. In this work, rubber is proposed as an alternative substrate material because of its flexibility, conductivity, efficiency, ease of fabrication, and water resistance, making it suitable for Wireless Body Area Network (WBAN) applications. The proposed antenna operates at 2.4 GHz with a relative permittivity (εr) of 3.0, a frequency commonly used for wearable devices. Two antenna designs, rectangular and circular microstrip patches, were developed, simulated, and analyzed using CST Microwave Studio (CST-MWS). Performance metrics including reflection coefficient, VSWR, gain, directivity, and bandwidth were evaluated under flat and bent conditions of 15°, 30°, 45°, and 90°. Results show that both designs achieved satisfactory performance, demonstrating their robustness and suitability for WBAN applications.

title: Behdiffnet: A Hierarchical Diffusion Model for Behavior-Aware Network Traffic Generation
authors: Jiaqian Jiang, Ke Yu, Xiaofei Wu
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: Network traffic data is vital for network optimization, security, and other fields, but high-quality real data is scarce due to privacy and commercial constraints, making high-fidelity synthetic traffic generation critical for research and applications. Existing traffic generation methods often struggle to capture dynamic user intent at the application level, suffering from limitations like mode collapse or oversimplified feature modeling. Rule-based and GAN-based approaches, for instance, struggle to capture the complex interplay of behavioral patterns and network protocols, leading to low controllability and realism. This paper proposes BehDiffNet, a two-stage hierarchical diffusion model for behavior-aware network traffic generation to address these challenges. The model employs a dual-conditional generation mechanism (behavioral sequence conditioning and protocol constraint conditioning): the upper layer models user intent at the application layer by embedding application order, frequency and temporal semantics in behavioral sequences, and re-generates them through diffusion models and protocol constraints, while the lower layer integrates conditional bootstrapping through a two-branch transformer. Utilizing adversarial training and cross-feature attention, the model effectively captures temporal dependencies and multidimensional feature correlations. Experiments demonstrate that the model is more advantageous in terms of statistical alignment and protocol consistency compared to the baseline.

title: Research on the Improvement of New Energy Incentive Policies Under the Electricity Marketization Reform
authors: Yunling Sun, Hongwei Li, Henan Wang, Lei Jiao, Jilong Wu, Yantong Liu, Ying Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Promoting the high-quality development of new energy is an essential pathway to achieving China's dual carbon goals. Given the generation characteristics of wind- and solar-dominated new energy and the current stage of China's power system development, it is crucial to design rational and effective incentive policies for new energy. This paper examines the experiences and lessons of new energy incentive policies in the United Kingdom and the United States, and traces the evolution of China's policy framework, with particular attention to the Chinese style of the contract-for-difference (CfD) mechanism. Building on this foundation, the study conducts an in-depth case analysis of Hebei Province and proposes a design scheme for China's new energy incentive policy. The proposed scheme provides valuable reference for the formulation of China's 15th Five-Year Plan.

title: A Fault Detection Method for 5G Core Networks Based on Graph Attention Networks
authors: Han Yang, Ke Yu, Yongqing Zhu
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: The increasing complexity and massive data volume of 5G core networks (5GC) bring significant challenges for timely and accurate fault detection, which is crucial to ensuring network reliability and user experience. However, existing methods often fail to effectively model the intricate spatiotemporal dependencies among multivariate Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) across different network elements, leading to limited generalization and poor detection robustness. In this paper, we propose GFD-5G, a Graph-enhanced Fault Detection model designed for 5GC. The model leverages a hierarchical Graph Attention Network (GAT) framework to capture correlations across element, time, and feature dimensions. A forecasting branch based on fully connected layers predicts future KPI values, while a reconstruction branch based on a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) models historical behavior. Moreover, a Peaks Over Threshold (POT)-based statistical module is introduced to automatically determine dynamic detection thresholds. The results verify the effectiveness and practical applicability of our approach in the maintenance of 5G networks.

title: Service Quality of Indonesian Customs Information and Automation System Version 4.0 and Stakeholders' Satisfaction-A Mediation Analysis
authors: Damar Aji Irawan, Rianto Nurcahyo, Tiurida Lily Anita, Dito Haekal Bagaskoro
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This research aims to analyze the mediating effect of export customer-oriented training and relative service quality on the relationships between service quality (SERVQUAL) dimensions of Indonesian customs information and automation system version 4.0 (CEISA 4.0) and freight forwarders (FF) and customs service's companies' satisfaction. There has been limited analysis in the past studies regarding CEISA 4.0, service quality, and FF and customs service's companies' satisfaction. This research adopts a quantitative research approach using a cross-sectional survey to collect primary data from 153 respondents. The collected data is analyzed using PLS-SEM to examine the structural relationships and mediation effects in the research model. The results showed that export customer-oriented training fully mediates the relationship between service quality and satisfaction while relative service quality does not.

title: UCapsNet: A Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Breast Cancer Classification
authors: L.K. Sravanthi Potti, S. Maruthuperumal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The present research resolves the deficiencies of traditional methods by presenting an improved U-Net model combined with a Capsule Network (referred to as UCapsNet). This technique uses more filters and skips connections to improve segmentation, while the capsule network uses dynamic routing to preserve spatial hierarchies to improve classification. In the proposed model robust approach for breast cancer segmentation and classification using the CBIS-DDSIM dataset is presented. The proposed methodology employs the Fuzzy C-Means (FCM) algorithm for clustering and segmenting breast cancer, followed by feature extraction through a combined convolutional neural network (CNN) + UNET model. These extracted features are then classified using Capsule-Net algorithms to identify images as benign or malignant, achieving an accuracy exceeding 96% on test data. The system is implemented as a web application comprising several modules. Users can upload the dataset, preprocess images, and extract features via FCM and CNN+UNET. The features are split into training and testing sets, where Capsule-Net algorithms are applied for model training and accuracy evaluation. Additionally, the application allows users to classify new test images, enhancing its practical utility for early breast cancer detection. It is observed by the proposed model that it has superior performance compared to state-of-the-art techniques such as CNN. Capsule-Net gives better performance than state-of-the-art techniques, which is shown using the Jaccard similarity score and dice score with metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F-score used for performance analysis. A confusion matrix is used for showing the colorful visualization of the difference between correct and wrong predictions.

title: SplitFedEE: Balancing Communication and Accuracy
authors: Erika Duriakova, Diarmuid O’Reilly-Morgan, Elias Tragos, Neil Hurley, Aonghus Lawlor
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: SplitFed algorithm is a novel collaborative learning framework that enables decentralised learning of large deep architectures without the need to share sensitive raw data. However, this approach comes with a drawback of large communication overhead, as both the intermediate weights for forward propagation and gradients for backpropagation need to be regularly communicated with the server.This paper presents an empirical evaluation of methods that have the potential to address the communication inefficiency of the SplitFed algorithm. In particular, we study Early Exit (EE) algorithms and their application in the context of SplitFed. In our empirical evaluation, we insert the EE at the last layer of the local client model and train this smaller model locally in tandem with the large model. We show that the communication overhead can be reduced even further by randomly sampling smaller subsets from the training set. We evaluate this approach on several deep model architectures across various datasets and demonstrate that this approach can significantly save on communication while maintaining the accuracy of the base model.

title: One-Shot Masked Face Identification via Contrastive Representations
authors: Anjali T, Masilamani V
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The degradation in the performance of the face recognition models after the regular usage of face masks due to viral infections such as COVID-19 leads to the importance of the research in occluded face recognition. This challenge is further worsened in real-world applications by the lack of large-scale labeled training data, making single-sample face recognition a particularly difficult task. In this paper, we propose a self-supervised learning based method to tackle the masked face recognition with single sample per identity. The proposed method uses a two stage training strategy. In the first step,a contrastive learning framework is employed to learn discriminative feature representations by contrasting positive pairs (different augmentations of the same identity) against negative pairs (different identities). In the second stage, the learned representations are used to extract embeddings, which are subsequently classified using a lightweight supervised classifier. Experiments demonstrate that our self-supervised learning approach significantly enhances feature discrimination, leading to improved classification accuracy in downstream masked face recognition tasks. The experiment results confirm that contrastive learning is a key enabler in building resilient face recognition systems for constrained scenarios such as masked or partially occluded face inputs.

title: A Multimodal and Real-Time Approach for Low-Resource Language Emotion Recognition
authors: Emmanuel M. J., Pranav Seelam, Mrithulasree N., Sankar Raja S., Siva Sankar P., Shoba S., Krithiga R., Satya Vankayala
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Emotion recognition is a crucial aspect of human-computer interaction, allowing systems to comprehend and react more effectively to users. Majority of emotion recognition models are developed using high-resource languages, hence constraining their efficacy for speakers of low-resource languages. This work uses a real-time multimodal emotion identification framework designed for low-resource languages for audio and visual features. The main objective is to incorporate deep learning-driven feature fusion approaches to improve emotion classification precision while mitigating the lack of labeled datasets via transfer learning and self-supervised learning. The real-time dataset created, named Tamil-Telugu Audio-Visual Speech Emotion Recognition (TTAVSER), has been tested with the deep learning models for audio-visual and demonstrated the results with different datasets and for low-resource languages of tamil and Telugu. The experimental findings of the model with real-time dataset attain significant accuracy with the state-of-the-art comparison with other low-resource languages.

title: Research on Evaluation Method of Power Regulation Capability of Urban Industrial Loads
authors: Yucheng Ding, Guanglong Xie, Xiaoling Jin, Yibo Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: In the context of constructing a new power system dominated by renewable energy, this paper addresses the core issue of discrepancies between theoretical assessments and practical realities in industrial load regulation capability. It proposes a city-level assessment method for industrial load power regulation capability. This method overcomes the limitations of traditional algebraic superposition by establishing a time-resolved analytical model that integrates real-time load fluctuations and historical operational features to accurately quantify the regulation potential of a single user. It innovatively introduces a confidence assessment mechanism using the dual parameters of user response willingness and communication reliability to effectively address the problem of uncertainty in industrial user response. The simulation case, based on a 6 -node network, validates the practicality of the model. It systematically reveals the regulation capacity of urban-scale industrial loads. This work provides a scientifically quantified basis for power grids to accurately tap into demand-side adjustable resources and enhance the accommodation of renewable energy. It holds significant engineering application value for alleviating power supplydemand tensions and promoting synergistic source-load interaction.

title: Resource-Aware Dual-Granularity Synchronization Strategy for Transportation Digital Twin
authors: Yutong Wu, Xiaoyan Huang, Xiwen Liao, Ke Zhang, Supeng Leng
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) are essential to smart city development, yet traditional control frameworks struggle to meet the demands of complex scenarios such as autonomous driving. Digital Twin (DT) technology emerges as a powerful solution by enabling virtual representations of physical entities to support real-time state estimation, predictive analysis, and coordinated decision-making. However, existing research often underestimates the high cost of DT synchronization and migration, and lacks coordinated update strategies across vehicles and road segments. To address these challenges, we propose a dual-granularity DT construction scheme that jointly optimizes synchronization strategies for both vehicles and road segments, while coordinating communication and computational resource allocation. We formulate a joint optimization problem that integrates vehicle DT deployment, adaptive road segment DT updating, and resource scheduling. This problem is decomposed into two interdependent subproblems, which are solved using a hybrid algorithm that combines heterogeneous agent proximal policy optimization (HAPPO) and block coordinate descent (BCD). Simulation results demonstrate that our scheme significantly enhances DT quality while reducing overall system costs.

title: Frequency-Aware Native Resolution Assessment of 8K Omnidirectional Images
authors: Jingwen Hou, Zengliang Li, Jiebin Yan, Weide Liu, Yuming Fang, Wei Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Omnidirectional images (ODIs) serve as fundamental visual medium for presenting virtual reality (VR) contents, supporting fully immersive experiences through 360-degree scene representation. Typically, a high pixel density is essential for visual quality in VR environments, which in turn requires sufficiently high-resolution imagery to achieve. However, capturing native high-resolution ODIs requires expensive omnidirectional cameras with large sensors (e.g., Insta360 TITAN). An alternative approach is to use low-resolution cameras to acquire original images and then enhance their resolution via super-resolution algorithms. In this work, we explore whether super-resolution ODIs can be easily distinguished from native high-resolution ODIs at 8K scale. To this end, we firstly construct the Native Resolution Assessment of 8K Omnidirectional Images (NRA- 8KODI) dataset, whose native 8K ODIs are collected with an Insta360 TITAN camera and 8K super-resolution images are generated from SOTA open-sourced algorithms. Recognizing high-frequency signals are essential for differentiating non-native 8K ODIs, a frequency-aware model is designed to capture high-frequency details. Specially, to maintain high-frequency details kept in high-resolutions while reduce computational costs brought by high-resolutions, we propose a frequency-aware compressor module to suppress feature channels dominated by low-frequency details. Finally, our model achieves 97.2% accuracy in detecting non-native 8K ODIs, implying that super-resolution for ODIs can still be improved for visual experience in VR applications.

title: Attentive Routing: Feasible Routing via Communication-Aware Attention in LEO Satellite Networks
authors: Xiujie Wang, Lizhe Liu, Bin Wang, Jianwei Li, Yuxiang Hu, Dongdong Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The rapid development of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks has elicited demand for intelligent routing solutions to balance traffic loads and enhance quality of service (QoS). However, constrained by the inability to systematically collect and process routing information for building supervised training databases, most existing methods rely on reinforcement learning approaches that assume a fixed number of satellites and a stable training environment, which need to reset state space and retrain model when encountering contingencies such as satellite failures. To improve the generalization capability of satellite routing, this paper innovatively proposes a structured approach for processing satellite communication information and a framework for feasible routing in LEO constellations via communicationaware attention, named Attentive Routing. This framework structurally encodes communication information, employs a communication-aware attention encoder to extract topological communication hotspot patterns, and utilizes a feasible satellite routing decoder to directly generate routing paths across different LEO topologies. For dynamic satellite quantity changes, we design multilevel embedding and positional encoding to serialize satellite topology and communication quality metrics into model inputs. Performance refinement is achieved through Reinforcement Learning from Environment (RLE), which continuously fine-tunes Attentive Routing using feedback from OMNeT++ simulations. Extensive evaluations demonstrate its capability to generate adaptive paths across varying satellite network topologies, which increases total throughput by approximately 5.5 %, reduces average end-to-end delay by at least 20 %, and improves bandwidth satisfaction by 27.5 %, all while maintaining an exceptionally low packet loss rate.

title: Timing Estimation for Deep Space Optical Communication Signals
authors: Bin Wang, Xiaoyan Wang, Yuning Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The deep space optical systems use the frames of pulse position modulation (PPM) of M slots and the inter-symbol guard times (ISGT) of P slots, and also use a single-photon-counting detector array on the flight terminal to detect the uplink optical signal at photon-starved (pW/m^2) power levels. An estimation method of timing offsets for the M PPM and P ISGT frame format, named ISGT timing offset estimation method, is proposed in this article. The timing information carried by the PPM and ISGT frames is extracted for demodulation and decoding. This method provides synchronization without using the data bandwidth compared with the pilot method, as well as eliminating or reducing the effect of the Earth and ambient background illumination. The algorithm of the ISGT timing offset estimation method is analyzed and the statistical modeling is implemented in the co-simulation of OptiSystem and MATLAB software. The feasibility of this method is proved by the simulation results.

title: An Improved K-means Algorithm based on Density-Weighted Canopy for Typical Wind Power Scenario Extraction
authors: Dongliang Nan, Jian Ma, Yinzhang Peng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: The stochastic nature of wind power poses challenges to power system planning. Accurate extraction of typical wind power scenarios is fundamental for optimizing energy storage and ensuring grid stability. While K-means is widely used, its performance is highly dependent on the pre-determined cluster number and random initial centroids, often leading to sub-optimal results. Furthermore, pre-clustering algorithms like Canopy rely on subjectively defined thresholds. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an improved K-means algorithm based on a density-weighted Canopy (DWC-Kmeans). This method introduces a density-weighting mechanism to automatically identify high-density data points as initial cluster centers. This approach adaptively determines the optimal cluster number and initial centroids, circumventing the arbitrary threshold settings. The proposed algorithm is validated using one year of measured output data from a 30MW wind farm. The clustering performance is quantitatively evaluated using the Silhouette Coefficient, Sum of Squared Errors, and the Davies-Bouldin Index. Results demonstrate that the proposed DWC-Kmeans algorithm significantly outperforms both the traditional K-means and the standard Canopy-Kmeans algorithms across all three metrics. The method provides an accurate, stable, and objective approach for extracting wind power scenarios, offering a robust data foundation for power system planning.

title: Entropy-Guided Active Learning Framework with Hybrid Acoustic and Deep Self-Supervised Features for Dysarthria Detection
authors: Vishwas Yeleshwaram, Sanjay Sayam, Y C A Padmanabha Reddy, Viswanath Pulabaigari
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Dysarthria is a neurological speech disorder characterized by a lack of clarity and intelligibility in speech due to damaged nervous systems. Although promising results have been achieved with automated detection methods, progress is limited due to the scarcity of labeled data and significant variability in speech patterns from speaker to speaker. In this paper, we present an active learning-powered data-efficient framework for dysarthria detection. Our approach avoids direct labeling efforts on large datasets; instead, the system repeatedly selects a batch of the samples it is most uncertain about and asks only for their annotation. Starting with a small labeled set, the model is gradually improved by adding high-uncertainty examples from a larger unlabeled pool, reducing the labeling effort while boosting accuracy. Our experiments show that this uncertainty-guided strategy can indeed yield strong performance with far fewer labeled samples, taking a practical step toward scalable and clinically usable dysarthria detection tools.

title: FRGDM: Pre- to Post-Contrast Medical Image Synthesis Based on Diffusion Models
authors: Shijian Chen, Chunlin He, Fuyou Fan, Yi Liu, Xinxiang Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Medical imaging offers essential insights into internal anatomical structures and disease progression. Contrast agents are commonly used to enhance image quality and improve diagnostic accuracy, but they may impose significant renal burdens, especially in patients with impaired kidney function. Diffusion models (DMs) have recently shown strong performance in medical image translation. However, conventional DMs rely on static guidance from the source image and often lose critical structural details during the forward diffusion, which are hard to fully recover. This limitation can result in anatomical distortion and diagnostic errors. We propose a Frequency-guided Recursive Diffusion Bridge Model (FRDBM) to address this issue. Unlike standard DMs, FRDBM defines the diffusion path between the source and target images and incorporates frequency-domain information to preserve structural fidelity. Our approach enables the generation of contrast-enhanced images from non-contrast ones, potentially avoiding the use of contrast agents. Experiments on MRI contrast enhancement demonstrate that FRDBM achieves state-of-the-art performance in preserving fine-grained anatomical details and diagnostic image quality, with quantitative metrics (PSNR: 29.775 dB on BraTs; SSIM: 0.917) surpassing all competing methods.

title: Transfer Learning Enabled Human Imaging in RIS-Aided Communication Systems
authors: Ruijie Fan, Yixuan Huang, Zhou He, Jie Yang, Shi Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Advances in mobile communication technology promote the development of integrated sensing and communication, where imaging is anticipated to be realized within communication systems. This motivates the need for improved wireless imaging techniques. Traditional compressed sensing and fourier transform imaging methods face the problems of unknown sparsity of the region of interest and requiring a large number of pilot signals. Although artificial intelligence based imaging methods in previous studies can partially solve these issues, they typically require large amounts of training data, which is hard to be obtained in wireless imaging scenarios. To address these challenges, we propose a new imaging method using transfer learning (TL). Specifically, we first train the neural network (NN) on a large synthetic dataset and fine-tune it using a small number of human images. We respectively employ a multilayer perceptron (MLP), a convolutional NN (CNN), and a U-NET to realize human imaging, where their performances are extensively compared. Simulation results reveal that our proposed method can achieve effective imaging of human body based on limited training data, even when considering multipath interference. We find CNNs exhibit better TL performance compared to MLPs.

title: Mesh-Based Planning for Distribution Networks Considering the Risk of Voltage Imbalance
authors: Xin Yang, Ran Xu, Fan Zhou, Tiancheng Shi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: The distributed access with single-phase and uncertain generation of renewable energy increases the risk of voltage unbalance limit violations in distribution network. With the increasing penetration of renewable energy generation, it is important to study how to mitigate the impacts of intermittent renewable energy generation on the risk of voltage unbalance limit violation in distribution network. This paper proposes a shared energy storage allocation strategy and optimal operation method based on global sensitivity analysis (GSA). First, a back propagation neural network (BPNN) based probabilistic voltage unbalance factor calculation model for distribution network is constructed, and the risk index of distribution network probabilistic voltage unbalance factor limit violation is defined. It can quickly and accurately quantify the impacts of uncertain renewable energy generation on the risk of voltage unbalance limit violation in distribution network. Then, a GSA method based on Wasserstein distance is proposed to identify the critical renewable energy sources affecting distribution network voltage unbalance. Finally, the GSA-based shared energy storage allocation strategy and the rolling prediction optimization-based operation method of shared energy storage are proposed. Simulation results on the IEEE 123-bus system demonstrate that the proposed framework reduces the maximum probabilistic voltage unbalance exceedance risk by over 74 % compared to the case without energy storage. The rolling optimization operation effectively smooths renewable power fluctuations, validating the practical efficacy of the method in enhancing distribution network security. The effectiveness of the proposed methods is verified through the simulation analysis of IEEE 123 bus distribution network.

title: Real-Time Anomaly Detection of Advanced Persistent Threats Using Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: S. Satheeshkumar, P. T. Kalaivaani, V. Lathika, Hema S J, K. Karthick, R. Rajalakshmi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs) pose a serious risk to modern digital systems because of their stealthy and multi-stage nature, which allows them to slip past traditional security defenses. This study introduces a real-time anomaly detection framework based on Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to accurately identify APT activities throughout their attack lifecycle. Unlike conventional detection models, the proposed system continuously learns normal network behavior and flags unusual patterns linked to reconnaissance, lateral movement, data theft, and initial compromise. A custom-built dataset capturing different APT behaviors, along with associated tactics, techniques, and indicators of compromise (IoCs), was used for model training and testing. The GAN design features a generator that replicates normal network traffic and a discriminator that identifies malicious deviations in real time. Through this adversarial learning process, the model effectively recognizes complex patterns and distinguishes between legitimate and suspicious activities. Experimental evaluations show that the model delivers high accuracy in detecting subtle and evolving APT attacks. By enabling real-time monitoring and full-stage threat coverage, this approach provides a reliable and scalable solution for proactive cybersecurity defense.

title: On the Natural Frequency of a Coriolis Vibration Gyroscope with a Cylindrical Resonator
authors: Vadym Avrutov, Serhii Golovach, Hryhorii Strokach, Viacheslav Tsisarz, Oleksii Petrenko
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: The eigenfrequencies of the Coriolis Vibrational Gyroscope with cylindrical resonator and their influence on the output signal of the gyroscope are considered. The obtained formulas for calculating the eigenfrequencies of CVG with a metal cylindrical resonator have been experimentally confirmed in practice by the acoustic method. The results of spectral analysis of a single impact on the resonator showed the splitting of the resonant frequency into two components with peaks of 5781.05 Hz and 5787.53 Hz. The non-ideal geometry of the cylinder causes this splitting. The results of the experimental study on the real split resonant frequency and Q-factor demonstrate the importance of considering the non-ideal resonator for the accuracy of modeling its dynamic characteristics. The results of modeling the movement of the CVG are presented, which demonstrate a significant impact on the characteristics of the oscillating system not only of the different frequencies, but also of its phases relative to the excitation axis X and the measurement axis Y. Assessment of the influence of multi-frequency on the properties of the CVG as an angular velocity sensor indicates the possibility of using this influence in the CVG control system. It is demonstrated that the use of the CVG control system in scanning mode for multi-frequency resonator characteristics has found practical application in CVG balancing operations, specifically in identifying and removing unbalanced resonator masses.

title: Efficient Cross-chain Framework for Privacy-Preserving and Auditable Data Retrieval
authors: Zheng Chen, Jiageng Chen, Kazumasa Omote, Jianqun Cui, Qianhong Wu, Willy Susilo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Blockchain-based information storage and retrieval systems face significant challenges in achieving efficiency, privacy, and auditability when operating across heterogeneous blockchain platforms. Existing solutions often struggle to balance these requirements, particularly in cross-chain environments involving both public and consortium blockchains. This paper proposes a novel framework that leverages cross-chain technology to address these limitations. The framework integrates multi-party threshold cross-chain consensus to optimize verification efficiency and reduce the computational burden on trusted nodes. To ensure privacy-preserving information querying and retrieval, advanced cryptographic techniques are employed. Additionally, a dedicated auditor set within the consortium blockchain is introduced to detect malicious behavior and enforce regulatory compliance. Comparative evaluations demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms existing methods in terms of privacy protection, efficiency, and auditability. Experimental results on Hyperledger Fabric demonstrate significant improvements in throughput, achieving at least 20 Transactions Per Second (TPS), along with latency below 3.5 seconds and 300MB memory utilization under standard PC configurations. These findings validate the framework's practical viability for secure and efficient cross-chain information retrieval while maintaining superior performance compared to existing solutions.

title: Security-Enhanced Decentralized Conditional Privacy-Preserving Authentication in VANETs
authors: Shuqin Luo, Xinghua Li, Yinbin Miao, Xuelin Cao, Zhan Zhang, Yunwei Wang, Robert H. Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: To ensure the legitimacy of communicators while ad dressing the privacy concerns of vehicles in vehicular ad-hoc networks (VANETs), conditional privacy-preserving authentication (CPPA) schemes have been proposed. Given that existing schemes suffer from single point of failure due to centralized authorities, several distributed CPPA schemes have been proposed. However, these schemes all ignore the tight cementation between system secret keys and the authority, which could be a serious threat to system security, that the compromised authority may leak the system secret key. To address these issues, we propose a security enhanced decentralized conditional privacy-preserving authentication (DCPPA) scheme. DCPPA first introduces a decentralized system master key generation (DSMKG) mechanism without a centralized secret sharer, ensuring that the system secret key remains hidden from any single authority. Based on DSMKG, DCPPA then implements a lightweight verifiable pseudonym self-generation strategy without the system master secret key escrow problem, thus providing flexible pseudonym updating and reliable de-anonymization. Moreover, we implement DCPPA over a hyperelliptic curve cryptosystem (HECC) to balance the system performance. Considering the additional communication processes due to the decentralized feature, we introduce a symmetric balanced incomplete block design (SBIBD) to enhance the communication efficiency. We demonstrate the excellent security of DCPPA through an in-depth security analysis, while demonstrate that the overhead of DCPPA is at ms level through experiments.

title: 50 GHz Piezoelectric Acoustic Filter
authors: Omar Barrera, Jack Kramer, Lezli Matto, Vakhtang Chulukhadze, Sinwoo Cho, Michael Evan Liao, Mark S. Goorsky, Ruochen Lu
abstract: This paper presents significant frequency scaling of acoustic filter technology to 50 GHz. This achievement is enabled by the periodically poled piezoelectric film LiNbO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> multilayer stack, in which piezoelectric thin-films of alternating orientations are transferred in sequence, thereby allowing efficient exploitation of high-order modes with high quality factor and coupling coefficient in a thicker piezoelectric stack. The demonstrated filter is comprised of twelfth-order symmetric mode lateral-field-excited bulk acoustic wave resonators (XBARs), built on a 4-layer periodically poled piezoelectric 128° Y-cut lithium niobate (LiNbO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>) stack. The filter exhibits 3.3 dB insertion loss and a fractional bandwidth of 2.9% . The miniature design, with a footprint of 0.36 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, makes it promising for future wireless front-end applications. These results represent the highest frequency acoustic filters reported to date, setting a new benchmark in piezoelectric filter technology. Upon further development, the platform could enable filters further into the FR2 range, essential for next-generation communication systems.

title: A Cryogenic 2T GC-eDRAM With an Inverter-Based Readout Scheme and an 80 ms Retention Time
authors: Elisheva Berkowitz, Yosi Greenblatt, Claudio G. Jakobson, Adam Teman, Joseph Shor
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: This article presents a novel sensing scheme for a 2T gain-cell embedded dynamic random access memory (GC-eDRAM) optimized for cryogenic temperatures. The proposed design integrates a highly accurate sense amplifier (SA) with a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm 3\sigma $</tex-math> </inline-formula> sensitivity of 11.16 mV and an internally generated, adjustable reference voltage (VREF) with 9.5 mV step resolution. Additionally, an on-chip ring-oscillator-based design-for-test (RO-DFT) mechanism is introduced to determine the optimal VREF postfabrication, improving readout reliability while adapting to process variations. Simulation results, based on a 65nm CMOS process calibrated for 77 K operation, demonstrate a significant extension of the data retention time (DRT) to 80 ms, reducing refresh cycle frequency and lowering power consumption—critical for cryogenic applications such as infrared imaging. The proposed architecture provides a power-efficient, high-density embedded memory solution for low-temperature computing environments.

title: Space-time Coded Differential Modulation for Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces
authors: Jiawei Qiu, Harry Leib
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) hold the promise of significantly improving coverage, as well as spectral and energy efficiency in wireless communication systems. Techniques based on RIS form a key technology for 6G systems, attracting a significant research interest. An important issue in RIS technology is Channel State Information (CSI), which is much more difficult to acquire in such systems. This work considers a Differential Space-Time Modulation (DSTM) technique integrated with Differential Reflecting Modulation (DRM) termed DRM-DSTM. Such a scheme does not require CSI, while providing error rate gains. The DSTM scheme is based on unitary group codes. We first consider uncoded DRM for RIS to serve as a reference point. Next we provide an overview of DSTM and outline the procedures for its integration with DRM. We consider the issue of reflecting patter selection in such systems, and explore the extension of both the original DRM and the coded DRM-DSTM scheme to a larger number of RIS reflecting patterns K. Furthermore we provide tables of cyclic and dicyclic codes for K' = 2, 3, 4 suitable for DRM-DSTM. Encoding and decoding complexities are studied as well. Extensives Monte-Carlo simulation results over quasi-static Rayleigh fading channels confirm the effectiveness of the DRM-DSTM coded scheme, illustrating its advantages over uncoded DRM with proper system parameters.

title: Unlicensed Millimeter-Wave NR-U and WiGig Coexistence: A Hybrid Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Xiaowen Ye, Xianxin Song, Liqun Fu
abstract: This paper investigates an unlicensed millimeter-wave (mmWave) coexistence system, where the new radio-based access to unlicensed spectrum (NR-U) network and the incumbent Wireless Gigabit (WiGig) network share the same spectrum resources to transmit data packets. The total data rate of NR-U is maximized by jointly optimizing the user equipment (UE) scheduling and hybrid beamforming, subject to the constraints of the quality-of-service (QoS) requirements of all UEs and the maximum transmit power at the gNB. Besides, NR-U needs to avoid excessive interference to WiGig, without acquiring any prior information about WiGig. To circumvent this problem, we put forth a model-free joint optimization scheme, referred to as Deep Q-Policy Gradient (DQPG), based on the deep reinforcement learning (DRL) technique. Specifically, a hybrid DRL framework is first proposed to support DQPG, where the deep double Q-network (D2QN) and double-critic-based deep deterministic policy gradient (D3PG) algorithms are invoked to optimize UE scheduling in the discrete action domain and hybrid beamforming in the continuous action domain, respectively. Thereafter, a new reward function and a scaled action selection policy are judiciously designed for DQPG to satisfy various constraints. To capture the complex coupling relationship between different optimization variables, we further propose a parallel experience replay mechanism to maintain training synchronization between D2QN and D3PG. In addition, a transfer learning approach is introduce to accelerate the convergence of DQPG. Simulation results demonstrate that while satisfying the QoS requirements of all UEs, compared with other coexistence schemes, DQPG (i) attains a higher total data rate of NR-U, (ii) causes less interference to WiGig, and (iii) converges faster. Furthermore, under different numbers of UEs and QoS requirements of UEs, DQPG is more robust than benchmarks.

title: Spatial-temporal Expanding-accumulating Strategy Based BACF Algorithm for ViSAR Dim and Small Target Tracking
authors: Shuncheng Tan, Jing Wang, Xiaoqing Tian, Jing Liu
abstract: A large number of publications on detection and tracking have appeared in the field of video synthetic aperture radar (ViSAR) due to its capability of forming high-resolution videos for a region of interest. In this work, a novel spatial-temporal expanding-accumulating strategy based background-aware correlation filter (BACF) algorithm, namely STEA-BACF, is proposed to tackle theViSAR tracking problem. In the proposed STEA-BACF algorithm, multiple response values are selected from the response map to generate multiple candidate bounding boxes. Each candidate bounding box is then expanded in both spatial and temporal domains to obtain a number of sub-candidate bounding boxes, resulting in a tree-structure composed of multiple candidate bounding boxes, the response values of multiple candidate bounding boxes are accumulated over multiple frames. Finally, a backtracking algorithm is used to select a path with the maximum accumulated response values, thus determining the target as well as its trajectory simultaneously. A number of experiments are conducted using the ViSAR data released by Sandia National Laboratory. The experimental results show that the proposed STEA-BACF algorithm outperforms several state-of-the-art model-based trackers.

title: Primal-Dual Strategy for Composite Optimization over Directed Graphs
authors: Sajad Zandi, Meisam Esfandiari, Sipei Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: We introduce a distributed optimization framework for directed graph networks that addresses composite objective functions with smooth local components and a shared convex regulariser. Our method employs a novel primal-dual (PD) algorithm that incorporates time-varying adaptive coefficient weights and operates efficiently under the constraints of directed network topologies. The proposed approach achieves provable convergence guarantees under strong convexity conditions. Unlike existing methods, our approach guarantees linear convergence under strong convexity assumptions and introduces new step-size bounds, improving both stability and performance. The adaptive weights dynamically optimize information sharing among agents, significantly reducing communication costs and enhancing robustness. We theoretically establish the convergence properties and demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed algorithm through simulations, showcasing faster convergence and improved accuracy compared to the methods in the literature. These results highlight the potential of the proposed method for scalable and efficient distributed optimization in directed networks.

title: Exact Penalty Design for Distributed Projection-Free Optimization with Event-Triggered Communication
authors: Xin Yu, Songsong Cheng, Xianlin Zeng, Yuan Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This paper investigates distributed convex nonsmooth optimization problems with different feasible constraint sets. To address these types of problems, we develop a projection-free algorithm that employs an exact penalty technique, which outperforms existing methods in the same category, such as the renowned Frank-Wolfe algorithm, in handling different feasible constraint sets. The proposed algorithm not only avoids the prohibitive computation involved in projecting subgradients, but also eliminates the projection of variables in the exact penalty function, unlike conventional exact penalty algorithms. Moreover, we employ an event-triggered scheme for the distributed projection-free algorithm to effectively save communication resources. Furthermore, we demonstrate that the proposed algorithm converges to its optimal solution through nonsmooth Lyapunov analysis and the Filippov solution, and we illustrate its effectiveness by two numerical examples.

title: Fine-Grained Feature Refinement and Spatial Alignment for Few-Shot Object Detection with Minimal Data
authors: Zhengheng Zhang, Suolan Liu, Hongyuan Wang
abstract: Few-shot object detection (FSOD) aims to detect objects from novel classes with minimal annotated examples. While prevailing meta-learning approaches extract class prototypes from the support branch and integrate them into the query branch for prediction, they often suffer from insufficient interaction between the two branches before final fusion, resulting in coarse prototype representations and less discriminative query features. This limitation becomes particularly critical in extremely data-scarce scenarios. To address this, we propose a novel framework that promotes fine-grained collaborative interaction between the query and support branches. Our approach introduces two key modules: the Fine-Grained Feature Coupling (FC) module, which aligns query features with class prototypes in the spatial feature domain via a similarity matrix, and further employs a foreground attention mechanism to suppress background interference; and the Prototype Fusion (PF) module, which adaptively integrates multiple feature interactions through learnable weights, thereby simultaneously enhancing intra-class compactness and inter-class separability. Experiments on PASCAL VOC and MS COCO datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance under various few-shot settings, validating the effectiveness of the proposed modules in improving feature discriminability and model robustness.

title: Flexible Antennas for 6G Communication and Harsh Environments: Design, Materials, Fabrication, Testing, and AI/ML Integration
authors: Archana T. Anilkumar, Zahid Hassan, Sharmin Shila, Rabab E. Zannat, Praveen Sekhar
abstract: Sixth-generation (6G) wireless communication systems impose stringent requirements on antenna performance, including ultra-high data rates, ultra-lowlatency, massive connectivity, high-frequency operation, advanced beamforming, and long-term reliability. Achieving these goals at mm-wave and sub-terahertz frequencies introduces major challenges such as severe path loss, hardware complexity, thermal stress, limited flexibility, and performance degradation under mechanical and environmental variations. This review presents a comprehensive overview of 6G antenna requirements, key antenna technologies, material platforms, fabrication techniques, AI/ML integration, and reliability assessment. Advanced antenna solutions, including hybrid antenna arrays, antenna collocation, metasurfaces and reconfigurable intelligent surfaces, and mm-wave and terahertz antennas are discussed alongside their limitations in flexibility and robustness. Flexible antennas are highlighted as a promising solution, enabled by emerging materials and additive fabrication methods such as inkjet printing, aerosol jet printing, screen printing, and additive manufacturing. In addition, standardized testing and reliability evaluation under harsh environments, such as bending, thermal cycling, humidity, and aging are reviewed. Finally, the integration of artificial intelligence and machine learning for antenna design optimization, fabrication control, and performance enhancement is discussed, outlining future directions for reliable and scalable 6G antenna systems.

title: Hot-Electron Hardening in GaN-Based Power Devices by Selective p-GaN Gate Epitaxy
authors: Haohao Chen, Jinjin Tang, Junting Chen, Jianxun Liu, Siyuan Ye, Junlei Zhao, Zhaojun Liu, Jun Ma, Qian Sun, Mengyuan Hua
abstract: A selective gate epitaxy strategy is proposed for p-GaN gate formation in enhancement-mode high-electron-mobility transistors (HEMTs) to enhance robustness against hot-electron effects. This approach avoids p-GaN growth and etching processes in the access region, preserving the high-quality as-grown barrier layer. Compared to the conventional p-GaN gate HEMTs, devices with selective epitaxial gate demonstrate <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.5\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher drain breakdown voltage at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {GS}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 6 V and significantly reduced <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>-resistance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${R}_{\text {on}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> degradation during long-term semi-on stress with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {DS}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> ranging from 100 to 400 V. The selective p-GaN gate epitaxy is also compatible with the gate recess process, maintaining normally-off operation with thick AlGaN barriers, achieving further <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>-resistance reduction.

title: Spatio-Temporal Implicit Neural Representation for Ionospheric Tomography with Multi-LEO Occultation Data
authors: Fangsong Yang, Wang Li, Jiale Li, Xiaoqing Zuo, Dongsheng Zhao, Kefei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Current ionospheric tomography faces a bottleneck: heavy reliance on empirical background fields leads to poor spatiotemporal accuracy and robustness. To resolve this, we propose an inversion strategy fusing interpretability analysis, Spatio-Temporal Implicit Neural Representation (ST-INR), and the Multiplicative Algebraic Reconstruction Technique (MART). By training a physics-informed ST-INR model on multi-LEO satellite occultation data, we overcome the spectral bias of traditional networks to generate high-fidelity prior fields with fine physical gradients. These priors serve as initializations for MART-based refinement using ground-based GNSS Slant Total Electron Content (STEC), ensuring alignment with real-time observations. Validation during an independent test period featuring a severe geomagnetic storm (Dst approximately -150 nT) reveals significant improvements. Compared to the IRI2020 model, our method reduces the 3D electron density reconstruction RMSE by 71.9% (quiet) and 61.2% (storm), while improving key physical parameters (NmF2 and hmF2) by 45.0% and 40.5%, respectively. It also significantly outperforms the traditional Back-Propagation (BP) neural network. Comparisons with Global Ionospheric Map (GIM) products from the International GNSS Service (IGS) further verify high horizontal consistency, particularly in regions lacking station coverage. This study confirms that the proposed method effectively mitigates reconstruction challenges in data-sparse areas, significantly enhancing physical realism under complex space weather conditions.

title: STQS: A Unified System Architecture for Spatial Temporal Quantum Sensing
authors: Anastashia Jebraeilli, Chenxu Liu, Keyi Yin, Samuel Stein, Erik Lentz, Yufei Ding, Ang Li
abstract: None

title: Utilizing Internet of Things in Human-Building Interaction to Support Sustainable Built Environments
authors: Suhas Prakash Devmane, Rana Omer, Charith Perera
abstract: None

title: Power-Delay Trade-Off Analysis of Low-Voltage Flip-Flop Architectures for Energy-Constrained Applications
authors: Pandrala Pravardhan, Yarra Sridevi, Shilagani Shivaprasad, J. Ajayan, Ponnaboina Charan Sai, MD Badhesha
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative analysis of three CMOS flip-flop (FF) architectures 18-Transistor True Single-Phase Clock (18TSPC), Sense-Controlled Delay Flip-Flop (SCDFF), and Sense-Square-Controlled Flip-Flop (S<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>CFF) to assess their suitability for low-power VLSI systems. SPICE simulations were run and tested with supply voltages of 1.0V-0.7V and operating temperatures of 27oC to 107oC. In experimental findings, the SCDFF becomes the ultimate optimal structure to adopt in the design of energy-saving and high-speed applications. The SCDFF has the lowest power consumption of 10.42μW at the ultra-low-voltage corner (0.7V, 27oC), which is much lower than the 18TSPC (10.9μW) and the S2CFF (16.09μW). Moreover, the SCDFF has better timing performance with clock-to-Q delay of 12.13pS at 1.0V compared to a S2CFF where latency and power dissipation is higher (59.45μW). Although the 18TSPC exhibits significant thermal performance with a 34.31% delay reduction as temperature decreases between 107oC and 27oC, SCDFF is the one with the most balanced Power-Delay Product (PDP) and therefore it is the best fit next-generation low-power VLSI systems.

title: Resolution, Compression, and GAN-Enhanced Evaluation of YOLO Models on the Poribohon-BD Vehicle Dataset
authors: Sabrina Masum Meem, Mohammed Tahmid Hossain, Sami Rashid, Ashraful Islam, Rashedur Rahman, Istiaq Ahmed, Asif Mahmud, Saadia Binte Alam, M. Ashraful Amin
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Object detection in resource-constrained environments poses challenges for real-time intelligent transportation systems (ITS), where low resolution, compression artifacts, and bandwidth limitations hinder performance. This study evaluates YOLOv8, YOLOv10, and YOLOv12 on the Poribohon-BD dataset of 15 native Bangladeshi vehicle classes using three dataset variants: resized images, JPEG-compressed images (Q50, Q75), and GAN-enhanced images restored with Real-ESRGAN. The results show that resolution is the dominant factor, and YOLOv8 delivers the most stable performance under all conditions. YOLOv10 generalized slightly better at very low resolutions, while YOLOv12 was competitive at higher resolutions under compression and GAN restoration but degraded at lower scales. GAN enhancement improved accuracy only when there was sufficient base resolution, revealing a gap between perceptual fidelity and detection accuracy. Overall, these findings provide a systematic framework for selecting YOLO architectures and preprocessing pipelines under degraded inputs, supporting deployment in real-world ITS.

title: Blink2Speak: Intelligent Blink Based Morse Code Interpreter
authors: Votarikari Shireesha, Yerram Sneha, Muntha Raju, Varkala Arun Kumar, Thummanapelli Yashaswi Naveena, Vantipenti Sai Krishna Reddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Blink2Speak was created to ease the communication with them. It is a smart device that offers the user to interact through blinking lights. The system uses a webcam and some programs such as Mediapipe and OpenCV to detect blinks. So that it can produce Morse code, it is able to differentiate between short and long blinks. This code is then translated to letters, words and full sentences using a Morse code dictionary. Pyttsx3 that is a python module is used to speak out the text and put it on the screen. This has enabled users to have conversations without the need to use their hands. You only need a computer. Blink2Speak is a comprehensive and convenient approach that improves access and independence, as well as the overall quality of life, of physically challenged people at a relatively low cost. A computer and a webcam is all you need.

title: MDT-TM: Multi-Dimensional Metamorphic Testing of Terminology in Machine Translation
authors: Zhifang Tan, Chuanqi Tao
abstract: Deep neural networks have substantially improved machine translation quality; however, terminology remains prone to mistranslation, leading to misinterpretation and communication barriers. Such terminology-related errors persist in MT, often causing biased understanding and hindering communication. Existing terminology evaluations frequently rely on local substitutions or deletions in the source sentence, an approach that is both noise-prone and cumbersome. To address this challenge, guided by the principle of perturbation invariance, this paper proposes a terminology-oriented, multidimensional metamorphic testing method. We introduce two terminology mutation operations: (i) inserting hyphens and (ii) repeating the term at the sentence-initial or sentence-final position, to assess systems’ robustness to terminology. Experimental results show that MDT-TM achieves detection accuracies of 81.55%, 80.18%, and 76.65% on Google, Microsoft Bing, and Baidu, respectively.

title: Pest-Forecaster: A Framework for Multi-Term Forecasting of Stored-Grain Pest Development Based on Trap Counts
authors: Shiying Li, Jiangtao Li, Zhaojun Cui, Chuanyang Ma, Huiling Zhou
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: Trap counts are always used as an indicator of population density during the early infestation of stored-grain pests within grain piles. In this paper, we introduce a framework for multi-term forecasting of the future trap counts over multiple time spans to estimate the population dynamics of storedgrain pests, given input involving previous trap counts, grain temperatures, air relative humidity values among grain bulks, semantic information of trap location, and the Degree-Day value indicating the pest population dynamic. Specifically, the multiterm forecasting framework comprises short-term, mid-term, and long-term models to forecast the mean value of future trap counts over the next 5 days, 10 days, and 20 days. The short-term model is a Gradient Boosting Decision Tree (GBDT) with long-term memory features involved to capture temporal relationships. The mid-term model is a two-layer stacked Long-Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network focusing on sequence learning. The long-term prediction model is an LSTM network incorporated with the attention mechanism to emphasize the importance of different time steps and longterm dependencies of the model in the sequences. Extensive experiments have been conducted to verify the rationality and validity of the Pest-Forecaster. The multi-term forecasting framework achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.913,0.933$</tex>, and 0.942 on future trap count prediction, demonstrating great potential for pest population dynamics forecasting.

title: A Packaging Structure for an Antenna-in-Package Module
authors: Ra-Min Tain, Chin-Sheng Wang, Chia-Fu Hsu, Po-Wen Hsiao, Chun-Hung Chen, Jun-Ho Chen, Tse-Wei Wang, Guang-Hwa Ma
abstract: Proof of concept has been successfully demonstrated for an electronic packaging structure of an antenna-in-package (AiP) module. This AiP module designed with a 2T3R array antenna has a compact form factor measuring <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 ~ m m} \times \mathbf{5. 5 ~ m m} \times \mathbf{1. 6 ~ m m}$</tex>. It includes a commercial chip-scale-packaging (CSP) RF IC featuring a mmWave radar sensor operating at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$57 \sim 60 \text{GHz}$</tex>, which is embedded in three stacked substrates: the antenna substrate, interposer, and IC carrier. The interposer contains coaxial vias to connect the antenna and RF IC, enhancing signal integrity. The innovation consists of separately making the antenna substrate, interposer, and IC carrier in panel format, allowing for symmetric build-up of each substrate to minimize warpage. Additionally, the yield of making each substrate is improved and cost is reduced. A key development involves the panel-to-panel polymer hybrid (or mixed) bonding of antenna substrate and interposer to avoid issues caused by asymmetric build-up processes. The polymer hybrid bonding technique employs a film of bonding resin filled with conductive paste in its through holes. The success of the bonding result depends on resin's rheological properties as well as the match with conductive paste's eutectic temperature. Therefore, the selection of suitable materials is important for bonding process. The warpage of the AiP after assembly can meet the JEDEC standard. Results of functional test indicate that this AiP is comparable to an existing commercial antenna-on-module (AoM) product. Preliminary reliability test also has been conducted and indicates that the AiP samples have passed the moisture, temperature cycling and high temperature storage tests.

title: Advanced XR Virtual Production Intelligent System Based on Multi-Source Sensor Fusion and Lagrangian-Constrained Optimization
authors: Wanju Fu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: This paper proposes an advanced intelligent system for XR virtual production based on multi-source sensor fusion and Lagrangian-constrained optimization (LCO) to address core challenges in virtual production, including insufficient perceptual consistency, low real-time rendering efficiency, and insufficient energy optimization. The system framework integrates multimodal inputs, including an IMU, RGB-D camera, microphone array, ambient light sensor, tactile feedback glove, and odor sensor, enabling unified modeling of position, pose, depth, acoustic, and environmental parameters. Methodologically, a multi-constraint optimization model is constructed, including constraints on spatiotemporal synchronization, rendering consistency, interaction latency, and energy optimization. Solvability is guaranteed using the Karush-Kuhn-Tucker condition. For scene rendering, the system combines multi-view data with an adaptive volume rendering strategy to achieve real-time 3D reconstruction under dynamic interaction conditions. Experiments were conducted in a virtual stage, collecting 50,000 frames of motion data, 10,000 frames of audio and video data, and 50 Hz tactile and odor signals. The results show that the perceptual consistency index (PCI) of the baseline method is 0.68 on average, while the Proposed-1 and Proposed-2 methods reach 0.84 and 0.87 respectively, and the standard deviation is significantly reduced from 0.12 to 0.08 and 0.07. In terms of dynamic region error (DRE), the error of the improved method during interactive operations is reduced by more than 60%. In terms of multi-view volume rendering efficiency (AMVR-E), the baseline method is 1.2 voxels/ms, while Proposed-1 and Proposed-2 reach 2.1 voxels/ms and 2.4 voxels/ms respectively. The above results verify the comprehensive advantages of the proposed method in perceptual consistency, interactive accuracy and rendering efficiency, and provide a scalable solution for virtual production in complex scenes.

title: Multi-Agent AI Chatbot for Proactive Threat Detection and Automated Incident Response in Hybrid Cloud Environments
authors: Sindhura Nimmakayala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The growing sophistication of hybrid cloud landscapes requires advanced offerings for threat detection, risk analyses and incident response. To contribute toward solving the above challenges, this paper introduces a Multi-Agent AI Chatbot Ecosystem to proactively solve them. The environment exploits a number of independent AI agents that interact, consider risks, and identify threats in real time as such the response to potential security insights should be fast and effective. The system is able to process security logs by leveraging NLP and machine learning algorithms, recognize vulnerabilities and automate incident response actions in a self-driven manner. This work investigates system the architecture and function of the ecosystem, applications and applicable scenarios in hybrid cloud infrastructures. The system is evaluated through performance metrics to show that it significantly reduces human intervention, improves response time and reduces risks. We also analyze the scalability of this system, and show that it can be deployed in different hybrid cloud environments. The present work is a notable advancement towards intelligent automation in cloud security operations, it provides an innovative breakthrough of proactive security management.

title: Adaptive Patterning®: Unlocking Scalable Density in Embedded Bridge Die Interposer
authors: Clifford Sandstrom, Erick Talain, Benedict San Jose, Jen-Kuang Fang, Ping-Feng Yang, Sheng-Feng Huang, Ping-Ching Shen
abstract: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) has created an escalating demand for high-density interposers to support increasingly complex and powerful system architectures.
