While silicon interposers are currently the predominant solution—particularly for processor-to-memory integration—molded fan-out embedded bridge die interposers are emerging as a highly scalable and cost-effective alternative for large AI devices. However, conventional manufacturing methods for embedded bridge die interposers face critical challenges, such as: extremely tight die placement tolerances <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\leq 1 \mu m)$</tex>, oversized via capture pads, which limit routing density, restricted bridge die aspect ratios reducing design flexibility. These limitations contribute to higher production costs, limited scalability, and yield issues—especially at volume. This paper introduces an innovative design using Adaptive Patterning ® (AP), a design-adaptive approach that fundamentally redefines the lithographic process. Leveraging maskless laser direct imaging (LDI), AP generates a unique, optimized pattern for each individual device, dynamically aligning interconnect features, such as dielectric vias and copper redistribution layers (RDLs), to the exact die placement on the molded reconstituted wafer or panel. This fully compensates for die shift introduced during assembly, ensuring accurate alignment and robust performance.

title: Simulation on a Hybrid Thermoelectric-Liquid Cooling Thermal Management System for 58Ah Power Lithium Iron Phosphate Batteries
authors: Changxing Lu, Tao Lin, Fengqin Han
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: To enhance the thermal safety of lithium iron phosphate batteries under high-rate conditions, this paper proposes a hybrid thermal management system based on the synergy of thermoelectric cooling and liquid cooling. A multiphysics field coupling simulation study was conducted for a 58 Ah square battery. The temperature rise patterns of the battery under different discharge rates were analyzed, and the influence mechanisms of TEC current and cooling water flow rate on the system performance were explored. The simulation results show that the battery heat generation rate increases sharply with the discharge rate, and at a 4C discharge rate, the temperature rise rate exceeds <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4^{\circ} \mathrm{C} / \min$</tex>. When the discharge rate is 1 C, the optimal TEC current is 2 A, which can control the battery temperature to be around the ambient temperature of 25° C. The cooling water flow rate in the range of 0.3 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> can effectively control the temperature at the TEC heat end.

title: An Integrated GCN–GAT–AE Framework for Robust Anomaly Detection in Industrial IoT Environments
authors: Suhani Panda, Sanjana B, Harsha Rajkumar, Saravanan Palani
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Over the past few years, the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) has quickly changed long established industries by connecting large networks of smart sensors, machines, and devices to permit real-time monitoring, data driven decision making, and automation. Yet, the growing scale and complexity of IIoT environments also make them highly exposed to various cyber threats and anomalies, which can disrupt operations, cause financial losses, and create danger. Existing anomaly detection methods often struggle to accurately capture the complex spatial, temporal, and structural dependencies in IIoT data, especially in real time and under resource-constrained edge deployments.To address these issues, a seven -stage hybrid deep learning framework combining Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN), Graph Attention Networks (GAT), Autoencoders (AE), and residual connections is proposed for robust real-time anomaly detection in Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) environments. The architecture shows spatial, temporal, and structural relationships among IIoT sensor data by transforming tabular traffic logs from the Edge-IIoTset dataset into graph representations using cosine-based KNN connectivity. This allows the model to detect subtle and complex anomalies in highly dynamic IIoT systems. By combining attention-based feature refinement with reconstruction loss, the model enhances sensitivity to rare attacks and improves robustness. The proposed approach achieves high performance on key metrics of 95.0% classification accuracy, ROC-AUC of 0.98, PR-AUC of 0.97, and an inference latency of just 8.1 milliseconds which makes it highly suitable for deployment on edge devices in real-time industrial monitoring systems.

title: Robust Beamforming via Graph Neural Networks and Attention Mechanisms for MU-MISO Systems
authors: Ruichang Li, Weijie Jin, Jing Zhang, Hengtao He, Yong Li, Shi Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Channel state information (CSI) uncertainty poses significant challenges in maintaining reliable signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) performance for multi-user multiple-input single-output (MU-MISO) systems. To solve this issue, this paper develops a robust beamforming design for MU-MISO systems under imperfect CSI. We investigate the average performance over the distribution of the actual CSI and reformulate the beamforming optimization problem to maximize the lower bound of the weighted sum-rate. Based on the derived algorithm, we analyze the structure of the optimal beamforming vector under imperfect CSI. Then we propose a model-driven deep learning network that integrates graph neural networks and attention mechanisms to enhance the beamforming performance and computational efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves superior robustness to imperfect CSI while reducing computational complexity compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Novel Capacitively Coupled Electrical Impedance Tomography Sensor for Stroke Detection
authors: Yandan Jiang, Zenglan Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Early and rapid detection of stroke is crucial for patient prognosis. Electrical impedance tomography (EIT) is a promising technique for developing wearable stroke detection and monitoring devices. In this work, with a 4-layer hemisphere model of head, a capacitively coupled electrical impedance tomography (CCEIT) sensor is developed for contactless 3D brain imaging. The electrode arrangement of the sensor is determined by comparing the response characteristics and sensitivity matrix of different sensor configurations. Image reconstruction experiment was carried out on a data acquisition system developed with an impedance analyzer to evaluate the imaging and stroke detection capability of the sensor. Anomalies of conductive agar balls with the diameters of 4cm and 2cm were used to simulate the stroke. Experimental results show that the developed CCEIT sensor is feasible and effective in contactless 3D stroke imaging. The reconstructed images can tell the existence and approximate location of the stroke, indicating the potential of the sensor in further clinical applications. The location error of the stroke in the reconstructed images is 14.29%.

title: Deep Learning-Based Content-Aware Video Coding for HTTP Adaptive Streaming
authors: Yiying Wei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Video streaming dominates Internet traffic, and the demand for efficient compression continues to grow with the increasing use of high-definition formats and the need for low-latency delivery. Although recent codecs have improved coding efficiency, they still rely on hand-crafted tools and face difficulties in adapting to diverse content under bandwidth constraints. This doctoral research explores deep learning-based, content-aware video coding for HTTP Adaptive Streaming (HAS). Two research directions are pursued: (i) a neural compression framework that leverages content-aware deep neural networks (DNNs) to enhance compression efficiency, and (ii) implicit neural representations (INRs) that enable adaptive and efficient video streaming. Preliminary results show significant bitrate savings and competitive quality compared with state-of-the-art approaches, underscoring the potential of content-aware deep learning to advance both efficiency and adaptability in video compression.

title: Active Eavesdropping Detection based on Multi-Feature Fusion
authors: Mingfang Li, Zheng Dou, Yanqi Ji, Jian Dong, Hang Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: With the rapid development of space-air-ground integrated networks, it is increasingly important to ensure secure and reliable data transmission in heterogeneous wireless environments. Due to the wireless nature of multi-domain networks, they are especially vulnerable to malicious attacks, such as jamming and active eavesdropping. Therefore, this paper proposes a cross-dimensional multi-feature fusion-based active eavesdropping attack detection (CMAD) scheme. The scheme integrates radio frequency fingerprints features and channel state information, utilizing deep learning to extract both device-specific physical-layer characteristics and the dynamic variations in the channel. CMAD can detect eavesdropping in a timely manner, effectively prevent malicious attacks, and ensure a safe and reliable communication environment. Simulation results show that the rate of CMAD based on Resnet can reach up to 99% in complex environments, showing strong robustness.

title: Research on Histopathological Image Identification of Intestinal Cancer Based on Deep Learning
authors: Jiarong Liu, Yujie Jing, Chen Li, Xiaoyan Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Colorectal cancer is the third most common malignant tumor globally and the fourth leading cause of cancer-related deaths. In developed countries such as the United States, it ranks as the second leading cause of cancer-related deaths, and its incidence remains high in Asia. Traditional diagnostic methods rely heavily on histopathological image analysis, as these images contain abundant phenotypic information. To enhance the objectivity and efficiency of such analysis, machine learning methods have been gradually applied. Based on existing image detection models, this study proposes a deep learning-based detection model for colorectal cancer histopathological images. The model employs the classical YOLO-v7 model integrated with an attention mechanism to detect six types of lesions and constructs a well-annotated image dataset for algorithm performance evaluation. By optimizing the model architecture, loss function, and image preprocessing process, the improved YOLO-v7 model demonstrates significant advantages over traditional methods in both accuracy and efficiency.

title: Environmental Rate Manipulation Attacks on Power Grid Security
authors: Yonatan Gizachew Achamyeleh, Yang Xiang, Yun-Ping Hsiao, Yasamin Moghaddas, Mohammad Abdullah Al Faruque
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: The growing complexity of global supply chains has made hardware Trojans a significant threat in sensor-based power electronics. Traditional Trojan designs depend on digital triggers or fixed threshold conditions that can be detected during standard testing. In contrast, we introduce Environ-mental Rate Manipulation (ERM), a novel Trojan triggering mechanism that activates by monitoring the rate of change in environmental parameters rather than their absolute values. This approach allows the Trojan to remain inactive under normal conditions and evade redundancy and sensor-fusion defenses. We implement a compact 14 µm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> circuit that mea-sures capacitor charging rates in standard sensor front-ends and disrupts inverter pulse-width modulation PWM signals when a rapid change is induced. Experiments on a commercial Texas Instruments solar inverter demonstrate that ERM can trigger catastrophic driver chip failure. Furthermore, ETAP simulations indicate that a single compromised 100 kW in-verter may initiate cascading grid instabilities. The attack's significance extends beyond individual sensors to entire classes of environmental sensing systems common in power electronics, demonstrating fundamental challenges for hardware security.

title: Study on Flexible Networking Scheme of Distribution Network for Large-Scale Integration of PV, Energy Storage and Charging Systems
authors: Jian Wang, Kuanxiang Zhao, Qingsheng Li, Xuepeng Mou, Dongjunming Yang, Zhen Li, Zhanpeng Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: With growing environmental concerns, large-scale PV power generation is critical for energy transition. However, its inherent instability poses threats to grid safety and economy, while existing PV-energy storage operation optimization often overlooks key constraints. To address this, this paper proposes a flexible distribution network solution integrating PV, energy storage, and charging systems, including a BELBIC-based adaptive controller, power coordination optimization, and a feeder fault restoration strategy. Innovations vs. traditional schemes: BELBIC-virtual synchronous generator deep coupling resolves droop control's voltage-current sharing conflict, and a full-scenario “normal operation-fault recovery” framework integrates load balancing, transformer loss minimization, and fault power supply. Simulation results verify effectiveness: accurate power tracking, load balancing, and loss reduction under normal conditions; stable DC bus voltage and critical load supply under faults.

title: Distributionally Robust Planning for Cyber-Physical-Coupled Distribution Systems Considering Multiple Uncertainties
authors: Sheng Zhang, Gang Zhang, Yi Ding, Feng Zhang, Shiyin Liu, Hongda Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: This paper addresses the complex challenges of cyber-physical-coupling, disaster intensity, and fault probability distribution uncertainties in power distribution systems under extreme typhoon disasters. A three-layer distributed robust planning method for cyber-physical-coupled distribution systems is proposed. This approach employs stochastic optimization and distributionally robust optimization to cope with multiple disaster uncertainties, constructing a three-layer optimization model of “upper-layer planning defense-middle-layer fault attack-lower-layer regulation and recovery” to effectively formulate pre-disaster strategies such as line hardening, distributed generation allocation, and pre-positioning of mobile emergency generators. Furthermore, this paper proposes model equivalent reconstruction and decomposition algorithms to enhance the model's solvability. Simulations on the IEEE 33-node system verify the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method.

title: User Intentions Toward Djp Online: Investigating the Role of E-Government Trust and System Usability
authors: Mochammad Fahlevi, Dimvy Rusefani Asetya, Cecep Bryan Firdaus, Cynthia Octania Siregar, Zainur Romli
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The adoption of e-Government services remains a challenge despite their potential to enhance efficiency and transparency. This study investigates user intentions toward DJP Online, focusing on the roles of trust in e-Government, system usability, privacy concerns, and information quality. Using Structural Equation Modeling-Partial Least Squares (SEM-PLS) on 609 valid responses, the findings reveal that Perceived Ease of Use (PEoU) significantly enhances Perceived Usefulness (PU), which in turn drives Intention to Use (ITU). Additionally, trust in e-Government strongly influences adoption, emphasizing the need for credibility and transparency in digital public services. Surprisingly, addressing privacy and security concerns increases trust, highlighting the importance of robust security measures. Moreover, high-quality information positively impacts perceived usefulness, reinforcing the necessity for clear and accurate communication. With 57.4% of ITU explained by PU and trust, the study underscores the need for user-friendly design, enhanced security, and transparent communication to boost adoption. These findings provide valuable insights for policymakers aiming to improve e-Government services, particularly in developing nations like Indonesia, by prioritizing trust, usability, and information quality to drive digital transformation in tax administration.

title: Farmnet -XG-A Hybrid Digital Twin model of Crop Recommendation in Smart Farming
authors: Lakshmi R, Deepa Jose
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: This research focuses on digital twin implementation of hybrid AI model for crop classification using NASA’s Distributed Active Archive Centers (DAAC) Virtual Environment (DAV) remote sensing data. By integrating climate, soil, and environmental parameters, we assess the efficiency gains of NASA DAV over traditional spectral analysis in precision agriculture. A novel hybrid Farmnet-XG model achieved 96.55% accuracy, outperforming Random forest, Support Vector Machines, K Nearest Neighbors, XG Boost, and Gradient Boosting. SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) were used to ensure explainability, demonstrating temperature, NDVI and soil moisture as the strongest features. This implementation result shows improvement in crop recommendation performance for enhanced yield prediction, increased efficiency of resources used, improved management of land-use, and real time monitoring using digital twins.

title: Research on Scalable Electric Vehicle Participation in Traceable Green Electricity Trading Mechanisms and Distributed Clearing Methods
authors: Xian Zhang, Peng Ji, Xin Chang, Hexiao Liang, Ziming Ma, Yuqing Yan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: This study proposes a quantitative analysis theory for electricity-carbon market coupling mechanisms based on aggregator electricity carbon emission intensity. It explores pathways for unlocking flexible regulation value and green low-carbon value centred on electric vehicle load aggregators, constructs an operational framework for electricity-carbon market coupling quantitative models, and designs standardized data interaction interfaces for multi-service platforms. The innovation lies in achieving electricity-carbon market coupling through the carbon emission intensity of aggregators, shifting carbon emission responsibility from the power generation side to the consumption side, quantifying the economic responsibility of electricity users for carbon emissions, and providing support for the implementation of electricity-carbon market business models.

title: Frequency–Spatial Synergy for SISR via Two-Level DWT and Window-Based Hybrid Attention
authors: Siying Liu, Yajuan Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Deep learning-based single-image super-resolution (SISR) methods have made significant progress, yet reliable recovery of high-frequency components and effective utilization of long-range dependencies remain challenging. To address this, we propose a structured frequency-spatial synergistic reconstruction framework grounded in a pre-posed two-level DWT prior. Specifically, we first employ a two-level discrete wavelet transform (two-level DWT) to extract and enhance local high-frequency components, thereby strengthening the model's ability to capture and reconstruct fine details. Subsequently, a window-based hybrid attention backbone aggregates global context to fully capture long-range dependencies. Finally, an Interleaved Grouped Convolutional Residual Block (IGC-ResBlock) performs refinement and outputs the reconstruction result. Experiments are conducted on mainstream benchmark datasets. Our experiments suggest that the proposed method achieves competitive performance on PSNR/SSIM metrics and tends to produce clearer visual details compared with existing approaches.

title: Hybrid Approach to Predict Agricultural Sales by Comparing Sarima and Moving Average Performance Evaluation
authors: Eka Anggraini, Rahmie Kurnia Putri, Iston Dwija Utama
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study compares the predictive performance of time series forecasting models for predicting short-term sales of fresh products by focusing on daily sales patterns and store performance variability. Using sales data from March to June 2024, three models-Moving Average, ARIMA, and SARIMA-were evaluated based on their predictive accuracy, measured through MAE, RMSE, and MAPE. Among the forecasting models, the Moving Average method showed poor performance and could not capture the strong fluctuations and weekly seasonality typical of fresh product sales. The ARIMA model demonstrated improved predictive precision by effectively recognizing non-seasonal and seasonal patterns, although it still struggled with extreme deviations. The SARIMA model performed best when configured with parameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(3,1,2)(1,1,1,7)$</tex> aligned with the underlying weekly cycles. It minimized forecast errors, recording the lowest MAE and MAPE values. Despite this, the model exhibited occasional sensitivity to outlier events, suggesting the need to consider external factors such as promotions or supply disruptions in future research. These findings offer valuable insights into inventory management and strategic planning in the fresh products supply chain for seasonal sales and expert-tuned models for enhancing forecasting accuracy in fresh product sales.

title: An Atomistic Investigation of Grain-Size-Dependent Cu-Cu Interfacial Void Reduction in Hybrid Bonding
authors: Junhyeok Park, Juheon Kim, Minki Jang, Hayoung Chung
abstract: Cu-Cu direct bonding is essential to hybrid bonding for fine-pitch interconnects, yet the presence of residual interfacial voids can significantly compromise electrical performance and reliability. Using molecular dynamics (MD) simulations, we investigate grain size dependent mechanisms of void reduction throughout the hybrid bonding process, encompassing heating, annealing, and cooling. The simulation employs Cu bilayer models that integrate surface irregularities and lateral constraints, effectively capturing the thermo-mechanical stress states induced by the expansion coefficient mismatch cu and adjacent dielectrics. We observed void changes with interfacial atomic displacement and atomic structure changes. During annealing, smaller grain size promotes void closure through atomic diffusion along grain boundaries. In contrast, during cooling, plastic deformation limits atomic mobility, and fine grains exhibit suppressed void growth, consistent with an increased fraction of HCP near the interface. These results indicate that grain size governs the mechanisms of interfacial void formation, influencing both closure and growth. By elucidating grain size effects on interfacial void evolution under hybrid bonding, this study highlights grain refinement as an effective microstructural design strategy to reduce residual voids and improve the reliability of Cu-Cu direct bonding.

title: Non-Bare-Metal User-Space Control-Flow Attestation
authors: Nikita Yadav, Hrushikesh Salunke, Dev Tejas Gandhi, Vinod Ganapathy
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Control-flow attestation (CFA) allows a verifier <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{V}$</tex> to obtain fine-grained measurements of a program <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{P}$</tex> executing on a remote computing platform. This CFA measurement allows <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{V}$</tex> to precisely audit the program path followed within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{P}$</tex> as it executes an input <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I$</tex>. However, CFA measurements must be collected and stored securely, e.g., in a TEE on the platform where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{P}$</tex> executes, failing which it will not be an accurate record of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{P}$</tex> 's execution. These requirements are satisfied relatively easily when <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{P}$</tex> executes atop bare hardware, as most prior CFA approaches have assumed. This paper considers non-bare-metal settings in which <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{P}$</tex> executes as a user-level process atop an OS on the remote computing platform. Prior approaches to CFA are insecure in the presence of OS-level adversaries. We describe the design and implementation of a system called Sulfurthat securely enables CFA of user-space applications in non-bare-metal settings. Sulfuraccomplishes this goal via a co-developed OS called Sulfuros. Sulfuros makes novel use of security extensions available in commodity AArch64 hardware—privileged-access never and privileged-execute never. We experimentally show that Sulfurenables secure CFA with low additional runtime overheads in non-bare-metal settings.

title: Side-Channel Attack on AES Based on KAN Network
authors: Li Zehao, Wang Min
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Side-channel attacks can recover keys by acquiring side-channel leakage information such as timing and power consumption during the operation of cryptographic devices. As a widely used symmetric encryption algorithm, the security of AES has attracted much attention. The Kolmogorov-Arnold Network can improve the model's ability to learn side-channel data through a way of learning activation functions different from traditional neural networks and a unique method of calculating losses. Meanwhile, by using the results of machine learning through belief propagation, complete key recovery can be achieved with only one side-channel observation.

title: Enhancing Electrochemical Migration Resistance of Sintered Silver by Ceria Additives for Die Attachment Applications
authors: W.J. Wang, K.S. Siow
abstract: Sintered silver is widely explored as a die-attach material due to its superior thermal and mechanical properties. However, its vulnerability to electrochemical migration (ECM) under humid and biased conditions remains a key reliability concern. This study investigates the role of ceria (CeO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) as a functional additive to improve ECM resistance in sintered silver joints. Silver pastes with 1 wt.%, 3 wt.%, and 5 wt.% CeO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> were sintered and evaluated for ECM resistance using the water drop tests (WDT) with deionized (DI) water. WDT were conducted under 3 V and 10 V bias with electrode spacings of 1 mm respectively. In-situ optical observations revealed dendritic growth behaviour, while SEM and EDS analyses confirmed the dendrite morphology and elemental distribution. Additionally, shear strength testing and corresponding fracture surface analysis were performed to evaluate their mechanical properties. Results show that 1 wt.% CeO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> addition provided the best overall performance in suppressing dendritic growth, delaying failure time for ECM, and maintaining mechanical integrity. The findings suggested that CeO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> incorporation is a promising strategy to enhance the reliability and functionality of sintered silver joints in electronics packaging.

title: Food Inventory and Expiry Management: A Four–Layer Cloud–Edge Architecture
authors: Hélder Pereira, Paulo Matos, Manuel Teixeira
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Household kitchens generate approximately 54 percent of all food waste in the European Union, amounting to roughly 72 kilograms per resident each year. Existing expiryreminder apps can mitigate waste but rely on manual entry and continuous connectivity, so engagement often fades after only a few weeks. This paper defines the goal of a low-friction, privacyaware solution and proposes an adaptive four-layer cloud-edge architecture that automates product registration, operates partly offline, and scales elastically with demand. At the edge, a mobile front end written in Kotlin decodes barcodes and consults a lightweight, least-recently-used (LRU) cache; on a cache miss, an optical character recognition (OCR) filter triggers a serverless enrichment pipeline that retrieves metadata from an open food database. Edge services normalize the payload, while a managed cloud core maintains the shared inventory and dispatches push alerts as best-before dates approach. A recommendation module ranks items by urgency and updates its model opportunistically when bandwidth permits. Cost and performance modeling indicate that the proposed design could reduce network traffic by approximately 25 percent and shorten end-to-end registration time by approximately 18 percent compared with a cloud-only baseline, while keeping backend costs below 0.27 EUR per thousand operations. These projections suggest that the architecture can deliver sub-second interactions and predictable operating expenses without proprietary infrastructure, laying the groundwork for sustainable, GDPR-compliant food management systems.

title: PyReach: A Multi-Agent Framework for Vulnerability Reachability Analysis in Python
authors: Yueqin Wang, Jingzheng Wu, Xiang Ling, Tianyue Luo, Xu Wang
abstract: Modern Python applications heavily rely on third-party libraries (TPLs), which can introduce security risks when vulnerabilities in these libraries silently propagate into client code. Determining whether a known vulnerability in a third-party library (TPL) can potentially be triggered in a specific downstream application is a key aspect of vulnerability reachability analysis, a research area that remains a manual, error-prone task due to the dynamic nature of the Python language and its implicit coding patterns. We present PyReach, a multi-agent collaborative framework that automates vulnerability reachability analysis for Python programs. Instead of statically resolving all dynamic behavior, PyReach decomposes the reasoning process into three semantically guided agents: 1) Context Modeling Agent that extracts auxiliary semantic context by analyzing and summarizing the semantic context of external dependencies for each function in a call chain; 2) Reachability Analysis Agent that determines whether a function in a call chain alters a vulner-ability’s triggering conditions by analyzing its inside semantics; 3) Reachability Verification Agent that determines if execution paths from user-facing entry points can reach the vulnerable code under the right conditions. We evaluate PyReach on a custom-built dataset, the largest of its kind for Python vulnerability reachability analysis, consisting of 15 real-world Python CVEs and 45 corresponding client projects. Experimental results show that PyReach achieves $90 \%$ precision and $83.3 \%$ specificity, significantly outperforming a call-graph-based baseline, Jarvis. PyReach effectively distinguishes between truly affected and unaffected clients by reasoning over code semantics and trigger profiles. Our results highlight the value of combining modular semantic reasoning with constraint propagation for accurate and scalable vulnerability analysis in dynamic languages.

title: ForexAgent: Identifying Trading Strategies in Forex Markets with Large Language Models
authors: Xin Shu, Mingchen Ju, Zebin Chen, Yi Ding, Wantong Zhang, Dong Wen, Zhengyi Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: Identifying traders’ underlying strategies from account level logs is vital for market surveillance and risk control, yet threshold rules and conventional learners often fail when behaviors deviate from textbook patterns or combine multiple styles. We introduce ForexAgent, a large language model (LLM) framework that classifies strategies such as Martingale and Kelly directly from trade sequences. ForexAgent couples a realistic data generation pipeline, which replays canonical strategies on historical foreign exchange prices to produce labeled, account level records, with an LLM classifier that operates either on raw executions (timestamps, direction, size, and profit or loss) or on concise, behavior centric summaries (risk–return profiles, equity linked sizing, and outcome responsive scaling). On realistic forex datasets, ForexAgent attains macro F1 up to 94.6%, surpassing rule based and heuristic baselines, and remains data efficient in zero and few shot settings. Ablation studies show that focused, compact prompts, or even raw logs alone, often outperform feature heavy descriptions, suggesting attention dilution from verbose inputs. By recognizing pattern regularities (for example, loss responsive size escalation versus equity proportional growth) rather than brittle thresholds, ForexAgent generalizes to noisy, variant, and mixed behaviors (for example, Martingale scalers in the range of 1.8× to 2.3× with occasional rule breaks). The framework offers a practical path for brokers and regulators to flag high risk styles without strategy specific retraining, and its prompt based architecture provides interpretable rationales as well as a foundation for extension to broader markets and hybrid style taxonomies.

title: Quadtree Partitioning-based Visual Token Pruning for MLLMs Considering Information Density
authors: Yuntao Wei, Jinming Liu, Shengyang Zhao, Zhibo Chen, Wenjun Zeng, Xin Jin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) excel at comprehensive understanding by integrating visual and textual information. However, their inference speed is often bottlenecked by redundant visual token inputs. Existing methods tend to alleviate this issue with a heuristic pruning strategy based on token importance, tailored to certain commonly adopted vision encoders like CLIP. In this paper, we propose a novel training-free token pruning method based on a well-designed metric of information density, where we decide which tokens are retained according to their entropy, following the classic information theory. Based on that, we further propose a quadtree partitioning strategy, in which we retain these tokens with higher entropy so as to preserve the visual spatial structure while allocating more tokens to more informative regions. Experiments on LLaVA-v1.5-7B and 13B across six benchmarks show our method achieves state-of-the-art performance—retaining over 90% of full-token accuracy even at a 6.25% token budget—while cutting TFLOPs by up to 20% compared to FastV and by 81% compared to the original LLaVA-v1.5.

title: LS-SSC: Lightweight Stealth-Aware Secure Semantic Communication Against Eavesdroppers
authors: Haiyang Cao, Tong-Xing Zheng, Jiansen Yin, Cong Li, Yiliang Liu, Zhou Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: As an emerging communication paradigm, semantic communication (SC) has garnered increasing attention, but its open transmission characteristics and deep learning dependency pose severe security challenges. In this paper, we propose a lightweight stealth-aware secure SC (LS-SSC) framework with adaptive weighting to balance adversarial and stealthy training objectives for a resource-constrained image transmission syst em under eavesdropping threats. By introducing a channel-spatial attention-enhanced reversible residual network, LS-SSC can conduct stealthy adversarial attacks against eavesdroppers, stealthily disrupting the semantic information perceived by eaves-droppers. Experimental results show that compared with the baseline scheme, the proposed LS-SSC saves 71% parameters and 41% floating point operations. Moreover, LS-SSC can effectively attack the eavesdropper's downstream artificial intelligent task network while guaranteeing strong stealthiness for the attack behavior, and meanwhile it can improve the decoding quality for the legitimate receiver.

title: Short-Term Power Load Forecasting Model Based on DWT-AFSA and Multi-Model Fusion
authors: Jing Zhi, Hongwei Hu, Yuantian Xue, Jie Wang, Zekun Wang, Tiejiang Yuan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Electric load forecasting is of great significance for the safe and stable operation and economic optimization of power systems. To improve the accuracy of short-term load forecasting, this study proposes a forecasting model based on discrete wavelet transform (DWT), artificial fish swarm algorithm (AFSA), and multi-model fusion. First, DWT is used to decompose the original load data. The low-frequency and high-frequency components are fed into suitable models: high-frequency components are input into convolutional neural network-long short-term memory (CNN-LSTM), multilayer perceptron (MLP), and random forest (RF), while low-frequency components are processed by ridge regression (RR). LightGBM is then employed as a meta-learner to integrate the prediction results. AFSA combined with 10-fold cross-validation is applied to optimize the model hyperparameters. Experiments were conducted using 2023 load data from a region in Northwest China, with meteorological input variables for each season determined through maximal information coefficient (MIC) feature selection. The results show that the proposed model achieves excellent performance in prediction error metrics across different seasons. Compared with single models and other combined models, it more accurately fits the electric load series, demonstrating its effectiveness and offering a new approach and methodology for electric load forecasting.

title: Adaptive Gamification and Ontological Modeling for Optimizing Retention in Personalized MOOCs
authors: Kaouther Kaabi, F athi Essalmi, Mohamed Jemni
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on ICT &amp; Accessibility (ICTA) co-located with 2025 2nd International Conference and Forum on Assistive Technologies for Special Needs (ATSN)
abstract: Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs) have transformed the educational landscape, becoming widely accessible learning environments that still face persistent challenges in maintaining learner engagement and sustained motivation, often leading to high dropout rates. This study introduces a novel personalization framework that adaptively integrates gamification elements such as leaderboards, badges, and points based on individual learner profiles. By combining artificial intelligence techniques, including learner clustering, with semantic web technologies, the proposed approach delivers context-aware and personalized learning pathways. The framework is implemented through a web-based platform LevelUp, which features a dynamic learner profiling engine, modular content organization, adaptive gamification mechanisms, and a pedagogical ontology enabling semantic alignment between learner characteristics and game mechanics. Experimental results reveal significant improvements in learner motivation, engagement, and retention. This research highlights the critical role of ontologies in M DOCs for supporting intelligent decision-making and advancing the effectiveness of personalized online learning environments.

title: Hybrid Intrusion Detection System for Network Security: Real-Time Signature and Machine Learning-Based Anomaly Detection
authors: Chandra Naik, Karthikeya Jayaram Shettigar, Prathamesh Shetty, Sambram Hedge, Rahul Koti
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The growing intensity of cyber attacks fueled by adversary machine learning and polymorphic malware has made traditional Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDS) inadequate for real-time, fault-tolerant defense. For this project, we propose and develop a next-generation hybrid NIDS synergistically integrating signature-based pattern matching with machine learning–based behavioral analysis, thus overcoming the limitations of unimodal detection mechanisms.Our approach proposes a quadripartite taxonomy that hierarchically organizes hybrid NIDS based on architectural integration topologies, intrinsic ML methodologies, decision fusion approaches. The suggested sequential hybrid architecture exhibits significant performance gains, registering 94.7% ±2.3% detection rate for temporal attack patterns and lowering false positives by applying transformer-based feature fusion, as opposed to baseline signature-based systems. Experimental verification identifies primary challenges as inference latency in deep learning subcomponents, adversarial evasion attacks leading to up to 23.4% ±8.1% accuracy loss, and the lack of transparent decision-making processes, with more than 82% of results having no interpretability. To address these challenges, we propose four research avenues: building inference pipelines for ultralow-latency processing based on neuromorphic computing, combination of explainability frameworks (SHAP/LIME) with dynamic trust thresholds. This work not only sets tangible performance standards for hybrid intrusion detection but also details a future-oriented roadmap designed to achieve balance between efficiency and resilience thus making a contribution towards the future of safe and reliable cyber-physical defense systems.

title: Improving Effect-Cause Diagnosis Performance with Minimal Memory Overhead on Asymmetric Partition Trees (APT)
authors: Wu-Tung Cheng, Szczepan Urban, Manish Sharma, Jakub Janicki
publish: 2025 IEEE International Test Conference in Asia (ITC-Asia)
abstract: Effect-Cause diagnosis procedures are used to diagnose failing dies and improve manufacturing yield. Several methods have been proposed to accelerate Effect-Cause diagnosis procedures by utilizing a small-sized fault signature dictionary. Recently, pre-silicon diagnosis simulation has been proposed to identify quality issues in diagnosis before silicon volume production. Asymmetric Partition Trees (APT) have been proposed to extract useful information in the fault dictionary for diagnosis simulation applications. APT has proven to have a minimum tree size. In this paper, we propose using fault group information in APT to distribute the fault signature dictionary across APT tree nodes. With APT, the small fault signature dictionary can be further reduced, thereby improving diagnosis performance. The larger the designs, the greater the savings.

title: Robust Dynamic Link Prediction on Temporal Graphs via Counterfactual Regularization
authors: Yuan Li, Kaiyu Zhang, Yixuan Li, Guoli Yang, Yuhai Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Dynamic link prediction in temporal networks is crucial for applications in the areas such as social network analysis, recommender systems, and financial risk forecasting. Although existing models could capture temporal interaction patterns between nodes, they may develop a reliance on spurious correlations within the networks. The reliance arises from the noise introduced by the dynamic evolution of community structures, which will limits the robustness and generalization ability. To tackle these challenges, we propose the CR-TGN (Counterfactually-Regularized Temporal Graph Neural Network) framework, that employs counterfactual consistency for regularization during training. First, CR-TGN not only learns link prediction patterns from real interaction but also introduces a counterfactual world, intervening and placing the node in a false community context to regularize the model. Second, the model is trained to learn stable and intrinsic node representations that are insensitive to changes in the macroscopic community structure. This ensures that the feature representations of the node in both factual and counterfactual worlds remain consistent. Experimental results on three real-world datasets show that CR-TGN outperforms various baseline models including TGN, in dynamic link prediction tasks and offers more robust predictive capability for temporal networks.

title: Are Organisations Ready for AI Security Compliance: A Cross-Sector Survey
authors: Harriet Farlow, Matthew Garratt, Gavin Mount, Timothy Lynar
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study investigated organisational readiness for Artificial Intelligence (AI) security compliance obligations. AI security refers to the technical and governance considerations that secure AI systems from threats that seek to disrupt, deceive or disclose information. There are already standards that govern AI security by ISO and NIST, and legislation like the EU AI Act and California laws. Based on 118 survey responses and 25 interviews, the study uncovered several insights. First, despite the increased adoption of AI technologies by organisations - over 77 % in this study - there is limited adoption of AI security measures (22%). Secondly, AI security literacy across job types is low as measured by testing respondents' knowledge of AI and AI security topics. Third, organisational compliance readiness is extremely low (19 %). Our goal is to identify how organisations can be better supported in adopting effective measures, and we recommend a number of actions, including a holistic approach to AI training, as well as cooperation between the AI and security fields to adapt effective policies on responsible disclosure of AI vulnerabilities and taxonomies that address AI safety versus security issues.

title: Defect Z-Depth Determination in 2.5D ICs Using Magnetic Field Imaging
authors: Fengkai Cai, Jayasanker Jayabalan, Lucas Lum, Bernice Zee, Jiann Min Chin, Yeow Kheng Lim
abstract: Defect localization and accurate depth determination in advanced heterogeneous integration (HI) packaging integrated circuits (ICs), particularly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 \mathrm{D} / 3 \mathrm{D}$</tex> ICs, is critical for failure analysis and reliability assessment. Beyond lateral XY defect localization, vertical Z-depth estimation is essential for identifying the failure layer in stacked configurations, which is a challenge that conventional non-destructive testing (NDT) methods cannot effectively address. Due to restricted penetration depth and inadequate spatial resolution caused by the complex, multilayered material composition of HI packaging, conventional NDT techniques face intrinsic limitations in localizing defects along the z-axis. Magnetic field imaging (MFI) has emerged as a promising alternative, providing non-invasive and highresolution sensing of current-induced magnetic fields while being insensitive to variations in complex material structure in HI packaging. However, despite its advantages, MFI inherently produces intricate magnetic field data that require algorithms for depth extraction, an area that remains underexplored in both research and practical applications. In this paper, we propose an MFI-based variational method (VM) that employs fast Fourier transform (FFT)-based magnetic inverse optimization, grounded in the Biot-Savart law, to enable Z-depth determination of shortcircuit defects in 2.5D ICs. The effectiveness of the proposed VM approach is demonstrated on two representative defective 2.5D graphics processing units (GPUs): a signal-to-ground short at the C4 bump and a power-to-ground short at the μbump. These defect types are among the most commonly encountered failure modes in advanced 2.5D ICs. Validation against X-ray scans shows defect depth estimation errors of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5}-25 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> and 5 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, respectively, thereby advancing the fault isolation capability for complex 2.5D/3D ICs.

title: Anti-Aliasing Snapshot HDR Imaging Using Non-Regular Sensing
authors: Teresa Stürzenhofäcker, Moritz Klimm, Jürgen Seiler, André Kaup
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Snapshot HDR imaging is essential to capture the full dynamic range of a scene in a single exposure, making it essential for video and dynamic environments where motion prevents the use of multi-exposure techniques or complex hardware set-ups. This work presents a snapshot HDR imaging sensor that is based on spatially varying apertures, implemented by combining two differently sized prototype pixels. The different light integration areas physically extend the dynamic range towards the lower end, compared to a standard high resolution sensor. A non-regular pixel arrangement is suggested, to mitigate aliasing and overcome a loss in spatial resolution that is associated with increased light integration area of the larger prototype pixel. Subsequent reconstruction in the Fourier domain, where natural images can be sparsely represented allows to recover the image with high detail. The image acquisition approach with the proposed non-regular HDR sensor is simulated and analysed with special emphasis on the spatial resolution. The results suggest the snapshot HDR sensor layout to be an effective way to acquire images with high dynamic range and free from aliasing artefacts.

title: GeRF3D-PCAC: Dynamic Point Cloud Attributes Compression with GFT Domain Motion Compensation
authors: Sajid Umair, Zhu Li, Anique Akhtar, Geert Van der Auwera
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: A point cloud is a standard 3D data representation that poses challenges due to its large volume, high dimensionality, and unstructured nature. This paper introduces the Graph Fourier Transform Neural Radiance Field for Dynamic 3D Point Cloud Attribute Coding, termed GeRF3D-PCAC. We present a geometry-guided compression method for dynamic point cloud attributes using a four-step encoder-decoder framework. First, a binary tree partitions both reference(F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">t</inf>) and target(F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">t+1</inf>) point cloud frames, and each target partition locates its top-k nearest reference partitions using centroid-based KNN search. Second, the optimal reference node is chosen by comparing the Graph Fourier Transform (GFT) based latent representations of the target node and its candidates using mean squared error. Third, the target node is predicted by transferring RGB colors from geometry-matching points in the reference frame to the target geometry. Finally, GeRF3D predicts the GFT latent representation of target nodes, and three bitstreams, such as reference indices, quantized GeRF3D weights, and Y-channel residuals in the frequency domain, are generated and transmitted to the decoder. Our method outperforms G-PCC-PredLift-v23, G-PCC-RAHT-v23, and Unicorn in both compression and reconstruction across benchmark datasets.

title: GG-R1: A Fine-Grained RFT Approach for GUI Grounding
authors: Jian Ye, Xin Zhao, Xuanzhen Feng, Xiang Gao, Xin Zhang, Lanting Li, Wentao Hong
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: Many current GUI agent works primarily focus on supervised fine-tuning based on Vision-Language Models (VLMs). However, this approach not only requires extensive data collection but also demonstrates limited generalization capabilities on out-of-distribution (OOD) tasks. Inspired by DeepSeek-R1, we introduce a rule-based reinforcement finetuning methodology for GUI grounding tasks. Specifically, we employ the GRPO algorithm with rule-based rewards (incorporating fine-grained rewards across format, action types, coordinates, length, and difficulty dimensions) and utilize only 152 high-quality samples to fine-tune Qwen2.5-VL3B, resulting in our model GG-R1-3B. This model achieves 26.8 on Screenspot-Pro, representing a 127% improvement over Qwen2.5-VL-3B (26.8 vs 11.8), and scores 84.3 on ScreenSpot, demonstrating a 29.7% enhancement compared to Qwen2.5-VL-3B (84.3 vs 65.0). Our methodology significantly improves both data utilization efficiency and model performance in GUI action prediction.

title: R+R: From Claims to Crashes: A Systematic Re-evaluation of Graph-Based Network Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Chenglong Wang, Pujia Zheng, Jiaping Gui, Cunqing Hua, Wajih Ul Hassan
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Graph-based Network Intrusion Detection Systems (GIDS) are increasingly used to model complex communication patterns and detect sophisticated enterprise threats, yet the reproducibility and replicability of GIDS research remain underexplored, limiting the reliability and generalizability of published results. We present a rigorous reproduction and replication of five state-of-the-art GIDS across four public datasets and a new large-scale enterprise dataset. Even with original code and configurations, reproducing claimed performance is difficult; detection metrics vary by up to 40 percent due to undocumented assumptions, preprocessing discrepancies, and hyperparameter sensitivity. Models also fail to generalize to real-world enterprise traffic, exhibiting high false positive rates and scalability issues. We identify key implementation factors: graph snapshot size and threshold-setting strategies significantly affect detection performance but are inconsistently documented, and several GIDS are vulnerable to evasion attacks. Beyond confirming known challenges (e.g., parameter sensitivity), our results expose a critical reproducibility crisis in the GIDS literature: without transparent and systematic evaluation, reported results may mislead researchers and practitioners. We provide recommendations to improve reproducibility, replicability, and robustness, and urge the community to adopt rigorous standards for empirical evaluation.

title: Energy Efficient Approximate MAC Architecture Using Segmentation Based Adder and Probability Driven 4:2 Compressor Based Multiplier
authors: Subhiksha S, Jenisha Merlin R, Jeba Christiya A, Kalieswari C
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Power efficiency is more important in today's computing world, because of huge amount of data are generated periodically due to numerous processes. Efficiency in power is more needed especially for battery-operated applications like smartphones, IoT devices, wearables, and embedded processors. The Multiply and Accumulate unit is present in it consumes more power especially in machine learning accelerators and digital signal processing applications. This paper presents a power efficient approximate MAC unit using segmentation based approximate adder and approximate Dadda multiplier design using approximate 4:2 compressor topology. Taking advantage of the error tolerance of most real-time applications, the design is able to minimize power consumption and hardware complexity significantly. Reducing accuracy in arithmetic units decreases switching activity, reduces path delay, and decreases silicon area with respect to exact designs. Simulations results shows that the proposed approximate adder and approximate multiplier design enhances power efficiency with little degradation in output quality. Therefore, reduces power in proposed MAC architecture. Thus, the proposed architecture is well-suited to image and signal processing applications, neural network accelerators, and power-efficient IoT edge devices, where high throughput and low energy consumption are highly required.

title: NeVES: Real-Time Neural Video Enhancement for HTTP Adaptive Streaming
authors: Daniele Lorenzi, Farzad Tashtarian, Christian Timmerer
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Enhancing low-quality video content is a task that has raised particular interest since recent developments in deep learning. Since most of the video content consumed worldwide is delivered over the Internet via HTTP Adaptive Streaming (HAS), implementing these techniques on web browsers would ease the access to visually-enhanced content on user devices. In this paper, we present NeVES, a multimedia system capable of enhancing the quality of video content streamed through HAS in real time. The demo is available at: https://github.com/cd-athena/NeVES.

title: An Integrated Computational Materials Engineering Approach for Anisotropic Conductive Films
authors: Jiaying Gao, Haohui Long, Jianhui Li
abstract: This paper presents an Integrated Computational Materials Engineering approach for Anisotropic Conductive Film material development. The information flow from Anisotropic Conductive Film constituents to final macroscale performance is leveraged to build understanding of ACF performance based on material degrees of cure, constituents' properties, and Anisotropic Conductive Film microstructure. Design guidance for Anisotropic Conductive Film is then given to optimize Young's modulus. By adopting Integrated Computational Materials Engineering approach, it is possible to identify optimal Anisotropic Conductive Film material design criteria to meet demanding needs in electronics packaging.

title: Anti-Interference Enhancement Design of the Synchronization Signal Burst Set in LEO-Based Non-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Zixi Fang, Tian Li, Shilei Shan, Chenhua Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Aiming at the anti-interference enhancement of synchronous broadcast channel in Non-Terrestrial Networks based on Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite downlink, this paper designs a novel synchronization signal burst set to improve the security and anti-interception capability of the satellite receiver without changing the existing frame structure, further preventing interference from non-cooperative parties to key parameters such as frequency, bandwidth and user identifier. Firstly, an improved Logistic mapping by high-dimensional calculation is proposed to generate chaotic sequences. Next, the Primary and Secondary Synchronization Signals are filled with the generated chaotic sequences. Moreover, we utilize a chaotic sequence to perform spread spectrum modulation on one Physical Broadcast Channel (PBCH) within the Synchronization Signal Block (SSB), thereby hiding important information in another PBCH. The generated structure is referred to as chaotic-based SSBs in this paper. The bifurcation graph verifies the rationality of the proposed mapping function, and the autocorrelation analysis of the generated chaotic sequences has been proven to be feasible. The simulation results show that the bit error rate of the PBCH is similar to that of the chaotic-based PBCH, indicating that the redesigned structure does not impact the transmission of the original data. To evaluate the performance of the proposed steganography scheme, the cepstrum analysis is further utilized in the time domain relying on signal processing.

title: Medical Visual Analytics with XAI for Exploring Personality Traits in Multi-Drug Addiction
authors: Wafia Abada, Abdelkrim Bouramoul, Moustafa Sadek Kahil
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Theoretical and Applicative Aspects of Computer Science (ICTAACS)
abstract: Predicting multi-drug use across substances is challenging due to class imbalance and limited interpretability in machine learning models. We propose a four-stage visual analytics workflow that integrates explainable AI and external validation, applied to the UCI drug dataset with seven personality traits. Logistic regression and random forest models achieved high predictive accuracy of 0.98 on frequent labels, while SHAP explanations highlighted Sensation Seeking as the most consistent risk factor, Conscientiousness as protective, and Openness often increasing risk. External validation with mortality and harm signals confirmed consistency, making the framework interpretable, auditable, and actionable for public health and addiction research.

title: Fuzzy-Enhanced Adaptive Model-Free Predictive Control for Robust Current Regulation in Three-Phase Grid-Connected Inverters
authors: Yugu Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: This paper proposes a fuzzy-enhanced adaptive model-free predictive control (FE-AMFPC) strategy to improve the current control performance of three-phase grid-connected inverters under parameter uncertainties and external disturbances. Unlike traditional model predictive control (MPC), which relies on accurate system models, the proposed method integrates a super-local model-free predictor with a real-time fuzzy error compensation mechanism. The adaptive predictor updates the system dynamics using historical data, while the fuzzy controller refines the predicted current based on prediction error and its rate of change. This hybrid structure enhances robustness without requiring explicit knowledge of inductance or resistance parameters. Simulation results under both matched and mismatched inductance conditions show that the proposed FE-AMFPC achieves lower total harmonic distortion (THD) compared to conventional MPC and standard model-free predictive control (MFPC). Specifically, THD is reduced from 2.34% (MPC) and 2.15% (MFPC) to 2.03% under nominal parameters, and from 3.15% (MPC) and 3.07% (MFPC) to 3.01% under parameter mismatch. The proposed strategy offers a promising solution for robust inverter control in uncertain environments.

title: Generation Scheduling in Vertically Integrated Utilities via Blockchain-Enabled Virtual Markets Including Cycling Costs
authors: Salman Tariq, Mohamed Shaaban, Hazlie Mokhlis, Irfan Ahmed Khan, Nuril Azmi Muslimin, Nurulafiqah N. Mansor
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Blockchain (BC) applications in the energy sector are often framed solely in terms of decentralized trading, overlooking their broader potential in vertically integrated utility environments. This paper extends prior work by designing a blockchain-assisted virtual market for generation scheduling that incorporates explicit market features, including ramping constraints and cycling ramp penalties. The framework addresses the operational challenges of renewable energy (RE)-induced net demand variations, particularly from photovoltaic (PV) generation, that strain slow-start, high-inertia fossil-fuel units. Implemented on a BC layer with smart contracts for automation and transparency, the proposed approach demonstrates how market design can reduce ramping stress, flatten net load demand profiles, and improve the integration of conventional units in renewable-rich systems.

title: Breaking the Curse of High-Dimensionality: A Transformer-Based Approach to Software Configuration Performance Prediction
authors: Zhengyue Pan, Mingjie Tu, Shijie Li, Dandan Wang, Jie Chen
abstract: Modern software systems are increasingly configurable, offering extensive options to meet diverse requirements. However, this flexibility results in exponentially growing configuration spaces, making exhaustive performance testing infeasible. Existing performance prediction models face significant challenges due to the curse of dimensionality, which limits their ability to generalize across high-dimensional spaces with sparse data. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a novel performance prediction framework comprising two components: Feature Transformer AutoEncoder (FTA) and Distance-Aware Greedy Sampling (DAGS). FTA leverages self-supervised learning and attention mechanisms to encode high-dimensional configuration data into compact, information-rich representations, effectively capturing long-range dependencies and mitigating data sparsity. DAGS, on the other hand, employs intelligent sampling strategies to maximize coverage within configuration spaces while minimizing measurement overhead. Extensive experimental evaluations across six real-world configurable software systems demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving up to a 79% reduction in Mean Relative Error (MRE). The results highlight the robustness and scalability of our framework in overcoming the traditional performance degradation associated with high-dimensional configuration spaces.

title: Masked Cross-modal Reconstruction Network (MCR-Net) for Multi-modal Industrial Anomaly Detection
authors: Li MAI, Chen DAI, Hongji MA, Xin LIN, Shiwei GUO, Guang YAN
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Industrial Anomaly Detection (IAD) has received increasing attention because undetected flaws on power-grid hardware, turbine blades or production lines can trigger blackouts, costly shutdowns or safety accidents. Considering singlemodality RGB or LiDAR data alone is easily invalidated by darkness, fog or specular reflection, this paper studies multi-modal detection and proposes the Masked Cross-modal Reconstruction Network (MCR-Net). The core idea is to learn bidirectional RGB to 3-D correspondence by reconstructing randomly masked tokens from the complementary modality; defects are located where reconstruction residuals deviate from the normal manifold learned without any anomalous training samples. The proposed method is evaluated by defect detection and segmentation tasks on the most challenging and largest multi-modal IAD database, MVTec 3D-AD dataset, to demonstrate its superiority over the state-of-the-art.

title: Physical Layer Security Enhancement Techniques for UAV Relay Systems Based on Artificial Noise Assistance
authors: Yunqi Hao, Youyang Xiang, Xianglu Li, Chen Ding, Qilong Du, Dong Hou, Jie Tian
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The broadcast nature of wireless channels introduces significant security risks during information transmission. When the eavesdropper is in close proximity to the legitimate receiver, their channel state information (CSI) may be highly correlated or even superior to that of the legitimate channel, rendering traditional power optimization methods ineffective in suppressing eavesdropping rates. To address this issue, this paper proposes an artificial noise (AN)-assisted unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) relay system. By leveraging the UAV relay to inject artificial noise while transmitting signals and dynamically adjusting the power allocation factor based on real-time CSI and the UAV's position, precise interference control over the eavesdropping channel is achieved. This study establishes a fournode communication system comprising a source, relay, legitimate receiver, and eavesdropper, formulating a joint optimization problem aimed at maximizing secrecy capacity. Given the nonconvex nature of the optimization problem, this paper employs slack variables and the successive convex approximation (SCA) method to equivalently transform the original problem into a convex problem. A closed-form analytical solution for the power allocation factor is derived using the staircase water-filling (WF) algorithm. Furthermore, an alternating iterative optimization algorithm based on artificial noise assistance is proposed to achieve global optimization of system parameters. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to traditional power optimization schemes, the proposed algorithm reduces the eavesdropping capacity and improves the information transmission efficiency, significantly enhancing both system security and transmission performance.

title: Interval Method based Workspace Evaluation towards Design Guidance of Redundant Manipulator
authors: Virendra Kumar, Veeraragavan S, Akash Pandey, Sambhunath Nandy
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: This study presents an approach of utilisation of interval method to evaluate workspace and design of redundant manipulators based on an optimization problem considering the uncertainties in the parameters. The optimization technique employs a global performance metric i.e., the Global Conditioning Index (GCI), derived from the manipulator’s jacobian. Knowledge of workspace of robot manipulator is required for the computation of GCI. This article describes a method to evaluate the workspace of a manipulator using interval method whose link dimensions are not accurately known. This inaccuracy can be arising due to manufacturing tolerances or approximations of complex geometries. This method lacks exploration for such particular problem and can result in design optimization of manipulators with capricious link dimensions. Finally, the problem for optimizing the design for a spatial 5 Degree Of Freedom (DOF) positioning redundant manipulator is formulated using the GCI as the objective function and solved using interval method. These redundant manipulators can be used in remotely operated underwater vehicles as well as in complex fruit-picking applications in the agriculture industry.

title: Enhancing Short-Term Load Forecasting with Multi-Head Attention and Hybrid Encoder-Predictor Architecture
authors: He Gan, Ke Nan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional sequence modeling in capturing time-dependent patterns and the insufficient adaptability of Convolutional Neural Networks to heterogeneous load variations due to fixed receptive fields, This study introduces an extended convolution mechanism in the TCN to expand receptive fields, effectively addressing the gradient vanishing problem in longterm power load sequence processing. The scalable design of the TCN module enables adaptive adaptation to multi-scale load fluctuation patterns. The system comprises four core modules: An input processing layer employs a bit-range threshold robust rescaler, a hybrid encoder implements position encoding through the TCN module, a multi-head attention mechanism dynamically evaluates feature importance and captures cross-feature correlations; and a hybrid predictor maps high-dimensional key feature weights to a 24 hour short-term load prediction space via a MLP regression head. In 24-hour short-term load prediction, the network filters outliers using a quantile robust rescaler and achieves precise mapping from highdimensional features to predictions through a streamlined MLP regression architecture. The system demonstrates excellent performance, leveraging TCN's extended convolution, multi-head attention synergy, and modular architecture to effectively capture time-varying patterns and heterogeneous load variations. This validates the enhanced accuracy of power load prediction achieved through extended convolution,attention mechanisms, and modular design.

title: Analysis and Countermeasures of AVC Remote Control Failure in Substation Based on NSD500M Measure-Control Device
authors: Xuefei Yang, Xiankun Qin, Youqiang Liu, Cong Huang, Ming Le, Junyan Ou, Shunjian Yang, Xiaodong Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: The reactive power distribution in power systems plays a crucial role in ensuring system stability. The Automatic Voltage Control (AVC) system, which enables online automatic adjustment of system voltage and reactive power, is beneficial for guaranteeing power quality, improving transmission efficiency, and reducing network losses. By exploring the working principle of the AVC system, this paper focuses on analyzing and discussing the occasional situation where the controlled measurement and control device fails to respond correctly after the AVC system issues a remote control command. Meanwhile, corresponding solutions are proposed.

title: Multimodal Transformers Tea Leaf Disease Detection in Precision Agriculture
authors: S. Sheeba, E. Vigneshkumar, T. Vibinkumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Tea (Camellia sinensis) leaf disease consumed beverages globally, with India serving as a significant producer and consumer. However, the presence of diseases affecting tea plants can severely impact both the quality and yield of tea production. Traditionally, identifying these diseases is done manually a time-consuming process that may compromise crop quality and productivity. Early detection of tea leaf diseases is essential to minimize overall damage and maintain production levels. This study introduces Hierarchical ViT, a deep learning-based model designed to identify and classify eight distinct tea leaf diseases, utilizing a fine-tuned architecture. ConvNext is a new CNN model that takes the inspiration from Vision transformer and adapts model from ResNet designs. Using the Vision Transformer (ViT) and the pre-trained convolutional neural network (ResNet-50), the proposed Hybrid-ViT model classifies photos from various tea diseases. For transfer learning, the ResNet-50 is used, which makes feature extraction and inductive bias easier. The hierarchical vision transformer reduces the quadratic complexity by introducing the shifting window technique. The local and long-range dependencies are modeled making it effective for fine-grained tasks

title: PaintUnicorn: Multi-view video dataset
authors: Eva Dubar, Daniele Bonatto, Sarah Dury, Gauthier Lafruit
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: We present PaintUnicorn, a real-world multiview video dataset designed for advancing research in dynamic neural rendering, 4D reconstruction, and view synthesis. Captured with 28 synchronized cameras at 1080p and 30 fps over 300 frames, the dataset includes complex non-rigid motion, specular and transparent surfaces, and varying lighting conditions. We provide calibrated camera intrinsics and extrinsics, color-corrected images, and generated depth maps combined from multiview geometry and learning-based estimates. PaintUnicorn is a dataset to support dense temporal and spatial benchmarking for methods like 3D Gaussian Splatting and dynamic NeRFs. The dataset is accessible in https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16312256

title: Evaluation of 2D Video Interpolation and Extrapolation Methods for Real-Time V-PCC Error Concealment
authors: Eiko Nakajima, Fangzheng Lin, Kasidis Arunruangsirilert, Jiro Katto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Packet losses in streaming 3D point clouds with V-PCC over RTP significantly impact reconstruction quality, affecting users’ quality of experience. Despite this, practical error concealment methodologies are not yet widely researched, as previous 3D-based methods suffer from real-time performance and restrict coding modes to all-intra only. In this work, we evaluate various state-of-the-art 2D video interpolation and extrapolation works as candidates for the real-time V-PCC error concealment task in the video domain. We show that although their effectiveness varies with the regularity of generated patches or V-PCC video types, they can run in real-time, significantly faster than 3D methods, and can perform effectively on V-PCC attribute loss and temporally consistent patch videos.

title: Unmanned Underwater Vehicle Magnetic Stealth Multiobjective Design
authors: B. Kuznetsov, I. Bovdui, T. Nikitina, K. Chunikhin, O. Voloshko, B. Kobylianskyi
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Strict requirements implementation (SRI) for unmanned underwater vehicle (UUV) magnetic field requirements stealth (MFRS) improving method developed. Geometric forward magnetostatics problems solution (GFMSPS) for UUV as elongated energy-saturated technical objects (EESTO) calculated by spatial extended spheroidal harmonics (SESH) in prolate spheroidal coordinate system (PSCS). UUV magnetic field signatures (MFS) mathematical model (MM) prediction problem (PP) and stealth problem (SP) calculated as geometric inverse magnetostatics problems solution (GIMSPS). GIMSPS with UUV magnetic characteristics uncertainties (MCU) calculated as vector game solution (VGS). VGS is calculated from the condition of minimizing by magnetic field initial source (MFIS) but maximizing by MCU of UUV for the «worst case». VGS payoff calculated by COMSOL Multiphysics software package based on GFMSPS. VGS calculated by hybrid heuristic optimization algorithm (HHOA) based on particle swarm optimization (PSO) and sequential quadratic programming (SQP) from Pareto optimal solutions (POS) with binary preference relations (BPR). UUV with MCU PP and SP solution results are given. MFIS used allows improving MFRS of UUV with MCU by more than 100 times.

title: Low-Complexity Perturbation-Based Fiber Nonlinearity Compensation for High-Speed Optical Coherent Systems
authors: Wanzhen Guo, Xuan Tang, Zhaoquan Fan, Jian Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: We propose a low-complexity perturbation-based nonlinearity compensation (P-NLC) method for optical coherent systems. This method converts the received high-baud-rate signal into multiple parallel narrow-bandwidth signals, which are then compensated by single-step P-NLC. In this way, the compensation accuracy is enhanced while the complexity is reduced.
