Simulations of a wavelength-division-multiplexed 64-GBaud/channel probabilistically-shaped 64-quadrature-amplitude-modulation system over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 \times 80-\text{km}$</tex> single-mode fiber were conducted. It is shown that the proposed method exhibits better NLC performance than conventional P-NLC and 2-steps/span split-step-Fouriermethod based digital back-propagation (DBP). In addition, the required complexity is lower than that of conventional P-NLC and is less than 8% of that of 2-steps/span DBP.

title: Atomistic Insights into Ag-Ti Supersaturated Solid Solution Mechanics and Interfacial Bonding Behaviours for High-Density Interconnects
authors: Sichen Liu, Jian Zhang, Taiyu Wang, Liangxuan Chen, Xiuchen Zhao, Gang Zhang, Yongjun Huo
abstract: With the continuous miniaturization of microbump interconnects in three-dimensional (3D) integration, pronounced size effects and the brittleness of intermetallic compounds (IMCs) in conventional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C u} / \mathbf{S n}$</tex> solder systems have raised serious reliability concerns. To address these challenges, silver-based solid-state bonding has emerged as a promising alternative owing to its superior electrical and thermal conductivity. In this study, we systematically investigated the mechanical response, short-range ordering, and interfacial diffusion behavior of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A g}-\mathbf{T i}$</tex> supersaturated solid solutions using molecular dynamics (MD) simulations. A series of single-crystal models with varying Ti concentrations and bonding-diffusion models with realistic interfacial roughness were constructed to assess the strengthening and diffusion effects of Ti incorporation in Ag matrices. The results demonstrate that increasing Ti concentration reduces peak compressive strength while maintaining comparable elastic modulus, thereby promoting plastic deformation and accommodating bonding-induced interfacial roughness. Monte Carlo/molecular dynamics (MC/MD) hybrid simulations revealed that Ag-Ti supersaturated solid solutions exhibit strong short-range ordering tendencies upon heating, with Ti atoms clustering preferentially, suggesting a potential phase separation mechanism that further enhances mechanical properties. Bonding-diffusion simulations showed that Ti incorporation accelerates interfacial diffusion, reduces void size, and improves interfacial closure compared with pure Ag. Collectively, these findings highlight Ag-Ti supersaturated solid solutions as a viable candidate for next-generation micro-bump interconnects, capable of enabling low-temperature, highreliability bonding in 3D integration.

title: Non-Invasive Iv Extravasation Detection Using Optical and Temperature Sensors
authors: S Prabakar, Priyadharshini R, Cibinisri R, Kaviya K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: IV therapy plays a vital role in the spheres of healthcare, yet its application may cause several complications such as infiltration and extravasation, in which a fluid enters the tissues around the area and may result in swelling, pain, and serious tissue damage. Existing system is based on visual inspection performed on a regular basis and on the inspection of that specific zone. The method tends to disregard early symptoms and late treatment. We have come up with a real time monitoring system to resolve this problem and it will measure the skin reflectance and temperature difference at the IV site. Any deviation in these two will generate audio and alerts.

title: Timeswap: Agile Based Microtask Bartering System
authors: Sakthi Sanjuktha S, Vaishnavi R, Varsha Sangari N K, Sahana P, Nithyasri A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The rapid rise of gig platforms has enabled flexible collaboration through microtask distribution, yet most systems remain heavily dependent on monetary transactions. This reliance creates barriers for individuals who may lack financial resources but possess valuable skills, especially in academic and professional communities. To address this limitation, we propose TimeSwap, an agile-based microtask bartering platform that facilitates skill and task exchange through time credits instead of currency. The system integrates agile task management principles with a digital credit economy to promote inclusivity, fairness, and collaboration. Its architecture consists of four modules: an agile workflow management framework that organizes tasks into micro-units, a credit bartering system that rewards contributions, a matching and recommendation engine that aligns tasks with participant skills and availability, and trust and accountability mechanisms that ensure transparency and reliability. By eliminating financial constraints and emphasizing cooperative participation, TimeSwap offers a sustainable alternative to conventional freelancing models. The proposed framework is expected to support equitable engagement, foster skill development, and strengthen peer-to-peer collaboration across academic and professional domains.

title: Deep Learning-Based Fast Victim Detection in Disaster Zones via IoT Framework
authors: Seeja G, Berlin Hency V, Arockia Selvakumar Arockia Doss
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Timely and accurate detection of disaster victims is critical to improving survival outcomes during Urban Search and Rescue (USAR) operations, particularly within the first 48 hours. This paper presents an integrated system for real-time human-victim detection in collapsed building environments using a deep learning-Internet of Things (IoT)- enabled robotic framework. A pre-trained ResNet-50 Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) was fine-tuned via transfer learning on a custom RGB dataset comprising five classes of human body parts. The optimised model achieved a classification accuracy of 97.2% and was deployed on a terrain-adaptable, tetherless snake-like mobile robot. Real-time inference results, including victim identification and location data, were transmitted wirelessly via IBM Cloud and Node-RED to a mobile application interface with a total response time under 2.8 seconds. The proposed framework demonstrates operational viability for rapid victim localisation in time-critical disaster scenarios and establishes a foundation for autonomous rescue support systems by integrating CNN-based classification, embedded computing, and cloud communication. This work paves the way for the future development of fully autonomous, multimodal robotic platforms capable of operating reliably in diverse, unstructured disaster environments.

title: Optimal Placement of Energy Storage System (ESS) In Rooftop Solar-Penetrated Distribution Network Using Evaluational Programming (EP) Technique
authors: Nurdiana Natasha Zailani, Mohd Abdul Talib Mat Yusoh, Nur Badzlin Nasreen Roslan, Kanendra Naidu A-L Vijyakumar, Azuwien Aida Binti Bohari, Ulfah Mediaty Arief
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: This research investigates the challenges and potential of varying levels of rooftop solar (RTPV) penetration in distribution networks. This study analyzing voltage profiles and power losses under 30% of RTPV penetration scenarios is applied to determines the best locations for ESS placement to balance solar generation, smooth power fluctuations, and improve network performance. The best ESS locations are at buses 5, 24, and 12. The integration of the ESS significantly improved network performance, increasing the minimum voltage from 0.9805 p.u to 0.9821 p.u and reducing total active power loss from 9.2909 kW to 8.6182 kW. These findings demonstrate that optimal ESS placement is essential for balancing solar generation, smoothing power fluctuations, and enhancing overall system efficiency in RTPV-integrated distribution networks.

title: Lightweight High-Fidelity Low-Bitrate Talking Face Compression for 3D Video Conference
authors: Jianglong Li, Jun Xu, Bingcong Lu, Zhengxue Cheng, Hongwei Hu, Ronghua Wu, Li Song
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: The demand for immersive and interactive communication has driven advancements in 3D video conferencing, yet achieving high-fidelity 3D talking face representation at low bitrates remains a challenge. Traditional 2D video compression techniques fail to preserve fine-grained geometric and appearance details, while implicit neural rendering methods like NeRF suffer from prohibitive computational costs. To address these challenges, we propose a lightweight, high-fidelity, low-bitrate 3D talking face compression framework that integrates FLAME-based parametric modeling with 3DGS neural rendering. Our approach transmits only essential facial metadata in real time, enabling efficient reconstruction with a Gaussian-based head model. Additionally, we introduce a compact representation and compression scheme, including Gaussian attribute compression and MLP optimization, to enhance transmission efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves superior rate-distortion performance, delivering high-quality facial rendering at extremely low bitrates, making it well-suited for real-time 3D video conferencing applications.

title: Machine Learning Modeling for Predicting Water Level in the Nabaganga: A Typical River in Bangladesh
authors: Md. Najmus Sakib Sourov, Asmaul Husna Juhana, Taif Ur Rahman, Afroza Sultana, Mahady Hasan, Md. Tarek Habib
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Predicting an accurate water level is essential for flood risk management, water resource planning, and agricultural activities in the river system. This research aims to compare the performance of five machine learning models, XGBoost, Random Forest, LightGBM, MLP Regressor and CatBoost for forecasting water levels in the Nabaganga River, Bangladesh. The models were evaluated using key performance metrics including Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and Coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{\mathrm{2}}$</tex>). Based on the findings CatBoost became the most effective model, achieving the lowest MAE (0.027), RMSE (0.0336) and the highest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{\mathrm{2}} ( \text{0. 9 9 8 2} )$</tex>, indicating its superior ability to identify complex, non-linear relationships inherent in the water level fluctuations. XGBoost was also performing well with an MAE of 0.069, RMSE of 0.093 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{\mathrm{2}}$</tex> of 0.9882. Random Forest and LightGBM also were good with a relatively high level of errors whereas the MLP regressor was the least effective. The findings highlight the need to identify the appropriate machine learning models to use in hydrological predictions, especially in dynamic settings, where seasonal and environmental variations take place. The future work would involve the combination of hybrid models with optimization methods and real-time predictive abilities to further improve the precision of forecasts of flood and water resources to manage them.

title: A First Look at Privacy Risks of Android Task-executable Voice Assistant Applications
authors: Shidong Pan, Yikai Ge, Xiaoyu Sun
abstract: With the development of foundation AI technologies, task-executable voice assistants (VAs) have become more popular, enhancing user convenience and expanding device functionality. Android task-executable VAs are applications that are capable of understanding complex tasks and performing corresponding operations. Given their prevalence and great autonomy, there is no existing work examine the privacy risks within the voice assistants from the task-execution pattern in a holistic manner. To fill this research gap, this paper presents a user-centric comprehensive empirical study on privacy risks in Android taskexecutable VA applications. We collect ten mainstream VAs as our research target and analyze their operational characteristics. We then cross-check their privacy declarations across six sources, including privacy labels, policies, and manifest files, and our findings reveal widespread inconsistencies. Moreover, we uncover three significant privacy threat models: (1) privacy misdisclosure in mega apps, where integrated mini apps such as Alexa skills are inadequately represented; (2) privilege escalation via interapplication interactions, which exploit Android’s communication mechanisms to bypass user consent; and (3) abuse of Google system applications, enabling apps to evade the declaration of dangerous permissions. Our study contributes actionable recommendations for practitioners and underscores broader relevance of these privacy risks to emerging autonomous AI agents.

title: Kinematic Analysis and Trajectory Optimization of a 6-DOF Robotic Arm for Spacer Installation on High-Voltage Overhead Transmission Lines
authors: Xiaofa Zhou, Xiaofeng Wang, Yongming Xu, Boyang Sun, Yanfeng Xia, Diqing Fan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: As a key component for maintaining the spacing between multi-split conductors of high-voltage overhead transmission lines, the installation of four-split spacers requires precise operation in complex high-altitude environments. To address the issue of stable gripping of four-split spacers on high-voltage overhead transmission conductors, this paper designs a six-degree-of-freedom (6-DOF) robotic arm based on the installation requirements of four-split spacer for transmission conductors. Taking the serial robotic arm as the carrier, its control method establishes a forward kinematics model by combining the improved Denavit-Hartenberg (D-H) parameter method, conducts kinematic analysis, and realizes the accurate mapping from the joint space to the pose of the end effector. A dynamic model is constructed using the Lagrange equations to analyze the influence of system inertia, Coriolis force, and gravity terms on control. A composite control strategy combining dynamic feedforward torque compensation and PID feedback is designed and verified through simulation on the MATLAB/Simulink platform. The motion trajectory of the six-DOF installation robotic arm with and without dynamic feedforward control and PID algorithm is compared. Simulation experiments show that the peak value of joint position tracking error decreases significantly after adding feedforward control. This indicates that the feedforward torque output by the dynamic model accurately compensates for the output of the PID controller, ensuring that the joint motion trajectory of the robotic arm during operation is basically consistent with the desired trajectory, thus optimizing the operation trajectory for installing spacers of the robotic arm.

title: ML-Based Joint CFO and Channel Estimator for RCP-OTFS System
authors: Tingguang Zhang, Aijun Liu, Kegang Pan, Xiaohu Liang, Xin Lin, Xin Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: In this paper, to enhance the performance of channel estimation and reduce the spectral resource overhead, we propose a robust joint carrier frequency offset (CFO) and channel estimation framework for orthogonal time-frequency space (OTFS) systems in multipath scenarios. The proposed maximum-likelihood (ML)-based estimation algorithm overcomes critical limitations of disjoint estimation methods. Firstly, considering the effects of the CFO and multipath channel, the received signal of the OTFS system is modeled as a discrete-time expression. Then, the joint CFO and channel estimator has been derived based on the ML criterion. Leveraging a structured grid search combined with QR-decomposed subspace projection, the estimator achieves near-optimal performance while maintaining computational feasibility. Moreover, its computational complexity is analyzed. Simulation results show that our proposed method can achieve excellent performance in both CFO and channel estimation for the OTFS system in multipath channels.

title: Feasibility Study of Stacked Sub-THz Band Antenna in Package Modules Based on Warpage and Stress Analysis
authors: Makoto Tsukahara, Hiroshi Taneda, Naoki Kuroiwa, Kei Murayama, Yoko Nakabayashi
abstract: The use of the sub-THz frequency band is being considered for mobile wireless communications in the 6G era. It is well known that antenna directivity increases proportionally with frequency; therefore, structural designs that accommodate both high gain and wide-angle coverage are under investigation. In this report, a novel stacked Antenna-in-Package (AiP) structure is proposed, and its feasibility is examined primarily from the perspective of structural mechanics. Structural analysis using the finite element method (FEM) showed that modifying vias and laminate resin in stacked AiP substrates reduces thermal stress on Cu-cored balls by approximately 11 %, without significantly increasing substrate warpage compared to the unmodified configuration. Furthermore, when stress reduction is prioritized, the stress on the Cu-cored balls can be reduced by up to 18% through optimization of via structures and laminate resin properties. In addition, a comparative analysis was conducted on the rate of change in the inter-antenna distance resulting from these structural modifications. The results indicated that the variation was negligible relative to the threshold that significantly affects electromagnetic wave propagation.

title: Compact 60 GHz Microstrip Patch Antenna Design for WiGig and 5G mmWave Systems
authors: Anslin Star R, Anitha G
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The millimeter-wave (mmWave) spectrum around 60 GHz has gained significant attention for next-generation wireless systems because of its wide bandwidth and suitability for high speed, short-range communication. This paper details the design and analysis of a compact, high-gain microstrip patch antenna specifically optimized for efficient performance in the 60 GHz band, emphasizing a simple, unmodified rectangular patch structure. Unlike many existing designs that rely on complex slots, parasitic elements, or array configurations to enhance performance, this work demonstrates that a straightforward patch can deliver strong results. The antenna's high radiation efficiency, minimal return loss, and stable directional characteristics are attributed to its simulation on a low-loss Rogers 5870 substrate. Simulations using HFSS shows the antenna resonating at 59.55 GHz, with a reflection coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1 1}}$</tex>) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 3 4. 8 7 ~ d B}$</tex>, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 5 9 ~ G H z}$</tex> impedance bandwidth, and a 7.43 dBi peak gain. These findings confirm that the proposed antenna meets the requirements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0 ~ G H z ~ m m W a v e}$</tex> applications. Its simple geometry allows easy integration into compact communication systems.

title: Dynamic Metasurface Antennas-Based Beamforming for Holographic MIMO Systems
authors: Yuanli Ma, Bin Yan, Zheng Wang, Yongming Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: In this paper, to maximize sum rate for downlink multi-user holographic MIMO (HMIMO) systems, we investigate the designs of dynamic metasurface antenna (DMA) weights matrix and precoding matrix based on strategies of alternating optimization (AO) and decoupling optimization (DO), respectively. Specifically, in AO strategy, these two matrices can be designed in an alternative way. Based on it, given the fixed precoding matrix, the problem of DMA weights matrix design is approximated by a novel one, which can be efficiently solved but with better array gain. Meanwhile, for further complexity reduction, the designs of these two matrices can be decoupled in DO strategy, so that the DMA weights matrix is optimized independently from the precoding matrix. Finally, the introduced array gains and complexity reductions of the proposed algorithms based on AO and DO are validated by the simulation results.

title: Robust Radar UAV Classification via Micro-Doppler Signatures and Dual-Stream Attention Networks
authors: Shiwei Zhang, Yekun Liang, Haiyin Qing
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: The detection and classification of Low, Small, and Slow (LSS) targets, particularly multi-rotor Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), presents a critical challenge for low-altitude airspace surveillance. While Micro-Doppler (m-D) signatures serve as a unique fingerprint for such targets, extracting robust features in low Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) environments remains difficult due to the resolution limits of traditional Time-Frequency (TF) analysis. In this paper, we propose a physics-aware deep learning framework that synergizes advanced signal processing with attention-based representation learning. First, we model the Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) radar returns of varying rotor dynamics and employ the Synchrosqueezing Transform (SST) to concentrate dispersed energy into sharpened instantaneous frequency ridges. Second, we propose a Dual-Stream Attention Network (DSAN) governed by a rigorous inference algorithm. This network explicitly disentangles global kinematic body motion from local micro-motion flashes using parallel convolutional streams, fused via a Coordinate Attention mechanism. Extensive simulations demonstrate that our method achieves an overall classification accuracy of 96.8%, specifically reaching 99% precision in distinguishing biological targets. Furthermore, it maintains exceptional robustness (>85% accuracy at -10 dB SNR) compared to baseline methods, effectively mitigating environmental clutter.

title: Spectrum Strategies for 5G Deployment in Remote and Underserved Regions of Indonesia: A Systematic Review of Global and Local Practices
authors: Ade Wahyudin, Muhammad Suryanegara, Alfin Hikmaturokhman
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study explores spectrum allocation strategies to support 5G deployment in remote and underserved areas of Indonesia. Using a systematic review of international and national policy practices, the paper identifies key challenges and emerging solutions in managing spectrum for remote and underserved regions' connectivity. The findings show that Indonesia has opened several critical bands (700 MHz, 2.3 GHz, 3.5 GHz, and 26 GHz), yet its current spectrum policy remains dominated by exclusive licensing and uniform pricing, which limit accessibility in remote and underserved regions. In contrast, global best practices from the UK, Brazil, India, and Finland demonstrate adaptive models such as local licensing, Licensed Shared Access (LSA), Dynamic Spectrum Access (DSA), and Neutral Host Network (NHN), which offer greater flexibility and cost-efficiency for the deployment in remote and underserved regions. The comparative analysis reveals policy gaps in Indonesia and highlights the need for reform, particularly in introducing region-based pricing, open-access models, and remote and underserved regions-specific spectrum provisions. The study concludes that hybrid spectrum strategies integrating shared access and localized licensing can significantly enhance 5G accessibility in remote and underserved regions of Indonesia, provided they are supported by regulatory innovation and targeted infrastructure policies.

title: Transform-Domain Semantic Communication System with End-to-End Learning for Anti-Interference Transmission
authors: Rui Ma, Jinhui Tan, Shuo Chen, Hui Gao, Wenjun Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Existing wireless communication systems face interference challenges and limited spectrum resources. In this paper, we propose a scheme integrating semantic communication with transform-domain waveform design to mitigate interference impacts by leveraging the rotational properties of fractional transform-domain processing. Specifically, our scheme first integrates Weighted Fractional Fourier Transform waveform by embedding it into the training process, which not only optimizes waveform parameters for anti-interference performance, but also enables the neural network to learn semantic features across multiple transform domains, directly and significantly improving image reconstruction quality under interference conditions. Additionally, by introducing channel characteristics and interference during the training process, the semantic codec learns the statistical characteristics of these interference sources, enhancing the semantic codec's adaptive coding capability. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with conventional schemes, the proposed method achieves significantly improved anti-interference performance while simultaneously reducing communication overhead for equivalent-quality image transmissions.

title: Smart Irrigation Systems: Leveraging AI and Weather Forecasting for Water Efficiency
authors: Md Bayzid Kamal, Mashuk Rahman Utsho, Arifa Ahmed, AFM Rafid Hassan Akand, Arif Hosen, Md Mohaimin Rashid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Global agriculture faces critical water scarcity challenges, with conventional irrigation systems wasting 60% of water through evaporation and runoff while struggling to adapt to climate-induced temperature increases of 2.8° C by mid-century. This study proposes an AI-driven smart irrigation system integrating high-frequency (10-minute interval) meteorological data and ensemble machine learning to optimize water efficiency. Leveraging a Kaggle dataset of 52,696 observations across 21 variables, the three-tier architecture combines IoT sensors, data fusion, and a hybrid XGBoost-Random Forest model to predict vapor pressure deficit (VPdef) with 99.99% accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.9999$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{R M S E}=0.044 \text{kPa}$</tex>). The system demonstrated 40% water savings across diverse environments, achieving 37.2% reduction in arid zones (VPdef <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>1.5 \text{kPa}$</tex>) and 23.5% in humid climates through adaptive irrigation scheduling. Key innovations include temporal feature engineering for diurnal weather patterns, Bayesian hyperparameter optimization, and a scalable cloud-edge deployment framework validated across seasonal variations. Experimental results revealed solar radiation (38.7% feature importance) and VPdef hysteresis as primary irrigation drivers, enabling precise sub-hourly decisions via automated valve control. The ensemble approach reduced seasonal performance drift by 44.7% compared to individual models, addressing scalability challenges in heterogeneous soils. This research establishes a replicable blueprint for climate-resilient agriculture, merging IoT connectivity with predictive analytics to balance water conservation and crop productivity. Implementation roadmaps target 2026-2028 commercialization, emphasizing modular designs for smallholder accessibility and integration with satellite-based soil moisture mapping.

title: STAG: A Graph-Enhanced Spatio-Temporal Transformer Model for Crowdsourcing Task Assignment
authors: Zhanjin Li, Qingxian Pan, Xinjie Xu, Bokai Ding
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: With the rapid development of the Internet, crowd-sourcing technology has been widely applied. In the crowdsourced task allocation problem, most existing studies focus only on the consecutive task sequences executed by workers, without considering the effects of non-adjacent and discontinuous executions on task allocation as well as the collaborative signals from other workers. Therefore, this paper proposes a graph-enhanced spatio-temporal Transformer model. The model first employs Time2Vec to achieve continuous time encoding, capturing both linear and periodic temporal patterns. Second, based on workers' historical task sequences, a global task transition graph independent of individual workers is constructed, and a graph neural network (GNN) is used to learn the transition relationships among tasks. Then, a Transformer aggregation layer is utilized to explore the spatio-temporal relationships between any two task locations. Finally, an attention-based matching mechanism is applied to perform task allocation. Experimental results show that our model outperforms existing baseline methods on three real-world datasets: NYC. TKY, and Gowalla.

title: Improving Video Smoke Detection Accuracy Using the DETR Model with Hybrid Training Data
authors: Rykhard Bohush, Yahor Adamovskiy
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Enhancing the accuracy of video-based smoke detection algorithms is essential for improving the robustness and reliability of automated fire monitoring within intelligent video surveillance systems. This paper presents an enhanced application of transformer-based architecture for video smoke detection, leveraging the DETR (DEtection TRansformer) neural network model. To address the challenge of limited and heterogeneous training data, we constructed an extended hybrid dataset comprising realworld and synthetic smoke images, enabling more robust model training. The proposed methodology includes detailed preprocessing steps, data augmentation strategies, and a tailored training schedule optimized for smoke detection tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that the DETR model, trained with our approach, achieves high detection accuracy and outperforms several existing methods in terms of key metrics. We provide a comprehensive comparison with state-of-the-art approaches, highlighting the advantages of using transformers in this domain. Our findings suggest that transformer-based models, when properly trained on diverse datasets, offer a promising direction for real-time, vision-based fire monitoring systems.

title: DSatSim: A Large-Scale Distributed Satellite Simulation System Based on a Microservices Architecture
authors: Yang Lai, Yu Liu, Yumei Wang, Konglin Zhu, Jiake Li
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of satellite technology, Distributed Satellite Systems (DSS) are increasingly being utilized in various fields such as global communication, remote sensing, and navigation. The increasing scale of satellite network requires sophisticated simulation tools capable of handling complex and dynamically changing network topologies. However, traditional simulation platforms often fall short when dealing with diverse tasks and intricate scenarios. To address this issue, we propose DSatSim, a large-scale distributed satellite simulation system based on a microservices architecture. DSatSim integrates discrete-event simulation with virtualization techniques to support collaborative simulations involving multiple nodes and tasks. By adopting a microservices-based design, DSatSim enables flexible integration and coordinated operation of functional modules, including plugins for performance evaluation, routing, and channel modeling. Experiment results demonstrate that DSatSim effectively simulates distributed satellite networks, highlighting its importance as both a development and analytical tool.

title: Research on Hybrid Word Segmentation Strategy for Tele-LLMs Integrating Domain Knowledge
authors: Qixin Du, Lianghua Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Textual data in telecommunication network operations and maintenance scenarios contains rich professional terminology and complex semantic structures. Traditional general-purpose tokenization methods struggle to accurately capture domain-specific features, leading to limited semantic understanding in models, particularly pronounced in lightweight small models. Addressing the characteristics of abundant terminology and dynamic vocabulary changes in the telecommunications industry, this paper proposes a hybrid tokenization strategy that integrates domain knowledge. The strategy combines dictionary-driven approaches, statistical subword modeling, and a reordering token merging mechanism based on telecommunication domain concept recognition, dynamically adjusting token merging priorities to ensure semantic integrity of key terminology. Through collaborative training of the tokenization module with downstream tasks such as fault localization, we achieve task-driven optimization of tokenization boundaries. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method maintains model lightweight characteristics while improving fault localization accuracy by over 6.8% compared to traditional approaches. This research provides effective insights for lightweight design and efficient semantic understanding in telecommunication large language models.

title: An Analytical Study of Energy Consumption for Multi-Architecture Hybrid Vehicles in Low State-Of-Charge Under Two Driving Conditions
authors: Shuang Liu, Heng Guo, Kai Zhao, Zhang Gao, Hang Liu, Kun Zeng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: To investigate the energy consumption characteristics of new energy vehicles with different power architectures, five testing vehicles were selected, including range-extended electric vehicles (V1, V2), plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (V3, V4), and a hybrid electric vehicle (V5). Based on the environmental chamber, vehicle electric consumption and fuel consumption tests were conducted under the world light vehicle test cycle (WLTC) and high-speed test cycle to comprehensively analyze the energy consumption performance of different architecture hybrid vehicles under the two conditions. The research results show that under the high-speed test cycle, the fuel flow of the range-extended electric vehicles is stable and highly adapted to the change of vehicle speed. Among them, the fuel flow curve of V1 is smooth, and the average value is stable. And the fuel flow of plug-in hybrid electric vehicles V3 and V4 fluctuates periodically with the condition. In terms of fuel consumption per 100 km, V5 has the lowest fuel consumption, which is 8.03 L/100 km, showing the adaptability advantage under pure high-speed Condition. In terms of electric consumption per 100 km, V2 has the highest electric consumption, which is 24.94 kWh/100 km. Under WLTC, the fuel flow smoothness and adaptability of the five testing vehicles are basically consistent with those under the high-speed test cycle, and the fuel consumption of range-extended electric vehicles is generally higher than that of plug-in hybrid electric vehicles, with V2 having the highest fuel consumption of 8.33 L/100 km. In terms of electric consumption per 100 km, V3 and V4 reduce electricity consumption by adjusting the intervention timing of the motor and engine through the electric drive priority strategy.

title: Multi-Hazard Dynamic Response of a Large-Scale Grid-Type Dual-Platform Offshore Photovoltaic (PV) Structure Under Wind-Wave-Seismic Excitations
authors: Shijie Guan, Xiaotian Liu, Yingchong Jiang, Jianqiang Yu, Chen Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: This paper presents a preliminary investigation on the dynamic behavior of a novel large-scale grid-type dual-platform offshore photovoltaic (PV) structure under multiple hazards, including wind, wave, and seismic excitations. Based on the structural characteristics and offshore environment, the structural configuration and fundamental modal properties were first determined. Multi-hazard excitations were individually obtained using corresponding generation methods. Specifically, wind load time histories acting on the two PV platforms were generated based on the Davenport spectrum at two spatially related points. Wave loads applied to the pile foundations were simulated using the wave pattern via nonlinear time history analysis in SAP2000. Three recorded earthquake ground motions (GMs), i.e., one far-field GM, one near-fault pulse-like GM, and one near-fault no pulse-like GM, were selected from FEMA P695 according to the design spectrum. Considering these multi-hazard excitations, a series of dynamic time history analyses were conducted to evaluate the structural responses under combined wind-wave, concurrent wind-wave-seismic, sequential wind-wave-seismic, as well as windward and leeward excitation scenarios. The analytical results indicate that the seismic effect dominates the dynamic responses, and that vertical vibrations of the offshore PV structure are non-negligible under concurrent wind-wave-seismic excitation. In addition, sequential multiple hazards may induce a slight increase in structural relative displacement. Both windward and leeward directional excitations should be considered in structural dynamic analyses due to the wind-wave-induced fluctuation trend shift. The main findings of this study provide a favorable reference for understanding the dynamic performance of offshore PV systems under multiple hazards. Further studies on vibration control methods could be pursued to address the excessive structural responses.

title: Multi-Font Chinese Character Recognition with a Hierarchical Structure-Aware Graph Neural Network
authors: Tao Wu, Xinling Yao, Xingping Xian, Kan Tian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Multi-font Chinese character recognition is particularly challenging due to the large visual variation of the same character in different font styles, which usually leads to large intra-class diversity and poor model generalization. However, existing approaches usually describe the task as standard image classification, ignoring the inherent structural laws of Chinese characters and their potential to enhance cross-font robustness. To this end, this paper proposes a hierarchical structure-aware graph network (HSAGNet) that aims to explicitly incorporate structural cues into the recognition process. Unlike models that rely solely on pixel-level appearance, HSAGNet first transforms the character skeleton into a graph structure and encodes it with multi-level structure. Meanwhile, a structure embedding module is designed to extract semantic representations of tree structures from character labels to provide external guidance for the training process. The model backbone integrates a graph convolution for local topology modeling and a graph Transformer block for global structural inference. In addition, we introduce a structural consistency loss function to align visual graph embeddings with semantic structural embeddings. To validate the effectiveness of the method, a new dataset (SCD-6367) covering multiple font styles is also constructed in this paper. Experimental results on CASIA-HWDB1.1, CASIA-AHCDB, and SCD-6367 show that HSAGNet achieves superior performance in terms of recognition accuracy and model interpretability compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: Enhancing the Identification of Wildlife via Self-Supervised Learning on Unlabelled Camera Trap Information
authors: Jayanthi M, K.V. Kanimozhi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Wildlife monitoring has been transformed by camera-trap systems, which offer a wealth of visual data for behavioural study, population calculation, and species identification. But the majority of this data is still unlabelled, which presents a big problem for supervised training deep learning models. Conventional networks find it difficult to generalise in a variety of environmental settings, including shifting topography, vegetation density, and lighting variations. This research suggests a novel framework, Wildlife Self-Supervised Contrastive Network (WSSC-Net), to get around this restriction. It uses self-supervised learning (SSL) to extract useful features from unlabelled wildlife images. By using contrastive learning to generate pseudolabels, WSSC-Net learns robust representations, allowing for effective model training with little assistance from humans. To improve generalisation across ecological zones, the model integrates temporal cues, camera information, and domainspecific augmentations. Experiments on several wildlife datasets show that WSSC-Net outperforms supervised and traditional SSL baselines in terms of detection accuracy, wildlife classification, and domain-specific adaptability. The outcomes demonstrate that WSSC-Net is a scalable and successful method for automatic wildlife tracking in realworld settings with limited data.

title: Generalized Deepfake Detection for Video Communication
authors: Xiao Yuan, Lan Yang, Honggang Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of video communication technologies, deepfake techniques have posed severe challenges to content authenticity and system trustworthiness. To address the limitations of existing detection methods in terms of generalization and accuracy, this paper proposes a deepfake detection method based on an optimized XceptionNet architecture. By refining the network structure and building dataset, the proposed model achieves over 95% accuracy across datasets involving multiple forgery techniques, with an inference speed of up to 409 frames per second. Furthermore, fairness and consistency evaluations demonstrate that the proposed method maintains stable performance and coherent video-level decisions across diverse forgery types, and outperforms three representative generalization-focused baselines, highlighting its superior generalization and applicability in real-world communication scenarios.

title: Copyright and Reprint Permission
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering, Materials and Automation Technology (MMEAT)

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Test Conference in Asia (ITC-Asia)

title: Testing IFMIF-DONES current-mode micro-ionization chamber during LIPAc Phase B+
authors: Pablo Araya, Victor López, Santiago Becerril, Florian Benedetti, Juan Manuel García, David Jiménez-Rey, Saerom Kwon, Lucas Maindive, Álvaro Marchena, Juan Carlos Morales, Iván Podadera, Narita Takahiro, Moisés Weber, Esther Puertas
abstract: The IFMIF-DONES (International Fusion Materials Irradiation Facility – DEMO Oriented Neutron Source), under construction in Granada, Spain, will serve as a neutron source to irradiate materials and critical technologies for fusion power plants. It will produce prototypical fusion neutron fluxes capable of generating damage similar to that expected in future fusion reactors. This work presents the evaluation of a current-mode micro-ionization chamber (μIC) tested under the pulsed radiation environment of the Linear IFMIF Prototype Accelerator (LIPAc) in Rokkasho, Japan. The μIC, developed for integration into the STUMM (Start-Up Monitoring Module) of IFMIF-DONES, is designed to measure gamma radiation as part of the STUMM’s overall neutron and gamma field characterization during commissioning. The experiments focused on assessing the performance of the μIC under radiation conditions simulating IFMIF-DONES operational scenarios. Positioned in the LIPAc High Energy Beam Transport (HEBT) line near the Beam Dump, the current-mode μIC demonstrated its capability to detect pulsed gamma irradiation.

title: Efficient Vehicle Data Synchronization Using IBF in VEC Networks: Method and FPGA Hardware Accelerator
authors: Ara Cho, Jinsol Lee, Hyesook Lim
abstract: With the advancement of vehicle-to-everything (V2X) environments and AI technology, vehicle services are continuously evolving. The vehicle-edge-cloud (VEC) network is a suitable structure for autonomous driving and AI-based vehicle services, as it enables real-time interaction between vehicles and the edge and access to cloud resources. To develop vehicle services with more diverse purposes and build high-performance AI models in the VEC network, edge servers collect increasingly large volumes of vehicle data. As the amount of data uploaded from edge servers to the cloud increases, the memory usage in the cloud also increases to accommodate the growing volume of data. Such increases in bandwidth and memory usage hinder the scalability of VEC communication. To address this issue, this paper proposes a vehicle data synchronization method-defined in this work as uploading and storing data-using an invertible Bloom filter (IBF) and its implementation using a difference- IBF (d-IBF) decoding hardware accelerator on FPGA. In the proposed method, for vehicle data with non-varying characteristics, the edge inserts the vehicle data into an IBF and uploads it to the cloud, which updates and stores the data through d- IBF decoding, thereby completing the synchronization process. This allows vehicle data with strong non-varying properties to be synchronized using minimal bandwidth, and memory usage can be reduced by storing only the d-IBF decoding results. This study analyzes real vehicle data to identify non-varying properties and devises an appropriate non-uniform quantization method to use with the proposed approach. Experiments validate the accuracy of the operations of the proposed method, including IBF programming and d-IBF decoding. Furthermore, a hardware accelerator for d-IBF decoding is designed and verified on an FPGA, proving the implementation feasibility of the proposed synchronization method.

title: A Heuristically Staged Multiagent Reinforcement Learning Method for the Coverage Search Problem of Decentralized Multiagent Systems
authors: Ziheng Wang, Jianlei Zhang, Chunyan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: This article addresses the collaborative coverage search problem of intelligent multiagent systems in complex environments. The focus is on a decentralized swarm in which agents independently make decisions for exploration and collaboration under constraints of limited sensor range and restricted communication capabilities. To replicate real-world limitations, the environments considered contain complex obstacles that are initially unknown to the swarm. The primary objective is to cover all unknown areas as rapidly as possible while avoiding collisions with obstacles. We propose a heuristically staged multiagent proximal policy optimization searcher (HMPOS) based on the actor–critic (AC) framework, incorporating a two-stage training process with corresponding neural network modules. The first stage emphasizes local information and individual agent behaviors, whereas the second stage refines the previously trained module with a focus on global information and interagent cooperation. The neural network modules are specifically designed to align with this stage-wise training paradigm. Simulation experiments substantiate the feasibility of our approach, and comparative analyses with existing methods demonstrate the efficiency and superiority of our proposed solution.

title: A Massive MIMO Framework for Power-Constrained Devices Without Error-Correcting Memory
authors: MD Salman Sakib, Mahmoud A. Albreem, Shahriar Shahabuddin
abstract: Error correcting codes (ECCs) are frequently avoided by power-limited devices due to hardware complexity and financial limitations. During transmission, memory errors produced by these devices may spread over the wireless channel, making decoding more difficult at the receiver. It can be a crucial problem in a large MIMO system. We address this challenge in this article by proposing a two-stage solution. The first stage employs an equalizer-based detection method to lessen interference and communication channel impairments, while the second stage uses gradient boosted decision tree (GBDT) and neural network (NN) algorithms to forecast and fix memory errors. Using the GBDT and NN model through the Scikit-Learn library, we used Python simulations to show the effectiveness of our method. Following approximate inversion-based equalization and NN-based error detection at the massive MIMO base station receiver, the results show a detection accuracy of over 99%. Finally, we present a motivating example demonstrating how our proposed model can be integrated into the base station.

title: Co-Operative Caching for Real-Time Content Retrieval in an Integrated Space-Air-Ground Network for a Post-Disaster Scenario
authors: Sangita Dhara, Hadi Tabatabaee Malazi, Saqib Rasool Chaudhry, Aqeel Kazmi, Siobhán Clarke
abstract: During environmental disasters, communication infrastructure on the ground may suffer partial damage, resulting in disruptions to critical monitoring and rescue services. To overcome such a challenge, current approaches typically rely on either terrestrial ad hoc networks or satellite and Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) systems to restore communication services. However, these devices have limited resources, resulting in inefficiencies when analyzing and utilizing various distributed data sources to facilitate post-disaster services. Effective data caching and content retrieval strategies can enhance real-time information access, improving Quality of Service (QoS) for diverse applications. Designing such a system is challenging due to heterogeneous nodes, mobility of data producers and consumers, limited storage capacity, and variable operational needs. This paper proposes an efficient, dynamic, multi-layered content caching and management system based on request forwarding and content retrieval techniques, capable of handling diverse data types using Space, Aerial, and Ground Integrated Networks (SAGIN). The proposed system enhances content availability and reduces retrieval latency by enabling UAVand vehicle-based retrieval, improving QoS. We designed and developed a Contextual Multi-Armed Bandit (MAB) leveraging content freshness, popularity, and size, further extended by a Federated MAB with distributed Q-value sharing. Simulation results show that Contextual MAB outperforms LRU, popularitybased, and even the original MAB by 25-85% across different configurations and settings, while Federated MAB achieves about 15% improvement over Contextual MAB under spatio-temporal dynamics and 8-12% higher hit ratios across vehicle and BS tiers.

title: Uncertainty Guided Iterative Optimization Dense Matching Network for Occlusion Aerial Stereo Imagery
authors: Ying Luo, Jun Wu, Xuemei Zhao, Chuanfeng Sun, Juan Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: To address the challenge of local disparity estimation errors caused by large occlusions in aerial stereo imagery, an UGNet is proposed for dense matching. The network begins with a multi-scale cost aggregation-based disparity uncertainty estimation module, which generates an initial coarse disparity map while estimating uncertainty to effectively localize occlusion areas. Subsequently, an uncertainty-guided GRU-based disparity optimization module is introduced to iteratively reconstruct the disparity map, incorporating cross-correlation and both local and global contextual information. This approach mitigates error propagation in heavily occluded areas and improves estimation accuracy. Experimental results on the public WHUStereo and UAVStereo datasets demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms existing dense matching networks in terms of EPE and 3PE, particularly in occlusion areas. The results indicate strong potential for accurately processing urban aerial stereo imagery under severe occlusion conditions.

title: Centralized Multi-Functional Power Management of an Integrated Microgrid–Electric Vehicle Charging Station
authors: Hossien Faraji, Amir Khorsandi, Seyed Hossein Hosseinian
abstract: Various power management systems for microgrids (MGs) integrated with electric vehicle (EV) charging stations (CSs) have been designed so far, each addressing different aspects of grid-connected or off-grid operation. However, no previous research has developed multi-objective power management schemes that encompass all possible operating modes, including MG operation with the grid (G) without CS, CS operation with G without MG, MG-CS integrated operation with G, MG and CS autonomous operation in off-grid mode, and the interaction between MG and CS without G. In this article, innovative multi-functional power management strategies utilizing centralized control system have been modeled and designed for the aforementioned operational modes and simulated in the time-domain nonlinear environment of the MATLAB-SIMULINK. The results indicate the effective performance of different schemes in various operational states.

title: Retraction Notice: Bangla Abusive Language Detection using Machine Learning on Radio Message Gateway
authors: Sumaiya Salim Ritu, Joysurya Mondal, Md. Moinu Mia, Ahmed Al Marouf
publish: 2021 6th International Conference on Communication and Electronics Systems (ICCES)
abstract: In the era of modern technology, machine learning and natural language processing has been adopted to be applied in several application areas. Natural language processing consists of diversified techniques such as text classification, text summarization, named entity recognition, sentiment analysis. Text classification is considered to be the area of research where the text gets segmented into different category sentences or paragraphs from a single text genre. This paper presents a mechanism for detecting Bangla abusive language from a realtime radio message gateway. Online radio stations nowadays accept communications and voices of their target audience from web-based applications or social media platforms, such as Facebook or Twitter pages. This paper has created a dataset with more than 45000 Bangla sentences, which are labeled as abusive and non-abusive. Sample online radio message gateway has been been introduced and machine learning algorithms such as multinomial naive bias (MNB), logistic regression (LR), and random forest (RF) classifiers are utilized to predict the abusive languages. One of the significant prospects of this work would be applied during live radio programs where listeners try to communicate by sending live messages. Our proposed mechanism can check and map the live messages with the dataset and segregate the positive comments or messages only, by filtering the abusive comments. Among the applied classifiers, it has been found that the random forest classifier has performed better than the other two classifiers by leveraging approximately 76% accuracy.

title: A Comprehensive Survey on Dynamic Graph Processing: Storage and Analytics
authors: Yuan Zhang, Huawei Cao, Yue Shen, Sibo Li, Yang Feng, Chunxiang Wang, Xuejun An, Xiaochun Ye
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Dynamic graph processing is becoming increasingly critical across a wide range of domains, including social networks, financial transactions, and business intelligence. Its effectiveness relies heavily on optimizations in both storage and analytics, which are essential for improving system performance, throughput, and scalability. While dynamic graph processing has attracted significant research attention and yielded notable progress, a comprehensive analysis that integrates advancements in both dynamic graph storage and analytics remains lacking. To address this gap, this paper presents a thorough review of stateof-the-art techniques that support dynamic graph processing, with a particular focus on storage and analytical methods. Specifically, we first outline the fundamental challenges and core design principles in the field. Then, we systematically classify and summarize existing approaches, encompassing dynamic graph storage and analytics optimizations across both CPU and GPU platforms. Finally, we identify key research gaps and suggest promising directions for future work. This survey presents a comprehensive and up-to-date review of the literature on dynamic graph processing, offering valuable insights for both new and established researchers and contributing to the advancement of the field. The related materials for this paper are available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/yzhang610/DynGraphSurvey</uri>.

title: Ensemble Domain Adaptation with Constructive Incremental Learning for Fault Diagnosis of UAV Actuators
authors: Jiang Liu, Wei Dai, Liang Cao, Yan Qin, Chau Yuen
abstract: The performance of actuators is essential for ensuring the safe and reliable cruise of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). However, limited data and constrained computational resources pose significant challenges for accurate and timely fault diagnosis of UAV actuators in practice. To this end, this paper proposes a novel lightweight fault diagnosis method termed ensemble domain adaptation with constructive incremental learning (EDA-CIL). First, a cloud feature extraction strategy is developed to adaptively extract fault-sensitive information from vibration signals using cloud entropy theory. Next, the proposed meta domain adaptation (MDA) is gained with node-based constructive incremental learning, which serves as a base classifier leveraging knowledge from the source domain and few-shot target domain data. Specifically, MDA minimizes discrepancies in marginal and conditional distributions across different domain features during this incremental process, benefiting the lightweight and compact structure for domain adaptation. Moreover, the convergence analysis of MDA is given to guarantee the efficacy of cross-domain performance and network compactness theoretically. Finally, to avoid the negative transfer arising from excessive dependence on a single-source domain, the stable diagnosis performance is obtained via the domain energy-based parallel ensemble learning of multiple source actuators. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves good accuracy and the fastest speed on hexacopter UAV actuator diagnosis with limited data. In comparison with the bidirectional LSTM-based multi-source transfer learning method, EDA-CIL improves the diagnostic accuracy by 3.45%, 5.61%, 9.34%, 11.86%, 2.29%, and 12.80% across six actuators, while achieving approximately 18 times faster diagnostic speed.

title: Distributed Online Task Assignment via Inexact ADMM for unplanned online tasks and its Applications to Security
authors: Ziqi Yang, Roberto Tron
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: In multi-robot system (MRS) applications, efficient task assignment is essential not only for coordinating agents and ensuring mission success but also for maintaining overall system security. In this work, we first propose an optimization-based distributed task assignment algorithm that dynamically assigns mandatory security-critical tasks and optional tasks among teams. Leveraging an inexact Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM)-based approach, we decompose the task assignment problem into separable and non-separable subproblems. The non-separable subproblems are transformed into an inexact ADMM update by projected gradient descent, which can be performed through several communication steps within the team. In the second part of this paper, we formulate a comprehensive framework that enables MRS under plan-deviation attacks to handle online tasks without compromising security. The process begins with a security analysis that determines whether an online task can be executed securely by a robot and, if so, the required time and location for the robot to rejoin the team. Next, the proposed task assignment algorithm is used to allocate security-related tasks and verified online tasks. Finally, task fulfillment is managed using a Control Lyapunov Function (CLF)-based controller, while security enforcement is ensured through a Control Barrier Function (CBF)-based security filter. Through simulations, we demonstrate that the proposed framework allows MRS to effectively respond to unplanned online tasks while maintaining security guarantees.

title: Adaptive noise reduction pipeline leakage detection method based on Multimodal Transformer and contrastive learning
authors: Xianming Lang, Hongmiao Sun, Jiangtao Cao, Edith C.H. Ngai
abstract: Under the dual constraints of strong industrial noise and small sample size, achieving high precision and reliability in pipeline leakage detection is a significant challenge. The root cause lies in the fact that traditional single-modal methods have a single perception dimension and insufficient anti-interference ability, making it difficult to effectively distinguish strong background noise with overlapping spectra, resulting in reduced measurement accuracy and reliability. To address this issue, this paper proposes an adaptive noise reduction detection method based on multi-modal Transformer and contrastive learning (MDT-CL). This paper uses pressure, vibration, and acoustic emission sensors to capture complementary signals, and synchronizes the collection of these signals through dynamic time warping technology. The proposed method comprises three key innovations. Firstly, we propose a multi-scale Transformer encoder based on physical principles, employing an asymmetric cross-modal attention mechanism to focus on pressure signals that are sensitive to leakage. We integrate vibration and acoustic features and fuse them at the feature level, fundamentally solving the heterogeneity and spatio-temporal mismatch problems of multi-source asynchronous sensor data. Secondly, we propose an embedded adaptive spectral normalization module, which is a dynamic frequency-domain filtering technology that collaboratively optimizes with the network. It intelligently suppresses noise-sensitive frequency bands and synchronously enhances leakage features, effectively overcoming the limitations of traditional static filtering. Thirdly, we propose a contrastive learning pre-training strategy for pipeline noise. By constructing a multi-modal noise enhancement library and feature decoupling mechanism, it significantly expands the inter-class distance between leakage and normal operating condition noise, enhancing the model’s generalization ability in complex noise conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a leakage detection accuracy of 96.8%, representing a 1.6% improvement over the current state-of-the-art method.

title: Hybrid Dielectric-Metallic-Loaded Horn Array Antenna with Enhanced Bandwidth and Aperture Efficiency Covering Entire K a-Band
authors: Jun Xiao, Jing Wu, Tongyu Ding, Chong-Zhi Han
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this letter, a hybrid dielectric-metallic-loaded (HDML) broadband horn array antenna is proposed, which achieves extremely high aperture efficiency (AE) and covers the entire Ka-band. The proposed antenna array is composed of four distinct components: a bottom full-corporate feeding network configuration, a second horn array layer, a third lattice structure acting as metallic loading, and a top embedded multi-step rectangular block (MRB) structure acting as the dielectric loading. High gain and high AE are achieved owing to the HDML technique. Additionally, the embedded MRB structure reduces the reflections between the air and the radiation configuration, which effectively enhances the impedance bandwidth (IBW). Ultimately, an HDML 8×8 horn array antenna was simulated, fabricated, and measured. The prototype achieves a 42.28% IBW ranging from 26.32 to 40.43 GHz. Thanks to the proposed HDML technique, the measured peak gain of the 8×8 horn array is 27.4 dBi and the AE is as high as 96.3%.

title: Hierarchical Offloading Optimization for Collaborative DNN Inference in Satellite Edge Computing Networks
authors: Yan Chen, Xiaolin Jia, Haiquan Wang
abstract: Satellite Edge Computing (SEC) enables intelligent onboard processing, which is essential for mission-critical tasks involving high data capture rates and compute-intensive Deep Neural Network (DNN) models. However, limited satellite computing capacity often causes significant processing delays, underscoring the need for efficient collaboration across the SEC network to ensure real-time responsiveness and optimal resource utilization. Since DNN models can be partitioned into sub-tasks executed in parallel across multiple nodes, partitioning naturally facilitates collaborative processing by exploiting idle resources and reducing transmission overhead, while also accelerating inference. Motivated by this, we propose HiO2, a hierarchical task offloading framework that maximizes system throughput by coordinating partitioned sub-tasks across satellites and ground stations. To realize this hierarchical design, HiO2 incorporates two key mechanisms: (i) a swarm-level offloading strategy that assigns tasks to swarms according to their optimal allocation, and (ii) a node-level runtime-aware partitioning and sub-task offloading scheme that dynamically determines both the number of partitions and the placements of cut-points, while coordinating the execution of sub-tasks across nodes based on workload and network conditions. Extensive experiments on a prototype system demonstrate that HiO2 not only outperforms state-of-the-art methods in system throughput and average task response time but also consistently meets real-time task deadlines.

title: Speech-Based Efficient Parkinson’s Disease Detection Using Hybrid Deep Learning Framework
authors: Vastav Tailwal, Subhayu Ghosh, Arjun Ghosh, Brahmanand Dubey, Nanda Dulal Jana
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Parkinson’s Disease (PD) is a type of progressive neurodegenerative disorder where early diagnosis is critical to improve the quality of life for the patient. Speech impairments are among the earliest and most prominent biomarkers of Parkinson’s disease, motivating the use of automated voice-based diagnostic systems. Conventional machine learning models relying on handcrafted acoustic features often lack the capacity to capture temporal and spectral dependencies in disordered speech, while deep learning methods like Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) or Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) alone can provide only partial representations. In this work, we propose a hybrid CNN–LSTM framework that leverages Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) to jointly model local spectral features and long-range temporal patterns in speech. The pipeline begins with MFCC feature extraction from sustained phonation samples, followed by convolutional layers for hierarchical spectral representation and LSTM layers for sequential modeling. Experimental evaluation on a balanced dataset of Parkinson’s and healthy control recordings demonstrates that the CNN–LSTM significantly outperforms baseline and traditional ML classifiers. The proposed framework achieves 90% accuracy, 90% macro F1-score, and 0.91 ROC-AUC score, establishing its robustness and reliability. These results underscore the potential of the proposed method as a scalable, voice-based screening tool for early PD detection. The source code is made available for reproducibility at: https://shorturl.at/5zfI9.

title: Template Matching Based Temporal Motion Vector Refinement for the Next Generation of AVS
authors: Hongbo Li, Yucheng Zhong, Jiabao Zhu, Wanglin Lai, LiCong Ma, Jie Chen, Ru-Ling Liao, Xinwei Li, Yan Ye, Li Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: In search for more efficient video compression capability than versatile video coding (VVC), joint video experts team (JVET) launched exploration work on new video coding technologies beyond VVC in 2021. In 2023, audio video coding standard (AVS) workgroup started to explore the new video coding technologies beyond AVS3 and released a software platform named exploration video model (EVM). EVM-0.7 adopted a subblock- level temporal motion vector (MV) prediction technology, namely enhanced temporal motion vector prediction (ETMVP). It allows motion inheritance at the subblock level, thus enabling more fine-grained prediction. However, although the MVs are derived at subblock level, the collocated block from which the MVs are derived is located at a reference picture which may have different motion from the current picture. Thus, the derived MV may not well match with the current block. To improve the accuracy of the MVs derived in ETMVP, a template matching (TM) based motion refinement method is proposed, in which each subblock-level MV is shifted by a same offset which is determined based on the TM cost. In addition, to reduce the complexity introduced by TM search, all subblock MVs derived from ETMVP are converted to uni-prediction MVs in low delay pictures. The proposed method is implemented on top of EVM-0.7. Experimental results show that it achieves BD-rate reductions of {-0.13% (Y), 0.14% (U), -0.21% (V)} and {-0.29% (Y), -0.44% (U), -0.36% (V)} under random access and low-delay B configurations, respectively. Due to the attractive coding performance, the proposed method has been adopted into EVM platform as a potential coding tool for the next generation of AVS standard.

title: AsLUT: Asymmetric LUT for Bayer Image Demosaicing and Denoising
authors: Ruhan Liu, Jingyun Liu, Zhenzhong Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: While deep learning has demonstrated impressive results in joint demosaicing and denoising (JDD), its computational and memory requirements hinder practical deployment. To address this, we propose an asymmetric look-up table (LUT)-based method, AsLUT, which performs asymmetric processing across different color channels and relative positions according to the mosaic pattern. First, Channel-wise Asymmetric Indexing is proposed to fully exploit intra-channel information under constrained input dimensions, obtaining initial full-resolution RGB images. Additionally, since the element fidelity varies periodically across spatial positions according to the distribution of known elements in the raw images, Mosaic Point-wise LUTs (MP-LUTs) and Mosaic Modulation (MM) are proposed to explicitly adapt to this fidelity variation. The former assigns distinct color mapping operators to elements at different relative positions, and the latter applies position-specific scalar weights to adjust pixel values in the image domain. Furthermore, Pattern-Guided Channel Fusion (PGCF) exploits spatial and channel correlations by performing fusion guided by mosaic pattern. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AsLUT achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods both quantitatively and qualitatively.

title: Three-Dimensional Reconstruction Based on Single-Frequency Phase-Shift Profilometry
authors: Weiqiang Sun, Zhenzhou Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Phase-shifting profilometry is recognized as a key method in structured-light 3D reconstruction, mainly due to its excellent accuracy, rapid processing, and non-contact imaging features. It plays an essential role in various application scenarios, including intelligent manufacturing, industrial inspection, and medical diagnostics. To address the issues of traditional phase-shifting profilometry, such as the requirement to acquire a large number of fringe images and the low measurement efficiency, this paper presents a three-dimensional reconstruction method that relies on singlefrequency four-step phase-shifting profilometry. This method involves designing a set of color phase-shifted fringes. Through phase boundary extraction and phase boundary correction algorithms, the phase boundaries are corrected and clustered. Subsequently, the clustered phase boundaries are employed to unwrap the wrapped phase column by column, thus enabling the 3D reconstruction of complex objects. Experimental findings show that, when contrasted with traditional techniques, the suggested method decreases the quantity of fringe images required. At the same time, it guarantees high reconstruction precision and successfully accomplishes the 3D reconstruction of intricate objects.

title: Optimization of Personalized Recommendation Strategies in Simulated E-Commerce Environments Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Lina Jiao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: This paper proposes a deep reinforcement learning framework for optimizing personalized recommendation strategies in simulated e-commerce environments. By integrating PPO with multi-head attention, curiosity-driven rewards, and multi-agent coordination, the model addresses dynamic user preferences and long-term engagement. Experimental simulations show promising performance over baselines, with improvements in CTR by 21.5% and NDCG@10 by 17.3%. While these results are obtained under controlled simulation conditions and may represent upper-bound estimates due to inherent simulator-model alignment, the approach demonstrates potential for enhancing ecommerce recommendations and warrants further validation on real-world datasets.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Routing for QKD-ON: Leveraging QAOA and QVE for Minimizing Blocking
authors: Sriansh Tripathy, Suman Palrecha, Vimal Bhatia
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Resource Allocation in quantum key distribution-enabled optical networks (QKD-ON) is an NP-hard combinatorial problem. Classical heuristics are greedy and suffer high blocking under dynamic traffic and security constraints, while deep reinforcement learning (DRL) methods can reduce blocking but introduce a processing bottleneck because their inference latency is often slower than the operational timescales of QKD-ON. We introduce hybrid quantum–classical frameworks using the quantum approximate optimization algorithm (QAOA) and variational quantum eigensolver (VQE) to perform request routing. The scheduling problem is encoded as a hamiltonian with penalty terms for wavelength and timeslot conflicts. We evaluate the approach on NSFNet and UBN topologies under dynamic traffic with time division multiplexing (TDM) and wavelength-time division multiplexing (WTDM). Our quantum-enhanced algorithms yield comparable or lower blocking with reduced inference latency, which can improve further on quantum hardware.

title: KAISA: Knowledge And Internal States Augmented Agent for Chemistry Knowledge Question Answering
authors: Weichen Dai, Yixuan Pan, Zhekai Ge, Xinhe Li, Yi Zhou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often struggle with rigorous scientific tasks, suffering from hallucinations and calculation errors due to the lack of precise domain knowledge. While Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) can mitigate these issues, standard approaches frequently encounter knowledge conflicts between parametric memory and retrieved context, as well as sensitivity to noise. To address these challenges, we introduce KAISA-Agent (Knowledge And Internal States Augmented Agent), a novel framework that synergizes external knowledge with internal inference states. Our approach employs an iterative tool invocation mechanism for precise computation and a dual-path hybrid sampling strategy that dynamically arbitrates between internal reasoning and external evidence using a sequence-level confidence metric. Experiments demonstrate that KAISA-Agent significantly enhances a lightweight 8B model, achieving a 10.34% accuracy improvement.

title: Interference Recognition Algorithm Based on Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Jianyu Liu, Chenyu Huang, Chengzhao Shan, Heng Dong, Zhuoming Li, Jiayan Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Traditional interference recognition algorithms rely on the separability of expert features and the design of classifiers, requiring targeted extraction of feature parameters based on the type of interference. This necessitates sufficient prior knowledge of the interference, thereby lacking universality. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are capable of automatically extracting features and obtaining high-dimensional characteristics of input data, and thus have begun to be introduced into the field of communication signal recognition. However, directly using onedimensional communication interference signals as the input to CNNs can easily lead to recognition results being affected by Gaussian white noise, particularly resulting in low recognition rates under low Jammer-to-Noise Ratio (JNR) conditions. Therefore, this paper proposes an interference recognition algorithm based on Convolutional Neural Networks, using the joint timefrequency representation of interference signals as the input to CNNs. This approach fully leverages the strengths of CNNs in image feature extraction while enhancing the algorithm's noise resistance. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm can effectively recognize six typical types of communication interference and four composite interferences.

title: Utilizing Multimedia Learning Systems to Train SME Owners in E-Commerce Technologies: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Sekar Wulan Prasetyaningtyas, Chairani Putri Pratiwi, Cindy Rahayu
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study offers a systematic literature review (SLR) of multimedia learning systems (MLS) employed to educate small and medium-sized firm (SME) owners in e-commerce technology.
