We employed the PRISMA framework to assess 23 papers published from 2010 to 2024. Our analysis emphasizes prevalent MLS categories (e.g., LMS, MOOCs, mobile platforms), regional variances, and significant obstacles include inadequate digital literacy and infrastructural constraints. A comparative thematic analysis indicates that mobile-first solutions are especially efficacious in low-bandwidth environments, whereas LMS platforms excel in organized, institutional situations. The research additionally highlights under-explored domains, such as adaptable MLS designs and the integration of AI. In light of these findings, we advocate for specific recommendations for forthcoming study and system development. This review enhances comprehension of how MLS might facilitate SME digital transformation in many socio-technical contexts.

title: A Conceptual Framework for ERP and AI Integration in Sustainable Agriculture in Indonesia
authors: Anung Andi Hidayatullah, Hari Purnomo, Hartomo Soewardi, Imam Djati Widodo
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Sustainable agriculture in developing countries faces persistent challenges, including fragmented data systems, low supply chain transparency, and weak traceability mechanisms. In Indonesia, these issues are further compounded by limited access to integrated digital infrastructure, which hampers the capacity of farmers, cooperatives, and government institutions to implement coordinated, data-driven sustainability practices. This study proposes a conceptual framework that integrates Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to address these challenges. The framework is structured into three layers: input data sources, a core ERP-AI processing system, and stakeholder-specific outputs. ERP modules are enhanced with AI capabilities for predictive analytics, resource optimization, and real-time decision support. The model is designed to promote collaboration, improve operational transparency, and strengthen climate resilience. By aligning technical design with the identified sustainability problems in agriculture, this framework offers a scalable and inclusive digital solution that supports the transition toward a more resilient and sustainable agricultural system.

title: Analysis of Robustness Against Node Attacks in Mega-Constellation Networks
authors: Siyue Lu, Xianyang Liu, Shiqing Peng, Weifeng Zeng, Mingze Li, Xin Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The mega-constellation network is an important part of the future integrated space-air-ground information network and has received great attention in recent years. However, when the satellite nodes in a mega-constellation are attacked or fail due to malfunction, the performance of the constellation network will be greatly affected, which may even lead to the failure of autonomous operation of the constellation network. To this end, we study the impact of different node attack strategies on the robustness of mega-constellation networks, measure the importance of nodes with the help of betweenness centrality, and define three node attack strategies to meet the actual attack or failure scenarios, and simulate them with three typical mega-constellation networks as prototypes. The results show that the dynamic betweenness centrality based attack strategy has the greatest impact on the robustness of mega-constellation networks and is more likely to lead to network collapse, while random attacks have the least impact.

title: Pyzbar vs. YOLOv8n for Real-Time Barcode Detection in Waste Banks: A Performance Benchmark with TF-IDF Waste Classification
authors: Yugo Pratama, Arfio Ardana, Khoerunnisa Alfin, Yulintyandra Puja Antanita, Rita Purnamasari, Khaerudin Saleh
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study presents an integrated approach to barcode detection benchmarking and machine learning-based waste classification for community waste bank applications. Two detection paradigms are evaluated: Pyzbar, a rule-based decoder optimized for low-latency execution on consumer devices, and YOLOv8n, a lightweight deep learning detector fine-tuned on a custom barcode dataset for robustness under real-world distortions, including low illumination and rotated orientations. A total of 120 live-scan trials were conducted across varied lighting, rotation, and barcode types using a laptop camera to simulate typical consumer hardware. Results show that Pyzbar achieves 100% accuracy under optimal conditions with an average latency of 67.6 ms but degrades in low-light or skewed scenarios, while YOLOv8n maintains higher robustness but incurs an average latency of 523.7 ms. For waste type classification, barcode metadata is vectorized using TF-IDF and classified with a Random Forest model, achieving a 97.7% F1-score and outperforming SVM and Naive Bayes in both accuracy and computational efficiency. The proposed modules are integrated into Resik, a Flutter-based mobile application with a FastAPI backend, featuring real-time waste categorization, value estimation, and a points-based incentive system to improve community recycling participation. The main contributions include: an empirical benchmark of barcode detectors under realistic distortions; a lightweight, high-accuracy waste classification pipeline; and a field-tested mobile prototype for community-based waste management.

title: Short circuit forecast in axial flux permanent magnet machine using LSTM network
authors: Richard Gilles Agbokpanzo, Dourodjayé Pierre Aguemon, Renaud Philippe D’Almeida
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Short circuit failure is an important issue to monitor in electrical machines in industrial applications. This paper deals with the short circuit forecast in an axial flux permanent magnet machine using a long short-term memory (LSTM) network to prevent stator winding failure. The axial flux permanent magnet machine (AFPM) has been modelled in 3D using Flux3D under constant speed analysis with 6000 rpm rotative speed. Results show good performance for the machine in terms of electromagnetic torque and back-emf. Then a model of a short circuit has been simulated with short circuit instances of 0.25% to 0.75% representing realistic fault scenarios of a number of equivalent electrical period variations. A waveform data set, which contains 6540 synthetic short circuit models has been generated to train an LSTM network to forecast future values of waveforms given the values from previous time steps using both closed loop and open loop forecasting. MATLAB has been used for implementing LSTM. After 1400 iterations, LSTM shows good results with RMSE 0.8927.

title: Dynamic Management Method of Network Slices in 5G for Improving QoS in VoIP
authors: Viktor Gnatyuk, Ivan Gorbachov, Roman Hamretskyi
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Dynamic variations in load, latency, jitter, and packet loss critically affect VoIP quality, especially in corporate systems, operator infrastructures, and emerging 5G technologies. Traditional mechanisms such as Differentiated Services (DiffServ) and Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS) cannot rapidly adapt to changing conditions, leading to degraded performance in high-load scenarios and within 5G network slicing.To address these limitations, this study explores the application of machine learning (ML) for predictive QoS management and adaptive slice control. A method is proposed that combines Long Short-Term Memory networks for forecasting QoS parameters with Reinforcement Learning for optimal slice selection. The approach enables predictive traffic redirection to minimize latency, reduces jitter and packet loss to stabilize voice connections, and automates management by reducing reliance on manual QoS policies.The results confirm the effectiveness of the method. A predictive ML-based algorithm was developed for early detection of potential QoS issues. The adaptive management mechanism optimizes bandwidth allocation and traffic prioritization, resulting in notable service quality improvements. Experimental analysis shows that the proposed approach outperforms DiffServ and MPLS, reducing average latency by 25-30% and significantly enhancing VoIP stability.The findings highlight the potential of ML-driven solutions for real-time optimization of IP telephony. Future research will focus on integration with cloud-based VoIP platforms and further development of deep learning models to improve prediction accuracy and scalability in next-generation networks.

title: Generalized Dual-Mode Index Modulation Based on OCDM Transmission System
authors: Jinbang Kong, Zhouhao Li, Fengyi Chang, Shutong Feng, Wei Deng, Yili Xia, Wenjiang Pei
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Orthogonal chirp division multiplexing (OCDM) has emerged as a promising multi-carrier technology, offering inherent advantages in combating severe multipath distortion. In this paper, we propose a novel generalized dual-mode index modulation OCDM (GDM-OCDM) transmission system, where the subcarriers within each OCDM subblock are partitioned into two groups modulated by distinct constellation alphabets. The number of subcarriers assigned to each constellation mode can be dynamically adjusted per subblock, while their indices are exploited to carry additional information bits, thereby enhancing the overall spectral efficiency. Moreover, two equalizers are employed to mitigate channel distortion, followed by a maximum likelihood (ML) detector for optimal symbol demodulation. To overcome the exponential complexity of ML detector, a low complexity log-likelihood ratio (LLR) detector is proposed. Simulation results show that GDM-OCDM achieves higher spectral efficiency than conventional OCDM and DM-OCDM with nearly identical bit error rate performance, and exhibits superior multipath resilience compared to conventional OFDM system.

title: Supply Chain Reaction: Enhancing the Precision of Vulnerability Triage using Code Reachability Information
authors: Harshvardhan Patel, Alexander Snit, Michalis Polychronakis
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Software bill of materials (SBOMs) have become a crucial component of large-scale vulnerability triage. Existing SBOM approaches operate at the granularity of software packages or binary executables, providing a very coarse and context-agnostic view of a program's dependencies. In turn, if a third party component has a vulnerability, existing SBOM tools will report it for remediation irrespectively of whether a given dependent program is actually affected by that vulnerability or not. Although determining the exact conditions under which a vulnerability in a third-party component may affect a dependent program remains a challenging problem, a first robust approximation can be obtained by considering the reachability of the vulnerable code from the parent program. Based on this observation, the main goal of this work is to assess the improvement that code reachability information can offer in the precision of vulnerability triage for C/C++ programs. To that end, we developed VPChecker, a system that i) captures the dependencies of a program at the granularity of individual functions, and ii) localizes CVEs in shared libraries to the corresponding functions that contain the flaw. Using VPChecker, we conducted a large-scale vulnerability assessment of the Debian Linux ecosystem. The results of our cross-examination of over 24,000 C/C++ binaries (shipped by more than 6,600 Debian packages) with 510 CVEs in shared libraries, show that function-level reachability information reduces the number of ELF binaries reported as affected by a particular CVE by 28% on average, while reducing the number of CVEs reported as affecting a particular ELF binary by 30%.

title: A Methodological Framework for Applying Multichannel Aerospace Imagery in Corporate Differential Game Pursuit Models
authors: Volodymyr Hnatushenko, Dmytro Hryshchak, Yuliia Olevska, Viktor Olevskyi, Natalya Sokolova
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: The study of mathematical models of pursuit in combination with methods of corporate differential games can help in the development of the latest vehicle control systems. The purpose of this study is to develop a methodology for using multichannel satellite imagery data in vehicle control systems based on pursuit models in corporate differential games. The research methodology is to integrate data from multichannel aerospace images into calculations of aircraft movement parameters using game theory methods and the creation of modern information technology to prevent enemy counteraction. Aerospace images are exposed to a large number of excitation factors that distort the information on them and often do not allow it to be used directly. This determines the urgency of the task of creating intelligent vehicle control systems of a new generation with information systems for processing and analyzing aerospace images included in them. The main direction of improving the accuracy of image processing and analysis in this direction is the use of a series of images of the same object. The scientific novelty of the study lies in the development of methodological foundations for the use of multichannel aerospace image data in vehicle control systems and the creation of modern information technology for optimal control of aircraft, which is based on pursuit models in corporate differential games and machine learning methods of neural networks.

title: Multi-Model Crowd-Sensing Driven CSS Optimization at Low SNR
authors: Zichao Meng, Dehao Zhu, Mingchen Ma, Guangzhen Si, Hanhong Wang, Lei Pan
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: With the rapid growth of mobile data services and the increasing demand for wireless spectrum resources, cognitive radio (CR) technology has become a key solution. Collaborative Spectrum Sensing is the core component of CR, which improves detection accuracy via multi-user participation. This paper proposes a collaborative spectrum sensing algorithm to address the challenge of complex decision making in multidevice scenario with varying channel quality and computational constraints. The algorithm establishes a utility function that links detection probability, false alarm probability, and sensing latency to determine optimal local actions, and introduces a multi-model selection mechanism enabling each secondary user to select from four modes: ConvLSTM, CNN, LSTM and silent mode. It adopts a proximal policy optimization (PPO) algorithm with collaborative sensing error minimization as the main objective. Additionally, it incorporates computational overload and individual utility penalty mechanisms to form multi-objective constraints. Simulation results show that the algorithm achieves low-latency, high-precision collaborative sensing under limited computing resources and low signal-to-noise ratio conditions. Our algorithm provides an efficient decision-making solution for resource-constrained multi-device collaborative sensing.

title: Edge-AI for Cardiac Healthcare: Classification of Aortic Stenosis on Resource Constrained Hardware
authors: Sumit Singha, Laxmi Narayan Sharma, Samarendra Dandapat
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a lightweight framework for the classification of Aortic stenosis on the edge with deployment on a resource-constrained edge computing device. This work demonstrates a real-world application of Edge-Ai for cardiac health monitoring. Edge devices generally come with resource constraints in terms of memory and processor. We have proposed a lightweight CNN model based on the ResNet-18 architecture. This trained model is computationally efficient and requires minimal memory for inference. This addresses the challenge of deploying deep-learning-based AI models on portable and cost-effective health monitoring devices. Our target device for this study is a Raspberry pi Zero 2W board. The trained model is optimized and deployed on the target hardware, which is equipped with only 512 MB of RAM, to study the feasibility of edge deployment and implementation. A separate set of test data was used to evaluate the classification performance of the trained and the deployed model. The deployed model, with a memory size of only 1.4 megabytes, managed to achieve an on-device accuracy of 97.99%, precision of 0.9712, recall of 0.9885, and F1 score of 0.9797. The real-time inference of the deployed model on the target device demonstrated consistency with the trained model, with a marginal and insignificant change in classification performance. Our study shows the feasibility of using a computationally efficient and memory frugal framework for automated, reliable, fast detection of Aortic stenosis at the edge using cost-effective, resource-constrained, and portable hardware devices.

title: Large Foundation Model Empowered Radio Frequency Fingerprinting for Wireless Networks
authors: Kaiqiang Li, Jingping Qiao, Xiwei Li, Chuanting Zhang, Haixia Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Radio frequency fingerprinting (RFF) is a crucial technology for identifying wireless devices, playing an essential role in network security. Recently, deep learning models have shown promising results in RFF but still face substantial challenges, including data noise and performance limitations. In this paper, we introduce large foundation models (LFM), the most advanced deep learning model, into RFF and propose a hybrid CNN and Transformer model termed RFF-CAT to solve the performance challenge. RFF-CAT integrates CNN's local feature extraction and Transformer's global dependency modeling, enabling comprehensive capture of both fine-grained and long-range patterns in RF signals. To solve the data noise challenge, we design a Multi-Packet Fusion (MPF) strategy to enhance RFF accuracy. This approach dynamically adjusts the weights assigned to each data packet, reducing the influence of packets with high interference and enhancing inference accuracy. To validate the effectiveness of our proposed methods, we conduct extensive experiments using a real-world LoRa dataset. The results demonstrate that RFF-CAT, together with the MPF strategy, outperforms the state-of-the-art models, particularly in low SNR conditions.

title: Improving Package Saw Quality for QFN Packages Through Surfactant-Assisted Sawing Process and High-Pressure Water Jet Implementation
authors: Mohd Rozaini Mohd Zali, Aswafi Bin Abdul Aziz
abstract: Burr formation in the dimple area of the lead region is a persistent quality concern during QFN package singulation. Residual burrs in these recessed areas can impact downstream electrical test reliability, solderability, and visual inspection. This study investigates the effect of two process enhancements of surfactant assisted sawing and high-pressure water jet implementation on burr performance, with a focus on dimple-area cleanliness. Controlled experiments were conducted using production QFN strips, with burr characterization focused on the lead dimple region. Three process conditions were evaluated: baseline, surfactant assisted coolant, and high-pressure water jet at varying pressures lower to higher (MPa). Results show that the high-pressure water jet at lower pressure successfully removed 100% of burrs within the dimple area, delivering a clean and well-defined lead surface. However, this condition also induced protrusion burrs in the Y-direction, attributed to water jet force displacing burr at the cut edge. Increasing the pressure further to higher eliminated both dimple and protrusion burrs, but caused torn carrier tape, indicating process-induced mechanical damage and poor strip stability at excessive pressure. In contrast, the surfactant-assisted sawing process showed negligible improvement in burr removal, particularly in recessed dimple areas, due to limited fluid penetration and mechanical interaction. These findings demonstrate that high-pressure water is effective in burr mitigation, but process parameters must be carefully optimized to balance burr removal and mechanical integrity. A lower pressure offers a potential sweet spot for dimple cleanliness with manageable trade-offs.

title: Usage of Social Media on Planned Behavior to Use Crypto Asset Trading Platform
authors: Bambang Leo Handoko, Nada Ayuanda
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This research aims to investigate the influence of social media use on planned behavior in using crypto asset trading platforms. By adopting the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB), this research explores the factors that influence an individual's decision to use the platform. The methodology used is a quantitative approach by collecting primary data through an online questionnaire distributed to cryptocurrency investors. Data analysis was carried out using Structural Equation Modeling - Partial Least Squares (SEM-PLS) with SmartPLS 4 software. The research results show that usage of social media has significant effect on attitude, subjective norm and perceived behavior control. Subjective norms and perceived behavior control have significant effect on use of crypto asset trading platform, while attitude has no significant effect. This result is expected to provide insight into how social media influences intentions and decisions to use cryptocurrency trading platforms, as well as the implications for marketing strategies and platform development in digital era.

title: In-Situ Package Level Relative Humidity Measurement Using Wet Bulb and Dry Bulb Temperatures
authors: Vidya Subramanian Iyer, Michael Joo Zhong Lim, Michael Goroll, Hai Guan Loh, Zhong Chen, Chuan Seng Tan
abstract: In this study, a novel approach to determine relative humidity inside the package using wet and dry bulb temperatures is proposed. Ceramic Dual Inline Package with test chip was used as test vehicle in closed, opened and saturated configurations corresponding to dry condition, dry bulb and wet bulb conditions. Electrical resistances of the test chips were measured in-situ and converted to temperatures to calculate the relative humidity for all three configurations for different set conditions inside the climatic chamber. Saturated test chip was found to have lower temperature compared to opened and closed configurations. Delta between relative humidity of the chamber and the calculated value from test chip measurements was greater than 10 % at lower relative humidity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \% R H$</tex>) compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{1}-\mathrm{2}\%$</tex> at higher relative humidity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{8 5 \%}RH)$</tex>. This method of calculating relative humidity using test chip resistance can be applied to any package dimensions unlike capacitive based sensors which are limited by their applicability to larger packages only. In addition, this method can be applied to measure humidity inside the moulded Integrated Circuit (IC) packages when subjected to stressed conditions like the Highly Accelerated Stress Test (HAST); which will help in understanding the reliability of semiconductor devices and their failure mechanisms.

title: AI-Driven Smart Framework for Indonesian Community Learning Centers: A TAM-SDT Integrated Approach
authors: Ika Suhartanti Darmo, Andi Wiliam, Viany Utami Tjhin, Marindra Bawono, Putri Mustika Susilo, Apriani Kurnia Suci
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Indonesia's informal education system continues to face structural challenges such as inadequate infrastructure, limited tutor digital literacy, and low institutional quality. To address these issues, this paper proposes an AI-driven smart framework for Community Learning Centers (PKBM) by integrating the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) and Self-Determination Theory (SDT). A PRISMA-guided systematic literature review of 292 sources (84 included) and a pilot implementation were conducted to ensure both theoretical rigor and practical validation. The framework adopts a five-layer architecture—Data Intelligence, AI Processing Engine, Smart Content Management, Interaction Interface, and Analytics & Evaluation—while embedding ethical safeguards for data privacy, algorithmic fairness, and system transparency. Empirical results indicate a 34% improvement in learning outcomes, a System Usability Scale (SUS) score of 82.5, an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.592 for student engagement, and an adoption rate of 78% among participating PKBM. The novelty of this research lies in the first integrated TAM-SDT model tailored for informal education, supported by a scalable and culturally adaptive AI architecture. Beyond Indonesia, the framework demonstrates transferability potential to other regions with diverse cultural and institutional settings. This study not only contributes to theory by advancing AI adoption models in informal education but also to practice by offering an ethically grounded, sustainable, and replicable framework for community-based learning transformation worldwide.

title: Smart Surveillance System for Accident Monitoring
authors: Amar Choudhary, Niveditha A, Madhumala Singh, Syed Khaja Mohiddin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: With the quick pace of life today, real-time monitoring and response for incidents is a top priority, particularly for road safety and public security. Smart Surveillance is an AI-based, low-cost system for detecting, recording, and reporting accidents or suspicious activity. It employs a Raspberry Pi with a PiCamera to observe an area, and a trained ML model to identify anomalies. Upon detection, it takes a photo, sends alarms through GSM, and saves data on Firebase. The system operates over Wi-Fi, hosted on AWS Ubuntu to be scalable. Pre-processing and classification improve detection and minimize false alarms. Outcomes indicate high accuracy and response time compared to conventional systems, with enhanced automation and mobility.

title: An empirical comparison of data transformation techniques for clustering-based unsupervised software defect prediction
authors: Zhengxiang Chen, Zhiqiang Li, Hongyu Zhang, Jie Ren, Feng Tian
abstract: As a key pre-processing step of software defect prediction, data transformation techniques aim to eliminate dimensional inconsistencies among software metrics and improve model performance. However, the impact of data transformation techniques on the performance of clustering-based unsupervised defect prediction (UDP) has not been fully explored. To this end, our aim is to empirically investigate the effect of data transformation techniques on the performance of clustering-based UDP models. In this paper, we systematically evaluated 23 unsupervised clustering models under 4 data conditions: (1) raw untransformed data (original), (2) logarithmic transformation, (3) z-score standardization, and (4) min-max normalization. Our experimental design consists of two dimensions: comparative analysis of individual models’ performance before and after data transformation and evaluation of relative performance across different models under various transformations. Extensive experiments conducted on 22 software projects indicate that: (1) Data transformation techniques have significant potential to improve the performance of unsupervised clustering models. They achieve improvements of 6.9% -22.9% in AUC,10.7% -41.6% in MCC, and 3.1% -12.27% in F_measure@20%. However, these transformations lead to a degradation in IFA performance, which inevitably increases code inspection efforts but within tolerable operational thresholds. (2) The performance of unsupervised clustering models varies depending on the data transformation techniques used, with no single clustering model showing consistent superiority. Empirical findings indicate that data transformation techniques exhibit general effectiveness in clustering-based UDP models, and the choice of an unsupervised clustering model should align with the specific data transformation technique employed.

title: Studies and Elimination of F-Induced Corrosion on Al Bondpads and Wafer Fabrication Process Improvement
authors: Younan Hua, Lois Liao, Lei Zhu, Ke Xu, Lingxiao Liu, Xiaomin Li
abstract: A case study on NSOP failure analysis is presented in this paper. Based on SEM/EDX and AES analysis results, white dot-like defects were identified on the Al bondpads, which were determined to be Al fluoride oxide caused by F -induced corrosion and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$[\text{OH}]$</tex> corrosion. These fluoriderelated defects are believed to have led to the NSOP issue at the assembly house. The source of F contamination was traced to the SMIF pods used in the wafer fabrication processes. By implementing dedicated SMIF pods for front-end and back-end processes, introducing a dry clean step prior to wafer fab-out, and enforcing strict vacuum packaging and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N}_{2}$</tex> storage protocols, the white dot-like defects were successfully eliminated. This paper investigates the failure mechanisms of F induced corrosion and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$[\text{OH}]$</tex> corrosion, providing a valuable reference for failure analysis and engineering teams in both wafer fabrication and assembly houses.

title: Analysis of Encryption Key Zeroization from System-Wide Perspective
authors: Toyofumi Sawa, Kuniyasu Suzaki
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Cryptographic keys are required not to be duplicated unnecessarily and to be zeroized from memory after use, as mandated by ISO/IEC 19790 and FIPS 140–3. Because applications run on an operating system, verifying key zeroization solely through their interactions is difficult. Although methodologies for enforcing key zeroization have been studied, there is a lack of empirical research confirming that zeroization actually occurs in practice. To address this gap, we propose a live memory forensic technique employing an FPGA to periodically detect AES keys, which we then use to investigate the key zeroization behavior of applications that use AES under Ubuntu Linux (with a FIPS-compliant kernel) on both AArch64 and x86-64, as well as on Windows 11 (x86-64). Our investigation reveals that AES instructions hold round keys in registers, but during OS context switches these register contents are copied into memory and remain unzeroized when the process terminates. Moreover, when an application terminates abnormally, insufficient signal handling prevents key zeroization, and cryptographic keys can be included in core dumps potentially leaking them externally. We further observe that, on non-FIPS-compliant kernels, keys can persist across OS reboots. These findings provide valuable information for developers following key zeroization guidelines and for cryptographic module testers. Finally, we propose and supply developers with feasible mitigation strategies to reduce key duplication and persistence at both the application and library levels.

title: False Promises of Passwordless: Defeating Windows Hello through TPM Misuses
authors: Jeongho Lee, Minkyeong Kang, Seunghwan Lee, Hyoung-Kee Choi
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Passwordless authentication is increasingly being adopted as an alternative to traditional knowledge-based authentication, primarily because of its convenience and security advantages. There is a clear lack of research geared towards the integration of passwordless authentication with host security and operating systems. We bridge that gap by uncovering critical vulnerabilities in Windows' passwordless authentication. Specifically, we identify misconfigurations where hardware-backed protection is silently bypassed due to improper TPM integration, undermining Windows' intended security guarantees. Furthermore, we show that the use of biometrics weakens data protection. We present three practical attacks that exploit these vulnerabilities. (1) “Template Injection”, bypasses biometrics by injecting forged templates into the system. (2) “Passkey Migration”, extracts and reuses passwordless credentials across devices. (3) “Phishing”, alters host authentication behavior to deceive users into exposing sensitive data. Our evaluation shows that these attacks work across diverse configurations, require no prior knowledge of the victim, and remain undetectable in practice. Among all tested cases, only systems with Enhanced Sign-in Security (ESS) prevented our attacks. Beyond Windows' native authentication, we examine third-party credential sync services, focusing on Google Password Manager (GPM). We find that GPM is vulnerable under certain misconfigurations, allowing abuse of synced credentials. Finally, we discuss the root causes of these issues and propose mitigation strategies.

title: Investigation of Cu Bonding Wire Lifetime Under Accelerated Temperature Environments
authors: Shinya Azuma, Noritoshi Araki, Motoki Eto, Takashi Yamada
abstract: The growing demand for reliable and cost-effective interconnection materials in automotive electronics has accelerated the adoption of palladium-coated copper (PCC) bonding wires as an alternative to traditional gold wires. High temperature storage life (HTSL) tests are widely conducted to evaluate the bond reliability. Elevated temperature conditions are often employed to shorten the test duration, and the lifetime is estimated based on the Arrhenius model. In this case, the apparent activation energy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E_{a a}$</tex>) has a strong influence on the accuracy of lifetime prediction. Since PCC wires exhibit superior reliability compared with bare copper (Cu) wires, their activation energy is expected to differ from that of bare Cu. In addition, the validity of the conventionally used <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E_{a a}$</tex> value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.7 \text{eV})$</tex> for the lifetime estimation of Cu wires, especially for PCC wires, is under question. In this study, HTSL tests were performed on bare Cu and PCC wires at various temperatures to evaluate bond reliability at elevated temperatures and to validate lifetime prediction based on the Arrhenius model. Bond lifetimes were determined based on the reduction in ball shear force. From Arrhenius plots, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E_{a a}$</tex> was determined as 0.64 eV for bare Cu wires and 1.24 eV for PCC wires. The value for bare Cu was comparable to the conventionally used 0.7 eV, whereas that for PCC was significantly higher. As a result, the calculated acceleration factor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A F(T)$</tex> for PCC wires based on the experimentally obtained <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E_{\text {aa }}$</tex> differed substantially from that using the conventional value. Our results indicate that applying conventional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E_{a a}$</tex> value to estimate the lifetime of PCC wires lead to underestimation. The microstructure analysis demonstrated the validity of lifetime determination in this study. In conclusion, this study provides useful parameters for the lifetime prediction of PCC wires.

title: A Software Architecture Resilience Assessment Method via Dynamic Bayesian Networks
authors: Hao Chen, Bixin Li, Chunguang Zhang, Zixuan Liu
abstract: Resilience is essential for software to maintain continuous service under unpredictable attacks and failures. However, most existing assessment methods are domain-specific and depend on post-deployment monitoring, which limits generalizability and raises secondary development costs. This paper proposes a resilience assessment method based on dynamic Bayesian networks (DBN) at the software architecture design stage. The method quantitatively evaluates resilience by modeling architectural dependencies and simulating dynamic attack and restoration scenarios, integrating both availability and restoration. Experimental validation in five architectural styles and 11 real-world systems demonstrates its effectiveness and generality. Furthermore, the method identifies key architectural factors that influence resilience, providing actionable guidance for early-stage design optimization.

title: Forecasting Hourly Electricity Consumption Using Ensemble Gradient Boosting and SHAP Insights
authors: Venkata Chandrasekhar Vinay Chanamallu, Siva Durga Sai Prasad Buthada
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Accurate hourly demand forecasts keep the grid steady and bills in check. We built a compact ensemble for India’s national Demand Met data (2017–2024) using tuned Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM), and Categorical Boosting (CatBoost) models; Optuna handled the searches and the learners were blended with learned weights. On the 2024 test slice, the ensemble cut RMSE by roughly 16% and MAE by about 20% versus a tuned LightGBM and stayed within reach of the best single model (CatBoost). Features mirror how operators think about load: calendar cycles, lagged values, weekly rolling statistics, and anomaly flags. SHAP explanations show that weekly rolling averages and lag features dominate the predictions. Error heatmaps and a handful of weekend case checks highlighted recurring Saturday deviations rather than random blips. Taken together, the tuned boosting trio and the accompanying diagnostics give an accurate, interpretable forecast that fits day-to-day grid use.

title: Research on Power Regulation of Variable-Speed Compressors in Compressed Air Energy Storage Systems Based on Dual-Modal Cooperative Control
authors: Xiankui Wen, Qiang Fan, Huayang Ye, Lingrong Pang, Shihai Zhang, Ke Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: The large-scale integration of renewable energy poses severe challenges to power grid stability, and Compressed Air Energy Storage (CAES) systems play an important role in frequency regulation and peak shaving. This paper proposes a dual-modal cooperative control method for power regulation of variable-speed compressors in CAES systems, establishing a control framework based on fuzzy logic. By real-time monitoring of system operating conditions, the method intelligently allocates control weights between frequency converters and intake valves, achieving cooperative operation of two regulation modes. This approach effectively combines the technical advantages of frequency converters fast wide-range regulation with intake valves precise small-range regulation, significantly improving the systems dynamic response performance and regulation accuracy while extending equipment service life. Simulation verification shows that compared to traditional single control strategies, the proposed method improves response time by 47.3% and regulation accuracy by 32.6%, providing important technical support for CAES system engineering applications and stable grid operation.

title: Semantic Segmentation and Phenotypic Trait Extraction of Sugarcane Plants Based on PPA-PointNet++
authors: Yan Chen, Hengyi Li, Xiangyu Lu, Fen Liao, Jinxin Chen, Xiyao Huang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Sugarcane phenotypic analysis is pivotal for monitoring crop growth and optimizing management. Conventional manual measurements are inefficient and prone to errors. With the advancement of low-cost, high-precision three-dimensional imaging technologies, point-cloud-based deep learning approaches for plant phenotyping are rapidly emerging. In this study, we propose a novel sugarcane point cloud segmentation and phenotypic extraction framework that integrates a custom PPA-PointNet++ model with the DBSCAN clustering algorithm. The proposed segmentation approach enables the extraction of sugarcane phenotypic traits, providing a robust solution for precision agriculture. In summary, we introduce a novel method for sugarcane point-cloud segmentation and organ-level phenotypic trait extraction by combining PPA-PointNet++ with DBSCAN. The PPA-PointNet++ model achieved an mIoU of 0.96 and an accuracy of 0.98, substantially enhancing semantic segmentation performance. Experimental results and validation indicate that the method attains high accuracy in extracting phenotypic traits such as plant height, stalk height, and leaf area, meeting the requirements for growth-process monitoring and high-precision phenotyping.

title: Adaptability Study of Multi-Object Tracking Algorithms in Maritime Radar Scenarios
authors: Min Zhang, Feng Ma
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Addressing the challenges posed by low Signal-toNoise Ratio, weak texture, and strong clutter interference in maritime radar imagery, this paper proposes a performance evaluation framework for object detection and Multi-Object Tracking based on YOLOv8. The study aims to systematically verify the applicability of the "Tracking-by-Detection" paradigm in maritime radar scenarios and to deeply investigate the practical value of appearance features in data association. Leveraging a self-constructed dataset of 1,816 high-precision annotated radar images, this paper first optimized and trained a YOLOv8 detector tailored to radar characteristics, establishing a high-performance detection baseline with a mAP50 of 99.42%. On this basis, a comparative evaluation was conducted on over ten mainstream tracking algorithms, including SORT, ByteTrack, and SparseTrack. In particular, addressing the lack of visual details in radar targets, this paper constructed a radar-specific Reidentification dataset and trained a multi-scale OSNet model for ablation studies.

title: Schedroid: LLM-Based Interactive Scheduling for Smart Manufacturing
authors: Lilin Xu, Jingyan Zhu, Ziyang Xiao, Jingrong Xie, Shisi Guan, Han Wu, Xiaojin Fu, WingYin Yu, Xiongwei Han, Tao Zhong, Mingxuan Yuan, Gang Chen, Dongxiang Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: This work presents Schedroid, an interactive and automated scheduling method to derive the optimal allocation of staff in production lines for smart manufacturing. Driven by natural language-based instructions, Schedroid is built upon a fine-tuned domain-specific LLM trained to decide whether and how to make scheduling changes, together with an incremental optimization modeling framework that adapts to manufacturing production needs in real-time. The mathematical model for the optimal scheduling is represented in textual form and we propose a component linking strategy to capture precise user intention and improve modeling accuracy. Finally, an explanation generator is provided to enhance system friendliness.

title: UG-NET: An Integration of U-Net and GRU for IoT Intrusion Detection
authors: Moufida Aibeche, Soufiane Boulehouache, Abdenacer Nafir, Smaine Mazouzi
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Theoretical and Applicative Aspects of Computer Science (ICTAACS)
abstract: The emergence of the Internet of Things has created a specific highly distributed platforms that connect and exchange information continuously and permanently. Their heterogeneity reflected in a variety of devices, architectures, operating systems, protocols, etc., makes them different of other networks. Therefore, well known IDSs are not appropriate for these platforms and their use makes them easy targets for cyber attackers. It means that actual Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs) are not really specific to IoT platforms and/or are not up to date with new type of attacks. Hence, the need to propose an up to date and specific IDS to protect IoT platforms is an absolute requirement. In this article, we present UG-Net, a hybrid IDS for real IoT platforms. It combines a 1D U-Net network for hierarchical feature extraction and a GRU layer for modeling sequential dependencies. Our proposed IDS is trained, tested, and evaluated on the CIC-IDS2017 and Bot-IoT datasets. The results demonstrate very encouraging performance compared to related works in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. It means that it significantly improves the intrusion detection rate and the intrusions types.

title: Sarcasm Aware Sentiment Analysis with BERT Transformer Models
authors: Samuel M, K Ramalakshmi, R Venkatesan, Agnes Chella Wanika R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Sentiment analysis plays a crucial role in understanding how opinions are shaped and expressed on social media platforms. However, detecting sarcasm remains one of the most challenging aspects of sentiment analysis, as it often requires advanced natural language processing (NLP) techniques to correctly interpret implied meanings beyond literal text. This study focuses on sentiment evaluation of Reddit comments, with a particular emphasis on sarcasm detection, by leveraging BERT and transformer-based models. Through qualitative analysis of sarcastic Reddit text, the work explores recurring patterns and linguistic cues that influence sarcasm and its impact on sentiment polarity. The methodology involves data preprocessing, embedding generation using BERT, model training, and sentiment classification through transformer architectures. By assessing how sarcasm alters polarity interpretation, the study highlights the limitations of traditional sentiment models and proposes enhancements for more robust sarcasm-aware sentiment detection. Ultimately, this research aims to improve classification accuracy, strengthen NLP frameworks, and contribute toward the development of scalable systems capable of capturing nuanced expressions in online discourse.

title: RD-Traj: Residual Denoising Diffusion Models for GPS Trajectory Generation
authors: Ming Xu, Xu Zong, Jiangtao Niu, Xianglan Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: In modern urban planning, traffic management, and location-based services, GPS trajectory data is of paramount importance. However, challenges related to privacy preservation and data acquisition constraints impede the availability of largescale, high-quality real-world data. Consequently, generating synthetic trajectory data has emerged as a critical research direction. Diffusion probabilistic models show significant promise by reconstructing trajectories from Gaussian noise, yet they face limitations in interpretability, efficiency in modeling complex trajectories, and balancing the trade-off between diversity and determinism. Inspired by Residual Denoising Diffusion Models (RDDM) [1], we propose RD-Traj (Residual Denoising Trajectory Diffusion Model). This model enhances interpretability by decoupling the diffusion process into residual diffusion and noise diffusion, which are designated to handle the deterministic structural components and stochastic perturbations of trajectories, respectively. RD-Traj introduces a residual denoising mechanism and employs independent coefficient schedules to meticulously control the diffusion rates, thereby improving the efficiency and fidelity of complex trajectory generation. Concurrently, the model inherits the intrinsic capability of diffusion models to generate data from random noise, ensuring privacy preservation. Experiments demonstrate that RD-Traj achieves state-of-theart performance and utility in generating high-fidelity, diverse trajectories.

title: Development of Pdk Library for Accurate Modeling of 2.5D Interconnect Structures in Heterogeneous Integration
authors: Raju Mani, Wu Jiaqi, Lim Teck Guan, Rahul Dutta
abstract: Accurate modeling of 2.5 D interconnect structures is essential for next-generation heterogeneous integration, where system complexity and higher signal speeds demand precise simulation workflows. This work presents the development of a comprehensive Advanced Package Process Design Kit (PDK) library, enabling predictive simulation and robust signal integrity evaluation and characterization for 2.5 D interposer-based system-inpackage (SIP) with acceptable model and fabrication correlation. The library contains parameterized cells, scalable technology files, advanced package design rules, validated electrical models, and electromagnetic extraction. The library supports diverse interposer materials and support various package design topologies. We demonstrate using a reference design workflow, the effectiveness of PDK for accelerating package co-design and verification. This library supports chiplet import, SIP physical design, and advanced digital/analog/RF integration, facilitating the design and realization of high-performance heterogeneous packages.

title: Implementation of the Use of Chatbot in Improving Consumer Services on E-Commerce Platforms
authors: Rianto Nurcahyo, Michellyn Queen Harly, Jesslyn Ivana Manopo, Beatrix Dei Cristabel, Garneta Christianto Binus, Fahm Ara Bilqis
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The growth of e-commerce in Indonesia has prompted Shopee to adopt AI technology, including chatbots. This study analyzes the influence of the use and response of AI-based chatbots on user reuse intentions. With a quantitative approach and purposive sampling techniques, data was collected through questionnaires and analyzed using PLS-SEM with SmartPLS 4.0. The results show that chatbot usage and response significantly affect social appeal and tasks, increasing user trust. This trust has proven to have a significant impact on users' intentions to return to using the service. An interactive and responsive chatbot can increase positive perceptions, both from the social aspect (ease of interaction) and functional (task efficiency). The R-square value shows that the trust variable explains 40.1 % of the variability, reuse intention 44.1 %, social interest 21.0 %, and task interest 20.2 %. These findings emphasize the importance of developing AI chatbots to improve user experience, strengthen trust, and drive loyalty on e- commerce platforms like Shopee.

title: Data Protection in Internet of Behaviors: A Review
authors: Yinan Xiao, Yi He, Yunkai Wei, Supeng Leng
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The Internet of Behaviors (IoB) has witnessed significant advancement in recent years, finding extensive and profound applications in diverse domains. Nevertheless, research on IoB remains in its early stages, lacking systematic reviews especially in the domain of data protection. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of the current status and challenges in IoB data protection. First, we analyze the evolution from traditional Internet data to IoB data across three key dimensions: data types, Internet development trends, and data ownership. Next, we examine the security and privacy risks associated with IoB user data throughout its life-cycle, including generation, transmission, dissemination, utilization, and storage. Building upon these risks, we then review existing IoB data management approaches, focusing on data organization, control mechanisms, and relevant legal/regulatory frameworks. Finally, we highlight critical challenges and propose future research directions to advance IoB data management, aiming to inspire further innovation in this emerging field.

title: Cybersecurity and Criminal Protection of DNA Fingerprinting Data in UAE Law
authors: Sultan Abdullah Al-kutbi, Mohammad Amin Alkrisheh, Khawlah M. Al-tkhayneh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Digitization of Systems and Services (IDSS)
abstract: The UAE legislator faces a critical challenge in balancing the protection of individuals' genetic fingerprint data privacy with the use of this data as an essential tool in criminal investigations. This balance ensures the practical application of genetic technologies without infringing on fundamental rights to privacy. The study aims to determine whether UAE legislation has successfully struck this balance and what legal mechanisms can prevent violations of individual rights. This research adopts an analytical legal methodology to examine Federal Decree-Law No. (39) of 2023, which regulates the collection, storage, and use of genetic fingerprint data. The study analyses the decree's provisions for privacy protection, legal safeguards, and penalties for misuse, while identifying gaps in technical security measures. The findings reveal that the decree provides a robust legal framework for genetic data governance but faces challenges in implementing cybersecurity measures and ensuring oversight mechanisms. Accordingly, the study recommends enhancing technological infrastructure, adopting international cybersecurity standards, and strengthening mechanisms for accountability and grievance handling. It further underscores the need for ongoing legal development to maintain a balance between privacy rights and law enforcement objectives.

title: Chart2Code-MoLA: Efficient Multi-Modal Code Generation via Adaptive Expert Routing
authors: Yifei Wang, Jacky Keung, Zhenyu Mao, Jingyu Zhang, Yuchen Cao
abstract: Chart-to-code generation is a critical task in automated data visualization, translating complex chart structures into executable programs. While recent Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) improve chart representation, existing approaches still struggle to achieve cross-type generalization, memory efficiency, and modular design. To address these challenges, this paper proposes C2C-MolA, a multimodal framework that synergizes Mixture of Experts (MoE) with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). The MoE component uses a complexity-aware routing mechanism with domain-specialized experts and load-balanced sparse gating, dynamically allocating inputs based on learnable structural metrics like element count and chart complexity. LoRA enables parameter-efficient updates for resource-conscious tuning, further supported by a tailored training strategy that aligns routing stability with semantic accuracy. Experiments on Chart2Code-160k show that the proposed model improves generation accuracy by up to 17%, reduces peak GPU memory by 18%, and accelerates convergence by 20%, when compared to standard fine-tuning and LoRAonly baselines, particularly on complex charts. Ablation studies validate optimal designs, such as 8 experts and rank-8 LoRA, and confirm scalability for real-world multimodal code generation.

title: Modeling and Evaluating Self-Repair in FPGA-Based UAV Embedded Systems
authors: Mohamed Sedik Chebout, Toufik Marir, Farid Mokhati
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Theoretical and Applicative Aspects of Computer Science (ICTAACS)
abstract: Ensuring resilience in Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) requires autonomous mechanisms capable of detecting, diagnosing, and recovering from both hardware and software faults during flight. This paper presents an extended mathematical framework for quantifying self-repair capability in FPGA-based embedded UAV systems. The model integrates four core self-X activities namely self-monitoring, self-diagnosing, self-configuring, and self-optimizing, and introduces additional UAV-specific resilience metrics, including Detection Latency (Ldet), Diagnosis Time (TMD), Reconfiguration Success (SRc), Quality of Recovery (QoR), and Flight Safety (SRsafe). A comprehensive dataset of 30 representative UAV faults, spanning sensor, actuator, communication, software, power, and FPGA layers, is used to evaluate the framework. Eight frequent and critical faults are selected for detailed assessment. A numerical and experimental case study focusing on a barometric sensor drift fault is conducted on a Xilinx Zynq-based UAV. The results show significant improvements in detection latency, diagnostic responsiveness, and reconfiguration time, achieving a self-repair effectiveness of 90% in the evaluated scenario. The proposed approach provides a quantitative foundation for assessing UAV resilience and supports future extensions toward real-time fault injection, cooperative resilience in multi-UAV missions, and AI-based predictive fault management.

title: Application of Deep Learning in the Optimization of $1 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$ TSV Process Development
authors: Mengyang Li, Yong Chyn Ng, Ling Xie
abstract: The patterning of high aspect ratio (HAR) ThroughSilicon Vias (TSVs) with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mu m$</tex> diameter on 12-inch wafers in backend fabrication processes challenges the resolution limits of stepper lithography tools, resulting in a constrained process window and variability in manufacturing outcomes. Conventional Critical Dimension (CD) measurements fail to reliably indicate proper TSV pattern formation, underscoring the need for more robust evaluation techniques. This work presents a novel automated defect classification approach based on a decision-tree architecture integrating three convolutional neural networks (CNNs), applied to CD-Scanning Electron Microscopy (CD-SEM) images of TSVs. By leveraging deep learning for detailed defect recognition, the method significantly enhances the consistency of TSV fabrication, reducing wafer-level CD variation to within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 20 \%$</tex> of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mu m$</tex> target. The approach offers a scalable and objective alternative to manual inspection, thereby enabling more precise process control and advancing high-density TSV integration technology.

title: An HMM-Based Behavior Analysis Approach for QoE in VR : A Case Study of QoL Assessment via Orientation and Mobility Test
authors: Yujie Huang, Alexandre Bruckert, Patrick Le Callet
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Virtual Reality (VR), as a leading form of immersive media, has rapidly expanded into various application domains, raising the need for standardized methods to evaluate its Quality of Experience (QoE). An ongoing recommendation, ITU-T P.IXC, titled "Interactive test methods for subjective assessment of XR communications", is currently under joint development by the Video Quality Experts Group Immersive Media Group (VQEG-IMG) and ITU-T Study Group 12 (SG12). One major challenge identified in this context is the insufficient attention paid to user behaviors during tasks, despite the increasing availability of behavioral data. Although such data are often collected, they are rarely analyzed in depth. In this paper, we proposed a Hidden Markov Model (HMM)-based behavior analysis approach. Using a VR-based Orientation and Mobility (O&M) test designed for Quality of Life (QoL) assessment–one of the promising applications of VR–we leveraged the collected behavioral data to model behavior patterns. Our results demonstrate that the proposed approach can effectively extract behavior-aware features, highlighting the potential of integrating behavior analysis into QoL assessment frameworks. We believe this work provides valuable insights into incorporating behavioral metrics into immersive media QoE evaluation and contributes to the development of ITU-T P.IXC.

title: Estimating Change in Postpartum Sexual Abstinence: A Survival Analysis Using Change-Point Models
authors: Shobhana Selvaraj Nadar, Vasudha Upadhyay, Savitri Joshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: This article explores the timing of postpartum sexual abstinence (PPSA) in eight countries across South East Asia, Africa and America using data from the National Demographic and Health Surveys (DHS). To identify shifts in the risk pattern of PPSA over time, we apply the exponential hazard change-point model [ECPM], the Lindley hazard change-point model [LCPM], the Weibull hazard change-point model [WCPM] and the log-logistic hazard change-point model [LLCPM] and compare its performance. The analysis reveals distinct behavioral transitions and region-specific variations in abstinence duration. Model comparisons based on goodness-of-fit criteria demonstrate the suitability of the WCPM in capturing nonlinear patterns of postpartum sexual behavior.

title: Optimizing Code Feature Extraction and Classification using Random Forest Models on Abstract Syntax Trees
authors: Saad Faheem Khan Pattan, Sreya Mynampati, Sudharson S
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The machine learning methods applied to code classification tasks that use Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) features has been explored in this study. The dataset includes parsed AST representations of more than 100,000 Python scripts, which we then applied to feature extraction. We aim to optimize Random Forest classifiers to classify code from the associated extracted features that have been created. To alleviate some of the computing overhead and train the model efficiently, we padded features, and capped the dataset to 20,000 entries. In addition, we used GridSearchCV to optimize hyperparameters by finding the best combinations of hyperparameters for the dataset. After we fine tune the framework parameters, we measured performance metrics of the model including: accuracy, precision, and recall. The final model was tuned to 50 estimators, maximum depth of 10, and minimum of 2 sample split. From our model evaluation of performance on the validation data, we had results of 90%, 89%, and 90% for accuracy, precision, and recall respectively. In addition to classification tasks we provided visual analysis and discussion using confusion matrices, precision-recall curves, and feature importances to analyze model behavior and other areas for improvement. This study highlights the challenges of using AST-based features for code classification and identifies avenues for further optimization including improvements to our feature extraction process and the selection of models.

title: Classification and Characteristics of Global Carbon Emission Patterns Based on K - Means Clustering
authors: Shuangshuang Chen
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: In the context of global climate change, classifying carbon emission patterns is crucial for implementing differentiated governance strategies. This paper utilizes the K-Means clustering algorithm to analyze carbon dioxide emission data from countries worldwide between 1990 and 2022. After data pre-processing and standardization, the optimal number of clusters (K=4) is determined via the elbow method and silhouette coefficient. The global carbon emission patterns are classified into four categories: high-emission and high-growth rate, high-emission and low-growth rate, high-emission and stable, and low-emission and high-growth rate. Corresponding emission reduction strategies are proposed for each category.

title: NOMA-Assisted Beam Hopping System Design for Airborne Platform Communication
authors: Zhongrui Tang, Xingguo Xu, Xuyang Zhang, Xinwei Yue, Caihong Gong, Peng Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: This paper investigates the performance of nonorthogonal multiple access based high orbit satellite beam hopping (NOMA-BH) airborne platform communication systems. To satisfy the time-varying and non-uniform traffic requirement, we formulate an optimization problem to maximize the achievable rate. With the intention of simplifying the problem's complexity, the original optimization problem is split to both power allocation and beam scheduling sub-problems which are solved by alternating optimization methods. The successive convex approximation method is employed for the power allocation sub-problem to solve non-convex constraints. The greedy algorithm based on equivalent channel gain is designed for the beam scheduling sub-problem to reduce computational complexity. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed NOMA-BH scheme is able to effectively tackle dynamic traffic demands and significantly improve system spectral efficiency and user quality of service. Compared with conventional schemes, the joint optimization algorithm validates the significant advantages of NOMA-BH technologies.

title: Soil Condition-Based Crop Recommendation System Using Machine Learning
authors: Ikhsan Habib, Istiqomah, Khilda Afifah
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study presents a machine learning-based crop recommendation system that integrates soil sensors and the Random Forest algorithm to analyze key soil parameters (NPK, EC, pH, temperature, and moisture). The system transmits realtime data via Bluetooth to a mobile application, enabling continuous monitoring and crop recommendation. Trained on a calibrated field dataset, the Random Forest model achieved 98.33 % test accuracy using 16 trees with the Gini criterion. The system operates reliably at 1-second intervals and can run for up to 3 hours on dual 2000 mAh batteries. Field testing yielded a 60% classification accuracy, demonstrating the system's potential as an early-stage tool for supporting precision agriculture.

title: Physical Fitness Test Assistant for Students Using Human Pose Estimation and Pose Classification
authors: Fitro Firmansyah, Suhono Harso Supangkat, Devi Willieam Anggara
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Physical fitness testing is crucial in evaluating students' physical readiness, particularly in educational institutions emphasizing discipline and physical excellence. Several fundamental exercise movements, such as push-ups, pull-ups, and sit-ups, are assessed by counting each student's maximum repetitions. These movements must be executed with proper form, as incorrectly performed repetitions are not counted by the evaluator. However, human evaluators may miscount repetitions or overlook form errors due to various factors. To address this issue, this study proposes developing a fitness test assistant system utilizing pose detection technology provided by MediaPipe, combined with a Gradient Boosting machine learning model to accurately classify different types of movements. The system can also correct form and provide real-time feedback to students, enabling it to verify the correctness of motion cycles before counting repetitions. Furthermore, both feedback and repetition counts are displayed on a screen. Consequently, this approach directly reduces the risk of injury and enhances the overall reliability of physical fitness assessments and also achieved an exercise classification accuracy of 99% based on the Gradient Boosting algorithm results.

title: IPSO: An Improved Particle Swarm Optimization-Based Method for Test Case Generation
authors: Peng Wang, Shunhui Ji, Yikun Guo, Pengcheng Zhang
abstract: Rapid advancement of DNNs has exposed significant vulnerabilities to NLP software defects, raising critical security concerns. Researchers have proposed various automated testing techniques to generate adversarial test cases. However, existing methods have two limitations: poor quality test cases and inefficiency in time. To address these issues, we propose IPSO, an automated test case generation method integrating word scoring via Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH) and adaptive probability mutation. The method firstly performs synonym substitution on the influential positions of the test case via word score selection and initializes the high-quality particle swarm. Then, by considering the fitness function of confidence, change rate and semantic perplexity, the test cases are evaluated and constrained comprehensively to improve the quality of the generated test cases. Finally, adaptive probability mutation is adopted to prevent the particle swarm from falling into local optima. Experiments on three datasets and five DNN models show that IPSO consistently has the highest success rate and generates semantically coherent test cases with at least $\mathbf{5 0. 5}$ % less time overhead than heuristic baselines. For example, in the IMDB dataset, IPSO reduces query numbers by 87.7% compared to traditional PSO.

title: Splash: Adversarial Defense with Short Perturbation Blocks Against Adversarial Training Aided Website Fingerprinting
authors: Runsheng Ma, Chengshang Hou, Gaopeng Gou, Junzheng Shi, Zhen Li, Gang Xiong
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Adversarial perturbation generation allows network users to mislead website fingerprinting (WF) classifiers without compromising real-time transmission or data integrity, causing misclassification. However, adversarial perturbations are vulnerable to adversarial training (AT), which enables attackers to improve their classifiers using perturbed adversarial samples, rendering user defenses ineffective. Due to reliance on real and non-redundant data, existing defenses against AT fail to scale to large-scale user scenarios. This paper proposes an improved adversarial perturbation generation method named Splash, which mitigates performance degradation caused by defense configuration collisions in traditional AT-aided attack defenses by applying two real-time traffic obfuscation steps using both global adversarial perturbations and Short Perturbation Blocks placed at random positions. Evaluation shows that Splash performs better traffic obfuscation than three other representative defenses, causing attacker classifiers to misclassify over 97% of traffic. In addition, it offers enhanced functionality by causing 45-60% of traffic to be misclassified into arbitrary target classes. Splash outperforms SOTA defenses such as AWA and ALERT against AT-aided attacks, reducing success rates to below 30%. Furthermore, it demonstrates significantly stronger resilience when attackers adopt the same defense configurations as users.

title: Fault-Detecting Randomized Benchmarking for Testing Quantum Processors
authors: Cynthia Kuan, Cheng-Yun Hsieh, Shan-Chi Shih, James Chien-Mo Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Test Conference in Asia (ITC-Asia)
abstract: Randomized benchmarking (RB) is widely used in the calibration process of quantum computers with the main focus on average gate fidelity. This work proposes a version with the ability of detecting user-specified faults, and successfully detected faults in simulations and real IBM quantum processors. Our proposal can evaluate error per gate (EPG) and detect faults at the same time. We have shown our FDRB successfully detect axial-tilt fault and rotation fault in both simulation and real experiments. The overhead of our proposal is the quantum native gate count increase depending on the number of faults specified by the user.

title: IoT-Enabled Smart Pill Dispenser for Medication Adherence and Remote Patient Monitoring
authors: Priyanka Kumari, Sonal Jaiswal, Amit Prabhakar, Deepti Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Medication non-adherence is a persistent challenge in healthcare, particularly among elderly and chronically ill patients. Traditional pill organizers offer limited support and lack the capability for real-time monitoring. This paper presents the design and development of an IoT-enabled smart pill dispenser that integrates sensing, actuation, and wireless communication technologies to automate medication management and support remote patient monitoring.The proposed system is built around an Arduino Mega 2560, servo-actuated dispensing mechanisms, infrared and ultrasonic sensors for dose verification, and an HC-05 Bluetooth module for communication with a mobile application. The dispenser automates pill delivery, provides real-time alerts, and notifies caregivers in case of missed doses. Experimental evaluation demonstrated a dispensing accuracy of 96.8%, an average notification latency of 2.4 seconds, and high usability for elderly users.Comparative analysis with traditional pill boxes highlights the advantages of automation, wireless sensor integration, and caregiver connectivity. The system offers a low-cost and scalable IoT solution for improving medication adherence in home healthcare. Future work includes cloud integration, AI-based adherence prediction, and interoperability with electronic health records to enable deployment in broader ICT-enabled healthcare ecosystems.

title: Simulation-Driven Analysis of AI-RAN Enabled Cooperative Content Delivery in Vehicular Networks
authors: Chenyuan Feng, Zhenyu Feng, Jiaxi Yang, Tony Q. S. Quek
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The explosive growth of data-driven applications in vehicular networks imposes stringent requirements on content dissemination in terms of latency, reliability, and scalability. Caching and cooperative distribution have emerged as promising techniques to alleviate backhaul pressure and enhance service efficiency; however, their evaluation is often constrained by over-simplified mobility assumptions and the absence of reproducible simulation environments. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a comprehensive vehicular content dissemination simulation framework that integrates SUMO for realistic traffic mobility, OMNeT++ for network simulation, and Veins for cross-layer interaction, further extended with Python/TraCI interfaces to enable fine-grained state monitoring and AI-driven decision making. We develop a system model that captures vehicular dynamics, wireless communication constraints, and cache storage limitations, and formulate the dissemination problem as a constrained optimization task that minimizes average service delay subject to storage and bandwidth budgets. Multiple dissemination strategies—including baseline caching, RSU-vehicle cooperation, and AI-RAN-driven learning-based policies—are implemented within the platform. Extensive experiments over real urban topologies demonstrate that cooperative schemes substantially improve cache hit ratio and reduce service latency, while learning-based approaches offer additional robustness under dynamic traffic conditions. The proposed framework provides a reproducible and extensible foundation for benchmarking content dissemination strategies in vehicular networks, and highlights the potential of AI-RAN to enable intelligent, adaptive vehicular communication systems.

title: Characteristics in the Quasi-Millimeter Wave Band of Planar Transmission Lines Formed on Flexible Substrates Using Direct Patterning Technology
authors: Ryoma Kimigawa, Masahiro Ito, Takeshi Ishibashi, Ayato Ueno, Haruichi Kanaya
abstract: In this study, high-frequency transmission lines were designed and evaluated on a flexible substrate using Direct Patternable Plating (DPP) technology. Coplanar waveguides fabricated on PET film exhibited stable transmission characteristics in the 20-30 GHz range, and no significant performance degradation was observed near the center frequency under bending conditions. Additionally, the capacitance was analyzed and measured when a 150 \mu m gap was introduced at the center of the signal line.

title: Fusion Networks for Advanced Block-Overlapping SMoE Image Regression
authors: Na Li, Thomas Sikora
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Our challenge is to develop efficient block-overlapping regression strategies for clean and noisy images. Block-based approaches allow massive parallel computation to achieve fast rendering - which is important for many applications. In this paper we focus on block-overlapping regression with sparse Steered-Mixture-of-Experts (SMoE) models which have shown to provide excellent results for divers applications. Block-overlapping SMoE regression provides multi-hypotheses for estimating each pixel value from clean or noisy observations - usually simple averaging of hypotheses is performed. We introduce and investigate several linear and nonlinear Filters/Networks for fusing the estimates more efficiently. On the clean Kodak image test set our best Fusion Network can improve over the "average" filter with an impressive 4.6-5.7 dB gain. For noisy observations the gain is around 1.6 dB - with denoising results in the same ball-park as BM3D.The developed strategies and results appear of more general interest beyond SMoE regression and have the potential to improve also on the plethora of other block-regression approaches such as DNN Autoencoders and kernel methods in 2D and 3D Gaussian Splatting

title: Network Attack Detection on Balanced Dataset Using Shallow and Deep Neural Networks
authors: Bilal Rafique, Xuyang Shi, Sania Kanwal, Yasir Rafique
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, modern networks face an increasing volume and variety of cyberattacks, making network intrusion detection a critical area of research. While Deep Learning (DL) techniques have shown promise in identifying sophisticated threats, challenges such as class imbalance and optimal network depth remain unresolved. This study addresses these issues by implementing a hybrid data balancing strategy that combines stratified undersampling and SMOTE-based oversampling to effectively balance the NF-UQ-NIDS-v2 dataset, which includes 21 distinct attack classes.
