Furthermore, a series of experiments is conducted using Deep Neural Network (DNN) architectures with varying depths, from DNN1 to DNN7, to evaluate the impact of model complexity on detection performance. Results demonstrate that the balanced DNN4 model achieved the highest Accuracy of 95.98%, along with superior <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 96.1%, outperforming both shallower and deeper configurations. These findings highlight the importance of integrating data balancing with carefully tuned DL architectures for robust and scalable intrusion detection in real-world network environments.

title: Research on the application of anomaly detection based on wireshark in software security testing
authors: Xian Ou, Jie Tian
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: With the continuous increase in the complexity of software systems and their reliance on networks, traditional security testing methods face limitations when addressing security risks at the protocol layer. This paper proposes a method for deeply integrating the wireshark network protocol analyzer into software security testing. By actively capturing and deeply parsing the network traffic generated during software testing, this method constructs a set of tests focusing on protocol compliance, behavioral anomalies, and potential threat patterns, providing powerful practical tools and theoretical support for building an active and deep software security protection system.

title: Energy-Efficient Maritime Communications via SIM-Equipped UAV: A Joint Optimization Approach
authors: Xia Zhong, Bin Lin, Hongyang Pan, Wenxuan Ma, Mingyang Pan
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Maritime communication networks (MCNs) are challenged by the dynamic and vast nature of maritime environments and the lack of communication infrastructures, leading to high energy consumption and limited communication performance. To address this problem, we employ stacked intelligent metasurfaceequipped uncrewed aerial vehicle (SIM-UAV) to serve MCNs. Specifically, we formulate a joint optimization problem to maximize energy efficiency (EE) in the SIM-UAV-assisted uplink MCNs, considering the optimization of SIM-UAV position, SIM phase shift, and maritime user (MU) transmit power. Due to the non-convexity of the problem, we decompose the problem into three subproblems, which are SIM-UAV position optimization subproblem, SIM phase shift optimization subproblem, and MU transmit power optimization subproblem, and then utilize an alternating optimization (AO) algorithm to solve them. Simulation results demonstrate that the AO algorithm outperforms the benchmark schemes.

title: Analysis of Significant Factors of E-Commerce Social Media Related to Customers
authors: William Elbert Adinugraha Christianto, Ahmad Nurul Fajar
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Indonesia's e-commerce sector has experienced rapid expansion, reshaping how consumers engage with digital marketplaces. Tokopedia, a dominant player in this space, has been at the forefront of this transformation. This study investigates the primary factors that drive customer loyalty within the e-commerce environment, focusing on Tokopedia as a case example. Four key variables are examined: interactivity, social media marketing, online trust, and product quality. A quantitative methodology was employed, applying Structural Equation Modeling with Partial Least Squares (SEM-PLS) to evaluate the relationships among these constructs. Data were collected from 314 Tokopedia users through an online survey targeting relevant respondents. The findings reveal that interactivity, social media marketing, online trust, and product quality each have a significant positive effect on customer loyalty. These insights provide practical guidance for e-commerce operators and digital marketers aiming to strengthen user engagement and long-term retention. Emphasizing interactive features, ensuring a secure and trustworthy online environment, implementing impactful social media campaigns, and maintaining consistent product quality can help platforms like Tokopedia retain their customer base and enhance competitiveness. This research enriches the literature on digital consumer behavior in emerging economies and underscores the evolving interplay between e-commerce service quality and customer loyalty in Indonesia's digital market.

title: Development of Temporary Bonding and Debonding (TBDB) Process for Hybrid Bonding Advance Packaging
authors: Hsiao Hsiang-Yao, Sharma Jaibir, Vasarla Nagendra Sekhar, Peng Suo, Andy Yong Chang Bum, Xue Yang Goh, Munehiro Hatai
abstract: This work investigates the applicability of three different temporary bonding/debonding (TBDB) materials for advanced hybrid bonding integration. A systematic evaluation was conducted across the key process steps, including bonding, wafer thinning, low-temperature chemical vapor deposition (CVD), debonding, and post-cleaning. Optical microscopy confirmed that all three TBDB materials exhibited clean bonding interfaces without adhesive residue or voids. During wafer thinning, all materials withstood backgrinding to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> without cracking or chipping; however, the film-type TBDB material demonstrated superior thickness uniformity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 2 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>) compared to spin-coated TBDB materials (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 5 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>). Subsequent low-temperature CVD processing (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 200^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>) showed that all bonded wafers maintained interfacial integrity under high vacuum and plasma power, with no delamination or void formation. In the debonding and post-cleaning steps, distinct differences emerged. The film-type TBDB material, debonded via UV irradiation, required no chemical cleaning and exhibited the lowest particle counts after automated optical inspection (AOI). In contrast, spin-coated TBDB materials, debonded by laser, required chemical cleaning to remove residual adhesive; while thermoplastic TBDB could be effectively cleaned, the thermosetting TBDB suffered from dicing tape erosion and dies loss, reducing yield. AOI revealed that spin-coated TBDB wafers retained > 60k particles, posing significant challenges for subsequent hybrid bonding. Overall, the results demonstrate that while all three TBDB materials are mechanically robust and compatible with bonding, thinning, and CVD processes, the film-type TBDB material offers clear advantages in thinning uniformity, post-debond cleanliness, and particle control. These findings highlight the film-type TBDB as a more suitable candidate for high-yield hybrid bonding in advanced 3D integration and semiconductor packaging applications.

title: AutoSAM: Auto-Prompting Mamba-Based Vision Foundation Model for Multimodal Remote Sensing Semantic Segmentation
authors: Jiayuan Li, Zhen Wang, Xiao Sun, Nan Xu, Zhuhong You, Deshuang Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Vision foundation models such as the Segment Anything Model (SAM) have advanced remote sensing (RS) tasks. However, extending SAM to multimodal remote sensing semantic segmentation faces two key challenges: 1) SAM is tailored for unimodal inputs and lacks RS-specific knowledge, hindering effective spatial modeling and cross-modal feature integration; 2) SAM depends on externally provided prompts (e.g., points or boxes), limiting its scalability and practicality in multimodal scenarios. To address these issues, we present AutoSAM, an end-to-end auto-prompting Mamba-based vision foundation model framework tailored for multimodal remote sensing semantic segmentation. Specifically, we introduce a CrossMamba Block (CMB) in the feature extraction stage to replace the conventional multi-head self-attention mechanism, where the core Reverse Interactive Scanning Adaptor-SS2D Module (RISASM) promotes semantic interaction and alleviates modality discrepancies. Additionally, a Multimodal Scale-Aware Fusion Module (MSAFM) is incorporated to enhance scale-aware fusion and suppress irrelevant features through cascaded residual interactions. Furthermore, we propose a plug-and-play Multimodal Mixture-of-Class-Expert Auto-Prompting Module (MMoEAPM), which enables the generation of pseudo-mask prompts for the original prompt encoder without additional training overhead, thereby supporting efficient auto-prompting. Extensive experiments and ablation studies on four benchmark multimodal remote sensing datasets demonstrate that AutoSAM consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse modality combinations. The code is available at https://github.com/NWPUFranklee/AutoSAM.git.

title: MathScrub: Single-Pipeline Strike-Out Removal and LaTeX Generation for Handwritten Calculus Expressions
authors: Sen Shen, Ning Liu, Lintao Song, Dongkun Han
abstract: With the rapid development of large language models (LLMs), image-to-LaTeX conversion has shown great potential for applications such as automated grading and mathematical expression recognition in educational and assessment settings. However, real-world handwritten STEM manuscripts often con tain strike-outs, overwrites, and other markings that disrupt handwritten content structure, reducing recognition accuracy and compilability. We present MathScrub, a single-pipeline multimodal framework that cleans and transcribes handwritten calculus expressions by grouping connected components with geometry-constrained clustering, detecting deletions via an early fusion Vision Transformer, applying geometry-aware inpainting, and generating compilable LaTeX with a parameter-efficiently adapted open-source multimodal VLM. To support this task, we introduce Math-Strike, a dataset of handwritten calculus answers with real and synthetic strike-outs, component-level annotations, and aligned LaTeX. Experiments show MathScrub outperforms open-source baselines on component detection and image-to LaTeX tasks, while remaining robust in real strike-out scenarios, providing a reliable pre-processing stage for LLM-based grading and formula recognition.

title: HiAnimal: Towards High-Fidelity and Animatable Mesh Reconstruction From Single-View In-the-Wild Animal Images
authors: Huimin Zhang, Zhongjin Luo, Kenkun Liu, Xihe Yang, Haolin Liu, Weikai Chen, Xiaoguang Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Animals are integral to our society, contributing to the richness and variety of life and providing companionship for many people. Estimating 3D shapes and poses from animal imagery is vital for studying animal behavior and creating lifelike representations for educational and virtual reality applications. Existing approaches for single-view animal reconstruction either struggle in generating 3D models well aligned with the input image or lack fine details and consistent topology. To address these issues, we propose HiAnimal, a novel framework that aims to generate high-fidelity and animatable 3D animals with strong scaling ability to novel data. HiAnimal tackles the challenge by integrating the merits of pixel-aligned implicit prior and the parametric model. Particularly, we predict the per-pixel occupancy-uv field based on an intermediate normal map representation inferred from the input image. This improves the model's ability to capture fine details and generalize across different instances. We further incorporate the parametric shape prior by aligning the SMAL model with the estimated reconstruction, ensuring consistent mesh topology and more regularized results with cleaner geometry. We achieve this by introducing a novel method that estimates a pixel-aligned UV correspondence field for precise alignment. Extensive experiments have verified our superior performance over the state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Graph-Based Anomaly APT Attack Detection via Threat Intelligence
authors: Chun-I Fan, Cheng-Han Shie, Ying-Chan Chang, Tao Ban, Tomohiro Morikawa, Takeshi Takahashi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computing
abstract: Among Advanced Persistent Threats in recent years, hackers have combined multiple defense evasion techniques to hide themselves from the detection of traditional antivirus software. For example, the combination of fileless malware and Living Off the Land techniques and abusing legitimate cloud services force the enterprises have gradually adopted the Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) instead. However, EDR has the disadvantage that this tool may produce massive false alarms. This situation force security maintainer and analysts to be burdened with a large amount of additional analyses. We proposed an anomaly detection system based on graphs. First, we input a provenance graph containing threat intelligence constructed by the normal behaviors of the system. After that, the system learns the potential structured information from the provenance graph for detecting the abnormal behavior of a host. The results show that the proposed system can effectively detect abnormal event logs. Moreover, we reduce the number of false alarms by up to 97.67%. The improvement dramatically reduces the heavy burdens on the security maintainers from the analyses of the records. Furthermore, the performance of the designed system shows that the abnormal detection based on the graph neural network is superior to a traditional neural network

title: How are trends in explainable AI redefining e-commerce? From black boxes to transparent decisions through semantic analysis
authors: Simona-Vasilica Oprea, Adela Bâra
abstract: Research interest in Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has surged since 2018, accelerating after 2020. Our research explores the role of XAI in e-commerce, emphasizing its ability to enhance transparency, interpretability and trust in AI-driven systems. To address the limited research on XAI in e-commerce, this paper conducts a bibliometric and semantic analysis of 8,845 publications from the Web of Science. It employs four advanced topic modeling techniques: Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA), BERT-LDA, BERT-Clustering and BERTopic. The research identifies XAI tools, popular XAI applications in e-commerce and emerging trends. The analysis also examines market type evolution and measures research momentum through the Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR). Keywords such as “Natural Language Processing”, “Neural Networks”, “Transformer Models” and “SHAP” exhibit the highest CAGR values, exceeding 80%. Using Hugging Face NER pipeline, we obtain that SHAP is the most frequently used tool with 2,182 mentions, followed by ICE, ALE and CEM. Extracting “market” from abstracts, the analysis reveals a significant shift in XAI research from traditional financial markets to emerging areas like energy, sustainability and environmental sciences. In e-commerce, fraud detection is the most prominent XAI application with nearly 40 mentions, followed by inventory management, price prediction, churn and demand forecasting with 15-20 mentions each. Topic 2 by BERTopic is closely related to e-commerce and focuses on the theme of recommender systems.

title: Corrosion-Resistant Cu/Graphene Nanocomposite Based Electrodes for Advanced Electrochemical Measurements_supp1-3666875.docx
authors: Ravindra Kumar Jha
abstract: TThe demand for copper-based composite materials is increasing in various sectors such as electronics, automobiles, electrochemical systems, etc. In the recent past, 2D materials such as graphene, TMDs, etc., have emerged, and incorporating into a metallic copper matrix for manifold capabilities. In the present work, the exfoliated graphene is reinforced (1-3 wt.%) with a copper matrix and prepared composite electrodes for investigating corrosion resistance, electrochemical sensing. Initially, various characterization such as XRD, Raman spectra, FESEM, HRTEM were employed for evaluating the phases, defects, morphologies, interfaces, etc. The corrosion resistance was examined in a harsh 3.5 wt.% NaCl electrolyte solution. It is observed that corrosion rates (CR) of copper-graphene composite (Cu-3.0G: CR = 0.21 ± 0.05 mpy) are significantly reduced, multiple folds than that of pure copper (CR = 3.08 ± 0.18 mpy). The graphene-embedded copper composite showed impressive electrochemical performances, e.g. cyclic voltammetry, impedance in Na2SO4 electrolyte/solvents. The composite electrode is able to differentiate the NaCl, Na2SO4 electrolytes based on the electrochemical interaction between the electrode surface and electrolytes. The insights are attributed to the infusion of graphene flakes, hydrophobic (WCA: 75.3  1.5°C) nature of the electrode surface, perhaps grow the passivation layer over the composite surface. Thus, graphene-reinforced copper composite electrodes can strongly be recommended for extreme corrosion resistance, electrochemical sensing, etc., for exploring harsh, underwater challenges.

title: Safety Guardrails for LLM-Enabled Robots
authors: Zachary Ravichandran, Alexander Robey, Vijay Kumar, George J. Pappas, Hamed Hassani
abstract: Although the integration of large language models (LLMs) into robotics has unlocked transformative capabilities, it has also introduced significant safety concerns, ranging from average-case LLM errors (e.g., hallucinations) to adversarial jailbreaking attacks, which can produce harmful robot behavior in real-world settings. Traditional robot safety approaches do not address the contextual vulnerabilities of LLMs, and current LLM safety approaches overlook the physical risks posed by robots operating in real-world environments. To ensure the safety of LLM-enabled robots, we propose RoboGuard, a two-stage guardrail architecture. RoboGuard first contextualizes pre-defined safety rules by grounding them in the robot's environment using a root-of-trust LLM. This LLM is shielded from malicious prompts and employs chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning to generate context-dependent safety specifications, such as temporal logic constraints. RoboGuard then resolves conflicts between these contextual safety specifications and potentially unsafe plans using temporal logic control synthesis, ensuring compliance while minimally violating user preferences. In simulation and real-world experiments that consider worst-case jailbreaking attacks, RoboGuard reduces the execution of unsafe plans from over 92% to below 3% without compromising performance on safe plans. We also demonstrate that RoboGuard is resource-efficient, robust against adaptive attacks, and enhanced by its root-of-trust LLM's CoT reasoning. These results demonstrate the potential of RoboGuard to mitigate the safety risks and enhance the reliability of LLM-enabled robots.

title: A Multi-Sensor Dataset for SLAM in Repetitive Scene Environments
authors: Doyeon Kim, Eunhui Han, Seokjin Kang, Seoyoon Lee, Heoncheol Lee
abstract: Accurate simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) is fundamental to autonomous navigation, as it requires both estimating the robot's motion and consistently constructing or aligning a map. However, most existing datasets are collected in feature-rich environments and do not adequately address repetitive scenes that cause perceptual aliasing, drift in odometry, and failures in loop closure. We present a new multi-sensor dataset specifically designed to evaluate SLAM performance in repetitive scene environments. It includes two representative scenarios: a riverside bikepath that exhibits frame-level repetition and an urban development district that presents block-level repetition. Additional campus sequences are provided as non-repetitive baselines. The platform integrates two 3D LiDARs, an RGB-D camera, three IMUs, and GNSS. By explicitly incorporating both frame- and block-level repetitive patterns, this dataset enables systematic analysis of perceptual aliasing in SLAM and serves as a reproducible benchmark for developing robust SLAM systems.

title: MICRORACER: Detecting Concurrency Bugs for Cloud Service Systems
authors: Zhiling Deng, Juepeng Wang, Zhuangbin Chen
abstract: Modern cloud applications delivering global services are often built on distributed systems with a microservice architecture. In such systems, end-to-end user requests traverse multiple different services and machines, exhibiting intricate interactions. Consequently, cloud service systems are vulnerable to concurrency bugs, which pose significant challenges to their reliability. Existing methods for concurrency bug detection often fall short due to their intrusive nature and inability to handle the architectural complexities of microservices. To address these limitations, we propose Microracer, a non-intrusive and automated framework for detecting concurrency bugs in such environments. By dynamically instrumenting widely-used libraries at runtime, Microracer collects detailed trace data without modifying the application code. Such data are utilized to analyze the happened-before relationship and resource access patterns of common operations within service systems. Based on this information, MicroRacer identifies suspicious concurrent operations and employs a three-stage validation process to test and confirm concurrency bugs. Experiments on open-source microservice benchmarks with replicated industrial bugs demonstrate Microracer’s effectiveness and efficiency in accurately detecting and pinpointing concurrency issues.

title: AI-Enabled Ambulance Coordination System for Emergency Healthcare
authors: Indira K.R, Gayathri G, Pon Jeyashree V J, Akshaya R, Akshaya B
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Timely ambulance coordination is critical in emergencies, where even minutes can determine survival. Existing systems face challenges such as traffic congestion, delayed hospital communication, and inefficient routing, all of which reduce the effectiveness of care. This paper proposes an AI-enabled ambulance coordination framework that integrates ambulance drivers, hospitals, and traffic control authorities through a secure mobile application. Drivers can log in, locate the nearest hospital with real-time bed availability, and receive AI-assisted shortest path navigation. The ambulance’s live location is transmitted to traffic control rooms, enabling dynamic traffic light adjustment to minimize transit delays. Simultaneously, IoT-based health monitoring devices collect patient vitals and transmit them to the selected hospital, ensuring that ICU or emergency units are prepared before arrival. By combining navigation, hospital readiness, and smart traffic systems in a unified platform, the proposed framework has the potential to reduce emergency response times by up to 30–40%, directly improving survival outcomes.

title: Joint Task Offloading and Function Deployment for LEO-Assisted MEC Networks
authors: Yiheng Zhou, Ducheng Wu, Jiong Liu, Changjiang Fei
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: This paper investigates function deployment and computation offloading in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) networks, aiming to minimize computational service latency for users with heterogeneous demands. Initially, by introducing queueing theory, the optimization problem is modeled as a mixed-integer nonlinear programming problem. Subsequently, a two-layer game-theoretic optimization framework is proposed based on potential game theory, specifically addressing the heterogeneous function deployment of satellite servers and computation offloading decision of ground users. In this framework, the function deployment of LEO satellite servers is modeled as the outer-layer sub-game, while the optimal offloading decision of ground users is formulated as the inner-layer sub-game. Finally, a joint task offloading and function deployment algorithm (JTOFD) algorithm is developed to solve this complex problem, with simulation results validating the algorithm's superior optimization performance.

title: Predicting the Risk of Developing Mental Health Disorders from Injuries with Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Philipus Adriel Tandra, Wilbert W. Ichwan, Feri Setiawan, Rizky Fajar Meirawan, Nunung Nurul Qomariyah
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Injuries-particularly long-term or permanent ones can significantly impact an individual's overall well-being, affecting both physical and mental health. While the physical consequences are often well-documented, the psychological and emotional effects remain less explored. Individuals who experience trauma may be at increased risk of developing mental health disorders such as anxiety, depression, or post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), often requiring ongoing psychological care. However, identifying which types of injuries pose the greatest risk for mental health complications remains a complex challenge. This research employs machine learning and artificial intelligence techniques to analyze the correlation between various injury types and the likelihood of developing mental health conditions. It also examines how socioeconomic factors—such as gender, education, and employment status—interact with these injuries to influence psychological outcomes. Results show that traumatic injuries like severed limbs and missing body parts are strongly associated with a higher risk of emotional and psychological disorders. Furthermore, factors such as being female, having a lower education level, or engaging in physically demanding jobs were also linked to increased vulnerability. The logistic regression model emerged as the most accurate predictive tool, achieving a performance of 69.55%, suggesting that machine learning can be a viable method for assessing mental health risk following injury. These findings highlight the importance of not only addressing the physical aftermath of trauma but also integrating mental health evaluations into post-injury care.

title: Protection Configuration Method for Pumped Storage Units Considering the Influence of Low-Frequency Current During Synchronous Startup
authors: Wanchao Liu, Xiaoyu Liu, Zhende Zhao, Jiajia Sun, Xiaoxue Meng, Zechen Tan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: During the protection configuration of pumped storage units, the synchronous oscillation of motor units during startup results in significant amplitude of low-frequency current. To address this issue, a protection configuration method is proposed that specifically considers the influence of low-frequency current during synchronous startup. A phased protection scheme is implemented for the transmission lines of pumped storage units through protection configuration and setting. This scheme ensures comprehensive protection of the pumped storage unit lines. Furthermore, based on the operational stages of motor units, two distinct setting methods are adopted to achieve overcurrent protection for pumped storage units, thereby reducing the risk of cascading failures in the transmission lines.

title: Thermo-Mechanical Coupling Simulation Study of the Fatigue and Fracture Behavior of Cu Traces and Cu/PI Interfaces in 2.5D RDL Interposer Package for Heterogenous Integration
authors: Jia-Ao Chen, Ming-Sheng Luo, Guang-Chao Lyu, Xin-Ping Zhang
abstract: This study performs a comparative investigation of the Cu traces fracture and Cu/polyimide (PI) interface delamination behavior within redistribution layers (RDLs) of 2.5D RDL interposer package during power cycling (PC) and thermal cycling (TC), by means of finite element simulation using virtual crack closure technique (VCCT). The warpage behavior of RDL interposer package with different sizes is characterized. The fracture risk of Cu traces is evaluated by von Mises stress and accumulation of equivalent plastic strain, and the influence of the ratio of line width to space (L/S) is also explored. Additionally, the strain energy release rate (SERR) and its range (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta G$</tex>) of the Cu/PI interface crack during cycling processes are also calculated to evaluate interfacial crack propagation behavior. The results indicate that the warpage of RDL interposer package increases significantly with the size, rising more under TC than PC. Notably, high warpage persists throughout the PC process due to uneven temperature distribution. RDL traces near the EMC/HBM junction experience an elevated stress due to chiplet heat under PC. SERR of Cu/PI interface crack changes significantly near the EMC/HBM junction under PC. The interface fatigue crack tends to propagate rapidly to the junction and then slow down.

title: Improving the Strategic Marketing for Coffee App Through AI Technology: Analysis Indonesian Customer Loyalty Using SmartPLS
authors: Zoyabelle Ratu Arbian, Rajiv Dharma Mangruwa
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The growth of coffeeshop in Indonesia has accelerated the adoption of AI technology as a strategic tool for enhancing customer engagement. Loyalty program has integrated with personalization technologies playing a critical role in sustaining customer retention. The objective of study is to investigate the mediating role of program loyalty in the relationship between reward programs, value perception of loyalty programs, and customer loyalty, within the operational context of an AI-enabled coffee application. Grounded in Transactional Utility Theory, the research will be determined the leverage of reward programs influence perceived value, and the value impacts both program loyalty and brand loyalty. A quantitative method was applied using purposive sampling to survey 150 customers of the coffee mobile application. Data analysis was conducted through Structural Equation Modeling–Partial Least Squares (SEM–PLS). The results proof that rewards programs significantly enhance value perception, which directly and indirectly leverage the customer loyalty through program loyalty. The study extends loyalty program literature by contextualizing it in an AI-enabled service environment and provides managerial implications for designing programs that integrate perceived fairness, operational efficiency, and personalization to strengthen customer retention in competitive service industries.

title:
Enhancing AI-Driven Digital Twin Platform for Optimizing Urban CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Capture and Climate Resilience
authors: Anitha K, Sabarna T, Santhiya P, Shriga V S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The proposal is to develop a digital twin platform at three sites with real-time data by leveraging IoT, data analytics, and web-based visualization. Air quality parameters such as CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration, temperature, and humidity are continuously monitored by sensors connected to an ESP32 microcontroller. Data is sent to a backend written in Python, where everything is stored and processed using MongoDB. Data is also obtained from Open Weather and Google Maps traffic APIs. A digital twin of the city is created to model carbon dispersion and analyze pollution under different environmental conditions. The data is processed through XGBoost to help in predicting future CO2 levels and identifying areas of the highest risk. An improvement method is employed to recommend the most efficient location of carboncapture devices to facilitate air quality with a limited budget. Besides this, the blockchain network custom-made for this purpose stores all the data collected and the predictions made. This architecture ensures secure storage along with transparency. The resulting web dashboard visualizes pollution trends, real-time sensor readings, and the potential impacts of proposed interventions. The system presents a realistic, data-driven approach to the problem of urban air pollution, which in turn, could be considered as a tool for smart cities sustainable development.

title: DICN: A Deep Interaction and Adaptive Weighting Network for Click-Through Rate Prediction
authors: Jiaman Ding, Zhaohui Chen, Shaoyu Zhu, Shaojie Qiao, Lianyin Jia, Hongbin Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Click-Through Rate (CTR) prediction is critical in recommender systems and online advertising. While mainstream models boost performance through feature interaction learning, they often overlook real-world semantic relationships between interaction features. Effectively capturing fine-grained highorder interactions and differentiating feature importance thus becomes crucial. To bridge this gap, we propose DICN-a deep interaction and adaptive weighting network. Its core innovations include: (1) A hierarchical interaction structure generating granular high-order representations via progressive feature splicing/compression; (2) Dual-perspective feature weighting using a Squeeze-and-Excitation network to dynamically model feature heterogeneity, suppressing noise from global and local views. Experiments on three benchmarks demonstrate DICN's superior accuracy and efficiency over state-of-the-art methods.

title: Research on Infrared Small Target Detection Based on Dynamic Convolution and Optimized Segmentation Loss Using MTMLNet+
authors: Yuhao Luo, Hyunbo He, Li Yiyi, Lai Wei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Infrared small target detection plays an irreplacea-ble role in key fields such as national defense, security, and mari-time security. However, core targets (such as long-range aircraft, micro-UAVs, and weak heat sources) exhibit significant “dynamic differences,” which directly leads to the insufficient adaptability of traditional fixed-parameter convolutions. For example, the fixed structure design of traditional methods (including existing deep learning models such as MTMLNet) has obvious bottlenecks and cannot meet the requirements of high accuracy and high ro-bustness. In addition, it also suffers from weak feature represen-tation, low semantic segmentation accuracy, and poor coordination of multi-task losses. This paper proposes MTMLNet+, which makes three key improvements on the basis of the original MTMLNet: 1) Introducing a dynamic convolution (DynamicConv) module, which enhances the ability to capture fine-grained features of small targets by adaptively adjusting the convolution ker-nel weights according to the input; 2) Optimizing the semantic seg-mentation loss (SegmentationLosses), combining dynamic cross-entropy and shape-aware SIOU loss to balance class imbalance and boundary localization accuracy; 3) Designing a dual-branch loss (LossDual) module to realize dynamic weight allocation be-tween detection and segmentation tasks, enhancing the multi-task mutual learning effect. Experiments on the IRSTD-1k and SIRST-V2 datasets show that MTMLNet+ outperforms the original version in terms of mAP50. The results show that the proposed method improves performance in terms of mAP50-95 and mAP50-95 while maintaining a real-time inference speed of 45.2 FPS, which verifies the effectiveness and engineering practicality of the improved scheme.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Power Flow Control in Smart Grids
authors: Ibanibo T. Sotonye, Lawrence E. Ekeng, Iyoloma I. Collins, Obasi-sam Ojah Ojobe
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources and distributed generation in modern smart grids has intensified the need for adaptive and efficient power flow control mechanisms. This paper presents a reinforcement learning (RL)-based framework for dynamic power flow management in smart grids, aimed at enhancing system stability, minimising transmission losses, and improving energy distribution efficiency. The proposed approach employs a Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent that continuously learns optimal control strategies through interaction with a simulated smart grid environment. The environment model incorporates real-time load variations, renewable generation uncertainties, and network constraints, enabling the agent to make informed decisions on power dispatch, voltage regulation, and load balancing. Simulation results demonstrate that the RL-based method achieves up to 15–20% reduction in line losses and improves voltage profile stability by over 10% compared to conventional optimal power flow (OPF) techniques. Furthermore, the trained agent exhibits strong adaptability to sudden load changes and renewable generation fluctuations without the need for explicit reprogramming. These findings highlight the potential of reinforcement learning as a robust and scalable solution for intelligent, real-time power flow control in next-generation smart grids.

title: Research on an Intelligent Invigilation and Real-Time Guidance Method and System for Relay Protection Remote Training
authors: Jiasen Zhang, Kun Wang, Weixing Pu, Wei Tang, Jianfeng Lu, Suisheng Zheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of traditional relay protection training, such as limited site space, high manual invigilation cost, delayed operation monitoring, and insufficient matching of guidance resources, this paper proposes an intelligent invigilation and real-time guidance method and system for relay protection remote training. This method constructs a “expert-trainee-equipment” tripartite interactive space, and realizes the full-process management and control of operational standard monitoring, principle verification, and shortcoming identification based on a behavior feature database containing over 200 typical relay protection operations and a “sampling-judgment-action” logical knowledge graph. Innovatively, a temporal similarity matching model based on the improved Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) algorithm and a three-level early warning mechanism with dynamic threshold adjustment are designed to solve the problems of missed detection and misjudgment of operational violations; a deviation calculation model for logical consistency verification and a multimodal collaborative interaction mechanism are proposed to break through the difficulties of weak principle association and poor interaction in real-time guidance; combined with a dynamic iteration algorithm for competency profiling and a virtual-real integrated evaluation system, a closed loop of “monitoring-error correction-guidance-evaluation-optimization” is formed. Practice shows that the system achieves an operational violation identification response time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant$</tex> 500ms, an error correction accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geqslant$</tex> 95%, and a guidance resource matching rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geqslant$</tex> 85%, accelerating the improvement rate of trainees' shortcomings by 40%, which provides technical support for the standardization and precision of relay protection remote training.

title: Power Coordination Control Strategy for Multi-Terminal Flexible Interconnected Distribution Systems
authors: Qingsheng Li, Yuanfeng Wang, Enwei Wang, Wen Yan, Xingjian Gu, Shuai Zhao, Yushi Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: To address the increasing complexity of power flow and stability issues in distribution networks with high renewable penetration, this paper proposes a power coordination control strategy for multi-terminal flexible interconnected distribution systems (MT-FIDS). Based on system operating conditions, three main states—normal interconnection, power transfer, and emergency—are defined and further refined into eight operating modes. A quantitative power model is developed using the critical redundant power index <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P_{\text {net }}$</tex>, enabling mode identification and adaptive coordination among AC, PV, and energy-storage ports. The control framework integrates virtual synchronous generator control, MPPT-based PV regulation, and SOC-constrained bidirectional battery management to achieve real-time multi-port energy balance. Simulation results in MATLAB/Simulink verify that the proposed method effectively maintains DC-bus voltage stability around 750 V, ensures continuous power supply to critical loads, and improves the reliability and economic performance of flexible interconnected distribution systems.

title: Classification of Satellite Data Applying Rayleigh Distribution Modeling
authors: Igor Prokopenko, Sofiia Alpert, Maksym Alpert, Anastasiia Dmytruk
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: This study proposes a method of satellite image classification based on the Maximum likelihood method and the Rayleigh distribution. It was considered the methodology, including data analysis in Microsoft Excel and data processing applying the ESA SNAP (European Space Agency’s Sentinel Application Platform) software. It was processed and classified a Sentinel-2 satellite image of a part of the Kyiv region, which contains vegetated land (fields, forests), water (rivers or lakes) and buildings. Three visible spectral bands were applied, such as Red, Blue and Green spectral bands. This classification method is based on modeling spectral distributions with the Rayleigh probability distribution. It was noted that the Rayleigh distribution parameters for each class-band combination are evaluated from training data via the proposed formula. The Maximum likelihood method was applied for the classification procedure. It is based on assigning each pixel to the class for that has the Maximum probability of belonging. This method is more accurate than other well-known supervised classification methods when classes are well-separated and normally distributed. It was noted that the normality assumption may not always hold, especially for non-negative and uncertain reflectance data. In such cases, the observed amplitudes of pixel signals follow a Rayleigh distribution. It was proposed that the mathematical algorithm of calculation of likelihood functions for each class-band combination, applying a Rayleigh distribution. Based on the criterion of the Maximum log-likelihood function, we look for the Maximum value among the log-likelihood functions for all classes. Depending on which value of the log-likelihood function for which class is maximized, the test sample will be assigned to the same class. The proposed classification approach can be applied for forestry, ecological monitoring, agriculture, climate studies and disaster monitoring.

title: PIFNet: Intention Fusion with Transformers for Cooperative Driving
authors: Zhenzhen Pan, Lu Hou, Jie Meit
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: In cooperative driving, message-sharing based multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) remains a significant challenge under constraint wireless communication in vehicular networks. To address this, we propose a Transformer network with specific-designed [CLS] token, which is referred as PIFNet (Past-Imagined Fusion Network) and leverages self-attention to jointly model historical and future driving trajectories, enabling compact representation and consistent intention sharing, thereby improving cooperative efficiency for autonomous driving. To further enhance the robustness and expressiveness of communication, we design a message generation network that integrates an Attention Module (AM) and a Past-Imagined Fusion Transformer (PIFT). The proposed method is evaluated in an signal-free intersection scenario under both ideal and non-ideal wireless communication conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed approach outperforms existing multi-agent cooperative driving strategies in terms of coordination efficiency and stability, and maintains strong robustness under deteriorate communication, demonstrating broad potential for real-world cooperative driving applications.

title: Lossless Coding Improvement beyond AV1
authors: Tianqi Liu, Liang Zhao, Madhu Peringassery Krishnan, Shan Liu, Minhao Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Lossless compression plays an important role in the storage and transmission of data with stringent quality requirements. There is a substantial demand for enhancing the lossless compression performance of the current AV1 codec. In this paper, two novel techniques, named Residual Block Refinement (RBR) mode and Multi-Residual Blocks (MRB) mode, are introduced to improve the lossless coding performance beyond AV1. For the RBR mode, the main idea is to perform a lossless block refinement within the residual block to further reduce redundancy. For the MRB mode, the first partial residual block utilizes a traditional transform and quantization process to generate a lossy representation of the original residual samples with efficient energy compaction. The second partial residual block is further coded to achieve a perfect representation of the difference between the original residual block and the reconstructed first residual block. The experimental results reveal that an average coding performance -2.76 %, -1.04 %, and -1.18 % are achieved on top of the AOMedia Video Model (AVM) v6.0.0 in terms of Bitrate savings for allIntra (AI), Random Access (RA), and Low Delay (LD) configurations, respectively.

title: Performance Analysis of Clearstream-Based RFID System for Hospital Asset Tracking via Simulation Modeling
authors: Ahmad Sabry Mohamad, Fatin Affiqah Mat Jah, Muhammad Noor Nordin
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Efficient hospital asset management is critical for ensuring smooth clinical operations, minimizing equipment loss, and enhancing patient care. This study presents the design and simulation of a hospital asset tracking system using Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology. A virtual hospital environment was created in ClearStream RFID software, incorporating departments such as triage, treatment, ambulatory care, reception, and consultation rooms. RFID tags and readers were modeled to enable real-time tracking and data collection without physical deployment. Simulation testing, including concurrent asset movements, achieved a 100% tag detection rate across all zones, demonstrating the system’s stability, responsiveness, and accuracy. In parallel, a staff survey evaluated awareness, current practices, and readiness for RFID adoption. Findings revealed that 88% of respondents frequently faced difficulty locating equipment, while 84% expressed willingness to adopt RFID solutions. Moreover, 88% agreed that improved tracking could reduce procedural errors and improve patient outcomes. By combining simulation results with user feedback, this study provides a comprehensive assessment of the feasibility, acceptability, and performance of RFID-based hospital asset tracking. The findings highlight RFID’s potential to advance hospitals from manual asset management toward intelligent, automated systems, with recommendations for scalability, staff training, and practical deployment.

title: Dual-Modal Biometric Authentication Using rPPG and Pupilometry
authors: Anirudh S, Afreen Samiullah, Thejesh B K, Manigandan Muniraj, Shoba S
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel multi-modal biometric authentication system that leverages remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) to extract individual heart rate signals and simultaneously measures pupil diameter, both using standard non-contact RGB cameras. The approach exploits unique physiological and involuntary traits: rPPG captures subtle color changes from facial skin reflecting cardiovascular rhythms, while pupil dilation is monitored as an involuntary ocular response. Facial and eye landmarks guide region-of-interest (ROI) selection for signal extraction. Fusion of these two biometric modalities enhances accuracy, robustness, spoof resistance, and user convenience. Experimental results (hypothetical for this work) indicate that multi-biometric fusion using rPPG and pupillometry achieves superior authentication performance compared to single-modality systems.

title: Affine Projection Algorithm for Transform Domain Adaptive Filter
authors: Mingze Ma, Kangkang Wang, Yang Feng, HuiTing Wang, Bin Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: In response to the unstable performance of the transform domain adaptive filtering algorithm in a non-Gaussian noise environment, affine projection algorithm for transform domain adaptive filter is proposed. This innovative approach is based on the transform-domain adaptive filtering algorithm, which constructs the transform-domain input vectors into transform-domain input matrices by the affine projection mechanism. This enables the efficient utilization of past time input signals to obtain a fast convergence rate while ensuring the advantages of the transform domain algorithm. In addition, the steady state performance of the transform domain affine projection algorithm is verified.

title: Evaluating Dummy Die Sizes and Compound Adjustments to Reduce Wafer Warpage in FOEB-T Packaging
authors: Tzu-Chi Zeng, Vito Lin, Andrew Kang, Yu-PO Wang, Don Son Jiang
abstract: Wafer warpage remains a criterial issue in advanced FO-EB-T (Fan-Out Embedded Bridge with TSV) packaging, impacting die-to-wafer bonding accuracy and overall manufacturing yield. This study systematically investigates the influence of dummy die size and compound properties on phenomenon of wafer warpage, employing a combination of finite-element modeling and comprehensive experimental data to derive meaningful insights. Three distinct dummy die sizes were rigorously tested across various compound types, with warpage measured at each major step to assess its effect on the overall assembly. The Simulation results demonstrated that reducing the dummy die size leads to a significant reduction in wafer warpage, thereby enabling the packaging process to meet criteria at the critical C4 step. Furthermore, the compound studies indicated that enhancing the coefficient of thermal expansion (CTE) of the molding compound proved to be a more effective for warpage reduction compared to merely increasing the modulus of the materials used. The utilization of high CTE compound, in conjunction with the implementation of small dummy dies, produced the most favorable outcomes, with wafer warpage measurements comfortably within the specified limits. In addition to these findings, strategies were meticulously evaluated to address the prevalence of defective RDL (Redistribution Layer) areas, which can randomly affect up to 10% of the wafer. The simulation data indicated that attaching a large dummy die to the affected areas achieves comparable warpage control to the standard configuration, thus avoiding unnecessary wastage of functional dies and maintaining the reliability of overall manufacturing process. Overall, the results highlight the importance of optimizing dummy die size and the CTE compound used, while also employing intelligent die allocation strategies for defective RDL areas. These combined approaches can effectively minimize wafer warpage and significantly enhance yield in FOEB-T packaging processes, contributing to more efficient and reliable semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Spatiotemporal BEV Feature Enhancement for Multi-Target Multi-Camera Tracking
authors: Duanjiao Li, Ying Zhang, Yun Chen, Juanwen Yao, Ziran Jia, Chao Yang, Ning Ding, Xufang Pang, Jianguo Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: This paper proposes a fusion network based on spatiotemporal BEV feature enhancement, aiming to strengthen the representation capability of spatiotemporal BEV information in global contexts. The network employs a spatial BEV feature enhancement module to deeply integrate individual BEV features with the initial global BEV features obtained from the stacking and aggregation of all BEV features, thereby generating guided BEV features with enhanced representational capabilities. These features are then re-stacked and aggregated to form a refined global BEV feature. Additionally, a temporal BEV feature enhancement module is introduced to fuse historical and current BEV features, expanding the receptive field to address the association challenges posed by rapidly moving targets. Experimental results demonstrated that this enhanced architecture achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Wildtrack and MultiviewX datasets, with MODA reaching 92.3% and 96.5%, and IDF1 scores reaching 95.0% and 84.8%, respectively. The results validated the effectiveness of our method from multiple perspectives.

title: Supercapacitor Integration in Hybrid Energy Storage Systems for Electric Vehicles: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Gowtham M, Suresh S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: This review paper presents a comprehensive analysis of supercapacitor integration in hybrid energy storage systems (HESS) for electric vehicles (EVs). The integration of supercapacitors with lithium-ion batteries addresses the limitations of individual technologies by combining high energy density batteries with high power density supercapacitors. This review examines various HESS topologies, control strategies, thermal management approaches, and performance optimization techniques. Key findings indicate that HESS can achieve up to 40% improvement in battery cycle life, 60% increase in power delivery capability, and 25% reduction in lifecycle costs compared to battery-only systems. The paper analyzes bidirectional DC-DC converter topologies, energy management concepts, and emerging applications in electric vehicle powertrains. Current challenges including cost optimization, thermal management, and control complexity are discussed alongside future research directions. The review concludes that HESS technology represents a mature and commercially viable solution for next-generation electric vehicles, offering significant improvements in performance, efficiency, and sustainability.

title: Experimental Study on Corona Inception Characteristics of Aviation Cables Under Low-Pressure and Temperature-Coupled Conditions
authors: Hongxu Zhao, Xilong Li, Qidong Yan, Xudong Shi, Dongmei Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of aircraft electrification, onboard electrical systems are evolving toward megawatt-level power, significantly increasing the risk of corona discharge under low-pressure conditions. In this study, corona discharge experiments were conducted on aluminum alloy aviation cables at a pressure of 18.7 kPa under various temperatures ranging from −55 ° C to 140 ° C. The results show that as temperature increases, the corona inception voltage decreases by 1 0. 6% - 2 7. 3%. The three-phase configuration exhibits better corona resistance than the single-phase configuration, improving by approximately 7. 7% - 1 3. 4%, and cables with larger conductor cross-sections demonstrate superior corona resistance.

title: Measuring E-Customer Loyalty as an Implementation of E-Commerce for Home Décor and Furniture in Jabodetabek
authors: Rianto Nurcahyo, Nathania Wiranda, Bagus Bagas Priyadi, Damar Aji Irawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This research aims to determine the influence of E-Service Quality, E-Trust, and E-Customer Experience, on E-Customer Loyalty on the e-commerce of home furnishing retail PT XYZ Indonesia. The method used is quantitative - associative with data collection conducted by distributing questionnaires to several online customers of PT XYZ in the Jabodetabek area, totaling 250 people. Data analysis in this study uses multiple linear regression with SPSS version 30. The results obtained from this research are that E-Service Quality does not have a significant influence, while E-Trust and E-Customer Experience have a significant influence on E- Customer Loyalty partially. Simultaneously, E-Service Quality, E-Trust, and E-Customer Experience have a significant influence on E-Customer Loyalty in the E-commerce Home Furnishing of a multinational company. The result suggests the company needs to monitor service quality levels to enhance customer satisfaction and the company should also focus on how e-commerce platform can provide a robust electronic security system, ensuring customers feel safe when transacting on the app or website.

title: YOLO-Based Packaging Air Leakage Method on K210 Platform
authors: ShiJie Lin, Qi Zeng, JunYing Zeng, MengYao Ou, KeYi Huang, SiQi Zhang, ZhongQian Luo, XinBang Liao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: Leakage of noble gas in packaging poses a major safety hazard, especially in the food industry. Traditional manual detection is inefficient, costly, and prone to missed or false judgments due to visual fatigue. To address this, this study proposes a YOLO-based packaging leakage recognition method. Hardware experiments were conducted on the K210 platform: mooncake packaging images were captured and preprocessed, and the proposed YOLO model was trained to recognize color changes of desiccant oxygen indicator strips in the packaging, thereby judging air leakage. Experimental results show the method' s accuracy reaches 95.4%, significantly outperforming manual detection and reducing visual fatigue-induced errors. With high cost-effectiveness, lightweight design, and high detection rate, it provides an innovative, efficient solution for food packaging quality inspection and has broad application prospects.

title: Conanj: Confidential Data Analysis for Confidential Computing of Java Programs
authors: Xinyuan Miao, Yuting Chen, Ziyi Lin
abstract: Confidential computing is a computing technology guaranteeing security of applications at runtime—it provides TEE (Trusted Execution Environment), which is hardware-based security isolation, for executing programs and protecting confidential data. Existing Java confidential computing techniques require engineers to annotate confidential Java methods in a Java application, followed by partitioning the application into confidential and non-confidential code running on the TEE and the REE (Rich Execution Environment) sides, respectively. Meanwhile, end-users/programmers may mistakenly annotate confidential methods, and thus confidential data may reside on the REE side, incurring vulnerabilities. We propose Conanj, a novel approach to confidential computing of Java applications. The core idea is a confidential data analysis technique, where confidential data is explicitly annotated, followed by propagation analysis and separate compilation. This enables code partitioning that ensures all accesses to, propagations of, and computations over confidential data are confined to the TEE side. We implement Conanj and evaluate it against Lejacon, a state-of-the-art technique for Java confidential computing, using a set of benchmark programs. The results show that Conanj provides a cost-effective approach to Java confidential computing: The programmer spends an affordable time/effort on annotating confidential code/data; the application code deployed on the TEE side, as part of the Trusted Computing Base (TCB), is smaller than that obtained by Lejacon, with method closures reduced by $\mathbf{1. 3 4 \%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 9. 6 \%}$ across the benchmarks.

title: AlignGS: Aligning Geometry and Semantics for Robust Indoor Reconstruction from Sparse Views
authors: Yijie Gao, Houqiang Zhong, Tianchi Zhu, Zhengxue Cheng, Qiang Hu, Li Song
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: The demand for semantically rich 3D models of indoor scenes is rapidly growing, driven by applications in augmented reality, virtual reality, and robotics. However, creating them from sparse views remains a challenge due to geometric ambiguity. Existing methods often treat semantics as a passive feature painted on an already-formed, and potentially flawed, geometry. We posit that for robust sparse-view reconstruction, semantic understanding instead be an active, guiding force. This paper introduces AlignGS, a novel framework that actualizes this vision by pioneering a synergistic, end-to-end optimization of geometry and semantics. Our method distills rich priors from 2D foundation models and uses them to directly regularize the 3D representation through a set of novel semantic-to-geometry guidance mechanisms, including depth consistency and multifaceted normal regularization. Extensive evaluations on standard benchmarks demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art results in novel view synthesis and produces reconstructions with superior geometric accuracy. The results validate that leveraging semantic priors as a geometric regularizer leads to more coherent and complete 3D models from limited input views. Our code is avaliable at https://github.com/MediaX-SJTU/AlignGS.

title: Low-Complexity Beamforming and Antenna Positioning in RIS-Aided Movable Antenna Systems
authors: Huanle Li, Jing Gao, Yanlin Liu, Jiarui Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) and Movable Antenna (MA) are key technologies for enhancing wireless communication performance. RIS leverages short wavelength benefits for widespread application in mmWave urban traffic systems, while MA optimizes antenna positions to achieve higher spatial diversity gain. This study enhances MA deployment at the user end in RIS-aided systems by formulating a Signal-toNoise Ratio (SNR) maximization problem, transforming it into an alternating optimization framework that employs Fixed-Point Iteration (FPI) and gradient-based search to solve the RIS-phase shift and antenna positioning subproblems, respectively.

title: Objective Assisted Analysis of TCM Constitutions Based on Image Processing and Thermal Feature Extraction
authors: Jingyan Wang, Jiawei Chen, Yanze Zhao, Baoan Song
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: TCM constitution identification is pivotal for personalized preventive healthcare ("Zhi-Wei-Bing"). However, traditional inspection remains limited by practitioner subjectivity, necessitating objective quantitative metrics. This study proposes an infrared thermography-based facial feature extraction method for objective constitution analysis. Leveraging data from 96 subjects (standardized scales and infrared radiation maps), a processing pipeline for ROI-based thermal field modelling was established. Key evaluation metrics, including average grayscale discrepancy and thermal asymmetry, were derived to quantify inter-constitutive thermal variations. Furthermore, a statistical model based on standardized relative temperature deviations was developed to map correlation rules for six typical constitutions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively captures facial thermal distribution patterns, providing a robust engineering pathway for intelligent, non-contact, and visualized TCM diagnostic support.

title: 2.5D PIC Photonic Interposer Engine for Next Generation Photonic Link CPO of High-Performance Computing and Data Communications
authors: Ting-Ta Chi, Feng Xu, Xiangyu Wang, Zhenyu Li, Wen Lee, Xiao Jun Yuan
abstract: The demand of HPC (High-Performance Computing) and Data Centre with Photonic Link (Photonic Connectivity Link) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 \mathrm{G} /$</tex> Lane capability are growing to fulfill next generation of data transferring loading requirements. This work aims at creating a PE (Photonic Engine) with CPO (Co-Packaged-Optics) architecture capable of integrating with multiple electronic integrated circuits (EICs), photonic integrated circuits (PIC), on-chip laser sources, fiber array unit (FAU), and TSV Interposer, to enable Photonic Link to be integrated between multiple 2.5D CoWoS (Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate) package HPC engine

title:
SYNCH
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    -Cal: A Synthetic Framework for Benchmarking Multi-View Calibration and Point Cloud Registration
authors: Theofilos Tsoris, Andriani Stamou, Efthymios Koukoulis, Serovpe Amprachamian, Dimitris Zarpalas, Konstantinos Moustakas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: The problem of multi-view spatial calibration remains challenging, particularly in scenarios characterized by minimal overlapping regions across sensor views, a configuration for which robust solutions are not yet fully established. To systematically address this issue, we introduce SYNCH<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-Cal, an evaluation framework designed to generate synthetic scenes containing fiducial and pattern-based calibration targets, alongside configurable camera viewpoints with precisely known spatial calibration parameters. Utilizing these synthetic datasets, we systematically evaluated several established methods for multi-view spatial calibration. Additionally, we extended the framework to incorporate the generation of 3D point clouds from the synthetic scenes, thereby enabling a rigorous assessment of various state-of-the-art point cloud registration algorithms, including traditional ICP-based and learned methods. The results obtained establish a valuable benchmark for the comparative evaluation of both multi-view calibration methods and 3D point cloud registration algorithms. Furthermore, they provide a foundation to guide future improvements of existing techniques as well as the development of next-generation algorithms.

title: Underwater Remote-Operated Vehicle Based on Optical-Acoustic Cooperative Communication
authors: Weicheng Yue, Fangjiong Chen, Zhicheng Zeng, Hua Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Underwater wireless communication technology, featuring high flexibility and low cost, has been widely applied in fields such as underwater environment monitoring and resource development. To enhance the stability and reliability of under-water wireless communication systems, this paper proposes an underwater remote-operated vehicle based on optical-acoustic cooperative communication. This system achieves high data-rate transmission of a large amount of data through underwater visible light communication. The system also uses underwater acoustic communication to transmit low data-rate control signals. Under-water acoustic links can assist in aligning optical communication links and provide feedback on packet loss. Therefore, the system can achieve error control and correction in optical communication transmission. The test was conducted in a water pool in the laboratory. When the underwater remote-operated vehicle was approximately 5 meters away from the static node, reliable visible light communication was achieved, with a transmission rate of up to 6 Mbps, meeting the requirements for transmitting large volumes of data and images.

title: Phase-Modulated Waveform Design for Stimulated Brillouin Scattering Mitigation
authors: Kangli Zhu, Mingqian Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: In a high-power laser amplification system, the laser is affected by various nonlinear effects in the optical fiber, among which the stimulated Brillouin scattering (SBS) effect, due to its low threshold, becomes the first major obstacle to achieving highpower output. Therefore, research on suppressing the SBS effect has garnered significant attention from scholars. This paper combines the piecewise parabolic signal and the tunable inversion probability sequence (p-tunable sequence) to design a new signal waveform for seed laser phase modulation. Through numerical simulation verification, our designed signal can broaden the spectral effective bandwidth of the seed laser after phase modulation, and the power spectral density is more concentrated within the effective bandwidth. This aligns with our desired goals of laser linewidth broadening and a flatter spectrum. Finally, in subsequent SBS threshold simulation experiments, using the three-wave coupling equation simulation, this paper further verifies that the designed waveform signal has a higher SBS threshold compared to the piecewise parabolic signal.

title: Ensuring the Availability and Integrity of Navigation Data Under Conditions of Active Interference with Satellite Navigation Systems
authors: Valeriian Shvets, Volodymyr Kharchenko, Oleksandr Kutsenko
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Ensuring the availability and integrity of navigation information in global navigation satellite systems (GNSS) under conditions of active interference is a critical challenge, especially for applications in critical infrastructure. Intentional jamming and spoofing significantly reduce the reliability of navigation signals. Spatial filtering, implemented through adaptive antenna arrays, is widely recognized as one of the most effective mitigation techniques. However, conventional spatial filtering methods developed for radar and telecommunication systems cannot be directly applied to GNSS due to the absence of reference signals and the need for specific adaptation criteria. This paper proposes a novel method for calculating antenna array weight coefficients without requiring a reference signal. A structural–functional model of the spatial filter is presented, along with a comparative analysis of existing optimization criteria, including Mean Squared Error, Signal-to-Noise Ratio, Maximum Likelihood, and Minimum Variance Distortionless Response. The proposed method was verified through the development of an experimental GNSS spatial filter prototype, implemented on the AD-FMCOMMS5-EBZ platform. Experimental results demonstrate the filter's ability to form deep nulls in the radiation pattern toward interference sources, thereby significantly improving the availability and integrity of GNSS information.

title: Hybrid Evaluation of Pure Argon Plasma Treatment for Enhanced Wire Bonding and Manufacturing Efficiency in Microelectronics
authors: Jorell D. Pelingo
abstract: Wire bonding in microelectronic packaging requires exceptionally clean surfaces, as even minor contamination can compromise bond integrity and device performance. This study evaluates pure Argon plasma cleaning as a cleaner, more controlled alternative to the conventional Argon-Nitrogen gas mixture. This study presents a first-of-its-kind hybrid evaluation approach that uniquely integrates established metrologies to assess pure argon plasma treatment. Rapid diagnostic tools-Plazmark™ plasma indicators and the Konica Minolta® FD-5 spectro-densitometer-were synergistically combined with advanced surface characterization techniques, including Time-of-Flight Secondary Ion Mass Spectrometry (ToF-SIMS) and Atomic Force Microscopy (AFM). This novel integration enables a comprehensive, multidimensional analysis of plasma effects, bridging rapid process feedback with indepth surface insights. A Design of Experiment (DOE) identified RF Power and Step Time as key parameters influencing cleaning performance. Results showed that pure Argon plasma significantly reduced surface contaminants, improved surface roughness, and enhanced metallurgical bonding. Ball Shear Tests (BST) confirmed stronger bonds, and Intermetallic Compound (IMC) analysis revealed more uniform coverage. While the Wire Pull Test (WPT) and Stitch Pull Test (SPT) remained consistent, a 21% increase in Wire Bond Units Per Hour (UPH) was achieved due to faster bonding after performing wire bond parameter optimization. Reliability testing, including Temperature Cycling (TC) and High Temperature Storage Life (HTSL), confirmed the durability of pure Argon plasma-treated samples. Evaluations showed higher shear strength, thicker IMC layers, and stable failure modes. No electrical failures were observed after Unbiased Highly Accelerated Stress Test (uHAST), validating performance under thermal and humidity stress. These findings support pure Argon plasma as a superior, scalable, data-driven, and cost-effective solution for high-throughput semiconductor packaging.

title: Machine Learning and Embedding Approaches for Social Network Link Prediction
authors: Renaldy Fredyan, Hafizh Ash Shiddiqi
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Accurately predicting the formation of new relationships in social networks is a fundamental problem with broad applications in recommendation systems, information retrieval, and social computing. This work presents a machine learning framework for link prediction based on network representation learning. The structural characteristics of the graph—including node degree distribution, clustering, and connectivity—are described using exploratory data analysis. Node2Vec learns vector embeddings for every node, therefore encoding both local and global topological characteristics and so capturing the latent structure of the network. Feature vectors for node pairs are then created using these embeddings; these feature vectors act as inputs to a LightGBM classifier trained to forecast link presence. Experimental findings on actual network data show that the suggested method has great predictive accuracy, therefore highlighting the use of combining graph embedding methods with gradient boosting models for link prediction activities.

title: Study on the Threshold of Sturgeon Positive Response Under Uniform Current Field
authors: Yongcong Wu, Shangmao Hu, Qi Mei, Lu Qu, Gang Liu, Hansheng Cai
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: The high reliability development and utilization of large-scale deep-sea wind power is of significant importance for achieving the “carbon peaking and carbon neutrality” strategic goals. The use of a symmetric bipolar system with an ocean grounding electrode has advantages such as low cost and high reliability, but its impact on marine organisms remains unclear. This paper uses sturgeon as the experimental subject and designs an experimental platform to simulate a direct current uniform electric field. Based on the background differential method, it develops target recognition and tracking algorithms, and uses a binocular camera to convert the behavioral characteristics of the fish into quantifiable speed indicators and trajectory indicators. It analyzes the trajectory distribution of sturgeon under various current densities and proposes a threshold determination method for the phototactic response of fish in a uniform electric field. The experimental results show that as the current density increases, fish exhibit a significant phototactic response; when the current density is 0.8 A/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> or higher, the behavioral differences between the experimental group and the control group are statistically significant, suggesting that the response threshold for sturgeon in a direct current uniform electric field is 0.8 A/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Predictive Modeling for Aircraft Noise Assessment and Pollution Impact Reduction
authors: S. Saroja Devi, T Senthilkumar, J. Jagan Babu, A. Arul Mozhi, M. Muthulekshmi, Angel Priya J
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Aircraft noise pollution adversely affects human health and environmental sustainability, especially in metropolitan regions next to airports. Extended exposure to elevated noise levels leads to sleep disruptions, cardiovascular ailments, and cognitive deficits. Furthermore, aircraft emissions increase air pollution, resulting in respiratory problems and climate-related difficulties. This paper introduces a predictive modeling approach using the Gradient Boosting Regressor (GBR) to evaluate aircraft noise levels and pollution effects. The model integrates real-time sensor data, meteorological conditions, and flight characteristics to predict noise propagation precisely. Experimental findings indicate that the GBR model achieves a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 2.13 dB and a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 3.1 dB in forecasting aircraft noise levels, improving traditional linear regression techniques by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8 \%}$</tex> accuracy. Moreover, after implementing optimized flight route recommendations, the pollutant effect study indicates a 15% decrease in high-risk exposure areas. The proposed approach allows airport authorities to formulate proactive noise reduction measures, strengthen regulatory adherence, and promote urban sustainability. These results underscore the efficacy of GBR in predictive noise evaluation, providing a substantial answer for reducing the environmental impact of aircraft.

title: Q-Trans: Quantization-Aware Fine-Tuning of Transformer Models for Energy-Efficient IoT and Edge Deployment
authors: Karthik Kumar Sayyaparaju
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
