abstract: Transformer models are operating on the cutting edge of technology within the field of Natural Language Processing and other areas; however, the challenge of their enormous memory and computational requirements prevent their deployment within energy-constrained environments, such as edge and Internet of Things (IoT) devices. In this context, this paper presents Q-Trans, a fully new framework that combines Quantization-Aware Fine-Tuning (QAT) and parameterefficient fine-tuning. During training, Q-Trans mimics the effects of quantization and trims the size of the set of parameters that are tuned during fine-tuning. As a result, the model size and inference latency are significantly decreased, yet the model retains its original accuracy. Q-Trans optimizes the performance of transformer models and enables their deployment on low-power devices without a reduction in performance. For this reason, it is an excellent candidate for IoT and edge computing. This paper discusses the design, methodology, and advantages of Q-Trans, proving its potential in improving the energy efficiency of transformer models for practical and real-world applications.

title: Low-Light Image Enhancement Based on Intrinsic Image Decomposition
authors: Diclehan Ulucan, Oguzhan Ulucan, Marc Ebner
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Low-light image enhancement is a widely studied field of image processing. Over the past decades, numerous successful algorithms have been proposed to address its challenges, such as low contrast, color fading, and noise. One way to enhance dim scenes with vivid colors while suppressing noise is to apply intrinsic image decomposition prior to enhancement. Intrinsic image decomposition separates an image into its fundamental components, such as reflectance and shading. In this study, we extent our recently proposed learning-free intrinsic image decomposition method based on Retinex theory. This approach utilizes scale-space computations and super-pixel segmentation to effectively estimate the reflectance and shading components of a scene. With slight modifications to the original method, we demonstrate its usability in the context of low-light image enhancement. To validate the effectiveness and generalizability of the method, we conduct comprehensive experiments on five benchmarks. Our results show that our method, originally designed for intrinsic image decomposition, can produce effective results for low-light image enhancement and achieve competitive performance compared to learning-based methods specifically developed for this task.

title: CAUA: A Realistic and Effective Attack on Machine Unlearning Under Limited Information
authors: Jing Zhang, Yichen Zhang, Jie Cui, Xianfeng Xie, Chunyang Fan, Bei Li
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Machine unlearning aims to remove specific data from models to meet privacy regulations. While prior work has explored potential vulnerabilities in unlearning mechanisms, most assume adversaries with privileged access—an unrealistic premise in real-world Machine Learning-as-a-Service (MLaaS) settings. This raises a fundamental question: Can unlearning be exploited under restricted access constraints? We answer this affirmatively by proposing Class-Aligned Unlearning Attack (CAUA), a novel attack framework tailored to realistic deployment settings. CAUA uses target-class examples and public out-of-distribution data to generate semantically aligned inputs that integrate seamlessly into training. These inputs maintain model performance during training but, when unlearned, induce localized representation collapse and significant shifts in decision boundaries. We comprehensively evaluate CAUA across multiple datasets and unlearning paradigms. Notably, unlearning just 0.2% of training data causes up to a 73.4% drop in target-class F1 and a 60.4% drop in overall accuracy, revealing a previously overlooked vulnerability. Our work sheds new light on the risks of machine unlearning and lays a foundation for building more robust defenses.

title: Advancing DSP Processor Computational Efficiency via Implementation of a High-Speed Floating-point ALU Based on Distributed Arithmetic
authors: M. Bharathi, Krithikaa Mohanarangam
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: A state-of-the-art approach for enhancing the computational efficiency of contemporary digital signal processing processors is proposed based on the design of a high-speed floating-point arithmetic logic unit with integrated distributed arithmetic. The proposed technique leverages the computational characteristics of Distributed Arithmetic (DA), which is based on the look-up table approach to simplify and accelerate SOP operations which is the core of many real-time signal processing tasks. By embedding this approach into the FP-ALU, the architecture significantly enhances overall DSP core performance. In order to explore different performance tradeoffs, we develop three FP-ALU architectures based on one, two, and six LUTs, respectively. Each configuration distributes the computational resources differently to meet certain design goals. For example, the single-LUT architecture favors numerical precision, while the two-LUT and six-LUT designs aim at an optimal balance between computational speed and numerical precision. From a comparative performance evaluation, it emerges that the two-LUT architecture is able to enhance processing speed by 46.04%, while the six-LUT configuration achieves a speed increase as high as 52.39% with respect to the single-LUT counterpart. These results highlight the efficiency of the DA-based FP-ALU architectures proposed for speeding up DSP operations at high levels of robust computational accuracy.

title: Delamination Root Cause Analysis in Leaded Package
authors: Mun Leit Chai, William K.K. Thong, Stephen C.C. Lee, Jasmine Lim, Dominic Koey Poh Meng
abstract: The leaded package such as QFP (Quad Flat Package) has been widely used in Semiconductor industry for decades. One of the commonly found reliability failure modes is delamination at either thermal or mechanical stress during packaging or reliability testing (e.g. Temperature cycling). This paper intends to show methods to determine failure mechanisms, root causes and corrective actions for delamination found at leaded packages. Failure analysis (FA) using CSAM (C-mode Scanning Acoustic Microscope), cross-sectioning and SEM (Scanning Electronic Microscope) has been conducted to determine the failure mechanisms of severe delamination found at die flag and mold compound interfaces. The Fish-Bone Diagram has been generated to hypothesize potential root causes, and DOE (design of experiment) has been used to prove and disprove each hypothesis. Additionally, corrective actions are proposed, and the best choice of corrective action will be disclosed in this paper as well.

title: Novel TIM1 Paste Development for Enhanced Thermal Management
authors: Yile, Senthil Kumar, Abito, Hui Shyan, Miew Wan, Tze Qing, Sungsig
abstract: This paper presents the development of a novel thermal interface material (TIM) tailored for high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) applications. The material achieves a thermal conductivity of more than 30 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{W} / \text{mK}$</tex> after curing, significantly reducing thermal resistance in electronic packages. Composed of a high percentage of conductive filler and a thermoset polymer matrix, the TIM ensures a robust conductive path between components. Key formulation parameters, including filler morphology, polymer properties, and curing conditions, were systematically optimized to balance thermal conductivity, adhesion, and mechanical reliability. Application tests demonstrated excellent dispensing consistency, void-free adhesion across large bond areas, and stability under ambient conditions. Detailed thermal-mechanical characterizations further validated the material's performance, positioning it as a promising solution for advanced thermal management.

title: Multi-view Leaderboard: Towards Evaluating the Code Intelligence of LLMs From Multiple Views
authors: Mengyuan Liu, Zexun Zhan, Cuiyun Gao, Yujia Chen, Guoai Xu, Chun Yong Chong, Shan Gao, Xin Xia
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown remarkable performance in code intelligence tasks, prompting the development of various benchmarks and leaderboards to assess their effectiveness across diverse programming scenarios. However, existing leaderboards often rely on coarse-grained metrics and overlook performance variations across different types of tasks. In this paper, we introduce Multi-view Leaderboard, a comprehensive evaluation framework designed to assess the coding capabilities of LLMs from multiple views. Our leaderboard partitions widely-used datasets such as HumanEval, MBPP, and ComplexCodeEval into subsets based on factors like prompt length, problem complexity, and task type. It supports four popular code intelligence tasks including code generation, code completion, test case generation, and API recommendation. Additionally, our leaderboard presents results using ranking tables, line charts, radar charts, and heatmaps. Based on LLMs’ performance on different subsets, we provide model recommendations tailored to different real-world scenarios via a Sankey diagram. A user study involving 11 participants revealed that 90% valued the leaderboard’s practical usefulness for analyzing LLMs’ code intelligence from multiple perspectives. The Multi-view Leaderboard is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/MVLLL/Multi-view-leaderboard. The demonstration video is available at https://youtu.be/J-zQiOYa1Y8

title: An Evaluation of a Visual Question Answering Strategy for Zero-shot Facial Expression Recognition in Still Images
authors: José Salas-Cáceres, Modesto Castrillón-Santana, David Freire-Obregón, Oliverio J. Santana, Daniel Hernández-Sosa, Javier Lorenzo-Navarro
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Facial expression recognition (FER) is a key research area in computer vision and human-computer interaction. Despite recent advances, challenges persist, especially in generalizing to new scenarios. In fact, zero-shot FER significantly reduces the performance of state-of-the-art FER models. The community has recently started to explore the integration of knowledge from Large Language Models for visual tasks. In this work, we evaluate a broad collection of Visual Language Models (VLMs), avoiding the lack of task-specific knowledge by adopting a Visual Question Answering strategy. We compare the proposed pipeline with state-of-the-art FER models, both integrating and excluding VLMs, evaluating well-known FER benchmarks: AffectNet, FERPlus, and RAF-DB. The results show state-of-the-art performance for some VLMs in zero-shot FER scenarios, suggesting a research line for further exploration to improve FER generalization.

title: Performance Analysis and Simulation of Meteor Burst Communication Adaptive Variable-Speed System
authors: Lipu Zhu, Zan Li, Haochen Niu, Jia Shi, Lei Guan, Zhuangzhuang Tie
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: To address the low data throughput of meteor burst communication, this paper proposes an adaptive variable-rate transmission method based on signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) estimation. This method fully leverages the exponential decay characteristics of the meteor burst channel by estimating the channel's SNR in real-time and dynamically adjusting the system's bit rate, thereby achieving adaptive rate control. This strategy significantly enhances the utilization efficiency of the meteor channel while ensuring reliable data transmission. Simulation results demonstrate that, under the same channel conditions, the proposed method achieves a data throughput 2 to 4 times higher than that of traditional fixed-rate transmission systems, thus effectively validating its performance and reliability.

title: Unified Modelling and Consistency Verification of UML Multi-View Models Using Alloy
authors: Yihui Guo, Shmuel Tyszberowicz, Zhiming Liu, Bo Liu
abstract: As software systems grow in complexity, Model-Driven Development demands precise and scalable verification techniques. UML enables multi-view modeling, yet its semiformal semantics frequently lead to inconsistencies across diagrams. This paper presents a consistency verification approach for class and sequence diagrams using the Alloy modeling language. We systematically transform UML models into Alloy logic through a modular abstraction strategy and a set of formal consistency rules spanning structural, behavioral, and cross-view semantics. The Alloy Analyzer then performs constraint solving, automatically detects violations, and generates counterexamples for debugging. Evaluated in a case study, the method demonstrates effective inconsistency detection, comprehensive rule coverage, and reliable validation of interaction logic. Results confirm that the approach enables automated, fine-grained consistency checking and integrates smoothly into formal verification workflows.

title: Distortion-Aware Error Resilient Mode Selection for Robust Low-Delay Hierarchical Video Transmission
authors: Bing Wang, Dan Song, Dong Tang, Jianxi Yang, Dong Xia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Low-delay hierarchical coding structure (LD-HCS) as a vital component of video coding technology greatly enhances coding efficiency by further exploiting the temporal dependency among frames. However, due to error propagation arising from the prediction loop, it also increases the vulnerability to transmission errors, resulting in significant video quality degradation. To address this deficiency in LD-HCS, this paper proposes a coding mode selection method to increase the error resilience of video streaming. Specifically, a general end-to-end distortion estimation model is devised for LD-HCS to estimate the perceived distortion of the decoder. Incorporated with this distortion, the rate-distortion optimization (RDO) process of coding mode is modified to suit the error-prone video transmission and select the most robust coding mode. The simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed probabilistic model is able to accurately predict the end-to-end distortion under LD-HCS, while the proposed coding mode selection scheme significantly reduces the effect of error propagation. Compared to the other reference approaches, the proposed method is capable of improving video quality over error-prone networks.

title: A Smart Mobile Waste Sorting App Based on Clean Architecture with Product Classification using Random Forest and KNN
authors: Khoerunisa Alfin, Arfio Ardana, Yugo Pratama, Yulintyandra Puja Antanita, Rita Purnamasari, Khaerudin Saleh
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This work presents the development of a mobile application for automatic waste classification using barcode decoding and machine learning techniques. The system is designed with a clean architecture approach to enhance maintainability, scalability, and modularity. A dataset of over 10,000 product entries was compiled and preprocessed to train two machine learning models: Random Forest and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN). Both models classify waste types (organic or inorganic) based on barcode metadata and product names, with the Random Forest model achieving a superior validation accuracy of 97.7% and an average F1-score of 0.98, indicating robust and consistent classification performance. The application also integrates a reward system and is evaluated using the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). Results show high user acceptance, with perceived usefulness (86.03 %), perceived ease of use (85.04 %), and behavioral intention to use (86.01 %). This work demonstrates a promising approach to digital waste management by combining product recognition, intelligent classification, and user-centered evaluation.

title: Empowering Silent Voices: Adaptive EEG-to-Text Systems for Assistive Technologies
authors: Ahmed Fares, Mostafa El Gedawy, Omnia Nabil, Omar Mamdouh, Mahmoud Nady, Nour Alhuda Adel, Basem M. ElHalawany
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on ICT &amp; Accessibility (ICTA) co-located with 2025 2nd International Conference and Forum on Assistive Technologies for Special Needs (ATSN)
abstract: Translating brain activity into natural language represents a transformative frontier in human-machine interaction and assistive communication technology for individuals with speech impairments. While electroencephalography (EEG) has shown promise for neural decoding, existing EEG-to-text methods remain constrained by closed vocabularies, limited semantic expressiveness, and inadequate accommodation of intersubject neural variability. This work presents a novel framework that transcends traditional closed-vocabulary limitations by synergistically combining subject-adaptive representation learning with advanced natural language processing architectures. Our approach employs deep neural networks to extract discriminative EEG features, enabling the generation of complex sentences that extend beyond the constraints of training data. Experimental evaluation on the ZuCo corpus demonstrates substantial improvements across multiple metrics, including BLEU, ROUGE, and BERTScore, surpassing state-of-the-art baselines. The framework effectively produces semantically coherent and grammatically accurate text while adapting to individual neural signal patterns through personalized modeling. By bridging open-vocabulary text generation with neural signal interpretation, this research establishes foundations for practical brain-to-text communication systems. The interdisciplinary implications span assistive technology innovation and personalized communication interfaces, advancing the paradigm of brain-computer interaction across clinical, research, and consumer applications.

title: A Novel Learning-Based Collaborative Filtering System for Real-Time Traffic Accident Detection
authors: Bing Wang, Dong Tang, Peng Tian, Jianxi Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Traffic accident detection is a crucial task in traffic management systems and plays an important role in improving operational efficiency and safety supervision. In recent years, deep learning-based automatic detection technology has gradually replaced traditional manual detection, owing to its high accuracy, real-time performance and anti-interference capability in complex traffic scenarios. However, traffic videos captured by surveillance cameras installed at intersections often suffer from low image quality and high noise, which degrades the detection accuracy of traffic recognition models. In this paper, we propose a deep learning-based traffic accident detection system that incorporates Gaussian filtering and non-local means filtering as preprocessing steps. Specifically, the proposed system is composed of four crucial modules, namely a preprocessing module integrating Gaussian filtering and non-local means filtering, an object detection module based on the YOLOv8 model, a tracking module based on Kalman filtering and the Hungarian algorithm, and an accident detection module for analysing and extracting vehicle speed, displacement and bounding boxes. Experimental results based on real traffic video data demonstrate that the proposed method exhibits feasibility in real-time traffic monitoring applications with low image quality and high noise, and it achieves the best performance compared with other comparison methods.

title: A Reliable Direct Torque Based Sliding Mode Technique Integrated with MRAS Speed Estimator for Four Wheel Electric Drives
authors: M P Mohandass, Divya Darshini B, Malla Pranay Sai, Hari L
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Electric Vehicles (EVs) are gaining widespread adoption owing to their energy efficiency, low emissions and reduced dependency on fossil fuels. Despite these advantages, EVs often face performance challenges when operating under varying terrain, abrupt acceleration or braking and unpredictable load disturbances. These factors can cause an imbalance between the load torque and electromagnetic torque, resulting in torsional vibrations that reduce passenger comfort and increase mechanical stress on the drivetrain components. To overcome these issues, the Direct torque control method utilizing the MRAS observer technique guarantees precise torque regulation and rotor speed estimation, which are particularly vital in sensorless configurations where both reliability and cost effectiveness are of utmost importance. This work introduces a sensorless Direct Torque Control (DTC) method integrated with a Model Reference Adaptive System based Integral Sliding Mode (MRAS-ISMC). The proposed approach is designed to enhance the robustness of four-wheel-drive electric vehicles (4WDEVs) by improving the dynamic response, minimising torque ripple and ensuring stability across a wide range of operating conditions.

title: Fabrication of n77/n79 Antenna-Plexer: Integration of BAW Filters with Broadband Glass-IPD Diplexer for 5G Applications
authors: Minsoo Park, Daehee Park, Jongsu Ha, Jong-Min Yook, Jong-Gwan Yook
abstract: This paper presents an n77/n79 antenna-plexer that integrates bulk acoustic wave (BAW) filters with a glassintegrated passive device (IPD) diplexer. The proposed antennaplexer combines two high-selectivity BAW resonators for the n77 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.3-4.2 \text{GHz}$</tex>) and n79 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.4-5.0 \text{GHz}$</tex>) bands with a low-loss, wideband diplexer on a glass-IPD platform. To enhance spiralinductor performance within the IPD, metal lines were thickened to increase the Q-factor. All components were assembled directly onto the glass-IPD substrate. Measurements show insertion losses below 3.3 dB across n77 and n79 both bands and better than 25 dB attenuation in the adjacent stopband.

title: Layered Decoding of DGLDPC Codes Based on a SPC-PC Codes
authors: Siqi Yu, Zhiping Shi, You Zhang, Wangning Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: In optical communication systems, channel coding is required to exhibit high code rates, superior performance, high throughput, and low complexity. While LDPC codes, adopted as the channel coding standard for 5G, deliver commendable performance, they often struggle to simultaneously achieve high code rates and low bit error rates. In contrast, DGLDPC codes, as an extension of LDPC codes, have been demonstrated to maintain excellent performance even at high code rates. This paper investigates a type of DGLDPC code and integrates it with layered scheduling decoding which is a technique originally developed for LDPC codes. The aim is to leverage their shared advantages: high error-correcting performance, strong structural regularity, and rapid convergence speed.

title: Impact of Storage Conditions on Solder Paste Performance in Power Package Assembly
authors: Marlon Bartolo, Ramil Vasquez, Vuenna Rizza San Juan, Dennise Payawal
abstract: The reliability and quality of power package assembly, particularly for MOSFET power products, are heavily dependent on the consistency and quality of solder paste used in the manufacturing process. Solder paste processability is sensitive to storage conditions, which can significantly impact its shelf life and application consistency. This paper presents the investigation of the solder void issues in the PQFP 8-leads package, highlighting the critical importance of proper storage conditions for solder paste. Through a systematic root cause analysis, employing the Global 8D methodology and utilizing tools such as fishbone diagrams and fault-tree analysis, the study identified that storage of solder paste at temperatures above 0° C can lead to solder particle settling, resulting in nonhomogeneous metal loading within the syringe. This phenomenon can cause inconsistent solder paste dispensing performance that leads to solder void. Storing the paste at lower temperatures (−5 to −18° C) can prevent particle settling and maintain its homogeneity.

title: Wire Bond Challenges of Copper Clip for Multi-Die Controller MOSFET Package
authors: Chantana Tangcharoensuk, Pey Fang Hiew, Eu Poh Leng, Nara Tappech, Zhi Wei Gong, Alongkorn Makmeesup
abstract: The increasing demand for high-performance multi-die controller MOSFETs has driven the adoption of copper clip interconnects, which offer enhanced power density, thermal performance, and electrical efficiency. However, this introduces assembly challenges that affect reliability. This study addresses two key challenges: (1) surface pitting on aluminum bond pads from aggressive titanium (Ti) etching during frontend processing and (2) stitch bond instability resulting from thickness variation between half-etched lead fingers and solid tie bars. Experiments show that reducing Ti etching time to 65% minimizes pitting and promotes uniform intermetallic compound (IMC) growth. Optimizing lead frame thickness to achieve a good clearance of less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, along with implementing an extended window clamp significantly improves stitch bond stability. These solutions were derived from Design of Experiments (DOE) and validated through AEC-Q100 Grade 1 reliability tests, which passed Temperature Cycling (TC), Highly Accelerated Stress Testing (HAST), and HighTemperature Storage Life (HTSL). These findings provide practical guidelines for robust copper clip packaging in automotive-grade power devices.

title: Progressive Image Enhancement Via Dual-Domain Entropy GAN with Attention Priors for Intelligent Data Transmission
authors: Ke Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: This research focuses on the dual challenges of visual quality and transmission efficiency in image transmission under the low illumination and complex scenes. Hence, this study proposes a progressive image enhancement method based on the dual-domain entropy-regularized generative adversarial network (DDE-GAN) with effcient attention priors. This algorithm conducts layer-by-layer decomposition and enhancement of the image in both the spatial and frequency domains. Low-frequency components are used for the global brightness and contrast adjustment, while high-frequency components are used for texture and detail compensation. Furthermore, an entropy regularization term is introduced into the adversarial loss to improve training stability. As an optimization, an attention prior mechanism based on joint channel-spatial modulation is designed. In intelligent data transmission scenarios, the DDEGAN encodes the enhanced image into a symbol sequence. Joint end-to-end optimization is achieved through power and bandwidth allocation strategies. Experimental evaluations were conducted using low-light natural scenes, underwater images of varying water quality, and low-light remote sensing images. Results showed that on the low-light test set, DDE-GAN achieved a PSNR of 24.1dB, a SSIM of 0.80, a LPIPS of 0.19, and a transmission efficiency (TE) of 91%. On the underwater image set, the PSNR was 24.8dB, the SSIM was 0.81, the LPIPS was 0.16, the TE was 93%, and the average latency was only 98 ms. On the remote sensing image test set, the PSNR was 23.0dB, the SSIM was 0.76, the LPIPS was 0.19, the TE was 90%, and the average latency was 100ms. Comprehensive evaluations showed that DDE-GAN achieved the best performance across all indicators.

authors: N/A

title: An Automated Method of Identifying Surface Coal Mining Disturbances by Autonomous Sample Generation_supp1-3665648.pdf
authors: Zhen Yang
abstract: Rapid and timely identification of surface coal mining disturbances is important for mining governance and environmental sustainability analyses. However, automated methods for detecting mining disturbances remain inadequate because current studies require manual tasks, such as sample training or multiparameter settings, when they are applied to a new region. This paper proposes an automated method for identifying mining disturbances using Landsat time series. The method automatically and adaptively generates Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) templates for each vegetation change trajectory according to the typical NDVI pattern in surface mining regions. Each pixel is subsequently classified as a specific vegetation change trajectory using the NDVI templates as training samples and the dynamic time warping (DTW)-based K-nearest neighbor (KNN) function as the classifier. The potential mining disturbance is extracted on the basis of the classification results of the vegetation change trajectory. Mining disturbances are identified using the topological relationship between the exposed coal generated by a spectral index and the potential mining disturbance. As no manual task is required in the sample generation and the classification task, the identification process is fully automated after data preprocessing. When tested in five globally distributed study areas featuring diverse vegetation types, the method shows high-level performance in identifying mining disturbances, disturbance years, and recovery years (±1 year), achieving accuracies of no less than 94.4%, 82.9%, and 73.3%, respectively. Given the high degree of automation and freely available Landsat imagery, the method is transferrable to other regions.

title: Unsupervised Hand Tracking for Sign Language Recognition via Optical Flow and Previous Tracking Data
authors: Naser Jawas, Tardi Tjahjadi
abstract: Accurate hand tracking is a fundamental requirement for sign language recognition and human computer interaction. However, reliable tracking remains challenging due to the high degrees of freedom of hand motion, frequent shape variations, and the need to localise both hands simultaneously under varying imaging conditions. This paper proposes an unsupervised, model free hand tracking framework that exploits dense optical flow and temporal consistency to estimate hand locations without requiring any training data. The method employs a multi-scale dense optical flow representation in which a colour-encoded motion map separates dominant hand motion from the background. Clustering and contour analysis are then applied to generate hand candidates, followed by motion refinement and validation using tracking information from previous frames to ensure temporal robustness. The proposed approach is evaluated on four public sign language datasets: RWTH-Boston-50, RWTH-Boston-104, RWTH-PHOENIX-Weather, and the BBC-Oxford British Sign Language (BOBSL) dataset. Quantitative comparisons are conducted against state-of-the-art learning-based detectors, including YOLOv3, YOLOv11, MediaPipe, and MMPose, as well as classical tracking baselines, using centre-of-location error, tracking error rate, and computational speed. Results show that although learning-based methods achieve the highest accuracy, the proposed unsupervised tracker attains competitive performance, stable tracking under fast motion and motion blur, and consistent cross-dataset generalisation without training or domain adaptation, offering a favourable trade-off between accuracy and computational complexity for data-limited or resource-constrained sign language recognition systems.

title: Keeping It Smooth: The Role of Haptic Feedback in Shaping Motor Performance_supp1-3666867.pdf
authors: Marcia K O'Malley
abstract: Training for complex motor tasks, such as those encountered in minimally invasive surgery, benefits from effective performance feedback mechanisms to accelerate skill acquisition and ensure retention. Prior work has demonstrated that haptic feedback based on movement smoothness quantified by the metric spectral arc length (SPARC), when provided in real-time as trainees perform complex motor tasks, can cause beneficial changes in task completion strategies resulting in faster completion times without loss of accuracy. The concept of movement smoothness is abstract, however, and more intuitive measures of movement smoothness like idle time and average velocity can be good alternatives to SPARC. Here, we demonstrate the effect of real-time objective performance feedback of movement smoothness, conveyed through a vibrotactile cue encoding alternative measures of movement smoothness, compared to feedback based on SPARC. Subjects receiving smoothness-based feedback based on average velocity performed the task fastest, but their accuracy was lower than the other two groups. We evaluated the effect of removing feedback for additional trials, and showed that performance improvements ceased. After training, the three groups were indistinguishable from each other.

title: AraOptical Testbed: Design, Field Trials, and Channel Analysis of Long-Range FSOC System With COTS Transceivers
authors: Xun Li, Ataberk Atalar, Md Nadim, Sarath Babu, Ozan Berk Boyraz, Christian Joseph Margison, Mustafa Mert Bayer, Arsalan Ahmad, Daji Qiao, Hongwei Zhang, Ozdal Boyraz
abstract: This paper presents araoptical, a 10.15 km rural free-space optical communication (FSOC) testbed designed using commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) 10 G SFP+ transceivers and integrated into the ARA wireless living lab. The system features a multi-level autonomous alignment algorithm and real-time link monitoring, enabling stable high-throughput communication in dynamically varying atmospheric conditions. A continuous 2-hour UDP-based bit rate test was conducted, and environmental data were logged to investigate turbulence-induced impairments. A two-stage polynomial regression approach was developed to estimate the refractive index structure parameter (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C_{n}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) from weather station data and predict link throughput by combining one-minute block-averaged received power with the Rytov variance predicted from the measured meteorological data. The proposed model achieved <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2} = 89.92\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> for turbulence estimation and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2} = 62.22\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> for throughput prediction. Our results demonstrate the feasibility of using in-situ environmental sensing and data-driven models to characterize and predict FSOC performance under real-world conditions.

title: Magnitude and Phase Compensation in the Phase-Locked Loop of a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor-Driven Single-Rotor Air-Conditioning Compressor
authors: Jing Ma, Yang Yang, Xiaolei Che, Guoji Liu, Yibo Guo, Wenxin Xu
abstract: In a single-rotor air-conditioning compressor, continuous gas compression and discharge induce periodic load torque fluctuations at the same frequency as the rotor’s mechanical rotation, thereby introducing periodically fluctuating signals in the motor rotor angle and angular speed. A position sensorless control scheme is implemented under variable load conditions. This scheme combines an observer designed based on the permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) model in the direct-quadrature (d-q) coordinate system with a conventional phase-locked loop (PLL) and field-oriented control (FOC). In this implementation, magnitude scaling and phase offset in the PLL module cause errors in the estimated rotor angle and speed. This angular error degrades the control accuracy of the FOC algorithm, leading to significant motor speed fluctuations. To address these limitations of conventional PLL architecture, this paper proposes a Magnitude and Phase Compensation PLL (MAPC-PLL). The proposed MAPC-PLL compensates for both magnitude and phase deviations in estimated rotor angle and speed signals through a compensation module with an additional control parameter, achieving enhanced performance over conventional PLL implementations while retaining the closed-loop transfer function’s original pole configuration. Experimental results demonstrate that regulating this control parameter in the MAPC-PLL controls the reduction level of rotor angle estimation error, thereby improving the accuracy and performance of FOC, significantly suppressing rotor speed fluctuations caused by the conventional PLL, and ultimately enhancing the overall system control performance.

title: Performance Analysis of Cooperative NOMA Networks over Compound Fading Channels
authors: Khaled M. Rabie
abstract: The emergence of non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) technology has redefined traditional resource allocation in wireless networks with its capability to superimpose multiple user signals over the same transmission resources. In this respect, this paper studies the performance of cooperative relaying NOMA systems over a compound fading channel, namely, the α−Lomax fading model. Specifically, we consider a network with one base-station communicating with one far-user and one near-user with the latter acting also as a relay based on the decode-and-forward protocol. The channels associated with the two users are assumed to be statistically independent. Novel closed-form analytical expressions for the probability density function and cumulative distribution function of the minimum of two α-Lomax random variables were derived. These expressions were then used to derive an accurate ergodic sum rate (ESR) analytical expression for the cooperative NOMA system and its corresponding upper bound expression. To highlight the achievable performance gains, results for the cooperative orthogonal multiple access (OMA) system were also included. The derived expressions enabled us to examine the impact of various system parameters, including the power allocation factors, fading parameters and transmit power, on the system performance. Monte Carlo simulations were included to verify the correctness and accuracy of our analysis. The findings revealed that to achieve maximum ESR, the power allocation factors should be carefully optimized. It was also shown that the optimized cooperative NOMA system always outperforms the cooperative OMA approach in all considered scenarios. Furthermore, the results clearly indicated that increasing α and/or λ will enhance the system performance for both the cooperative NOMA and OMA schemes.

title: Audio-Visual Approach for Multimodal Concurrent Speaker Detection
authors: Amit Eliav, Sharon Gannot
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Concurrent Speaker Detection (CSD), the task of identifying active speakers and their overlaps in an audio signal, is essential for various audio applications, including meeting transcription, speaker diarization, and speech separation. This study presents a multimodal deep learning approach that integrates audio and visual information. The proposed model utilizes an early fusion strategy, combining audio and visual features through cross-modal attention mechanisms with a learnable [CLS] token to capture key audio-visual relationships. The model is extensively evaluated on two real-world datasets, the established AMI dataset and the recently introduced EasyCom dataset. Experiments validate the effectiveness of the multimodal fusion strategy. An ablation study further supports the design choices and the model's training procedure. As this is the first work reporting CSD results on the challenging EasyCom dataset, the findings demonstrate the potential of the proposed multimodal approach for CSD in real-world scenarios.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)

title: Design of Dielectric Waveguide Filter Based on Sector-Shaped Dual-Mode Resonators
authors: Kaihong Shen, Jiajin Wang, Yuqiang Xie, Zihang Qi, Xiuping Li
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: A novel sector-shaped dual-mode dielectric waveguide resonator (DWR) is proposed in this letter and utilized to design a bandpass filter. Two tuning blind holes and a through hole were fabricated at the base of the resonator. The tuning blind holes were employed to independently adjust the resonant frequencies of the dual modes, while the through hole was strategically implemented to modulate the coupling strength between these modes. The proposed filter is composed of two single-mode resonators and two dual-mode resonators, the magnitude of the intercavity coupling coefficient is predominantly governed by the geometric parameters of the coupling window, enabling precise tuning through window dimension optimization. The filter was ultimately simulated, fabricated, and experimentally tested, thereby validating the feasibility of the proposed design. The fabricated dual-mode resonator filter demonstrates a center frequency of 3.7GHz with a 200MHz (7.0% 3-dB fractional bandwidth), achieving both compact size and wide bandwidth.

title: Autoregressive models for fast breathing rate estimation with distributed elevated radars
authors: Mareike Wendelmuth, Alexander Yarovoy, Francesco Fioranelli
abstract: The problem of contactless accurate monitoring of vital signs of patients in psychiatric settings is addressed in this work. Most state-of-the-art radar-based approaches rely on measurements acquired at short distances with the sensor positioned directly in front of the subject’s chest, typically within 1 m, to ensure a strong line-of-sight signal. In contrast, we investigate a substantially more challenging and clinically safer configuration, where multiple Frequency-Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) radars are mounted at ceiling height in a tilted position, remaining completely out of reach and outside the direct line of sight of patients. An experimental dataset involving 30 participants performing 7 activities was collected using two of these ceiling-mounted radars and one reference radar mounted at hip-height. A new processing pipeline is proposed with automatic range bin selection and fast autoregressive (AR) spectral estimation, including an approach that leverages all chirps per frame to improve performances with short observation window lengths. Despite the unfavourable sensing geometry and increased radar-to-subject distance, the results demonstrate that ceiling-mounted radars achieve mean absolute respiration rate errors comparable to the hip-height reference radar. The best configuration yields a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.18 respirations per minute (rpm) for participants in a sitting position at distances exceeding 4m from the ceiling-mounted radars. Moreover, the proposed AR-based method significantly improves estimation accuracy for short windows of 3.1 s, achieving a MAE below 4.3 rpm for all radars and participant positions.

title: Electrical Interconnect Loss Cancellation for MEMS Gyroscopic Resonators via In-Situ Integrated Virtual Negative Resistance
authors: Yang Zhao, Guoming Xia, Jinyang Huang, Qin Shi, Anping Qiu
abstract: Electrical interconnect loss degrades the intrinsic high Q-factor of MEMS gyroscopic resonators and alters their phase response. This paper proposes, for the first time, a loss cancellation approach utilizing an in-situ integrated negative impedance converter (NIC), which requires no additional tuning structures or electrodes. Controllable positive feedback is embedded into the conventional transimpedance amplifier (TIA), yielding a virtual negative resistance (NR) that cancels the interconnect loss or even the intrinsic loss of the resonator itself. We further demonstrate that through proper zero-pole arrangement of the Opam and its feedback network, the NIC can be degenerated into a single-pole system, thereby eliminating stability risk. Serving as the front-end of a split-mode MEMS gyroscope, this scheme recovers the sense Q-factor from a damped value of 2.8k to its intrinsic value of 42k and passively reduces its thermal variation from 52% to 2.1%. Furthermore, it corrects the gyrof’s phase shift from 1.25deg to 0.06deg, corresponding to a reduction in the quadrature leakage coefficient from 2.2% to 0.1% and maintaining a variation of 40ppm over a 100°C-temperature range, even without the use of a high-accurate active stabilization loop.

title: A 3D-Printed Full-Metal Dual-Band Circularly Polarized Antenna with Large Frequency Ratio
authors: Chenqi Dong, Jun Xu, Jiexuan Liu, Wei Hong
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents a dual-band circularly polarized (CP) antenna that integrates sub-6 GHz and millimeter-wave (mm-Wave) radiation structures compactly through structural reuse method. The sub-6 GHz CP radiator is a magneto-electric dipole (ME-dipole) fed by a single ℾ-shaped probe, and the mm-Wave CP one is a 2×2 stepped septum waveguide array fed by a one-to-four waveguide power divider. Fabricated using 3-D printing technology, the antenna achieves an impedance bandwidth (|S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sub>|<-10dB) of 15.98% (4.55-5.34 GHz), a 3-dB axial ratio (AR) bandwidth of 15.38% (4.68-5.46 GHz) and a peak gain of 7.48 dBic in the sub-6GHz band. In the mm-Wave band, an impedance bandwidth of 30.93% (23.42-31.99 GHz), a 3-dB AR bandwidth of 27.27% (23.75-31.25 GHz) and a peak gain of 15.23 dBic have been achieved. Moreover, good port isolation has been obtained. Owing to the merits of wide bandwidth, high-efficiency, low-cost, etc., the proposed large frequency ratio dual-band CP antenna can be a promising candidate for B5G/6G communications.

title: Generative Deep-Learning Design of Single- and Dual-Polarized Four-Port MIMO Antennas for Full-Duplex Communication
authors: Mustafa Ayebe, Rob Maaskant, Marianna Ivashina, Henrik Holter, Johan Malmström
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This work presents a machine-learning-driven framework for designing single- and dual-polarized, four-port MIMO antennas for in-band full-duplex (FD MIMO) operation. A symmetry-constrained, pixel-based layout enables efficient exploration of the design space. A multi-task convolutional neural network (CNN) surrogate is trained to jointly predict return loss and multi-port coupling from symmetric pixel layouts, accurately modeling the amplitude of the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4\times 4$</tex-math></inline-formula> S-parameter matrix across 5–8 GHz. By embedding this surrogate within a binary genetic algorithm, the framework synthesizes antenna layouts that simultaneously achieve impedance matching and high inter-port isolation across the operating band. Fabricated prototypes show close agreement with predictions and simulations, achieving a return loss greater than 10 dB, isolation up to 36.25 dB, and realized gain of 7.7 dBi at 6.2 GHz. Unlike prior multi-port designs, the approach delivers low loss and robust isolation without relying on additional decoupling structures such as defected ground planes, resonators, or stacked patches, establishing a scalable methodology for next-generation FD MIMO antenna systems.

title: Improved Equivalent Circuit Model of HTS Dynamo based on Integrating a Feature on Frequency Dependency of Critical Current
authors: Yuechen Bai, Zhenan Jiang, Mohammad Yazdani-Asrami, Wenjuan Song
abstract: The charging current and output voltage of the High temperature superconducting (HTS) dynamo flux pumps can be calculated using a simple equivalent circuit model (ECM). Thus, the charging curve prediction would be faster. Creating a model that accurately and quickly simulates HTS Dynamos' operation enables the obtaining of expected results with sufficient accuracy before experimentation and prototyping, thereby improving design, research and development efficiency. Previous studies on ECM-based simulation indicate a growing discrepancy between simulation and experimental results as the operating frequency increases. Yet, no model has addressed this problem. In this paper, a frequency-improved ECM for HTS Dynamo based on the integration of the frequency dependence of critical current into ECM has been proposed and developed in SIMULINK, which directly addresses the aforementioned research gap. The model takes into account the impacts of dynamic resistance, the E-J Power Law, and the frequency dependence of the critical current of the HTS tape on the HTS Dynamo performance. The simulated charging current and output voltage of the HTS Dynamo were investigated by varying the rotor rotation frequency and the air gap between the permanent magnets and the HTS tape. An analysis has been carried out regarding the simulation results from the frequency-improved ECM and the original ECM, compared with the corresponding experimental data under the same operating conditions. Finally, the simulation accuracy of the frequency-improved ECM is compared with that of the ECM without considering the frequency dependence of the critical current under the same operating conditions, yielding a significant improvement, with up to a 96.7% reduction in error.

title: LLM-Driven Adaptive Cloud Resource Scheduling: Bridging Reasoning Intelligence with Optimization Guarantees
authors: Guanyu Ding, Shiyu Yang, Han Lin, Zifan Chen, Jie Si Yang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Cloud resource scheduling presents a fundamental challenge in modern distributed computing, where heterogeneous workloads, complex task dependencies, and multi-objective optimization requirements exceed the capabilities of traditional rule-based and small-scale machine learning approaches. Existing schedulers struggle to dynamically adapt to evolving workload patterns while simultaneously satisfying Service Level Agreement (SLA) constraints, resource efficiency targets, and fairness policies. This paper introduces LLMSched, a novel Large Language Model (LLM)-driven adaptive scheduling framework that synergistically combines the contextual reasoning capabilities of foundation models with the execution guarantees of classical optimization techniques. Our approach transforms heterogeneous cluster states-including task dependency graphs, real-time resource utilization metrics, and SLA specifications-into a unified structured-textual representation that leverages LLMs' few-shot learning and causal reasoning abilities to generate intelligent scheduling candidates. These candidates are subsequently refined through a lightweight Integer Linear Programming (ILP) module that ensures feasibility and optimality under resource constraints. We evaluate LLMSched on Google's production cluster trace dataset, demonstrating significant improvements over state-of-the-art baselines: 23.7% reduction in average job completion time, 18.4% improvement in resource utilization efficiency, and 31.2% decrease in SLA violations across diverse workload scenarios. Extensive ablation studies validate the contributions of each architectural component, while robustness analysis under workload perturbations confirms the framework's practical viability. Our work establishes a new paradigm for intelligent resource management that bridges the gap between neural reasoning and algorithmic precision, opening avenues for LLM applications in systems optimization domains.

title: Study on the Internal Resistance Characteristics of Lithium-Ion Batteries Considering State of Charge
authors: Bin Yi, Yu Zhang, Hui Ye, Cheng Chen, Aikui Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: As the most widely deployed new energy storage technology, lithium-ion battery energy storage plays a crucial role in supporting the stable operation of the power grid. To optimize the operational efficiency of lithium-ion battery energy storage stations, it is necessary to model and study the internal resistance characteristics of lithium-ion batteries. This paper first introduces several equivalent circuit models and, according to the requirements of energy consumption analysis, finally selects the Full-Frequency Domain Equivalent Circuit Model (FECM) as the research focus. The electrochemical impedance spectra (EIS) of a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$280 \mathrm{A} \cdot \mathrm{h}$</tex> lithium iron phosphate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{LiFePO}_{4}\right)$</tex> battery were experimentally tested under both open-circuit and operating conditions, and the parameters of the equivalent circuit model were fitted accordingly. Based on the experimental data, the internal resistance characteristics of the battery were analyzed, and both the electrochemical polarization resistance and the concentration polarization resistance were globally corrected. Mathematical models for the correction coefficients were then established and applied to calculate the polarization resistance of the battery under different series configurations, state-of-charge (SOC) levels, and operating time conditions.

title: Research on the Ice Melting Method of LC Oscillatory Discharge Based on Voltage Control
authors: Wenbin Wang, Zhongming Li, Huayu Xu, Jin Yang, Zongying Song, Jingwei Liu, Xiaoyu Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: In order to accurately solve the problem of icing on winter transmission lines and rail transit contact networks, and to address the shortcomings of traditional ice melting technology such as high energy consumption and harmonic pollution, this paper conducts research on LC oscillation discharge ice melting method based on voltage control. Firstly, based on the LC series resonance principle, the oscillation frequency of 1500~2000Hz (rated 1800Hz) is determined, taking into account the ice melting efficiency, device characteristics, and harmonic suppression requirements; Next, design a power topology that includes a PWM rectifier and an H-bridge inverter, using a feedforward decoupling voltage current dual loop control strategy to ensure stable DC voltage and output frequency; Finally, through MATLAB/Simulink simulation, four types of wire icing lines were tested, and the output currents all met the requirements for ice melting. This method has high energy efficiency and fast response, providing a reliable technical solution for the treatment of line icing.

title: Study on Backside Metallization for the S-SWIFT™ Package
authors: Dambi Jo, JiHyun Kim, TaeKyeong Hwang, WonChul Do, KyungRok Park
abstract: As semiconductor package modules continue to increase in size and functionality, the demand for efficient heat dissipation technologies has grown significantly even in semiconductor packaging, especially for large, high- functionality modules. S-SWIFT™ packaging, using SWIFT® High-Density Fan-Out (HDFO) technology, requires enhanced thermal management due to high copper density and molded structures. A metal lid and thermal interface material (TIM), particularly a solder TIM (sTIM) with indium alloys, improve heat transfer. These require backside metallization (BSM) for thermal/electrical connectivity. However, traditional BSM methods are unsuitable due to high temperatures, so low- temperature BSM processes must be developed for molded S- SWIFT modules. This study investigates low-temperature BSM fabrication methods-plating and sputtering-along with three stack-up configurations nd addresses the requirements of BSM in a fan-out package.

title: High-Aspect-Ratio TSiCV Etch Development in 4H-SiC for RF Interposer Applications
authors: Ng Yong Chyn, Li Bin, Yoo Jae Ok, Arvind Sundaram, B. S. S. Chandra Rao, Tsang Yat Fung, Lim Teck Guan, Li Hongyu
abstract: This work focused on the inductively coupled plasma (ICP) etching of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> diameter, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> deep ThroughSiC Vias (TSVs) in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 \text{mm} 4 \mathrm{H}$</tex> monocrystalline SiC wafers, utilizing a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> electroplated Nickel (Ni) hard mask. Impact of the addition of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex> flow and chamber pressure on the process has been characterized to achieve SiC Via profile. Results confirmed that the presence of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex> is key process knob for achieving clean surfaces by reacting with carbon byproducts to suppress “grass” formation. Furthermore, exploring a lower chamber pressure regime to enhance bombardment and improved etch depth, achieving a 25% higher etch rate compared to a higherpressure regime. Similar to TSV, SiC via formation also observed Aspect ratio dependent etching (ARDE) i. e, etch rate of SiC via etch rate declined from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.87 \mu \mathrm{m}/\min$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.62 \mu \mathrm{m}/\min$</tex> as the via depth increases from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$52.1 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$111 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> with the reduction of sidewall angle from 87.8° to 84.0°. Unlike Si etching, ARDE effect and profile angle strongly influenced by Ni redeposition within SiC via.

title: FLAME: Flexible and Lightweight Biometric Authentication Scheme in Malicious Environments
authors: Fuyi Wang, Fangyuan Sun, Mingyuan Fan, Jianying Zhou, Jin Ma, Chao Chen, Jiangang Shu, Leo Yu Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Privacy-preserving biometric authentication (PPBA) enables client authentication without revealing sensitive bio-metric data, addressing privacy and security concerns. Many studies have proposed efficient cryptographic solutions to this problem based on secure multi-party computation, typically assuming a semi-honest adversary model, where all parties follow the protocol but may try to learn additional information. However, this assumption often falls short in real-world scenarios, where adversaries may behave maliciously and actively deviate from the protocol. In this paper, we propose, implement, and evaluate FLAME, a Flexible and Lightweight biometric Authentication scheme designed for a Malicious Environment. By hybridizing lightweight secret-sharing-family primitives within two-party computation, FLAME carefully designs a line of supporting protocols that incorporate integrity checks with rationally extra overhead. Additionally, FLAME enables server-side authentication with various similarity metrics through a crossmetric-compatible design, enhancing flexibility and robustness without requiring any changes to the server-side process. A rigorous theoretical analysis validates the correctness, security, and efficiency of FLAME. Extensive experiments highlight FLAME's superior efficiency, with a communication reduction by 97.61x 110.13x and a speedup of 2.72x 2.82x (resp. 6.58x 8.51x) in a LAN (resp. WAN) environment, when compared to the state-of-the-art work.

title: Optimization assisted image-denoising technique based on a modified non-linear diffusion filter
authors: Himanshu Gupta, Anil Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: In medicine, a detailed prognosis about the disease, obtained from any imaging technique, facilitates the effectiveness of clinical procedures performed by the medical practitioner. In accordance to that, a good quality image not only consists of high-information content but also has a superior visualization. Therefore, it is necessary to curb the unwanted disturbances, i.e. noise present in the image. In the wake of such requirements, this paper introduces a denoising technique that employs variational mode decomposition along with the spatial filters to its intrinsic mode functions for noise suppression and enhancing the quality of images. The algorithm utilizes the region-concentric filtering for edge preservation of the diffusion filter. The conductance function included in the study is biased towards diffusing in wider regions rather than in smaller areas. The parameters here are optimized to relinquish the hand-driven operation of assigning values, for boosting the performance of the algorithm using the Harris hawks and particle swarm optimization technique. The effectiveness of the proposed method is measured by involving the peak-signal-to-noise-ratio (PSNR), mean square error (MSE), feature similarity index (FSIM), and structural similarity index (SSIM) as the assessment metrics. The assertion towards the quantitative and ocular superiority of the technique in image denoising and information retention is fair, because the algorithm attains an average decrease of 89% in MSE, a 61% rise in PSNR, a 63% increase in FSIM, and a 445% increase in SSIM, when compared with different denoising algorithms for a range of noise levels.

title: Detection of Corrosion Types on Ship Hull Surfaces Based on Unmanned Vessel Inspection
authors: Fei Zeng, Sheng Feng, Xiaochuan Zhang, Yukai Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering, Materials and Automation Technology (MMEAT)
abstract: To achieve safe and precise detection of hull surface corrosion, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv8-based algorithm for identifying hull corrosion types, utilizing unmanned surface vehicles (USVs) as the inspection platform. To meet task requirements, modifications were made to the backbone and neck network structures of the original model, along with optimization of the loss function. First, the backbone network of YOLOv8 was replaced with DCNv4 modules, leveraging deformable convolutional kernels to adaptively adjust the receptive field shape. Second, the BiFormer attention module was introduced into the neck network to enhance feature response through dynamic sparse attention mechanisms, ultimately outputting three refined multi-scale feature maps. Finally, the EIoU loss function was employed to optimize bounding box regression, improving both the accuracy and speed of corrosion size detection. The experimental results demonstrate that the improved DCN-YOLOv8 algorithm achieves a significant enhancement in detection accuracy compared to the original algorithm, better meeting the requirements of hull corrosion detection tasks.

title: A Planning Method for the Flexible Evolution Path of Distribution Networks with Large-Scale Integration of PV, Storage, and Charging Systems
authors: Kuanxiang Zhao, Jian Wang, Qingsheng Li, Xuepeng Mou, Dongjunming Yang, Zhen Li, Zejie Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: With the rapid rise in the penetration of distributed resources-exemplified by photovoltaics, energy storage, and charging infrastructure (PV-storage-charging)-in distribution networks, traditional planning methods have become inadequate to cope with the resulting high-intensity uncertainties and operational challenges. To address this issue, this paper proposes a scenario-driven adaptive flexible evolution path (SAFEP) planning method for large-scale access of PV-storage-charging. The method models the long-term development of a distribution network as a multi-stage dynamic optimization problem and, by integrating scenario analysis, risk quantification, and dynamic programming, seeks an optimal sequence of technology investments that balances economic efficiency and reliability. Based on real operational data from a regional power grid in Guizhou Province, we conduct experimental validation. The results show that, compared with reactive problem-driven planning (RPP) and static long-term target planning (SLTP), the proposed SAFEP method reduces the total cost over the entire planning horizon by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4. 5 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0. 0 \%}$</tex>, respectively; reduces the standard deviation of cost fluctuations under future uncertainty by 64.3 % and 52.0 %; and significantly lowers the risks of voltage violations at critical nodes and line overloading. The study demonstrates that the method can provide scientifically sound and effective decision support for the flexible and adaptive upgrading of modern distribution networks.

title: The Analysis of Electric-Thermal Coupled Transient of a Schottky Diode Employing Epitaxial Layer Dynamic Resistivity Simulation
authors: Khairul Ikhsan Bin Yahaya, Rüdiger Weber, Marc Schikowski
abstract: To virtually estimate the current limit of a Schottky diode during an I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FSM</inf> test, it is necessary to perform a transient electric-thermal simulation. Existing modelling approaches are ineffective in estimating the temperature gradient of the die. In this work, the epitaxial layer dynamic resistivity is used in electric-thermal coupled transient simulation as a temperature-dependent Ohmic resistance. The dynamic electrical resistivity of the epitaxial layer was obtained by curve fitting calibration based on real test data. The iterative calibration started with tabulating the Vf from test data against the temperature. Steady-state electrical simulations were performed to obtain the initial estimation of epitaxial layer dynamic resistivity. Transient electrical-thermal simulations were then performed for the next iterations until the control conditions were reached. Two control conditions were set for the calibration iteration to end. The first condition is at the current when a real diode has already failed the IFSM test; the maximum temperature on the die is expected to exceed the solder melting point. The second condition is that the trend of the anode voltage from the simulation for all data points is required to match the Vf from the test data. As Vf and temperature are interdependent, an appropriate increase in Vf corresponding to the rise in test current accurately indicates the temperature rise. Based on simulation results as well as measured Vf data in the range between 1.3 V and 1.7 V at room temperature, the electrical resistivity of the epitaxial layer shifts to a lower value by a factor of about 0.26 when the junction temperature reaches 145°C. Then, it gradually shifts to another lower value, potentially reaching saturation by a factor of approximately 0.26. The simulation results are validated against experimental observations of the first solder melting using a high-speed camera. Simulation results with a higher junction temperature also indicate that the failure mechanism is predominantly determined by the periphery of current crowding, regardless of die size. The simulation results in this study demonstrate that the dynamic shift in epitaxial layer resistivity may be represented as temperature-dependent function, which can be employed in electric-thermal coupled simulations. The simulation outcomes will facilitate the design of novel diodes.

title: Air Quality Forecasting Using a Hybrid BiLSTM and XGBoost Model with Indoor and Outdoor Comparative Analysis
authors: Yeshi Lhazom, Anusha Seles S, Suganya R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Air pollution is a serious global concern that continues to intensify as industrialization and urban growth escalate. The implementation of preventive and control measures of air pollution necessitates a reliable forecasting method. This study introduces a hybrid Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) network with Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) for the prediction of air pollutants. The model was applied to city-level data from Chennai and data from VIT Institute Chennai (Indoor and Outdoor) with air pollutants as features, alongside meteorological features such as temperature and humidity. The indoor and outdoor data were collected using MQ135 and DHT11 sensors, deployed under different occupancy conditions, while meteorological and air pollutant data were obtained from the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB). The temporal dependencies of data were captured with BiLSTM, while residual errors were corrected by XGBoost to strengthen. This ensemble technique demonstrated that the proposed approach achieves higher performance compared to standalone models. This research not only validates the influence of meteorological factors on pollutant behavior but also provides a useful solution for both small- and large-scale air quality forecasting and monitoring. The findings offer practical insights that can be implemented for urban planning, developing effective public health policies, for air pollution management, and contribute to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

title: Design of a Meteorological Monitoring Device for Photovoltaic Power Station
authors: Yubin Ye, Chengbin Ye, Minghui Xiao, Shijie Ling, Junhao Gong, Jiaxin Wu, Ziheng Wu, Huangshen Luo, Jiaquan Xu, Xitao Ling
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: In response to the urgent demand for fine meteorological data in photovoltaic power plants, this article proposes a photovoltaic power plant meteorological monitoring device. The device uses a non-contact ultrasonic phase difference sensor to measure and obtain real-time wind speed and direction data, effectively overcoming the shortcomings of mechanical sensors such as easy wear and poor anti-interference. The collected data is uploaded in real-time to the cloud platform server through the 4G-DTU IoT communication module, and users can access visual and historical data through the cloud configuration interface. The actual test results show that the device can stably measure wind speeds ranging from 0-13 levels (0-40 m/s) and wind directions in all directions (0-360 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }^{\circ}$</tex>). This solution meets the meteorological monitoring requirements for long-term stable operation of photovoltaic power plants, and provides real-time and reliable data support for intelligent operation and maintenance, disaster warning, and efficiency optimization of the power plant through cloud data integration.

title: Aerosol Jet Printing of Copper Nanoparticles Ink by Controlling the Wetness of Aerosols
authors: Cheng Zheng, Alfred A. Zinn, Chee Lip Gan
abstract: Aerosol jet printing (AJP) is one of the advanced 3D printing methods for electronics. Generation of tiny aerosols from nanoparticles ink and deposition of aerosols are the key to achieving high resolution and precise printing. The effect of aerosol properties on the printing performance needs to be understood for designing the ink and printing conditions. This paper quantified the wetness and sizes of aerosols deposited by AJP of copper nanoparticles ink and investigated approaches to control the wetness of aerosols through printing conditions and ink composition. The printing processes and film properties could be effectively bridged with the knowledge on controlling aerosol characteristics and their effect on printed structures. By including less volatile solvent in the ink formulation, this study fabricated continuous and conductive micro-scale copper path.

title: Research on Hierarchical Collaborative Scheduling Method for Multi Energy Systems Considering Peak Shaving Initiative
authors: Juan Li, Yuejuan Yu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: We propose a hierarchical scheduling strategy for wind-solar-hydro-thermal-storage systems. The upper layer smooths net load and maximizes storage arbitrage by coordinating charging/discharging, reducing peaks and valleys seen by thermal units. The lower layer economically dispatches thermal and renewables, minimizing cost and curtailment while enforcing incentive constraints so every participant profits from deep peak shaving. Tests on a modified IEEE 30-bus grid cut total cost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 5-7\%$</tex> and sharply reduce wind curtailment.

title: Intelligent Fault Identification and Lightweight Deployment for Vehicle Power Systems Using LightGBM
authors: Chunping Niu, Yiqi Li, Yongliang Ni, Jie Zheng, Weiqiu Zhang, Guoyan Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: The reliable operation of the vehicle power system is crucial to the success of the task. However, these systems are prone to various potential failures under harsh operating conditions. To meet this challenge, we have proposed an intelligent diagnostic framework based on LightGBM. Our method converts time series sensor data into static feature vectors through sliding window segmentation and feature flattening technology. Combined with the lightweight deployment scheme, it realizes high-precision identification and diagnosis of a variety of fault types. The experimental results show that the trained model can be directly deployed for intelligent analysis of vehicle data to accurately locate the fault time window. This study provides an efficient and practical solution for equipment maintenance in the on-site environment.

title: AI Agents in Cloud-Based Healthcare Enhancing Patient Care Through Intelligent IT Services
authors: Pranith Kumar Reddy Myeka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The collaboration of artificial intelligence (AI) agents into cloud-enabled healthcare systems has redefined the traditional care process by allowing real-time service delivery, predictive diagnosis and data exchange among medical parties. In this paper, we introduce an Intelligent Agent Framework (IAF) based on multiagents that utilizes cloud-native deep learning architecture to improve clinical workflows and personalize care services. The framework utilizes context-aware reasoning and adaptive learning to analyze diverse health data sources such as Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), electronic health records (EHRs) and cloud services and provides scalable capacity and low-latency processing in mobile and changing healthcare locations. In addition, the model includes an XAI module ensuring interpretable insights guiding decisionmaking by agents, hence enabling trust from clinicians and patients. Through extensive experimentation conducted on benchmark healthcare datasets, such as MIMIC-III and PhysioNet, we show that our approach performs significantly better than the traditional cloud-based analytics with up to 95.6% diagnostic accuracy, 94.8% precision, and reduced inference time by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 21 \%$</tex>. This work demonstrates the role of intelligent cloud agents in the improvement of patient outcomes, proactive interventions and the foundation work of future cloud healthcare services.

title: V-RAG: Competitive Tree Reranking and Static Distillation for Answer-Source Alignment
authors: Hanyan Zhao, Wenhui Lin, Shengrong Cai, Fan Duo, Jie Yang
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become a mainstream paradigm for enhancing the factual consistency of large language models (LLMs). However, existing RAG frameworks still face two major challenges: improving retrieval recall to provide more relevant context for generation, and reducing hallucinations in LLM-generated answers. In this paper, we propose V-RAG, a novel RAG framework augmented by a validation mechanism. In the reranking stage, V-RAG employs a multi-round filtering process based on a competition tree structure. An unfinetuned LLM is used to eliminate redundant documents from the initially retrieved set, enhancing contextual relevance while reducing computational overhead. In the generation stage, V-RAG introduces a three-way answer evaluation module that classifies responses into acceptable, hallucinated, or irrelevantly cited. It also dynamically updates prompt strategies based on feedback. To improve deployment efficiency, we further distill the 0.6 B -scale validator into a lightweight BERT-based classifier. We conducted experiments on two Chinese datasets: the CRUD_data and the Chinese tax and finance QA dataset. The results demonstrate that V-RAG outperforms multiple baselines in terms of recall and answer similarity (generated answer vs. standard answer), without requiring large-scale model fine-tuning.

title: An Efficient GRU Based Approach for Next Word Prediction
authors: Richa Gupta, Rohit Rawat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The world has advanced technologically, with new inventions emerging that are better than the ones that came before. One such invention is the smartphone, which is used daily by almost everyone for purposes such as taking pictures, browsing the internet, texting others, etc. Typing text is one of the things that people use most frequently, and it can take a long time to write every word. Word prediction is used to make people's lives easier by displaying the word even before they finish it. It also helps to avoid vocabulary errors and increase productivity at work. This is categorized as natural language processing.
