In, this paper we talk about the next word prediction models that can help to predict the next word and compare them to find the model with higher accuracy. The models are Long Short-Term Memory Network (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and Bi-Direction LSTM. The libraries used are NumPy, keras, TensorFlow, nltk, regex, pickle and matplotlib.

title: Explainable Ai for Encrypted Traffic Detection: a Comprehensive Survey
authors: Xinyue Zhang, Man Hua
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: With the rapid proliferation of encrypted network traffic, traditional signature-based and port-based detection methods have become ineffective against encrypted protocols such as HTTPS, TLS, and VPN. Deep learning models have achieved remarkable success in encrypted traffic classification due to their ability to learn complex hierarchical representations. However, these models suffer from a lack of transparency, as their decision-making processes remain largely opaque, limiting their reliability and applicability in critical cybersecurity environments. Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has emerged as a powerful solution to address this interpretability challenge. By integrating techniques such as feature attribution, visualization, surrogate modeling, and counterfactual reasoning, XAI enables human-understandable explanations for deep learning predictions. This paper provides a comprehensive review of recent advances (2023-2025) in XAI-based encrypted traffic detection. The review systematically analyzes representative approaches-SHAP, LIME, attention visualization, counterfactual explanation, and GNNExplainer-across different model architectures (CNN, Transformer, and GNN). A comparative analysis of ten contemporary studies reveals that XAI not only enhances the interpretability and reliability of deep models but also improves anomaly detection and policy optimization in encrypted networks. Finally, we discuss open challenges including real-time interpretability, lightweight deployment, cross-model fusion, and human-AI collaborative security analysis.

title: Advanced Deep Learning for Binary Fake News Classification Across Diverse Dataset
authors: Dhanush Gogineni, Pratyusha Das, Aakanksha Medasani, Meghana Nangireddy, Anupoju Tejaswi, Murali Krishna Enduri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The rise of fake news is a serious threat to public conversation and trust in society. This research tackles the issue of detecting automated fake news by creating and testing advanced deep learning models that classify news as either Fake or True across three different datasets: Fakeddit, LIAR 2, and Fake News Classification. The study uses a Multi-Task Learning(MTL) approach with RoBERTa for analysing test data, particularly focusing on the LIAR 2 dataset, and takes advantages of rich metadata to boost performance. For detecting fake news in a multimodal context on the Fakeddit dataset, a hybrid BERT+ResNet model is used to combine both text and visual information. Additionally, various machine learning and deep learning models are evaluated on the purely textual Fake News Classification dataset. The results show that RoBERTa(MTL) performs exceptionally well on LIAR 2, highlighting the benefits of using additional information. Meanwhile, the BERT+ResNet model offers a solid framework for handling multimodal data, but its performance on Fakeddit reveals the challenges of merging different types of information and spotting inconsistencies between text and images. On the other hand, the Fake News Classification dataset, which has simpler binary labels, shows high performance across a variety of models. This comparative analysis emphasizes how the characteristics and complexity of datasets significantly influence model effectiveness, offering valuable insights for developing future fake news detection systems.

title: Review of Novel Wind Power Generation and Key Technologies Exploring
authors: Shuai Feng, Bin Song, Shuju Hu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Wind power generation is a crucial solution for optimizing global energy schemes and mitigating climate change. However, the traditional horizontal axis wind turbine faces significant challenges including large size and weight, as well as in manufacturing and installation. Against this backdrop, numerous countries worldwide are actively exploring novel wind power technologies distinct from conventional approaches, aiming to achieve higher efficiency, lighter weight, and lower-cost solutions. To enhance wind energy utilization efficiency and broaden application scenarios, emerging technologies such as multi-rotor wind turbines, high-altitude wind power, diffuser wind turbines and triboelectric/vibration-electric wind power have garnered significant attention in recent years. This paper summarizes the technical characteristics and development progress of various novel wind power generation technologies. It reviews research advancements in key areas such as theoretical principles, integrated design, and aerodynamic simulation analysis for innovative wind turbines. The analysis examines the development trends and potential opportunities presented by these emerging technologies, offering insights and references for future innovation and sustainable development in the wind power field.

title: SAR Ship Target Detection Model Deployment Optimization Method for Real-Time Embedded System
authors: Bingzhi Hou, Panzi Zhao, Xiaodong Yang, Daiwei Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: This paper introduces a real-time ship detection system of synthetic aperture radar (SAR) based on resourceconstrained embedded communication platforms such as unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) and space-based radar. In the overall architecture design, the general model is used as the teacher model. Lightweight modules and multi-layer deep supervised distillation training methods are designed to train and optimize the the Lightweight UNet model for SAR images(LUSNet). It is also optimized for the architecture of self-developed spacebased embedded chips to balance performance and efficiency. The experimental results show that the model based on the proposed method achieves a good balance between detection accuracy and decision speed. This method provides an effective way for realtime applications in resource-constrained environments.

title: LoRa Collision Decoding Algorithm Based on Multi-Symbol Peak Phase Consistency
authors: Jiajia Chen, Lin Zheng, Chao Yang, Zhenghong Liu, Yuwei Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Collision decoding is a critical challenge in LoRa's multi-user access. This paper proposes a novel collision decoding algorithm based on multi-symbol phase consistency detection. The peak phases of adjacent symbols are analyzed under aligned and unaligned demodulation windows conditions. Phases consistency among adjacent symbols persist in aligned windows even under Doppler effects, while ones in unaligned windows demonstrate statistically significant inconsistency. In addition, detailed implementation steps are provided for the phase-consistency-based collision decoding algorithm. Through simulations, the proposed algorithm demonstrates both better multi-user collision decoding performance at low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and greater robustness to inter-user delay differences compared to the state-of-the-art AlignTrack algorithm.

title: Detection of Pneumonia and Tuberculosis from Chest X-Ray Images Using Deep Learning With a Streamlit Interface
authors: Clement Infant Raj, Sebastian Terence
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Lung illnesses like pneumonia and tuberculosis remain a world health issue, especially where access to radiologists is low, mostly in the developing countries. Primary diagnosis (manual inspection of chest X-rays) is subjective and liable to delays. The present research will present an automated diagnostic system to identify pneumonia and tuberculosis based on deep learning and machine learning models that will be deployed in real-time within a Streamlit system. They were five models, which were applied and compared: (i) LightGBM on the basis of eigendomain (PCA) features, (ii) a sequential Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), (iii) XGBoost, (iv) Densenet121, and (v) MobileNetV2 as a transfer learning-based solution. The curated chest X-ray dataset was tested on the models, and their performance was measured in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. The experimental findings indicate that tree-based ensemble algorithms including LightGBM, XGBoost, and Densenet121 have competitive performance with comparatively small computational cost, deep learning models including CNN and MobileNetV2 have better performances with respect to recall and ROC-AUC, which is why they are more useful in reducing false negative in critical diagnostic environments. The suggested Streamlit interface shows how complex models can be converted into a useful tool in the work of clinicians by providing an interactive and user-friendly interface where images can be uploaded, analyzed, and visualized. This combination not only guarantees a higher diagnostic performance, but also transparency and accessibility, and, thus, closes the gap between research-grade models and actual clinical implementation.

title: Regulatory and Quality Requirement Elicitation Framework for the Sustainability of the Biometric Payment System in Malaysia based on Test Driven Development
authors: E Long Chua, Ahmad Husaini Ahamad Zakeri, Wan Muhammad Wafi Wan Arit, Ashok Sivaji, Azahari Bin Hassan, Adly Izmir Bin Ramly
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: As the usage of biometric payment systems continue to rise globally, it is important to understand the localization strategies before the design, development and deployment of these systems in Malaysia. Although conventional payment systems like physical cash, paper-based cheques credit and debit cards continue to dominate the Malaysian market, these system face challenges such as fraud risk, slower processing times and the need for extensive infrastructure making them less sustainable and less efficient than newer digital payment methods. This study introduces a new biometric payment system known as palm payment to enhance payment management for the Malaysian merchant and payee ecosystem. The main objective of this study is to elicit the regulatory and quality requirements for the pam payment as part of the software development lifecycle based on a test-driven development approach. Results of the elicitation forms the requirement framework for the solution integrator (developer) to design and develop a system that is sustainable to be deployed in a mass scale in Malaysia. From the first 2 sprints of Agile development, the results of the proof of concept looks promising. Following this further testing and development will take place based on this quality and regulatory requirement framework to ensure the palm payment system is sustainable for the Malaysian payment ecosystem.

title: Efficient Pruning in Subset Sum Backtracking Using Residual Sum Checks
authors: Ayush Upadhyay, Kaja Mohideen A, Senthil Prakash PN
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The Subset Sum problem is a classical NP-complete problem with applications in resource allocation, cryptography, and combinatorial optimization. Conventional backtracking methods search the solution space by either adding or removing elements recursively and pruning by bounding conditions including surpassing the target sum. Nevertheless, these techniques can still explore huge areas of state space that are not necessarily needed. A new pruning criterion is proposed in this research work based on residual sum checks. Particularly, when the amount of remaining unexplored elements (r) is equal to the difference between the target amount (m) and the amount already explored (m − s), or when the amount of remaining unexplored elements (r) is itself equal to the target amount (m), then a valid solution can be immediately asserted without future exploration. This improvement also avoids duplicated computations through problem-oriented lookahead by narrowing down the search tree. We rigorously prove the correctness of the pruning strategy, compute the effect of its implementation, and compare the resulting algorithm to the classical one. On synthetic and benchmark data, experimental assessments reveal that our method reduces dramatically the number of visited nodes whereas ensuring precision. The algorithm is generalizable and can be applied to other similar combinatorial search problems, like Knapsack and Partition. This work provides both theoretical refinement and a practical enhancement to the exact algorithms with time-expenses.

title: A Method for IoT Topology Measurement and Vulnerability Adaptive Construction for Heterogeneous Devices
authors: Xiaodong Shi, Yu Huang, Fengming Sun, Lingtao Zhou, Dong Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: With the widespread deployment of the Transportation Internet of Things (IoT), covert video surveillance systems have been extensively deployed across urban intersections, industrial parks, and border outposts, serving as critical infrastructures for information acquisition and security control. However, due to characteristics such as hidden deployment, heterogeneous protocols, and dynamic connectivity, these systems are vulnerable to penetration attacks, often lacking effective mechanisms for real-time topology awareness, risk evaluation, and dynamic response. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an integrated security framework that combines multi-source modeling, adaptive risk assessment, and reinforcement learning-based defense. First, a dynamic topology recognition model is constructed using Graph Attention Networks (GAT) to integrate protocol features and communication behaviors. Then, a customized risk function targeting covert nodes is designed, and an SIR-based propagation model is introduced to achieve quantitative perception and control of high-risk paths. Finally, an RL-Defense strategy is developed to perform resource-aware dynamic interception. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms traditional rule-based and graph inference defenses in terms of topology reconstruction accuracy, attack mitigation efficiency, and system recovery performance, showing strong generalizability and practical value.

title: Bridging Research and Practice: A Step-by-Step Methodology for Building Lighting Optimization
authors: Nuril Azmi Muslimin, Nurulafiqah Nadzirah Mansor, Hazlie Mokhlis, Mohd Noor Abdullah, Hushairi Zen, Siti Syafinah Ahmad Hassan
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Lighting systems play a critical role in building performance, with a significant impact on energy consumption, visual comfort, and occupant well-being. Over the past two decades, research on lighting optimization has advanced considerably, supported by simulation tools and intelligent controls. However, most studies remain theoretical or simulation-based, with limited emphasis on how optimized results can be implemented in real building environments. The absence of structured methodologies for post-optimization implementation has created a gap between academic research and industry practice. This paper addresses that gap by presenting a structured 12-step procedure for the design, optimization, and implementation of lighting systems in buildings, originally proposed in IEEE Access. The procedure provides clear guidelines for designers and contractors, covering luminaire selection, layout planning, dimming optimization, and energy analysis. Preliminary results from simulation studies suggest that the framework can achieve compliance with lighting standards, improve illuminance uniformity, and reduce energy consumption. By following this structured approach, optimized designs can be more effectively translated into practice, ensuring that benefits demonstrated in research and simulations can be realized in actual building projects.

title: A Multi-Stage Orderly Charging Scheduling Strategy for Electric Vehicles Based on Time-of-Use Pricing
authors: Yujie Chen, Jiaqi Li, Jiamin Feng, Yongshi Lu, Jialv Li, Xingyu Zhuang, Lixiao Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: Uncoordinated charging of large-scale electric vehicle fleets strains power grids and increases user costs. This paper proposes a multi-stage orderly charging scheduling strategy for electric vehicles based on time-of-use pricing to address this challenge. The strategy employs an optimization model to systematically shift charging loads from high-price to low-price periods while satisfying user demand and infrastructure constraints. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to uncoordinated charging, the proposed strategy reduces total user costs by 15.24% and significantly improves grid stability through effective peak shaving and valley filling. This work provides a practical and efficient solution for the harmonious integration of EVs into smart grids.

title: Thick 4 Mils Heavy Cu Wire Ball Bonding Feasibility Study
authors: Edsel De Jesus, Loic Renard, Shei Meng Loo
abstract: Heavy copper (Cu) wire bonding is dominant in high power Integrated Circuit (IC) devices at various packages known for good mechanical characteristics, better electrical resistivity, and excellent cost. Larger wire diameter has more opportunity to replace Gold (Au) and Aluminum (Al) wire for Quad Flat No-lead (QFN) package. This study used 4 mils diameter Cu wire aimed to develop heavy wire ball bonding process on high IC power technology in QFN package. The experimental trials challenge the current machine capability on heavy ball bonder to meet bonding criteria. Capillary tool dimensions and leadframe clamping design are critical for maximizing the parameters limit of the machine for successful and stable bonding. Important wire bonding steps such Free Air Ball (FAB), Ball Bond, Looping formation and Stitch bond were characterized and examined for the bondability test through Design of Experiment (DOE) and Robust Validation (RV) test. Accelerated High Temperature Storage (HTS) was conducted to verify the bonding integrity with thermal ageing by Destructive Physical Analysis (DPA) through ball shear and wire pull test. In this paper, the wire ball bonding development on 4 mils copper wire showed remarkable results using current auto ball bonder but it requires modification and system upgrade for manufacturability.

title: caSPESC2Vyper: Conformant and Automatic Generation from DeFi SPESC Legal Contract to Vyper Smart Contract
authors: Yue Yu, Jiahui Wang, Bo Meng, Dejun Wang
abstract: Decentralized Finance (DeFi) can provide traditional financial services through blockchain and smart contract technology. The generation of DeFi smart contracts from DeFi legal contracts has become a hot topic. However, we found that current approaches for generating DeFi smart contracts from legal contracts fail to ensure conformance between the two. To address this, we propose caSPESC2Vyper, a method to generate Vyper smart contracts from SPESC legal contracts while guaranteeing conformance. First, we define the executable formal semantics K-SPESC. Next, we establish a syntactic structure mapping from the SPESC language to the Vyper language, based on which caSPESC2Vyper is implemented. Finally, we analyze the conformance and demonstrate that caSPESC2Vyper effectively ensures conformance between DeFi legal contracts and Vyper smart contracts.

title: Towards Edge Anomaly Detection: Lightweight Multi-Resolution Spectral Features from High-Bandwidth Vibration Data
authors: Adin Okta Triqadafi, I Gede Made Adnyana Wibawa, Novanto Yudistira, Didik Rahadi Santoso, Setyawan Purnomo Sakti
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Reliable anomaly detection in rotating machinery depends not only on algorithms but also on the quality of the sensed signal. In vibration analysis, the sampling rate fundamentally governs the visibility of fault signatures. High rates capture critical high-frequency anomalies but at the cost of data volume and processing load, whereas low rates enhance frequency resolution but obscure higher-frequency content. Although wideband sensing offers clear advantages, it is often viewed as impractical on embedded platforms with limited computational resources. To address this challenge, This study introduces a multi-resolution frequency-domain feature extraction pipeline that leverages sequential downsampling and fixed-size FFTs to capture both low and high frequency fault signatures. By explicitly enriching the machine learning input space with complementary low and high frequency information, this method improves class separability and potentially strengthens downstream method. The method have been implemented on a microcontroller, achieving real-time operation while consuming only one-third of a one-second measurement window with negligible numerical error. These findings demonstrate that multi-resolution spectral features not only unlock the benefits of wideband vibration sensing on embedded platforms but also provide machine learning models with richer and more discriminative inputs, enabling lightweight anomaly detection at the edge.

title: Wavelet Transform-Assisted Traffic Interaction Graph for Encrypted Traffic Classification with Graph Neural Networks
authors: Yaru He, Jiaqi Gao, Yaxian Hao, Daoqi Han, Yueming Lu, Yaojun Qiao
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: Encrypted traffic classification (ETC) is crucial for network security management in the Internet of Things (IoT). However, existing ETC with Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) methods often rely on dataset-specific parameter tuning to achieve high accuracy, which compromises their generalization. To improve generalization while maintaining high accuracy, this paper introduces a Wavelet Transform-assisted Traffic Interaction Graph (WT-TIG) for ETC with GNN. WT-TIG represents encrypted traffic as a graph, where the number of unidirectional traffic segments (UTSs) is dynamically and adaptively determined by utilizing the short-time nature of the wavelet function, thereby enhancing the semantic richness and generalization of the WT-TIG. The GNN model captures both local and global topological characteristics, improving feature representation and classification accuracy. The proposed method achieves over 98% accuracy on two datasets (self-collected IoV, public CIC-IoT-2022), outperforming the state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Comprehensive Survey of Machine Learning Techniques for Parkinson's Disease Prediction: Trends, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Krithiga L, Feroz Khan A. B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease (PD) is a complex and progressive neurodegenerative disorder that significantly impacts motor and non-motor functions, necessitating early and accurate prediction to improve patient outcomes. In recent years, machine learning (ML) has emerged as a powerful tool in the prediction and monitoring of PD by analyzing diverse biomarkers including voice, gait, handwriting, and neuroimaging data. This survey paper presents a comprehensive review of current ML-based approaches for Parkinson's Disease prediction, classification, and progression monitoring, covering work published between 2019 and 2025. Categorize the literature based on input modalities, learning paradigms (supervised, unsupervised, deep learning, ensemble methods), and feature engineering techniques. Emphasis is placed on voice-based diagnosis, wearable sensor data, imaging modalities, and hybrid fusion models. The paper critically discusses the strengths and limitations of existing studies, benchmark datasets, performance metrics, and real-world deployment challenges such as data heterogeneity, interpretability, and model generalization. Finally, Identify open research gaps and propose future research directions, including multimodal data fusion, explainable AI, and integration into telemedicine frameworks.

title: Research on the Construction and Application of a Grid Investment-Indicator-Target Conduction Model
authors: Xiaoyong Yang, Chi Zhang, Qi Ying, Fuyan Liu, Yapeng Tang, Songkun Yang, Qian Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: In recent years, the scale of investment in the power grid has continued to grow. However, the quantitative transmission relationship between investment and operational performance remains unclear. This makes it difficult to answer the question, 'How much investment is needed for optimal performance? Much improvement?” Constructing a scientific transmission model for power Grid investment indicators and objectives can reveal the marginal impact of investment on operational performance and strategic goals has become. This is a key way of improving the accuracy of investment decisions. This article proposes a system engineering-based investment transmission modelling method. This method establishes a threelayer mapping structure of 'investment—Investment Indicator Improvement—Target Achievement” to form a complete logical chain from funds to performance and then to strategic goals. This method uses a unit investment matrix and the marginal improvement coefficient regression model as core, which quantitatively characterizes the benefit transmission paths of different investment types, achieving temporal tracking and rolling optimization, investment benefits. Empirical research has shown that the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> value of the model exceeds 0.8 in the main operational indicator dimensions. The RMSE is below 0.03. The research results demonstrate that investment in distribution networks is the dominant factor in improving safety and power supply capacity. While investment in information technology plays a prominent role in economic goals and enhancing long-term resilience.

title: A Proximal Descent Approach for Low Complexity Detection in Uplink OTSM
authors: Arijit Datta, Rangeet Mitra, Uday Kumar Singh, Vimal Bhatia, K. Venkateswaran
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Orthogonal Time Sequency Multiplexing (OTSM) is a single-carrier modulation technique capable of transmitting high data rates effectively and reliably in fast-changing wireless channels. However, signal detection of OTSM is challenging due to the non-linear relationship between the time domain and the delay-sequency (DS) domain. This article presents a low-complexity proximal descent-based approach for robust signal detection in OTSM systems. The proposed approach iteratively detects the transmitted signal utilizing a hyperbolic tangent-based penalty. An extensive convergence analysis of the proposed detection approach is performed considering the existence of strong convexity and bounded subgradients of the regularized cost function. Analytically, it is shown that the proposed approach has a linear rate of convergence in expectation. The simulation results also indicate that the proposed detection approach significantly outperforms several conventional detection approaches for uplink OTSM systems. Furthermore, the proposed approach exhibits lower computational complexity in terms of floatingpoint operations over several conventional detection approaches for uplink OTSM, which are capable of achieving near-maximum likelihood performance. Hence, the proposed detection approach offers a low complexity and promising solution to achieve signal detection with reliability in OTSM systems, especially beyond 5G wireless communication systems.

title: Big Data-Driven Multilingual and Multimodal Deep Learning Framework for Twitter Sentiment Analysis
authors: Sunil Guguloth, Sirisha Veluri, Raju Muntha, Santhosh Kumar Banka, Sarika Gude, Rambabu Gugulothu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Social media sites like Twitter have spread so fast that huge quantities of multilingual and multimodal content have been produced and sentiment analysis has become an indispensable tool to comprehend the opinion of the population. The paper outlines a cohesive approach to the analysis of large-scale Twitter sentiment analysis with the use of a Big Data-engineered Deep Learning. The architecture combines the Apache Spark and HDFS to process the data in a distributed manner and uses the XLM-RoBERTa and ResNet50 to extract the sentiment of the text and image respectively. It is useful in analyzing multilingual and code-mixed data and is able to extract information in various linguistic backgrounds. The end results of those sentiments are visualized in an interactive dashboard. The proposed solution will be efficient and comprehensive because by incorporating Big Data scalability, Deep Learning accuracy, and visual analytics, the software will yield an efficient and successful multilingual and multimodal sentiment interpretation on social media.

title: FLOW: Federated Large Model for Wireless Traffic Prediction
authors: Ruining Wang, Chuanting Zhang, Haixia Zhang, Liang Zhang, Basem Shihada, Jingping Qiao
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The rapid growth of wireless traffic makes accurate forecasting vital for network management. Centralized learning faces high communication costs, while federated learning struggles with spatio-temporal dependencies and non-IID data. This paper proposes FLOW, a federated large language model framework for wireless traffic prediction. FLOW incorporates a LoRA-based local predictor with lightweight alignment and mapping layers, preserving strong modeling ability under edge resource constraints. Experiments on real-world Milano and Trento datasets show significant gains, reducing MSE by 11% and MAE by 9.5% compared to baselines. Interestingly, smaller models such as BERT and GPT-2 outperform larger ones in most cases, benefiting from LoRA's efficient fine-tuning on limited data. Moreover, FLOW demonstrates strong few-shot learning, surpassing baselines with only 5% of training data, and exhibits robust zero-shot cross-domain generalization. Ablation studies confirm the critical role of federated aggregation and LoRA fine-tuning, highlighting the effectiveness of the proposed framework.

title: Time-Frequency Attention Fusion Network with Wavelet-Transformer for Time Series Classfication
authors: Yanmei Song, Lingyu Zhang, Yuming Yan, Zhiming Li, Meng Yang, Zhongrui Hu
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: Time series classification is essential for many real-world applications such as human activity recognition, healthcare monitoring, and fault detection. In this paper, we propose a novel deep learning architecture that integrates continuous wavelet transform (CWT) with a Transformer encoder through an attention-based fusion mechanism. The model first extracts multiscale time-frequency features using realvalued Morlet wavelets, and then applies a Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) module to adaptively fuse raw and spectral components. These fused representations are passed into a Transformer encoder to capture long-range temporal dependencies. We evaluate the model on the KU-HAR dataset and compare it with standard baselines including CNN, CNNLSTM, and plain Transformer. Experimental results demonstrate that our model significantly outperforms baselines, achieving a classification accuracy of 97.4% and F1-score of 97.1%. The results validate the effectiveness of combining adaptive time-frequency representations with self-attention mechanisms for robust multivariate time series classification.

title: Determining Ripening State of Banana Fruit Using with Different CNN Deep Learning Approach
authors: Manas Bisht, Amit Gupta, Richa Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Banana fruit are rich in fibres, vitamins and minerals which plays great role providing health benefits, in cheap and affordable price compared to other fruits. In production level, edible banana fruits are separated from rotten or overripe, but due to large quantity and less time, some are misclassified and stored with normal once, this can easily damage other normal or fresh bananas when stored together increasing loss, this research aims to provide the solution. In this research, an image dataset of five categories of banana ripening stages were used, i.e. Fresh Unripe or Underripe(freshUnripe), Fresh or Barely ripe(fresh), Ripe (ripe), Overly Ripe (overripe) and Rotten fruit (rotten), having a total of 4935 images. The dataset was used to train different models of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) i.e. ResNet50, AlexNet and MobileNet to classify the category of ripeness with best accuracy and performance in terms of confusion matrix. The Image dataset was taken from Roboflow Universe which was then subjected to Image preprocessing, Data augmentation and Image embedding and later to respective models of CNN. MobileNet model gave best results with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5. 2 4 \%}$</tex> accuracy on Validation Dataset of images, while showing great performance in terms of confusion matrix classification, precision, recall and f1-score.

title: Invited Talk: Time-Varying Mesh Compression
authors: Guodong Chen, Mallesham Dasari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: The proliferation of immersive applications such as telepresence, AR/VR streaming, and 3D digital twins demands efficient capture, compression, and delivery of dynamic 3D scenes. Time-varying meshes (TVMs), sequences of meshes with evolving geometry and topology, are a compact and expressive format for volumetric video, but their high data rates and irregular structure make real-time streaming challenging. This paper presents an overview of the compression of TVMs. The talk also includes two complementary contributions from our group: TVMC, a time-varying mesh compression framework for deformable objects utilizing volume-tracked references, and an extension designed for full, unbounded scene meshes with both static and dynamic parts. Together, these systems demonstrate high compression ratios, low decoding latencies, and robustness to real-world scene complexity, paving the way for scalable, low-bitrate 3D video streaming.

title: A Novel GBSM for Long-Distance Skywave MIMO Communications
authors: Yu Yang, Huan Huang, Jie Huang, Cheng-Xiang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Shortwave communications serve as a critical enabler for global coverage and emergency connectivity in future sixth-generation (6 G) communications systems. This paper proposes a three-dimensional (3D) geometry-based stochastic model (GBSM) designed for ionospheric skywave multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) channel. The model incorporates dynamic ionospheric parameters to adaptively regulate the birth and death processes of clusters in spatial and temporal domains, significantly improving its response to ionospheric disturbances. By utilizing real-world geographic coordinate transformations, the model achieves more accurate representations of moving transmitter (Tx) and receiver (Rx) positions, thereby generating deterministic delay components. These deterministic delays are then combined with randomized delay components to model the total delay of each propagation path accurately. The model undergoes a thorough statistical assessment, and its derived characteristics are validated against empirical data, demonstrating high fidelity and reliability.

title: Significance-Aware Feature Map Compression for Neural Implicit Surfaces Using Video Coding
authors: Kohei Okahara, Takuya Matsuda, Kento Yamazaki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Neural implicit surfaces have demonstrated high performance in both reconstructing accurate geometry and generating novel view images, and they have a potential to be a key component of digital twins. However, the memory footprint of neural implicit surface model has become larger with structural improvements to handle large-scale scenes, complex geometry, or reflective surfaces. Therefore, storing and transmitting those models can be a practical issue, and reducing the memory footprint is required. In this paper, we focus on a high-capacity neural implicit surface model, primarily composed of compression-friendly 2D feature maps, and propose an efficient compression method for the model. The proposed method calculates the significance of each feature map in relation to geometry and applies significance-aware compression. The effectiveness of the proposed method is confirmed through the experiment with one of the latest neural implicit surface models.

title: Employee Promotion Prediction Using XGBoost Algorithm and Hyperparameter Optimization
authors: Ade Chandra Nugraha, Santi Sundari, Aini Nurul Azizah, Helsa Alika Femiani
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Employee promotion is a critical aspect of human resource management, often subjective and time-consuming. This article outlines the application of the XGBoost Algorithm as a Machine Learning approach to build a predictive model for assisting employee promotion processes. The research methodology employs the SEMMA (Sample, Explore, Modify, Model, Assess) approach, beginning with data exploration, feature selection, and model development using XGBoost. The employee promotion dataset from the WNS Analytics Hackathon was used, containing 12 attributes representing promotion assessment factors such as training score, number of trainings, KPI achievement, awards, previous year's employee rating, length of service, department, region, age, gender, education, recruitment channel, and a binary target attribute indicating promotion eligibility. The imbalanced nature of the dataset provided opportunities for optimizing the machine learning model through cross-validation, data sampling (SMOTE), and expanded hyperparameter tuning. Among these optimizations, the best F1-Score was achieved with cross-validation but without SMOTE, combined with Bayesian optimization for hyperparameter tuning. The best result improved the F1-Score from 0.4865 to 0.5168.

title: Residue Free TaN Etch Method for MIM Capacitor in Advanced Packaging
authors: Hexin Zhou, Yun Xiao, Xiaohui Ren, Yushan Chi
abstract: This study presents a novel approach to fabricate Tantalum Nitride (TaN) based Metal-Insulator-Metal (MIM) capacitors, which are essential for enhancing power integrity in advanced packaging applications. TaN is a critical electrode material of MIM capacitors due to its high thermal stability and excellent diffusion barrier property. However, the Ta based residue is a critical challenge for TaN MIM capacitors fabrication. An innovative, residue free TaN etch method is developed by synergistically optimizing plasma etch and wet clean. The plasma etch with highly volatile by-products significantly reduces Ta residues. Combining a lift-off wet clean, this etch method can realize residue free TaN MIM capacitors fabrication. The synergistic process development scheme in this study will be inspiring for the fabrication process development of advanced packaging with increasing complexity.

title: Enhancing Latency Performance Using Non-Local Scalable Quantum Neural Network in Ultra-Dense IoT Networks Beyond 5G
authors: Ravi Kumar M, P. S. V. Srinivasa Rao, Jayant Tyagi, Amit Barve, Natrayan L, M. SivaramKrishnan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: In ultra-dense Internet of Things (IoT) over 5G networks, the rapid increase of latency-critical applications like autonomous systems and real-time healthcare has imposed strict demands on low-energy and low-latency computation. Ultra-dense networks are faced with dynamic traffic, large task diversity, and high-density base station deployment, rendering conventional optimization techniques invalid. To mitigate these issues, in this paper, a Enhancing Latency Performance using Non-Local Scalable Quantum Neural Network in Ultra-Dense IoT Networks Beyond 5G (NLS-QNN-SBiA) is suggested. The system initiates with federated data collection from mobile IoT devices and edge nodes, collecting parameters such as task complexity, link quality, and computing capability. The data are fed into a communication-computation model that defines a latency-energy joint optimization problem. Non-Local Scalable Quantum Neural Network (NLS-QNN) uses quantuminspired non-local learning that captures global task relationships to create offloading and resource allocation solutions with low latency, which are then optimized through Swarm Bipolar Algorithm (SBiA). The technique has astounding improvements in key metrics: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 9. 1 2 \%}$</tex> less latency, 99.75% better energy efficiency, 99.36% accuracy of task offloading, and 99.93% resource usage. These are better than state-of-the-art techniques in dynamic environments. In summary, the NLS-QNN-SBiA system offers strong, scalable, and adaptive latency optimization, facilitating real-time responsiveness in next-generation ultra-dense IoT networks and facilitating effective MEC-based orchestration over 5G.

title: LMIF: A Large-Scale Multi-Focus Image Fusion Dataset Based on Smartphones
authors: Qi Hu, Yue Yu, Defang Fu, Zijia Wang, Xiaoxiao Zheng, Shuaiqi Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: In reality, due to the limitations of optical lens depth of field, the images captured by the imaging system only have clear imaging within the depth of field range, and the imaging is blurry outside the depth of field range, making it difficult to focus all targets in the same shooting environment in a single photographic image. Therefore, multi-focus image fusion technology has significant research significance. With the rapid development of technology and the popularization of smartphones mobile phones have become the most convenient and widely used photography tool. At present, the public multifocus data set is mostly collected by professional equipment, which cannot well reflect the photo effect of mobile phones in real environment, so this paper uses devices that are more suitable for real environments to construct multi-focus image datasets based on mobile phones. The dataset was collected from different models of mobile phones and covers most of the mobile phone brands on the market today. In the process of data acquisition, jitter and environmental light will inevitably occur, and at the same time, the collected data will also have the problem of excessive resolution, which is not conducive to the fusion work, so this paper preprocesses the collected data. The dataset is currently divided into three parts: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A}$</tex> (containing 66 pairs of images), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$B$</tex> (containing 63 pairs of images) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C$</tex> (containing 100 pairs of images). The dataset contains two parts: unprocessed data and preprocessed data. The URL of the dataset is: https://github.com/cvmdsp/LMIF.

title: Single-Attribute Controllable Text Generation Based on Continuous Prompt Contrastive Learning
authors: Zongfeng Qu, Xiaochun Ma, Kexin Jiang, Lun Jin, Ruihan Zhu, Bo Wang, Qinghua Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: In controllable text generation, single-attribute control is fundamental. While existing methods using continuous prompts have enhanced controllability, they often absorb unintended features during training, such as style or theme, causing confusion between the learned prompts and the target attribute. This results in generated text reflecting not only the desired attribute but also non-target characteristics. To address this, we propose a novel method based on continuous prompt contrastive learning. By freezing the pre-trained language model's parameters and fine-tuning only the prompt vectors, our approach reduces computational costs and shortens training time. The trained prompts are plug-and-play, directly guiding text generation. Additionally, a masking strategy is introduced to focus the target prompts on learning from opposing attributes, preventing interference during generation. Experiments on sentiment control and toxicity mitigation show our method outperforms baseline models in both text quality and attribute precision. Our approach effectively resolves the critical issue of attribute entanglement in continuous prompt learning, ensuring precise and pure attribute control.

title: INFOD: A Lightweight Detection Algorithm for Foreign Objects on Airport Runwaysin Complex Weather Conditions Based on the Improved NanoDet-Plus
authors: Xiaoyu Sun, Xia Zhuang, Zetan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Foreign object detection on airport runways is crucial for airport safety, especially under the influence of complex weather conditions, which significantly affect detection accuracy. This paper selects a lightweight detection model-NanoDet-Plus-and improves it based on the original model. In the backbone network, a lightweight attention module, ECA-Net, is embedded to efficiently capture channel attention through one-dimensional convolution. A lightweight SE attention mechanism is also added before the fusion path of GFPN to recalibrate the dataset, thereby improving detection accuracy while maintaining the model's lightweight nature. Meanwhile, the Albumentations augmentation library is used to perform data augmentation on images, simulating complex weather conditions to generate a new FOD dataset. Experiments on this dataset show that the improved algorithm's model mAP increased by 8.2%, a 70% improvement relative to the original accuracy. Compared with other algorithms for airport runway foreign object detection, this experiment effectively enhances detection accuracy without increasing experimental complexity, better meeting the accurate and rapid detection needs of airport runway foreign objects. Although this method has achieved a significant improvement in detection accuracy, its robustness under extremely harsh weather conditions still needs further verification. Future work will focus on the real-time deployment optimization of the model on mobile devices and explore its adaptability in a wider range of weather scenarios.

title: Lightweight Deep Learning Model for Multi-Scale Helmet Detection in Construction Scenarios Based on LBi-FPN and CBAM
authors: Yang Du, Qian Chen, JunFeng Li, JingBo Hao, Zhen Liang, DongHui LiJiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Real-time helmet detection is core to construction safety monitoring, critical for preventing high-frequency accidents like falling object impacts. However, existing models face three key deployment challenges: significant helmet scale variation (distant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<32 \times 32$</tex> pixels) with traditional FPNs having poor cross-scale fusion and high computation; construction interferences (uneven illumination, dust, occlusion) contaminating features and raising false/missed detection rates; and hard accuracydeployment balance (heavy models fail on edge devices like surveillance-embedded GPUs, lightweight ones sacrifice accuracy). This study proposes a synergistic lightweight deep learning framework with three modules: 1) LBi-FPN: Integrates bidirectional feature interaction and depthwise separable convolutions, solving multi-scale channel mismatch, reducing fusion computation, and boosting mAP@0.5 by 3.5% vs. baseline; 2)CBAM (in feature refinement): Uses channel (suppressing dust) and spatial (enhancing helmet edges/colors) attention, improving mAP@0.5 by 2.3%; 3)Differentiated detection head: Applies edge enhancement for small targets and coordinate awareness for large ones, cutting parameters and speeding up detection.Experiments on self-built and public HelmetDet datasets show its mAP@0.5:0.95 reaches 0.905 (4.7% higher for small/occluded helmets). The 18.7 MB model hits 42 FPS on edge devices, realizing cross-scale adaptability, antiinterference, and edge deployment synergy, offering a paradigm for industrial safety monitoring.

title: Reasoning stability improvement for knowledge graph question answering based on gated attention and contrastive graph neural networks
authors: Kaicheng Xu, Siqi Xiao, Chunlei Zhang, Hongxiao Gan, Jingyang Zhu, Jun Liu, Yue Zhao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have recently shown strong potential in modeling structured data and performing multi-hop reasoning, making them effective for knowledge graph question answering (KGQA) tasks. By recursively aggregating semantic information over graph structures, GNNs capture higher-order dependencies among entities and support complex reasoning processes. However, existing GNN-based approaches still face challenges when handling complex queries, particularly due to unstable attention distributions and noisy relational propagation, which can negatively affect reasoning reliability.To address these challenges, we propose an enhanced GNN-based reasoning framework. The framework integrates a gating mechanism and exponential moving average (EMA)–based attention smoothing into a multi-head attention architecture. The gating mechanism is designed to adaptively balance query semantics and structural signals from neighboring nodes, thereby mitigating the influence of redundant relations. During multi-hop message passing, EMA smoothing is applied to stabilize attention dynamics.In addition, a contrastive learning objective is introduced to improve representation discriminability between correct answer entities and irrelevant candidates.Experiments on two public benchmark datasets, WebQSP and ComplexWebQuestions (CWQ), show that the proposed method outperforms representative GNN-based baselines, achieving notable gains in both F1 and Hits@1 for multi-hop reasoning tasks. Ablation studies further indicate that each component contributes to the overall effectiveness of the proposed framework.

title: A Novel Base Station Sensing Performance Testing Scheme Based on Programmable Phase-Shifting and Attenuation Matrix
authors: Yuhang Guo, Feilong Wang, Xu Chen, Zhiqin Wang, Xiaolong Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a key characteristic in 5G-A/6G systems. With the widespread application of massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) technology, the number of radio frequency (RF) ports of ISAC base stations (BSs) increases sharply, posing greater challenges to the construction of sensing performance testing systems. This paper proposes a new target angle emulation scheme based on the programmable phase-shifting and attenuation matrix (PSAM). By introducing a programmable PSAM that connects all the RF ports on the ISAC BS antenna panel, dynamic amplitude and phase adjustment of each link can be performed to achieve the target emulation of different angles and dynamic movements. Simulation results prove that this scheme can significantly reduce the requirements for system sites and instruments while maintaining high angle emulation accuracy, saving nearly one million dollars in construction costs.

title: Anomalyspy: A Generative Defect Localization in Semiconductor Packages, with X-Ray Microscopy
authors: Riko I Made, Pawan Kumar, Pablo Quijano Velasco, Ng Chee Koon, Leong Chang Jie
abstract: Semiconductor packaging reliability is critical for advanced electronics, yet detecting rare defects like voids and misalignment remains challenging due to their subtlety and volumetric complexity. Traditional inspection methods often struggle with 3D X-ray microscopy (XRM) data, necessitating advanced computational approaches. This study introduces anomalyspy [1], a machine learning framework that leveraged Vector Quantized Variational Autoencoder (VQ-VAE) to detect anomalies in XRM images of semiconductor packages. By reshaping the z -dimension into channels (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$z_{\text{kernel }}$</tex>) and using 3D peak detection, we achieve precise defect localization. Trained solely on a minimally defective sample, our model demonstrates robust anomaly detection, offering a scalable solution for semiconductor failure analysis.

title: An End-to-End Class-Aware and Attention-Guided Model for Object State Classification
authors: Filippos Gouidis, Konstantinos Papoutsakis, Theodore Patkos, Antonis Argyros, Dimitris Plexousakis
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Object State Classification (OSC) is a critical task in computer vision, enabling systems to understand the functional state of objects. This work proposes a novel end-to-end architecture for OSC that leverages the inherent relationship between object classification and state recognition. Our approach first classifies the object and then uses object-specific attention mechanisms to focus on relevant features for state classification. This two-stage design allows the model to effectively capture object-state dependencies while maintaining modularity and flexibility. We conduct an extensive ablation study to analyze the impact of key parameters, such as attention mechanisms and loss weighting, and evaluate our method against three baselines across four benchmark datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms competing methods by a significant margin, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

title: Distributed Processing of kNN Queries over Moving Objects on Dynamic Road Networks
authors: Mingjin Tao, Kailin Jiao, Yawen Li, Wei Liu, Ziqiang Yu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: The k Nearest Neighbor (kNN) query over moving objects on road networks is essential for location-based services. Recently, this problem has been studied under road networks with distance as the metric, overlooking fluctuating travel costs. We pioneer the study of the kNN problem within dynamic road networks that account for evolving travel costs. Recognizing the limitations of index-based methods, which become quickly outdated as travel costs change, our work abandons indexes in favor of incremental network expansion on each snapshot of a dynamic road network to search for kNNs. To enhance expansion efficiency, we present DkNN, a distributed algorithm that divides the road network into sub-networks for parallel exploration using Dijkstra’s algorithm across relevant regions. This approach effectively addresses challenges related to maintaining global distance accuracy during local, independent subgraph exploration, while minimizing unnecessary searches in irrelevant subnetworks and facilitating the early detection of true kNNs, despite the lack of constant global search monitoring. Implemented on the Storm platform, DkNN demonstrates superior efficiency and effectiveness over traditional methods in real-world road network scenarios.

title: Deep Learning Model and Location Accuracy Improvement Method for Cable Fault Location Based on Spatiotemporal Feature Fusion
authors: Dongnan Huang, Hui Liao, Minggui Fan, Bin Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Traditional traveling wave methods for cable fault location are susceptible to noise interference, resulting in large location errors. This paper utilizes a dual-stream CNN-LSTM hybrid architecture to collaboratively extract the spatial features and temporal evolution patterns of cable fault traveling wave signals. The spatial feature extraction module utilizes an improved residual dense block and multi-scale convolution, while the temporal feature extraction module learns the temporal dependencies of fault development through a bidirectional LSTM network. Finally, a channel-attention mechanism is used to achieve adaptive feature fusion and weighting. Experimental results demonstrate that this method maintains excellent performance across a signal-to-noise ratio range of 5-40 dB, achieving a mean error error (MAE) of only 2.91 m in a 5 dB strong noise environment. Practical engineering applications demonstrate that this method can shorten the mean fault repair time from 4.8 hours to 2.1 hours, reducing annual maintenance costs by 42.8 %, validating its precise location capability and engineering application value in complex electromagnetic environments.

title: VirtuHeart - A Patient-Centric Digital Twin Approach for Continuous Cardiac Monitoring and Management
authors: Polagoni Sowmya, Dasa Hemanth Kumar, Muntha Raju, Nukala Revathi, Gaddam Vechan Reddy, Pavan Patil
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Prompt diagnosis and continuous care in chronic heart failure remain major challenges. This study introduces VirtuHeart, a comprehensive digital twin platform for personalized cardiac modeling and real-time monitoring, that synthesizes biomechanical simulations, wearable sensor data and cardiac MRI segmentation. The cloud-based system offers interactive dashboards for clinicians and patients to enable early risk stratification and intervention. VirtuHeart advances the field by integrating predictive analytics and patient-specific cardiac metrics that surpass conventional episodic monitoring, demonstrating both precision and scalability for chronic heart failure management.

title: Research on BiLSTM Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Method Based on Meteorological Clustering
authors: Ming Xu, Jiahan Wang, Ze Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: To realize secure and economical grid operation with high penetration of distributed PV systems, this paper focuses on PV power forecasting by reviewing and integrating mainstream methods. A comprehensive framework combining multi-source meteorological data, output characteristic modelling and deep learning is proposed. It first clusters weather factors via K-means, then utilizes BiLSTM to capture temporal critical information of PV output, and finally integrates the two to build an innovative forecasting model for high-precision prediction across weather scenarios. Simulation experiments verify its effectiveness in robust and accurate predictions for distributed PV sites under various meteorological conditions.

title: Double Deep Q-Learning for Adaptive Resource Management in LoRa Networks
authors: Mythily K, Shanija S R, Sridharan D
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT) is transforming modern communication by leveraging Low-Power Wide-Area Networks (LPWANs) to connect a vast number of end devices (EDs). These networks are essential for figuring out End Device capacity and making sure data is distributed effectively. However, excessive consumption of energy, ineffective decision making and higher latency are common problems with current systems. In order to improve resource allocation and lower transmission hazards, this study proposes an optimised Double Deep Q-Learning (DDQL) technique for LoRaWAN. Although DDQL provides intelligent choice-making, network safety is challenged by its computing requirements and data sharing. By enhancing action efficiency and enabling low-power systems operations, the suggested approach strikes a compromise between these trade-offs and improves the overall network speed. Results from experiments show that the DDQL method works better than Random Forest (RF), Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) and K-Means clustering algorithms. The results indicate a total energy consumption of 15.637 J, a high packet delivery ratio (PDR) of 0.98802, and a low packet loss ratio (PLR) of 0.011984, underscoring the efficiency of the proposed model in energy-sensitive IoT scenarios.

title: An Exploratory and Predictive Analysis of Travel Patterns and Demand in Bike-Sharing Systems
authors: Pooja C, Priyadharsini C
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Rental bike-sharing systems are gaining momentum and being introduced in many cities to meet the growing demand for mobility while mitigating the consequences of vehicle use, such as pollution and health problems. It is crucial to understand the demand period when the requirement for rented bikes is high, so that sufficient bikes are stocked. Various factors, including temperature, season, and time of day, influence the number of rented bikes required. To address this important problem, this study conducts an extensive exploratory data analysis to uncover patterns in the data, employs one-hot encoding and cyclical encoding techniques for the time variables, and then performs predictive analysis using models like linear regression, polynomial regression, and random forest to forecast the rental bike count. The approach demonstrated that the random forest model with cyclic encoding and the inclusion of lagged variables outperformed the other models, achieving a 0.95 R-squared value.

title: Systematic Literature Review of Security Challenges in Cloud-Edge Computing: Comparative Analysis of Common Criteria ISO 15408
authors: I Wayan Widi Pradnyana, Mochammad Febriyan Masyhudi, Vega Setiawan, Reyza Fajar Rabbani
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Cloud-edge computing offers real-time processing by distributing computational resources closer to data sources, supporting latency-sensitive applications like smart grids and autonomous vehicles. However, this paradigm introduces critical security issues, including multi-tenancy risks, data sovereignty concerns, and vulnerabilities in federated learning systems. Despite the severity of these threats, the application of structured evaluation standards such as the Common Criteria (ISO/IEC 15408) in cloud-edge environments remains underexplored. This study aims to analyze how ISO/IEC 15408 can be applied to address security challenges in such architectures. A Systematic Literature Review (SLR) was conducted using PRISMA guidelines, reviewing 25 peer-reviewed articles from 2021–2025 across IEEE, Springer, ACM, and ScienceDirect. The review focused on threat classification, affected system layers, impacted cybersecurity attributes, and alignment with ISO/IEC 15408 components, including Security Functional Requirements (SFRs) and Evaluation Assurance Levels (EALs). Results indicate that although some studies address trusted execution, access control, and audit mechanisms, few integrate full assurance practices or protection profiles like VIRT_SDN.1. The findings highlight a need for broader adoption of Common Criteria to improve certification readiness and balanced cybersecurity coverage in cloud-edge systems.

title: An Efficient Betweenness Centrality Algorithm Based on Sampling Strategy
authors: Huaixi Wang, Chen Wang, Xuanling Feng, Dongxu Guo, Shuiqing Tan, Yi Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The robust evolution of informationization in human society, particularly with the proliferation of mobile Internet and Internet of Things (IoT), has led to a substantial increase in network complexity and device inter-connectivity. In order to deeply understand the network properties of the real-world networks, network scientists propose multiple centrality measurements of networks to analyze the network characteristics, which indicates the importance of nodes and edges in the networks. Betweenness centrality is a crucial centrality measurement in the network, however the traditional algorithm for computing betweenness centrality struggles with inefficiency in large networks. In this paper, we introduce an efficient algorithm for betweenness centrality, leveraging a sampling strategy to address the computational challenges due to the large networks. In order to evaluate the algorithm, we introduce the accuracy and efficiency for the proposed sampling algorithm. The experimental results show that the algorithm can make notable improvements in computational performance without significantly compromising accuracy, thereby offering an efficient and scalable algorithm for centrality measurement in complex networks, especially in the scale-free networks.

title: OTABase: Enhancing Over-the-Air Testing to Detect Memory Crashes in Cellular Basebands
authors: CheolJun Park, Marc Egli, BeomSeok Oh, Tuan Dinh Hoang, Suhwan Jeong, Martin Crettol, Insu Yun, Mathias Payer, Yongdae Kim
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Baseband processors (BPs) in cellular devices implement complex radio protocols, and memory corruption vulnerabilities in these implementations can lead to critical security breaches, including remote code execution. Traditional approaches to detecting such vulnerabilities rely on reverse engineering or emulation. However, these methods face significant scalability challenges due to proprietary firmware and architectural complexities. Over-the-air (OTA) testing offers broader applicability but poses challenges in managing UE state, detecting crashes, and ensuring protocol coverage. We present OTABase, an OTA testing framework that enables efficient detection of memory crashes in LTE base-bands by leveraging protocol specifications. OTABase combines three key techniques: a network-side state control mechanism for efficient management of UE states and connections, a specification-guided test case generation targeting memory crashes in NAS and RRC protocols, and a two-phase crash detection oracle utilizing protocol-based liveness checks and manufacturer debug features. Evaluating OTABase on six commercial BPs from three major manufacturers, unearthed seven previously unpatched memory crashes. Among these, three were assigned CVEs, including one out-of-bounds write vulnerability that allows remote code execution. Additionally, we extend OTABase to 5G basebands for PoC, demonstrating its generalizability and practical utility.

title: MAAP: A Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Automated Vulnerability Repair Framework for Python
authors: Junfan Chen, Zhiyuan Fu, Ting Yang, Hongyu Sun, Ruidong Li, Yuqing Zhang
abstract: Automating vulnerability repair (AVR) in Python remains constrained by low accuracy, long feedback loops, and rapidly escalating token spend when large repositories must be reasoned about. These challenges are amplified by the prevalence of multi-file, environment-dependent CVEs whose fixes span configuration, tests, and cross-module semantics. We introduce MAAP, a self-evolving, role-specialized LLM architecture for end-to-end automated patch synthesis in Python codebases. MAAP decomposes a vulnerable repository into fine-grained, dependency-aware subtasks and dispatches them via a contextual-bandit router that jointly optimizes correctness, latency, and cost. An experience-centric knowledge base surfaces successful semantic exemplars and failure signals to prune search. An agent factory dynamically spawns, retires, or coordinates agents when novel patterns or degraded rewards emerge. We evaluate MAAP on 120 real-world Python CVEs. MAAP achieves a 70.3% patch success rate, surpassing the SWE-agent baseline, which has a 56.7% success rate. This improvement incurs a 62.8% higher average token cost, but it results in a $\mathbf{1 5. 5 \%}$ lower cost per successful patch. Ablation studies show substantial drops in success when disabling the router, knowledge base, or cooperative spawning, underscoring the necessity of each component. Collectively, these results indicate that MAAP’s design can deliver robust, largely hands-free vulnerability repair for Python at practical cost envelopes and is readily extensible to other language ecosystems.

title: LoFT-IIoT: A Lightweight Trust Feature Extraction Method for Industrial Internet of Things
authors: Qiuguo Guan, Zhiyu Ren, Qiuli Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) exhibits highly dynamic traffic and tight edge resources, rendering highdimensional, heavy trust-evaluation pipelines difficult to deploy. We propose LoFT-IIoT, a protocol-agnostic lightweight feature extractor that organizes raw variables via a three-way semantic taxonomy (statistical, behavioral, protocol), applies variance prefiltering and mutual-information scoring with optional mRMR redundancy control, and selects a compact subset under perbucket budgets. On DNN-EdgeIIoT and under a unified evaluation, LoFT-IIoT attains Accuracy 93.57%, F1 0.9371, ROCAUC 0.9750, and PR-AUC 0.9591 using only 8 features-about 80% fewer than the 42-feature full set-while cutting featureextraction time by ~ 82% versus an autoencoder baseline (9.03 s vs. 49.92 s) and sustaining millisecond-per-sample latency. The method balances interpretability and resource efficiency, enabling practical trust assessment at the edge.

title: Optimizing Urban Circular Economy Policy Through Artificial Intelligence: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Rezky Kinanda, Ridwan Sutriadi, Nurrohman Wijaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The rapid growth of urbanization has significantly strained natural resources, contributing to environmental issues in large cities. As a result, sustainable development strategies are becoming increasingly important, with Circular Economy (CE) policies emerging as a viable solution. CE focuses on reducing waste, reusing materials, and recycling products, promoting resource efficiency and sustainability. Artificial Intelligence (AI) plays a critical role in advancing CE by optimizing production processes, enhancing waste management, and improving resource efficiency through technologies such as machine learning, predictive analytics, and IoT integration. AI-driven tools also support real-time monitoring and data analytics, fostering smarter decision-making and promoting sustainable practices. Despite these opportunities, several challenges hinder the effective implementation of AI in CE, including data quality, integration with existing infrastructure, regulatory complexities, and public skepticism. This research explores how AI can support urban circular economy policies, highlighting its potential benefits and the obstacles that must be addressed for successful integration. Key to this transformation is the development of strong digital infrastructure, interdisciplinary collaboration, and comprehensive regulatory frameworks to ensure the responsible and effective deployment of AI in urban sustainability efforts.

title: Research on Expert Knowledge-Guided Visual-Semantic Feature Extraction Algorithms
authors: Shengping Yao, Lingxiao Cui, Fei Wang, Yuchen Liu, Weiran Xu
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: Accurate interpretation of complex visual information requires effective extraction of both low-level features and high-level semantic clues, such as abnormal regions and specific patterns. Traditional methods often struggle with this due to visual complexity, making it crucial for models to capture and understand semantic content. This study proposes a three-stage deep convolutional neural network (CNN)-based algorithm guided by expert knowledge for visual-semantic feature extraction. In the first stage, a segmentation model identifies key features in each image; in the second stage, a multi-scale semantic attention-enhanced model assigns probability scores to multiple images associated with a subject; in the third stage, a majority voting strategy is applied to determine the final classification result. Experimental results demonstrate the algorithm's high accuracy and strong capability in semantic interpretation.

title: Establishing Reference Performance for Imbalanced Ensembles: An Empirical Study on 15 Imbalanced Ensemble Methods
authors: Zimo Lei, Jianjun Zhang, Ting Wang, Wing W. Y. Ng, Xinfu Yan, Xing Tian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: We conduct an empirical comparison among 15 ensemble methods on 27 imbalanced datasets using 4 comprehensive performance metrics. The experimental results indicate that SelfPacedEnsemble yields the best overall performance. BalancedRandomForest, UnderBagging, SMOTEBagging, BalanceCascade, and EasyEnsemble yield promising results in terms of several performance indices. These results help establish a new reference performance for future imbalanced learning. Notably, RUSBoost has the worst overall performance compared with the other methods. This contrasts with previous findings, which could be attributed to the fact that previous studies used small and biased datasets, whereas this study used relatively large datasets from various domains. To ensure reproducibility, we release our codes at this URL: https://github.com/Ntriver/imbens-results.

title: MiENet: A Mixture Expert Network for Dynamic Computing Resource Prediction
authors: Mingdong He, Zhaoyi Yang, Chuangye Zhao, Lei Xu, Hao Wei, Bo Li, Xiaobo Huang, Liquan Luo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: Computational resource load prediction is crucial for improving system efficiency and stability in cloud computing, edge computing, and grid computing interface integration. Traditional methods have limitations in multimodal feature fusion, adaptation to dynamic non-stationary environments, and long-range dependency capture, making it difficult to meet the high accuracy and real-time requirements of intelligent scheduling. This paper proposes MiENet, a computational resource load prediction method based on the Mixture of Experts (MoE) and Selective State Space (Mamba) mechanism. This method first utilizes the Mamba attention mechanism (Mamba-Att) to efficiently extract long-range dependencies and dynamic weight distributions among multi-source temporal features, achieving high-dimensional feature refinement and low-complexity modeling. Subsequently, a Mixture of Experts network composed of a convolutional neural network (CNN), a bidirectional long short-term memory network (BiLSTM), and a transformer is constructed. A gating network is used to dynamically route and weightedly fuse different load modes, enhancing the differentiated prediction capability for complex tasks. Finally, a closed-loop mechanism integrating rolling prediction, exponential smoothing correction, and model adaptation is designed to ensure the robustness of the prediction and continuous optimization. Experiments demonstrate that MiENet achieves consistently superior predictive performance, reducing error metrics by over 10% on average compared with strong baselines, thereby providing reliable support for intelligent scheduling and resource optimization.

title: Stability Prediction Method and Implementation for Intelligent Wheelchairs Based on Depth Vision
authors: Xingyuan Wu, Tao Chen, Xindong Liu, Zhengzhong Mi, Fuping Wei, Shuguang He
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Prediction and analysis of wheelchair stability through outdoor roadways using image depth information obtained from depth sensors. The environment is identified and mapped using Palaune and Poisson's 3D point cloud reconstruction techniques. Finally, the obtained recognition data was applied to the human-wheelchair dynamics model to predict and evaluate the wheelchair stability over a masonry floor. The experiments show that the experimental data are in good agreement with the simulation analysis data, which verifies the accuracy of the human-wheelchair dynamics model and the feasibility of the simulation analysis process for a variety of complex real-terrain conditions.

title: TeTRIS: General-purpose Fuzzing for Translation Bugs in Source-to-Source Code Transpilers
authors: Yeaseen Arafat, Stefan Nagy
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Amid the rise of heterogeneous computing and concerns over systems and application security, developers are increasingly embracing transpilers: a growing class of tools for converting code from one programming language into another. As languages differ greatly in constructs, syntactic sugar, security mitigations, and more, transpilers face difficulties in faithfully translating software between source and target languages—sometimes causing outright failures, or worse, subtle-yet-incorrect execution behavior. Proactively testing transpilers' correctness is thus critical to the success of code-translation-oriented development tasks, but unfortunately, no effective techniques currently exist. Although fuzz-testing appears a natural fit, current general-purpose fuzzing tools mostly generate invalid, junk code that fails to engage transpilers' core translation logic; while dedicated compiler fuzzers cannot keep pace with the ever-expanding set of languages targeted by existing and emergent transpilers.
