Thoroughly vetting transpilers' correctness thus demands a fuzzing approach combining the reach of general-purpose fuzzing—with the precision of dedicated compiler fuzzers. This paper presents TeTRIS: a general-purpose fuzzer for testing source-to-source code transpilers. At its core, TeTRIS bridges the flexibility of general-purpose fuzzing, abstracting away language-level differences into a unified interface for fine-grained code mutations, with the precision of compiler fuzzers by rigorously enforcing syntactic and semantic correctness. Relying solely on minimal language specifications, TeTRIS supports fuzzing of any transpiler—irrespective of input or output language—producing high-quality programs that extensively probe its underlying translation logic. In an evaluation against four state-of-the-art fuzzers across seven popular transpilers for C, Go, and Haxe, TeTRIS is the only solution to uphold both high language validity and high transpiler code coverage—whilst supporting the broadest range of transpilers. Moreover, TeTRIS reveals the most code translation bugs—all 12 of which were previously unknown—underscoring its effectiveness in vetting today's diverse transpiler ecosystem.

title: InteractionShield: Harnessing Event Relations for Interaction Threat Detection and Resolution in Smart Homes
authors: Zhaohui Wang, Bo Luo, Fengjun Li
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: The widespread adoption of IoT devices and applications in smart homes has transformed the way we engage with our living environments. While enabling seamless automation and intelligent functionalities, interactions between different IoT applications, typically through trigger-condition-action (TCA) rules, may introduce new interaction threats due to rule conflicts, which sometimes lead to severe security and safety risks. However, existing detection and defense approaches often tackle specific threat categories in isolation, thereby failing to deliver a holistic perspective and robust, comprehensive protection. In this paper, we present InteractionShield, a novel framework that systematically detects and resolves rule conflicts by leveraging a logic analysis model based on event relations. The InteractionShield framework formalizes event relationships, detects event interferences, and classifies rule conflicts. It generates risk scores and conflict rankings to enable comprehensive conflict detection and risk assessment. To address the identified interaction threats, an optimization-based approach is employed to mitigate risks while maintaining system functionality. Evaluated on large-scale real-world IoT datasets, InteractionShield effectively enhances system reliability, offering a robust solution for detecting and resolving rule conflicts in smart environments.

title: Resilient Edge-IoT Architectures by Optimizing Energy, Security and Adaptability : A Review
authors: Shri Janani Muruganantham, N. Ramasubramanian, B. Shameedha Begum
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) networks has accelerated the transition of computation from cloud infrastructures to edge devices, enabling latency-sensitive intelligence in domains such as healthcare, precision agriculture, disaster response, and smart cities. Yet, constrained computational and energy budgets introduce a tri-fold challenge: achieving energy efficiency, embedding hardware-level security, and sustaining adaptability across heterogeneous applications. Prior efforts address these dimensions in isolation custom ASICs, FPGA accelerators, and neuromorphic processors reduce memory and computation overhead; security-oriented designs integrate trusted execution environments, cryptographic co-processors, and side-channel–resilient logic; while domain-specific architectures leverage near-memory computing, dynamic voltage scaling, and lightweight sensor fusion for mission-critical reliability. However, a unified framework that co-optimizes these layers remains missing. This review consolidates advancements across the three directions, critiques their limitations, and outlines future opportunities for hardware-software co-designs with federated learning support, adaptive workload scheduling, and joint energy–security optimization to realize next-generation edge intelligence.

title: Hybrid AI-Driven Content Moderation
authors: Nitheeshwari S, Malar T, R. Abirami
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: User-generated content on online platforms has increased the risks to ambiguous forms of toxic and harmful comments that may occur in forums or other subtle ways online. Existing rule based filtering methods are interpretable but can be fragile, while deep learning language models such as BERT capture the semantic context but may not perform against adversarial spelling words and implicit toxicity. To fill this gap, the hybrid content moderation framework is proposed, it integrates BERT embeddings with fuzzy string similarity scores. This architecture design combines the strengths of the BERT in capturing the contextual meaning and Fuzzy matching detects the manipulated or disguised toxic terms. In addition to increasing the detection coverage, the fuzzy component also contributes an interpretable layer, enabling more transparent and justifiable moderation outcomes. The proposed framework adapts to and strengthens the existing approaches to automated content moderation by supporting the necessary triage of accuracy, robustness, and explainability in a scalable way.

title: Decision-Making During the Runway Inspection by the UAV Group
authors: Illia Marienkov
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: This study presents a decision-making framework for UAV group operations during runway inspection, employing multi-criteria analysis to evaluate alternative courses of action under varying operational conditions. Three decision criteria - Wald’s, Laplace’s, and Hurwicz’s - were applied to operator assessments collected through the expert judgment method. The results indicate that optimal decisions vary depending on the chosen criterion, reflecting differences in operator risk attitudes and situational priorities. The study concludes with perspectives on the integration of UAV groups into airport safety systems, highlighting potential for enhanced foreign object detection efficiency, reduced inspection time, and improved overall runway condition monitoring.

title: Small-Sample Rotor Fault Data Augmentation via Diffusion Generative Adversarial Network
authors: Yue Chen, Chao Shen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: In small-sample rotor fault diagnosis, data scarcity often leads to a decline in model performance. To improve diagnostic accuracy under small-sample conditions, this paper proposes a data augmentation method based on a Diffusion Generative Adversarial Network (DDPM-GAN). First, the denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) is employed to generate high-quality samples, ensuring both physical consistency and diversity of the data. Then, the Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) is utilized to rapidly expand the dataset, addressing the issues of small sample size and class imbalance. Through the combination of DDPM and GAN, the proposed approach achieves higher data fidelity and significantly strengthens the precision and stability of fault diagnosis models, especially in identifying minority fault categories.1. Example section heading.

title: SleepSensei-AI: Revolutionizing Sleep Coaching
authors: Pradeep Nazareth, Shantveer Kesti, Akash Nayak, Madhusudhan G, Pavan Kumar A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Sleep is an essential factor of a human body in terms of health, mental activity, and emotional stability, although contemporary life and environmental stresses have led to the mass sleep deprivation and disturbed sleep patterns. The behavioral therapy and the manual tracking are the traditional methods that are still limited in terms of their availability, personalization, and price. In this paper, the author introduces SleepSenseiAI, an intelligent and cross-platform AI-based sleep tracking and coaching system that is meant to enhance the quality of individual sleep by using multimodal sensing, edge machine learning, and conversational AI. This application is created based on Flutter and Firebase and uses on-device accelerators like the accelerometer and microphone with Google Fit and Apple HealthKit to track and analyze sleep. It also works with sleep stage detection and daytime energy prediction models to work with data in real time whilst preserving user data through offline analysis. An inbuilt virtual sleep coach will provide contextspecific recommendations to assist users in establishing more healthy habits, and the Smart Alarm, Wind-Down Library, and similar features can help users sleep by determining the best time to wake up and encouraging relaxation at bedtime. Conducted on 20 participants over 8 weeks in a pilot study, findings indicated a 20% increase in sleep consistency, 15% decrease in sleep debt, and an overall satisfaction rate of 85.

title: Analysis of Error Root Cause Identification Algorithms
authors: Akash Gaurav, Muralidhara VN
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Root Cause Analysis (RCA) is essential for improving manufacturing systems, particularly under Industry 4.0 standards that emphasize precision, efficiency, and reliability. Errors in manufacturing processes-caused by equipment failures, process variations, or human factors-lead to defects, increased costs, and yield loss. This issue is especially significant in semiconductor manufacturing, where wafer fabrication requires high levels of accuracy and consistency. Recent approaches in intelligent manufacturing employ data mining to automatically detect patterns and causal relationships that impact yield. This paper introduces five advanced sequence analysis algorithms as data-driven methods for identifying tool-related patterns in wafer fabrication data. The extracted patterns enable the localization of critical root cause nodes that drive yield degradation. A comparative evaluation of these algorithms is conducted to assess their effectiveness and efficiency in root cause detection. The results demonstrate the potential of sequence analysis techniques to enhance RCA and improve overall manufacturing performance.

title: Tool-Augmented Multi-Turn Academic Paper Recommendation via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Jiarong Zhang, Junping Du, Zhe Xue, Guanhua Ye, Yingxia Shao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: Recent progress in large language models (LLMs) has reshaped the landscape of academic literature retrieval. However, most existing systems still rely on rigid, stage-wise pipelines with limited reasoning depth, making them ill-suited to capture evolving user intents or maintain precision during iterative exploration. We propose a multi-turn, tool-augmented agent that unifies clarification, retrieval, and filtering within a single decision loop. The agent learns the "clarify–retrieve–filter" process end-to-end, enabling dynamic refinement of search results through dialogue. We first employ an LLM-based user simulator to generate realistic multi-turn trajectories for supervised finetuning, and then introduce a Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) algorithm with process reward masking to address the long-horizon credit assignment problem. To support training and evaluation, we construct AnchorScholar, a 30k-instance dataset in which each sample uses an anchor paper’s references as the ground-truth target set. Built on Qwen3-8B, Qwen2.5-7B, and LLaMA3.1-8B, our agent consistently outperforms strong baselines—including Google, GPT-4o, PaSa-7B, PaperFinder, and SPAR—demonstrating superior adaptability and retrieval efficiency in multi-turn academic recommendation.

title: VCIP 2025 Grand Challenge on Live Broadcasting Video Quality Assessment: Methods and Results
authors: Pengfei Chen, Wenqi Fei, Xinrui Xu, Yuhua Zhang, MohammadAli Hamidi, Hadi Amirpour, Erjia Xiao, Lingfeng Zhang, Zhenjie Su, Hao Cheng, Yu Liu, Wei Zhou, Yanbiao Ma, Renjing Xu, Long Chen, Xiaoshuai Hao, Yipo Huang, Tushar Shinde
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: This paper reviews the VCIP 2025 Grand Challenge on Live Broadcasting Video Quality Assessment. The competition aims to foster innovation in both subjective and objective VQA techniques tailored to live broadcasting videos, addressing the unique challenges posed by live streaming impairments while emphasizing the evaluation of QoE. The grand challenge used live broadcasting database LBVD which consists of 1013 videos focusing on distortion in live broadcasting videos. The competition had 14 participants and 5 teams submitted valid solutions for the final testing phase. The proposed solutions have shown significant progress in areas such as combining traditional feature engineering with deep learning models, achieved state-of-the-art performances for LBVD. Team ATHENA-Live-QoE and Team HZX Force tied for the first position. The dataset can be found at https://github.com/cpf0079/LBVD.

title: A Compact, High-Speed Energy Efficient Silicon Mach-Zehnder Modulator
authors: Feng Xu, Zhenyu Li, Ting-ta Chi, Wen Lee, Xiaojun Yuan
abstract: The design and simulation results of a differential travelling wave silicon photonic Mach-Zehnder modulator (MZM) is presented. At 2V reverse bias, the 3dB electro-optic bandwidth of the modulator with 2.8 mm interactive length is 34 GHz and can meet PAM-4 100 Gbps or PAM-8 200 Gbps application. Due to the high characteristic impedance of modulators (75 Ohm), Its power consumption is 33% lower than conventional 50 Ohm modulators in theory. Its footprint is only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.8 \text{mm} \times 0.09 \text{mm}$</tex> which is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\times 3$</tex> narrower than 50 Ohm one of which the width is typically 0.3 mm. This reported modulator can meet high-speed, high-density and low power photonic communication applications in future.

title: Performance Analysis for IRS-Assisted Wireless Communication and Power Transfer
authors: Fei Xu, Xueyan Cao, Gerile Ge, Shuqi Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: This paper analyzes the performance of the intelligent reflecting surface (IRS)-assisted downlink single-input single-output simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) system. An IRS-assisted SWIPT system model is established, covering the channel, communication, and energy transfer models. Employing the Log-Normal approximation and Gamma approximation methods, the probability density functions (PDFs) of the equivalent cascaded channels of the base station (BS)-information detection (ID) device and BS-energy harvesting (EH) device links are derived. Furthermore, the expressions of the outage probability and the ergodic capacity at the ID or the EH device are obtained. Monte Carlo simulation results show that the PDF and cumulative distribution functions obtained using the two approximation principles agree with the actual values, verifying the impacts of the IRS and power control on comprehensive performance.

title:
ZrO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -Based AFTJs Enabling Sub-1.2 V Switching and Superior Endurance for Synaptic Weight Cells
authors: Minglei Ma, Miaomiao Zhang, Haoji Qian, Xinda Song, Yian Ding, Yan Liu, Jiajia Chen, Chengji Jin, Genquan Han
abstract: We experimentally demonstrate ZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>-based antiferroelectric (AFE) tunnel junctions (AFTJs) with logic-compatible write voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {w}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) down to 1.2 V and enhanced endurance. Nonvolatile resistance switching of the TiN/Ti/ZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sub>/TiN structure is realized through precisely controlled built-in bias (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{\text {int}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). By systematically correlating polarization behavior with interface charge dynamics, we clarify that the tunneling electroresistance (TER) effect is predominantly governed by trapped charge at the ZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sub> interface, while polarization switching plays an assisting role by regulating the trapping and detrapping processes and stabilizing the resistance states. This cooperative mechanism results in a robust and stable <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\text {on}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\text {off}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> ratio. The AFTJs further exhibit excellent conductance tunability with 40 states, low nonlinearity (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha _{\text {p}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha _{\text {d}} = $</tex-math> </inline-formula>–0.52/−1.24), and low asymmetry (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\vert \alpha _{\text {p}} -\alpha _{\text {d}}\vert = $</tex-math> </inline-formula>0.72), which highlights their strong potential for synaptic weight cells. This work provides important physical insights into the resistance switching mechanism of AFTJs and offers guidance for the design and optimization of future FE and AFE memory devices.

title: ICCECE 2026 Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)

title: Fabrication of 2.7-MV Oil-Immersed Tesla-Transformer Secondary Winding
authors: Liang Zhao, Xi Bo Zhang, Rui Li, Xu Sun, Jiancang Su, Jiangchang Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The inter-turn breakdown phenomenon of the secondary winding in Tesla transformers is studied in order to fabricate a 2.7-MV oil-immersed Tesla-transformer secondary winding. Microscopic photos show that the gradual inter-turn discharges are one of the main reasons causing the secondary winding of the Tesla transformer to be open-circuit. General improvements are implemented during the fabrication: 1) the ratio of turn diameter to the inter-turn distance is set about 0.4; 2) a new type of wire, three-layer-insulation wire, is used to replace the original enamel wire; 3) the insulation liquid where the wire is immersed is fresh and periodically purified; 4) the glue is slightly heated to preclude the voids. With these measures, a 3500-turn secondary winding for a pulsed power generator, TPG2000, is designed, fabricated and tested, in order to boost a primary voltage of 900 V to a secondary voltage of 2.7 MV. Application of this secondary winding in TPG2000 shows that the 2.7-MV secondary voltage can be realized and this winding can work reliably for an output power of 40 GW and a pulse duration of 60 ns.

title: Analysis of a Halbach-Array Dual Permanent Magnet Vernier Machine With High Torque Density and Low Loss
authors: Wanshu Yan, Zhengmeng Liu, Hao Ke, Deru Jiang, Qian Chen, Guohai Liu
abstract: This article aims to analyze and design a Halbach-array dual permanent magnet fault tolerant vernier machine (HEDPM-FTVM) that achieves “high-torque, low-loss, and robust fault tolerance.” Unlike conventional surface-mounted permanent magnet fault tolerant vernier machine (SMPM-FTVM), the proposed design employs an alternating-pole rotor structure and embeds Halbach-arrayed permanent magnets (PMs) within the split stator teeth. These innovations enable the machine to attain higher torque while significantly reducing PM eddy current losses. The study proceeds as follows: The topology and operating principles are introduced, followed by building a magnetomotive force (MMF) permeance model to investigate torque enhancement and PM eddy current loss reduction mechanisms. Critical design parameters are analyzed via finite element analysis (FEA) and optimized holistically through multiobjective optimization. Finally, prototype fabrication and testing validate the design and analytical findings.

title: A DFT-based SQM method for GNSS spoofing detection
authors: Yanfeng Hu, Xiangtai Ma, Shaofeng Bian
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) are vulnerable to spoofing attacks due to their inherent weaknesses. While conventional signal quality monitoring (SQM) methods such as classic SQM methods (SQM_Raw) using raw SQM metrics (Delta/Ratio/ELP), moving average based method (SQM_MA), and moving variance-based (SQM_MV ), based on I/Q correlator outputs can identify spoofing during the pull-off stage, their performance is suboptimal—especially in multipath environments, where effectiveness deteriorates significantly or even fails entirely. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a normalized discrete fourier transform (DFT) based signal quality monitoring method (SQM_NDFT) designed for spoofing detection. By applying DFT normalization to conventional SQM metrics (Delta/Ratio/ELP), the method significantly enhances discrimination capability against spoofing attacks without frequency locking. Experiments on open-source datasets and self-collected experimental data demonstrate the method's superior performance especially in dynamic scenarios with multipath interference, where traditional approaches degrade. Delta_NDFT, Ratio_NDFT, and ELP_NDFT demonstrate greater than 92% detection probability at approximately 1% false alarm rate in static overpowered & frequency-unlocked scenario. Besides, the proposed method combines low computational complexity with hardware compatibility, making it highly suitable for real-world engineering applications.

title: On the Prediction of Varroa Mite Infestations in Honeybee Colonies via Acoustic Monitoring
authors: Mahsa Abdollahi, Yi Zhu, Heitor R. Guimarâes, Nico Coallier, Ségolène Maucourt, Pierre Giovenazzo, Tiago H. Falk
abstract: Honey bees play a vital role in global ecosystems and agriculture through their pollination services. However, the Varroa destructor mite remains one of the most damaging parasites affecting honeybee health, contributing significantly to colony decline. Early detection of Varroa infestation is essential for effective beekeeping management. In this study, we propose a machine learning approach for predicting Varroa mite infestation levels based on acoustic analysis of hive recordings. We introduce a set of nine spectral shape descriptors (SSD) computed from conventional and modulation spectrograms to characterize the acoustic environment within hives. We evaluate the predictive power of these features against traditional cepstral features across multiple classifiers, including Support Vector Machines, Random Forests, and k-Nearest Neighbors. Our results demonstrate that the proposed SSDs significantly improve classification performance relative to conventional features across numerous figures-of-merit, particularly in hive-independent test settings. This work highlights the potential of spectral acoustic monitoring combined with supervised learning as a scalable and non-invasive tool for precision beekeeping and early detection of parasitic threats.

title: Reconstructing Sparse-view Indoor Scenes in View Space with Global Monocular Prior Alignment_supp1-3667058.mp4
authors: Cheng Wang
abstract: Although 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has greatly advanced the development of novel view synthesis (NVS) and surface reconstruction tasks, it still faces serious challenges in indoor scenes with only sparse views due to the lack of initialization point cloud. Existing GS-like methods are geometrically initialized using point clouds from Structure from Motion (SfM), and their performance is heavily limited by the point cloud quality. However, in indoor scenes containing large areas of textureless or weakly textured regions, the SfM algorithm struggles to generate point clouds in these areas, especially when only a limited number of input views are available. This situation can cause the GS optimization process to overfit the photometric error, resulting in severe geometric degradation in regions lacking proper initialization. To address the above problem, we propose a novel sparse-view 3DGS method using global scale-aligned monocular depth to initialize Gaussian primitives and optimize them in view space, named VGA-GS. First, we design a global scale-aligned monocular depth initialization strategy, which provides geometric priors in textureless or weakly textured regions while avoiding the local geometric distortions in vanilla alignments. Second, we propose a novel Gaussian primitive representation based on view space and a refinement strategy based on pixel errors, which can constrain each primitive to always be within the field of view where it is observed to be fully optimized. Finally, we extract the surface prior from monocular depth to regularize the rendering depth and normal, and further propagate the constraints of the training view to the neighboring pseudo-views via image warping. Our method achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction performance in both textureless regions and local details for indoor scenes with sparse views. Moreover, it is highly scalable, supporting integration with various 3DGS rasterizers and depth estimation methods. We release the code at https://github.com/XT5un/VGA-GS.

title: Analysis of Landscape Morphological Characteristics of Traditional Villages in Qiandongnan Region Based on Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Yanli Xiang, Cheng Zou, Peiyao Ren, Linfei Fu, Xiaoxiang Tang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The formation of traditional Chinese village landscapes profoundly reflects the long-term interaction between humans and the land, as well as ecological wisdom. Systematically exploring their multidimensional wisdom and elucidating natural adaptive mechanisms serves as an effective pathway for cultural preservation. This study examines 415 traditional Chinese villages in the Qiandongnan region to explore landscape classification systems, morphological patterns, and their natural adaptive mechanisms. Results indicate: (1) Topography and landforms are the dominant factors shaping landscape differentiation, demonstrating the rigid constraints of natural foundations on spatial patterns. (2) Mountain plateau type villages adhere to the site selection principle of “facing the sun while sheltering from the shade, with mountains behind and water in front,” forming a vertical zonation structure of “residential core-terrace farming-forest protection.” Tree-like street layouts and single-nucleus settlement patterns adapt to steep terrain; (3) River valley terrace villages follow the principle of “settling near water and building close to fields,” forming a parallel zonal system of “waterfront dwellings-terrace farming bank protection forests.” Fishbone-patterned streets and multi nucleated, bead-like building clusters adapt to the constraints of elongated terrain; (4) Peak-and-depression karst villages primarily follow the principle of “building settlements around peaks and choosing depressions for habitation,” orming a centripetal layered structure of “depression core- ring-shaped cultivation- peak-and-depression protection.” They adapt to extreme karst topography through grid-like streets and multi nucleated dispersed layouts. (5) Villages exhibit a construction logic of “following mountain contours, aligning with water flow, and leveraging terrain advantages,” revealing a natural adaptive mechanism of “reliance-conformity-utilization-integration.”

title: Wide-range dynamic strain sensing based on dual-frequency fixed slope-assisted chaos BOCDA
authors: Jinglian Ma, Yahui Wang, Haokun Zhang, Na Jia, Yixiu Tian, Lintao Niu, Mingjiang Zhang
abstract: This paper proposes a dual-frequency fixed slope assisted scheme for wide-range and high-precision dynamic strain sensing within a chaotic Brillouin optical correlation domain analysis. The method addresses key limitations in conventional slope-assisted techniques, such as the requirement for multiple pre-analysis Brillouin gain spectra (BGS) and the presence of demodulation empty areas. By utilizing two fixed frequencies on a single slope, the scheme halves the number of required BGS and effectively eliminates errors caused by empty regions. Compared to conventional schemes requiring eight BGS for 8,000 μϵ, it achieves nearly 50 μϵ accuracy using only four gain spectra in the sequential arrangement and two gain spectra in the cross arrangement, reducing errors by approximately 50%.

title: Leveraging Static-Dynamic Scene Parsing to Enhance Progressive Symbolic Reasoning for Video Question Answering_supp1-3665004.pdf
authors: Guanglu Sun
abstract: Video question answering (VideoQA), a critical task in vision-language understanding and reasoning, encounters significant challenges in integrating visual concepts for compositional spatio-temporal reasoning, due to the inherent complexity of video data. Existing methods struggle to effectively capture both static and dynamic visual concepts and establish practical symbolic reasoning, which are essential for answering compositional questions. To address these challenges, we propose a neural-symbolic framework called Progressive Symbolic Reasoning based on Static-Dynamic Scene Parser Network (PSR-SDSP) for VideoQA tasks. PSR-SDSP features two key innovations: (1) the Scene Parser Network, which converts unstructured video data into static-dynamic symbolic representations, extracting structured representations of persons, objects, relationships, and action chronologies. (2) the Symbolic Reasoning Machine (SRM), which conducts top-down question decomposition and bottom-up compositional reasoning. Specifically, the SRM applies a program execution algorithm that integrates static-dynamic visual concepts with predicted programs to deduce the answers. Our results on AGQA Decomp show that PSR-SDSP outperforms existing models in compositional spatio-temporal reasoning. Experiments on the Novel Compositions and More Compositional Steps tasks highlight its potential generalization. Evaluations on datasets with varying degrees of domain differences, including STAR, ActivityNet-QA, and TVQA+, confirm PSR-SDSP's scalability.

title: Suppressed Thermal Conduction to Adjacent Devices and Enhanced Hotspot Confinement in 200 nm SOI MOSFETs at Cryogenic Temperatures
authors: Kosuke Hatta, Takayuki Mori, Takahiro Mori, Hiroshi Oka, Jiro Ida
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: Thermal problems of transistors, particularly self-heating (SH) effects, pose significant challenges for the accurate design of cryogenic control circuits for quantum computers. This study evaluated the SH characteristics of 200-nm silicon-on-insulator (SOI) metal-oxide semiconductor field-effect transistors (MOSFETs) and thermal conduction to adjacent devices at cryogenic temperatures using the four-terminal gate resistance method. A rapid rise in channel temperature of the SOI MOSFET due to SH was confirmed at 3 K. Additionally, it was found that thermal conduction from the heater exhibits greater localization at cryogenic temperatures compared to room temperature. Furthermore, the experimental findings were validated through two-dimensional thermal simulations.

title: SAIL: Seeking Attribute in Language to Improve Chinese Pretraining
authors: Tong Zhang, Zhongyi Deng, C. L. Philip Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Imbalanced exposure of Chinese characters during pretraining often leads to inconsistent performance across downstream tasks. This problem stems primarily from the randomness of the masking strategy and is further aggravated by the semantic granularity mismatch between Chinese and English. To address these challenges, we propose the seeking attribute in language (SAIL) scheme to enhance Chinese language modeling. SAIL constructs a hierarchical attribute pyramid grounded in the rich semantics of Chinese and comprises three core tasks: masked language modeling (MLM), masked character restructuring (MCR), and masked quality evaluation (MQE). MQE employs a Chinese association graph to dynamically assess the quality of randomly masked samples and uses this assessment as a supervisory signal during pretraining. MCR integrates character-level structural information to mitigate the imbalance caused by varying word frequencies. To facilitate multilevel semantic interaction, MLM not only models word-level semantics but also bridges the character-level structural semantics from MCR and the sentence-level semantics from MQE. In turn, MCR and MQE reevaluate and enhance the performance of MLM from the lower and upper layers of the hierarchical pyramid, respectively. Experimental results across a range of Chinese language understanding tasks, including multidomain comprehension, fine-grained entity recognition, and diverse text classification, demonstrate that our model consistently achieves superior average performance. These findings show the generalization capability and transferability of the SAIL framework in modeling complex Chinese semantics.

title: Behaviour-Aware Adaptive Path Planning with Q-Learning Weight Adaptation
authors: Jichao Zhang, Meiyi Yang
abstract: This article proposes a dynamic path cost modelling framework that integrates fuzzy C-means clustering, association rule mining, and a Q-learning-based weight adaptation mechanism to achieve multi-objective collaborative path planning based on active perception of obstacle behaviour patterns. The method constructs an obstacle behaviour pattern library from multimodal perception, mines context-dependent mappings between environment features and the weights of three cost terms, namely energy, safety margin, and path smoothness, and uses Q-learning to adjust these weights online. The planner is embedded in a two-layer navigation scheme, where an improved artificial potential field, APF, generates a global reference path and a dynamic window approach, DWA, local planner executes it under dynamic obstacles. Experiments on the ROS/Gazebo platform with TurtleBot3 in environments containing up to 25 obstacles per 100 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> show that the proposed adaptive planner maintains a success rate of about 80% in high-density scenes, improving over classical APF by 30% and DWA by 55%, and remaining comparable to the deep reinforcement learning baseline TD3 at 82%. At the highest obstacle density, the method reduces normalised energy per metre by 9.4% and 6.1% compared with APF and DWA, respectively, while improving the smoothness index by 6.7–12.7% and the safety-margin index by 25–42.9%. These results indicate that the framework effectively balances real-time performance, energy efficiency, and safety, and provides a promising building block for adaptive multi-robot collaboration under communication constraints.

title: Secure Control Strategies for Stochastically Sampled Systems Against DoS/Deception Attacks: A General Design Algorithm
authors: Zhipei Hu, Peng Shi, Baishu Xu, Jin Zhang, Huiyan Zhang, Levente Kovács
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: In this paper, secure control strategies are developed for stochastically sampled systems under Denial-of-Service (DoS)/deception attacks. The actual sampling intervals deviate from their nominal values, and these sampling inaccuracies are modeled as uniformly bounded random variables following a truncated Gaussian distribution. Unlike existing studies on sampled-data systems affected by attack-induced packet losses, the obtained stability condition can be directly utilized regardless of variations in the maximum number of consecutive DoS attacks. We begin by developing a continuous-time modeling framework that enables stabilization analysis for the stochastically sampled systems exposed to DoS and deception attacks. To ensure stochastic stability and facilitate secure control, a discrete-time reformulation of the continuous-time model is then introduced. It is shown that the continuous-time system is stochastically stable if and only if its discrete-time counterpart maintains stochastic stability. Following this, a stability condition is derived and a controller synthesis algorithm is proposed to guarantee the stochastic stability of the continuous-time stochastically sampled systems subject to DoS/deception attacks. Finally, simulation results are presented to demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed controller algorithm.

title: Concise Complete Uniform Asymptotic Expansion of Dyadic Green’s Function for Dielectric Half-Space Including Low-Loss and Lossless Media
authors: Il-Suek Koh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: The first-order uniform asymptotic expansion of the dyadic Green’s function for a dielectric half-space has been used for a long time, but its applicability is limited for a large contrast case between the permittivities of two media. The complete uniform asymptotic expansion of the Green’s function has been formulated, which shows that it can be applied to any propagation scenario except for the small distance between the source and the observation point and for very low-loss ground. The uniform expansion was derived by subtracting the pole of the integrand for the half-space from that for the impedance plane. Here, a new uniform asymptotic expansion is formulated by modifying the subtraction. Then, the conventional branch-cut contribution is incorporated into the final formulation to extend its valid region. The performance of the new formulation is compared with that of the original expansion for both ordinary and plasmonic media.

title: Semi-Analytical Modal Solution for a Class of Two-Layer Biaxial Anisotropic Cylindrical Waveguides
authors: Raul O. Ribeiro, Guilherme S. Rosa, Rafael A. Penchel, Fernando L. Teixeira
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a semi-analytical method for decoupling and solving Maxwell’s equations in biaxial anisotropic media in cylindrical coordinates. The problem is reduced to two coupled differential equations that describe the axial electric and magnetic fields, and a decoupling condition is established. This formulation allows for classifying the problem in uniaxial anisotropic media along the radial, azimuthal, and axial directions, as well as a class of biaxial anisotropic scenarios. We obtain the modal solutions in unbounded two-layer cylindrical waveguides with and without the presence of an inner conductor. The field solutions are expressed as a linear combination of scaled cylindrical functions with non-integer orders. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed approach can effectively obtain the modal field solutions even in extreme lossy scenarios. Furthermore, our results show excellent agreement compared to finite element reference simulations.

title: Implementation of Multiple Deep Learning Models in Near-Infrared Image Segmentation of Forearm Blood Vessels
authors: Qianru Ji, Haoting Liu, Song Wang, Qing Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Near-infrared (NIR) imaging is a noninvasive and effective technique for visualizing subcutaneous blood vessels, which plays an important role in clinical applications such as venipuncture and vascular access assessment. In this study, three deep learning-based segmentation models: deeplab version 3 (DeepLabv3)-UNet, fully convolutional network (FCN)-UNet, and pyramid scene parsing network (PSPNet)UNet, are implemented for automatic segmentation of forearm blood vessels in NIR images. All models follow a unified data processing pipeline that includes image preprocessing steps such as size normalization, background removal, and denoising, followed by a two-stage enhancement process combining residual convolutional auto-encoder (RCAE) and contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE) to improve vessel contrast and boundary clarity. Experimental implementation demonstrates that all three models can effectively identify vascular structures in NIR images and generate clear vessel segmentation results, providing a feasible technical foundation for the development of intelligent vascular detection systems based on deep learning. Experimental results indicate that the DeepLabv3-UNet model achieves the most accurate segmentation, demonstrating its superior ability to extract vascular structures in NIR images.

title: FireAI: A Framework for Dynamic Firewall Policy Generation using Generative Artificial Intelligence
authors: Amine Rahmani, Sem Sahki, Imene Nadji, Amine Abbas
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Theoretical and Applicative Aspects of Computer Science (ICTAACS)
abstract: Traditional network firewalls rely on static, manually configured rule sets that are ill-suited for the dynamic nature of modern networks and the rapid evolution of cyber threats. This manual approach is not only resource-intensive but also prone to human error, often resulting in security gaps or network disruptions. In this paper, we introduce FireAI, a novel framework that leverages Generative Artificial Intelligence to automate the entire lifecycle of firewall policy management. FireAI continuously monitors the network state, analyses real-time traffic data, and intelligently generates context-aware firewall rules. The system utilizes a Large Language Model (LLM), guided by carefully engineered prompts, to translate network metrics into syntactically valid and secure policies for a target firewall. We present a proof-of-concept implementation within a simulated network environment using pfSense firewall, demonstrating its capability to dynamically adapt security postures in response to network changes. Our results show that FireAI can successfully generate and deploy effective firewall rules, establishing a paradigm for a more adaptive, autonomous, and resilient network security infrastructure.

title: Positive-Unlabeled Hyperspectral Image Classification using nnPU Loss and Masked Autoencoder
authors: O. S. Melashchenko
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Hyperspectral image classification has seen significant progress with deep learning, especially CNN- and Transformer-based architectures that capture spectral–spatial relationships. However, two fundamental challenges remain: (i) the scarcity of labeled data due to the cost of pixel-level annotation, and (ii) the difficulty of fully utilizing abundant unlabeled data without overfitting. Positive–unlabeled learning addresses these issues by exploiting unlabeled data alongside a small set of positive labels, but its accuracy depends heavily on the quality of the unlabeled samples and the precision of class prior estimation. In this work, we investigate the case where the prior is known and focus on improving representation learning within positive–unlabeled frameworks. We propose a joint masked autoencoder and positive–unlabeled learning method for hyperspectral image classification. Experimental results show that our approach achieves consistent gains over standard positive–unlabeled baselines under scenarios with limited labeled data. These results highlight the effectiveness of combining masked autoencoder-based representations with positive–unlabeled classification. Code is at: https://github.com/melalex/hsi-classification-playground

title: Copper Pillar Bump FCBGA Underfill Process Characterization for Automotive Application
authors: Poh Meng Dominic Koey, Khai Soon Ha, Loganraj Kanagaraj, Mohd Afiz Hashim
abstract: The reliability of Flip-Chip Ball Grid Array (FCBGA) packages utilizing Copper Pillar Bumps (CPB) is critical for automotive applications, where stringent AECQ-100 standards apply. Capillary underfill (UF) material is employed to mitigate thermomechanical stresses caused by the coefficient of thermal expansion (CTE) mismatch between the silicon die with CPB and organic substrate. However, inconsistent UF flow can lead to incomplete coverage and void formation, compromising package integrity and long-term reliability. This study systematically investigates key process parameters that influence UF flow and void occurrence namely UF dispense weight, substrate temperature, unit staging time after pre-bake, and curing conditions (pressurized vs. atmospheric). Through optimization of these attributes, the study achieved uniform UF coverage with zero UF void formation, thereby enhancing the robustness and reliability of automotive-grade FCBGA packages.

title: AHNet: A Lightweight and High-Accuracy Framework for Ship Detection in SAR Images
authors: Guoyan Xiong, Yuntao Yang, Yuxin Xia, Hongli Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: The task of identifying ships in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery plays a crucial role in ocean monitoring, navigation management, and defense-related operations. However, the progress of this field is constrained by inherent speckle noise, complex background interference, and the high computational efficiency required for real-world deployment. To tackle these challenges, we propose a lightweight detection framework called AHNet. In particular, we design an ACmix module that integrates convolution and self-attention mechanisms: local convolution extracts strong backscattering features of ships, while self-attention models global pixel dependencies to suppress isolated noise and strengthen target-scene contextual representation, thereby achieving higher detection accuracy for tiny targets. In addition, to reduce computational overhead without compromising accuracy, the HetConv module is introduced. The module leverages channel redundancy and replaces part of high-cost convolutions with a heterogeneous convolution structure, effectively reducing computational complexity and parameter count, thereby satisfying the real-time deployment needs of resource-limited platforms. Experiments conducted on the SSDD dataset demonstrate that AHNet attains 98.2% mAP50 and 71.3% mAP50-95, surpassing the majority of advanced detection approaches. At the same time, the parameters are reduced by 20.3%, GFLOPs drop by 16%, and the inference speed reaches 227 FPS. Experimental findings indicate that AHNet attains an optimal balance between detection precision and computational cost, highlighting its great promise for real-time ship detection in SAR-based remote sensing scenarios.

title: Early Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy Using a Hybrid SIFT–CNN Framework on Multimodal Datasets
authors: Ammisetti Jayaram, Shaik Hamsha Jasmine, Gunakala Archana, Brundavanam Satyasai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy is a severe microvascular complication of diabetes and a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide. Early detection and accurate grading of DR are critical for timely intervention and treatment. In this approach, a deep learning-based approach for automated DR classification using a merged dataset created by combining two publicly available sources: the Diabetic Retinopathy 224×224 Gaussian Filtered dataset and the Messidor-2 dataset. The integration of these datasets significantly increased the volume and diversity of retinal fundus images, encompassing variations in image quality, illumination, and patient demographics, thereby enhancing the model's generalization capability. The merged dataset was pre-processed through contrast enhancement, resizing, and noise reduction to standardize image quality. Data augmentation techniques were employed to address class imbalance and improve robustness. A image feature extraction method SIFT is used to extract the features and then applied to the model. A convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture was implemented and fine-tuned to classify diabetic retinopathy into five severity grades, ranging from No DR to Proliferative DR. Experimental results demonstrated that training on the combined dataset improved accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity compared to models trained on individual datasets. The proposed approach highlights the effectiveness of dataset merging in improving DR detection performance and suggests its potential for deployment in realworld screening systems to aid ophthalmologists in large-scale diabetic eye care programs.

title: Scalable Intelligent Tutoring for African Universities a LiveKit-Based Architecture for Variable Connectivity Environments
authors: Jeanne Roux Ngo Bilong, Mamadou Ba, Gervais Mendy, Samuel Ouya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Faced with the increasing number of students, the shortage of qualified teachers, and the challenges of variable connectivity that characterize African universities, this research proposes an innovative intelligent tutoring solution for the Université Numérique Cheikh Hamidou Kane (UNCHK). The developed architecture integrates the LiveKit real-time communication platform with a multilingual conversational agent (French/Wolof) orchestrating an optimized STT-LLM-TTS pipeline designed to maintain latency below 500 ms. The system demonstrates its ability to provide interactive synchronous teaching sessions while enabling a seamless transition between virtual assistance and human intervention, thus addressing the specific educational needs of the Senegalese context. Beyond technical feasibility, this contribution fundamentally questions the role of artificial intelligence in the democratization of quality education in Africa: while the virtual tutor offers promising prospects for expanding teaching capacity and promoting equitable access to knowledge, its social acceptability, alignment with local pedagogical practices, and actual effectiveness on student learning still require empirical validation. This research represents a first step toward a hybrid teaching model in which technology enhances the teacher rather than replaces them, placing the human being at the center of the digital transformation of higher education in Africa.

title: SeekBot: A Remote-Controlled IoT-Based Rover for Search Operations During Natural Calamities
authors: Vaishali Jabade, Derick Denny, Ananya Halkare, Aryan Trivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of SeekBot, a low-cost, mobile IoT-based robotic system for postdisaster search and rescue operations. The rover is equipped with an ESP32 camera for real-time video, a gas sensor for chemical hazard detection, and an ultrasonic sensor for obstacle avoidance. This work emphasizes the integration of off-the-shelf components to create a scalable solution. The system was evaluated in a simulated disaster environment, achieving an obstacle avoidance success rate of 95.2% and a gas detection accuracy of 90% for a simulated gas leak. The video transmission demonstrated an average latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 0 m s}$</tex> within a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0}$</tex>–meter range. SeekBot serves as a valuable tool for providing critical situational awareness and mitigating risks to human rescuers.”

title: Dynamic AP Association for Fronthaul Efficiency in Cell-Free Massive MIMO via Thresholded Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Huaqiao Zhou, Lei Zhu, Weijun Zeng, Lu Yu, Kaixin Cheng, Shiyue Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Cell-free massive MIMO systems encounter three fundamental challenges in dynamic access point (AP)-user association: 1) exponential complexity from high-dimensional discrete action spaces, 2) spectral efficiency (SE)-quality of service (QoS) interdependencies, and 3) non-differentiable fronthaul optimization objectives. These issues collectively induce severe fronthaul congestion. To enhance fronthaul utilization while guaranteeing QoS, this study proposes a thresholded deep reinforcement learning framework: Thresholding Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient for AP Selection (TDDPG-AP). Our approach uniquely addresses the combinatorial action space explosion through a novel thresholding mechanism that binarizes continuous actor outputs into discrete AP-selection decisions. Leveraging largescale fading coefficients as states, TDDPG-AP employs this threshold-based discretization to dynamically associate APs per user while jointly optimizing SE and QoS via a constrained reward function. Simulations demonstrate that TDDPG-AP achieves 23.1% higher fronthaul SE and 82.3% QoS compliance versus benchmarks-effectively balancing efficiency and service reliability in scalable networks.

title: CNN–PVT: A Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Accurate Pneumonia Detection from Chest X-Rays
authors: Vinoth Kumar Sathish Kumar, Kalaivani T, S. Sindhuja, Norulhidayah Isa, Shri Hari Satheeshkumar, Gowrisanker M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Accurate detection of pneumonia from chest X-ray images is essential for early diagnosis and effective medical treatment. Traditional manual examination of X-rays is time-consuming and prone to human error. To classify whether the chest X-ray images are pneumonia or normal, a standalone CNN were used against a hybrid CNN– Pyramid Vision Transformer (CNN+PVT). The standalone CNN that extracts the local features in the image, whereas the PVT acquire the global contextual insight and long-range dependencies, thereby boosting the overall learning of the model. Five evaluation metrics which includes accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC metrics were used to detect how well the model performs. The standalone CNN obtained a training accuracy of 96.59 %, testing accuracy of 71.96 % and validation accuracy of 97.02 %. On the other hand, the hybrid CNN+PVT obtained a training accuracy of 97.42 %, testing accuracy of 73.40 %, and validation accuracy of 97.13 %. This clearly indicates that the CNN+PVT model handles much complicated medical image effectively and the better proficiency of the hybrid model shows, the use of transformer-based attention mechanisms into convolutional networks supports more effective contextual and spatial learning. By the results, it can be stated that CNN+PVT delivers a more trustworthy and effective way to detect pneumonia from chest X-ray images.

title: Sniffing Out Code Smells with Precision: Evaluating Two Data Balancing Strategies for Severity Classification
authors: Vishesh Kumar Singh, Shweta
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Code smells are defects in software design that reduce software quality and maintainability. Furthermore, it is challenging to detect them using machine learning techniques due to the small and biased datasets. We compare two data augmentation strategies for creating balanced datasets that target four code smells: Blob, Long Method, Data Class, and Feature Envy. To add more data points of lexical and syntactic variations, the first strategy uses noise injection and synonym replacement; the second strategy uses code refactoring to create data points that are both structurally and semantically different.By finetuninga BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) based model for severity classification and detection accuracy, we investigate both strategies. The outcomes show the benefits and drawbacks of each strategy for resolving class imbalance problem. This comparison of the two data augmentation techniques shows how they can be used practically to create reliable datasets.

title: Codec-agnostic Lenslet Video Coding with Edge Pixel Estimation and Optimization
authors: Hao Peng, Zhuo Tan, Xin Jin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Moving Picture Expert Group (MPEG) launched Lenslet Video Coding (LVC) standard project in June, 2025 to investigate codec-agnostic tools for lenslet videos, recording spatial and angular light field information captured by plenoptic cameras or synthetically generated from multiview data, to ensure standard compatibility with Versatile Video Coding (VVC) while achieving much higher compression efficency. In this paper, a novel codec-agnostic coding framework for lenslet video is proposed. It integrates microimage cropping and alignment tool (MCA) in the pre-processing step. Then, edge pixel value estimation and optimization are proposed in both pre-processing and post-processing steps to signal and reconstruct the edge pixels that are multiview-rendering less effective by 60-degree directional compensation and pixel value fitting, thereby achieving a better trade-off between rendered multiview quality and lenslet quality. The proposed method achieves an average of 26.98% bitrate reduction relative to H.266/VVC, which is much higher than state-of-the-art methods, with much higher subjective quality in the reconstructed lenslet videos.

title: Spectrum and Energy Efficiency Optimization for UAV Enabled Integrated Sensing and Communication
authors: Hansen Dai, Yan Yang, Kaiqiang Zhang, Jiahui Hao, Bin Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) is becoming a crucial aerial platform to provide enhanced communication and sensing services benefited from its unique features, including agile mobility and high probability of line-of-sight coverage. This paper investigates the novel problem of UAV trajectory optimization within the integrated communication and sensing design, where the UAV communicates with multiple users and simultaneously senses the target. Addressing the inherent conflict between energy efficiency (EE) and spectrum efficiency (SE) in UAV-ISAC system, this paper proposes a joint optimization of communication scheduling, transmit power allocation, and UAV trajectory design to navigate the SE-EE trade-off. The formulated non-convex optimization problem is decomposed into three subproblems, which can be addressed via Dinkelbach method, successive convex approximation (SCA) and relaxation methods. A suboptimal solution to the original problem is achieved via alternating optimization of its subproblems. The simulation results indicate that the proposed optimization scheme can make a balance between SE and EE.

title: Wireless Network Topology Inference Based on Domain Adaptation
authors: Wenbo Du, Weijun Zeng, Ye Liu, Chuang Wang, Lei Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Wireless network topology inference is a key technology for optimizing network performance and resource management. Previous topology inference methods are mostly based on the assumption of ideal channels and are not applicable to wireless networks with various channel randomness. To address this problem, We divide different fading scenarios into the source domain and the target domain, and transform the connectivity problem between nodes into the causal relationship problems between time series. We proposed a recursive variational autoencoder to reconstruct causal structures with different lags. Based on the causality, the decoder was used to predict the time series to further optimize the causal structure. The simulation results show that in various channel fading scenarios, the proposed method improves the accuracy of topology inference compared with other algorithms and has certain universality.

title: AI-Mediated Push and Pull Factors in Online Gambling Among Southeast Asian Youth
authors: Dwi Novitasari, Anusuiya Subramaniam, Riyan Sisiawan Putra, Syavira Sabaya, Khoirun Nisa', Imelda Dwiyanti Agustina, Fadia Jasmine Has Shiela
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The rapid growth of online gambling platforms, combined with advancements in AI, has significantly impacted user behavior, especially among the iGeneration in Southeast Asia. This study examines the influence of internal (push) and external (pull) motivational factors on online gambling intentions, and the mediating role of AI, among Indonesian and Malaysian youth. Using survey data from 155 respondents and SEM-PLS analysis, the findings reveal that both push factors (e.g., emotional escape, entertainment seeking) and pull factors (e.g., ease of access, promotional attractiveness) enhance gambling intentions. AI-driven recommendation systems act as behavioral catalysts, amplifying the effects of push and pull motivators through personalized content, trust-enhancing interfaces, and predictive strategies. These systems mediate the relationship between motivations and gambling intentions, by dynamically adjusting user experiences based on real-time data. Comparative analysis highlights differences between Indonesian and Malaysian respondents, with socio-economic stressors being more influential in Indonesia, while technology appeals are stronger in Malaysia. The study integrates motivational frameworks with AI-driven behavior models and calls for ethical AI deployment and targeted policymaking to mitigate gambling risks. Limitations include the narrow demographic focus and reliance on self-reported data, suggesting the need for broader research using longitudinal methods and exploring moderating variables. This research offers valuable insights for researchers, platform developers, and policymakers in the digital age.

title: $\mathrm{B}^{2}\mathrm{S}$ Former: Hyperspectral Remote Sensing Image Classification Based on a Bilateral Branch Sparse Transformer Model
authors: Leyuan Fan, Pengyuan Lv, Jaijia Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Hyperspectral images (HSIs) comprise numerous sequential spectral bands. This rich spectral data delivers detailed attribute information for material characterization. Recently, transformer models have been increasingly applied to HSI classification, owing to their capability to directly model long-range dependencies across spectral bands and achieve promising performance. However, the traditional transformer structure has difficulty in training on imbalanced HSIs because of the increased parameters of the model. In this paper, a bilateral branch sparse transformer <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{B}^{2}\mathrm{S}$</tex> Former) framework is proposed to consider the class imbalance problem of hyperspectral data while emphasizing the key spectra in the classes with limited samples. In <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{B}^{2}\mathrm{S}$</tex> Former, sparse self-attention is introduced, which focuses on the features which contribute more, to better distinguish the different classes. A bilateral branch architecture is also proposed to learn a more robust network on imbalanced data. In the bilateral branch structure, normal and reverse sampling strategies are used for each branch, respectively. The first branch is used to model the original distribution of the data, and the second branch is used to improve the learning ability of the model on the imbalanced classes. The output features of the two branches are fused before the softmax layer to obtain the final prediction.

title: A Comparative Study Towards Designing a Hybrid Architecture of Microservices and LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Peyman Yazdanian, Yan Liu, Zheng Li
abstract: LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (LLM-MAS) present an emerging paradigm for constructing intelligent and adaptive applications that enable autonomous reasoning and collaborative problem-solving. Empirical studies show that current LLM-MAS still suffers from overlapping agent roles, unclear capabilities, and goal misalignment. In contrast, Microservice Systems (MS) are designed with modularity, and each service encapsulates a well-defined context and interface with structured and deterministic execution. From the system architecture principles, these paradigms demonstrate parallel attributes and complementary strengths that lead to a synthesized hybrid architecture. In this paper, we analyze the design factors of hybrid MS and LLM-MAS, as well as the main challenges, through a comparative study across eight architectural dimensions, including function encapsulation, orchestration, API design, auto-correction, data communication, operations, quality attributes, and environment awareness. The analysis reveals critical mismatches, design synergies, and transferable best practices. To motivate future work, we define four research questions to categorize the challenges. The goal is to create a converging design space for exploring architecture design towards intelligent, autonomous, modular, and adaptive systems.

title: Towards Edge Deployment: An Ultra Lightweight Encoder for Learned Image Compression
authors: Peijie Diao, Linfeng Liu, Qiu Shen, Xun Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Learned Image Compression has shown superior performance over traditional codecs. However, the high computational complexity of existing LIC methods hinders their deployment on resource-constrained edge devices. To address this challenge, we propose an ultra lightweight encoder with an asymmetric architecture: while the encoder is designed to be extremely simple, the decoder employs a more complex synthesis network to maintain high reconstruction quality. The proposed method achieves better rate-distortion performance than BPG with an encoder even lighter than JPEG2000.

title: Elderly Companion Health App
authors: Praveen J M, Vinay S, Sinthuja P M
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: This paper explores the development of an Elderly Companion App, a mobile healthcare solution designed to optionally connect with IoT and wearable devices, all crafted for easy use by older adults. Many seniors, particularly those living alone, face challenges in managing their health daily, like keeping up with their medication schedules, dealing with emergencies, and fighting feelings of social isolation. Unfortunately, traditional apps can be too complex for older users, resulting in low adoption rates and limited accessibility. The app we’reproposing boasts a user-friendlydesign featuring largebuttons and voice assistance to enhance its approachability. Key features include medication reminders with dosage alerts, health monitoring through manualinputor linkedwearables, emergency SOSnotifications with live location sharing, and fall detection using smartphone sensors. To keep familiesconnected, thesystemincludesacaregiver dashboardthatallows real-time monitoring of medication adherence, health status, and emergency alerts. Beyond these functionalities, the updated system incorporates a Deep Learning-based health prediction model that analyzes long-term trends in vital signs like heart rate, blood pressure, glucose levels, and activity patterns. This model predicts potential health issues—such as hypertension risk, glucose fluctuations, or abnormal cardiac behaviors—and provides a straightforward, easy-to-understand summary of any detected issues for both the elderly user and their caregivers. This enhancement makes the app more proactive, allowing for early awareness and timely intervention. Overall, the design encouragesindependent living for seniors while giving caregivers greater peace of mind through predictive insights and continuous health monitoring.

title: Crater Machine Learning-Enhanced Cloud Ransomware Response Platform With Automated GitOps Recovery
authors: Jeya Priya D, Sanjai K, Santhosh S
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Cloud ransomware attacks have become more advanced than mere file encryption. Current attacks involve cloud-native APIs for deleting data and disrupting services. Tools used for cloud ransomware identification have poor accuracy with an abundance of false positives, which reach up to 30%, with no automated recovery capability. To address this challenge, this research proposes CRATER (Cloud Ransomware Automated Threat Elimination and Recovery) based on a combination of machine learning behavioral analysis with a GitOps-Based infrastructure recovery technique. Our solution can detect cloud ransomware with a total accuracy of 94.7%, with a false positive rate of 3.2%, using Adaptive Containment and API sequence analysis. Our average recovery time is 87 seconds with a service availability of 99.1%, a 12-fold performance improvement over manual solutions. Lateral spread is successfully contained in 98.6% of attacks.

title: Trust-free Personalized Decentralized Learning
authors: Yawen Li, Yan Li, Junping Du, Yingxia Shao, Meiyu Liang, Guanhua Ye
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: Personalized collaborative learning in federated settings faces a critical trade-off between customization and participant trust. Existing approaches typically rely on centralized coordinators or trusted peer groups, limiting their applicability in open, trust-averse environments. While recent decentralized methods explore anonymous knowledge sharing, they often lack global scalability and robust mechanisms against malicious peers. To bridge this gap, we propose TPFed, a Trust-free Personalized Decentralized Federated Learning framework. TPFed replaces central aggregators with a blockchain-based bulletin board, enabling participants to dynamically select global communication partners based on Locality-Sensitive Hashing (LSH) and peer ranking. Crucially, we introduce an "all-in-one" knowledge distillation protocol that simultaneously handles knowledge transfer, model quality evaluation, and similarity verification via a public reference dataset. This design ensures secure, globally personalized collaboration without exposing local models or data. Extensive experiments demonstrate that TPFed significantly outperforms traditional federated baselines in both learning accuracy and system robustness against adversarial attacks.

title: Causal Multiscale Convolution and Hierarchical Attention Transformer Based Structure for Automatic Modulation Classification
authors: Hengbo Li, Zheng Dou
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Automatic modulation classification (AMC) is a key technology widely used in both military and civilian domains, bridging the gap between initial signal detection and subsequent demodulation processes. Currently, deep neural networks have been extensively applied in this field, playing a crucial role in advancing AMC. However, existing studies exhibit less satisfactory classification performance at lower signal-to-noise ratios (SNR) and do not sufficiently extract signal features. To address these issues, we propose a novel model called MCHNet, which denoise signals via wavelet transform and integrates causal multiscale convolution with a hierarchical attention mechanism for feature extraction to enhance classification performance. On the commonly used public datasets RML2016.10a and RML2016.10b, our model demonstrates more outstanding performance.

title: GPAR: Optimizing Reliable Transmission of Deadline-Constrained Services in GEO Satellite Communication
authors: Ziyi Yin, Chunxi Li, Yongxiang Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: End-to-end communications based on Geostationary Earth Orbit (GEO) satellites, which feature high round-trip time (RTT) and relatively high channel bit error rates, present significant challenges for supporting high-priority services—such as emergency applications requiring strict deadlines and high reliability. Existing techniques have limitations: Automatic Repeat Request (ARQ) suffers from long acknowledgment delays due to feedback-based retransmissions, while Forward Error Correction (FEC) has limited recovery capability under consecutive packet losses. In this paper, we model the GEO satellite-based end-toend communication channel using a Gilbert-Elliott loss model to capture the bursty nature of packet losses. We propose a periodic active repetition (GPAR) transmission strategy, in which each high-priority service packet is actively repeated transmission at predetermined intervals. The transmission count and interval are determined based on the packet deadline, reliability threshold, and efficiency requirements. To implement this strategy, we design a periodic active repetition scheduling algorithm that creates multiple transmission queues according to the computed optimal parameters. Packets in these queues are actively transmitted at precisely calculated intervals to ensure reliable delivery within deadline constraints. Simulation results demonstrate that our proposed GPAR scheme effectively ensures timely delivery of deadline-constrained service packets under the challenging conditions of a GEO Satellite-based network, compared to other methods.

title: Design and Implementation of a Precision Segmentation System for Cardiac Ultrasound Images Using HA-UNet++ Hybrid Network
authors: YaLi Peng, Fang Fang, Xing Xu, XiaoQian Huang, WenLi Huang, SuPing Xiao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of cardiac ultrasound images is crucial for quantitative cardiac function assessment and auxiliary diagnosis of cardiovascular diseases. To address limitations of traditional methods-including image noise sensitivity, blurred boundary segmentation, and clinical applicability-we propose a deep learning-based adaptive multi-model fusion segmentation framework. This study further develops a full-stack intelligent cardiac ultrasound analysis system tailored for clinical workflows. The core innovations of this paper are threefold: First, we developed a dynamic model selection mechanism that integrates U-Net, U-Net++, and TransUNet networks with an anatomy-based attention gating module, achieving adaptive and precise segmentation of the left ventricular structure. Second, we proposed the HA-UNet++ hybrid architecture, which combines channel-space dual attention mechanisms with improved dense skip connections. This architecture achieved a Dice coefficient of 53.87% on the CAMUS public dataset, representing a 4.43% improvement over U-Net. Third, we created a lightweight real-time inference engine utilizing PyTorch Lightning for model quantization and TensorRT acceleration. This enables 520 ms per frame inference speed on NVIDIA RTX 3050 Ti GPUs, achieving 2.3 times faster performance than traditional methods and meeting clinical real-time interaction requirements. This study effectively breaks through the bottleneck of clinical application of cardiac ultrasound image segmentation. Its technical framework can be extended to CT, MRI and other multimodal image analysis, providing a new solution for real-time assisted diagnosis in smart medical scenarios.

title: GRIDMIND: AI-Based Power Forecasting and Fault Detection System
authors: Pullaiah Chowdary Vutla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The growing sophistication of electrical power grids today requires sophisticated intelligent systems that can forecast demand patterns as well as anomaly detection in real-time. This research introduces the concept of GridMind, a fully integrated Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based system aimed at end-to-end management of electrical power using load prediction and fault detection. GridMind is a hybrid machine learning model that uses Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to predict temporal load and a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with attentional mechanisms to recognize spatial fault patterns. This system integrates multi-source data fusion that works on historical consumption data, weather patterns, and economic indicators to produce accurate load forecasts with 97.2% accuracy on 24-hour prediction horizons. The fault detection module employs state-of-the-art anomaly detection methods that are founded on isolation forests and autoencoders, and demonstrate 98.8% detection accuracy and less than 0.3% False Positive Rates (FPR). The scalable nature of the system supports a wide range of grid sizes, including microgrids and large-scale utility networks; thus, it is used in different power management applications in smart grid settings short term.

title: Heterogeneity-Aware Spatial-Temporal Graph Convolutional Network
authors: Cheng Long, Wang Juan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Accurate traffic flow forecasting is essential for mitigating congestion, enabling dynamic route planning, and improving real-time traffic control in intelligent transportation systems.
