However, spatial-temporal heterogeneity—manifested in varying traffic patterns across different regions and dynamic evolution over time—poses significant challenges for existing prediction models. To address this, we propose a Heterogeneity-Aware Spatial-Temporal Graph Convolutional Network (HASGCN), which explicitly models heterogeneity in traffic dynamics through dedicated embedding and interaction mechanisms. HASGCN introduces two key innovations: (1) a cross-axis fusion mechanism that enables deep information exchange across different representational dimensions; and (2) a dual-scale attention mechanism that jointly models local neighborhood dependencies and longrange global correlations. Through extensive comparisons on four real-world datasets, HASGCN exhibits clear advantages over conventional and state-of-the-art models, consistently yielding lower prediction errors across all evaluation metrics.

title: Vision-Based Real-Time Occupancy Mapping for Zone-Specific HVAC Optimization in Airport Buses
authors: Nattachai Pothi, Irin Wanthong, Akkharachai Kosiyanurak, Nitisak Numanoy, Jiraphon Srisertpol
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Electric airport buses heavily rely on their HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning) systems for passenger comfort, often consuming more energy than propulsion due to short travel distances. To efficiently utilize battery power, we propose a novel approach using computer vision for real-time passenger estimation and zone identification. This allows us to control the temperature and airflow speed of the HVAC in specific occupied areas. Our system incorporates front and rear cameras inside the bus cabin, which is divided into three distinct zones (A, B, C). The YOLOv8 model processes these images to accurately detect passengers in real time within each zone, providing a dynamic understanding of the distribution of passengers throughout the bus. The detection model achieved a high accuracy of 85.6 percent, and when combined with Centroid Calculation and Region of Interest (ROI) techniques, it provides precise, up-to-date data on passenger numbers and their locations in each zone. This enables the air conditioning to be adjusted specifically to the actual occupancy. Our strategy aims to reduce energy consumption while ensuring optimal thermal comfort for passengers. This vision-driven, zone-specific control mechanism serves as an intelligent energy management solution, ultimately extending the battery life for electric airport buses.

title: Optimization of Surge Arrester Configuration for Collector Lines in Mountainous Wind Farms
authors: Zhijia Zheng, Bo Li, Honghua Hu, Shiyang Zhou, Jing Luo, Langtao Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: In the context of China's “Carbon Peaking and Carbon Neutrality” strategic goals, the scale of new power systems continues to expand, making the impact of lightning disasters on collector lines in wind farms increasingly predominant. Particularly, wind farms in mountainous and hilly areas are more susceptible to lightning strikes due to altitude, terrain, and other factors. While full coverage of surge arresters throughout the entire system is the most effective solution, wind farm owners often have a limited budget for lightning protection, which means surge arresters can only be installed on a portion of the overhead line towers. In response to this issue, this study proposes a lightning protection priority ranking methodology for collector lines in mountainous wind farms. It first identifies key factors influencing the lightning strike risk and employs ATP-EMTP-empowered simulation and the CRITIC-TOPSIS method to calculate the lightning protection priority ranking for overhead line towers. Finally, the study discusses the practical application of this methodology in a wind farm in South China and is expected to provide a reference for similar projects.

title: Automated Plant Health Monitoring Using Collaborative Robot
authors: C.M. Nalayini, A.R. Sathyabama, S. Priyadharshini, Sumathi S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: In the modern agricultural landscape, collaborative robots have emerged as key technology for enhancing precision, efficiency and sustainability in farming practices. This research introduces a Smart Cobot Greenhouse Assistant developed to automate plant health monitoring and management with controlled environment. It identifies plant's condition and take suitable actions like watering dry plants, raising alerts for diseased ones, and leaving healthy plants unharmed. A two-dimensional workspace is modelled where the cobot is initialized at the origin <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{0, 0})$</tex> and plants are located at respective positions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{x, y})$</tex>. Dynamic states such as healthy, dry and diseased are assigned and simulated through python code using a well-defined synthetic dataset. Cobot's movements and actions are animated using Matplotlib visualization to produce an efficient and sustainable greenhouse management and the information are recorded into the blockchain distributed ledger and alerts are sent to the owner for further decision. Compared with the traditional system, the proposed smart cobot achieved operational responsiveness, dynamic visual feedback, and trustworthy data handling with full transparency.

title: Generative AI Chatbot Using Retrieval Augmented Generation for Financial Insights
authors: Anjali K S, Pankaj Krishna, Nagadevi S
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Technology has rapidly advanced in recent years, especially in the field of intelligent systems that assist users with data-driven decision-making. Among the many sectors impacted, finance has seen significant improvements through the use of automated tools that simplify complex data interpretation. This project introduces a conversational chatbot tailored to address financial questions by extracting insights from CSV-based datasets. The system is developed using a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach, combining the strengths of Hugging Face Transformers and pre-trained models such as Mistral 7B, GPT-4, Falcon 7B, LlaMA3 to enhance response quality. A custom-built retriever identifies relevant data points within the documents, while the language model produces clear and contextually accurate answers. The technical framework includes a responsive SvelteKit frontend, a FastAPI-driven backend for managing asynchronous communication, and ChromaDB as the vector store for handling embeddings efficiently. Natural Language Processing (NLP) enables the chatbot to interpret user input and generate human-like replies. Overall, this project demonstrates how domain-specific conversational tools can effectively be built by blending RAG pipelines and modern LLMs for real-world financial query resolution.

title: Graph-Theoretic Modeling and Explainable AI for Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Satyakam Baraha, Shruti Chandrasekar, Shweatha B, Sirisha R N
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The rapid expansion of credit card usage has been matched by a steady rise in fraudulent activities, causing annual financial losses that run into billions of dollars. In response, financial institutions have invested heavily in advanced anti-fraud systems that aim to detect and block suspicious transactions in real time, thereby protecting both customers and service providers. Among the different approaches developed, machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) have attracted considerable attention because of their ability to recognize subtle patterns in transaction data and deliver strong predictive performance. Typically, such models process inputs such as cardholder and merchant identifiers, transaction amounts, and time stamps, and then output confidence scores indicating whether a transaction is more likely to be genuine or fraudulent. The proposed approach thus combines good predictive performance with transparency, which is required for real-world application in financial systems.

title: Automated Corrosion Classification based on Deep and Handcrafted Feature Extractors
authors: Salah Eddine Agab, Nafaa Nacereddine
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Theoretical and Applicative Aspects of Computer Science (ICTAACS)
abstract: Automatic corrosion classification in images is essential for timely maintenance and structural safety, especially in industrial environments. This work explores two approaches for this task: lightweight texture descriptors and deep learning-based representations. We propose a fusion pipeline based on uniform Local Ternary Patterns (uLTP) and localized GLCM standard deviation features, evaluated across grayscale, RGB, and HSV color domains, where a comprehensive ablation study assessed the contribution of each individual feature as well as the combination of multiple features. Experimental results on two public datasets demonstrate that the proposed handcrafted pipeline combined with PCA and SVM provides competitive accuracy (up to 96.67%) at significantly lower computational cost than the deep model. While ConvNeXt achieves the highest accuracy overall, its training overhead highlights the trade-off between performance and efficiency. These findings support the use of optimized texture features in scenarios requiring fast and resource-aware solutions.

title: Prompt Culture and Visual Creativity in AI-Assisted Design: Perspectives from University Students
authors: Athika Dwi Wiji Utami, Trias Widha Andari, Putra Uji Deva Satrio, Sonhaji Arif, Ragil Noviyanti, Amalia Hartiningrum, Akhya' Muhammad Khaidzir
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study explores how Indonesian university design students use generative AI in their creative process. The use of AI in the creative process is related to the extent to which AI is utilized in the process of creating design works, the ability to compose prompts, and how AI is interpreted amidst the automation of design technology. The study used a quantitative descriptive approach with supporting qualitative insights with 110 respondents taken using a purposive sampling technique. Surveys were given to respondents, then further explored by interviewing selected informants. This study identifies seven dimensions of engagement, including prompting behaviour, prompting strategies, perceived creativity, ethical reflection, designer identity, engagement with others' AI work, and the habit of sharing AI works. The results indicate that students primarily use generative AI as a tool for idea exploration, concept visualization, and style experimentation. Most still consider themselves designers, viewing AI as a supportive partner rather than a creative substitute. They demonstrated moderate to high levels of creative self-efficacy, ethical awareness, and adaptive identity transformation. However, active engagement with others' AI work remains limited, and while rapid sharing is considered a beneficial form of knowledge exchange, it is often treated as a personal design asset. Overall, students are undergoing a transitional phase in which they learn to integrate AI into their creative practices while maintaining human-centered control and ethical reflection. This study contributes to the growing body of work on AI-assisted design by suggesting a versatile framework for understanding how students interact with AI creatively and ethically. AI occupies a multi-layered role in the design ecosystem, serving not only as a medium for idea generation but also as a space for identity reflection and a place to negotiate the evolving role of the designer. This study highlights practical considerations for integrating critical AI literacy, ethical design frameworks, and collaborative prompt practices into design education curricula, ensuring students are well-equipped for evolving creative industry practices.

title: Novel Breakout Local Search for Offloading Tasks in Multi-Tiered Cloud Environment Considering Transmission and Processing
authors: Mina Kato, Tiago Koketsu Rodrigues, Shikhar Verma
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: 6G end devices will need to balance offloading processingheavy tasks to cloud servers while executing transmissionheavy tasks locally. The decision of which task gets executed where is a complex one, as it depends on the wireless environment of the devices, the network topology connecting them to servers, and the characteristics of devices, servers, and tasks. In this paper, we utilize Breakout Local Search and its exploration/exploitation techniques to find near-optimal configurations for task offloading in this scenario. The algorithm is customized to make it more adept at solving this particular problem, and the resulting proposal is shown to be much better than baseline solutions and outperforms a Machine Learning algorithm commonly used for task-offloading decisions.

title: A Stage-Aware Masking Strategy for Domain Adaptive Semantic Segmentation
authors: Tao Sui, Zehua Chen, Xiuzhi Liu, Zhishuo Ji, Jiahao Zhang, Jingqi Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: In unsupervised domain adaptation for semantic segmentation, random masking is commonly used to enhance robustness. However, as pseudo-label quality improves during training, it may suppress informative regions, especially for rare classes and boundaries. To address this, we propose a training strategy that combines semantic scoring with stageaware progressive masking. The scoring mechanism ranks image blocks based on class rarity and boundary response, guiding the masking process. The masking strategy maintains randomness in early training for generalization, and gradually shifts to semantic-guided masking as label reliability increases. Experiments on GTA5 → Cityscapes <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</sup> show that this method improves mIoU by 1.64 over the DAFormer <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[2]</sup> baseline, with clear gains on rare and boundary categories, validating its effectiveness.

title: Operational Efficiency Analytical Tool with Machine Data Integration
authors: Jian Liang Lim, Li An Tee, Mohamad Syafiq Bin Mokhtar, Qiu Yin Wong, Kim Chuan Lee
abstract: Enhancing equipment productivity is a key objective in semiconductor manufacturing where operational efficiency directly impacts throughput and cost. In the context of manufacturing processes, wafer thinning process is one of the bottleneck processes which may slow down the overall production of a product and often face several challenges including inconsistent output, limited visibility on processing time and insufficient detailed loss time analysis. This paper will focus on the automated analytical tool to address these challenges through applications like wafer-level effective processing time (EPT) analysis, back-to-back (B2B) monitoring and modeling, hidden loss time identification. Wafer-level EPT analysis feature breaks down the machine mechanisms for each wafer, identifying bottlenecks using staircase, pareto and boxplot analyses. B2B monitoring and modeling replicate the continuous flow of wafer lots, evaluating operational procedures to minimize loss time through algorithm-based assessments. Hidden loss time is attributed to inefficiencies in B2B practices and EPT performance, with an interactive dashboard developed to illustrate and analyze these losses. Few case studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of the automated analytical tool in improving processing times and reducing loss time percentages, showcasing significant improvements in specific machine operations. By having this analytical tool with the integration of machine data, we can streamline and facilitate the process of problem-solving through digitalization capabilities and eliminate manual data collection. This not only boosts productivity but also lays the groundwork for future enhancements and broader implementation across manufacturing processes.

title: MLego: Fast and Scalable Analytic Queries through Model Materialization and Reuse
authors: Fei Ye, Lu Jia, Bin Huang, Zhenying He, Yinan Jing, Kai Zhang, X. Sean Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: With massive texts on social media, there is an increasing desire to leverage first-hand information about local events in texts to extract insights and identify hot topics. A straightforward approach is to leverage topic modeling techniques and return hot topics via the range given by a user. However, constructing topics from massive texts is time-consuming, making it challenging to apply in real-time query scenarios. Despite recent efforts to accelerate the model training process using distributed training techniques and rapid sampling methods, the high training expenses restrict its practical use. In this paper, we study how to efficiently conduct an analytic query in an interactive manner. We propose MLego, an analytic query framework by leveraging the concepts from data management - materialization and reuse. Our solution builds the approximate model by merging materialized models. Additionally, we optimize the workflow of MLego by leveraging the hierarchical plan searching process to reduce the query cost. The extensive experiments on different datasets show the efficiency and scalability of our method.

title: Energy-Efficient Dual Power 2-Connected Backbone Design in Wireless Sensor Networks: A Theoretical Analysis
authors: Prasanna Lakshmi M, Rohit Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are key to many real-world applications, offering the ability to monitor and collect data from various environments. However, since these networks rely on energy-constrained battery sources, optimizing energy consumption is a major challenge. Backbone nodes play a crucial role in improving routing protocols and network topology, significantly impacting performance and energy efficiency. A central issue in WSNs is the dual power assignment problem, which involves assigning high or low transmission power to each node to minimize power usage while maintaining network connectivity. To improve reliability, it is essential to design the network with multiple alternative paths between nodes, ensuring robustness even during node failures. The approach of constructing a 2-connected network is employed to achieve fault tolerance in the network. This research focuses on the dual power backbone node 2-connected problem, which involves designing a network that is both 2-connected and includes a specified number of backbone nodes, while using only two transmission power levels. The challenge is to optimize the configuration to reduce power consumption, meet the 2-connectivity requirement, and choose the backbone nodes effectively. The authors propose an approximation algorithm and respective theoretical analysis for a near-optimal solution and for practical cases where exact optimization is computationally expensive. The goal is to advance energy-efficient and reliable WSN designs for better real-world applications.

title: Mapping the Landscape of Cryptocurrency Investment Decision: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Bambang Leo Handoko, Mazlina Mustapha, David Halim, Ang Swat Lin Lindawati
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Cryptocurrency investment decisions have emerged as a critical area of study, reflecting the rapid growth and global acceptance of digital currencies. This bibliometric analysis explores the development of research in this domain, identifying key themes, influential studies, and emerging gaps. The analysis utilizes VOSviewer for mapping co-occurrences of keywords, co-authorship, and citation networks to highlight predominant research areas such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, investor behavior, risk assessment, and market dynamics. Findings indicate that while substantial research has been conducted on market behavior and risk perception, gaps remain in research of Tether and Stablecoin, understanding the psychological aspects influencing investment decisions, such as heuristic biases and herding behavior. Furthermore, there is limited exploration of sustainable investment practices in cryptocurrency markets. These findings suggest avenues for future empirical studies focusing on behavioral finance perspectives, sustainable blockchain technologies, and regulatory impacts on cryptocurrency investment decisions. Addressing these gaps may contribute to more informed decision-making processes, enhanced market stability, and robust regulatory frameworks in the evolving landscape of digital finance.

title: Adaptive Multi-Model Weighting for Wind Power Forecasting Based on Proximal Policy Optimization
authors: Qike Bai, Peidong Xu, Zeyu Mu, Jun Zhang, Tianlu Gao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: With the widespread deployment of wind power, accurate forecasting has become pivotal to the secure and economical operation of power systems. However, under pronounced environmental variability, single models often fail to deliver reliable predictions; moreover, conventional ensemble approaches typically assign fixed weights to base models based on the training set, resulting in limited adaptability. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an adaptive multi-model weighting method for wind power forecasting based on Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). By integrating an improved PPO algorithm into the forecasting pipeline, the method senses environmental changes and dynamically adjusts sub-model weights to enhance predictive accuracy. Empirical results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms all individual models and fixedweight ensemble baselines.

title: SETMM: Space Enhanced Text-to-Motion Model
authors: Yiming Wang, Boda Lin, Jiachen Li, Genying Li, Yanxian Bi, Si Li
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: Although significant performance has been achieved by text-to-motion generation models based on diffusion and transformer architectures, these models often neglect the physical plausibility and spatial relationships in the generated motions, resulting in outputs that lack realism and semantic consistency. To address this issue, we propose the Space Enhanced Text-to-Motion Model (SETMM). SETMM utilizes a Vector Quantized-Variational AutoEncoder (VQ-VAE) based motion tokenizer and a masked transformer model with a spatial enhancement module. The specialized spatial enhancement module effectively captures the spatial relationships between joints and reinforces their connections across different hierarchical levels, thereby improving motion generation quality. Experiments on the HumanML3D dataset show that SETMM outperforms current state-of-the-art models.

title: Solving the Problem of Guiding an Aircraft to a Point with Certain Coordinates
authors: S. Ponomarenko, D. Liasota
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: The paper considers the construction of an algorithm for controlling an aircraft to ensure a given coordinate and speed at the end point of the trajectory. A structure of control system to achieve a targeting state is created. It was created an algorithm of a glider to achieve a point with certain position. A computer simulation of the trajectory of a glider moving by aerodynamic and gravitational forces was made using an algorithm for aiming at a given point. Aiming errors for different initial conditions and target positions were calculated. The dependence of the aiming error on a frequency of corrections of control parameters is shown.

title: A Multimodal Federated Deep Learning Framework for Real-Time and Sustainable E-Waste Management
authors: Surya S, Sivachidambaram K S, Jeya Priya D
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The exponential rise in electronic waste presents a critical global challenge that requires intelligent, privacy-preserving, and environmentally sustainable management solutions. Building on the success of Adaptive V3 with Federated Learning, known as Adaptive V3-FL, this paper introduces a new multimodal and geo-aware framework for real-time e-waste classification and collection optimization. The proposed approach leverages image data, sensor readings, and metadata from multimodal deep learning and deploys lightweight models on edge devices for low-latency, on-site classification. Besides, federated learning is extended across IoT-enabled collection infrastructure for decentralized model training and data privacy. Against imbalance and rarity of certain waste types, the self-supervised learning strategy of contrastive learning is adopted to increase robustness against variability in real-world data. Explainable AI is used to provide more interpretable outputs. An integrated module assesses ecological risk in the case of improper disposal. Experimental results obtained through testing the enhanced system against the benchmark datasets confirm its effectiveness, with high accuracy of 98.9

title: Generating Linear Ranking Functions for Program Termination Using Transformers
authors: Yuxing Cai, Changbo Chen, Yi Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Synthesizing ranking functions for loop programs is an important technique for proving termination. Unlike previous deep learning approaches that depend on program execution to obtain training data and synthesize ranking functions, we propose the first Transformer-based framework capable of directly synthesizing ranking functions, supporting both classification and generation tasks. The classification task determines if a linear simple loop has a non-trivial linear ranking function. If it has, the generation task generates the coefficients of such a function. We address the data scarcity problem by introducing a method to increase the number of samples with non-trivial linear ranking functions, enabling an effective generation of a large-scale dataset of linear simple loops. To improve classification performance, we employ a Mixture of Expert Encoders architecture that exploits both deep and shallow features. For the cases of one and two variables, classification models achieve accuracies ranging from 76.9% to 99.2%, while the accuracies of the generation models range from 68.5% to 100%. The proposed classification models outperform the baseline models on most of the datasets. The generation models, processing batches of 128 programs in parallel, demonstrate faster inference than the Z3 solver.

title: Physics-Informed Graph Convolutional Neural Network for Scalable, and Accurate Thermal Analysis of 2.5D Chiplet-Based Systems
authors: Rahul Sahay, Nagarajan Raghavan
abstract: The work addresses the challenge of performing scalable, rapid, and physically accurate thermal analysis for chiplet-based 2.5D System-in-Package (SiP), which are becoming increasingly important in high-performance computing due to their inherent modularity and integration density. Traditional methodologies such as finite element or finite difference simulations, while accurate, are computationally expensive and either lack the ability to scale to new chiplet layouts or fail to respect the underlying physics, leading to unreliable or nonphysical thermal predictions. To solve this problem, we developed a Physics-Informed Graph Convolutional Neural Network (PI-GCN) tailored for SiP's thermal analysis. PI-GCN could transform SiP and its material stack (layer) into a 3D (2D) network consisting of nodes and edges, where nodes represent spatial points, and edges encode thermal conductance based on material properties and geometric adjacency. Here, node features include power density, normalized spatial coordinates, and chiplet flag, while edge features include thermal directionality and conductance to capture anisotropic and heterogeneous thermal paths. The PIGCN employs neighborhood aggregation to aggregate information from neighboring nodes, capturing both local and global thermal effects. An edge-based attention mechanism further enhances PI-GCN by weighting neighbor contributions according to their relative thermal significance, as determined by material and geometric framework. Furthermore, PI-GCN combines mean squared error (MSE) on temperature with a gradient regularization that penalizes violations of Fourier's Law. In this work, we considered the “worst-case scenario” of lateral (in-plane) heat transfer for a chiplet on an interposer layer to benchmark the most thermally challenging scenario in SiP. The PI-GCN was trained on a simulated thermal dataset of 4-chiplet SiP (with varying positional and power configurations), and the performance was then evaluated on unseen 6-chiplet SiP using both regression metrics and visual comparison of predicted versus actual temperature heatmaps.

title: SpectralCA: Utilising Hyper-Spectral Imaging in UAV Navigation
authors: D.V. Brovko
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: This paper presents SpectralCA, a novel approach to enhancing unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) navigation by leveraging hyperspectral imaging and cross-attention mechanisms. Conventional UAV navigation systems often rely on GPS and visual cues, which can be limited in environments with signal interference, poor visibility, or camouflaged objects. SpectralCA introduces a spectral-spatial cross-attention framework capable of extracting both spectral and spatial features from hyperspectral imagery, allowing UAVs to perceive terrain, vegetation, and man-made structures with higher accuracy. The methodology combines 2D and 3D convolutions with bidirectional attention layers to improve classification performance and robustness under challenging conditions. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that SpectralCA improves object recognition, path planning, and obstacle avoidance, supporting reliable autonomous navigation. These results highlight the potential of integrating hyperspectral imaging with advanced deep learning models to extend UAV operational capabilities in surveillance, environmental monitoring, and search-and-rescue missions.

title: Research on Performance Evaluation Method for Virtual Power Plant Participation in Frequency Regulation Based on AHP-CRITIC-TOPSIS
authors: Zhimin Liu, Lirong Xie, Gangding Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy in modern power systems, virtual power plants (VPPs) have emerged as flexible aggregation platforms for distributed energy resources (DERs). They enhance system flexibility, support secure grid operation, and provide frequency regulation ancillary services. However, current evaluation approaches for VPP frequency regulation performance remain limited, as they often adopt single-dimensional perspectives, lack coordination between technical and economic indicators, and fail to follow unified standards. To address these gaps, this paper develops a comprehensive evaluation index system encompassing technical capability, economic benefits, and environmental impacts. An improved AHP is applied to determine subjective weights, while the CRITIC method is employed to calculate objective weights. The Technique for Order Preference by TOPSIS is then utilized to obtain an integrated performance ranking. Furthermore, three representative VPP scenarios are analyzed to verify the proposed evaluation framework. The results provide a balanced and multidimensional assessment of VPP frequency regulation performance, offering theoretical and methodological support for the market-oriented development of VPPs.

title: Development of a Microwave Communication System for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Denys Bakhtiiarov, Bohdan Chumachenko, Oleksandr Lavrynenko, Oleksandr Kryvonosenko, Bohdan Kotyk
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Currently, the problem of creating high-bandwidth communication links between unmanned aerial vehicles and ground control stations remains in the limitations of traditional satellite and VHF communication systems. These systems not only lack bandwidth but also have very high losses in signal propagation at 30-300 km ranges which is their actual range of operation, especially when they are payload-limited less than 20 kg that is typical for medium to long-range UAVs. Technologies that should be used when developing military unmanned aerial vehicle microwave communication systems at Ka-band are optimized antenna configurations consisting of lightweight planar strip transmitter arrays (≈ 15 dBi gain) and high-gain parabolic offset ground receivers (≥ 36.6 dBi gain); power-efficient onboard transmitter architectures operating at < 0.3A/12V DC consumption; atmospheric attenuation compensation techniques for supporting 2.048 Mbps data rate over 30 km line-of-sight distance. This method uses a 39 GHz carrier frequency with BPSK modulation and achieves up to 8.4 dB calculated radio link budget margin using a 0.4 m ground antenna system. Also, the maintenance of the communication link characteristics in stable and transition modes of operation as well as methods for control of signal parameters and their implementation in an automatic link management system need to be defined. There is an issue regarding the provision of adaptive communication control reliability, operation capability in real-time harsh electromagnetic environments, remoteness in signal generation, and accuracy in maintaining data transmission parameters at different modes of operations.

title: Research on the Calibration and Verification Methods Based on Ergonomic Digital Standard Humans
authors: Zhihu Wang, Yating Zhang, Jing Zhao, Xin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of digital human technology, the lack of standardized methods for controlling and evaluating digital humans has led to inconsistencies in their appearance, behavior, and interaction quality. This paper proposes a comprehensive calibration framework based on an ergonomic digital standard human to enhance the realism, functionality, and interactivity of digital humans. The framework comprises four key modules: data acquisition, data analysis, calibration evaluation, and optimization. By integrating morphological, functional, and interaction metrics, the system enables precise calibration of digital humans against a reference model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves the naturalness, responsiveness, and user satisfaction of digital humans in virtual environments.

title: An Improved Informed-RRT* Mobile Robot Path Planning Method for Large-Scale Complex Environments
authors: Jingjing Chen, Baocheng Yu, Wenxia Xu, Guojun Chen, Yuming Ai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: To address the need for high-precision and high-efficiency path planning for mobile robots in large-scale complex environments, this paper proposes an improved Informed-RRT* algorithm, aiming to overcome the bottlenecks in planning efficiency and path quality caused by uniform sampling and fixed step size. The core of this algorithm lies in introducing an adaptive Gaussian mixture sampling mechanism, dynamically concentrating computational resources on high-value areas such as the target direction and path optimization; and combining this with an adaptive step size strategy to balance exploration speed and obstacle avoidance accuracy based on environmental obstacle information. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm significantly outperforms RRT* and the standard Informed-RRT* in terms of path cost and planning time, generating shorter and smoother paths. This research provides an efficient and reliable new path planning solution for autonomous robot navigation in complex structured environments.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Approach to Classification of Hematological Diseases
authors: Tasriful Haque, Tanmoy Mondal, Md Shohel Rana, Md Maruf Hasan, Md Abdul Ahad, Sahab Uddin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Anemia and blood-related diseases are a great challenge in the health sector and proper and prompt diagnosis is essential to ensure proper treatment. Conventional diagnostic techniques like manual blood smear examination require substantial time, are susceptible to operator errors and rely on skilled pathologists. This study aims to overcome these issues by proposing fully automated hematological diseases classification approach using deep learning and (CNNs). The dataset contains image files of each blood cell category (basophil, eosinophil, erythroblast, immunoglobulin (Ig), lymphocyte, monocyte, neutrophil and platelet) was separated into training, validation and testing sets with an equal number of images to ensure an unbiased training and model evaluation. The dataset is preprocessed with the help of data augmentation techniques to achieve better model to find generalization and accuracy. VGG19, widely-used variation of CNN, performed best among the different variations with the highest accuracy of 97.83% indicating its superior feature extracting and classification capabilities. Use several metrics such as precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrix and ROC curve analysis to evaluate the performance of the model to ensure stringent validation for its effectiveness. This method improves the speed and accuracy of hematological disease detection, minimizing reliance on manual analysis and enabling timely diagnosis. As such, this paper will empower the field of AI-based medical diagnostics by proposing a promising and parallelized blood cell classification method. Future work will enhance the dataset, include multimodal data and optimize the model for real-time clinical utility.

title: An Environment-Aware Digital-Twin Online Channel Modeling Platform for Indoor Scenarios
authors: Mingchuan Yao, Xingyao Shangguan, Shiyu Xiao, Tianrun Qi, Junling Li, Chen Huang, Cheng-Xiang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The sixth-generation (6G) mobile communication systems necessitate a dynamic and accurate channel model for effective system design and optimization. For indoor scenarios, sensing of human activities and precise modeling of dynamic channels remain critical challenges. This paper presents an environment-aware digital-twin online channel modeling (DTOCM) platform and the corresponding Unity-based interactive interface for indoor scenarios. First, a real-time method for dynamic sensing is proposed, which achieves online, time-aligned 3D pedestrian trajectories with IDs and velocities, improving sensing accuracy. Then, a sensing-driven deterministic-stochastic hybrid channel model is employed for characterizing dynamic channels. Sensing data are exploited to model human blockage effects by attenuating deterministic paths, while dynamic clusters are introduced to capture additional scattering. The platform provides an interactive visualization interface that renders spatial heatmaps and displays the delay power spectrum density (delay PSD) and angular spectra with low latency. In addition, it delivers high-precision dynamic channel information, enabling adaptive and real-time 6G network planning and optimization.

title: Design of an IoT-Based Intelligent Monitoring Platform for Community Health Management
authors: Jinlian Wu, Zheng Li, Alanoud Alsawaii, Maryam Alzaabi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: To address the lack of real-time monitoring in community health management, an IoT-based intelligent monitoring platform was designed. Employing an edge-cloud collaborative architecture, it integrates multi-source heterogeneous data fusion and intelligent analysis algorithms to achieve real-time monitoring and risk assessment of residents' health. Experiments show that this platform can improve event response speed, chronic disease management adherence, and resource allocation efficiency, promoting the transformation of community health management towards a proactive prevention and data-driven model.

title: Digital Twin-Assisted Causal Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Large-Scale Network Service Migration
authors: Chang Che, Luping Xiang, Jie Hu, Kun Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Dynamic service migration is essential for guaranteeing quality of service (QoS) for mobile users in multi-access edge computing (MEC). However, conventional reinforcement learning and other strategies frequently result in suboptimal policies. These methods are unable to differentiate between actual cause-and-effect relationships and fake patterns due to their reliance on correlational data, resulting in inefficient and expensive migrations. This paper introduces a novel digital twinassisted causal multi-agent reinforcement learning (DT-CausalMARL) framework to address this fundamental limitation. We change the optimization objective from minimizing direct system cost to maximizing the causal gain of migration actions. This is accomplished by employing a digital twin as a counterfactual analysis engine to assess the actual consequences of decisions in comparison to a baseline policy. This causal gain is subsequently employed as a robust reward signal by a causal multi-agent deep deterministic policy gradient (Causal-MADDPG) algorithm to train cooperative agents. Our framework outperforms standard MARL baselines by reducing harmful ‘regretful migrations' and improving system stability under dynamic traffic loads.

title: Deep Learning Based Beetle Insect Detection for Agricultural Monitoring Following Industry-Grade Best Practices
authors: Sagnik Mazumdar, Soham Banerjee, Satyanarayana Vollala, Tanishq Bhattacharjee, Tushar Kanti Bera
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The management of pests is essential to the health and productivity of agricultural crops. Pests represent many species that can cause economic harm to crops, but beetles, of which there are many different species, can be particularly damaging due to their potential to harm a wide variety of crops. This research focuses on the challenge of managing beetle pests through an automated classification system built on computer vision. We investigated a dataset that contains images of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 8}$</tex> species of beetles. Each species had a set of 200 images of high quality for adequate coverage and variability. We developed and trained a convolutional neural network (CNN) model on this dataset to produce an accuracy of 93% in classifying the 58 beetle species. Applying this technology in agriculture offers the opportunity to reduce damage to crops, increase yield, and contribute to sustainable forms of agriculture. Based on our study findings, computer vision has the potential to enhance agricultural pest management and can be a useful tool for both farmers and researchers.

title: ARN - Make SRv6 Services Visible to End Users
authors: Weiqiang Cheng, Feng Yang, Wenying Jiang, Xiaoqiu Zhang, Tingting Han, Geng Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: SRv6 has been widely deployed globally, but its use is limited to specific domains, making it inaccessible to users outside of these domains. This restricts its ability to directly provide services to users. To address this issue, some attempts have been made to use technologies such as ACL and DPI to classify traffic and steer it onto the expected SRv6 path. However, these technologies pose security risks and scalability issues, making them unsuitable for large-scale deployment. Our solution introduces a middle layer between the application and network layers, which encapsulates the SRv6 policy as a service ID that can be accessed outside of these limited domains. Through field trials, we have confirmed that this approach enables efficient programming of the SRv6 service across different network domains. We believe that this new mechanism, called ARN, will provide a way for applications to invoke network services, particularly in terms of differentiated services.

title: For Industrial Design: Exploration of Physical-Information-Guided Parameter-Free Function Generation Model for Airfoil Design
authors: Wang Yutong, Zhou Zhu, Liu Hongyang, Chen Qisheng, Yu Yonggang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Although generative models have demonstrated convincing cases in images and texts, ensuring the physical feasibility of generated results is an important challenge in engineering design. This paper focuses on a parameter-free function generation model for airfoil design—though it enables more flexible shape variation and diverse airfoil parameterization than generative models combined with traditional methods, its development is limited by poor smooth continuity of outputs. Considering airfoil application scenarios, this paper proposes general loss functions from geometric(crossed airfoils) and aerodynamic(rough airfoils) perspectives, integrating physical information into model training. Experimental results show the proposed method increases the physically valid rate of generated airfoils from 49.1% to 92%, without restricting diversity. The physical loss selection approach provides a reference for generative models in other engineering fields. The approach of selecting physical loss functions balancing generality and effectiveness provides a reference for generative models in other engineering design fields.

title: Automatic Recognition and Classification of Communication Modulation Signals based on Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Zheyuan Deng, Yang Qiu, Ziwen Li, Lantian Deng, Xiaoyu Yu, Deyue Zou, Suien Zhong, Yicheng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The wave of informatization has swept the world with unprecedented depth and breadth. In the face of massive growth of data and increasing demand, the integration of communication and artificial intelligence has become the core force leading a new round of scientific and technological revolution and industrial transformation. This paper studies the AMC(automatic recognition classification) technology based on deep learning. Aiming at the limitations of traditional modulation recognition methods in complex channel conditions and diverse signal environments, an automatic modulation recognition classification model based on convolutional neural network is proposed. After data preprocessing of the original I / Q sampling signal generated by the software radio device XSRP, as the input part of the model, the signal features are automatically extracted through multilayer convolution and pooling operations, and the full connection layer is used for classification. The experimental results show that the proposed CNN model shows excellent recognition performance under the condition of real environment signal-to-noise ratio, and its performance is significantly better than the traditional feature-based method. Through multiple sets of confusion matrix tests, the effectiveness and robustness of the model are verified.

title: Selection of a Subset of Effective Project Solutions Under Conditions of Interval Uncertainty
authors: Volodymyr Bezkorovainyi, Volodymyr Kobziev, Artem Khovrat, Yaroslav Luchenko
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: A new method for forming subsets of effective solutions under conditions of interval uncertainty of local criteria values was developed. The proposed approach addresses multi-criteria optimization challenges in design automation where project characteristics are determined through mathematical modeling with inherent errors. The method employs generalized Hukuhara difference estimates and associated comparison indices to evaluate project solutions when interval intersection occurs, utilizing situation analysis based on risk and reward assessment. The approach involves preliminary ranking based on averaged utility function values of local criteria, followed by pairwise comparisons using interval arithmetic. An additive utility model prevents substantial expansion of output data intervals. Experimental validation using UAV design optimization demonstrates effectiveness. Comparison indices based on generalized Hukuhara difference correspond to initial ordering via interval centers. The practical application increases efficiency of automated design technologies by formalizing procedures for selecting Pareto-optimal options for decision-maker evaluation in combinatorial multi-criteria problems.

title: iCBurnOut: A Web-Based YOLOv8 Segmentation Platform for Distinguishing Proximal Dental Caries and Cervical Burnout in Bitewing Digital Radiograph
authors: Mohd Isyrafuddin Bin Ismail, Nooritawati Md Tahir, Wan Syahirah W Samsudin, Mas Suryalis Ahmad, Mohd Yusmiaidil Putera Mohd Yusof
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Problem/Research Gap: Accurate differentiation between proximal dental caries and cervical burnout in bitewing radiographs remains a significant diagnostic challenge. These radiolucencies often overlap in appearance, leading to frequent misdiagnosis and unnecessary treatment. Despite advances in artificial intelligence for medical imaging, most solutions in dentistry are either experimental or require specialized hardware, limiting scalability and clinical adoption. Objective: This paper introduces iCBurnOut, a secure web-based artificial intelligence platform that leverages YOLOv8 segmentation to provide real-time detection and delineation of proximal caries and cervical burnout. The goal is to deliver an accessible, scalable, and privacy-preserving diagnostic support system for dental professionals and educators. Methodology: Annotated bitewing radiographs were preprocessed (resizing to 640 × 640 pixels, normalization, and augmentation) and used to train a YOLOv8m segmentation model. Model performance was validated internally on a general population dataset and externally on medically compromised patients, with evaluation metrics including precision, recall, mAP0.5, and mAP0.5-0.95. The validated model was embedded into a browser-based platform enabling secure image upload, real-time inference, and automatic data deletion. Usability and adoption potential were further assessed using the System Usability Scale (SUS), Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), and education-specific evaluation items.

title: TempoNet: Learning Realistic Communication and Timing Patterns for Network Traffic Simulation
authors: Kristen Moore, Diksha Goel, Cody James Christopher, Zhen Wang, Minjune Kim, Ahmed Ibrahim, Ahmad Mohsin, Seyit Camtepe
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Realistic network traffic simulation is critical for evaluating intrusion detection systems, stress-testing network protocols, and constructing high-fidelity environments for cybersecurity training. While attack traffic can often be layered into training environments using red-teaming or replay methods, generating authentic benign background traffic remains a core challenge-particularly in simulating the complex temporal and communication dynamics of real-world networks. This paper introduces TempoNet, a novel generative model that combines multi-task learning with multi-mark temporal point processes to jointly model inter-arrival times and all packet- and flow-header fields. TempoNet captures fine-grained timing patterns and higher-order correlations such as host- pair behavior and seasonal trends, addressing key limitations of GAN-, LLM-, and Bayesian-based methods that fail to reproduce structured temporal variation. TempoNet produces temporally consistent, high-fidelity traces, validated on real-world datasets. Furthermore, we show that intrusion detection models trained on TempoNet-generated background traffic perform comparably to those trained on real data, validating its utility for real-world security applications.

title: WiID: WiFi-based Identity Recognition Using In-Air Handwriting Gestures
authors: Fangya Jia, Chuxin Kong, Min Peng
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: WiFi-based identity recognition has garnered significant attention in recent years owing to its contact-free operation, low deployment cost, and non-intrusive nature. This paper presents a WiFi-based identity recognition system founded on a concentric circle model. The proposed system employs a single transmitter–receiver pair and models wireless signal propagation through a carefully designed spatial configuration, enabling robust user identity recognition. Specifically, signal amplitudes are first extracted from the WiFi Channel State Information (CSI). Based on these amplitudes, a multidimensional feature vector is constructed, incorporating the raw amplitude, amplitude variance, signal energy, and amplitude first-order difference, to comprehensively characterize the temporal dynamics of user interactions. The resulting feature tensor is then fed into a two-dimensional convolutional neural network (2D-CNN) for end-to-end learning, enabling automatic user identity classification. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method achieves robust identity recognition performance across various test gestures.

title: Gaussian process based Semantic communication for translational pose prediction in vehicles
authors: Simy M Baby, E.S. Gopi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Semantic communication enables the transmission of only task-relevant information, reducing redundancy, improving efficiency, and enabling low-latency processing in data-driven applications. In vehicular networks, where high-dimensional sensor and visual data are generated continuously, transmitting full feature embeddings for pose estimation is bandwidth-intensive and computationally expensive. We propose a decoupled semantic Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) framework, where visual features extracted from pretrained VGG-16 networks are projected into compact kernel representations that are transmitted through a MIMO wireless channel. At the receiver, MMSE-detected kernels are then used with per-dimension GPR models to predict the translational poses. Experiments on the KITTI odometry dataset demonstrate that semantic kernel transmission preserves task-relevant information, ensuring good pose prediction while significantly reducing communication overhead.

title: Enhancing WSN Data Security using a Randomized Asymmetric Certificateless Arbitrated Quantum Signature Scheme
authors: Ahmed M. Khedr, S. Sheeja Rani, Abo Bakr A. Khedr
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs), consisting of spatially distributed sensor nodes (SNs) that collect and transmit data to a base station, often operate in environments where secure communication is critical. Classical cryptographic techniques, while effective, frequently struggle to balance strong confidentiality with low latency, leading to vulnerabilities and inefficiencies. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes the Randomized Asymmetric Certificate-less Arbitrated Quantum Signature (RACAQS) model, a novel quantum-assisted security framework for WSNs. The model integrates the McEliece Certificate-less cryptographic scheme with Arbitrated Quantum Signature mechanisms, ensuring robust confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of transmitted data. RACAQS operates through three core phases: key pair generation, arbitrated quantum signcryption, and arbitrated quantum unsigncryption. Through these processes, SNs achieve secure key distribution, quantum-based encryption, and reliable verification with minimal overhead. Simulation results demonstrate that RACAQS achieves up to 4% higher confidentiality, integrity, and packet delivery ratio, and a 14% reduction in computation overhead, compared with existing schemes.

title: Development of MEMS Pressure Sensor with High Proof Pressure for Multiple Discrete Adjustable Ranges up to 10...60 kPa
authors: Mikhail Basov
abstract: The various application conditions of MEMS pressure sensors require achieving a wide range of specific parameters. The ability of performance retention for pressure sensor by repeated proof pressure is one of the important requirements. The research presents a new microassembly design for the piezoresistive pressure sensor chip for multiple discrete adjustable differential ranges, where the minimum range is from -10 to 10 kPa and the maximum range is from -60 to +60 kPa. The microassembly design contains stops on both sides of the chip to increase the proof pressure to P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">proof</sub> = 1.5 MPa. Additionally, the microassembly also allows for significant improvement of mechanical decoupling of chip from packing and all existing mechanical connections. The research demonstrates the effectiveness of error reduction in the case of preliminary operation by thermocycling and barocycling for pressure sensor. In results, more than 120 samples of developed pressure sensor have sensitivity S = 0.61 ± 0.15 mV/V/kPa and low error for nonlinearity 2K<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">NL</sub> < 0.20%/FS, mechanical hysteresis H < 0.10%/FS and mechanical repeatability R < 0.05%/FS, as well as for the effect of proof pressure dU<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Pproof</sub> < 0.30%/FS and all-round compression with pressure of 16 MPa dU<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">comp</sub> < 0.15%/FS. The significant advantage of the microassembly design for removing the residual mechanical stresses (RMS) is also summarized in the achievement of relatively low uncompensated error for long-term storage stability during 1 year for the zero signal dU<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> <sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">stab</sub> < 0.15 %/FS and sensitivity dU<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> <sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">stab</sub> < 0.20%/FS.

title: Effective Practices for User-Centered Instant Localization of a Screen Reader Software
authors: Zsuzsanna B. Palmer
abstract: Introduction: This case study's purpose is to make visible the skills and knowledge necessary for the instant localization of screen readers. About the case: The case study examines the work of localization experts at a nonprofit organization in Hungary, who localize the proprietary Job Access with Speech (JAWS) screen reader software and support its target users. Situating the case: The study was informed by research in translation studies and localization-focused literature within the field of technical and professional communication. Research on accessible usability and software design was also consulted. Methods/approach: Participant observations and interviews with employees of the nonprofit organization and with the software's users were conducted. Data were transcribed, then coded using qualitative data-analysis methods. Codes that emerged from the data were grouped into themes to create a narrative interwoven with quotes about the activities of localization experts. Results/discussion: The findings from this study show that the instant localization process used by localization experts of this software requires a specific set of skills in addition to those used in project-based approaches to localization. Additional language and communication skills, as well as programming knowledge to develop additional program features and training materials, were found to be essential for addressing all users’ needs. Conclusion: Technical and professional communication practitioners can contribute to the localization of adaptive technologies through their strong usability, user experience, and communication skills.

title: Mixture-of-Expert Large Language Models for text-based Personality Assessment from Asynchronous Video Interviews
authors: Tianyi Zhang, Shan Liang, Wenming Zheng, Antonis Koutsoumpis, Janneke K. Oostrom, Reinout E. de Vries
abstract: In selection and assessment, Large Language Models (LLMs) are deemed suitable for personality assessment of Asynchronous Video Interviews (AVI) due to their advanced linguistic understanding and semantic interpretation capacities. However, most of the previous works have focused on personality trait classification rather than regression. Since current LLMs are trained on massive corpora, they are more attuned to text-based structures than to numeric data. The classification-centered approach fails to take into account the fact that personality traits are continuous, instead of categorical, variables. In addition, LLMs suffer from low rating validity due to their tendency to be over-lenient, assigning higher than average personality scores to a large number of individuals (i.e., issues of Positivity Biases). To address these challenges, we designed a text-based, two-stage, Mixture-of-Experts based personality assessment framework (MoE-Personality) to provide fine-grained personality ratings and regulate the positivity biases of LLMs. The designed model first rates the coarse-grained personality category of the individual (classification stage). After that, the model rates fine-grained personality scores by merging the obtained personality category and empirical score ranges of different personality categories. Inspired by the the way human annotators rate personality traits, each stage comprises multiple annotator LLM experts and one aggregator LLM expert to promote validity. Our experiments in two datasets show that the designed framework outperformed open-sourced, medium-sized LLMs (e.g., Llama 3.1-8B, qwen2-7B) and achieved comparable results with close-sourced, large-sized LLMs (e.g., GPT-3.5 and GPT-4).

title: Calculating the Volume-Averaged Space-Charge-Limited Current Density by Simulating Vacuum Capacitance
authors: Jack K. Wright, Samuel T. Unzicker, Allen L. Garner
abstract: While analytic results for the space-charge-limited current density (SCLCD) exist for relatively simple geometries, more realistic geometries often require computational approaches, such as particle-in-cell (PIC) simulations. We applied a recent theory that relates the volume-averaged SCLCD (VASCLCD) to the vacuum capacitance to calculate the VASCLCD for 1-D and multidimensional geometries by performing electrostatic simulations using the commercial code COMSOL Multiphysics without requiring particles. We recovered the Child–Langmuir law (CLL) for a 1-D planar diode by choosing the computational domain to eliminate fringing field contributions. We also obtained the VASCLCD for a 1-D cylindrical geometry in agreement with the analytic solution. For 2-D geometries, the VASCLCD using the capacitance from COMSOL agreed well with the analytic results for computational domains sufficiently large to account for fringing fields. For 3-D geometries, the VASCLCD obtained analytically without corner fringing fields (CFFs) agreed well with COMSOL results without CFFs. COMSOL calculations incorporating CFFs disagreed strongly with theory for 3-D geometries where the electrode width <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$W$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and length <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L$</tex-math> </inline-formula> were both smaller than the gap distance <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$D$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. The difference between the VASCLCD obtained with or without CFFs diminishes for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${L} / {D} \gt {1}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and/or <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${W} / {D} \gt {1}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, which mitigates the contributions of CFFs since the geometry becomes effectively 2-D. These results demonstrate the feasibility of using electrostatic simulations to rapidly approximate the SCLCD without requiring particles or time-domain simulations.

title: Reconfigurable Multiscroll Memristive Neural Network With Application to Telemedicine Privacy Protection
authors: Qiang Lai, Minghong Qin, Xiao-Wen Zhao
abstract: Constructing memristive neural networks (MNNs) with multiscroll chaotic attractors helps advance both theoretical and applied research on neural networks. However, the existing models mainly utilize complex memristor models with polynomial functions, nested composite functions, and so on, to generate multiscroll chaotic attractors, which leads to increased model complexity and difficulties in on-demand adjustment. Hence, this article proposes a reconfigurable multiscroll MNN (RMMNN) that can yield different types of multiscroll chaotic attractors merely by altering the memristive parameters without modifying its model. Through numerical methods, the complex dynamics of the RMMNN in different cases are analyzed, such as parameter-controlled multiscroll chaotic attractors, adjustable multistability, and parameter-induced transitions of multistability. In addition, the reliability of the numerical analysis is verified via the hardware circuits. Moreover, to address the issues of image security and low quality in telemedicine, a bidirectional rotation medical image encryption scheme (BRMIES) is developed based on the good pseudorandom chaotic sequences generated by RMMNN. Performance analysis demonstrates that BRMIES can effectively protect medical image and robustly handle various potential adverse interferences within telemedicine process.

title: Cogformer: A unified multi-scale brain representation for visual decoding and reconstruction from fMRI
authors: Xu Yin, John Q. Gan, Haixian Wang
abstract: With the rapid development of deep generative models (DGMs), the performance of decoding language and reconstructing images from Functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI) has been improved. Nevertheless, the accurate representation of brain activity remains highly challenging, primarily due to the limited paired samples and the low signal-to-noise ratios of fMRI. To tackle these challenges, we introduce Cogformer, a unified multi-scale brain representation method. It is the first to learn brain representation from multi-scale fMRI activities via self-attention, and integrate a synchronized decoding and dynamic decoupling strategy for structural and semantic features through cross-attention. We conduct a systematic evaluation of Cogformer on the large-scale Natural Scenes Dataset (NSD) across a broad range of visual decoding tasks, including category classification, multi-label classification, image retrieval, image captioning, and image reconstruction. To the best of our knowledge, this represents the most extensive task coverage reported in related research. Cogformer achieves superior performance compared to a range of transformer-based baselines in category classification, multi-label classification, and image retrieval tasks. Moreover, in the more challenging tasks of image captioning and image reconstruction, Cogformer leverages a prior diffusion module to enhance the alignment with image semantics. This further improves the semantic consistency for caption generation and visual fidelity in image reconstruction. Across multiple evaluation metrics, Cogformer demonstrates competitive performance against existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods, highlighting its strong decoding capabilities and generalization potential.

title: Privacy-Preserving Digital Publishing Framework for Next-Generation Communication Networks: A Verifiable Homomorphic Federated Learning Approach
authors: Yanxu Lin, Renzhong Zhong, Jingnan Xie, Yueting Zhu, Byung-Gyu Kim, Saru Kumari, Shakila Basheer, Fatimah Alhayan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Next-generation communication networks are revolutionizing digital publishing through intelligent content distribution and collaborative optimization capabilities. However, existing federated learning approaches face fundamental limitations, including trusted third-party dependencies, excessive communication overhead, and vulnerability to collusion attacks between servers and participants. This paper introduces VHFL-DP, a verifiable homomorphic federated learning framework for digital publishing environments operating within 6G network infrastructures. The framework addresses critical privacy and scalability challenges through four key innovations: a distributed cryptographic key generation protocol that eliminates trusted third-party requirements, Chinese remainder theorem-based dimensionality reduction, auxiliary validation nodes that enable independent verification with constant-time complexity, and an intelligent incentive mechanism that rewards digital publishing platforms based on objective contribution quality metrics. Experimental evaluation on MNIST and Amazon reviews datasets across six baseline methods demonstrates that VHFL-DP achieves superior performance with accuracy improvements of 4.2% over the best baseline method. The framework maintains constant verification time ranging from 2.73 to 2.91 seconds regardless of platform count, increasing from ten to fifty, or dropout rates reaching thirty percent. Security evaluation reveals strong resilience with only 2.4 percentage point accuracy degradation under poisoning attacks compared to 6.7-7.0 points for baseline method, inference attack success near random guessing at 51.3%, and 92.4% successful aggregation under Byzantine adversaries.

title: Steering Committee
authors: N/A

title: Air-Core Resonant Converter Design With Reduced Secondary Reactance and Primary Current for Wide Voltage Range CC/CV and ZVS
authors: Dukju Ahn, Waqar Hussain Khan, Seongmin Kim, Inkui Cho
abstract: This paper proposes an air core, minimum frequency variation, and wide output range resonant converter for lightweight aerial mobility with full zero-voltage switching (ZVS) region. Traditionally, an air-core transformer suffers from low magnetic coupling between primary and secondary windings, thereby increasing resonant current at primary winding. Meanwhile, even if wireless power transfer (WPT) has been successful with air-core magnetics, the parameter design and control strategies of air-core WPT cannot be directly used due to reduced ZVS region, lower power factor, and increased circuit components. Unlike conventional converters which resonate only with leakage inductance, the proposed converter's secondary winding resonates with total self-inductance (which includes both leakage and magnetizing inductances) of air-core transformer. In this way, lower amplitude of primary current is enough to output a given power. The proposed design also achieves a full-range ZVS and exhibits a natural constant-current (CC) characteristic with negligible frequency variation. The air-core magnetic is lightweight, low volume, and resistant to mechanical shock which are required by Aerial Drone. The measured peak efficiency is 96.9%. The output voltage range is 35-120V with 10.5A maximum current across the frequency variation of 380 kHz∼445 kHz.

title: Towards Object Segmentation Mask Selection Using Specular Reflections
authors: Katja Kossira, Yunxuan Zhu, Jürgen Seiler, André Kaup
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Specular reflections pose a significant challenge for object segmentation, as their sharp intensity transitions often mislead both conventional algorithms and deep learning based methods. However, as the specular reflection must lie on the surface of the object, this fact can be exploited to improve the segmentation masks. By identifying the largest region containing the reflection as the object, we derive a more accurate object mask without requiring specialized training data or model adaption. We evaluate our method on both synthetic and real world images and compare it against established and state-of-the-art techniques including Otsu thresholding, YOLO, and SAM2. Compared to the best performing baseline SAM2, our approach achieves up to 26.7% improvement in IoU, 22.3% in DSC, and 9.7% in pixel accuracy. Qualitative evaluations on real world images further confirm the robustness and generalizability of the proposed approach.

title: Strategies for Vehicle Localization Fusing IMU/WSS/SAS Measurements During GNSS Outages
authors: Costanzo Manes, Davide Pezzuti
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: This paper considers the localization problem of wheeled vehicles when absolute GNSS positioning is unavailable. The case is considered where measurements from low-cost on-board sensors, like Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs), Wheel Speed Sensors (WSSs), and Steer Angle Sensors (SASs), are available. This problem can be framed as a problem of sensor fusion, for which, in general, a broad literature is available. In this paper the peculiarities of the localization problem and of the sensors in use are discussed, and a new model is proposed to exploit the wheel speed measurements that does not assume the absence of lateral slipping, like most models in use do. The vehicle kinematics together with the new sensor model are used in an Extended Kalman Filter to provide position, orientation, and velocity of the vehicle during GNSS outages. The resulting, unavoidable, error drift is evaluated when different sets of measurements are used.

title: Study on heat transfer characteristics during the melting process of triple-tube latent heat storage unit with snowflake-shaped fins
authors: Shenao Li, Maocheng Tian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Phase change energy storage technology is an effective means to alleviate the contradiction between energy supply and demand and promote the consumption of renewable energy, but the low thermal conductivity of phase change materials (PCMs) restricts its practical application. In this paper, a bionic snowflake-shaped fin applied to the triple-tube latent heat storage unit (TTLHSU) is designed. Through numerical simulation, the effects of characteristic size, Stefan number, and Grashof number on the melting process of lauric acid are systematically studied, and the dynamic coupling mechanism between heat conduction and natural convection is revealed. The study shows that increasing the Stefan number can significantly accelerate the melting process, but the enhancement effect exhibits a diminishing marginal effect; when the Stefan number is the same, reducing the characteristic size can effectively shorten the heat transfer path and significantly reduce the total melting time, and its effect is better than simply enhancing natural convection. Finally, a liquid fraction prediction correlation based on the Fourier number, Stefan number, and Grashof number is established, which provides theoretical support for the heat transfer enhancement mechanism of fractal fins and the optimal design of energy storage units.

title: Knowledge Graph-Enhanced Chinese Intelligent Agent for Tumor Rehabilitation
authors: Yu Wang, Zenghui Ding, Min Yang, Dong Ding, Ninguang Yao, Shu Xu, Zheng Chang, Xianjun Yang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: This study develops a knowledge graph-based system for tumor exercise rehabilitation, integrating retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to enhance question-answering capabilities. The system leverages large language models (LLMs), knowledge graphs to establish a complete reasoning process, from data acquisition to precise rehabilitation recommendations. It adopts a three-tier architecture comprising the perception layer, logic layer, and application layer, enabling efficient data processing and prescription reasoning. The frontend is implemented using Python, while the backend employs MySQL for relational data storage and Neo4j for graph-based data management.In the tumor exercise rehabilitation knowledge graph visualization module, this study details the construction of the display interface, retrieval module, and storage update mechanism. The retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) question-answering module includes a retrieval component, a scoring mechanism, and a historical record storage module, facilitating accurate and context-aware responses.

title: Effect of Inductive Compensation Design on Wireless Power Transfer Efficiency in Low Power Applications
authors: Muhamad Syazmie Sepeeh, Miriam Elena Ondo Nguba, Hanis Farhah Jamahori, Shamsul Aizam Zulkifli, Ronald Jackson, Nadiah Ismail
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) has emerged as a game-changing technique for transferring energy without the use of physical connectors.
