However, heat dissipation continues to be a significant barrier in terms of efficiency. This study investigates inductive circuit compensation techniques to optimise WPT efficiency, focusing on minimising heat losses and enhancing power transfer stability. A comparative analysis of compensation circuits with varying capacitance values of 47 μF for a few numbers of capacitors is conducted using MATLAB/Simulink simulation. The results show that effective compensation increases efficiency significantly at 98.2%, lowering losses while maintaining a stable output DC voltage of 12 V at 2.1 A. This effort adds to the advancement of low-power WPT applications by improving compensation strategies for practical feasibility.

title: Modeling and Optimization of Passive IoT Based Wireless Sensing with Fresnel Zone Theory
authors: Yingting Yuan, Hongjun Wan, Yuanhang Li, Xize Wang, Tao Li, Lulu He
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Passive Internet of Things (IoT) based on UltraHigh Frequency Radio Frequency Identification (UHF RFID) has recently advanced from identification to wireless sensing, yet conventional monostatic architectures suffer from limited coverage and network scalability. The emerging transmitter-receiver separated design mitigated these issues but creates asymmetric links and aggravates position dependence problem. This paper investigates passive IoT based wireless sensing modeling and optimization under the transmitter-receiver separated architecture by extending the Fresnel zone reflection model. We reveal that tag phase control reshapes the relationship between static and dynamic signal components, thereby establishing it as a key design dimension for passive wireless sensing, and propose two optimization strategies. The first, dynamic Fresnel zone reconstruction, aligns targets with sensitivity zones, thereby enhancing the detectability and accuracy of fine-grained activity sensing. The second, position-dependence compensation, adaptively mitigates spatial phase variations, thus improving the stability of passive wireless sensing. Simulation results demonstrate that higher tag phase resolution yields better performance.

title: Toward a Low-Carbon Kitakyushu: Opportunities and Roadmap for Urban Renewable Energy Utilization
authors: Qianqian Lin
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: This paper applies a spatial–technical–economic screening to a 30-building municipal portfolio in Kitakyushu. Using conservative inputs (usable roof ratio 0.55; 180 W/m² density; 1,100 kWh/kW.y yield), the method identifies 9.7 MW of rooftop PV producing 10.9 GWh/y. With 2 h batteries, median selfconsumption increases from 0.55→0.70, raising on-site use to 7.6 GWh/y. A simple LCOE model (CAPEX ¥160,000/kW; OPEX ¥3,000/kW.y; 20 y; 3% discount) yields a rooftop-PV LCOE of ¥12.5/kWh (±15%: ¥10.6–14.8/kWh). A short Eco-Town residualheat spur (95–65 °C, 12 kg/s, 1.2 km) delivers ≈1.6 GWhₜʰ/y to nearby public loads. The framework is auditable and reproducible, and the roadmap is grounded in these quantitative outputs.

title: Towards Function-Level Correctness Assessment of System Software with LLMs: A Case Study
authors: Ming Zhong, Xin Sun
abstract: System software is highly complex and requires significant manual development effort. With the wide application of large language models (LLMs) in software engineering, LLMs are increasingly used to automatically generate system software at the function level. However, due to their limited accuracy, the generated functions often contain errors, which prevent them from being compiled and executed for regression testing. As a result, developers must manually debug these functions, which is time-consuming and heavily dependent on expertise. In this paper, we first emphasize the emerging need for assessing function correctness without relying on regression tests in the context of LLM-generated system software. Next, we propose a preliminary data-driven approach, TeleSys, which generates a substantial amount of function variants (covering correct and erroneous functions) to fine-tune LLMs for correctness assessment. Preliminary results show that an LLM (QWen2.5-Coder-1.5B) fine-tuned with TeleSys-generated data outperforms two prompt-based baselines (ICE-Score and SWE-Judge, combined with QWen2.5-Coder-32B and GPT-4o-mini), demonstrating the effectiveness of our data-driven approach in improving the accuracy of correctness assessment for LLM-generated system software. Finally, we propose two future directions to enable more effective deployment of LLMs for system software correctness assessment in practical scenarios.

title: Towards Engineering Multi-Agent LLMs: A Protocol-Driven Approach
authors: Zhenyu Mao, Jacky Keung, Fengji Zhang, Shuo Liu, Yifei Wang, Jialong Li
abstract: The increasing demand for software development has driven interest in automating software engineering (SE) tasks using Large Language Models (LLMs). Recent efforts extend LLMs into multi-agent systems (MAS) that emulate collaborative development workflows, but these systems often fail due to three core deficiencies: under-specification, coordination misalignment, and inappropriate verification, arising from the absence of foundational SE structuring principles. This paper introduces Software Engineering Multi-Agent Protocol (SEMAP), a protocol-layer methodology that instantiates three core SE design principles for multi-agent LLMs: (1) explicit behavioral contract modeling, (2) structured messaging, and (3) lifecycleguided execution with verification, and is implemented atop Google’s Agent-to-Agent (A2A) infrastructure. Empirical evaluation using the Multi-Agent System Failure Taxonomy (MAST) framework demonstrates that SEMAP effectively reduces failures across different SE tasks. In code development, it achieves up to a $69.6 \%$ reduction in total failures for function-level development and $\mathbf{5 6 . 7 \%}$ for deployment-level development. For vulnerability detection, SEMAP reduces failure counts by up to $47.4 \%$ on Python tasks and $28.2 \%$ on $\mathrm{C} / \mathrm{C}++$ tasks.

title: Beyond Engagement: Factors Driving AI Satisfaction and Continued Use in Indonesian Universities
authors: Yedija Barnabas Sugiarto, Ahmad Syamil
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The development of artificial intelligence tools has opened many opportunities for various institutions in the world. One of the fields affected by artificial intelligence is the field of university education. This research aims to analyse the effect of AI on improving university student learning outcomes in Indonesia. The data to be used in this research is primary data using a Likert scale type of survey to determine 236 students' tendencies towards certain questions. The study is conducted to highlight the benefits of using AI in the educational sector, i.e., how AI aids students in conquering obstacles in their academic pursuits and in determining whether any negative consequences might arise as a result. The analysis using SmartPLS shows that AI has positive effects on helping students in their academic endeavours.

title: HEEF: A Hybrid Engineering Evaluation Framework for AI R&D Team Efficiency Prediction using Meta Kaggle
authors: Jianjun Liu, Xiaolong Niu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Accurately evaluating the efficiency of Artificial Intelligence (AI) research and development (R&D) teams is a critical yet challenging engineering problem, as traditional productivity metrics fail to capture the iterative, data-dependent, and non-linear nature of AI workflows. This paper proposes HEEF (Hybrid Engineering Evaluation Framework), a novel data-driven framework designed to predict and analyze AI R&D efficiency by leveraging the large-scale Meta Kaggle dataset. HEEF introduces a multi-dimensional feature engineering strategy that integrates individual capability baselines, R&D investment intensity, graph-based collaborative structures, and dynamic learning curves. To balance computational efficiency with modeling complexity, the framework employs a two-stage learning architecture: utilizing Node2vec for unsupervised representation of collaboration networks and LightGBM for robust supervised regression. Furthermore, SHAP-based interpretability is incorporated to transform "black-box" predictions into actionable diagnostic insights. Experimental results demonstrate that HEEF significantly outperforms conventional machine learning baselines, achieving a 12.7% improvement in NDCG@10 and an 18.2% reduction in RMSE. Beyond predictive accuracy, our analysis reveals critical industrial insights, such as the inverted U-shaped relationship between team size and efficiency and the superiority of adaptive experimentation rhythms. This work provides a systematic tool and empirical foundation for optimizing AI engineering resources in large-scale information systems.

title: MSVD-Pred: A Multi-Scale Variational Diffusion Model for System Load Forecasting
authors: Mingdong He, Zhaoyi Yang, Hao Wei, Xiaodong Zhang, Yunde Liang, Bo Li, Xuewu Li, Jie Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: The rapid convergence of computing and communication networks makes system load highly complex, nonlinear, and stochastic across multiple time scales. Accurate forecasting of operating load is essential for resource scheduling, SLA assurance, and capacity planning, yet traditional time-series and deep models struggle to capture both long-term trends and short-term fluctuations under uncertainty. We propose MSVD-Pred, a multiscale variational diffusion based probabilistic forecasting framework for system load. Historical load sequences are decomposed into second-, minute-, and hour-level components and encoded by frequency-adaptive GRU, TCN, and attention modules to learn complementary multi-scale latent representations. A conditional diffusion generator in latent space models the distribution of future load under heterogeneous contextual conditions. To better handle extreme peaks and abrupt changes, an Adaptive Peak Enhancement (APE) module dynamically emphasizes highintensity segments during training, while a Residual Closed-Loop Optimization (RCLO) mechanism iteratively refines predictions by feeding back multi-step residual patterns. Experiments on computing-network load datasets show that MSVD-Pred consistently outperforms representative statistical, deep learning, and generative baselines in terms of accuracy, sharpness, and calibration, while maintaining robust performance across different time resolutions and load regimes.

title: Learning to Optimize Low-Latency Live Streaming from Expertise: An Offline Meta-Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Yuhui Du, Nuowen Kan, Chenglin Li, Junni Zou, Ziyang Zheng, Shaohui Li, Wenrui Dai, Qingli Li, Hongkai Xiong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: In low-latency live streaming (LLLS), the adaptive bitrate algorithm plays a critical role in optimizing encoding bitrates to meet the strict end-to-end latency requirement, which is mostly several seconds or less. Existing methods usually rely on a fixed preset latency target, limiting their generalization capabilities across the heterogeneous scenarios. To address this, we propose an offline meta-reinforcement learning-based bitrate decision framework for LLLS, which incorporates the expertise of multiple state-of-the-art LLLS algorithms from their collected experience and constructs a universal bitrate selection policy via the offline training. Specifically, we establish an RL-based policy that adaptively adjusts the network throughput measurements and selects the encoding bitrates for LLLS based on the network conditions. The policy’s performance is enhanced by improving the robustness of short-term throughput estimations. To leverage the expertise of current LLLS algorithms, we collect their decision-making trajectories, and directly train the policy on these offline trajectories with implicit Q-learning. In addition, a meta-RL paradigm is further adopted to learn a universal policy that performs uniformly well across the heterogeneous network conditions and varying target latencies. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to the other baselines, the proposed method achieves at least a 5.8% performance gain in terms of the overall quality of experience (QoE), across the diverse target-latency scenarios in the real-world throughput traces.

title: GATUNER:Genetic Algorithm Applied to Floating-Point Precision Tuning
authors: Jianing Zhang, Jinchen Xu, Hongru Yang, Tao Zhang, Shuai Wang, Bei Zhou
abstract: Floating-point arithmetic is widely used in high-precision computing fields such as national defense, aerospace, and finance. Different applications have varying requirements for floating-point computation precision, and how to maximize program performance while meeting precision requirements is a crucial challenge in high-performance program design. Mixed-precision technology is a common optimization strategy to address this issue, which involves using multiple precision types within the same program. However, most existing research on mixed precision tends to fall into local optima and fails to directly provide users with usable mixed-precision programs. Since genetic algorithms are optimization methods with global search capabilities, we propose a genetic algorithm-based mixed-precision tuning method and develop a tool called GATUNER. Specifically, GATUNER first analyzes the variables and statements of the program to construct a dependency graph of the program. Then, it uses the Leiden algorithm to detect community structures in the dependency graph, employs an improved genetic algorithm to search for mixed-precision configurations, and finally automatically generates the corresponding mixed-precision program. We evaluated GATUNER on 14 benchmark tests and application programs. The experimental results show that GATUNER can find valid mixed-precision configurations for 76.79% of the test programs, with an average performance improvement of 31.65% for the found mixed-precision configurations. Compared with HIFPTUNER, ADAPT, and CHEF-FP, the average performance improvement of the mixed-precision configurations found by GATUNER increased by 14.1%, 24.09%, and 15.18% respectively. In addition, compared with PESA, the average performance improvement of the mixed-precision configurations obtained by GATUNER increased by 18.67%.

title: Domain Adaptation for Camera-Specific Image Characteristics using Shallow Discriminators
authors: Maximiliane Gruber, Jürgen Seiler, André Kaup
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Each image acquisition setup leads to its own camera-specific image characteristics degrading the image quality. In learning-based perception algorithms, characteristics occurring during the application phase, but absent in the training data, lead to a domain gap impeding the performance. Previously, pixel-level domain adaptation through unpaired learning of the pristine-to-distorted mapping function has been proposed. In this work, we propose shallow discriminator architectures to address limitations of these approaches. We show that a smaller receptive field size improves learning of unknown image distortions by more accurately reproducing local distortion characteristics at a low network complexity. In a domain adaptation setup for instance segmentation, we achieve mean average precision increases over previous methods of up to 0.15 for individual distortions and up to 0.16 for camera-specific image characteristics in a simplified camera model. In terms of number of parameters, our approach matches the complexity of one state of the art method while reducing complexity by a factor of 20 compared to another, demonstrating superior efficiency without compromising performance.

title: Smart Supply Chain Management with Artificial Intelligence: Toward Resilient and Sustainable Industrial Automation in Industry 4.0
authors: Irma Rizkia, Hubertus Davy Yulianto
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The global supply chain landscape is being reshaped by the convergence of Industry 4.0 technologies, especially Artificial Intelligence (AI), which enables smarter, more resilient, and sustainable logistics and production systems. This study proposes a design science approach to developing an AI-enabled decision support system (DSS) tailored for supply chain resilience and environmental sustainability in emerging industrial contexts. By applying the Design Science Research Methodology (DSRM), we present a system composed of predictive analytics, real-time optimization, and digital twin simulation modules. A simulation-based case study in Indonesia's fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) sector shows that the DSS improved order fulfillment rate by 9 %, reduced logistics costs by 18 %, and lowered carbon emissions by 19 %. The study contributes a validated and modular framework for smart SCM implementation aligned with the principles of Industry 4.0 and the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

title: Tackling Food Loss and Waste in the Food Supply Chain Through AI Technology: Current Trends, Use, and Future Agenda
authors: Fauzan Romadlon, Karina Amanda Larasati, Abdul Hamid
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The human population in the world is expected to reach 9.8 billion people in 2050. Due to this, food production will be needed to satisfy more than average consumption, and an increase in wastage in all supply chain stages. The most probable action to respond to the situation is to promote more dietary knowledge. Dietary knowledge is one of the actions to minimize food loss and waste (FLW). FLW is caused by inefficient activities, especially at the post-harvest level, and it needs technology adoption, one of which is AI. Adopting AI technology can emphasize the intervention in production and eliminate FLW. AI technology can support tracking perishable food, food delivery, and crop selection to tackle FLW in the food supply chain. This article examines a systematic literature review on the applications of AI technology to prevent FLW. The review used the reporting standard for the systematic evidence synthesis (ROSES) protocol and selected articles from Scopus, ScienceDirect, and Google Scholar. This review was separated into the supply chain level: farmer, collector, food industry, retailer, and consumer. This review suggests several contributions to the practice and body of knowledge about a new perspective on using AI technology in FLW prevention. It offers an alternative strategy for related stakeholders to apply AI technology from farmers to consumers. The challenges of AI technology implementation can vary based on regional conditions, crop product variety, integration with existing systems, and collaboration among the stakeholders in the supply chain.

title: Collaborative Surface Reconstruction and Tracking with Infrared Coding Multiplexing for Image-Guided Surgery
authors: Hongxun Liu, Zhao Zheng, Zhiqi Huang, Long Shao, Jingfan Fan, Jian Yang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Rapid and high-precision preoperative patient-to-image registration is a crucial challenge in surgical navigation. Existing navigation systems acquire point clouds using marker-based probes or scanners with attached optical markers. However, these methods inevitably cause skin deformation and human factor interference, reducing the efficiency and stability of the registration. Moreover, the heavy reliance on preoperative Computed Tomography (CT) images or scanners for surface reconstruction significantly increases medical costs. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel collaborative framework for surface reconstruction and tracking that integrates the 2D Gaussian Splatting (2DGS) technology. 2DGS enables high-quality and information-rich surface reconstruction using only a visible light camera, thereby decreasing the reliance on CT or scanners. We propose infrared coding multiplexing technology, which integrates an infrared digital projector with optical tracking system to achieve tracking of the patient and surgical instruments without multisensor calibration. Additionally, we recover the scale of the 2DGS reconstruction model using a Scale-Adaptive Iterative Closest Point registration algorithm. We validated the effectiveness of the system through a phantom head model experiment. While providing richer texture information, the surface target localization error of the 2DGS model was 1.14 mm, which is comparable to the scanner model. This demonstrates the potential value of our method for partial open surgeries and skin surgeries.

title: AirComp Enabled Adaptive Power Control and Loop Scheduling for Industrial IoT Systems
authors: Ling Lyu, Ziyi Zhang, Yanpeng Dai, Jindou Xie, Yuhuan Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The widespread application of wireless communication technologies in the industrial automation has facilitated the emergence of the industrial Internet of Things (IoT) systems, where the reliability of state information transmission plays a critical role in making control decision and executing control actions. However, the limitation of spectrum resources and the uncertainty of wireless environment make it challenging to guarantee transmission reliability and close all control loops. Considering the advantage of Over-the-Air Computing (AirComp) technology on the high spectrum utilization, this paper proposes an AirComp enabled adaptive power control and loop scheduling scheme for industrial IoT systems with limited spectrum resources. Specifically, a novel performance indicator, the total reduction of Lyapunov function (TRLF), is introduced to evaluate the performance gain of scheduling control loops. Then, an AirComp communication architecture is built, which could inherently reduce the transmission latency by simultaneously delivering the state information of multiple sensors with the same spectrum resource. Moreover, an AirComp enabled adaptive power control scheme is proposed to effectively cope with the heterogeneities of sensing dimension and observation distribution to maximize TRLF with limited spectrum resources. Finally, an alternate convex search algorithm based on the closed-form solution is proposed to optimize the transmission and reception power of scheduled control loops. Simulation results show that the adaptive proposed power control and loop scheduling algorithms significantly improve the TRLF.

title: Predicting Electric Vehicle Adoption: A Machine Learning Approach Using Socioeconomic and Infrastructure Data
authors: Oneal Waseem, Sir Tyrone Raphael Joseph Vargas, Xian Onnodhia Nanhai Habibie, Nunung Nurul Qomariyah, Raymond Bahana
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The adoption of EVs are dependent on socioeconomic factors and existing infrastructure that is available to support it. This research employs regression machine learning techniques, to analyze the relationship between EV population within a specified county and key socioeconomic factors such as, GDP, population, population density, and EV chargers. This research utilizes datasets from multiple states in the U.S., including Washington, California, Texas, Florida, and New York. It implements the XGBoost, Random Forest, Linear Regression, and Support Vector Regression models to predict EV populations. Our findings indicate that existing EV infrastructure such as chargers heavily influence EV population rates, with XGBoost demonstrating the highest predictive accuracy with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> score of 0.841 and an accuracy score of 89.58 % (1000 car tolerance). The results underscore the importance of infrastructure development to enhance EV adoption.

title: Optimization Method of Control Set and Its Derivative Models Based on Genetic Algorithm
authors: Dingtian Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Warehouse location is a core issue in logistics network design, crucial for improving material dispatching efficiency and reducing costs. This paper proposes a warehouse location optimization method based on weighted control set theory. Four models, namely the weighted control set, weighted weak Roman control set, weighted Roman control set, and weighted double Roman control set, are constructed to represent multi-level emergency material requirements. A hybrid optimization approach combining greedy and genetic algorithms is applied to enhance solution quality and adaptability. Experimental results on a 600 -node dataset show that the genetic algorithm improves overall fitness by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$36.2 \%-41.7 \%$</tex> compared with the greedy approach, with the weighted double Roman control set performing best under high coverage demands. The proposed method provides an effective framework for scientific and efficient emergency warehouse location planning.

title: Revealing the True Indicators: Understanding and Improving IoC Extraction From Threat Reports
authors: Evangelos Froudakis, Athanasios Avgetidis, Sean Tyler Frankum, Roberto Perdisci, Manos Antonakakis, Angelos D. Keromytis
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Indicators of Compromise (IoCs) are critical for threat detection and response, marking malicious activity across networks and systems. Yet, the effectiveness of automated IoC extraction systems is fundamentally limited by one key issue: the lack of high-quality ground truth. Current extraction tools rely either on manually extracted ground truth, which is labor-intensive and costly, or on automated ground truth creation methods that include non-malicious artifacts, leading to inflated false positive (FP) rates and unreliable threat intelligence. In this work, we analyze the shortcomings of existing ground truth creation strategies and address them by introducing the first hybrid human-in-the-Ioop pipeline for IoC extraction, which combines a large language model-based classifier (LANCE) with expert analyst validation. Our system improves precision through explainable, context-aware labeling and reduces analysts' work factor by 43% compared to manual annotation, as demonstrated in our evaluation with six analysts. Using this approach, we produce PRISM, a high-quality, publicly available benchmark of 1,791 labeled IoCs from 50 real-world threat reports. PRISM supports both fair evaluation and training of IoC extraction methods and enables reproducible research grounded in expert-validated indicators.

title: When Digital Payment Accessibility Drives Impulsivity: An Empirical Study on the Impacts of QRIS
authors: Mikail Ali Gibran, Mahir Pradana, Yogi Suprayogi
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: The aim of this study is to examine the effects of QR payments on impulsive buying behavior among consumers. With the increasing popularity of mobile payments, there is a growing interest in understanding how these payment methods affect consumer behavior, particularly in the context of impulse buying. This study employed a mixed-methods approach, including a survey and consumer interviews. The results show that QR payments significantly affect impulsive buying behavior, with consumers who use QR payments more likely to engage in impulsive behavior on financial spending due to the accessibility aspect of QR payment as significant factor with T-statistics 2.498, meaning that the Accessibility factors of QR payment variable has a significant influence on impulse purchase.

title: A Divide-Merge Clustering Algorithm Based on Dimension Importance and Inter-Subcluster Tightness
authors: Jiaman Ding, Qingmeng Hu, Siqi Wang, Shaojie Qiao, Lianyin Jia, Hongbin Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Traditional clustering algorithms often assume equal importance for all dimensions and rely on all of them to divide the data, leading to the problem of fuzzy boundaries that affect the clustering performance. Additionally, selecting appropriate similarity measures for data with varied distributions presents a challenge for clustering algorithms. To address these issues, we propose a divide-merge clustering algorithm based on dimension importance and inter-subcluster tightness (DMC-DIIT). This algorithm combines an iterative partitioning strategy based on dimension importance and a new inter-cluster logical distance calculation, effectively overcoming the issues of fuzzy boundaries and merging errors in traditional clustering methods, especially in high-dimensional and redundant feature spaces. Additionally, by using self-representation feature selection, it effectively removes redundant and irrelevant features, significantly improving clustering accuracy and efficiency. The algorithm demonstrates exceptional performance and robustness, particularly on datasets with complex shapes and uneven densities. To evaluate the performance of DMC-DIIT, we conduct comparative experiments on 20 benchmark datasets. The experimental results clearly demonstrate that DMC-DIIT consistently outperforms the comparison algorithms across all evaluation metrics, with a notable enhancement in runtime efficiency.

title: Simulation and Testing of Motion Control Systems for Moving Platforms with Payload
authors: Olha Sushchenko
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: The paper deals with the features and testing of systems for controlling the motion of platforms with a payload assigned for operation on land-moving objects. Features of designing motion control systems are represented. Structural diagrams of the motion control loops are shown. The set of tests necessary for designing and manufacturing of studied motion control system is represented. Four phases of tests are characterized. The possibilities of tests by the dynamic stand and its structural diagram are described. Features of full-scale tests are described. The list of basic checked parameters is given. The techniques for determining dynamic error and angular rigidity are represented. The influence of the imbalance moment is characterized. The approaches to computer checks of motion control systems with the representation of transient processes are presented. The obtained results can be useful for moving platforms with the payload assigned for operation on mobile objects of a wide class.

title: Prediction of Suitable Growth Areas for Camellia Sinensis Based on Maxent Model
authors: Mengjin Wu, Jia Fan, Yuntao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Southwest China is a crucial production area for Chinese Camellia sinensis, and fluctuations in its tea output are closely related to farmers' income and industrial development. To safeguard the premium quality and high productivity of tea cultivation in Southwest China, promote rural revitalization, and further increase farmers' income, this study predicted the suitable growth regions of Camellia sinensis in Southwest China under modern and contemporary climatic conditions using the MaxEnt model (Maximum Entropy Modeling), based on 704 Camellia sinensis distribution points and 22 environmental variables. The results showed that when FC (Feature Combinations) = LQHPT and RM (Regularization Multiplier) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.50$</tex>, the AUC value reached 0.83, revealing high accuracy in predicting suitable areas for Camellia sinensis. The core factors regulating the distribution of Camellia sinensis suitable regions were the minimum temperature of the coldest month, precipitation of the wettest month, temperature annual range, and precipitation of the driest month. The moderately-highly suitable regions for Camellia sinensis in Southwest China were mainly concentrated in the south-central area of Sichuan Province, the western area of Chongqing, the southern area of Guizhou Province, and the southwestern area of Yunnan Province. This study is expected to provide fundamental information and practical references, thereby supporting the rational planning of the spatial layout for tea production and the sustained advancement of the tea industry in Southwest China.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Education: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Yunita Kartika Sari, Muhtosim Arief, Sri Bramantoro Abdinagoro, Evelyn Hendriana
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The bibliometric analysis artificial intelligence in education research use Scopus data from 2020 to 2025 encompassing 3,872 articles from 707 journals reveals a dynamic and rapidly growing field. A primary finding is the consistent and accelerating increase in annual scientific production, marked by a particularly sharp rise in publication in 2024. It indicates that AI and education is a trending topic that continuously attracts researchers. Geographically, the UK leads in both number of published articles and total citation, indicating significant research activity and influence

title: Coherent Probabilistic Forecasts for Business Decision-Making: A Novel Framework
authors: Pramath Parashar
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel framework designed to generate coherent probabilistic forecasts for such constrained business scenarios. We extend established reconciliation techniques to integrate full probabilistic distributions, ensuring consistency and alignment across all predicted series. Through practical applications in key sectors like domestic tourism and energy demand, this framework demonstrates its significant utility in providing businesses with more reliable, comprehensive, and actionable future outlooks, thereby optimizing planning and risk management.

title: CodeBuddy – An AI-Powered Code Learning Buddy
authors: Jayashree Pasalkar, Tushar Gajbhiye, Omkar Ovhal, Sahil Hanwate
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Data structures, algorithms, and problem-solving have become crucial skills that a computer science student must master. However, debugging and logical reasoning remain the hardest issues for many learners. Previous online platforms suffer from extensive solutions or generic advice, which cannot offer much opportunity for independent learning. This paper introduces CodeBuddy, an AI-assisted coding support system designed to give structured hints, debugging assistance, and conceptual explanations without providing final answers to the learners. The system is built upon the Gemini API for intelligent guidance, a Node.js-Express.js backend for system management, a React.js frontend integrated with Monaco Editor for coding interaction, and MongoDB for progress tracking. This contains gamification features such as leaderboards and activity streaks, too, aimed at improving learner engagement. The system fine-tunes the generation of the hint with regard to the performance of the user. Experimental evaluation has proved that AI-based mentoring improves problem-solving efficiency, reduces over-reliance on predefined solutions, and prepares students better for coding interviews and competitive programming.

title: Research on Moisture Barrier Technology and Reliability Evaluation of Flip-Chip Circuit with Organic Substrate
authors: Jingwen Zang, Linfeng Chen, Shimeng Xu, Xiaochen Xie, Pengrong Lin, Lois Liao
abstract: Flip-chip circuits with organic substrate are widely used in modern microelectronic packaging due to their high-density interconnections and low-cost advantages. However, the hygroscopic properties of the polymer materials used in package process lead to interfacial delamination and electrochemical corrosion in humid environments, severely limiting their reliability under high-temperature and highhumidity conditions. To enhance the reliability of flip-chip circuits with organic substrate in humid environment, this study employed two methods-nano-coated and Parylene-coated-to shield the circuits. Moisture barrier effectiveness was comprehensively assessed through moisture absorption weight gain tests, heavy water tracing combined with Time-of-flight Secondary Ion Mass Spectrometry (ToF-SIMS) analysis, and Moisture Sensitivity Level 1 (MSL 1) preconditioning (reflow soldering with 260° C peak temperature). Both protective layers significantly inhibit moisture penetration, with saturated moisture absorption rates of 0.207% for the nano-coated sample, 0.224% for the Parylene-coated sample and 0.256% for the unprotected sample. The protective layers did not alter the diffusion pathway but effectively prolonged the permeation process. Heavy water tracing revealed a one-order-ofmagnitude reduction in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{D}^{-}$</tex> intensity at the interfaces of both protected samples, with the nano-coated showing slight superiority over Parylene due to its molecular-level hydrophobic structure. After the MSL 1 preconditioning, both barrier layer prevented the formation of microcracks at the interface between the chip edge and the underfill. The underfill itself did not crack, and the adhesion between the underfill and both the solder mask and the polyimide (PI) remained intact. Barrier layers enhance moisture resistance through distinct mechanisms: nanoparticle coatings rely on nanoporous barrier effects and surface hydrophobicity, while Parylene leverages its conformal and pin-hole free coating. These findings provide reference and experimental data support for the characterization and optimization of moisture barrier technology for flip-chip circuits with organic substrate.

title: Adaptive AI Agent Placement and Migration in Edge Intelligence Systems
authors: Xingdan Wang, Jiayi He, Zhiqing Tang, Jianxiong Guo, Jiong Lou, Liping Qian, Tian Wang, Weijia Jia
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The rise of LLMs such as ChatGPT and Claude fuels the need for AI agents capable of real-time task handling. However, migrating data-intensive, multi-modal edge workloads to cloud data centers, traditionally used for agent deployment, introduces significant latency. Deploying AI agents at the edge improves efficiency and reduces latency. However, edge environments present challenges due to limited and heterogeneous resources. Maintaining QoS for mobile users necessitates agent migration, which is complicated by the complexity of AI agents coordinating LLMs, task planning, memory, and external tools. This paper presents the first systematic deployment and management solution for LLM-based AI agents in dynamic edge environments. We propose a novel adaptive framework for AI agent placement and migration in edge intelligence systems. Our approach models resource constraints and latency/cost, leveraging ant colony algorithms and LLM-based optimization for efficient decision-making. It autonomously places agents to optimize resource utilization and QoS and enables lightweight agent migration by transferring only essential state. Implemented on a distributed system using AgentScope and validated across globally distributed edge servers, our solution significantly reduces deployment latency and migration costs.

title: Voltage Risk Assessment of Wind Power Grid Connection Systems Based on CVaR
authors: Zhengyu Wang, Yingtong Liu, Fang Chen, Yanyi Fu, Ming Yang, Zhuhua Chen, Jie Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: To effectively account for the impact of wind power output randomness on grid operation status, this paper proposes a method for assessing the risk of node voltage in wind power grid-connected systems based on the Condition Value at Risk (CVaR) theory. First, an improved kernel density estimation (IKDE) is employed to establish a wind speed probability model that accounts for the randomness and boundedness of wind speed, enabling the precise calculation of cumulant containing wind speed randomness information. This enhances the accuracy of probability load flow (PLF) calculation using the improved cumulant method (ICM) and yields the probability distribution of voltage fluctuations at each node. Based on this, a quantitative analysis of voltage risks is conducted for each node's voltage fluctuations using CVaR theory. Finally, the proposed method is validated through testing on the IEEE 30-node system.

title: AI and Investor Stress: Identifying Key Factors through Interpretive Structural Modeling
authors: Jayashree Bhattacharjee, Ranjit Singh, Sakshi Pandey, Lokendra Puri
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into finance has reshaped investment decision-making by enhancing efficiency, client engagement, and innovation. However, rapid adoption also introduces psychological challenges, particularly investor stress. This study investigates key stress-inducing factors using interpretive structural modeling (ISM), which identifies and hierarchically structures variables based on expert input and iterative comparisons. The analysis reveals twelve significant factors, among which decision fatigue, transparency issues, perceived loss of control, and excessive reliance on technology exhibit the highest driving power, indicating their foundational role in inducing investment stress. Demographic factors such as age and gender are found to be dependent variables that influence the extent to which these stressors affect individuals. These findings suggest that addressing the root drivers through targeted strategies, such as improving algorithmic explainability and offering tailored investor education, can substantially reduce stress and enhance trust.

title: A Deep Neural Network Model for Unit Commitment
authors: Zhaomin Lyu
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: As the unit commitment (UC) problem incorporates growing uncertainties, its NP-hard nature places a prohibitive computational burden on classical mixed-integer programming (MIP) solvers, often preventing them from finding a solution within a practical limited time frame. This work investigates the application of deep neural networks (DNNs) in reducing the time for solving UC problems. Instead of playing a model-reduction role to assist the existing optimization solvers, like many machine learning (ML) model do in related work, the DNN model in this work is trained to independently predict the solution of UC problems, thereby its time-saving potential is fully utilized. Moreover, a novel engineering approach to address the feasibility issue is proposed: With an emphasized focus on the power balance constraint during the training process, the violation rates of the other constraints would be reduced to a negligible level. The case studies demonstrate the fair accuracy and minuscule feasibility loss of the proposed DNN model. In more stringent application cases where higher accuracy and zero infeasibility are required, the DNN model of this study could still serve to find the initial solutions.

title: Comparative Analysis of Application and API Based AI for Infographic Development
authors: Zaky Alif Muhammad
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The use of AI or Artificial Intelligence has begun to increase rapidly since the release of ChatGPT at the end of 2022. Since then, many companies have implemented AI or even created their own AI such as Meta, Samsung, Apple, Google etc. Technological advances have brought drastic changes to companies including the media. This research aims to analyzing by comparing 10 AI's, 5 App / Web based, 5 API Based from HuggingFace. This research uses a qualitative descriptive method by collecting data through literature review, direct experiment.

title: A Literature Review: Implementation of Facial Expression Recognition for Classifying Mental Health Condition
authors: Aina Nur Fadlilah, Suhono Harso Supangkat, Devi Willieam Anggara
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: As mental health issues continue to rise and significantly affect human quality of life, sometimes even leading to suicide, preventive and mitigative actions are urgently needed. Affective computing, supported by artificial intelligence (AI), offers promising tools such as Facial Expression Recognition (FER) for assessing mental health conditions. FER can detect and classify facial expressions associated with mental health symptoms, including depression, autism, and anxiety. The implementation of FER in healthcare settings depends on the readiness and capacity of the health service providers. Key aspects to consider include system performance and validity, the availability of infrastructure and devices, adherence to ethical protocols, and the necessity of clinical trials before broader adoption. This study aims to provide a comprehensive overview of the requirements for implementing FER, explore its limitations and challenges, compare various techniques, and discuss the potential of multimodal approaches for enhanced mental health assessment.

title: Development of a Wideband Energy Harvesting Circuit Utilizing Terrestrial Digital Broadcast Signals
authors: Hayato Tanaka, Nobuya Tagawa, Kaito Nakabuchi, Haruichi Kanaya
abstract: Recent IoT growth demands new energy sources. One of the technologies attracting attention is energy harvesting (EH), which converts ambient microenergy into electricity. High-frequency energy emitted by wireless devices is ubiquitous. Obtaining power from these weak radio frequency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(R F)$</tex> waves requires an efficient RF-DC rectifier circuit. In this study, we focused on the frequency band used for terrestrial broadcasting radio waves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{4 7 0}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 1 0 ~ M H z})$</tex>. And we have developed a broadband RF-DC rectifier that can cover this frequency range. Additionally, using the developed circuit, we conducted an experiment to drive the battery-less Bluetooth sensor system using the UHF radio waves.

title: Predictive Acoustic Analysis of the Graha Medika Ballroom: A Comparative Study of Ecotect Simulation and the Sabine Method
authors: Andi Baso Mappaturi, Thomas Ari Kristianto, Fitria Ningsih, Andi Pramono
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Multipurpose spaces, such as the Graha Medika Ballroom at Brawijaya University, face unique acoustic challenges as they must accommodate conflicting reverberation time (RT60) requirements for both speech and music. A research gap exists in the lack of predictive acoustic analysis for this specific ballroom at the design stage. This study, therefore, aims to analyze and predict the space's acoustic performance, focusing on two key parameters: reverberation time and the risk of acoustic defects. The primary focus is on evaluating the RT60 and potential acoustic defects based on the interior design. The methodology used is a quantitative approach comparing digital simulation via Autodesk Ecotect with manual calculation using the classical Sabine method for verification. The simulation yielded an RT60 of 1.52 seconds, while manual calculation gave 1.74 seconds at 500 Hz. This slight difference confirms the validity of both methods, as both results fall within the ideal range. Geometric analysis also revealed no significant acoustic defects, indicating even sound distribution. In conclusion, this ballroom has good acoustic quality for its various functions, especially vocal activities and light music. For further optimization and to align with modern technological trends, a hybrid model combining traditional simulations with machine learning is recommended for more efficient acoustic prediction and dynamic design improvement. We also recommend adding carpet in the stage area and optimizing acoustics in the entrance and behind the ceiling.

title: Predicting Diabetes Using Machine Learning: Model Ensemble Implementation
authors: Amit Gupta, Paras Rawal, Richa Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus is one of the global health issues of nowadays it's a chronic metabolic disorder which interferes with the glucose absorption by the cells of body. Early diagnosis and treatment may play a crucial role in better management and prevention of life- threatening complications. In this research paper, three machine learning methods, Random Forest, KNearest Neighbors (KNN), Logistic Regression are used, then these models are combined and an ensembled model was used for diagnosing diabetes with the available clinical and demographic parameters. This model has been tested on a dataset which comprises publicly available diabetes containing attributes such as age, BMI, blood glucose levels, and HbA1c levels. Results also are shown where our ensembled model surpasses all the models with the maximum accuracy of [96.63%]and maximum AUC(are under the curve)[0.96]. This research paper demonstrates the potential that machine learning can exhibit by improving the diabetes diagnosis, hence assisting the healthcare staff to intervene before conditions become impossible to treat or even prevent it.

title: Mamba-iT-ECA: A Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Photovoltaic Power Forecasting via Dual-Stage ECA Attention and State Space Modeling
authors: Nangui Fan, Zhenbin Chen, Peixin Li, Ruorui Lin, Kanghui Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Accurate photovoltaic (PV) power prediction is of great significance for the integration of renewable energy and real-time dispatch of smart grids. To address the high computational complexity and insufficient feature fusion in existing PV forecasting models when handling long-sequence multivariate time series, this paper proposes a Mamba-iT-ECA hybrid prediction model. The model utilizes iTransformer for multivariate correlation feature extraction and incorporates the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O(n)$</tex> linear complexity Mamba state space model to efficiently process meteorological sequences, significantly enhancing the model's adaptability to short-term climate changes. Concurrently, The dual-layer ECA mechanism enables multi-level feature enhancement and demonstrates strong seasonal adaptability, offering critical performance gains in high-volatility seasons. Evaluated on an Australian PV dataset, the proposed model achieves the lowest MAE and RMSE, delivering an average performance improvement of 47–63% over baseline models.

title: Machine Learning-Based Anomaly Detection and Classification in 36,000 BTU Air Conditioners
authors: Manaschanok Wangphithakwong, Irin Wanthong, Nattachai Pothi, Jiraphon Srisertpol, Sorada Khaengkarn
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: This research proposes a novel approach for diagnosing and analyzing faults in air conditioning (AC) systems using artificial intelligence (AI) to classify failure types. The study focuses on developing a fault diagnosis system for a 36,000 BTU air conditioner to reduce human decision-making errors and improve diagnostic efficiency. Machine learning techniques are used to classify system anomaly faults into 5 categories: (1) Normal Operation, (2) Dirty Indoor Unit, (3) Low Refrigerant Level, (4) Insufficient Outdoor Heat Dissipation, and (5) Others. Operational data were recorded using a Transmission Monitor (TM2016), comprising 54 features including temperature, current, and control signals under both normal and abnormal conditions. Due to the small and imbalanced dataset, the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was applied during preprocessing. The dataset was then split into training and test sets. A 5-fold cross-validation was used for model evaluation. Among the evaluated models, the Random Forest demonstrated the highest prediction performance with an accuracy of 88.75% on the training data and 44.44% on unseen data. The Random Forest successfully diagnosed a “low refrigerant” fault, which was later confirmed and repaired by experts. After repair, the model accurately identified normal operation, aligning with expert assessments. The proposed machine learning approach significantly enhances the efficiency of AC fault diagnosis. While expert technicians typically spend hours manually analyzing data, the developed model delivers accurate diagnoses within minutes, enabling rapid evaluation of numerous AC units. This not only reduces the workload for technical personnel but also improves diagnostic accuracy and allows technicians to focus on novel or uncertain cases.

title: Evaluation of GPU Video Encoder for Low-Latency Real-Time 4K UHD Encoding
authors: Kasidis Arunruangsirilert, Jiro Katto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: The demand for high-quality, real-time video streaming has grown exponentially, with 4K Ultra High Definition (UHD) becoming the new standard for many applications such as live broadcasting, TV services, and interactive cloud gaming. This trend has driven the integration of dedicated hardware encoders into modern Graphics Processing Units (GPUs). Nowadays, these encoders support advanced codecs like HEVC and AV1 and feature specialized Low-Latency and Ultra Low-Latency tuning, targeting end-to-end latencies of <2 seconds and <500 ms, respectively. As the demand for such capabilities grows toward the 6G era, a clear understanding of their performance implications is essential. In this work, we evaluate the low-latency encoding modes on GPUs from NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD from both Rate-Distortion (RD) performance and latency perspectives. The results are then compared against both the normal-latency tuning of hardware encoders and leading software encoders. Results show hardware encoders achieve significantly lower E2E latency than software solutions with slightly better RD performance. While standard Low-Latency tuning yields a poor quality-latency trade-off, the Ultra Low-Latency mode reduces E2E latency to 83 ms (5 frames) without additional RD impact. Furthermore, hardware encoder latency is largely insensitive to quality presets, enabling high-quality, low-latency streams without compromise.

title: Exploring the Effect of Data Augmentation on Neural Machine Translation Between Indonesian and Madurese Languages Using the LSTM Model
authors: Fairuz Iqbal Maulana, Yaya Heryadi, Gede Putra Kusuma, Widodo Budiharto
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Neural Machine Translation (NMT) has shown significant potential in producing high-quality translations, particularly for well-resourced language pairs. The efficacy of NMT is significantly dependent on extensive parallel corpora, presenting a difficulty for languages with scarce linguistic resources. This study focuses on developing an NMT system to translate Indonesian to Madurese, leveraging the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) architecture. LSTM is particularly well-suited for sequence-to-sequence learning tasks, as it effectively captures long-term dependencies in text data. To mitigate the challenges posed by limited datasets, this research employs data augmentation techniques to expand the training corpus. These methods enhance the diversity and volume of input data, allowing the model to learn linguistic patterns more effectively. The experimental results demonstrate that the LSTM-based NMT model achieves an average BLEU score of 53%, reflecting a significant step toward bridging the gap in machine translation for low-resource languages. This work highlights the potential of combining advanced machine learning techniques with innovative data strategies to support the preservation and accessibility of underrepresented languages, such as Madurese.

title: Transitional Scenario Channel Characteristics Analysis for 6G Wireless Communications
authors: Shenghan Luo, Jiale Huang, Ziyao Xie, Zhejing Zhao, Jun Wang, Zhen Lv, Jie Huang, Cheng-Xiang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Global coverage is one of the fundamental visions for the sixth generation (6G) networks, requiring accurate channel models to support diverse scenarios. Existing channel models are designed for clearly defined scenarios. However, in the real word, the defined scenarios cannot be perfectly connected. Developing appropriate channel models for transitional scenarios is necessary. This paper proposes a simulation framework to characterize transitional scenario channels and analyze their statistical properties. First, we extend conventional scenarios by incorporating detailed quantitative environmental parameters, enabling efficient configuration of the 6G pervasive channel model (6GPCM) for individual scenarios. Then, we employ Sobol sensitivity analysis to quantify the influence of environmental parameters on channel characteristics, such as root mean square (RMS) delay spread and Doppler spread. Building on these insights, we develop a sensitivity-rank-oriented strategy to simulate two representative transitional scenarios. This strategy has successfully captured abnormal change in RMS delay spread and Doppler spread in these two scenarios. These findings highlight the framework's capability to detect and analyze transitional scenario dynamics, offering critical insights for optimizing 6G network performance in heterogeneous environments.

title: Spatio-Temporal Hotspot Detection and Flyway Network Analysis of HPAI Outbreaks in Wild Birds
authors: V.S.V.S. Murthy, J. N.V.R. Swarup Kumar, Srinivas Gorla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza poses OneHealth challenge because of recurrent transboundary spread caused by migratory wild birds. Outbreak models that are presently existing emphasize temporal prediction. But they overlook ecological reasoning and spatial validation. This paper introduces a unified Spatio-Temporal Hotspot Detection and Drift Analysis Framework that combines temporal clustering, spatial autocorrelation, and network inference to characterize dynamic Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza transmission patterns. The HPAI Wildbirds surveillance dataset is utilized that consists of 16304 records and has data within the timeline of 1980 to 2020. Using this dataset, Moran's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I$</tex> and Getis-Ord <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$G i^{\ast}$</tex> statistics confirmed significant non-random clustering, with with 14.7% of grid cells identified as persistent hotspots. Monthly hotspot drift revealed north-eastern propagation as being consistent with migratory flyways. This is quantified by the ST-DBSCAN module. The inferred flyway network highlighted coastal and estuarine junctions as critical connectors. Spatial overlap validation is done using the Jaccard index (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$J=0.13$</tex>). This supported the robustness of identified clusters. Agent-based and explainableAI layers remain conceptual. Qualitative interpretation indicates that major hotspot regions coincide with known waterfowl habitats. Surveillance data is transformed into transparent, reproducible, and ecologically interpretable intelligence by the framework. Thus, it offers a scalable foundation for data-driven HPAI monitoring and One-Health preparedness.

title: Real-Time Multi-Parameter Railway Monitoring with Axle Counting & Speed Estimation Using Fiber Bragg Grating Sensors
authors: Dennis Yap Jian Yuan, Chang Yoong Choon, Pua Chang Hong, Shee Yu Gang
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: For safe railway operations, a dependable train monitoring system is essential. Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) currently relies on balises and conventional axle counters, both of which are vulnerable to electromagnetic interference (EMI). This study proposes Fiber Bragg Grating (FBG) sensors as an EMI-immune alternative for real-time train detection. A total of 50 FBG sensors, arranged in 25 pairs along a 500-m rail segment, enabled axle counting, speed estimation, and precise train position tracking. Axle counting was achieved using a rolling buffer with low-pass filtering and derivative analysis, while train speed was derived from sensor spacing and axle timestamps. Field trials achieved 98.91% axle-counting accuracy across 457 datasets and demonstrated stable speed tracking with only 0.1084 km/h deviation. Although freight trains required adaptive thresholding due to variable loading, passenger trains were consistently and accurately monitored. These results confirm that FBG sensing offers a scalable, cost-effective, and EMI-immune solution for modern railway monitoring and signaling.

title: Chain of Command: Context-Aware Android Malware Detection via Permission-to-API Call Chains and Graph Representation Learning
authors: Zhichao Shi, Qiang Han, Zihao Zhang
abstract: Android malware detection has become increasingly critical as mobile applications continue to proliferate. Traditional detection methods primarily rely on static analysis of permissions and API calls, but often fail to capture the complex behavioral patterns and contextual relationships inherent in malicious applications. To address this limitation, we introduce Chain of Command, a novel framework centered on the Permission-to-API Call Chain (PACC). A PACC represents a complete execution path from an application entry point to a sensitive API invocation, effectively preserving the contextual link between a required permission and its use. Our methodology comprises three stages: first, extracting PACCs through a backward analysis of the application’s call graph; second, performing semantic abstraction to map concrete method signatures to generalized behavioral categories; and third, constructing a compact behavior graph from these abstracted chains, which is then classified using a Graph Attention Network (GAT). Evaluation on a large-scale Android dataset demonstrates that our framework significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both accuracy and robustness. Our findings underscore the efficacy of integrating contextual feature engineering, semantic abstraction, and graph representation learning for developing more reliable and interpretable malware detection systems.

title: Design and Simulation of an Ammonia Fuel Supply and Auxiliary System Based on MWORKS
authors: Xiaoliang Dou, Yintao Li, Meng Cui, Xin Chen, Chuanrui Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: This article is based on Modelica and MWORKS simulation platform, and constructs a model of marine ammonia fuel supply and auxiliary system, including key components such as booster pump, fuel tank, heat exchanger, control valve, etc., and integrates system level simulation. Through simulation analysis of typical operating conditions, the dynamic response and control capability of the system's supply pressure and temperature under variable load conditions have been verified. The results show that the system can stably maintain the design requirements of supply pressure of 85 ± 2 bar and temperature of 50 ± 5 ° C in all gears, with good engineering feasibility and control accuracy, which can provide theoretical basis and simulation support for the design and optimization of actual ship ammonia fuel system.

title: Diffusion-Based Image Semantic Encoding and Decoding at Ultra-low Bitrate
authors: Zhewen Sun, Xiaoyan Xie, Yukun Zhang, Tao Fu, Jiankai Wang, Junjie Zhao, Qinghang Wu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Ultra-low-bitrate image compression that preserves both semantic consistency and perceptual quality is a challenging task. In this study, a diffusion-based semantic encoding and decoding framework was proposed. The method first encodes a downsampled image using a fine-tuned low-resolution codec, then reconstructs high-quality details by adapting to transmission-induced distortion, leveraging intrinsic spatial structure for semantic guidance, and enforcing semantic consistency through adversarial training. Experimental results showed that the proposed approach outperforms benchmark methods in both perceptual quality and semantic fidelity at lower bitrates, demonstrating strong potential for next-generation image storage and transmission systems.

title: Survey on Ensemble Deep Transfer Learning Models for Robust Multi-Class Classification of Oral Cancer Stages
authors: Dasyam Chandra Mouli, Pullela SVVSR Kumar, Dasari Haritha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Oral cancer poses a greater threat when not detected in its early stages, as early diagnosis enables more effective treatment planning and improved patient outcomes. Traditional diagnostic methods often lack accuracy and reliability. Image processing (IP) techniques play a crucial role in enhancing cancer detection by reducing noise, filtering input images, and refining them for precise analysis. Deep learning (DL) algorithms such as Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), VGGNet (VGG16, VGG19), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) have been effectively utilized for multi-classification in oral cancer detection, identifying different cancer stages with high precision. This study further employs advanced pretrained models—ResNet50, InceptionV3, and DenseNet121—integrated through a weighted fusion technique to enhance classification accuracy. Transfer learning is leveraged to adapt these models to new datasets with minimal training effort, utilizing both feature extraction and fine-tuning of lower network layers. Experimental evaluations on multiple real-time datasets demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed deep learning models in the detection and classification of oral cancer.

title: RAG4Test: Retrieving GUI States for Multilingual Bug Report and Test Case Generation via LLMs
authors: Zhiyao Wang, Xiujing Guo, Tatsuhiro Tsuchiya
abstract: The scalability and efficiency of software testing are persistently hindered by a reliance on manual practices for creating test cases and bug reports. Moreover, the valuable insights from post-release user feedback are often lost due to the lack of an automated pipeline connecting them to regression testing. To overcome these challenges, we present a novel framework that synergizes the structural representation of software with the generative power of Large Language Models (LLMs). We first represent the application’s Graphical User Interface (GUI) as a directed graph, capturing its components and navigational logic. This queryable graph provides essential, structured context for a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) model, which then autonomously generates and populates high-quality test cases and bug reports. A key innovation of our work is a fully automated pipeline that processes unstructured user feedback and error reports, transforming them into standardized test cases. We selected some reviews from a popular mobile App for preliminary experiments and verified the feasibility and efficiency improvement of this method.

title: AI-Driven Solutions for Power Systems: A Systematic Review on Efficiency Optimization, Fault Detection, and Renewable Energy Integration
authors: Salsabila Amalia Putri, Nadya Misakiputri, Dinda Aulia Waranggani, Ali Gunawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This review examines how AI contributes to improving efficiency, reliability, and sustainability in the energy sector. Utilizing technologies such as Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL), AI enables smarter energy management, predictive maintenance, and more efficient integration of renewables. Additionally, AI optimizes grid operations, enhances fault detection, and mitigates renewable energy fluctuations, thereby strengthening energy resilience. However, challenges such as data inconsistencies, cybersecurity threats, and infrastructure limitations still need to be addressed. This study synthesizes various research findings to identify gaps and opportunities in AI implementation, while emphasizing its role in modernizing power systems and accelerating global decarbonization efforts.

title: Research on Ground Wet and Slippery Sensing System for Outdoor Robots Based on Millimeter-Wave Radar
authors: Yu Qin, Aiqing Wang, Hui Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: To address the demand for wet and slippery detection of the road in outdoor mobile robot navigation, this paper proposed a dry / wet recognition scheme based on millimeter-wave radar. By constructing a millimeter-wave radar platform and employing narrow beam scanning technology, we collected eight types of pavement data, including dry / wet asphalt, concrete, bricks, and muddy ground. We extracted 16-point intensity value features from Doppler heatmap and generated feature vectors through noncoherent accumulation. Comparative performance analysis of four machine learning algorithms (BPNN, LMSVM, RF, CNN) revealed that the random forest (RF) achieved an impressive test accuracy rate of 94.53%. The study validates the capability of millimeter-wave radar's electromagnetic characteristics in distinguishing dry and wet pavements, providing a reliable solution for robots to perceive road conditions around the clock.

title: Characterization of Press-Fit Pin Insertion Behavior in Power Inverter Modules for Vehicles
authors: Sosuke Mori, Hibiki Mori, Junichiro Tsuchiya, Hirotoshi Sasaki, Qiang Yu
abstract: This study focuses on the characterization of press-fit pin insertion behavior in power inverter modules for electric vehicles (EVs), aiming to improve the reliability and efficiency of electrical connections while minimizing environmental impact. Using Ansys Workbench, both explicit dynamic and static structural simulations were performed and validated against experimental results. The research examines how variations in material properties, friction coefficients, and pin geometry affect insertion and extraction forces, as well as plastic strain in the PCB. The finished diameter and copper plating thickness of the through-hole were identified as critical factors. Results showed that decreasing hole diameter increases insertion force, while thicker plating increases it. Plastic strain was concentrated near the contact points corresponding to the pin's maximum diameter. Based on these findings, linear approximation models were developed to predict maximum insertion force and strain behavior with about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 10 \%$</tex> accuracy. This work provides a foundation for practical design guidelines and virtual evaluation methods for press-fit connection technology in EV applications, contributing to more sustainable and cost-effective manufacturing processes.

title: Estimating Bidirectional Causal Effects with Large Scale Online Kernel Learning
authors: Masahiro Tanaka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: In this study, a scalable online kernel learning framework is proposed for estimating bidirectional causal effects in systems characterized by mutual dependence and heteroskedasticity. Traditional causal inference often focuses on unidirectional effects, overlooking the common bidirectional relationships in real-world phenomena. Building on heteroskedasticity-based identification, the proposed method integrates a quasi-maximum likelihood estimator for simultaneous equation models with large scale online kernel learning. It employs random Fourier feature approximations to flexibly model nonlinear conditional means and variances, while an adaptive online gradient descent algorithm ensures computational efficiency for streaming and high-dimensional data. Results from extensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior accuracy and stability than single equation and polynomial approximation baselines, exhibiting lower bias and root mean squared error across various data-generating processes. These results confirm that the proposed approach effectively captures complex bidirectional causal effects with near-linear computational scaling. By combining econometric identification with modern machine learning techniques, the proposed framework offers a practical, scalable, and theoretically grounded solution for large scale causal inference in natural/social science, policy making, business, and industrial applications.

title: TEM/EDX/EELS Analysis the Thermal Growth of Cu Oxide Layer Under Different Surface Medias
authors: L. J. Tang, Inez Kwek Jing Lin
abstract: The formation of copper oxide on the surface of PVD-grown copper films has been studied in relation to its reaction with different epoxies at high temperatures. In the experiment, three types of epoxies were selected to cover the copper surface and were cured at varying high temperatures. EDX and EELS were used to analyze the composition of both the epoxies and the copper oxide layer. The analysis shows that cuprous oxide grows uniformly on the copper surface once the heating temperature reaches 200°C, regardless of the type of epoxy used. The thickness of the copper oxide layer showed slight variations depending on the epoxy applied. The original polycrystalline copper grains expanded under high temperature and stabilized before oxidation occurred.

title: UE-Assisted Multistatic Cooperative Localization in 6G Mobile Network
authors: Wenxiao Guo, Zixiang Han, Jing Jin, Jingbo Tan, Qixing Wang, Guangyi Liu, Jintao Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: A novel multistatic cooperative localization system assisted by user equipment (UE) for sixth-generation (6G) mobile network is proposed. In this system, the base station (BS) act as sensing transmitter and the widely distributed UEs, such as mobile phones, in the cell of BS work as sensing receivers. By transmitting the existing communication reference signals, multiple UEs can be selected to measure the time sum of arrival (TSOA) of sensing objects which are fused to perform multistatic localization. Specifically in this paper, we firstly derive the Cramer-Rao lower bound (CRLB) for the UE-assisted multistatic cooperative localization system based on TSOA measurement. Then we propose an efficient optimization method to rapidly obtain the optimal selection of UE receivers with minimum CRLB to provide the best sensing coverage. Simulation results show that the proposed system achieves localization accuracy of 1 meter for over 90% sensing objects by using three UEs receivers in a sector with 350 meter radius, outperforming the conventional method of selecting UEs with largest received signal power as receivers. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed system in the future 6G mobile network.

title: PosePerfect: Yoga Posture Detection and Real-Time Guidance Using Computer Vision
authors: Jayashree Pasalkar, Dariyan Naagar, Shraddha Hiwrale, Kajal Hake
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The lack of personalized guidance and accessibility of traditional yoga instruction poses challenges for practitioners, including incorrect posture and alignment, risk of injury, and limited feedback outside physical classes. Conventional training methods depend heavily on in-person instructors, which may not be feasible for remote learners and individuals seeking affordable, on-demand guidance. While some AI-based fitness applications have been proposed in recent years, many focus on generic exercises, lack pose-specific accuracy, and require expensive hardware. In this paper, we propose an AI-powered yoga instructor system that leverages deep learning for real- time yoga pose detection, correction, and feedback. The system is trained on curated datasets of yoga postures, utilizes pose estimation models integrated with a MongoDB backend, and applies evaluation metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score to ensure robustness. Practitioners interact with the system through a user-friendly interface that provides live feedback, visual corrections, and performance tracking. To accommodate varying skill levels, the system also supports guided sessions, progress monitoring, and customizable routines. By combining machine learning, computer vision, and accessible web integration, this approach offers a cost-effective, scalable, and intelligent yoga assistant that reduces the risk of injury, enhances learning, and promotes well-being for users across diverse populations.

title: Transformer Fault Detection Based on Feature Fusion of Partial Discharge and Thermal Image
authors: Qiang Chen, Yi Long, Wenqi Fu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Early fault detection in high-voltage power transformers is critical for preventing unexpected failures. This paper proposes a multimodal feature fusion framework that leverages partial discharge (PD) signals and infrared thermal imaging for transformer fault diagnosis.
