We design a dual-branch deep learning model: one branch employs a wavelet transform and LSTM to extract time-frequency features from transient PD signals, while the other uses a CNN and Transformer encoder to capture spatial-temporal patterns from thermal images. The feature embeddings from both modalities are fused via a Tensor Fusion Network (TFN) to capture cross-modal interactions, and a final classifier outputs the fault type. The proposed approach addresses the complementary nature of the modalities - PD signals provide high-frequency insight into electrical insulation defects, whereas thermal images offer low-frequency monitoring of temperature anomalies. Experimental results on a simulated transformer fault dataset show that the multimodal fusion model outperforms single-modality models, achieving improved fault classification accuracy and robustness to noise. The paper also discusses the challenges of multimodal learning for non-stationary PD and low-frame-rate thermal data, and demonstrates that the feature fusion strategy effectively combines disparate features. This work highlights the potential of integrated UHF/ultrasound PD sensing and IR thermography with advanced deep learning for reliable early fault diagnosis in power transformers.

title: The Effect of Artificial Intelligence Application and Economic Growth on Carbon Emissions in Indonesia (2010-2023)
authors: Celine Marsally, Nur Azmi Karim
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Climate change has become one of the most pressing global challenges, drawing significant attention from academics, policymakers, and practitioners. This study investigates the relationship between Artificial Intelligence (AI), Economic Growth (EG), and Carbon Emissions (CE) in Indonesia over the period 2010–2023. A quantitative research method is used, with time series data collected from journal articles and official sources. The data are analyzed using multiple linear regression in EViews software. The results reveal that a 1% increase in AI usage is associated with a 1% rise in carbon emissions, while a 1% increase in economic growth leads to a 3% increase in emissions. When both variables increase simultaneously, carbon emissions rise by approximately 3.2%. The model's R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.83 indicates that AI and economic growth together explain 83% of the variation in carbon emissions, with the remaining 17% attributable to other factors not included in the model. These findings suggest that, despite its potential environmental benefits, AI adoption may currently be contributing to higher emissions, highlighting the need for more sustainable AI deployment and green economic growth strategies.

title: A VAE-LSTM-Transformer Model for Photovoltaic Power Prediction and Uncertainty Quantification
authors: Ruiheng Mai, Nan Ke, Na Shen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: Reliable prediction of photovoltaic (PV) power is essential for the secure operation, cost-effectiveness, and effective management of power systems. However, PV power generation is influenced by solar irradiance and meteorological conditions, exhibiting pronounced intermittency and volatility. Traditional prediction methods often fail to adequately characterize complex meteorological dynamics, nonlinear correlations, and long-term temporal variations, resulting in limited accuracy. To mitigate these challenges, this study proposes a hybrid VAE-LSTMTransformer framework for PV-power prediction and uncertainty quantification. The approach first preprocesses the data and constructs a 96-step sliding window (24 h at 15 min resolution), then encodes the data using a dual-channel VAE to quantify epistemic uncertainty. Short-term dependencies are extracted via a unidirectional LSTM, while long-term dependencies are modeled using a Transformer. Feature fusion and prediction outputs are synthesized by an attention-gating mechanism, and decision-making reliability is enhanced by generating dynamic confidence intervals. Validation using one year of real-world data from a grid-connected power station demonstrates that the proposed model provides actionable insights for PV system planning and grid operation.

title: Depth map coding using depth range decomposition
authors: Evangelos Alexiou, Emmanouil Potetsianakis, Emmanuel Thomas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Depth map sequences are commonly compressed using standard video codecs. However, the bitdepth of acquired depth data often exceeds the maximum bitdepth supported by deployed codecs, resulting in significant quantization loss. To overcome this, depth data are frequently mapped on multiple streams before encoding. In this paper, we propose a depth range decomposition of a high-bitdepth source depth map sequence into two lower-bitdepth sequences: a depth band mask sequence and a residual map sequence. The former indicates the depth band for each pixel, while the latter carries the residual within that band. The presented transformation is lossless and exploits the piecewise smoothness of depth data. Both sequences can be compressed independently with any standard video codec, allowing seamless integration into existing video processing pipelines.

title: Target Interception Using a Multilayer UAV Swarm Formation
authors: D.V. Taranov
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: The work is devoted to the algorithm of control of a swarm of multi-rotor interceptors for air defense in linear sectors with three-dimensional patrol. A multilayer V-shaped formation is proposed, which provides simultaneous overlap of the width and height of the corridor, as well as sensor scheme based on frontal lidar cones with target confirmation by at least two independent drones. The effectiveness of the approach is confirmed by 3D simulations in three typical scenarios: frontal entry, oncoming movement and at with a sinusoidal change in height.

title: Geospatially-Aware Multi-Modal Fusion for Satellite Image Manipulation Detection
authors: Matthew W Chapman, Andrew Tewkesbury, Doreen S Boyd, Boguslaw Obara, Deepayan Bhowmik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: In recent years, the rapid advancement of generative AI tools, such as Stable Diffusion and Generative Adversarial Networks, have enabled the realistic manipulation of images, raising concerns about misinformation, fraud, and the integrity of imagery. While these tools have been predominantly applied to conventional images, there is a substantial risk that geospatial images, in particular satellite images, could be manipulated using these tools. Satellite images are being increasingly utilized in journalism, environmental monitoring, and military and humanitarian planning, due to their ever-increasing spatial resolution and accessibility. Therefore, we can determine that there is a substantial risk that these images may become an attractive target for malicious manipulation. In this work, we introduce the development of a novel dataset consisting of high-resolution context-aware manipulated satellite images, designed to reflect realistic adversarial scenarios. Furthermore, we develop a multimodal, geospatially-aware manipulation detection system, which utilizes contextual information and deep learning to identify manipulated regions within satellite images. Our approach aims to advance the state of the art in satellite image forensics and contribute to a robust framework to mitigate the risk posed by manipulated satellite images. We report a macro-averaged F1 score of 0.82, representing a 43% improvement over the current state-of-the-art benchmark.

title: Dynamic Programming-Based Subcarrier Allocation Scheme for MIMO-OFDM ISAC Systems
authors: Meng Liu, Wanying Su, Rugui Yao, Xi Nan, Hong Xu, Yongjia Zhu, Ye Fan, Xiaoya Zuo
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) is increasingly regarded as an effective solution to address spectrum shortages by enabling spectrum sharing between sensing and communication functions. The rational allocation of limited resources between communication and sensing functions is crucial for improving system performance. To solve this problem, this paper explores adaptive subcarrier allocation in Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM)-based Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) ISAC systems. To evaluate system performance, a performance weighting factor is introduced, using the weighted sum of the communication data rate (CDR) and sensing mutual information (MI) as the optimization objective. Subcarriers are divided into communication-exclusive and sensing-exclusive modes, and their allocation is optimized to maximize overall system performance. A Dynamic Programming (DP) algorithm with backtracking solves the integer programming problem, preserving allocation results. Simulation results show that the proposed subcarrier allocation scheme outperforms scenarios with independent communication and sensing.

title: A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables
authors: Zifan Zhang, Xingye Yu, Ming Gao, SiGan Hu, Hanlin Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: In the context of accelerating urbanisation and the transformation of energy structures, high voltage power cables have emerged as a pivotal transmission method within contemporary power systems. The operational reliability of these entities has been demonstrated to have a direct impact on grid security and power supply quality. Within cable structures, the metallic shielding layer fulfils a dual function: it both suppresses electromagnetic interference and protects communication facilities, while also providing a safe return path for ground faults and preventing electric shock accidents. The present study investigates the mechanisms and characteristics of short-circuit faults in cable shields. Utilising the electromagnetic transient simulation software PSCAD, a refined cable model incorporating frequency-dependent parameters was constructed. The electrical characteristics and influencing factors of such faults were revealed through analysis of the variation patterns of shield currents and zero-sequence currents under different operating conditions. The present research provides both theoretical and empirical support for the rapid diagnosis and condition assessment in engineering applications.

title: A Predictive Framework for Stroke Diagnosis Leveraging Neuroimaging and Deep Learning
authors: Gowri R, Nivetha K, Priyadharshini M, Prajen J
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Stroke is considered to be one of the most common causes of death and chronic disability across the globe, with early diagnosis being very important in enhancing the outcome of a patient. Computed Tomography (CT) scanning is seen as the most common procedure used to evaluate initial stroke because of its accessibility and fastness but interpretation of the CT scans manually is time-consuming and can be easily misinterpreted by a human. To overcome these shortcomings, this paper suggests an automated deep-learning-driven process of precise and efficient stroke identification using CT brain scans. The system applies the use of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to automatically learn and extract discriminative imaging features, allowing a system to identify strokes reliably without a great deal of manual assistance. Moreover, Neural Architecture Search (NAS) is also included, which allows to optimize network layout automatically to guarantee better performance and less expert-based tuning. The model distinguishes between CT scans into stroke and non-stroke, and provides a scalable realtime decision-support tool to radiologists. Experimental analysis shows that the suggested approach is more effective and efficient than being used with traditional CNN structures regarding accuracy, precision, and resilience. The findings indicate how this intelligent diagnostic system can be used to help clinicians detect strokes early, minimize diagnostic delays, and improve clinical decision-making in healthcare settings.

title: Parameter-Efficient Cross-Layer Feature Fusion via Chebyshev Polynomial Unit
authors: Yue Xin, Jiarui Zhang, Ziyang Zheng, Yaoming Wang, Wenrui Dai, Chenglin Li, Junni Zou, Hongkai Xiong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Cross-layer feature fusion critically influences the performance of deep neural networks, where mainstream approaches like additive fusion of residual connections struggle to model high-order nonlinear interactions, limiting representational capacity, while concatenative fusion of dense connections incurs significant memory and computational overhead. Although recent attention-based feature fusion methods refine feature representations and strengthen hierarchical interactions, they often struggle to balance expressiveness and efficiency. To address these limitations, we propose the Chebyshev Fusion Unit (CFU), a lightweight yet effective cross-layer fusion mechanism. Specifically, CFU computes high-order Chebyshev polynomial terms between residual features and current-layer features, each of which is aggregated through learnable scalar weights, forming enhanced fusion features. This design enables explicit modeling of complex cross-layer dependencies with minimal additional parameters. Extensive experiments across various commonly used base models on image classification, medical image segmentation, and physical law learning tasks demonstrate its superior performance.

title: Longitudinal Cognitive Scores for Alzheimer's Disease Progression Prediction: An Interactive Patient Dashboard for Clinical Decision Support
authors: Gurusigaamani Ayyanar Muthulingam, P. Nagaraj, Rajesh Kanna R, Sakthi Sanjay S, Vijayakumar M, Kesani Rohith
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: AD) Alzheimer Disease is a serious concern on development since it is a progression of (MCI) Mild Cognitive Impairment that is important to diagnose and intervene in early stages. This article describes a deep learning system using transformers to use longitudinal cognitive scores to predict the evolution of AD. The model gives two-output predictions; a binary classification to convert MCI to AD and regression to predict time-to-conversion. Based on ADNI data, the model is used to capture temporal dependencies of cognitive decline. Transformer model has been chosen due to its theoretical advantages over recurrent models (LSTM/GRU) in the process of modeling long-range dependencies. The model constructs an interactive clinical dashboard that provides time predictions and real-time actionable risk scores to clinicians. Having an AUC of 0.94 and 91.7% sensitivity, the model can contribute to personal care planning and decision-making and offer a promising approach to precision medicine in AD care. The paper contributes to AI in healthcare (SDG-3), providing an evidence-based instrument in the early detection of AD that enables clinical decision-making.

title: Robustness of a Tensor-Train Based Two-Stage Algorithm for Color Video Recovery
authors: Jianan WANG, Hengyou WANG
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Color video is widely used in various fields. However, it often suffers from substantial data loss and noise interference during acquisition and transmission. To address these issues, we propose a two-stage recovery algorithm based on TensorTrain decomposition to complete the tensor recovery task under challenging conditions, including high missing rates and severe noise corruption. In the first stage, key frames are selected and recovered in high quality using a graph-regularized Tensor-Train completion method to serve as anchors for the next stage. The second stage introduces a confidence-guided Laplacian smoothing method to recover intermediate frames, with the support of key frame “anchors” for initialization. A confidence map is constructed to guide the update process based on the spatiotemporal information of the neighboring pixels. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art tensor completion approaches in both PSNR and SSIM metrics, while also producing superior visual results.

title: Adaptive L1 Guidance with Real-Time Lookahead Scaling Based on Aircraft Constraints
authors: Talha Ulukır, Yusuf Sıncak
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Adaptive three-dimensional L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> guidance dynamically scales the look-ahead distance in real time using explicit aircraft constraints. Unlike fixed L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> settings, the proposed scheme computes a feasible look-ahead from instantaneous geometry and vehicle motion while honoring limits on maximum bank angle, roll-rate capability, and inner-loop bandwidth. The result is guidance command shaping that preserves maneuverability in high-curvature segments and avoids overshoot and oscillations on gentle tracks. We derive closed-form bounds and introduce a lightweight smoothing filter that ensures responsive yet non-oscillatory updates. The algorithm integrates into standard flight-control architectures without altering the core L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> logic and remains suitable for embedded implementation. Simulation studies over representative 3D waypoint-following tasks—climbs, level turns, and descents—demonstrate faster convergence, reduced cross-track error and overshoot, and improved constraint adherence relative to fixed-L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> baselines. The method is model-agnostic, robust to sensor noise, and requires only routinely available states. Overall, constraint-aware look-ahead adaptation offers a practical path to higher performance and safety margins in UAV guidance while retaining the simplicity that makes L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> attractive in practice.

title: Algorithm for Detecting High-Speed Targets Using the Hough Transform
authors: Serhii Smaliuha, Ihor Prokopenko
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: This paper investigates the application of the Hough transform (HT) to detect linear trajectories of fast targets in the presence of significant interference and missed observations. Unlike approaches that focus on range migration (RCM), the main emphasis here is on robustness to noise/clutter and local interference. First, the factors that cause the migration of the target echo signal between range cells within the coherent accumulation are analyzed. Then, using the property of the HT transform to detect straight lines, the relationship between HT parameters and the initial range and radial velocity of the target is established. Building on this model, designed a manual accumulator in the (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>, υ) space with a voting "tube" matched to range jitter, and derive a Neyman–Pearson decision rule with threshold calibration under the noise-only hypothesis to control the false-alarm probability. Monte Carlo simulations with stochastic clutter, dropouts, and Gaussian range jitter demonstrate that the proposed HT detector concentrates dispersed target returns into a single, interpretable peak, yielding higher detection probability at fixed false-alarm rate than cell-wise thresholding and least-squares line fitting on sparse detections. Simulation examples are presented to confirm the effectiveness of the algorithm for detecting high-speed targets.

title: Automated Detection of Rotten and Fresh Fruits and Vegetables with Shelf Life Prediction Using YOLO
authors: Adarsh Kumar, Vineet Banashankari, V. Arun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Food spoilage contributes significantly to global food loss, making early identification of quality degradation essential for supply-chain optimization and consumer safety. This work presents an integrated system for real-time detection of fresh and rotten fruits and vegetables using the YOLOv8 object detection framework, combined with a temperature- and humidity-aware shelf-life prediction model. A dataset comprising eight commonly consumed produce types, each captured in fresh and rotten states, was compiled from publicly available sources and custom acquisitions, with manual labelling and standardized train–validation–test splits. The proposed YOLOv8 model achieves high detection performance, obtaining a mean Precision of 82.9% and mean Recall of 86.3%, with an mAP@50 value of 88.5%. Shelf-life estimation is performed through a modified Arrhenius-based mathematical formulation optimized using a Genetic Algorithm, with experimental evaluation yielding MAE and RMSE values indicative of accurate real-world prediction. Comparative analysis shows that YOLOv8 outperforms Faster R-CNN and SSD in both accuracy and inference speed, making it suitable for deployment in supermarkets, warehouses, and mobile quality-inspection applications. The integration of freshness classification with quantitative shelf-life prediction demonstrates a promising approach for reducing food waste and improving inventory management across the supply chain.

title: MIMO Radar Waveform Design in Spectrum-Crowded Environments with Uncertain Steering Vectors
authors: Xu Meiyingzi, Yang Wei, Zhang Wenpeng
abstract: The growing density of communication and radar devices renders multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) radar systems susceptible to severe detection performance degradation caused by even slight steering vector mismatches. To ensure robust detection under such mismatches, this paper presents a robust waveform design approach based on a steering vector uncertainty-constrained Max-Min signal-to-interference-plus noise ratio (SINR) formulation. Compared to conventional Max SINR designs, the proposed method optimizes waveforms that maintain high SINR even in the presence of steering vector errors. The problem incorporates constraints for spectral compatibility and peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR). To solve this non convex problem, we develop an efficient iterative algorithm that employs successive convex approximation (SCA) to transform the original problem into a sequence of convex subproblems, which are then solved in parallel via the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM). Numerical simulations show a reduction in convergence time of up to 30% compared to existing techniques.

title: Robust Modified SOGI-Based Control Scheme for Grid-Connected Bidirectional Multifunctional Residential Solar EV Chargers
authors: Allu Bhargav, Indrajit Sarkar
abstract: This work presents a solar PV fed bi-directional EV charger (PVBEVC) designed for residential applications to facilitate charging/discharging of EV battery and simultaneous power quality enhancement by compensating reactive and harmonic powers at the point of common coupling (PCC). To achieve effective harmonic compensation, the system employs a Modified Discrete Second-Order Generalized Integrator (MDSOGI) algorithm to enable high-precision extraction of the fundamental load-current component. Moreover, the grid synchronization is achieved using an MDSOGI-based Frequency Locked Loop (FLL) featuring superior DC-offset rejection, improved transient dynamics, and reduced computational burden. For managing bidirectional power flow during EV battery charging and discharging, a Dual Active Bridge converter (DABC) is utilized. Extended Phase Shift (EPS) modulation is applied to improve efficiency by suppressing backflow power and minimizing the circulating current on the battery-side converter. First, a detail analysis of various operating modes of the PVBEVC is presented focusing on its dual function of battery charging/discharging and power quality improvement feature with enhanced performance using MDSOGI algorithm and MDSOGI-based FLL. To validate the proposed system and its functionalities like harmonic and reactive power compensations, an experimental prototype is developed and tested under various operating modes to demonstrate its practical viability.

title: A Two-Stage Biologically Inspired Robot Navigation Framework Via Reward-Modulated STDP and Obstacle-State Encoding
authors: Rizwana Kausar, Vidya Sudevan, Jaime Viegas, Jorge Dias
abstract: This article presents a two-stage, biologically inspired neuromorphic framework for autonomous robot navigation in cluttered environments. The proposed architecture combines unsupervised spiking neural network (SNN)-based sensory abstraction with reward-modulated spike-timing-dependent plasticity (R-STDP) for decision-making. In the first stage, raw 360° LiDAR measurements are transformed into a compact, interpretable obstacle state through a lateral-inhibition-driven STDP network, yielding a low-dimensional, behaviorally relevant perception of the environment. In the second stage, navigation actions are learned via reward-modulated STDP operating on this abstracted state, supporting long-horizon goal-directed behavior without backpropagation or deep reinforcement learning. Two navigation paradigms are investigated: conventional goal-oriented navigation using relative bearing information, and a probabilistic field–based formulation that enables source-seeking behavior under goal uncertainty. The proposed approach is evaluated extensively in Gazebo and NVIDIA Isaac Sim using a TurtleBot3 platform across static, dynamic, and near-realistic environments. Experimental results demonstrate reliable navigation performance, competitive success and collision rates compared to state-of-the-art SNN and hybrid SNN–RL methods, and substantially lower estimated energy consumption. These findings highlight the effectiveness of modular, biologically plausible neuromorphic architectures for energy-efficient autonomous navigation in complex environments.

title: AI-Assisted Metasurface Antennas Design/Optimization and Performance Enhancement Techniques: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: M. Hamza Boulaich, Said Ohamouddou, M. Ali Ennasar, A. El Afia
abstract: Metasurface optimization employing evolutionary algorithms (EAs) for forward design and machine learning (ML) for inverse design has emerged as a rapidly developing field that uses artificial intelligence (AI) to design and optimize metasurface antenna systems. Metasurface antenna design encompasses the manipulation of electromagnetic waves by controlling their amplitude, phase, and polarization. The optimization process focuses on enhancing the metasurface antenna characteristics to achieve desirable performance metrics, including gain, bandwidth, radiation pattern, and directivity. The advancement of metasurface antennas aims to improve their performance, functionality, and adaptability to satisfy contemporary communication and sensing requirements. This study aims to provide novel recommendations for designing and optimizing metasurface antennas using AI techniques such as intelligent optimization algorithms, ML, and deep learning (DL). Therefore, this paper presents a comprehensive survey of recent literature on metasurface antenna technologies from forward-looking perspectives. Specifically, this study examines recent progress in evolutionary algorithms and AI-enabled approaches, develops and validates a multi-objective fuzzy logic genetic algorithm (MOFLGA) for complementary split-ring resonators (CSRR), and establishes a machine-learning-based classification framework for frequency-selective surfaces through comparative analysis of multipleMLmodels. Currently, Numerous metasurface designs have been developed by researchers worldwide. However, a comprehensive study integrating different types of electromagnetic metasurfaces, metasurface antennas, and their applications with AI and performance enhancement techniques remains lacking.

title: Analysis and Mitigation of Power-Transfer-Dependent Small-Signal Instabilities in Interconnected GFM-GFL Systems
authors: Rui Liu, Han Zhang, Zhiheng Lin, Yunwei Ryan Li
abstract: In interconnected systems comprising grid-forming (GFM) and grid-following (GFL) converters, different types of small-signal instabilities may occur. However, an analytical interpretation of their underlying mechanisms and dependence on active power transfer remains lacking, hindering the understanding of instability phenomena and the determination of stable operating ranges. To address this gap, this article proposes a single-input single-output model for interconnected GFM-GFL systems. Based on this model, the mechanisms and power-transfer dependence of two distinct types of instabilities are analytically interpreted: one arises from the interaction between the GFM converter's inner voltage control and the GFL converter's phase-locked loop, with instability risk intensified when the GFL converter transfers higher active power to the GFM converter; the other arises from the interaction between the GFM converter's outer active power control and the GFL converter's phase-locked loop, with instability risk intensified under higher active power transfer in either direction. Furthermore, to ensure effective mitigation of these instabilities across the full range of power transfer, analytical boundaries for critical converter control parameters are derived. Finally, the theoretical analyses and the derived parameter boundaries are validated through real-time simulations and experiments.

title: A Graph Neural Network-Based Approach for Critical Node Detection in Dynamic Satellite Networks
authors: Hengyi Lv, Lifeng Cao, Xiaohan Wang, Xingchen Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: To address the challenges in critical node identification within complex satellite networks, such as difficulties in dynamic modeling and limited computational resources, this study proposes an innovative spatiotemporal feature learning-based identification scheme. First, by coupling a dynamic geographic constraint mechanism with complex network modeling, a generative satellite network model (BS-TN) incorporating satellite orbit characteristics was developed, enabling generation of controllable simulation datasets. Second, a spatiotemporal fusion node importance evaluation model (ST-FNIM) was introduced. A lightweight L-GAT network was designed by incorporating nonlinear attenuation into the attention mechanism and pruning the multi-head attention to reduce the computational overhead, thereby extracting the multi-dimensional spatial features of satellite nodes across time steps. An event-driven ET-LSTM architecture was also proposed, enabling responsive sequential analysis of node behaviors via an optimized gating mechanism. By fusing spatiotemporal features, the model enabled the accurate dynamic identification of critical nodes. The experimental results demonstrated that compared with conventional centrality algorithms and existing deep learning methods, the proposed approach significantly improved computational efficiency and dynamic adaptability, while increasing identification accuracy by approximately 10%. It enabled real-time assessment and response to sudden events such as link failures and traffic surges, offering a novel technical framework for satellite network management, resource optimization, and security assurance.

title: Synchronization of Stochastic Networked Systems via Nonlinear Distributed Control
authors: Yuetong Xiao, Shuyuan Zhang, Lei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: The synchronization control of stochastic networked systems (SNSs) is still an open problem when manually-crafted quadratic Lyapunov functions (QLFs) are unavailable. This paper introduces a novel hierarchy of polynomial Lyapunov functions (PLFs) that unifies the search of distributed control protocol and the assurance of synchronization for SNSs. Based on a general nonlinear form of the distributed control protocol, a sufficient criterion is established by PLFs with conditions relaxed from the Lipschitz-like conditions. For polynomial SNSs, the synchronization control problem is formulated as a convex sum-of-squares (SOS) optimization problem, where the polynomial control protocols (PCPs) can be found and PLFs are automatically computed in polynomial time. The proposed hierarchy of PLFs is broadly applicable for SNSs where QLFs are non-existent. Moreover, the hierarchy of PLFs can be extended to address the synchronization control problem for systems perturbed with communication noise between nodes. Comparative numerical results demonstrate the effectiveness and advantages of our approach.

title: EmotionVibe: Human Emotion Recognition Through Footstep-Induced Floor Vibrations_supp1-3666144.pdf
authors: Yuyan Wu
abstract: Emotion recognition is critical for various applications, including the early detection of mental health disorders and emotion-based smart home systems. Previous studies utilized various sensing methods for emotion recognition, such as wearable sensors, cameras, and microphones. However, these methods are often intrusive or raise significant privacy concerns, which may reduce user acceptance for continuous, long-term deployment. This paper introduces a non-intrusive and privacy-friendly personalized emotion recognition system, EmotionVibe, which leverages footstep-induced floor vibrations for emotion recognition. The main idea of EmotionVibe is that individuals' emotional states influence their gait patterns, subsequently affecting the floor vibrations induced by their footsteps. However, there are two main research challenges: 1) the complex and indirect relationship between human emotions and footstep-induced floor vibrations and 2) the large between-person variations within the relationship between emotions and gait patterns. To address these challenges, we first empirically characterize this complex relationship and develop an emotion-sensitive feature set including gait-related and vibration-related features from footstep-induced floor vibrations. Furthermore, we personalize the emotion recognition system for each user by calculating gait similarities between the target person (i.e., the person whose emotions we aim to recognize) and those in the training dataset and assigning greater weights to training people with similar gait patterns in the loss function. We evaluated our system in human walking experiments with 20 participants, summing up to 37,001 footstep samples. EmotionVibe achieved the mean absolute error (MAE) of 1.11 and 1.07 for valence (unpleasant to pleasant) and arousal (calm to excited) score estimations, respectively, reflecting 19.0% and 25.7% error reduction compared to the baseline method (using only gait-related features without personalization).

title: Visual Exploration of a Historical Vietnamese Corpus of Captioned Drawings: A Case Study_supp2-3660122.pdf
authors: Kailiang Fu
abstract: This paper presents a case study focusing on the exploratory visual analysis of a unique historical dataset consisting of approximately 4000 visual sketches and associated captions from an encyclopedic book published in 1909-1910. The book, which offers insight into Vietnamese crafts and social practices, poses the challenge of extracting cultural meaning and narrative structure from thousands of drawings and multilingual captions. Our research aims to explore and evaluate the effectiveness of multiple visualization techniques in uncovering meaningful relationships within the dataset while working closely with professional historians. The main contributions of this study include refining historical research questions through task and data abstraction, combining and validating visualization techniques for historical data interpretation, and involving a focus group of historians for further evaluation. These contributions offer generalizable insights for the development of domain-specific visualization tools and support interdisciplinary engagement in historical data visualization and critical digital humanities research.

title: Experimental Investigation into the Magnet Structure of Wave Energy Piston Generators
authors: Ruzhen Liu, Jun Yu, Wenbiao Hu, Haifeng Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Approximately 71% of Earth's surface is covered by oceans, whose abundant resources underpin the development of ‘Smart Oceans’ and ‘Transparent Oceans’, with marine equipment serving as the core safeguard. Marine wireless sensor networks (WSNs) face constraints from the short battery life of conventional power sources, high battery replacement costs, and associated pollution, creating an urgent need for sustainable power supply. Wave energy holds immense potential, yet traditional conversion devices suffer from insufficient efficiency; piston-type generators offer a viable solution. This paper focuses on a magnetic fluid-sealed oscillating water-column linear piston generator, investigating the influence of different magnet-piston configurations. Results indicate that the dual-repulsion magnet-sandwiched-iron-core configuration (Structure 2) delivers optimal overall performance, providing technical support for sustainable power supply to WSNs.

title:
Quasi-in-Situ XPS Investigation of Surface Chemical State Evolution in Plasma Activation for SiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Hydrophilic Bonding
authors: Haibo Yang, Fei Ding, Yihao Meng, Yu Zhang, Yudong Yang, Qiushi Kang, Hongchao Zhang, Lingyun Zhang, Renxi Jin, Qidong Wang
abstract: With the increasing demand for highperformance integrated circuits, hybrid bonding has emerged as a key solution for enabling high-density interconnects beyond the limitations of conventional solder-based methods. The success of hydrophilic bonding heavily relies on plasma activation to modify the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SiO}_{2}$</tex> surface. However, the high reactivity of the activated surface makes it prone to environmental influence, complicating accurate characterization. This study employs a quasi-in-situ X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) approach to investigate the evolution of the chemical state of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SiO}_{2}$</tex> surfaces activated by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}_{2}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex>, and Ar plasmas. The results reveal that all three plasmas effectively remove carbon contaminants, but re-adsorption occurs upon air exposure. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N}_{2}$</tex> plasma successfully incorporates nitrogen into the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SiO}_{2}$</tex> network, forming <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NSi}_{3}, \text{NSiO}_{2}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NSi}_{2} \mathrm{O}$</tex> species, which undergo decomposition and transformation upon air exposure and deionized water rinsing. In contrast, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex> and Ar plasmas failed to introduce new functional groups and exhibited minimal chemical changes. These findings provide new insights into the influence of subsequent processing steps on the surface chemical state following plasma activation.

title: A Physics-Informed Computer Vision Framework for Firefighting Assistance
authors: Abhijeet Singh Sambyal, M Sesha Sai Vamsi, J. Joshan Athanesious, S. Kiruthika
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Real-Time fire detection and accurate prediction of fire spread are critical challenges in the development of intelligent emergency response systems, particularly in complex indoor environments where conventional sensor-based approaches often prove inadequate. This study proposes a novel framework that integrates YOLOv8-based computer vision techniques with physics-informed predictive modeling to enhance the detection and tracking of fire incidents. A multi-perspective feature analysis methodology is introduced to differentiate true fire events from false positives, leveraging temporal stability assessments, color variance analysis, and dynamic shape evaluation. To enable robust tracking of fire propagation, a Kalman filter grounded in a constant acceleration model is employed. Furthermore, a weighted forecasting mechanism is developed to predict the spread of fire, incorporating spatial and temporal information such as fire centroid position, morphological changes, intensity gradients, and color features, while dynamically adjusting decision confidence thresholds. The system also includes an environmental risk assessment module, which estimates the vulnerability of nearby objects based on material flammability, spatial proximity to ignition sources, and expected combustion behavior. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed system achieves a 92% reduction in false positive detections and attains 89% accuracy in identifying high-risk objects and predicting fire spread. Operating at 12–15 frames per second on consumer-grade hardware, the framework is well-suited for real-time deployment in emergency scenarios. By synergizing deep learning-based pattern recognition with domain-specific physical modeling, this work significantly advances the development of reliable, responsive, and interpretable fire monitoring solutions.

title: Device-to-Package Electrothermal Performance Prediction of Power MOSFETs via Coupled Iterative Dual-Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Yuxuan Dai, Jing Chen, Jiafei Yao, Ziwei Hu, Shoukun Huang, Maolin Zhang, Jun Zhang, Zhikuang Cai, Yufeng Guo
abstract: Electrothermal effect across the micrometer-scale device to millimeter-scale package causes thermal gradients and reliability issues. Assessing device and package structural parameters on such cross-scale electrothermal effects is essential for device-package co-design and improving thermal management. This paper proposes a coupled iterative dualArtificial Neural Networks (CIDA) framework for fast, accurate device-to-package electrothermal performance assessment of power MOSFETs. The CIDA framework integrates an electrical ANN and a thermal ANN, offering an efficient alternative to Technology Computer-Aided Design (TCAD) and ANSYS simulations. The electrical ANN predicts device drain current and calculates power loss, while the thermal ANN computes chip temperature. These ANNs iteratively exchange power and temperature data, achieving a self-consistent electrothermal solution. As a result, from device-package structural parameters, bias conditions, and environmental factors, the device-topackage electrothermal performance can be accurately and quickly predicted. Compared to the iterative simulation using ANSYS and TCAD, the efficiency of the CIDA framework is improved by 75 times. Moreover, the error is less than 3 % compared to experimental results.

title: A BGP Network Model Based on SDN and REGEX
authors: Jun Tao, Can LingWang, Lei Yue, Hao Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: BGP is a widely deployed routing protocol designed for inter-domain communication between AS, due to the complex connectivity paths among AS, route determination often relies on the BGP AS_PATH attribute. However, BGP incurs high maintenance costs, as network changes typically require manual configuration reloading, the operational burden on network administrators will be increased sharply. To address this issue, a network framework based on SDN and REGEX mechanisms is proposed, it enables centralized management of the AS_PATH attribute through a programmable controller. Coupled with SDN enforcement, this approach improves network management efficiency by leveraging REGEX-based path matching and optimization. The new network model is validated through simulation experiments, which yielded promising results. Subsequently, the framework in a physical network environment is implemented, all operational parameters performed are as expected, the network modal's feasibility and effectiveness is confirmed.

title: Device Assembly and Modeling Validation to Analyze Stress for Different Size iBGA Image Sensors Induced by Encapsulant Material
authors: Shahram Seyedmohammadi, Ning Liu, Howard Yun, Matthew Tsai, Xuan Hong, Tim Champagne, Lina Bian, Jaeik Kang, Rejoy Surendran
abstract: This work pinpoints mechanical stress implied by encapsulation in an iBGA/CMOS imaging device. There is currently a trend to go toward larger size iBGA/CMOS imaging devices for example in auto industry application. The level of mechanical stress and location of stress in a device strongly correlates with device size, which is the main subject of this study. The findings in this work assist to design and formulate encapsulant materials to adapt and tolerate mechanical stress in different locations of iBGA device structure such as interface between encapsulant material and glass sidewalls as well as the interface between encapsulant material and the substrate.

title: Performance of Positioning by Joint Network of Radio Navigation Aids in the Baltic Sea Region
authors: Ivan Ostroumov
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Accuracy of primary navigation system is one of the most important factors that could affect the safety and security of aviation. Nowadays air transportation system is affected by a wide area of unintentional interference in Eastern Europe and core of Middle Eastern countries. Interference in Global Navigation Satellite System signals could be resulted to system lock, which requires to use of Attitude Heading and Reference System (AHRS) for defined by performance duration or navigation by set of navigation aids: VOR-DME, DME-DME, and VOR-VOR. Accuracy of positioning by navigation aids depends on performance and configuration of ground networks of radio beacons. In this paper, the performance of joint navigation aid network is validated to meet requirements of basic area navigation specifications (RNAV 1) for the Baltic Sea Region. Military activity due to high invasion risk in this region caused unintentional jamming of civil aviation that requiring to use of conventional navigation equipment for airplanes maintaining on a cleared trajectory. Results of analysis show that DME/DME positioning method supports requirements of RNAV 1 specification. Navigation by co-located VOR/DME could be useful in closed areas to navigation aids.

title: Deep Feature Extraction Using Channel Self-Attention for Focal Liver Lesion Classification
authors: Sunkanaboina Chandra Lingamaiah, Priyanka Kokil
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Contrast-enhanced ultrasound (CEUS) image analysis plays a critical role in the detection and characterization of focal liver lesions (FLL) such as hemangioma, hepatocellular carcinoma, and focal nodular hyperplasia, which is essential to improve diagnostic efficiency and treatment outcomes. Its diagnostic accuracy is influenced by the size, contrast, and location of the lesion. Deep learning techniques enhance the feature representation of the liver lesions and facilitate more accurate differentiation between benign and malignant lesions. However, timely identification of FLL is essential to improve the diagnostic efficiency and treatment outcomes. Therefore, this work proposes a framework for the automated classification of FLLs in CEUS frames. The proposed framework leverages DarkNet-19 as the cues extraction from CEUS frames. A channel self attention module is integrated to emphasize informative features by recalibrating inter-channel dependencies from the backbone cues. The modified cues are passed through pooling and dense layers for multiclass prediction. The model is validated on the public SYSU-FLL-CEUS dataset, and the experimental findings show improved classification performance when compared to traditional deep CNN models, indicating its potential for reliable clinical application.

title: Empowering Smart Entrepreneurship through IoT: A Bibliometric Analysis for Sustainable Economic Development
authors: Salsabila Ainun Mahya, Sahnaz Ubud
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is reshaping the landscape of entrepreneurial innovation, enabling the development of intelligent, interconnected systems that support strategic growth, operational agility, and sustainable development [1], [3], [4]. This study conducts a comprehensive bibliometric analysis using the Bibliometrix <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> package, examining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 6 0 9}$</tex> peer-reviewed publications retrieved from the Scopus database. The objective is to map key trends, influential sources, and emerging themes in the evolving academic discourse on IoT's role in fostering smart entrepreneurship [14], [17]. The results reveal a significant rise in scholarly engagement over the past decade, driven by digital transformation agendas and the global pursuit of sustainability [2], [10], [21]. Core themes such as technological innovation [5],data-driven decision-making [19], and entrepreneurship [22] emerge as central pillars of the intellectual landscape. Visualization tools such as co-occurrence networks, word clouds, and thematic maps further expose the strategic convergence of IoT and sustainable business practices [24], [25]. This study offers a novel synthesis of how IoT-enabled innovation is conceptualized within academic literature and identifies clear pathways for practical application in digital enterprises. The findings provide valuable insights for entrepreneurs, researchers, and policymakers, highlighting IoT's potential not only as a technological enabler [6], [8] but also as a transformative strategy for building resilient and future-ready businesses in a connected economy [7], [23].

title: Statistical Evaluation of Bond Strength Variation in Hybrid Bonding Interfaces
authors: Daiki Kobayashi, Shunsuke Teranishi, Yuki Yoshihara, Marie Sano, Fumihiro Inoue
abstract: Cu-Cu hybrid bonding has gained attention, yet ensuring process reliability requires robust methodologies for bond strength characterization. The widely used Double-Cantilever-Beam (DCB) test is restricted to wafer edges. To address these limitations, this study employs the 4-PointBending (4PB) and Nanoindentation (NI) tests to evaluate bond strength variation at both wafer and die levels. The 4PB test revealed strong environmental dependence: higher bond strength was obtained under anhydrous conditions, whereas degradation occurred in ambient air due to water stress corrosion (WSC). For high-strength samples after post bond annealing (PBA), fracture of the Si substrate occurred before interfacial delamination, indicating the measurement limit of this method. In contrast, the NI test, inherently localized and destructive, was reinforced with statistical approaches. This ensured reliability and enabled high-resolution visualization of spatial variations in bond strength. Wafer-level analysis showed radial and angular variations, with locally elevated bond strength in middle regions and stable values at edges. At the die level, PBA-free samples exhibited reduced bond strength at corners and higher strength near the center. After PBA, average bond strength increased substantially, but pronounced nonuniformities persisted. These findings demonstrate that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 P B}$</tex> and NI tests together provide a comprehensive framework for optimizing bonding processes and ensuring reliability in nextgeneration 3D integration.

title: Understanding the Security Impact of CHERI on the Operating System Kernel
authors: Zhaofeng Li, Jerry Zhang, Joshua Tlatelpa-Agustin, Xiangdong Chen, Anton Burtsev
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Capability Hardware Enhanced RISC Instructions (CHERI) is a set of hardware extensions that allow enforcement of spatial and temporal safety for unsafe programming languages like C. CHERI utilizes the concept of hardware capabilities to enforce bounds checks on all memory accesses and a hardware-assisted revocation scheme to enforce temporal safety. In theory, CHERI offers a surprising mix of practicality and strong security guarantees for traditionally unsafe environments like operating system kernels: capability extensions block a range of safety-related vulnerabilities common to low-level systems code while requiring only modest engineering effort. Our work takes a deep look at the potential impact of CHERI on the security of a commodity operating system kernel. We analyze a total of 439 kernel vulnerabilities in Linux and FreeBSD kernels. Our analysis shows that CHERI can block 61 % of kernel vulnerabilities if temporal safety is implemented in the kernel (35% if capability revocation is off). Enabling CHERI requires a modest effort, e.g., porting the FreeBSD kernel to support pure-capability mode of execution took 7 months. Finally, compared to Rust, which is able to mitigate 84% of kernel exploits, CHERI achieves the rate of 70% (38% if revocation is off). While CHERI is less effective, enabling it in the kernel requires a much lower development effort.

title: The Potential Adoption of an AI-Based Crowd-Shipping Platform on Trans Banyumas: The Passengers' Viewpoint
authors: Muh Idwar Ramadhani, Fauzan Romadlon, Yudha Saintika, Abdul Hamid
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: High population density impacts the transportation system's performance in serving the community's mobility needs, and the Banyumas Regency is classified as an area with high population concentration. In December 2021, the Ministry of Transportation of Banyumas Regency launched Trans Banyumas. This bus service is not only used to transport passengers, but also has the potential to be used to transport goods, such as crowd-shipping with AIbased technology. This study aims to measure the factors needed to develop the AI-based crowd-shipping platform through the Trans Banyumas service based on the passengers' viewpoint. The quantitative method used involved 300 Trans Banyumas passengers as respondents. Data were collected through a questionnaire covering sociodemography, travel characteristics, and crowd-shipping variables. The results show that sociodemography and travel characteristics significantly influence AI-based crowd-shipping implementation. Trans Banyumas passengers indicate a strong potential for developing an AI-based crowd-shipping platform. Most passengers are students and young workers who demonstrate a high level of interest and readiness to participate in the AI-based crowd-shipping system, due to the opportunity to earn a reward. Those groups are more open to technological innovation, exhibit high mobility, and are accustomed to switching between different modes of transportation. Therefore, it is essential to tailor AI-based crowd-shipping planning to passengers based on their patterns while emphasizing a sender and carrier behaviors simulation model to capture participation decision, feedback loops, and a dynamic network as a crucial aspect in crowd-shipping.

title: G-SPVD: Image and Sketch Guided Point Cloud Generation with Sparse Point-Voxel Diffusion Models
authors: Ioannis Romanelis, Vlassis Fotis, Adrian Munteanu, Konstantinos Moustakas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: We propose a novel framework, Guided Sparse Point-Voxel Diffusion (G-SPVD), for Point Cloud generation guided from a single visual input - either an image or a rough hand-drawn sketch, both from an unknown viewing angle. G-SPVD combines a Vision Transformer with a Diffusion Model that iteratively forms a noisy set of points to match the requested input. Our quantitative evaluation demonstrates that our framework achieves state-of-the-art results compared to other methods in single-image reconstruction on the ShapeNet dataset. Moreover, despite the reduced information available in sketchbased inputs, our sketch-guided model still attains competitive reconstruction metrics. We present several qualitative results for both tasks to further illustrate the effectiveness of our method. Finally, we evaluate our method on unconditional generation, demonstrating that our model can generate shapes with quality and diversity on par with the current state-of-the-art. Our code will be released upon publication.

title: Data-Driven Insights into Halting Stage of Pigs Cross-Breeding
authors: Likhitha Koppula, Pravati Swain, Amiya Ranjan Sahu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Crossbreeding in livestock aims to enhance traits like growth, fertility, and feed efficiency while minimizing issues such as aggression, lameness, and tail-biting. However, deciding the optimal generation to stop crossbreeding is challenging, as it involves balancing multiple conflicting traits. This study simulates pig crossbreeding across five generations (G0–G4), and identifies the ideal generation to halt for maximum benefit. Multi-objective optimization using NSGA-II is applied to improve productive traits. A reinforcement learning model is trained using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) in a custom simulation environment and learns effective stopping strategies based on trait progression. Moreover, the XGBoost model combined with SHAP analysis helps interpret the influence of key features such as genetic ratios, serotonin levels, and behavioral traits. This paper represents an extensive study on the different behavior and risk status of pigs after crossbreeding in different generations. Moreover, different prediction models are used that indicate the halting at Generation 2 (G2) offers the best balance, and the proposed model shows a predictive accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.89). This approach supports data-driven decisions for sustainable and efficient crossbreeding of pigs with behavioral prediction and risk factors.

title: CVBench: Benchmarking and Comparing Video Generation with Large Multimodal Models
authors: Jiarui Wang, Huiyu Duan, Yuke Xing, Wei Zhou, Guangtao Zhai, Xiongkuo Min
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Large multimodal models (LMMs) have revolutionized both text-to-video (T2V) generation and video-to-text (V2T) interpretation. However, despite these advancements, issues such as imperfect perceptual quality and inconsistent text-video alignment continue to limit the practical deployment of AI-generated videos (AIGVs). Consequently, there is a pressing need for a reliable benchmark and automatic evaluation framework tailored for AIGVs. To this end, we propose CVBench, the largest and most comprehensive dataset for Comparative Video Benchmarking, including 60K video pairs generated by 30 state-of-the-art T2V models and 600K pairwise comparisons annotated with over 1.7 million human judgments from perspectives of both perceptual quality and text-video correspondence. This dataset enables bidirectional benchmarking and evaluation of both T2V generation models and V2T interpretation models. Based on CVBench, we propose VComp, a novel LMM-based evaluation metric that captures fine-grained quality differences from multiple perspectives for pairwise comparison at both the instance level and model level. Extensive experiments show that VComp achieves state-of-the-art alignment with human preferences. Both the CVBench dataset and VComp metric will be available at https://github.com/IntMeGroup/CVBench.

title: A Catalog of Participatory Reuse Challenges, Solutions, and Lessons Learned
authors: Xingru Chen, Muhammad Usman, Deepika Badampudi
abstract: Organizations are increasingly adopting Participatory Reuse, an InnerSource practice where consumers of the reusable assets also contribute to their development and maintenance. While existing literature has reported various challenges, solutions, and lessons learned from organizations engaging in participatory reuse, a consolidated synthesis of these findings remains lacking. This study aims to collect and analyze the challenges, solutions, and lessons learned in participatory reuse from empirical works conducted within industrial settings. We conducted a case survey of $\mathbf{3 4}$ primary studies in $\mathbf{1 5}$ case organizations that have implemented participatory reuse practices. Based on the core characteristics of software reuse, we categorized the identified challenges, solutions, and lessons learned into three themes: developing reusable assets, using reusable assets, and managing participatory reuse. We organized the identified evidence as a catalog of participatory reuse challenges, solutions, and lessons learned. This catalog is intended to support both practitioners and researchers in identifying relevant strategies to address specific participatory reuse challenges within their organizations. We also pilot tested the catalog with four industry experts, who found it valuable in identifying solutions and lessons learned from the challenges.

title: Service-Based Village Information System with Artificial Intelligence (AI) Chatbot
authors: Murhadi, Wahju Tjahjo Saputro, Hamid Muhammad Jumasa, Ike Yunia Pasa, Dewi Chirzah, Hanifatus Sa'diah
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study presents the development of a services-based Village Information System (VIS) integrated with an AI-powered chatbot to enhance public service delivery in Krandegan Village, Central Java. The methodology includes problem identification, technology selection, system design, user interface development, and scenario-based testing. The system leverages Large Language Models (LLMs), specifically OpenAI's GPT-4, and incorporates components such as a conversational interface, knowledge store, conversation logic, and a vector-based database for storing training data. The chatbot was trained using crawled data from the official village website to ensure domain relevance. System testing was conducted using formal Indonesian, everyday language, English, and Javanese to assess its adaptability and inclusiveness. Results show that the chatbot is capable of accurately responding to diverse queries while providing access to official links and communication channels such as WhatsApp. A key limitation of the system is its dependence on the knowledge base; it cannot answer questions outside the scope of the training data. This limitation can be addressed through periodic updates and the inclusion of more comprehensive data.

title: Predicting Tsunami Risk from Earthquakes in Indonesia using Machine Learning
authors: Haniif Satria Wardana, Rayan Ferhat Al Jaidi, Nunung Nurul Qomariyah, Raymond Bahana
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study presents an advanced machine learning-based system for accurate tsunami risk prediction in Indonesia, one of the world's most seismically active countries. We employ several advanced models, including Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting techniques, to significantly enhance the efficiency and accuracy of existing tsunami early warning systems. Through comprehensive analysis with advanced data preprocessing and extensive feature engineering, including critical features such as azimuth gap and phase count, we substantially improved the model's capability for accurate tsunami prediction. The Gradient Boosting algorithm demonstrated superior performance, outperforming all other models with the highest predictive accuracy. Specifically, it achieved a ROC-AUC of 0.9612, an accuracy of 0.85, a precision of 0.73, a recall of 0.89, and an F1-score of 0.79, surpassing the performance metrics of all other tested algorithms. These findings demonstrate that machine learning methodologies can significantly improve disaster preparedness and reduce the devastating effects of tsunamis on Indonesia's population and infrastructure.

title: Attention-Guided Hybrid Deepfake Detection Model Using VGG-19, Optical Flow, and Face Embeddings
authors: Bhavya M, Nithesh Kumar M S, Abhinav Singh, Saurabh Verma, Tejas M Shetti, Sudarsh B S
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference for Women in Innovation, Technology &amp; Entrepreneurship (ICWITE)
abstract: The authenticity of deepfake content makes verification of authentic media extremely difficult because trust in information has completely vanished. A deepfake detection method combines style and content features through attention enhancement according to this study. Style features based on VGG-19 network and Gram matrices analyze face texture faults while FaceNet generates content features through optical flow and face embeddings. The MLP system uses attention modules to process tampered regions following a combination step of features. Research tests utilize FaceForensics++ DFS as well as DFDC and Celeb-DF benchmarks to establish high precision rates and generalization abilities. The real-time detection systems for deepfakes can use this method because it demonstrates both scalability for attack protection and automatic interpretation capabilities for detection purposes.

title: Investigation of Integrated Deep Learning Based Electrical Connector Anomaly Detection
authors: Ahmad Shahrizan Abdul Ghani, Ee Chern Ting, Nurul Haziyani Aris, Azri Hizami Abd Rasid, Mohd Yazid Abu, Mohd Fauzi Abu Hassan, Xin Jin, Fusaomi Nagata
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: The automotive industry’s pivotal role underscores the urgent demand for high-precision anomaly detection in electrical connectors, driven by the surge in connector production and the corresponding increase in defective units. Electrical connectors, integral to signal and energy transmission, are widely deployed across diverse electronic and automotive systems, where defects can lead to safety hazards, equipment malfunctions, and operational downtime. This research proposes an improved deep learning framework, RES-YOLO, designed to augment manual inspection and mitigate human error. The model synergistically integrates ResNet, recognized for robust feature extraction and defect identification, with YOLO, renowned for real-time object localization and classification. The focus is on Fakra connectors, commonly used in automotive signal transmission, where defect detection accuracy is critical. The architecture employs YOLOv8n as the backbone for rapid detection, while ResNet enhances hierarchical feature extraction, improving sensitivity to subtle connector anomalies. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLOv8n achieves balanced performance across all classes (Precision: 0.770, Recall: 0.779, F1-score: 0.774), albeit with limitations in color-based defect recognition. When excluding the "color" category, the YOLOv8n-ResNet101 hybrid exhibits superior performance in defect detection (Precision: 0.849, Recall: 0.960, F1-score: 0.901) and pin anomaly classification (Precision: 0.979, Recall: 1.000, F1-score: 0.989). These findings validate the effectiveness of the proposed RES-YOLO model in enhancing detection precision and efficiency for automotive connectors, providing a scalable solution for intelligent quality inspection systems in modern manufacturing.

title: Pig Weight Prediction Using RT-DETR and 3D Point Cloud Analysis
authors: Battu Kiranmai, Pravati Swain, Amiya Ranjan Sahu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Pig farming is a critical component of global agricultural systems. However, traditional weight measurement methods using mechanical scales are labor-intensive and prone to error owing to pig movement. Despite the importance of accurate weight measurements, existing methods are hindered by these challenges, highlighting the need for a more efficient and precise alternative. This study introduces a noninvasive approach that uses image detection technology to accurately predict the weights of pigs. By employing the Real-Time Detection Transformer (RT-DETR) for pig detection, the Segment Anything Model (SAM) for segmentation, and 3D point cloud analysis to extract morphological features, this system achieved a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 2.707, a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 2.904 kg, and a coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) is 0.98. These results indicate a prediction error of approximately ±3 kg, which offers a practical and scalable solution for modern pig farming. The proposed method reduces labor, enhances animal welfare, and supports precision livestock management.

title: WPRFormer: Window-aware Pixel Rearrangement in Window-based Transformers for Shadow Removal
authors: Shoma Shimmura, Masaaki Ikehara
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Shadow removal is an essential task in image processing and high-level vision applications. While recent methods with window-based attention have shown promising results, their limited ability to exchange information across local windows often leads to suboptimal performance in complex shadow scenarios. In this paper, we propose WPRFormer, a novel shadow removal framework that incorporates Window-Aware Pixel Rearrangement (WPR) into the attention module to facilitate more effective spatial interactions beyond individual windows. Since WPR rearranges pixels based on their original spatial layout, it largely preserves relative positional information within each window, allowing for the use of relative position bias without significant distortion. Our framework applies WPR Blocks at multiple resolution levels, enhancing feature representation while maintaining computational efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms existing approaches in both shadow and non-shadow regions, with particularly notable improvements in challenging shadow region, all while maintaining a comparable model complexity to strong baselines.

title: Comparative Analysis Of Financial Fraud Detection Models
authors: N Saranya, R. Sheeba, T. Siron Anita Susan, Srikavin S, Dhinakar K M
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The rise in web-based financial transactions and online banking has raised financial fraud detection to be considered an essential area of focus for financial institutions across the world. Rapid advancements in financial fraud techniques make it challenging for existing financial fraud detection approaches to be effective, making it necessary to explore and implement innovative Deep Learning (DL)-related techniques like Machine Learning (ML). This paper proposes to conduct a comparative analysis of four different hybrid financial fraud detection models consisting of LSTM with Decision Trees, LSTM with KNN, Autoencoder with Logistic Regression, and Autoencoder with Random Forest to explore the feasibility of integrating DL-based feature learning with existing Supervised Machine Learning (ML)-related approaches. The paper utilizes a real world-inspired imbalanced financial transaction dataset, simulated using the popular PaySim simulator, with SMOTE handling to treat it with a minority class related to financial fraud. Experimental outcomes illustrate that the Autoencoder and Random Forest hybrid model performs best in terms of F1 measure and Precision in real world-inspired financial fraud detection applications.

title: Modelling and Smart in Situ Monitoring for Wafer-to-Wafer Bonding Mechanism
authors: Christian Mühlstätter, Lukas Koller, Yan-Wen Tsau, Markus Wimplinger
abstract: Wafer level fusion bonding is an established method in advanced packaging and 3D integration in semiconductor architectures. This study investigates wafer-to-wafer fusion bonding dynamics using integrated in situ monitoring, multiphysics simulation, and in-plane distortion metrology. Variations in wafer and process conditions affect bond wave velocity and symmetry, which leads to distinct in-plane distortion fingerprints.

title: Heterogeneous Integration of High-Performance Compute, Memory and Optical Engine Chiplet on Large Reconstituted Interposer Package
authors: Chai Tai Chong, Mihai Rotaru, BG Sajay, Ji Lin, Han Yong, Chia Lai Yee, Sharon Lim Pei Siang, San Sandra, Svimonishvili Tengiz, Wu Jiaqi, Ye Yong Liang, Surya Bhattacharya
abstract: A very large reconstituted interposer with embedded Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe), High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM), and Optical Engine (OE) bridges is used as a packaging test vehicle to demonstrate the heterogeneous integration of accelerators/processors (xPU), HBM, and OEs directly on the interposer, enabling improved power efficiency, higher memory density, and greater off-package input/output (I/O) bandwidth. The packaging test vehicle supports process integration flow and material evaluations for this multi-chiplet system-in-package (SiP). The electrical, thermal and mechanical analysis considerations and measurements of test structures have been obtained.

title: Photonic Generation of Wideband Tunable Millimeter Wave Signals for Radar Application
authors: Xiaolong Zhao, Jin Li, Tao Pu, Jilin Zheng, Hua Zhou, Jiaqi Zhao, Xianshuai Meng, Shuya Liu, Xinyu Zhang, Yunming Zhang, Shuntao Xiao, Ganheng Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: As spectrum resources become increasingly congested, millimeter-wave (MMW) signals are gaining critical importance owing to their large bandwidth and high-speed capabilities. In this paper, we propose a novel photonic method for generating wideband tunable MMW signals based on photonic frequency multiplication and optical injection locking. In this system, an optical beat local oscillator (LO) is generated through frequency-selective injection of an optical frequency comb (OFC) into a semiconductor laser. Photonic frequency multiplication is achieved by combining the higher-order modulation sidebands of an electro-optic modulator with channelized processing. Then the frequency multiplied signal is subsequently up-converted to the MMW band via heterodyne beating with optical beat LO. Thanks to the fast-switching characteristics of wavelength-selective switches (WSS) and optical switches (OS), the proposed method provides flexible tunability and reconfigurability. Through simulations, we verified the generation of linear frequency modulated (LFM) signals with a center frequency of 102.5 GHz and a bandwidth of 5 GHz, as well as signals with a center frequency of 133.75 GHz and a bandwidth of 10 GHz. Furthermore, the proposed method enables tuning across the K, U, V and W bands, and can generate various radar waveforms, demonstrating significant flexibility. This method has the potential to greatly benefit radar systems, offering a promising solution for generating wideband tunable MMW radar signals.

title: Multi-Modal Spatiotemporal Graph Convolutional Network for Skeleton-Based Table Tennis Action Recognition
authors: Gaoyang Liu, Dazhuang Sun
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: This paper proposes a Multi-modal Spatiotemporal Graph Convolutional Network (MST-GCN) method for skeletonbased action recognition. In addition to traditional human skeletal sequence data, MST-GCN introduces relative positional information as an additional modality, significantly improving the recognition accuracy of highly similar actions. Experiments conducted on the large-scale table tennis action dataset P2A demonstrate the superior performance of MSTGCN in fine-grained table tennis action recognition tasks and validate the effectiveness of incorporating multi-modal information.

title: XPOZ-HUB: Privacy Infiltration in Payment Channel Hubs through Balance Probing and Transaction Discovery
authors: Alvi Ataur Khalil, Mohammad Ashiqur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: In the ever-evolving realm of blockchain technology, payment channels have become crucial for achieving competitive throughput. Payment channel network (PCN) was the ini-tial attempt to ease blockchain congestion by enabling off-chain transactions through a network of payment channels. However, routing paths can become long, causing high fees and delays. Payment Channel Hubs (PCHs) solve this by using a central entity (aka tumbler) for faster, cheaper transactions; without revealing sender, receiver, or amount details. Thus, PCHs aim to enhance privacy through promising relationship anonymity and concealed transaction amounts, which go beyond base layer where all transaction details are public. Despite these benefits, PCHs are vulnerable to balance discovery attacks (BDAs) due to channel capacity-driven transaction processing, which is fundamental to any system utilizing payment channels. In this work, we propose xPOZ-HUB, a novel attack technique designed to expose transactions occurring within the PCH system.
