Our approach involves a set of collaborative probers that exploit information leaks in PCH response messages to infer sensitive user information (i.e., balance) in an economic and stealthy way. The attackers further collude with the tumbler to access user interaction data, which is leveraged to formulate a constraint satisfaction problem (CSP). By solving this CSP, aided by deep learning (DL)-based transaction pattern identification and incorporating insights from BDA reconnaissance, the attackers uncover PCH transactions with high accuracy. The attack demonstrates that the privacy guar-antees advertised by PCHs are not absolute: the unlinkability and value confidentiality can be systematically undermined. We assessed the attack impact by extending the TumbleBit protocol to create a PCH simulator and experimented with PCH mainnet snapshots to illustrate real-world effects. While the attack works in real time on open channels; it cannot deanonymize settled transactions.

title: A Remaining Useful Life Prediction Method for Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistors Based on Transfer Learning
authors: Yongyi Li, Wei Ge, Wenwei Wang, Jinsong Liu, Gaige Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor (IGBT) is recognized as having its Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction constitute a crucial component in the implementation of Prognostics and Health Management (PHM). The investigation of IGBT RUL under varying operating conditions is considered to possess significant theoretical importance and engineering value. A transfer learning-based RUL prediction method is proposed in this study. Initially, corresponding current and voltage signals are extracted from aging data according to the operational characteristics of IGBTs, from which the on-state resistance is calculated and characterized. Subsequently, features from both source and target domain data are systematically selected and fused to construct a health indicator with high transferability. Finally, a Maximum Mean Discrepancy-based Domain Adversarial Neural Network (MMD-DANN) is employed to minimize cross-domain discrepancies, thereby enabling high-accuracy RUL prediction to be achieved across different operating conditions. Experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed method is proven to provide an effective solution for reliability assessment of power devices under different operational conditions.

title: Green Innovation Types and Environmental Performance in Malaysia: The Moderating Role of Government Initiatives
authors: Xiao Xuan Lim, Kui Ming Tiong, Lee Fong Cheah
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: This study aims to examine the effects of green innovation on environmental performance, focusing on green product innovation, green process innovation, and green managerial innovation, as well as the moderating role of government initiatives. Using partial least squares structural equation modelling on firm-level survey data, the analysis reveals that both green product innovation and green process innovation significantly improve environmental performance. Government initiatives play a significant role in moderating the relationship between green product innovation and environmental performance; however, the effect is negative, signalling a potential "crowding out" effect. The findings provide insights into the importance of firms strengthening their internal green capabilities and the need for policymakers to develop more targeted and innovation-aligned green initiatives.

title: Robust Nonlinear Electromechanical Tracking Systems Multi-Objective Design based on Hybrid Heuristic Optimization Algorithm
authors: B. Kuznetsov, I. Bovdui, O. Voloshko, T. Nikitina, V. Kolomiets, I. Nefodova
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Purpose of work is improving control accuracy by nonlinear electromechanical tracking system (NEMTS) with various technical requirements in different operating modes (VTRDOM). NEMTS design with nonlinear friction dependencies (NFD) and plant characteristics uncertainties (PCU) based on multiobjective nonlinear robust design (MNRD). Nonlinear robust regulators (NRR) and nonlinear robust observers (NRO) MNRD reduced to Hamilton-Jacobi-Isaacs equations solution (HJIES). NRR by NEMTS design as two degrees of freedom (TDF) regulators simultaneously combined open and closed loops control. MNRD target vector calculated as vector game solution (VGS). Payoff of VGS calculated as VTRDOM by modeling of NEMTS closed by designed NRR and NRO under various reference and disturbing influences and various NEMTS parameters. VGS is calculated from the condition of minimizing the NRR and NRO but maximizing the PCU of NEMTS for the «worst case». VGS calculated by hybrid heuristic optimization algorithm (HHOA) based on particle swarm optimization (PSO) and sequential quadratic programming (SQP) from Pareto optimal solutions (POS) with binary preference relations (BPR). NEMTS theoretical and experimental studies results are given. MNRD used allows improving control accuracy by NEMTS with VTRDOM compared to NEMTS with standard controllers.

title: Intelligent Cascaded Quasi Z-Source DC–DC Converter with ANN-Based Control for Enhanced Voltage Stability in Solar Photovoltaic Systems
authors: Vinod Kumar P, K. Sakthivel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: In this paper, the performance evaluation of Artificial Neural Network (ANN) based cascaded quasi Z-source DC-DC converter are presented for solar photovoltaic (PV) systems. The proposed configuration has the objective of improving the voltage stability and reducing the harmonic distortion under the condition of varying irradiance by integrating smart control strategy. The cascaded quasi-Z - source DC - DC Converter with ANN controller is designed with feed - forward architecture and trained with Levenberg - Marquardt algorithms and will be adjusting the duty ratio dynamically to maintain voltage regulation and will be achieving high quality output of power. The system's behaviour was simulated in the Matlab/Simulink software and compared with the conventional increment conductance (INC) maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithm. Performance indicators, such as peak voltage, settling time, ripple voltage and total harmonic distortion (THD) were tested with different irradiance levels. The superior transient response in the ANN controlled converter as compared to the INC based system was proved in a reduced settling time of ANN controlled converter 0.35 s and the improved quality of power supply in ANN controlled converter as the low ripples of 0.4 V and THD of 1.08 % both of which are good as compared to INC based system. These results support that the ANN based cascaded quasi-Z Source converter achieves improved dynamic adaptability, good harmonic suppression, and solid voltage stability thus making it a very suitable architecture for grid integrated PV applications.

title: Synchronous Switching Prediction Method for VSC-Based Power System Simulation
authors: Yinsheng Su, Jiajun Luo, Qian Ma, Ziwei Zhao, Haicheng Yao, Yifu Liaozou, Shujun Yao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: With the increasing proportion of power electronic devices in modern power systems, the high-frequency switching actions of converters have a significant impact on system operation and simulation accuracy. In electromagnetic transient (EMT) simulations, synchronous switching actions often cause voltage and current discontinuities, leading to numerical oscillations, peak disturbances, and cumulative errors that affect simulation reliability. Traditional synchronous switch processing methods rely on iterative calculations of the entire system to determine the stable switching combination, resulting in high computational costs and low convergence speed. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an improved synchronous switch processing method based on the general equivalent circuit of the converter. By establishing the equivalent topology model of the converter's bridge arm, the proposed method can quickly and accurately determine the switch conduction state under various operating conditions without requiring global iteration. The algorithm covers both normal and fault or lockout conditions, achieving adaptive synchronous switching prediction with high computational efficiency. Simulation results based on a T-type three-level converter and a photovoltaic system verify that the proposed method maintains high accuracy compared with PSCAD, while significantly reducing the number of switching state calculations, thus improving simulation efficiency and stability. The method provides an effective approach for high-fidelity and real-time EMT simulation of power electronic systems.

title: RDL-First FOWLP Development for III-V Semiconductor Chips in RF Applications
authors: Soon Wee Ho, Soh Siew Boon, Hsiao Hsiang-Yao, Eva Wai Leong Ching, Xu Feng, Lim Teck Guan, Vempati Srinivasa Rao
abstract: The increasing demand for high radio frequency (RF) output power in advanced communication, radar, and high-resolution sensing applications drives the necessity for integrating III-V semiconductors within RF front-end modules. This integration aims for optimal performance through heterogeneous configurations of III-V and silicon technologies packaged compactly and cost-effectively. Gallium Nitride (GaN) and Gallium Arsenide (GaAs) devices are crucial for achieving the stringent efficiency and output power standards required in these applications. Fan-Out Wafer-Level Packaging (FOWLP) has emerged as a promising solution to meet the demands of high-speed, high-density RF front-end solutions. However, existing FOWLP methodologies, particularly mold-first approaches, face significant challenges regarding the integration of fragile RF structures due to thermal management and interconnect reliability issues. This paper proposes a RDLfirst approach that enhances robustness by mitigating risks associated with embedding delicate air-bridge structures while maintaining efficient chip-to-chip and chip-to-RDL interconnects. It presents detailed methodologies for both the mold-first and RDL-first FOWLP structures, including process flows and results from implementing the RDL-first technique.

title: Stress Classification using Deep Learning and AI-Driven Music Therapy
authors: E Bharat Babu, B Janardhana Rao, S. Manoj, S. Pranathi, S Arun Sai, R Prashanth
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Stress is a common issue that affects decisionmaking, productivity, and overall health. Conventional stress assessment methods often rely on lengthy surveys or specialized medical devices, which may not be practical for everyday use. In this work, we propose a real-time stress detection system based on facial emotion recognition using a live camera feed. The system classifies stress levels as high, moderate, or low and provides instant feedback to the user. Along with recognition, the system includes music-based interventions to reduce stress, combining detection with relief. The model is designed to be accessible, relying on regular webcams, freely available frameworks, and efficient algorithms so that it can be applied in everyday scenarios without specialized equipment. Possible applications include workplace monitoring, educational support, and personal wellness systems. To make it practical, the system is designed to work with easily available webcams, open-source frameworks, and efficient algorithms. This setup allows it to run smoothly in everyday situations. This approach aims to make stress monitoring less intrusive and more usable for nonclinical applications. The framework was trained and validated using established facial expression datasets such as FER2013 and Affect Net. Evaluation results indicate that the integrated system achieves an accuracy of over 82 % in identifying stress-related emotional states. in distinguishing stress-related emotional states. By merging recognition and intervention in one framework, the system aims to promote healthier coping habits in daily life.

title: Eyesight AI: An Intelligent Deep Neural Network-Based Framework Work for the Detection and Classification of Retinal Diseases Through Fundus Image Analysis
authors: Priyadharshini R, Paulin A, Umayal AR
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The main causes of blindness that have been identified are diabetic retinopathy, age-related macular degeneration, and glaucoma; thus, these three diseases are at the top of the list of prevalent leading causes of vision impairments all over the world. The early diagnosis of retinal diseases is a key factor in their efficient treatment plan. To this end, we introduce Eyesight AI, an integrated framework for the detection and classification of multiple retinal diseases using holistic assessment of fundus images identified with deep neural networks. The framework utilises advanced CNNs to hypothesise the presence of one or more diseases after the model extracts features, without sacrificing reliability and accuracy in healthcare decision-making. The fundus images undergo preprocessing to enhance contrast and eliminate incidental noise before having the model assess associated pathological features and output the classified disease. Furthermore, this framework has been established and developed with a Django-based web interface to support deployment and end-user utilisation with optometrists and medical professionals. The experimental results on standardised retinal imaging databases demonstrate robustness and accuracy, to prepare this framework for future working health clinical applications, such as aiding early screening and diagnosis of retinal diseases.

title: Automated Diagnosis System for Brain Tumor Detection using Feature Refinement Technique
authors: Sunkanaboina Chandra Lingamaiah, Priyanka Kokil
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Brain tumors are uncontrolled growth of cells within brain tissues and causing symptoms such as ongoing headaches, seizures, memory impairment, and behavioral disturbances. These conditions pose significant health challenges and affect the lives of individuals. This paper presents a computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) framework to address these challenges and improve diagnostic efficiency. The system is built upon lightweight, convolutional neural network (CNN) to collect cues from MRI images. To enhance the quality of these cues, a channel attention model is incorporated in baseline model to emphasize more relevant information within the feature maps. These enhanced cues are then forwarded through a fully connected layer followed by a softmax layer for classification. Experimental evaluations indicates that the proposed CAD system yields better performance than traditional and current CNN techniques.

title: Multi-Agent Graph-Attention and Adaptive-Reward Driven Reinforcement Learning for Satellite Formation Control
authors: Jinkun Chen, Jiake Li, Yumei Wang, Yu Liu, Konglin Zhu
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: In recent years, with the rapid growth of satellite formation flight applications, there is an increasing demand for higher control accuracy and autonomy. To address this, we propose multi-agent graph-attention and adaptive-reward (MAGAR-SAC), a novel multi-agent reinforcement learning method that builds upon Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) and integrates graph-attention communication and adaptive reward weighting for satellite formation control. MAGAR-SAC utilizes a Graph Attention Network (GAT) to enable dynamic inter-satellite communication, adopts a hierarchical decision strategy to decompose global and local tasks, and adaptively balances multiple objectives through reward scaling. Experimental results demonstrate that compared with MASAC and MADDPG, MAGAR-SAC achieves superior formation accuracy, a higher training success rate, and faster convergence, thereby highlighting its great potential for autonomous satellite formation missions.

title: The KNN-Based Intrusion Detection Method via VMD and Autoencoder Feature Fusion
authors: Jing Lu, Yizhun Tian, Ruchun Jia, Hechao Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: With the increasing sophistication of network attack techniques, how to enhance feature selection and feature representation capabilities has become a critical research issue in the field of intrusion detection. This paper proposes a KNN-based intrusion detection method that integrates Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and Autoencoder (AE) for feature fusion. First, VMD is used to decompose traffic data into multiple Intrinsic Mode Functions (IMFs), and the data is reconstructed using IMF1 and IMF2. A Balanced Random Forest is then applied to evaluate and select the most discriminative feature subset based on the reconstructed data. Subsequently, an auto encoder is trained using normal traffic data reconstructed from IMF2 and IMF3 to capture latent representations of normal behavior patterns. These learned representations are fused with the previously selected feature subset to enhance feature representation capabilities. Finally, a KNN classifier is constructed based on the fused features to identify various types of attacks. Experimental results on the CICIDS2017 dataset demonstrate that the proposed method improves overall Recall and macro-average F1-score by approximately 5 % compared to directly using a Balanced Random Forest for feature extraction combined with a KNN classifier, validating the effectiveness of the proposed approach in enhancing intrusion detection performance.

title: Spatio-Temporal Prediction and Benefit-Driven Hierarchical Closed-Loop Energy-Saving Method for 5G Base Stations
authors: Haosong Gou, Na Luo, Gaoyi Zhang, Yuan Yuan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: To address the challenges posed by the continuously increasing energy consumption of 5G base stations and growing network traffic, as well as the difficulty of quantifying energy-saving benefits, this paper proposes a spatio-temporal prediction method and a profit-driven, hierarchical, closed-loop energy-saving approach for 5G base stations. A spatio-temporal graph model is constructed by integrating historical data and topological features. A hybrid prediction framework combining a Graph Convolutional Network and a Temporal Convolutional Network is introduced to achieve multi-step load forecasting and uncertainty quantification. Furthermore, an innovative spatio-temporal energy efficiency coefficient is proposed which combines temporal deviations and spatial fluctuations to inform hierarchical closed-loop energy-saving decisions, enabling intelligent, unified control of prediction, evaluation and execution. Real-world experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method improves load prediction accuracy from 84% to 92%, reduces the average drop rate from 0.12% to 0.05% and shortens the average site recovery time from 213.27 seconds to 127.01 seconds.

title: Adaptive Intelligence-Driven Virtual Synchronous Generator Control for Enhanced Transient Stability in Multi-Terminal HVDC Systems
authors: Qihang Sun, Xiangyu Li, Jiaqi Yao, Yuxing Dai, Kamal Al- Haddad
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: The proliferation of inverter-based renewable energy resources fundamentally challenges power system stability due to diminished inertia and weakened grid-forming capabilities. This paper proposes an adaptive virtual synchronous generator control framework integrating particle swarm optimization for real-time parameter adaptation and transient stability enhancement mechanisms specifically designed for multi-terminal HVDC systems with offshore wind integration. The methodology synthesizes electromechanical dynamics modeling with multi-objective cost function minimization, achieving autonomous adjustment of virtual inertia and damping coefficients responsive to grid strength variations. Experimental validation on a 15 kW hardware-in-the-loop testbed demonstrates 64% reduction in performance cost metrics, 51% decrease in maximum angular excursion during faults, and tripled stability margins compared to conventional fixed-parameter approaches, validating the framework's efficacy for robust grid-forming operation under diverse operational scenarios.

title: Building an AI Platform Using Databricks Agentic AI for Tourist Behaviour Analysis and Forecasting in Indonesian Government Applications
authors: Davy Ronald Hermanus, Danny Robert Hermanus
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This research proposes an AI platform leveraging Databricks Agentic AI for tourist behaviour analysis and forecasting in Indonesian government applications. By integrating multimodal data sources such as WiFi signals, cameras for video recognition (from CCTV and drones), airport inflows, and BPS statistics, the platform enables real-time perception, multi-faceted reasoning, and autonomous decisionmaking to support sustainable tourism. Drawing from Databricks Mosaic AI Agent Framework and Agent Bricks, it conceptualizes efficient processing of diverse data for predictive modelling, drawing insights from global case studies to align with SDGs like SDG 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth) and SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities). In a simulated case study using synthetic data on tourist arrivals, the platform achieved an RMSE of 950 and MAE of 800 using LSTM, outperforming ARIMA's RMSE of 1200 and MAE of 1000, demonstrating up to 20 % improvement in forecasting accuracy. The methodology integrates data into the Databricks Lakehouse for governance and scalability, employs models like ARIMA for baseline time-series analysis and LSTM for deep learning-based forecasting, and includes performance comparisons on synthetic datasets to validate efficacy. The conceptual framework enhances policy formulation, resource optimization, and economic resilience in Indonesia's tourism sector, addressing gaps in traditional methods and promoting data intelligence for equitable development.

title: Machine Learning Driven CSI Motion Detection for Smart Homes with Energy Efficiency and Safety
authors: Chinta Harichandan, Vijayakumar Krishnasamy, Praveenkumar Rajendiran, Garaga Prem Sri Balaji
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Monitoring human activity serves as a vital element in evaluating occupancy levels and security risks in indoor spaces, including smart homes. However, most traditional sensors and vision-based methods require a direct line of sight, involve high installation costs, or raise privacy concerns. To mitigate the issues, a device-free motion detection presented in this study, which leverages fine-grained multipath dynamics under channel state information (CSI) inherently available in the WiFi infrastructure. The RSSI-based technique monitors single-path signal strength changes, which is challenging in non-linear environments. Whereas the proposed method infers human presence and activity trends by analysing CSI variations in real time. To achieve reliable occupancy detection and motion classification, the proposed two-stage machine learning pipeline is used. The model was trained by CSI data from commodity IoT WiFi devices in multiple indoor scenarios. The proposed method highlights robust performance even when targets are hidden from view, highlighting the potential of the method for pervasive ambient intelligence.

title: Sensing as a Service in the Age of AI from Philosophy of Science Point of View
authors: Mindit Eriyadi, Muhammad Farhan Imanudin, Ahmad Ali Hakam Dani, Fetty Fitriyanti Lubis, Fadhil Hidayat, Suhono Harso Supangkat
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The Sensing as a Service (SenaaS) model, a cornerstone of the Internet of Things (IoT), is rapidly evolving with the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI), transforming it from a data-provisioning utility into an automated decision-making ecosystem. This paper presents a philosophical inquiry into this paradigm, using the recently proposed Unified and Sustainable Sensing as a Service (USSenaaS) framework as a central case study. We deconstruct the SenaaS architecture as a distributed cognitive system and analyze its objectives through the lens of epistemological theories such as process reliabilism. This analysis is then extended to address the profound challenges and transformations brought by AI. We explore the shift from simple analytics to opaque black box AI models, the critical need for Explainable AI (XAI) to ensure trust and accountability, and the tension between the confirmationist nature of machine learning and the classical scientific principle of falsifiability. The paper argues that the convergence of SenaaS and AI necessitates a new philosophical grounding a critical holistic pragmatism that moves beyond the technical performance metrics exemplified by the USSenaaS framework to encompass the epistemological, ethical, and societal implications of automated, data-driven intelligence.

title: Attention-Enhanced UNet for Segmenting Hepatic Tumors on Computed Tomography
authors: Jiaen Xu, Sicheng Wang, Guowei Zu, Chang Shu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: The present study proposes a novel approach to addressing the limitations of conventional U-Net models in accurately segmenting tumor edges in CT images and detecting small lesions with low precision. The proposed algorithm, GCTUNet, is a refinement of the U-Net network model and is designed to enhance the segmentation accuracy of liver tumor images. The algorithm introduces GCT channel attention blocks during feature fusion to enhance tumor features and suppress irrelevant information. The efficacy of the proposed algorithm was validated using the LITS dataset, where its Intersection over Union (IOU), precision, and recall rates exhibit significant enhancement in comparison to those of the conventional U-Net segmentation algorithm. The experimental results demonstrate that the liver tumor regions segmented by this algorithm exhibited a 1% increase in IOU and a 2% increase in recall rate compared to the original model. This enhancement effectively improves the segmentation accuracy of liver tumors.

title: Research on Intelligent Sensor Networking Method Based on 485 Bus
authors: Fuqiang Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: This article proposes an intelligent method for networking various safety monitoring system sensors in underground mines. This method mainly draws on the principles of sending, transmitting, and receiving Ethernet UDP message transmission protocol, making the networking of various sensors more intelligent and transparent. This method changes the star communication mode between the original substation and sensors, allowing sensors in different areas to communicate freely and control each other, greatly reducing the pressure on ground servers. After using this method to achieve data transmission and logical command control between sensors, the vertical topology structure of mine safety monitoring becomes flattened, and the corresponding level of safety monitoring is greatly improved.

title: Design of a Switchable Metasurface Absorber/Reflector with Polarization Independent Control Characteristic
authors: Yunfei Xia, Zhaowang Cao, Weikang Xie, Zishan Chen, Huiwen Yang, Jianjun Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: This paper presents a switchable metasurface absorber/reflector featuring polarization independent control for x- and y-polarization E M waves in the 2–6 GHz via spatially isolated FSS layers. By controlling the operating state of the PIN diodes, the proposed structure achieves broadband absorption through the envelope of multiple absorption states, while exhibiting high reflection across the entire band in the OFF state. The performance was analyzed through full-wave simulations and its operational stability under oblique incidence was analyzed. Besides, the physical mechanism of the polarization independent control is elucidated by electromagnetic field distribution analysis. The thickness is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.053 \lambda_{L}$</tex> (where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda_{L}$</tex> is the wavelength of the lowest operating frequency). This design offers significant potential for electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) applications in communication systems such as Wi-Fi, contributing to enhancing signal integrity and communication quality.

title: Flexural Properties of Sn-Based Solder Alloy and Reliability of its Joint
authors: Tan Tze Qing, Sarangapani Murali, Agala Joel, P.A. Mani Siva, Olakkankal Edrina Risson, Chong Mei Hoe, Kang Sungsig
abstract: This paper aims to present the flexural strength and modulus properties and reliability results of solder alloys SAC305, low temperature solder (LTS - SnBiAg), SAC alloyed with Ni, Sb, and Bi commercially known as Innolot®. The reliability performance of a solder joint typically relies on the mechanical, thermal and microstructural stability of the solder material. It is well known that Sn-based solder alloys with addition of Bismuth (Bi), Nickel (Ni), Antimony (Sb) elements can enhance the mechanical and thermal performance. However, this type of commercialized paste is generally using T4 and T5 powders. This paper reports the experimental reliability data of these solder alloys focusing on automotive advanced packaging applications, such as ADAS modules for autonomous driving, which requires both ultra-fine pitch soldering and high reliability that uses solder paste with T6 powder. For this evaluation, the flexural strength measurement is established with SAC305 sample as reference and adopting 3point bend fixture. The dimension of test specimens is prepared as per ASTM D790-17 standard. The conducted reliability tests include thermal cycling test (TCT) and unbiased highly accelerated stress test (uHAST) as per JESD22-A104-B and AEC-Q200 test standards. The test boards are subjected to thermal cycling between the temperatures <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-55^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+150^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and temperature of 130° C and relative humidity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{R H})$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5 \%}$</tex> for 192 hours for uHAST test, respectively. The reported flexural strength and modulus measured at room temperature and elevated temperature clearly showed that Innolot® has highest flexural strength, expected to withstand mechanical stress, reliable solder joints. The reliability results revealed Innolot® performed 10% better than SAC305 in terms of component shear strength soldered to ENIG surface finish. Indicating, Innolot® competes SAC305 in extreme harsh conditions, potential to be used in automotive.

title: Deep Learning Ensemble Approach for Eye Diseases Detection
authors: Richa Gupta, Uma Shanker Singh Ujjwal, Vikas Tripathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Preventing visual damage and blindness requires early and accurate identification of retinal disorders. Nevertheless, the majority of deep learningbased diagnostic techniques currently in use rely on a single network architecture, which, while useful, frequently has poor generalisation, overfits on small datasets, and inconsistent performance across a range of data distributions. In order to get over these restrictions, this research presents a novel ensemble deep learning architecture for robust and accurate retinal illness categorisation that combines the complementing strengths of ResNet-50, ResNet-152, MobileNet, and EfficientNet. Within the suggested framework, MobileNet guarantees lightweight and computationally efficient feature extraction, EfficientNet offers balanced scaling across depth, width, and resolution for optimal performance, and ResNet models efficiently capture deep hierarchical representations through residual learning. The model is better able to capture both fine-grained and global retinal characteristics because these networks are combined using a weighted ensemble technique. Comprehensive tests on the ODIR-5K dataset show that the suggested ensemble approach routinely achieves better accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity than individual baseline structures and a number of current state-of-theart techniques. The results validate that the robustness and generalisability of retinal disease classification systems are much improved by multi-architecture ensemble learning, which makes the suggested framework an excellent contender for scaled clinical implementation in actual healthcare environments.

title: Characterization of the AMPLE Channel Model for IEEE 802.11ah-Based IoT
authors: Luquan Xiao, Jihong Liu, Lingyou Zhou, Qinghe Hu, Zhong Hua, Ran Zhang, Wei Gu, Jiliang Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: This study provides a systematic performance characterization of the adaptive multiple path loss exponent (AMPLE) radio propagation model, that is firstly tailored for a campus Internet of Things (IoT) scenario. In this paper, extensive real-world measurements are performed based on IEEE 802.11ah IoT devices. The entire area covered by the test range is classified into several different area types by categorizing the raster maps, and each area type corresponds to a specific path loss exponent, which is calculated by the type of area crossed by a straight line between the transmitter and receiver and the weighted distance. The results show that the AMPLE model predicts the path loss of IEEE 802.11ah devices with a mean absolute error of 7.10 dB. This performance is notably more accurate than the 3GPP-UMa Model, which exhibits a mean absolute error of 11.86 dB under the same conditions.

title: Function-level Optimization Automatic Tuner for Numerical Programs
authors: Xinni Liu, Xinpeng Li, Guangping Yu, Hengbiao Yu, Xin Yi, Chun Huang
abstract: Numerical programs are widely used in high-performance computing, graphics, finance, deep learning, and other fields. The use of high-precision floating-point numbers can ensure the accuracy and robustness of the program and reduce the accumulation of errors. However, this approach also increases the program’s execution time, memory usage, and energy consumption. Therefore, the reasonable use of mixed precision is beneficial to balance the accuracy and performance of program results. To look for efficient mixed-precision configurations, we propose an LLVM toolchain, FuncTuner, that obtains the program transformation range by identifying the #pragma directive in the front end of Clang, uses a recursive search algorithm to access the configuration information of functions in the range, and uses LLVM Pass to modify the LLVM IR of the program according to the given configuration, pioneering the use of function-level mixed-precision optimization. Our approach achieves significant results in the HPL-AI program, with a maximum floating-point performance of 24.78 GFLOPs at fp16 precision and a performance improvement of 304.73 % at the max scale.

title: Intelligent Navigation for UGV/UAV: Semi-supervised Mapping and Risk-aware Planning
authors: O. K. Ruchkin
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Currently, the development of intelligent navigation systems for unmanned ground and aerial vehicles requires the integration of advanced machine learning methods with control and decision-making frameworks. The problem is to ensure reliable recognition of terrain and surface conditions under limited labeled data, in order to adapt vehicle motion parameters to dynamic environments. Within the framework of intelligent control and motion planning, the proposed approach combines contrastive representation learning with graph-based semi-supervised diffusion. Embedding models trained on multi-modal data (vision and IMU) provide discriminative features of terrain surfaces, while a k-nearest neighbor graph with Poisson-Seidel label propagation enables the expansion of sparse terrain labels to large-scale environments. This ensures robust classification of surface types such as asphalt, gravel, mud, and ice, and allows estimation of traction-related risk factors ("slippery / rough / normal"). The predicted terrain properties are integrated into the automatic control system through adaptive speed limits, dynamic path cost functions, and real-time planning constraints. The system provides stable unmanned ground and aerial vehicles motion both in nominal and transition conditions, improves the accuracy of terrain-aware planning, and increases the safety of autonomous navigation.

title: LLM-Based Semantic Modeling and Cooperative Evolutionary Fuzzing for Traffic Violation Scenario Generation
authors: Yangyang Liu, Xinyu Li, Yan Xiao, Miao Zhang, Pengcheng Zhang
abstract: Ensuring the safety of autonomous driving systems (ADS) in a cost-effective and efficient manner remains a critical challenge. Existing law-guided scenario generation approaches are typically limited to a narrow subset of legal rules, resulting in insufficient scenario diversity, and search-based methods often struggle with large and sparse search spaces. To address these limitations, we propose SLaFE (Semantic Law Modeling and Fuzzing based on Cooperative Evolution), a novel traffic violation scenario generation framework designed to systematically evaluate the safety of ADS. SLaFE harnesses the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) to convert traffic laws into structured scenario constraints. These scenarios are then optimized via a cooperative evolutionary fuzzing algorithm that explores the parameter space to identify boundary cases likely to trigger abnormal ADS behaviors. We evaluate SLaFE on the Apollo platform within the LGSVL simulator using ten realworld traffic regulations. Experimental results show that SLaFE successfully triggered all 10 types of traffic law violations (10/10), outperforming the best existing method, VioHawk (9/10), while others detected no more than 3. Moreover, SLaFE achieved an average triggering time of 5.1 minutes per law type, significantly faster than VioHawk (9.0 minutes) and other baselines. These results highlight SLaFE’s effectiveness in discovering diverse and critical law-violating scenarios for ADS testing.

title: Research on Electric Vehicle Charging Pile Planning Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wei Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: This study proposes an optimal planning framework for electric vehicle charging piles that integrates Deep Q-Network (DQN) and Spatiotemporal Graph Neural Network (ST-GNN). Specifically, ST-GNN is used to extract the spatiotemporal correlation features of charging demand for prediction; an adaptive decision-making model is constructed; a multi-objective reward function is designed; and the experience replay and target network update mechanisms are combined to ensure training stability. The effectiveness of the model's learning process is verified through the Q-value convergence curve. The results show that the proposed framework can realize the intelligent optimization of charging pile layout and power scheduling in dynamic scenarios. On the test set, the charging demand satisfaction rate reaches 88.1%, and the power grid load fluctuation is controlled within 15%.

title: A Literature Review of AI-Driven Cloud Computing
authors: Kelvin Chandra Winata, Junius Djodi Eka Putra, Reina Setiawan, Reinert Yosua Rumagit
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: In the ever-increasing development of cloud computing technology, many businesses are starting to use cloud computing to improve performance optimization and cost efficiency. The increasing use of cloud computing in business also increases the complexity and workload in resource management, by using artificial intelligence the above problems can be overcome with machine learning models that help in improving performance efficiency, lightening the workload, and also reducing cost requirements. The integration of artificial intelligence with cloud computing has redefined the concept of digital infrastructure by enabling refined automation, predictive analytics, and optimized resource management to improve efficiency and scalability.

title: Cross-Domain Collaborative Slicing Optimization for Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks
authors: Xinyi Yue, Hui Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Cross-domain network slicing in the space-air-ground integrated networks (SAGINs) enables the virtualization and logical isolation of resources across heterogeneous network infrastructures. This paradigm provides end-to-end quality of service guarantees for a massive and diverse range of services. However, conventional centralized control and management approaches suffer from inherent drawbacks, such as low resource utilization efficiency and high response latency, particularly in the highly dynamic and multi-domain environment of SAGINs. To achieve efficient orchestration of heterogeneous resources, this paper adopts a distributed management and control strategy and proposes a cross-domain collaborative slice planning framework based on a multi-agent deep deterministic policy gradient. The framework proposes targeted modeling constraints based on the unique characteristics of SAGINs and designs independent decision-making centers to coordinate control resources by slice type, effectively optimizing resource allocation logic. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme, compared to benchmarks, demonstrates significant advantages in key performance indicators such as slice acceptance rate, long-term average revenue, and overall resource utilization.

title: Deep Learning Assisted Hybrid Beamforming for THz Hybrid Near and Far Field Communications
authors: Zijun Lin, Xufang Wang, Deyuan Zeng, Yi Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: For reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted terahertz systems, a hybrid beamforming scheme under a mixedfield environment is investigated. A deep learning-based framework that integrates channel estimation and beamforming is proposed to maximize the weighted sum-rate. To obtain independent channel state information (CSI), a positioning-based approach is employed, and a mixed-field channel estimation model is trained using deep learning techniques. Inspired by the gradient descent (GD) method, a system model capable of iteratively updating the target matrix is designed, which optimizes the beamforming based on the estimated CSI to maximize the weighted sum-rate. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms traditional schemes in weighted sum-rate and approaches the performance under perfect CSI conditions.

title: Electrical Modeling and Analysis of Hybrid Bonding Vias with Misalignment and Void Defects
authors: Vignesh Shanmugam Bhaskar, Mani Raju, Mihai Dragos Rotaru
abstract: This paper discusses about the electrical modelling and analysis of hybrid bonding vias with misalignment and void defects. Structures involving hybrid bonding vias such as daisy chain sections are used to study the hybrid bonding defects. Daisy chain unit cells are considered as a basic element in characterizing daisy chain sections. A simple polynomial fitting method is used to model the variation of the DC resistance with the misalignment or void defects. The effect of resistance variation in a single hybrid bonding via when scaled to few thousand vias in a daisy chain is analyzed. In addition to the resistance variations of the hybrid bonding due to misalignments, the high-frequency performance of hybrid bonding vias with defects will also be analyzed using scattering parameters.

title: A Self-Supervised MADAR-Net for GearBox Fault Diagnosis under Strong Noise and Limited Labeled data
authors: Jinfei Fan, Tao He, Ziyang Gong
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering, Materials and Automation Technology (MMEAT)
abstract: Vibration signals collected by sensors from gearbox are usually interfered by strong background noise. It is difficult to label the large amount of unlabeled data collected due to the extremely high human cost and expert knowledge required. To solve the problem of gearbox fault diagnosis under strong noise and few-labeled data, this paper proposes a self-supervised learning framework (MADAR-Net) that combines a mean anti-noise module and a dynamic attention module. The framework uses pre-training to learn potential features from unlabeled data. Noise is recognized and suppressed by adding a mean-anti-noise module to the ResNet. A dynamic attention module is used to make the model more attentive to the important information in the features. Fine-tune the model obtained in the pre-training phase with a small amount of labeled data. Experiments demonstrate that the model proposed in this paper achieves competitive results (99.72%) in the fault diagnosis task.

title: Deep Q-Networks for Optimization of Backlogged Inventory Systems with Space Limit and Stochastic Demand
authors: Le My Duyen, Pham Duc Tai, Jirachai Buddhakulsomsiri
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: This paper addresses an inventory problem for slow-moving items under capacity constraint, backlog, deterministic lead time, and stochastic demand. The total inventory cost includes ordering, holding, backlog, and over-storage costs. To deal with this problem, a Deep Q-Network reinforcement learning model is developed. The model learns a data-driven replenishment strategy to minimize total inventory costs over time based on observed system states. To illustrate the benefit of using such a model, it is evaluated against a traditional (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$s, S$</tex>) inventory policy. The results demonstrate that our Deep Q-Network model is on bar with the traditional counterpart under a simple demand pattern. This implies a possibility of our model outperforming the conventional inventory policies under more complex demand patterns thanks to the model's learning capability.

title: Content-Adaptive Video Coding for Efficient Adaptive Streaming
authors: Mohammad Ghasempour
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Video streaming now accounts for the majority of global internet traffic, making efficiency in both compression and energy consumption a critical challenge. Despite advancements in video coding and adaptive streaming, most methods remain content-agnostic and fail to fully exploit the unique characteristics of video content. This doctoral study focuses on encoder-side optimizations to develop content-adaptive methods that improve both energy and compression efficiency. This research integrates bitrate ladder optimization with representation-level enhancements to enable more energy-efficient streaming while maintaining high video quality.

title: Infrared/Visible Image Fusion for Underground Spaces Based on Deep Learning and Image Enhancement
authors: Fei Geng, Shengfei Ye, Yan Xu, Zhixiang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of visible light images in underground spaces such as cable tunnels and coal mine roadways-including low brightness, uneven illumination, monotonous colors, and susceptibility to dust and water vapor interference-as well as infrared images being prone to false detection due to machine radiation interference, a single type of image cannot meet the requirements of personnel detection. This paper proposes an infrared/visible image fusion algorithm based on deep learning and image enhancement. A fusion network consisting of an encoder, a fusion module, and a decoder is constructed. The encoder adopts a dual-branch structure to realize multi-scale feature extraction, avoiding information loss and artificial noise introduction caused by image decomposition. The decoder draws on the bidirectional weighted feature pyramid network and uses multi-scale features to reconstruct the fused image, improving the detail restoration effect. The Retinex algorithm improved by guided filtering is used for image enhancement to solve problems such as overenhancement and halo artifacts. A loss function including edge strength loss, pixel loss, and structural similarity loss (excluding brightness comparison) is designed to balance the information retention between the fused image and the source images and improve the perceptual similarity. Experiments show that the proposed method outperforms other algorithms in various indicators and can effectively improve the accuracy and robustness of personnel detection in underground spaces.

title: Fuzzy Logic-Based Smart Irrigation Using Internet of Things in Precision Agriculture
authors: Steffany Harwella, Suhono Harso Supangkat, Devi Willeam Anggara
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Smart farming technologies are increasingly adopted to enhance productivity and resource efficiency in rural agriculture. This study presents a fuzzy logic-based smart irrigation model to improve water and nutrient use in Wates Village, Blitar-an officially designated smart village in Indonesia. The research employed five main stages: IoT system design configuration, environmental data collection, fuzzy logic-based control system design, fuzzy-IoT integration, and simulation-based evaluation. The fuzzy controller used soil moisture, temperature, and estimated humidity as input parameters. Due to limited access to high-resolution humidity data, monthly rainfall statistics and rain event records from Blitar Regency were used to generate synthetic daily humidity profiles. While this introduced some estimation uncertainty, it enabled the system to simulate adaptive responses. Simulation results showed that the fuzzy system reduced total water use by 46.67 %, saving 3160 liters over 100 days, and required significantly fewer irrigation activations than a fixed-schedule IoT system. Local farmers emphasized the importance of semi-automated control, offline functionality, and intuitive interfaces for real-world adoption. The model demonstrates the feasibility of applying fuzzy logic and IoT for context-aware, scalable, and cost-effective solutions in precision agriculture, particularly in rural, low-infrastructure environments.

title: Anomaly Detection Virtual Simulation System for Meter Installation and Connection: A GAN-Based Approach
authors: Wenming Wang, Genkuan Lian, Wei Tan, Jing You, Wanying Kang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: The meter installation and connection process is an essential element in power distribution systems, where anomalies such as wiring errors or tampering can lead to significant safety hazards, economic losses, and operational inefficiencies. Traditional anomaly detection methods often rely on rule-based systems or supervised learning, which require extensive labeled data and struggle with novel anomaly patterns. This paper proposes a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)-based virtual simulation system for anomaly detection in meter installation and connection processes. The system leverages DCGAN architecture to generate realistic anomalous scenarios, integrating image and time-series data from electricity meters to simulate virtual environments for training and testing. By training on the Electricity Meter Data dataset, the model achieves highfidelity generation and effective detection, with an accuracy of 91.8%, outperforming benchmarks like VAE and baseline methods by up to 17%. Experimental results demonstrate the system's robustness, with FID scores decreasing to 9.1 after 200 epochs, indicating close distribution matching between real and generated data. The virtual simulation interface allows interactive training for power maintenance personnel, enhancing detection capabilities in GPS-denied or resource-limited settings. This approach not only addresses data scarcity but also provides a scalable framework for smart grid applications, contributing to improved reliability and security in power systems. Future extensions could incorporate real-time data streams for dynamic anomaly prediction.

title: An Ensemble Learning Approach with Moth-Flame Optimization for Early Detection of Respiratory Diseases in Campus Students
authors: Siyu Chen, Yuhao Cai, Weijia Liu, Shifeng Wu, Deyan Huang, Jitang Zhang, Jun Yuan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Respiratory diseases (RD) pose a serious health risk to school students, especially in high-density environments. Traditional diagnostic methods often rely on symptoms, leading to delays in intervention, and while machine learning (ML) has shown promise in disease prediction, challenges remain in achieving high accuracy and specificity for school-age populations. This study proposes an ensemble learning approach combined with the Moth-Flame Optimization (MFO) algorithm to predict RD and No_RD in students, using a dataset with demographic and clinical features. The results demonstrate that a three-model ensemble (Random Forest, Gaussian Naive Bayes, and Backpropagation Neural Network) optimized by MFO achieves an accuracy of 91.67%, recall of 81.19%, and an AUC of 0.8681. These findings highlight the potential of optimized ML models for early detection of RD, providing valuable support for school health management and public health interventions.The related source code and dataset have been published and can be found at Github

title: Augmented Reality-Assisted Digital Twin for Fault Detection in Electric Vehicle Wiring Systems
authors: Rajalakshmi M., Sheik Mastan S A R, Karthik C., Bibhuti Bhusan Dash, Utpal Chandra De, Sudhansu Shekhar Patra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Due to complex wiring harnesses, electric vehicles (EVs) might suffer from performance degradation, increased maintenance costs, and safety risks with faulty cables. On the other hand, traditional methods of fault detection take much time to think about and cost a lot, and usually require an expert to solve them. In this work, we propose a new system- a real-time fault detection system for EV wiring harnesses-where an Augmented Reality (AR) system is integrated with AR visualization, Unity Digital Twin simulation, ESP32-based embedded programming, and Firebase for real-time synchronization. In short, faulty wires turn red on the AR gazebo while working wires remain green, which should thus be easier to diagnose and repair. It will enhance maintenance efficiency, reduce downtime, and lower costs by enabling the identification of faults without physical disassembly. An experimental evaluation found that it was better at finding faults and faster at fixing them than traditional methods. Time Domain Reflectometry (TDR) will be added to the system to help find faults more accurately, and AI predictive maintenance will be added to make the system smarter.

title: Multi-Objective Optimal Scheduling of Integrated Energy Systems Based on Dynamic Demand Response Mechanism with Wind and Solar Prediction
authors: Weixi Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Aiming at addressing the problems of the integrated energy system, including the uncertainty of wind and solar power output, insufficient flexibility in multi-energy coupled load regulation, and the difficulty in balancing economy and environmental protection, this paper proposes an integrated “prediction-response-optimization” scheduling approach. An VMD-BWO-KELM model is established to achieve high-precision wind and solar prediction; based on the results of wind and solar prediction, a dynamic demand response model is designed to mitigate fluctuations; and an improved Multi-Objective Particle Swarm Optimization is used to solve the multi-objective optimization model. Case study results show that the cost is reduced by 5.6%, carbon emissions are decreased by 5.5%, and the wind and solar power accommodation rate reaches 99.74%, which verifies its effectiveness.

title: Optimization Allocation Method of Power Supply Load in Distribution Network Based on Improved Adaptive Genetic Algorithm
authors: Cheng Wang, Lei Chen, Hui Huang, Ru Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: In response to the problems of low efficiency and increased losses caused by uneven load distribution in the distribution network, this study constructs a distribution network load optimization allocation model with the goal of maximizing power supply capacity and load balancing. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}-1$</tex> safety verification constraints and operating capacity limitations are introduced, and the main transformer load rate balancing strategy is used to improve the economic and safety of the power grid. Given the problem of traditional genetic algorithms easily converging to local optima during the solving process, this study introduces an improved adaptive genetic algorithm aimed at significantly improving the global exploration performance of the algorithm by dynamically adjusting the parameters of crossover and mutation operations. Experiments based on the IEEE 33 node system show that the proposed method achieves a reduction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$17.8 \%-18.3 \%$</tex> in distribution line loss, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$39.8 \%-40.3 \%$</tex> in node voltage deviation, only 45% in isolated nodes when the N-K fault level is 9, and the costbenefit ratio remains stable at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 1 8}-\mathbf{1. 3 2}$</tex>. The convergence time is 20, and the iteration error is 0.5, which is still below 0.5 at 150. The performance degradation in extreme scenarios during robustness testing is only 6.6%.

title: Japanese Language Learning Application Development Using SwiftUI on iPhone Devices
authors: Filbert Chai, Zener Sukra Lie, Hendy Reginald Cuaca Dharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Many apps available are not useful for professional use, especially in reading, listening, and speaking features. This research proposes the development of an iOS app with SwiftUI based on interactive storytelling, quizzes, and AI-technology speech feedback for learning Japanese. The app has gamification and Spaced Repetition System to facilitate motivation and word memory. Results show positive user satisfaction and accuracy, justifying the app as a viable learning instrument for business Japanese communication. The vocabulary used in this app was obtained from JLPT N3 and N4, thus users need basic knowledge of the Japanese language to learn here. This app has undergo user testing phase and a questionnaire have been shared to get feedback from users. From a total of 30 respondents aged <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21-25$</tex>, around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80 \%-90 \%$</tex> of the respondents found this app to be useful and effective while the system usability scale (SUS) average score for this app reaches 84.83 indicating excellent usability

title: Compressing Cell Execution Logs Embedded within Jupyter Notebook Files
authors: Tomoki Nakamaru
abstract: Jupyter notebooks inevitably become messy through exploratory programming. NBGather is a Jupyter extension that helps programmers manage such messes by logging executed cells and slicing out programs to reproduce outputs from the log. However, its space efficiency is suboptimal because it naively records executed cells in the JSON format. This paper presents a straightforward but effective method for compressing cell execution logs in Jupyter notebooks. In a preliminary experiment using an open dataset, our method achieved a median size reduction of 91% compared to the original $\log$ size and 40% compared to the size achieved by naively applying gzip compression.

title: Enhancing Cybersecurity through Artificial Intelligence: Foundations, Challenges, and Perspectives
authors: Boutaghane Rafika, Mazouzi Smaine
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Theoretical and Applicative Aspects of Computer Science (ICTAACS)
abstract: Cybersecurity has become a central concern in the digital age, particularly due to the growing complexity and volume of cyber threats. Artificial Intelligence (AI) offers new approaches for automating the detection, prevention, and response to these threats. This paper presents a theoretical and practical overview of the foundations of cybersecurity, then explores the integration of AI techniques in intrusion detection systems (IDS). Through an analysis of IDS evolution and AI models such as neural networks and deep learning, we highlight the benefits and limitations of current approaches while underlining the need for interpretability, robustness, and adaptability in future solutions.

title: An Actual Measurement-Based Intelligent Transfer Framework Towards Cross-Altitude KPI Prediction for UAV Communications
authors: Yutong Sun, Jialin Wang, Zhigang Zhong, Zuoyong Nan, Honghui Xu, Wenbin Li, Rui Ban, Sen Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The growing unmanned aerial vehicle economy requires reliable communication, where accurate prediction of key performance indicators (KPIs) is crucial for network optimization. However, high-altitude measurement data remains scarce. We propose a Measurement-based Intelligent Transfer (MIT) framework for cross-altitude KPI prediction, featuring a novel Transfer Learning-enhanced Random Forest (TLRF) algorithm based on TrAdaBoost.R2. Our innovation includes the first dense urban 5G NR aerial measurement campaign, collecting over 24,000 RSRP and SINR samples at 110 m, 170 m, and 200 m altitudes. This unique dataset addresses the critical scarcity of high-altitude interference data. TLRF uniquely adapts knowledge from abundant low-altitude measurements to predict high-altitude KPIs, effectively mitigating domain shift with minimal target data. The TLRF method enables accurate high-altitude prediction. Trained on data from 110 m, it achieves Mean Absolute Error (MAE) reductions of approximately 3 dBm for RSRP and 1.5 dB for SINR at prediction altitudes of 170 m and 200 m, even with few target domain samples.

title: CBSM-YOLO: A Lightweight Automatic Recognition Algorithm for Commodity Outer Packaging Based on the Improvement of YOLOv8
authors: Zikai Lin, Bingxue Tian, Peixian Liang, Chunming Mai, Haoran Deng, Xiuquan Yue
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: With the upgrading of various automatic retail scenarios and the rapid development of intelligent supermarkets, the recognition of product outer packaging is facing challenges such as complex background interference and lightweight deployment. To address the issues of accuracy, real-time performance and lightweight deployment in commodity detection, this paper improves it based on the YOLOv8 framework. The algorithm is as follows: First, introduce the CMUNeXt module and integrate it with the C2f at the Backbone position. By combining its lightweight multi-branch structure and feature verification mechanism, it retains more key product information, including packaging shape, edge texture, etc.Subsequently, the Bidirectional Feature Pyramid (BiFPN) is introduced at the feature concatenation point to enhance the efficient fusion of multi-scale features. By dynamically dividing the fusion weights, more commodity information is retained, invalid paths are deleted, and the computational cost of Concat is reduced. Then, the SCConv module is constructed at the Neck position to refine the local features on the dense commodity shelves and integrate various features, improve the fusion adaptability of the incoming features, and at the same time reduce the computational load and parameter scale of the model, achieving efficient detection of the outer packaging of goods on the dense shelves. Finally, the model parameters are optimized based on the MPD-IoU loss function of the minimum point distance. The innovation of CBSM-YOLO lies in integrating efficient modules to jointly address the challenges faced in supermarket retail scenarios. According to the final experimental results, under the same hyperparameters and training dataset (Ultralytics SKU-110K), the CBSM-YOLO model is compared with the original YOLOv8 model. It has improved by 1.69%, 1.21%, 1.12% and 1.35% respectively in terms of accuracy, recall rate, mP@50 and mP@95-50, demonstrating its effectiveness in real-time detection of supermarket goods.

title: Deep Learning-Based Audio Denoising for Disaster Response Systems
authors: Sivsakthiselvan S, Siddharth Hariharan, Dhruv Arjun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: In disaster situations, high-quality communication is crucial to coordinate among emergency response teams. Such communication gets degraded, though, with the presence of extremely high levels of background noise in the form of sirens, alarms, destruction of buildings, and human cacophony. This paper reports an audio denoising system based on deep learning for improvement in speech intelligibility under such unfavorable acoustic environments. The system employs a light-weight 1D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model trained from Mel spectrogram representations of speech. The training data is composed by augmenting clean speech of the Microsoft Scalable Noisy Speech Dataset (MS-SNSD) using samples of noise related to disaster. The evaluation metrics are Short-Time Objective Intelligibility (STOI), Perceptual Evaluation of Speech Quality (PESQ), and Mel spectrogram visual assessment. Results indicate substantial improvement in both speech clarity and perceived quality, which affirm the real-time applicability of the system to disaster-response communication devices.

title:
PIV-Based Study of Heat Dissipation and Clogging Phenomenon of TiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Nanofluid in Microchannels
authors: Tieliang Li, Si Chen, Wenchao Tian
abstract: As the power density of electronic devices continues to increase, traditional cooling strategies such as air cooling can no longer meet stringent thermal management requirements. Microchannel cooling technology based on nanofluids has emerged as a promising solution for achieving efficient thermal management due to its exceptional thermal conductivity. However, nanoparticle deposition and channel blockage are among the key factors affecting the thermal performance of nanofluid cooling in microchannels. This study systematically investigates the heat transfer and flow characteristics of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TiO}_{2}$</tex> nanofluid in microchannels through experimental analysis. Particle Image Velocimetry (PIV) is employed to reveal the flow field distribution and identify sensitive deposition zones. The results reveal that using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TiO}_{2}$</tex> nanofluid reduces the chip surface temperature by 7% at the same flow rate. However, deposition is prone to occur in high shear gradient and recirculation zones, leading to flow field distortion and increased pressure drop. This study provides theoretical justification and experimental data support for the application of nanofluids in microchannel cooling.

title: Method for Analytical Solution of Heating Network Based on Spectrum Decomposition and Vectorization Calculation
authors: Xuting Du, Kelun He
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue of low computational efficiency of traditional temporal iteration methods in dynamic simulation of heating network systems, and proposes a frequency-domain temporal coupling solution framework based on the heat flow method. For the primary network water supply process, a sparse matrix-vectorized temporal iteration algorithm is adopted to achieve a synchronous solution of temperature at all network nodes. For the dynamic response of the secondary network, the root-finding of characteristic equations and the principle of multi-order harmonic superposition are used to accurately analyze the transient heat transfer process of building envelopes.

title: Development and Monitoring of Gold Electroplating Process on 300 mm Wafer Scale: Impacts of Bath Chemistry and Process Parameters
authors: Tran Van Nhat Anh, Meng Tao
abstract: This paper presents a systematic study of gold electroplating process on 300 mm wafer scale for under bump metallization (UBM) applications. By investigating effects of the key process parameters such as hot entry, rotation speed, current density, pulse waveform and bath chemistry, the uniformity of Au plating layer was successfully improved, with a center-to-edge variation of 7 nm. The process development is important for next generations of advanced packaging, with the scaling of UBM for high performance computing systems.

title: Study of Procedures for Autonomous Air-to-Air Refueling of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: M.K. Filyashkin
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: The article discusses the problems of automation of aerial refueling of unmanned aerial vehicles. A method is proposed for forming an approach trajectory that minimizes the possibility of the refueled unmanned aerial vehicle getting into the turbulent wake of a tanker. The issues of information support for the stages of approach and docking are analyzed, in particular, determining the parameters of the relative position of the refueling system elements "probe-drogue" when implementing laser homing of an unmanned aerial vehicle on a drogue and a drogue on the refueling probe. Options for counteracting special disturbances that occur during the approach and docking stages are being considered.

title: PrismGS: Physically-Grounded Anti-Aliasing for High-Fidelity Large-Scale 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Houqiang Zhong, Zhenglong Wu, Sihua Fu, Zihan Zheng, Xin Jin, Xiaoyun Zhang, Li Song, Qiang Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has recently enabled real-time photorealistic rendering in compact scenes, but scaling to large urban environments introduces severe aliasing artifacts and optimization instability, especially under high-resolution (e.g., 4K) rendering. These artifacts, manifesting as flickering textures and jagged edges, arise from the mismatch between Gaussian primitives and the multi-scale nature of urban geometry. While existing "divide-and-conquer" pipelines address scalability, they fail to resolve this fidelity gap. In this paper, we propose PrismGS, a physically-grounded regularization framework that improves the intrinsic rendering behavior of 3D Gaussians. PrismGS integrates two synergistic regularizers. The first is pyramidal multi-scale supervision, which enforces consistency by supervising the rendering against a pre-filtered image pyramid. This compels the model to learn an inherently anti-aliased representation that remains coherent across different viewing scales, directly mitigating flickering textures. This is complemented by an explicit size regularization that imposes a physically-grounded lower bound on the dimensions of the 3D Gaussians. This prevents the formation of degenerate, view-dependent primitives, leading to more stable and plausible geometric surfaces and reducing jagged edges. Our method is plug-and-play and compatible with existing pipelines. Extensive experiments on MatrixCity, Mill-19, and UrbanScene3D demonstrate that PrismGS achieves state-of-the-art performance, yielding significant PSNR gains around 1.5 dB against CityGaussian, while maintaining its superior quality and robustness under demanding 4K rendering.

title: Rational Approximation of Fractional-Order Interval Models Using Genetic Algorithms with Application to Mobile Robots
authors: Athul Krishna M J, Ajai V Babu, Suraj Damodaran, Chinchu Varghese, Tessy Ninan, Tripti S Warrier
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Fractional-order (FO) models provide an accurate description of the actuator dynamics, wheel drives, and uncertain system parameters in mobile robots. However, their direct use in real-time control increases computational complexity, particularly when interval uncertainty is included. This paper presents a Genetic Algorithm (GA) based approach for rational approximation of fractional-order interval transfer functions (FOITFs). The method integrates Oustaloup Recursion Algorithm (ORA) with GA optimization to obtain integer-order (IO) models that are remain valid across the full parameter interval, rather than being limited to a single nominal system. For a 1.8th-order interval system, rational approximants of second and third order are generated to illustrate this method. A comparative analysis of step and frequency responses illustrates the trade-off between achieving an accurate approximation and keeping the model simple enough for real-time use. The GA optimized models maintain stability and performance across a wide range of parameter alterations, as further confirmed by Monte Carlo robustness validation. These findings suggest that effective real-time modeling of mobile robotic systems operating in unpredictable and constantly changing settings might be based on a suggested approximation approach.

title: Robust Radio Frequency Fingerprinting Under Cross-Receiver Conditions via Adaptive Augmentation and Attention
authors: Jiawen Shao, Weijie Zhang, Tiantian Tang, Guan Gui
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Radio frequency fingerprinting (RFFI) has emerged as a promising technique for identifying Internet of Things (IoT) devices by exploiting hardware-induced features embedded in RF signals. However, most existing deep learning-based RFFI approaches assume fixed receivers, which substantially degrades robustness under cross-receiver conditions in practical deployments. To overcome this limitation, we propose a receiver-agnostic RFFI framework that achieves 85.83% identification accuracy, representing a 3.49% improvement over state-of-the-art methods. The framework leverages a progressively enhanced ResNet architecture with advanced squeeze-andexcitation blocks, multi-scale attention mechanism, and adaptive data augmentation strategy (contributing 3.38% improvement). An intelligent ensemble selection scheme further provides 2.67% performance gain. The framework demonstrates superior receiver-agnostic capability with 0.912 robustness score while maintaining real-time performance (2.3 ms inference time). Extensive experiments on a 6-transmitter, 12-receiver WiFi dataset validate significantly improved identification accuracy and robustness compared with existing baselines.

title: No atomics, no problem. Developing a RAG pipeline for shared CXL memory
authors: Alfred Bratterud, Gisle Dankel, Amin Farajianzadeh, John Groves, Chengyi Juan, Joshua Sutterlein, Andrés Márquez, Petter Gustad, Arnt Emil Ingulstad
abstract: We share early experiments with software development for shared CXL memory on the H3 Falcon C5022 CXL switch. We describe how the different stages in the development of a commercial RAG pipeline were impacted by the presence of CXL memory. All of Wikipedia is split into 46 million text passages which are embedded into a high-dimensional embedding space and subjected to heavy load in single-and multi-host experiments. We observe significant performance advantages available to DuckDB and Faiss without changing the software; we measure up to 12× latency reduction with real workloads and 40× reduction with synthetic workloads on CXL, compared to the same queries run with demand paging on NVMe. We explain the current challenges of working with two disjoint cache coherency domains and explain how the Fabric-Attached Memory File System (famfs) and famfs producer-consumer queues provide synchronization patterns without atomic operations on current ×86 CPUs. We then discuss open challenges remaining for high performance atomics and locking mechanisms in shared memory. Lastly we show how famfs page-level interleaving enables near-linear throughput scaling when a second host serves queries from the same Faiss index on shared fabric-attached memory and how shared memory allocations can be orchestrated by Kubernetes in a full vertical RAG deployment.

title:
X-Band TM
                    <sub>02</sub>
                    Klystron-Like Relativistic Backward-Wave Oscillator Design via Tree-Encoding-Based Hierarchical Parameter Optimization
authors: Wenjin Yang, Hongguang Wang, Yongdong Li, Yonggui Zhai, Meng Cao, Chunliang Liu
