abstract: This article presents a tree-encoding-based multiobjective optimization algorithm (TMOA) for the hierarchical parameter (HP) optimization of high-power vacuum electronic devices (HPVEDs). The TMOA considers the structural parameter correlations of the HPVEDs, enabling integrated optimization of both the number of periods in the device’s high-frequency structure and the parameters of each unit. An efficient X-band TM<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathbf {{02}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> klystron-like relativistic backward-wave oscillator (KL-RBWO) is designed using the TMOA. Particle-in-cell (PIC) simulations show that the TM<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathbf {{02}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> KL-RBWO achieves an output power of 2.5 GW at a diode voltage of 736 kV, a current of 9.2 kA, and a guiding magnetic field of 2.8 T, with a frequency of 9.6 GHz and a beam-to-microwave conversion efficiency improved to 36.9%. Mechanism analysis indicates that the efficient KL-RBWO exhibits improved phase matching between the axial electromagnetic field and electron current, more reasonable energy spread in the electron beam, and more favorable harmonic current distribution.

title: Pulsating Period-Doubled Soliton Molecules in a Passive Mode-Locked Fiber Laser_supp1-3666934.pdf
authors: Dong Dong Han
abstract: The period-doubling bifurcation, which marks a transition from steady states to chaotic regimes, has been widely studied both theoretically and experimentally. However, the complex nonlinear dynamical phenomena underlying these transitions remain to be fully investigated. In this paper, the complex dynamics of pulsating period-doubled soliton molecules (PDSMs) were experimentally and numerically investigated in a passively mode-locked fiber laser. Two distinct pulsating period-doubled soliton molecules (PDSMs) were experimentally generated by adjusting the polarization controller (PC), including the single-period and double-period varieties. Transient pulsating characteristics of these soliton molecules were captured using dispersive Fourier transform technique, revealing the presence of multiple energy modulation periods in PDSMs. Numerical simulations reproduced the interplay of multiple bifurcation behaviors, which was in good agreement with the experimental observations. These findings provide new insights into complex bifurcation phenomena, offering valuable perspectives for nonlinear science and applications.

title: Turn-off Performance Comparison of High-Power SiC MOSFET and IGBT Modules in Near-ZCS operation
authors: Yue Li, Xizheng Guo, Chenchen Wang, Kai Li, Xiaojie You, Xin Hao
abstract: In medium-voltage, medium-frequency applications, high-power silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFET and insulated gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) modules commonly employ near-zero current switching (near-ZCS) technology to minimize turn-off losses. In these scenarios, the turn-off behavior of the IGBT module is determined not only by the gate drive parameters and turn-off current but is also significantly influenced by the dynamic stored charge in the N-base region during the conduction phase. However, existing comparative studies fail to consider the effect of the dynamic stored charge, leading to incomplete and less precise performance evaluations between SiC MOSFETs and IGBTs. To overcome this limitation, this paper investigates the turn-off mechanisms of both devices under near-ZCS conditions and introduces an analytical model for the turn-off transient process of the IGBT, taking into account the effect of the dynamic stored charge during the conduction phase. Using this model, the turn-off performance of SiC MOSFETs and IGBTs is compared and experimentally validated under varying conditions, including turn-off currents, turn-off gate resistors, junction temperatures, and dynamic charge effects. Furthermore, the proposed IGBT turn-off model is verified using both simulation and experimental results. Compared to existing literature, the model proposed in this paper offers a more precise prediction of IGBT turn-off behavior, providing clearer guidelines for device selection in the design of converters for medium-voltage, medium-frequency applications.

title: Geometric Partition for Billion-Scale Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search
authors: Yujian Fu, Cheng Chen, Yao Chen, Weng-Fai Wong, Bingsheng He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Large-scale approximate nearest neighbor search (ANNS) has become a fundamental operation in a wide range of modern applications, including recommendation systems and large language models. Partition-based indexes have emerged as a popular solution for billion-scale ANNS tasks, serving as the basis for many ANNS approaches. However, our analysis indicates that, to achieve optimal search performance on billion-scale datasets, an extremely large number of partitions (tens or even hundreds of millions) is often required for fine-grained partitioning of the feature space. Relying on full-precision distance calculations for constructing and querying such a large number of partitions imposes significant time costs. In this work, we propose a novel geometric distance inference mechanism that leverages geometric relationships to expedite distance computations between the vector and space partitions. By reusing intermediate or offline-computed distance information, this method substantially reduces the overhead of full-precision calculations. We also introduce a clustering paradigm for generating space partitions that incorporates this geometric distance pattern, which can be seamlessly integrated with other indexing schemes such as vector quantization and proximity graphs. Through detailed complexity analysis and extensive experiments on billion-scale datasets, we confirm the efficiency of our geometric index (GI) design. Empirical results show that GI-based solutions consistently surpass various baseline methods on search efficiency. In particular, they offer considerable acceleration (exceeding a factor of 2.0) at high recall levels (e.g., Recall10@10 = 95%) to partition-based solutions, while also demonstrating comparable or superior query throughput relative to leading graph-based indexes.

title: Improving Park-and-Ride Occupancy Prediction With Feature-Augmented Cross-Variate and Temporal Transformer
authors: Hongguang Ma, Xiang Li, Jingyi Li, Ruiqiang Ma, Witold Pedrycz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Accurate prediction of parking occupancy at Park-and-Ride (P+R) facilities is crucial for enabling efficient multimodal transportation systems and supporting dynamic urban mobility management. P+R parking behavior is influenced by multidimensional factors encompassing temporal patterns, meteorological conditions, socioeconomic indicators, and transit-related attributes. To address this challenging prediction task, this study proposes CvTFormer (Cross-variate and Temporal Transformer), a novel Transformer-based model specifically designed to jointly capture temporal dependencies and intricate cross-variate interactions within P+R occupancy data. CvTFormer employs a dual-attention architecture: self-attention mechanisms capture time-dependent patterns, while cross-attention mechanisms facilitate the deep integration of heterogeneous features. Experiments on a real-world dataset spanning 24 P+R facilities in Beijing demonstrate that CvTFormer consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. It achieves average reductions in MAE, RMSE, and MAPE of 10.04%, 9.88%, and 13.45%, respectively, compared to the standard Transformer model. Further ablation studies highlight the importance of incorporating auxiliary features, yielding additional reductions of 6.05% in MAE, 5.34% in RMSE, and 3.15% in MAPE. These results underscore the effectiveness, robustness, and generalizability of CvTFormer for practical urban mobility applications.

title: Winding Design of an Induction-type Bearingless Motor Based on the Higher-order Suspension Equation
authors: Qifeng Ding, Zebing Yang, Zhanwen Ding, Chengling Lu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: To enhance the suspension control precision of a bearingless induction motor (BIM), the mathematic model as well as winding structure are improved in this article. First, a higher-order Maxwell force equation based on precise air-gap permeance is established, which reveals the common pulsating force phenomenon in bearingless machines. Second, this paper studies the harmonic suspension force theory and proposes an improved suspension winding with a specific short-pitch to suppress the magnetic field harmonics. Third, by analyzing the Maxwell force distribution and utilizing their similarity under different pole conditions, a novel field-weakening winding to reduce the higher-order components of rotor eccentricity is proposed. Finally, an integrated experimental platform is built and the suspension performance of the initial machine and the proposed one are compared. Theoretical analysis, simulations as well as experimental results all validate the feasibility of the proposed principle and the effectiveness of designed windings.

title: ARGfore: A multivariate framework for forecasting antibiotic resistance gene abundances using time-series metagenomic datasets_supp3-3667074.pdf
authors: Liqing Zhang

title: Enhancing Intrusion Detection Via Interpretable Inter-Flow Spatio-Temporal Graphs and Intra-Flow Features
authors: Xiaowei Zhao, Mingshu He, Liu Yang, Jianjin Zhao, Xiaojuan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The performance of network intrusion detection systems (IDS) critically depends on the completeness of traffic feature representation. Existing methods predominantly focus either on intra-flow features (e.g., packet length sequences) or single-dimensional inter-flow relationships, limiting optimal classification. To address this restriction, we propose IF<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-STG to enhance intrusion detection via interpretable inter-flow spatio-temporal graphs and intra-flow features. The key innovation of our scheme is the construction of an Inter-flow Spatio-Temporal relationship Graph (ISTG), with two-dimensional edges explicitly quantifying the correlation strength between flows. On this basis, a GNN learns the inter-flow context. In parallel, a CNN extracts fine-grained intra-flow features from raw bytes of individual flows. Finally, the two modal features are fused to acquire a comprehensive representation. This representation can effectively capture both intrinsic behavior patterns of flows and contextual inter-flow relationships, thereby significantly enhancing the detection performance and robustness of model. Experimental results show that the proposed IF<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-STG achieves over 99% accuracy on three real-world datasets, outperforming existing approaches. The constructed ISTG provides explicit visual interpretation of flow dependencies. More importantly, IF<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-STG-Inter, using only inter-flow features, maintains 97.78% detection accuracy in cross-dataset tests involving time-disparate datasets, validating superior generalization of inter-flow spatio-temporal features. This advantage renders it particularly promising for practical intrusion detection applications.

title: LymphAware: Domain-Aware Bias Disruption for Reliable Lymphoma Cancer AI Diagnosis
authors: Teerapong Panboonyuen, Chanon Dechsupa
abstract: Lymphoma histopathology classifiers frequently achieve high accuracy by exploiting non-biological visual cues such as scanner color signatures, stain-saturation drift, background texture, or slide-processing artifacts. These shortcut signals inflate in-domain performance while causing brittle behavior under cross-acquisition domain shift. We propose LymphAware, a domain-aware bias disruption framework that explicitly separates lymphoma-relevant morphology from shortcut-sensitive acquisition factors. The method is designed for realistic multi-source histopathology settings, without assuming verified institutional separation. LymphAware integrates three complementary mechanisms: (1) morphology-centric feature isolation, (2) adversarial and orthogonality-based shortcut suppression, and (3) cross-domain stability constraints that enforce acquisition-invariant representations. Each component addresses a distinct shortcut failure mode, avoiding redundant architectural complexity. To expose latent shortcut dependence, we introduce artifact-shift perturbations that simulate staining and scanner variability and enforce counterfactual consistency during training. Evaluated on a heterogeneous lymphoma histopathology benchmark, LymphAware consistently improves cross-domain robustness, degrades gracefully under loss-weight variation, and yields attribution maps aligned with pathology-relevant regions. We emphasize that these attributions reflect associative alignment rather than proven causal inference. These results highlight the importance of representation-level shortcut disruption for reliable and clinically meaningful lymphoma diagnosis.

title: Mitigating Motion Sickness in Autonomous Vehicle Cockpits: A Design-Oriented Systematic Review
authors: Xiaojing Niu, Zhiang Chen, Liling Chen
abstract: With the advancement of intelligent cockpits and autonomous vehicles, passengers are increasingly disengaged from driving and more frequently engage in non-driving-related tasks such as reading or screen viewing, which significantly elevates the prevalence of motion sickness (MS) and challenges passenger comfort and acceptance of automated mobility. To address this challenge, this paper conducts a systematic evidence mapping-based review of 53 studies published between 2020 and 2025. Specifically, five core MS inducers including sensory conflict, non-driving-related tasks, motion predictability, vehicle motion, and individual differences are identified and categorized under three foundational theoretical frameworks, i.e., sensory conflict theory, evolutionary theory, and postural instability theory. Chord-diagram-based evidence mapping is employed to structurally link mitigation measures to their inducers, revealing research concentration patterns, underexplored intervention pathways, and cross-inducer design relationships. Beyond descriptive mapping, a comparative synthesis is conducted to examine mitigation mechanisms, consistency of reported effects, and contextual dependencies across experimental settings. The results indicate that strategies supporting sensory anticipation, motion control, and non-visual cueing generally demonstrate more robust and consistent mitigation effects than visual-only or display-based interventions. Key research gaps are identified, including limited multimodal integration for predictability-oriented mitigation and insufficient personalization for susceptibility-driven MS. Building upon the synthesized evidence, an Adaptive Motion Sickness Mitigation conceptual framework is proposed, enabling dynamic, data-driven, and personalized interventions through real-time perception, conflict analysis, and feedback control. This review provides a structured, design-oriented evidence reference for developing intelligent, human-centered MS mitigation systems, contributing to safer, more adaptive, and comfortable autonomous mobility experiences.

title: Pleth-SleepNet: A Unified Network For Simultaneous Sleep Stages And Disorder Classification With PPG Signal._supp1-3666950.docx
authors: Manish Sharma

title: Investigation of YBCO Thin-Film Surface Impedance for THz Microscopy Using Josephson Junctions
authors: Paul Julius Ritter, Yayi Lin, Max Propper, Marc-Andre Tucholke, Renjith Mathew Roy, Julius Mumme, Marc Schefler, Meinhard Schilling, Benedikt Hampel
abstract: Josephson junctions based on high-temperature su-perconductors enable the detection of terahertz (THz) radiation at frequencies up to 5 THz. Their current-voltage characteristics provide information on the incident intensity and frequency of gigahertz and terahertz radiation, with coupling eficiency enhanced through the use of integrated antennas. This detector, called a Josephson cantilever, is employed in our THz scanning microscope to map three-dimensional far-field patterns. The cantilevers are fabricated on lanthanum aluminate-strontium aluminum tantalate (LSAT) or magnesium (MgO) substrates with epitaxially grown yttrium barium copper oxide (YBCO) thin films. Focused-helium ion beam direct writing is used to write Josephson junctions in the antenna feed points. These narrowband u-shaped antennas are designed for specific resonant frequencies, requiring the determination of the YBCO's surface impedance. The temperature-dependent surface impedance of YBCO was determined using terahertz spectroscopy (300 GHz to 2 THz, 10 K to 300 K) and validated against measured antenna resonances that were compared to finite integration simulations in CST Studio Suite 2023. Finally, spatially resolved measurements at 1.4 THz demonstrate the imaging capabilities with symmetric Gaussian beam profiles.

title: Adaptive-Awareness for RIS-enhanced Semantic Communications (RISemCom) in Dynamic Random Environment
authors: Zhengyu Zhu, Xinyue Liang, Zheng Chu, Gangcan Sun, De Mi, Merouane Debbah
abstract: In this paper, we propose a multi-SNR adaptive Semantic Communication (SemCom) System based on Recon figurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) to solve the problem of insufficient adaptability of traditional SemCom in dynamic chan nel environments. We firstly design a RIS-enhanced Semantic Communication (RISemCom) System that innovatively combines a programmable wireless environment with Deep Learning (DL) to achieve joint optimization of channel environment and se mantic feature extraction. Next, two training algorithms are proposed: Dynamic Random Environment Adaptive Multi-SNR (DREAMS) algorithm and Two-Stage Training (TST) algorithm. The DREAMS dynamically adjusts SNR values during training, allowing a single model to adapt to a wide range of SNR conditions while significantly reducing deployment complexity. The TST serves as a comparison baseline, providing a dedicated optimized model for each specific SNR environment. Numerical results are demonstrated to confirm that the DREAMS algorithm maintains excellent performance across a wide range of SNRs with a single model, and significantly improves the PSNR and SSIM metrics compared to traditional methods under low SNR conditions. The performance gain is particularly notable in challenging low SNR environments, proving the system's robustness in adverse channel conditions. This work not only expands the applicability of SemCom but also provides new insights for reliable communication in variable channel environments in future 6G networks.

title: Generalized Singular Spectrum Analysis Associated with Fractional Fourier Transform
authors: Hongxia Miao, Jun Shi
abstract: In this study, an adaptive nonstationary signal decomposition technique is developed, which is associated with the singular spectrum analysis (SSA) and the discrete fractional Fourier transform (DFrFT). The SSA is a data-adaptive discrete signal analysis method that does not require a parameter model in advance, of which the core operation is singular value decomposition (SVD). It has been proven that the singular values of the constructed Hankel matrix are equally distributed to the power spectrum of the discrete signal, which bridges the discrete signal and time-frequency analysis. However, the power spectrum of a nonstationary signal is often wide-band or even non-bandlimited, which degrades the performance of the SSA. The DFrFT contains the Fourier transform as a special case and can be regarded as a linear time-frequency representation. It has achieved significant success in non-stationary signal processing, especially in radar and communications signal processing. A wide-band (or non-bandlimited) signal may become narrow-band (or bandlimited) in the DFrFT domain. To enhance the SSA with the help of this property, a new Hankel matrix is constructed using the fractional time-shift operation, and its singular values are proven to be related to the fractional power spectrum of the sequence. To handle nonstationary signals, the generalized SSA is designed associated with DFrFT. It is more flexible and suitable in nonstationary signal processing than the SSA due to its free parameter. Its superior performances over other popular data-driven methods are verified and displayed using simulations.

title: Agent-Based Digital Twin Communication Framework for Distributed Edge-Cloud Multimodal Perception in 6G Networks
authors: Ruirui Xu
abstract: Digital twin (DT) technology is becoming a foundational element of next generation 5G and 6G communication systems, where virtual counterparts of physical assets support real time monitoring, prediction, and closed loop control. When these DTs are tightly coupled with AI driven multimodal perception and distributed edge cloud processing, they also introduce an expanded attack surface, complex data governance issues, and new requirements for AI trustworthiness and standardization. This article presents a tutorial style agent-based communication framework for secure DT operations in 6G networks. We decompose the DT ecosystem into perception, communication, control, security, and governance agents that operate across device, edge, and cloud layers, and show how zero trust authentication, identity anchored sensing, privacy preserving AI analytics, and fine-grained access control can be embedded at each interface. A threat role mapping systematically links typical cyber physical attacks to affected agents and concrete counter-measures, guiding engineers toward structured risk analysis rather than ad hoc defenses. We further outline a standards-oriented roadmap by surveying relevant activities in 3GPP, ISO/ IEC, ITU T, and IETF, and identifying gaps where DT specific requirements are not yet captured, such as lifecycle management of virtual identities, protection of federated learning updates, and AI governance for safety critical control loops. An industrial 6G smart factory use case illustrates how the proposed agent-based DT framework can deliver predictive maintenance and secure closed loop control in practice. The article aims to equip practitioners and standardization stakeholders with actionable guidance for deploying secure, privacy preserving, and standards ready digital twin solutions in future communication networks.

title: Compact Pattern-Reconfigurable Dielectric Resonator Antennas with 360° Horizontally Switchable Beams
authors: Xu Zhou, Kwok Wa Leung, Peng Fei Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Pattern-reconfigurable dielectric resonator (DR) antennas (DRAs) generating three and eight beams are investigated. Both designs consist of a slotted DR, with the slotted region fed by a U-shaped strip printed on a feeding substrate. The bottom of the DRs is silver-plated and divided into two parts for the three-beam design and four parts for the eight-beam design. A key innovation lies in their grounding design to achieve the reconfigurability. A metal plate is perpendicularly placed beneath the feeding substrate to obtain the equivalent electric dipole (E-dipole) mode. Simultaneously, the DR TEy1δ1 mode acts as an equivalent magnetic dipole (M-dipole). Together, these two modes form a complementary magnetic/electric (M/E) dipole pair, giving a high front-toback ratio (FTBR). By turning ON/OFF the diodes between the separated silver plating differently, the orientation of the equivalent M-dipole can be electronically controlled, altering the radiation pattern. An eight-beam antenna prototype with a compact size of 0.22×0.26×0.43 λ03 was fabricated, where λ0 is the wavelength in vacuum at the center frequency. Measured results show that it has –10-dB impedance bandwidth of 12.1%, an antenna gain of 5.42 dBi, an average FTBR of 16.7 dB, elevation plane signal coverage exceeding 98°, and 360° horizontally switchable beams. This design has potential applications in intelligent wireless systems to enhance signal reception.

title: Reconstructing Sparse-view Indoor Scenes in View Space with Global Monocular Prior Alignment_supp3-3667058.mp4
authors: Cheng Wang
abstract: Although 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has greatly advanced the development of novel view synthesis (NVS) and surface reconstruction tasks, it still faces serious challenges in indoor scenes with only sparse views due to the lack of initialization point cloud. Existing GS-like methods are geometrically initialized using point clouds from Structure from Motion (SfM), and their performance is heavily limited by the point cloud quality. However, in indoor scenes containing large areas of textureless or weakly textured regions, the SfM algorithm struggles to generate point clouds in these areas, especially when only a limited number of input views are available. This situation can cause the GS optimization process to overfit the photometric error, resulting in severe geometric degradation in regions lacking proper initialization. To address the above problem, we propose a novel sparse-view 3DGS method using global scale-aligned monocular depth to initialize Gaussian primitives and optimize them in view space, named VGA-GS. First, we design a global scale-aligned monocular depth initialization strategy, which provides geometric priors in textureless or weakly textured regions while avoiding the local geometric distortions in vanilla alignments. Second, we propose a novel Gaussian primitive representation based on view space and a refinement strategy based on pixel errors, which can constrain each primitive to always be within the field of view where it is observed to be fully optimized. Finally, we extract the surface prior from monocular depth to regularize the rendering depth and normal, and further propagate the constraints of the training view to the neighboring pseudo-views via image warping. Our method achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction performance in both textureless regions and local details for indoor scenes with sparse views. Moreover, it is highly scalable, supporting integration with various 3DGS rasterizers and depth estimation methods. We release the code at https://github.com/XT5un/VGA-GS.

title: Edge-AI Enabled Sustainable Consumer Electronics for Remote Health Monitoring
authors: Xueping Liao, Ting Li, Juan Luo, Poschanan Niramitchainont, Yang Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: To address the high energy consumption and network dependence issues of remote health monitoring devices, this study explores the integration of edge artificial intelligence and sustainable design in such devices, aiming to resolve these problems associated with existing solutions. A multi-level collaborative design framework is proposed, integrating algorithm lightweighting, hardware energy efficiency optimization, and intelligent energy management to achieve efficient processing of physiological data on the terminal device while improving environmental performance. Experiments show that the optimized model significantly reduces energy consumption and storage requirements while maintaining high detection accuracy; the dynamic energy scheduling strategy effectively enhances the device's self-sufficiency. This research provides a systematic technical path for developing high-performance, sustainable intelligent health devices.

title: Enhancing Semiconductor Wafer Defect Detection using Hybrid CNN-ViT with Denoising Autoencoders
authors: Abhishek. S. B, Sukhesh Kanna, Benedict Johnson, Suganeshwari G
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Semiconductor wafer defect classification is very important for maintaining high manufacturing quality and throughputs. This paper presents a novel approach that combines Denoising Autoencoders (DAE) along with a hybrid CNN with Vision Transformers (ViT) architecture for improved defect classification performance. The process starts by preprocessing of the wafer map data where we are using DAE to reduce noise and mark important features in the defect. This emphasized data is then passed to the hybrid model that makes use of the strengths of CNNs which are excellent in extracting the local features and the ViT which are excellent in extracting the global features and long range dependencies in the data. On evaluation the proposed model got an overall accuracy of 96%, with a precision, recall, and F1-score of 0.97, 0.96, and 0.96, respectively, outperforming traditional models. These results highlight the models performance which is better when compared to the traditional approaches and models which makes it a more suitable solution for real world defect classification of the wafers with high reliability.

title: Characterization on Fan-Out Heterogeneous Integration Packaging for Premium Smartphone
authors: Ching Chia Chen, Yu Po Wang, Chia Chu Lai, Hsing Yu Liu
abstract: For years, package-on-package (PoP) has been the dominant integration scheme for smartphone application processors (AP), stacking SoC and DRAM vertically to achieve compact form factors. To further accommodate the demand for thinner packages and finer I/O density, fan-out (FO) technology has been developed, replacing laminated substrate with redistribution layers (RDL). However, with SoC power density continuously increasing, thermal dissipation has emerged as a critical challenge, as the stacked DRAM blocks the primary heat conduction path. As a result, the side-by-side placement of SoC and DRAM has been proposed as an alternative integration scheme, enabling direct heat dissipation to the system and allowing thicker SoC chip and DRAM for further performance improvement. This study evaluates PoP and side-by-side structures on both substrate and FO platforms, focusing on thermal, electrical, and mechanical performance, as well as process warpage challenges. Simulation and experimental validation show that side-by-side integration provides approximately 70% improvement in thermal performance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\theta_{\text{JC}}\right)$</tex> compared with PoP, while customized DRAM placement can recover eye diagram degradation and even outperform PoP. Additional metal coating on the surface of Stress and warpage modeling using finite element analysis (FEA) predicts failure mode post reliability test and correlates well with experimental measurements, providing guidance for material selection and process optimization. Reliability test also verified after modeling evaluation. These results demonstrate the potential of FO side-by-side packaging as a promising solution for next-generation premium smartphones.

title: Research on High-Resistance Ground Fault Protection Based on the Ratio of Power Frequency Variation of Phase Currents
authors: Zhifeng Chen, Yinlian Ji, Zhixin Gao, Yingming Zeng, Kaitao Zheng, Xinyi Chen, Ruijue Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: In 10 kV distribution systems, high-resistance ground faults generate weak currents. Existing methods mainly rely on network-wide zero-sequence components, which are often unreliable under high resistance or distributed generation. Protection devices in distribution automation switches cannot compare zero-sequence currents of other feeders, further complicating detection. To address this, a feeder selection method based on the ratio of fundamental-phase current variations before and after faults is proposed. By analyzing current changes, the method accurately identifies high-resistance faults. PSCAD simulations show reliable distinction between faulted and healthy feeders, effective detection with transition resistance up to 2000Ω, and accuracy above 95%, supporting improved supply reliability.

title: SBR-RAG: Efficient Subgraph-Based RAG with Lightweight Filter
authors: Xinda Chu, Lan Ma, Jincheng Bao, Fan Duo, Yuanyuan Qiao
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: Contemporary artificial intelligence (AI), particularly Large Language Models (LLMs), focuses on emulating human capabilities for knowledge acquisition and utilization. Relying solely on pre-training data is insufficient for up-to-date knowledge access, yet continuously retraining or fine-tuning LLMs with internal data is impractical. Many existing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) [1], [2], [3], [4] methods significantly enhance LLMs' access to external knowledge through vector-based or graph-structured retrieval from knowledge bases. However, the complexity and diversity of real-world knowledge bases hinder RAG systems from effectively retrieving complex multi-hop data and abstractly related information; moreover, increased volumes of erroneous retrieved data readily induce LLM hallucinations. To address these limitations, we propose SBR-RAG, a knowledge retrieval and filtering framework. SBR-RAG identifies relevant documents within a query-relevant subgraph and employs a lightweight discriminator for filtering, significantly improving accuracy while mitigating hallucinations. Experiments demonstrate that our method achieves superior Recall and F1 scores across multiple public datasets, demonstrating superior performance over many existing strong baselines.

title: Research on Self-Circulating and Forced-Circulation Evaporative Cooling Systems for Large Offshore Wind Turbines
authors: Tao Zeng, Wenbiao Hu, Chenyu Xie, Haifeng Wang, Yunyi Zhou, Huan Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue of insulation breakdown in large offshore wind turbines caused by uneven internal temperature distribution, which leads to motor failure. It proposes an evaporative cooling method capable of penetrating deep into the motor for direct cooling. To enhance the efficiency of the motor evaporative cooling system and clarify the required cooling levels under different operating conditions, a comparative analysis was conducted on the effects of self-circulating and forced-circulating evaporative cooling systems on the motor. Through an experimental platform, this study conducted tests on the cooling effects of self-circulation versus forced circulation under varying thermal loads, as well as the impact of medium flow rates on system performance under different configurations. Experimental results demonstrate that the evaporative cooling system can directly extract heat from the motor interior that other cooling methods cannot remove. Compared to self-circulation, forced circulation reduces the maximum temperature of stainless steel heat transfer pipes by approximately 20°C. Evidently, incorporating a pump as the circulation power source enables the system to achieve more effective temperature reduction under high thermal load conditions. Analysis indicates that although the evaporative cooling system designed in this paper is based on a 6MW capacity, experimental results demonstrate that the system is fully capable of handling the thermal load of larger wind turbines through simple fine-tuning.

title: RC-Informer: Reliable Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Under Extreme Weather Events
authors: Jiner Cheng, Qingshan Xu, Yongbiao Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Extreme weather events pose critical challenges for photovoltaic power forecasting, as conventional methods fail to capture ramp events under severe meteorological conditions. An RC-Informer method was designed in this paper, adopting a three-stage encoder-decoder-corrector framework to couple explicit ramp detection with temporal modeling. Multi-scale slope perception addresses nonlinear interactions between power gradients and meteorological covariates, while sparse attention captures long-range temporal dependencies. Under extreme weather conditions, RC-Informer achieves normalized mean absolute error of 0.0052 and coefficient of determination of 0.9994, substantially outperforming existing transformer architectures. These results demonstrate that physically informed deep learning enables reliable forecasting under challenging meteorological scenarios.

title: Pathfinder: Exploring Path Diversity for Assessing Internet Censorship Inconsistency
authors: Xiaoqin Liang, Guannan Liu, Lin Jin, Shuai Hao, Haining Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Internet censorship is commonly enabled by authorities to enforce information control. So far, existing censorship studies have largely focused on country-level characterization, primarily because (1) censorship enforcement is often mandated through nationwide policies and (2) it is difficult to control the routing of probing packets to trigger censorship across different networks within a country. However, censorship mechanisms can vary significantly at the ISP level, revealing a more diverse landscape than previously assumed. In this paper, we investigate Internet censorship from a new perspective by scrutinizing diverse censorship deployments within a country. We design and deploy a measurement framework that utilizes multiple geo-distributed backend servers to probe various network paths from a single vantage point. By generating traffic targeting the same domain but different backend server IPs, we induce path diversity that exposes the traffic to distinct transit networks, and potentially, different censorship devices, thereby enabling a more granular analysis of censorship practices. Through our large-scale experiments and in-depth analysis, we reveal that diverse censorship resulting from varying routing paths within a country is widespread, implying that (1) the implementations of centralized censorship are commonly incomplete or flawed and (2) decentralized censorship is also prevalent. Moreover, we find that different hosting platforms also contribute to inconsistent censorship behavior due to their varying peering relationships with ISPs within a country. Finally, we present detailed case studies to illustrate the configurations that lead to such inconsistencies and to explore their underlying causes.

title: A Synergistic Multi-Objective Rime Optimization and Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Dynamic and High-Dimensional Communication Network Traffic Prediction
authors: Lang Yu, Jinhong Xia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: This paper proposes a robust architecture for dynamic high-dimensional communication network traffic prediction that combines the Multi-Objective Rime Optimization Algorithm (MO-RIME) with Hierarchical Deep Reinforcement Learning (H-DDPG). This framework deeply integrates a physics-inspired multi-objective swarm intelligence optimization mechanism with an adaptive policy learning mechanism. This innovation enables a multi-layered optimization process of “global search-local refinement-dynamic equilibrium.” MORIME leverages the condensation nucleus formation, particle growth, and heat diffusion mechanisms of rime to initialize the solution space, perform local refinement search, and perform global information diffusion. H-DDPG dynamically adjusts multi-objective weights based on real-time prediction performance and network state feedback. The model enhances prediction robustness by combining constrained distribution similarity (Jensen-Shannon divergence) and sparsity metrics. Experiments were conducted on Abilene, GÉANT, MAWI, and high-dimensional synthetic datasets <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{N}=500, \mathrm{D}=5, ~\mathrm{K}=10)$</tex>. The framework also covers standard scenarios, bursty traffic, topology changes, and noise/encryption interference. The results show that on the Abilene dataset, the RMSE, MAE, and MAPE of MO-RIME + H-DDPG are reduced by more than 60 % compared to ARIMA. This result is about 30 % lower than that of LSTM and ST-GCN. On the high-dimensional synthetic dataset, the RMSE is 36.8 and the MAPE is 9.7. This result is more than 40 % lower than that of LSTM, ST-GCN, and MOEA/D+DNN. The multi-objective adaptive mechanism reduces the sparsity ratio to 34 %. Experimental results show that the framework has high accuracy and strong robustness in the complex highdimensional dynamic environments.

title: Quantum-Inspired Algorithms for Enhanced Skin Analysis in Dermatology
authors: Chellshe Love Simrochelle, Maria Seraphina Astriani
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study presents a hybrid artificial intelligence (AI) model that integrates quantum-inspired algorithms with classical deep learning techniques to improve skin condition analysis in dermatology. Traditional AI models often face challenges such as high computational cost, limited generalization across diverse skin tones, and difficulty in detecting subtle dermatological features. By applying a quantum-inspired optimization strategy—simulated through differential evolution—the proposed system enhances classification accuracy, training efficiency, and interpretability. Experimental results using public dermatological datasets demonstrate that the model achieved an F1-score of 0.91 and an overall accuracy of 88.3%, outperforming classical baselines. Notably, the quantum-inspired model converged in just 32 epochs, compared to 49 and 52 for conventional architectures like ResNet-50 and EfficientNet-B0, respectively. These improvements underscore the model's capability for faster, more accurate, and scalable dermatological diagnostics, positioning quantum-inspired AI as a viable tool for nextgeneration digital health applications.

title: Operation Optimization for Grid-Connected PV Farm Cluster with Shared Thermal Energy Storage
authors: Jie Wang, Lijun Xu, Yingchao Dong, Yue Liu, Xiaochao Fan, Yali Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: To address the issue of enhancing the consumption capacity of new energy, a grid-connected optimization method is proposed for joint photovoltaic (PV) plant clusters sharing thermal storage from the perspective of a shared mechanism. Firstly, with the distribution network operator as the leader and the shared thermal storage operators using solid heat storage mode in conjunction with the PV station clusters as the followers, the master-slave Stackelberg game optimization model is constructed. Secondly, the main body develops the time-of-use price to implement peak scheduling, introduces the conditional value at risk (CVaR) balance of PV output uncertainty caused by the operation risk of the system, and realizes comprehensive benefit maximization. The followers respond to the time-of-use price to maximize the comprehensive benefit of photovoltaic station operators and shared thermal energy storage, achieving the maximum cooperative interests of the alliance. The simulation examples verify that the proposed optimization model can reduce safety risks while considering the cost risks of the distribution network, promote the consumption of PV output, and reduce the operating costs of photovoltaic stations and shared thermal storage. The proposed coordinated grid-connected optimization strategy incorporating CVaR-based photovoltaic clusters and shared thermal energy storage systems will provide a significant practical reference value for the actual regulation of an integrated cooling-heating-electricity-gas multi-energy flow system, considering complex uncertainty scenarios involving wind, solar, and load variations.

title: Memristor-Based Circuit-Level Design of FFT Calculator for OFDM Systems in Internet of Things
authors: Luhan Wang, Haozhe Jin, Kehan Wang, Zhaoming Lu, Xiangming Wen
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: In OFDM-based <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \mathrm{G} / 6 \mathrm{G}$</tex> IoT systems, the continuous time-frequency domain conversion of signals requires FFT/IFFT computations, which consumes a substantial amount of power and processing time. Memristor-based computing circuits, known for their low power consumption and reduced complexity, have been increasingly applied to neural networks and other computationally intensive tasks, offering a promising solution for low-power signal processing in the IoT. In this paper, we propose a novel memristor-based FFT implementation for energy-efficient IoT systems. We propose a memristor-based large-radix FFT calculator and validate its performance in an IoT-OFDM simulation environment. Compared to FPGA, our 4096-point IFFT/FFT implementation achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$333 \times$</tex> speedup and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times$</tex> energy savings, with minimal BER degradation from computation inaccuracy.

title: Leveraging MySQL and AI Algorithms for Real-Time Clinical Decision Support in Healthcare Systems
authors: Pranith Kumar Reddy Myeka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: This paper explores the use of MySQL databases and Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms to link up to provide a flexible, real-time Clinical Decision Support System (CDSS) for enhanced healthcare delivery. MySQL is used to effectively save, process and retrieve medical data on a large scale with high performance, providing AI algorithms ML machine learning models and DL deep learning for real-time accurate predictions. This integration enables the system to deliver actionable insights and predictive analytics and personalised decision support on both real-time and historical patient data. The study demonstrates the powerful role that AI has in clinical decision-making, thereby increasing diagnostic accuracy and treatment planning as well as operational efficiency. The paper further discusses implementation challenges such as the issues of data privacy, interpretability of models, and integration with current healthcare systems. We learned that using MySQL with AI can improve the decision-making, reduce human error and eventually lead to better patient outcomes along with improved hospital management. This article helps pioneer the development of new intelligent, large-scale CDSS frameworks and may be used in future clinical settings globally.

title: Cropcure Companion: An Explainable AI System for Plant Disease Diagnosis and Integrated Remedy Management
authors: Lokesh Khedekar, Ayaan Khan, Vaishnavi Kharat, Krish Khandelwal, Sumeet Khandagale, Harsh Khillare
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Due to a lack of specialized knowledge, managing plant diseases is the most difficult tasks for small-scale nursery culture and urban farmers. This study introduces “CropCure Companion”, an AI-powered tool for the early and most importantly, it is easy to identify and categorize leaf diseases in pea and corn plants. To provide precise diagnostics, our solution uses powerful deep learning models, ResNet50 and EfficientNet-B0, which are implemented via an easy-to-use Flask web application, to deliver accurate diagnostics. One of the key contributions of this work is the integration of Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), which provides readable, visual explanations of the model's predictions and increases user confidence. The system achieves over 95% accuracy on our test datasets. By connecting predictions with a structured local database that includes chemical solutions, home remedies, and prevention advice, the application provides a comprehensive support framework which goes beyond simple diagnosis. Also, our integrated chatbot provides initial guidance and improves usability for users who are not experts. This work provides a useful, comprehensive tool that enables new farmers and urban farmers to efficiently maintain crop health.

title: Development of Risk Prediction Model for Cervical Cancer
authors: Kashish Singh, Amit Kumar, Kalaivani J, Angayarkanni V, Rama P
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: This project introduces an intuitive and accessible web-based application developed to assist in the early identification of cervical cancer. It utilizes a combination of proven machine learning models—such as AdaBoost, XGBoost, logistic regression, and stacking classifiers—to analyse health-related data and predict cancer risk. The system works on a well-structured dataset that includes both demographic and medical history attributes, allowing it to generate accurate and meaningful results. By integrating multiple algorithms, the framework enhances the overall reliability of predictions and minimizes the chances of incorrect classifications. Ultimately, the system serves as a powerful tool in the healthcare setting, offering timely insights that support early diagnosis and help improve outcomes for patients through informed decision-making.

title: Field Test of 5G New Radio (NR) UL-MIMO and UL-256QAM for HD Live-Streaming
authors: Kasidis Arunruangsirilert
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: The exponential growth of User-Generated Content (UGC), especially High-Definition (HD) live video streaming, places a significant demand on the uplink capabilities of mobile networks. To address this, the 5G New Radio (NR) standard introduced key uplink enhancements, including Uplink Multi-Input Multi-Output (UL-MIMO) and Uplink 256QAM, to improve throughput and spectral efficiency. However, while the benefits of these features for raw data rates are well-documented, their practical impact on real-time applications like live-streaming is not yet well understood. This paper investigates the performance of UL-MIMO and UL-256QAM for HD live-streaming over a commercial 5G network using the Real-Time Messaging Protocol (RTMP). To ensure a fair assessment, we conduct a comparative analysis by modifying the modem firmware of commercial User Equipment (UE), allowing these features to be selectively enabled and disabled on the same device. Performance is evaluated based on key metrics, including dropped video frames and connection stability. Furthermore, this study analyzes 5G Radio Frequency (RF) parameters to quantify the spectral efficiency impact, specifically examining metrics derived from the Channel State Information (CSI) framework, including Reference Signal Received Power (CSI-RSRP), Reference Signal Received Quality (CSI-RSRQ), and Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (CSI-SINR).

title: A Spatio-Temporal Generative Adversarial Network for High-Resolution Rainfall Nowcasting
authors: Sheeba Jeya Sophia, Badugu Aryavardhan, Surampudi Latish Sri Veerendra ai Venkat, Likhitha Bhumireddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Precise short-term rainfall nowcasting is vital for agriculture, hydrology, and urban flood management in Country's monsoon-prevailing climate. This paper describes an end-to-end data-driven approach extending from point-based precipitation regression to spatio-temporal rainfall map prediction. A stacked LSTM is initially trained on a multi-modal tabular dataset combining meteorological, air-quality, and astronomical variables to develop a baseline. Based on this, a spatio-temporal generative model is presented, featuring a Convolutional Long Short-Term Memory (ConvLSTM) generator and a PatchGAN discriminator to predict the next-day rainfall field based on recent gridded observations. The model captures spatial organization and propagation dynamics well, with high structural similarity and low reconstruction error. Datasets, preprocessing, architectures, and evaluation metrics are explained, illustrated by qualitative tripanel visualizations and benchmark tables. Results show that spatio-temporal context modeling produces more coherent and realistic nowcasts than point-wise methods. Multi-variable input, multi-step forecasting, and real-time operational use are topics of future work.

title: Temporal-Global Dual-Path Nesting Network for Complex Time Series Representation and Fault Diagnosis
authors: Chunping Niu, Yongliang Ni, Yiqi Li, Weiqiu Zhang, Guoyan Zhang, Kaibin Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: The capacity to simultaneously grasp long-range dependencies as well as capturing the global contextual features are still central issues in the modeling of complex time series data. This paper proposes a Nested Deep Learning Network (NDLN). The model has the parallel architecture that includes a temporal feature extraction path, and a global feature extraction path. The multi-scale features of both paths are optimally integrated and complemented with a new composite fusion mechanism. The experimental findings of a GCU fault diagnosis dataset provide evidence that the proposed NDLN can achieve a test accuracy of 97.06% in just 30 training epochs. This result is better than the one of a conventional LSTM model (92.16%) in the same conditions. This work presents a new theoretical approach and an efficient engineering solution to the bottleneck of representation learning of complex time series analysis.

title: A Deep Learning Based CSI Localization Algorithm Using Tensor Decomposition and Data Augmentation
authors: Tianyu Hu, Jun Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Since Channel State Information (CSI) can provide rich wireless channel characteristics, it has become an important measurement for high-precision indoor localization. In this paper, a deep learning based CSI localization algorithm using tensor decomposition and data augmentation is proposed. First, the original CSI amplitude data is reconstructed as a three-dimensional tensor with time, frequency and spatial dimensions. With the optimal rank of tensor decomposition obtained from Kfold cross-validation, The CP decomposition and Tucker decomposition methods are proposed to obtain a stable lowdimensional feature representation. Then, in order to enhance the generalization ability of the model, the diffusion model is utilized to perform data augmentation on the extracted lowdimensional features. Next, a dual-branch feature extraction network based on long short-term memory network is constructed to extract the feature of the two tensor features. A more comprehensive feature representation is obtained through the feature fusion. At last, a classification network is constructed for offline classification learning to obtain the position estimation model. The experimental results show that the proposed algorithm can achieve better localization performance than some existing algorithm.

title: Avatars and Adaptive Publishing for Inclusive and Collaborative Metaverse Learning
authors: Rania Hamzaoui, Fathi Essalmi, Mohamed Jemni
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on ICT &amp; Accessibility (ICTA) co-located with 2025 2nd International Conference and Forum on Assistive Technologies for Special Needs (ATSN)
abstract: The rapid growth of the educational metaverse is opening up new opportunities for higher education and professional training by offering immersive and interactive environments. However, these spaces present major challenges in terms of accessibility and inclusion. This article explores the role of avatars as facilitators of collaboration and accessibility, with a particular focus on adaptive digital publishing and inclusive reading solutions. We offer a conceptual framework integrating avatars, adaptive publishing, and collective intelligence, illustrating how these technologies can promote equitable participation for all learners. Finally, we discuss the pedagogical and technological implications for designing universal, inclusive, and collaborative learning environments.

title: A Big Data-Oriented Robust Local-Region Image Watermarking Framework with Adaptive Geometric Correction
authors: Yantao Li, Ziqiao Liu, Bingwen Feng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: Robust and imperceptible image watermarking remains a long-standing challenge in digital document protection, particularly when images are degraded by geometric and photometric distortions during camera capture or reproduction. Existing deep-learning-based watermarking schemes such as StegaStamp and RIHOOP achieve good performance in the digital domain but fail to maintain robustness in real-world print–photograph scenarios due to misalignment and physical noise.This paper presents a novel local-region watermarking framework that jointly embeds and retrieves binary payloads under complex real-world distortions. The proposed system integrates two neural modules: an encoder that transforms an l-bit message into a visual residual embedded within an entropy-driven foreground mask, and a decoder that performs adaptive geometric correction followed by message reconstruction via up-convolution layers. A two-stage training strategy is adopted: the first jointly optimizes both networks through a differentiable perturbation layer simulating scaling, blur, color jitter, Gaussian noise, JPEG compression, and perspective distortion; the second freezes the decoder and fine-tunes the encoder with a compound perceptual–adversarial loss. Extensive experiments on document photographs demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves high watermark recovery accuracy and strong robustness against complex camera-based distortions. Compared with existing methods, it consistently delivers improved embedding efficiency, geometric stability, and visual imperceptibility, indicating its practical potential for large-scale document protection.

title: Bridging the Digital Divide: A Review on Enhancing Dark Skin Representation in Facial Recognition
authors: Risa Tioria Marlini Purba, Fadhil Hidayat
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Facial recognition technology has experienced remarkable growth across diverse sectors, ranging from security applications to consumer identity verification. However, this widespread adoption has exposed significant bias-related challenges that undermine the fairness and reliability of these systems. This paper presents a comprehensive examination of bias in facial recognition systems, exploring its manifestations, underlying causes, and societal implications. Through systematic analysis of current literature, we investigate demographic bias patterns, particularly those affecting underrepresented groups, and examine how algorithmic limitations and dataset imbalances contribute to discriminatory outcomes. Furthermore, this study evaluates existing mitigation strategies and proposes a framework for developing more equitable facial recognition systems. Our findings reveal that bias in facial recognition extends beyond technical limitations to encompass broader social and ethical considerations, necessitating multifaceted approaches to achieve genuine fairness and inclusivity.

title: Cooperative Two-warehouse Inventory Control Systems: A Multi-agent Perspective via Distributed Model Predictive Control
authors: Sutrisno, Joga Dharma Setiawan, Do Duc Thuan, Taycir Ben Abid
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Inventory control problems often face challenges due to unpredictable elements such as fluctuating demand and time delays of the arriving goods due to transhipments. When multiple storage facilities work in coordination, they can respond to such variability by satisfying the demands cooperatively. This study presents a control design framework for the integration of two cooperating warehouses via a multi-agent perspective by considering each warehouse is an agent. Built on a linear state-space representation, the model generalizes the traditional inventory control setup typically used for a single warehouse. It aims to identify optimal procurement quantities for each warehouse while maintaining inventory within acceptable safety thresholds set by management. A predictive control strategy is employed to determine purchasing decisions dynamically. Through a numerical simulation, the effectiveness of the approach is demonstrated, highlighting its ability to provide optimal decisions and stabilize inventory around the desired targets. This model offers practical value and is relevant for practitioners in supply chain and logistics operations.

title: Enhancing Network Simulation with Container: A Scalable and Real-Time Supported Platform
authors: Meiyu Liu, Bo Yi, Lei Huang, Xu Yan, Jiaying Wang, Ruoxuan Yan, Xingwei Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Network simulation systems are an important technology for future networks. Through this technology, service testing in real networks can be achieved in the context of complex network architectures and services. In order to study and simulate real networks and provide a near-optimal validation environment for regional actual networks, we have developed and implemented a network simulation platform using container technology. This platform primarily simulates various types of network topology nodes and container management. Additionally, it allows for realtime monitoring of various network performance metrics. Verification results demonstrate that the platform effectively simulates network virtual environments. It has three main advantages: (1) Constructing the network simulation architecture using containers. (2) Implementing a user-friendly and interactive page design through a front-end and back-end separation approach. (3) Real-time monitoring of various network performances and the collection and analysis of network metrics.

title: Weathering the Future: Hybrid Deep Learning for Daily Energy Price Prediction
authors: Saichand Raghupatrini, Surya Michrandi Nasution
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Electricity energy price forecasting faces significant challenges due to non-linearity, extreme volatility, and external factors like weather conditions. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning approach that combines weather classification with electricity price forecasting to address these challenges. The proposed system integrates weather data from five regions with electricity price data using ensemble learning with majority voting. For weather classification, CNN achieved the highest accuracy in regions 1-3 (0.97690, 0.91587, 0.95779), while Encoder-Decoder and LSTM performed best in regions 4 and 5 (0.94439, 0.98702). For electricity price forecasting, the CNN-LSTM model achieved superior performance with 1.53317, 4.36494, 2.08924, and 2.23039 for MAE, MSE, RMSE, and MAPE, respectively. It significantly outperforms traditional and other hybrid methods. The research demonstrates that hybrid models combining CNN for feature extraction and LSTM for temporal modeling effectively capture both short-term and long-term patterns in volatile energy markets. This study contributes to energy forecasting by providing a framework that integrates meteorological data with ensemble learning for improved daily electricity price prediction accuracy.

title: Bridging the Gap Between DLP and MDM a Real-Time on-Device Framework for Camera Security
authors: Sri Vidya Bommineni, Venkata Bhanu Pratap Bommineni, Shiridi Prasad Revanth Bhamidipati
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Corporate smartphones remain a hidden vector for data leakage, as cameras can instantly capture sensitive visual content beyond the reach of conventional Data Loss Prevention (DLP) and Mobile Device Management (MDM) tools. Existing DLP systems are reactive, while MDM policies often disable cameras entirely, creating a trade-off between security and usability. We present Synoptic, a real-time on-device defense framework that bridges this gap by analyzing camera frames at capture time to decide ALLOW or BLOCK before persistence. Given a camera frame (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${I}_{{t}}$</tex>) Synoptic computes a risk score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{R}\left(\boldsymbol{I}_{\boldsymbol{t}}\right)= \max \left\{\phi_{\{k w\}}(T), \phi_{\{r e\}}(T), \phi_{\{s e m\}}(T)\right\}$</tex> based on on-device OCR text <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T\left(I_{t}\right) \geq \tau$</tex> and lightweight policy detectors for keywords, regular expressions, and semantic cues. A capture is blocked if <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{R}\left(\boldsymbol{I}_{t}\right) \geq \boldsymbol{\tau}$</tex>, where thresholds and detector parameters are tuned exclusively on validation data. The system workflow is depicted in We compare Synoptic with 2 datasets: (1) 1000 images pilot (balanced sensitive/benign) and (2) a corpus of multi-sources with similar size (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 40 \mathrm{k}$</tex> strong against 200k weakly labeled): pulled together as (1) at corescorpus against (2) weakly at corescorpus (weakly wrong) with respect to COCO-Text, ICDAR'15, MLT'19, RVL-CDIP, and MIDV-500. In these environments, Synoptic provides a total F1-score of 96.1, average latency of 470 ms per capture, and minimal processor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<20) /$</tex> memory <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<150 \text{MB})$</tex> overhead, which proves that it can be applied in enterprise settings. The results have shown that content-aware and proactive visual data loss prevention is achievable on current mobile hardware without sacrificing user experience, and has given the field of enterprise mobile security a new direction.

title: Investigating Evolutionary Characteristics of Deep Learning Framework Bugs in the Large Language Model Era
authors: Kaishen Xie, Jiale Shen, Hao Zhou, Fuyang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: Since ChatGPT’s release in November 2022, deep learning frameworks like PyTorch and TensorFlow have undergone extensive updates to support the transition from traditional pre-trained models to large language models (LLMs). To investigate how framework bugs have evolved in this new era, we conducted a large-scale empirical study analyzing 552 bugs from these two frameworks spanning December 2022 to September 2025. We categorized bugs using a comprehensive taxonomy covering 13 root cause types and 6 symptom categories. Our findings reveal significant distributional shifts in bug characteristics driven by the adaptation to LLMs, providing actionable insights for framework testing and maintenance in the evolving landscape of deep learning infrastructure.

title: Hybrid Optuna-PSO Optimization Algorithm for CatBoost-Based Terahertz Channel Prediction
authors: Siqi Liu, Xiaoli Yang, Xue Wang, Qi Zang, Tianli Zhang, Yi Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Terahertz (THz) channel modeling for sixth-generation (6G) communications presents difficulties due to the complexity of data and the high costs associated with measurements. This study proposes a novel prediction method, termed OPCatBoost, which builds upon the Categorical Boosting (CatBoost) algorithm. The method is further enhanced through a two-stage hyperparameter optimization framework that integrates Optuna and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). The Tree-structured Parzen Estimator (TPE) algorithm in Optuna is first employed to perform a global search for hyperparameters. Subsequently, PSO refines the search around the best configurations identified. A distance-weighted strategy is incorporated to improve prediction accuracy for weak and distant channels. The model utilizes features that classify Line-of-Sight (LoS) and Non-Line-of-Sight (NLoS) to effectively capture the low-loss properties of LoS channels and the significant multipath effects of NLoS channels. Simulation results demonstrate that the OPCatBoost model achieves superior prediction performance compared to the baseline CatBoost, Optuna-CatBoost, and PSO-CatBoost models. This indicates its significant engineering applicability and highlights its effectiveness in THz channel prediction for 6G communications.

title: XRepair - Unifying Retrieval, Repair, and Evaluation for Explainable LLM-Based Vulnerability Fixes
authors: Alfred Amoah, Yan Liu
abstract: This paper presents XRepair, a web-based tool for automated and explainable software vulnerability repair. XRepair integrates retrieval, generation, and evaluation into a single interactive framework. It augments large language models (LLMs) with knowledge retrieved from CWE/CVE datasets through both embedding-based and graph-based strategies. XRepair enables repairs that are contextualized with vulnerability metadata and related examples. The tool provides end-to-end functionalities, including users inputting a CWE/CVE identifier and vulnerable code snippet, configuring the retrieval strategy and LLM, and receiving repair recommendations. Unlike patch-only systems, the recommendations consist of an issue description, explicit repair guidance, and concrete code diffs. To assess repair quality, XRepair incorporates an LLM-as-a-Judge evaluation framework that scores outputs across five criteria, namely relevance, completeness, correctness, identification of vulnerable code, and guidance. This design supports reproducible comparison of retrieval strategies and produces transparent recommendations. Built using both the strategy and factory design patterns, XRepair allows new retrieval algorithms and data sources to be added. The tool and documentation are publicly available with a tool for comparative evaluation and practical application of explainable vulnerability repairs. The demo for XRepair can be accessed at https://youtu.be/5IcGzNJXAY.

title: Impact of Practical Impairments on Adaptive Cascaded Channel Estimation for IRS-Aided mmWave Hybrid MIMO System
authors: Rajat Kumar, Rangeet Mitra, Atul Verma, Vidya Bhasker Shukla, K. Venkateswaran, Vimal Bhatia
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRS) combined with millimeter-wave (mmWave) MIMO systems represent a possible approach for improving coverage and capacity in next-generation wireless networks. Since mmWave signals suffer from severe path loss, beamforming is essential to concentrate energy in desired directions, and accurate channel state information (CSI) is required for efficient beamforming in these systems. However, standard channel estimation algorithms frequently assume Gaussian noise, which does not accurately reflect real-world conditions. This study analyses the performance of channel estimation under realistic impairments such as IRS phase noise, channel estimation errors, and impulsive noise. By incorporating these effects into the system model, we evaluate their impact on estimation accuracy and illustrate the limitations of ideal assumptions. The findings emphasise the importance of robust estimation methodologies and offer insights into the practical implementation of IRS-assisted mmWave systems.

title: Analysis of Smart Traffic Clearance System for Emergency Vehicle Services
authors: Rajesh G, Abuzar Yaseen, Pratheeksha B Shetty, Shalini A, Charanya S Reddy, Rachana D M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: One of the most significant challenges in modern urban growth is traffic jam management, especially during emergency situations. With increases in population and vehicle numbers to rise, emergency vehicles such as ambulances are delayed due to traffic signals that are static or unresponsive. To this end, our Paper represents a Smart Traffic Clearance System specifically. designed to identify and give priority to ambulances at traffic lights. The main purpose of this system is to allow for fast and smooth passage of ambulances via dynamic management of traffic lights in response to their approach. The system supplants conventional fixed-time signals with smart traffic control, which detects an incoming ambulance and immediately makes the associated signal green to create a free route. By applying this system at traffic intersections, ambulances are automatically allowed priority lanes, reducing delays during emergencies and even saving lives. It is very simple to extend or scale this model for other emergency services in smart city infrastructures. This system enhances significantly the urban traffic management by minimizing waiting time for ambulances, improving public safety, and enhancing overall efficiency and responsiveness of smart city traffic systems.

title: Flash Lamp Annealing for Wafer Debonding
authors: Nader Jedidi, Koen Kennes, Alice Guerrero, Alain Phommahaxay, Gerald Beyer, Eric Beyne
abstract: Debonding temporarily-bonded wafers at imec, is carried out using a 308nm Xenon Chloride excimer laser. This is indeed a well-established process in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 \mathrm{D} / 3 \mathrm{D}$</tex> packaging industry, since it comes with multiple advantages, particularly when compared to mechanical debonding. In this work, the authors investigate the implementation of Flash Lamp Annealing (FLA) as an alternative to laser ablation. Similarly to the latter, FLA debonding requires a release layer (RL) made of a light-absorbing material. The results of both heat diffusion modeling and debonding experimentation, show the importance of two key aspects for a successful debonding process: a highly visible light-absorbing material for a maximum effective fluence, and an insulating stack sandwiching the release layer.

title: Research of Flight Lifting Control Surfaces in a Wind Tunnel
authors: Oleksander Zhdanov, Olha Sushchenko, Victor Dehtiarov
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: The paper deals with the calibration of measuring instruments during experiments in the wind tunnel. A blowing model suspended in the wind tunnel is described. The kinematic diagram of the six-component balances is represented. The approach to calibrating the six-component balances is represented. Calibration errors for balances are calculated. To determine the aerodynamic loads directly is proposed. The transformation functions for measurement channels are represented by a linear regression model. Polynomial coefficients for calculating dependencies are obtained. The geometric angularity and inductive wash are estimated. The results of testing the wing model in the wind tunnel are shown. The appropriate dependencies for the averaged velocity head and static pressure coefficient are shown. The research results improve the aerodynamic characteristics of unmanned aerial vehicles of the considered type. The obtained results are of great importance for the research on the aerodynamic characteristics of aircraft of different types, particularly unmanned aerial vehicles.

title: CAMUS: Context-Aware Neural Mutation Selection
authors: Mingda Chen, Bo Wang, Youfang Lin, Jie M. Zhang
abstract: Mutation analysis is a fundamental technique in software engineering, playing a vital role in software testing and debugging. It introduces small artificial faults (mutants) into a program to simulate realistic defects. These mutants are systematically injected and then leveraged in downstream tasks such as mutation testing, mutation-based fault localization (MBFL), and mutation-based test case prioritization (MBTCP). However, all of these applications require generating and executing a large number of mutants, leading to substantial computational overhead. This scalability issue significantly limits the applicability of mutation analysis to large-scale software systems. To address this challenge, we propose CAMUS, a context-aware neural mutation selection approach. CAMUS models each mutation and its surrounding context as a graph enriched with AST hierarchy and type information. It then employs an encoder with self-attention mechanisms to produce embeddings, from which the selection probability of each mutant is predicted. We evaluate CAMUS on three downstream tasks, i.e., mutation testing, MBFL, and MBTCP, by comparing it with state-of-the-art mutation selection techniques, including the recent large language model DeepSeek-v3. Experiments are conducted on the Defects4J 2.0 and ConDefects benchmarks. Our results show that CAMUS consistently outperforms existing methods across a wide range of mutation retention rates. In mutation testing, CAMUS achieves over 95% accuracy using only 50% of the mutants. In MBTCP, it reaches near-optimal prioritization performance with as little as 5% of the mutants, demonstrating its strong efficiency and effectiveness.

title: A Time-Aware Competition Ranking Algorithm Based on Win-Rate Graph
authors: Dazhuang Sun, Xu Du, Shuhan Feng, Yitian Xiao, Hao Zhang, Na Jia, Jun Liu
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: This report presents the design and implementation of a competition ranking algorithm based on a win-rate graph framework, aimed at overcoming the shortcomings of conventional Elo-like systems.
