By building a global win-rate matrix from historical and live match data, the algorithm applies a PageRank-inspired iterative process to reflect each player's overall competitive standing, rather than relying solely on isolated match outcomes. Furthermore, the approach integrates time decay to reduce the influence of outdated matches and subgraph splitting to handle large-scale or sparsely connected data efficiently. The core innovation lies in adopting a real-time scoring mechanism—each player's dynamically updated TR (Table Tennis Rank) value directly determines the immediate adjustment of their rating after every match, thus capturing the momentum of recent performances. Empirical evaluations on both simulated and real-world table tennis data confirm the robustness and fairness of the algorithm: it mitigates rating manipulation, adapts well to new or rapidly improving competitors, and keeps the ranking landscape responsive. Overall, this integrated approach of win-rate graph iteration plus real-time score updates offers a scalable solution that maintains accuracy and stimulates frequent participation, making it suitable for modern competitive events both online and offline.

title: Laser Groove Replacement Through Low Stress Mechanical Sawing Optimization
authors: Wai Wai Lee, Ke Ming Zhou, Khaoula Mahzouli, Lilian Yang
abstract: PCM (Process Control Monitoring) in wafer is needed for testing and process monitoring purpose. While it can be available in either drop in or populated on the saw lane. The saw lane PCM tends to have defects like metal peeling & topside chipping after mechanical sawing. Mitigating these issues can be through laser groove process, where metal is first removed then followed by the mechanical saw. However, this additional process is cost and time consuming. Therefore, the purpose of this study is to provide a solution to replace the laser groove process through low stress mechanical sawing. This low stress mechanical sawing is achieved by optimizing the sawing blade selection and process parameters optimizations. The samples are subjected to visual inspection and capture the sawing output responses like kerf width and side wall crack measurements. The best run samples will be submitted to Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) for more thorough inspection on the die side wall surface morphology. At the same time, a control sample which runs with laser groove process will also be submitted to SEM as comparison. The results show using organic bond diamond blade as first cut to replace the laser grooving is feasible since it passed all the sawing output responses. The optimizations results shows that high spindle rotation speed, high blade thickness and high cut depth is in favor to produce the samples with good sawing performance and higher productivity. (Abstract)

title: Dynamic Path-Controllable Deep Unfolding Networks with Planet Optimization: A Scalable and Efficient Approach for Software Defect Detection
authors: Kevin N. Shah, Chandrasekhar Rao Katru, Umesh Kulkarni, Jay Gandhi, D. Kavitha, Ramya Maranan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Software defect prediction (SDP) is an important building block in enhancing software reliability through the identification of faulty modules prior to their deployment. In spite of progress made in applying deep learning to SDP, current models are prone to static learning processes, higher computation costs, and poor scalability for application to intricate software environments. These are the reasons behind elevated error rates, increased processing times, and decreased responsiveness in dynamic defect detection applications. Mitigating these challenges, a new framework, PathOpt-DUN, is introduced, which combines a Dynamic Path-Controllable Deep Unfolding Network (DPDUN) with Planet Optimization (PO) for optimal and precise defect detection. The approach utilizes software defect repositories under NASA's Metrics Data Program (MDP), with primary attributes including Lines of Code (LOC) and Halstead complexity measures. Preprocessing is applied via Global-Local Depth Normalization (GLDN) to provide consistent scaling and equally distributed features. The DPDUN model adaptively modulates learning pathways during training in response to changing patterns of defects, whereas PO optimizes hyperparameters to achieve the best convergence rate and prediction accuracy. Such dynamic coupling successfully balances defect detection accuracy with computational complexity. Experimental results on NASAMDP datasets show that PathOpt-DUN reaches 99.9% accuracy, 0.1% error rate, 99.7% fault detection rate, and lower computational time compared with state-of-the-art baseline models. These findings validate the superiority of PathOpt-DUN in providing robust defect prediction with greater reliability, scalability, and lower computational overhead. By overcoming major limitations of traditional methods, PathOpt-DUN becomes a useful and versatile solution for enhancing software defect detection in large-scale and dynamic software systems.

title: Development of Warpage Predictive Models Using Physics-Driven Simulation
authors: Wei Yu, Faxing Che, Yeow Chon Ong, Ling Pan
abstract: This paper presents the development of predictive models for warpage in NAND flash memory packages, leveraging physics-driven finite element (FE) simulations and advanced data analytics. Addressing the increasing complexity of NAND package designs—spanning densities from gigabytes to terabytes and incorporating up to 16 dies per package. The study underscores the necessity of accurate warpage predictions to meet JEDEC compliance and ensure product reliability. Traditional FE simulations, while robust, require significant time investments for optimization across diverse configurations. To accelerate and enhance predictive accuracy, the study utilizes a dataset of 171 simulation cases, reflecting real-world NAND package designs and encompassing a broad spectrum of structural and material factors. Two modeling methodologies were employed: JMP regression and artificial neural networks (ANN) deep learning, with analyses further stratified by silicon layout. The models incorporate a total of 30 numerical input factors and produce warpage predictions at 11 temperature points from 30° C to 260° C. By converting categorical attributes into quantitative variables, the research demonstrates improved model clarity and performance. The resulting predictive frameworks offer high-confidence, data-driven tools for rapid and reliable assessment of package warpage, providing critical support for future NAND package design and optimization.

title: Knowledge Distillation Based Lightweight Classification for Encrypted Traffic with Long Tailed Distribution
authors: Xiang Zhang, Tiankui Zhang, Yinlong Liu, Weiqiang Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Current deep learning-based methods for encrypted traffic classification face three key limitations: vulnerability to cryptographic protocol updates and high computational requirements, which restrict their practical applications. Additionally, the long tailed distribution of network traffic further degrades model performance, as rare classes are often overlooked during training. These issues highlight the need for more robust and balanced feature representation approaches. To address these challenges, this paper proposes the Network Traffic Data-efficient Transformer, a lightweight flow classifier that combines flow characteristic maps with a multi-head attention mechanism to capture cross-flow dependencies. Specifically, it introduces a knowledge distillation framework, mitigating bias from long-tailed data. Experiments on three real-world datasets show that our method exceeds 95% in both precision and the F1 score, significantly outperforming existing approaches.

title: Analysis of Students Level of Trust in the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Scientific Writing
authors: Lifia Yola Febrianti, Rina Patriana Chairiyani
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study aims to describe the application of artificial intelligence in writing scientific papers and assess students confidence in utilising artificial intelligence for this purpose. The method used in this research is a mixed approach. A quantitative method was employed to measure students perceptions using a closed-ended questionnaire. In contrast, the qualitative method was used to explore in-depth views through narrative analysis through an open-ended questionnaire. The questionnaires were distributed to 50 high school students who participated in a training session on writing scientific papers as the final assignment for the Indonesian language subject. In addition, the five indicators used to analyse the data of this study are Capability Trust, Reliability Trust, Security and Ethical Trust, Usability Trust, and Intention to Use. The results showed that students have a relatively high level of trust in the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in writing scientific papers, especially in the Capability Trust and Usability Trust indicators. However, students still consider ethical aspects and information security. The data variation indicates that although some students remain cautious or sceptical, the majority have begun to accept and trust the potential of AI in scientific writing.

title: Can Augmentation Help Small Survey Datasets: Machine Learning Models for Predicting Academic Impact of Social Media Addiction and Procrastination
authors: Tausia Tahsin Nuzum, Sumita Tasnim, Tasnia Rafique Simran, Nabarun Halder, Ashraful Islam, M. Ashraful Amin
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Social media use is prevalent among students and is associated with procrastination that can negatively affect academic performance. Detecting at-risk students from small, imbalanced surveys is challenging. This study examines whether synthetic data can improve prediction. Risk prediction was performed using seven classifiers: logistic regression (LR), random forest (RF), gradient boosting (GB), support vector machine (SVM) with RBF kernel, k-nearest neighbors (KNN), Gaussian Naive Bayes (GNB), and a multi-layer perceptron (MLP), on two small student surveys. Data augmentation techniques used are: Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique for Nominal and Continuous Features (SMOTENC), Gaussian Copula (GC), and Conditional Tabular Generative Adversarial Network (CTGAN). Without augmentation, SVM, GB, and RF achieved similar accuracy on Dataset-1 (≈0.93). GNB led Dataset-2 (0.907 accuracy), with RF, GB, SVM, and MLP following closely (≈0.89-0.90). Augmentation effects are model and dataset dependent and generally modest with Tree ensembles remaining largely stable. The model with the largest improvement was RF, gaining a +0.013 increase in F1 on GC for Dataset-2. The MLP declined with synthetic data. Hence synthetic augmentation might not be a universal remedy for small-scale datasets. Overall, lightweight models trained on a modest set of smartphone-like features can predict the risk of procrastination affecting academic performance and are suitable for on device use.

title: Orthogonalized Parallel Variable Step Size Nonlinear LMS Algorithm for Adaptive Digital Interference Canceller
authors: Zhongpu Cui, Songhu Ge, Yedi Xin, Jinling Xing
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Since the basis functions of the nonlinear model are mutually correlated, the fixed step size nonlinear least mean square (LMS) algorithm in the digital canceller suffers from slow convergence rate, high steady state error and instability. In this paper, an orthogonalized parallel variable step size nonlinear least mean square (VSSNLMS) algorithm is proposed to improve the convergence and cancellation performance of the adaptive digital interference canceller. First, the input basis functions are orthogonalized to decouple the correlation, which enables the individual channel coefficients estimation in parallel structure. Then, the orthogonalized basis functions are reorganized for adaptive process. Finally, an advanced VSSNLMS algorithm is induced to improve the trade-off between the steady state error and convergence rate The simulation results show that the proposed algorithm achieves effective cancellation and convergence performance improvement in present of high memory length and nonlinear order.

title: Regional Suitability Evaluation for Nuclear Power Plant Site Selection Based on IGA-SOM-G1
authors: Xuelong Tan, Zongyao Cui, Yuqing Xu, Xiangwei Zhao, Xianfei Huang, Chen Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Site selection for nuclear power plants is a comprehensive decision-making problem that involves multi-source data and complex criteria, with strict requirements on safety, social factors, technology, and economics. Addressing the limitations of traditional methods, such as strong subjectivity and weak ability to mine high-dimensional nonlinear data in regional suitability evaluations, this paper proposes an improved Self-Organizing Map (SOM) model integrated with an Immune Genetic Algorithm (IGA) and the G1 method (IGA-SOM-G1). The proposed method first establishes a dual-layer indicator system of “Negativity” and “Suitability,” utilizing Boolean logic method and GIS for initial regional screening. Then, the IGA optimizes the SOM connection weights to enhance training efficiency and clustering accuracy. Finally, the G1 method is introduced to determine subjective weights and compute the suitability score of neurons, integrating data-driven approaches with expert knowledge. Experimental results demonstrate that this method effectively integrates multi-source data, balancing objective patterns with subjective cognition, completing multi-level regional suitability evaluations, and reducing quantification errors to 0.7343. It provides reliable technical support for nuclear power plant site selection.

title: The Effects and Mechanism on Bump Reliability of Wafer Surface Protection by Bump Support Film
authors: Keisuke Shinomiya, Rikiya Kobashi, Reina Kainuma, Takuo Nishida, Tomoko Noguchi
abstract: In packages that connect dissimilar materials using solder bumps, such as Wafer Level Chip Size Package (WLCSP), the junction between the solder and other metals forms an intermetallic compound (IMC) layer, which tends to become a crack initiation point. To address this issue, we developed a Bump Support Film (BSF) that can be applied during the back-end process to protect the base of the solder bump. The BSF adopts a shape that robustly reinforces the bump base, protecting it from stress. This report details reliability tests (Ball shear test, Board-level reliability test) comparing packages with BSF to those without. The results show BSF enhances package reliability. Analysis of failure modes and stress analysis via simulation revealed this effect concentration points to shift.

title: Knowledge Hypergraph Link Prediction Based on Convolutional Neural Networks and Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Yifeng Xu, Xiaojie Chu, Jie Mei, Xiaoli Lin, Ying Song, Shuang Li, Jun Pang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Knowledge hypergraph is a knowledge graph with a hypergraph structure. Knowledge hypergraph link prediction aims to predict unknown entities or relations based on known entities and relations. However, existing models mainly have the following shortcomings: a) These models use random initialization methods during the initialization phase, ignoring the rich textual information in the hypergraph, resulting in low-quality initial embeddings for entities and relations; b) These models have limitations in capturing global structural features. To address these issues, we propose a knowledge hypergraph link prediction model based on convolutional neural networks and self-supervised learning, i.e., LPCH. Firstly, the model encodes the hypergraph textual information using BERT, integrating rich textual information into the embedding representations of entities and relationships. Secondly, based on a convolutional neural network, an improved graph attention network and a direction-aware hypergraph modeling representation method are proposed. Through using fewer attention layers and increasing the discriminability of entity features, we integrate the global features of the hypergraph into the entity representations. Additionally, a self-supervised learning framework based on mutual information maximization is proposed to optimize the model LPCH. It incorporates the existing link prediction in knowledge hypergraph combining pretrained model and hierarchical attention, called LPPH, as auxiliary tasks, and LPCH as the main task. By maximizing the mutual information between the embedding representations learned from the both tasks, it transfers the local modeling capability of the auxiliary tasks to the main task. Extensive experimental results on real datasets demonstrate the superior performance of LPCH compared with state-of-the-art baselines.

title: SRv6-Enabled Routing Optimization in LEO Satellite Networks: A Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Ci He, Haisong Sun, Zhaoxing Bian, Yasheng Zhang, Mingwu Yao
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: To address dynamic topology challenges of the Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks for the routing problem, this paper proposes a reinforcement learning (RL)-based routing method. Leveraging Segment Routing over IPv6 (SRv6), we design a two-layer architecture decoupling physical dynamics from routing decisions. A Deep Q-Network (DQN) model selects paths at source satellites considering predicted Maximum Link Utilization (MLU) across future time slots. Numerical results on an Iridium-like constellation (66 satellites) show that the proposed RL-based algorithm performs much better than the traditional heuristic method. The proposed method converges within 600 episodes, demonstrating robust optimization of stability, latency, and resource utilization in dynamic LEO environments.

title: QEC-LLM: Training Query-focused Extractive Compression Model with LLM-guiding for Open Domain QA
authors: Sijia Wang, Jy Duan, Xin Li, Haoguang Wen, Yizhuo Zhang, Chunming Liu, Zibo Yi
abstract: The current Retrieval-Augmented Generation(RAG) pipeline incorporates retrieved contexts as external knowledge to reduce hallucinations, but the large and redundant context makes it difficult for the model to identify key information. In Question-Answering (QA) tasks, large language models (LLMs) can identify query-focused information through extractive compression, simplify redundant long texts and improve the accuracy, but they are resource-intensive and less efficient. To ensure the accuracy of extractive compression and improve its efficiency, we propose a framework for query-focused extractive compression (QEC-LLM) that leverages the LLM’s knowledge and a queryguided mechanism to construct sample sets, effectively training a smaller student model. The student model sorts and scales paragraph sentences based on similarity scores for compression and performance optimization. In addition, since LLM may generate some information that is missing from the student model during the extraction process, our framework will add this supplementary information to the QA prompt and combine it with the extracted information to achieve generative augmentation. We conduct extensive experiments on QA datasets with different compression ratios. The results demonstrate that QECLLM significantly improves end-to-end QA performance and inference efficiency, while demonstrating strong generalization across different retrieval strategies.

title: DWTGS: Rethinking Frequency Regularization for Sparse-view 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Hung Nguyen, Runfa Li, An Le, Truong Nguyen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Sparse-view 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) presents significant challenges in reconstructing high-quality novel views, as it often overfits to the widely-varying high-frequency (HF) details of the sparse training views. While frequency regularization can be a promising approach, its typical reliance on Fourier transforms causes difficult parameter tuning and biases towards detrimental HF learning. We propose DWTGS, a framework that rethinks frequency regularization by leveraging wavelet-space losses that provide additional spatial supervision. Specifically, we supervise only the low-frequency (LF) LL subbands at multiple DWT levels, while enforcing sparsity on the HF HH subband in a self-supervised manner. Experiments across benchmarks show that DWTGS consistently outperforms Fourier-based counterparts, as this LF-centric strategy improves generalization and reduces HF hallucinations.

title: Correlation Between Thermal Cycling Ramp Rates and its Respective Solder Joint Reliability
authors: Yong Tat Chan, Kim-Sing Wong, Lian Geok Joen Oh, Daniel Yap, Phone Maw Hla, Bin Gu
abstract: In this study, the correlation between thermal cycling ramp rate and characteristic life are presented for 2 types of packages, namely Ball Grid Array (BGA) and Wafer-Level Chip Scale Package (WLCSP). Both fast and slow ramp rate thermal cycling were executed, following the JEDEC and AEC standards respectively. The results show that characteristic life of these packages increased significantly in the slower ramp rate test. Finite Element Analysis (FEA) was also performed to locate fatigue crack sites and predict characteristic life based on material properties and design. Cross-section and SEM imaging were employed to confirm the package failure mode. This paper serves to provide reliable results that support previous literature while providing insights into future standards and design guidelines.

title: Human Ants are Beneficial for Team Performance
authors: Iflaah Salman, Alvaro Francisco Gil, Juan Garbajosa, Peter A. Gloor
abstract: Background: A novel framework in management science introduces collaboration-style focused personas (bee, ant, and leech) built on human values and their relationship to team performance. Studies on human values in software engineering until now have only focused on adapting human values to the engineering process. The persona framework leverages how values may affect performance in software engineering. Aims: We investigate the relationship of individual and team performance with computationally detected personas based on the content of different communication channels. Method: We conducted an empirical study with 61 students studying software quality management and correlated personas with their exam-based individual and project-based team performance. Bee, ant and leech personas were detected by an AI tool using intra-team WhatsApp and MSTeams textual communication. Results: We found a significant positive correlation of ant persona and a significant negative correlation of leech persona with team performance. In addition, the detection of computational personas differed significantly between communication channels. Conclusions: Informal meetups among team members and team-building training can promote antness among team members by improving a sense of security and belonging for a healthy competitive environment. Additionally, multiple distinct communication data channels are essential for forming reliable results when employing AI and other computational tools.

title: Computational Profiling of Antibiotic Resistance Genes in the Indian Gut Microbiome
authors: Ankish Arya, Sankalp Patil, Prabhat Tripathi, Nidhi Dubey, Srishti Chakravarty, Pritish Varadwaj
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The human gut microbiome is a vital aspect of human health, but it also serves as a reservoir for antimicrobial resistance (AMR), which is a major global health burden. Antibiotic resistance genes (ARGs) are responsible for AMR and can spread through horizontal gene transfer (HGT), which increases the risk of resistance dissemination. Globally, studies have examined the gut resistomes, but there is limited knowledge about the indian population. In this study, we analysed 110 whole-genome sequencing (WGS) datasets of the Indian gut microbiome to investigate bacterial community structure and ARG diversity. After quality control and host genome decontamination, microbial profiling was performed using MetaPhlan4, assemblies were generated with more than 500 bp with MEGAHIT, and ARGs were identified using Prodigal and AMRFinderPlus. Statistical analysis showed a core set of bacterial species in the gut microbiome, with Faecalibacterium prausnitzii detected in 93.6% of individuals. Correlation analysis was done to identify 52 bacterial species which are significantly associated with ARG richness (FDR < 0.1), with Parabacteroides distasonis (ρ = 0.50, FDR = 3.3 × +10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup>) and Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron (ρ = 0.48, FDR = 4.5 × +10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup>) showing the strongest positive correlation. These results provide the first large-scale insights into the indian gut microbiome, which reflects species-level associations with resistance gene diversity. It establishes the baseline for understanding AMR in the indian population and give the importance of microbiome-informed therapeutics for public health.

title: MAJD: Intent-Aware Multi-Agent Framework for Jailbreak Defense
authors: Jigang Xie, Zhengfan Wang, Junchao He, Wenjing Jiao, Pengfei Dang, Yadong Zhou, Wei Zhang, Ming Fan
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown remarkable capabilities across diverse applications, enabling broad user interaction. Despite advances in AI alignment, their susceptibility to adversarial jailbreak attacks presents serious safety challenges. To mitigate these risks, we propose MAJD, a Multi-Agent Jailbreak Defense Framework that operates entirely at inference time without retraining. Each agent performs specialized tasks: the Coordinator routes inputs based on semantic ambiguity, the Mutation Agent compresses prompts with predefined rules, the Analyzer conducts intent analysis and response generation, and the Revalidation Agent performs secondary response classification. By incorporating rulebased mutation and intent-based reasoning, our framework identifies the core malicious intent in both single-turn and multi-turn jailbreak attempts. Experimental results across multiple attack methods (AutoDAN, GPTFuzzer, PAIR, and MHJ) and across multiple target models demonstrate that MAJD significantly improves detection success rates. These results validate the efficiency and generalizability of MAJD as a scalable jailbreak defense solution for LLM applications.

title: Development of Embedded Bridge Die Interposer Using Fan-Out Packaging for Heterogeneous Integration of NPUS and HBMS
authors: Jay Kim, In Soo Kang, Jung Won Lee, Sung Hyuk Lee, Han Ju Yu, Min Ji Lee, Seong Hwan Han, Jae Kyung Lee, Hailey Hwang, Jun Gi Kim, Chan Young Hong, Jade Park, Soo Hyun Kim, Myeong Jin Kim
abstract: The increasing demands of high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) systems have intensified the need for advanced heterogeneous integration of neural processing units (NPUs) and high-bandwidth memories (HBMs) within compact, high-density packages. Conventional silicon interposer technology, constrained by 300 mm wafer size and associated yield and cost limitations, poses significant challenges for scalable system-in-package (SiP) designs. In this work, nepes presents a novel Embedded Bridge Die Interposer architecture utilizing fan-out wafer-level packaging (FOWLP) to overcome these limitations. The proposed solution integrates three NPUs and six HBM stacks interconnected via embedded silicon bridges, supporting UCIe-compliant 45μm bump pitch for NPU-to-NPU links and 73μm bump pitch for HBM-to-NPU interfaces. Encapsulation within the fan-out structure enables efficient signal and power redistribution through fine-line redistribution layer (RDLs), while a co-developed Cu Post structure enhances vertical power delivery and thermal performance. Comprehensive process developmentencompassing precise die placement, molding, RDL formation, and dual-pitch bumping-has established a robust and scalable manufacturing flow. Additionally, parallel development of 600 mm × 600 mm panel-level-packaging (PLP) demonstrates scalability beyond wafer-level constraints. This work establishes fan-out Embedded Bridge Interposer technology as a high-performance, cost-effective, and scalable alternative for nextgeneration chiplet-based AI and HPC systems

title: Heat Release Tape Characterization for Panel Level Packaging
authors: Shanthini, Chen, Loo
abstract: Heat Release Tapes (HRT) plays a vital role in Panel Level Packaging (PLP) to secure temporary bonding during PLP processes. Comprehensive characterization of the tape focusing on mechanical, chemical, and environmental combinational performance can be done on tape to understand the tape characteristics securing process robustness. Hardness and deformation resistance reflecting tape's robustness under stress indentation test was conducted. Adhesive strength before and after heat budget was done to quantify tape bonding performance. The behavior of foaming particles was analyzed to understand the optimal activation temperature. Chemical compatibility test was conducted with various types of chemicals to test foaming particle behavior. This paper emphasizes the importances of characterization tests to mitigate production issues, enhance process stability and support the development for high-performance panel-level packaging solutions.

title: Instance Segmentation Method for Building Structural Defects Based on Improved YOLOv8 and Fine-Tuned SAM
authors: Yangyang Guan, Yujun Qi, Jiayuan Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: To solve the problem that ordinary building defect detection systems always ignore small problems, cannot handle complex backgrounds well, and have poor segmentation capabilities, we have developed a new method. This method cleverly combines an upgraded version of the YOLOv8 algorithm with a specially tuned SAM model. YOLOv8 performed well on our own dataset dedicated to finding building defects because it added a mechanism called Multi-scale Large Kernel Attention (MLKA), which made it better at understanding image content. It also uses Inverted Residual Mobile Block (iRMB) to better analyze characteristics. To make feature fusion smoother and improve positioning capabilities, this system also uses Multiorder Gated Aggregation (Moga) Block and DIoU loss. At the same time, we have specially trained the SAM model to make it better at recognizing defects such as cracks and spalling. The results showed that the upgraded YOLOv8 received a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P} \boldsymbol{@} 0.5$</tex> score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5. 5 \%}$</tex>, which was much better than the original model. Moreover, the improved SAM model also solves the problems of too much segmentation and erroneous segmentation. This detect-segment workflow, which links detection and segmentation, demonstrates that our new technology is both effective and reliable in complex building environments. Unlike conventional methods that address detection or segmentation separately, our approach unifies both tasks in a modular pipeline for end-to-end defect analysis.

title: MS-IPM: Multi-Scale Inverted Patch Mixing for Multivariate Time Series Forecasting
authors: Ming Xu, Jiangtao Niu, Xianglan Gao, Xu Zong
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Multivariate time series (MTS) forecasting, crucial for many applications, is challenged by complex dependencies and multi-scale dynamics. While Transformer models have advanced the field, they often face limitations in capturing local temporal patterns, explicitly modeling inter-variable interactions, and effectively integrating information across different time scales. To overcome these issues, we propose the Multi-Scale Inverted Patch Mixing (MS-IPM) model. MS-IPM employs a hierarchical architecture featuring three key components: (1) A multi-scale pyramid representation derived from down-sampling captures sequence dynamics at varying temporal resolutions. (2) An Intra-Scale Patching Transformer (IPT) module processes each scale independently, combining time series patching with a scale-specific inverted variate attention mechanism. This design captures local temporal patterns within patches while simultaneously modeling inter-variable interactions specific to that scale's context. (3) A Cross-Scale Decomposed Mixing mechanism operates on the feature representations output by the IPT modules; these features already encode intra-scale variate relationship information. This mechanism decomposes features into seasonal and trend components and performs directional mixing across scales to fuse spatio-temporal dynamics effectively. MS-IPM learns temporal dependencies implicitly without external timestamp features. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that MS-IPM achieves strong predictive capabilities across diverse forecasting horizons, spanning from short-to-midterm to long-term predictions, showing particular stability on high-dimensional datasets and offering a robust approach for MTS modeling.

title: Investigations on Mutual Effects of Electromigration and Thermal Cycling Failure of TSV Interconnects
authors: Tian Cheng, Jie Zhang, Junliang Lu, Jianguo Yang
abstract: In this work, a methodology is proposed for exploring the mutual effects of electromigration (EM) and thermal cycling (TC) failure of through silicon vias (TSVs) in three-dimensional integrated circuits (3-D ICs). To achieve this, the underlying failure mechanisms of EM and TC failure are reviewed to facilitate the identification of the key parameters that are affected by both failures. By inducing the impacts of one failure to another on these parameters, the two failures can be evaluated concurrently when subjected to the coupled high cur-rent and thermal cycling loads. Finite element analysis (FEA) is implemented, the complex interactions among diffusion, electric, thermal, and mechanical fields taking place in the two failures of TSV interconnects are illustrated. Lifespan assessments on independent EM failure, thermal cycling failure, and EM- TC failure co-evaluation are carried out, followed by discussions on the mutual effects in the co-evaluation.

title: MSHNet-CSCA: Fused Channel-Spatial Co-Attention and Adaptive SLS Loss for Infrared Small Target Detection
authors: Mingrui Li, Yajun Chen, Ruipeng Wang, Hao Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: In recent years, infrared small target detection is faced with challenges such as feature redundancy, scale deviation and training inefficiency due to small target, low contrast and strong background interference. In this paper, an improved method of channel-space collaborative attention and adaptive SLS loss is proposed to optimize MSHNet: the collaborative attention module integrates CA and SA at the model end, and reconstructs ResNet attention integration; The loss side extends SLSIOULOSS, fuses the two-scale difference and adds a convergence adaptive mechanism. Experiments on the IRSTD-1K dataset show that the proposed method improves the IOU and FA metrics of the basic MSHNet. The research shows that double-ended optimization can enhance the accuracy of weak feature capture and estimation, and provide an efficient scheme for complex background detection.

title: Gamified Edge-AI Interfaces for Smart Retail
authors: Rajshree Misara, Saurabh Mishra, Vrijendra Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Edge Artificial Intelligence (Edge-AI), is being more and more taken up by smart retail setups to aid real-time decision making, in aspects such as inventory, customer behavior, and overall operational activities. Although it is potentially viable, the reality of adoption, of such systems is constrained, in large part by lapses in user and engagement and mistrust. To address that gap, this paper presents a gamified, Edge-AI interface, which is built with interactive elements, such as, badges, missions and instant feedback, which, in turn, was supplemented by the Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM), and subsequently, by analysing the results using Artificial Neural Network, ANN. The conjoined design assists in revealing both, the linear and non-liner patterns of the influence of gamification, trust, user engagement, and system adoption intention. The insights can serve as a useful baseline towards creating more user friendly, user centric Edge- AI devices that meet the real time retail setting.

title: S-Band Load-Modulated Balanced Amplifier Based on Hybrid Asymmetric Architecture
authors: Peijun Wang, Ran Chu, Linlin Sun, Jia Liao
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: This paper presents an in-depth theoretical investigation of novel load-modulated balanced amplifiers. To address the efficiency degradation in power back-off operation, an asymmetric architecture is proposed by implementing differential gate voltage configurations, which enables sequential activation of dual transistors in the balanced amplifier. This methodology induces multiple efficiency peaking points in the power amplifier, thereby significantly enhancing back-off efficiency. Furthermore, to resolve the carrier power amplifier over-saturation phenomenon, a three-way load modulation technique is systematically analyzed and developed, effectively alleviating the carrier power amplifier over-saturation while maintaining system linearity and operational stability. Building upon the aforementioned technique, this work implements a hybrid asymmetric load-modulated balanced power amplifier (H-ALMBA) operating over the 2.4-3.0 GHz frequency band. Module measurements demonstrate that the implemented power amplifier achieves a saturated output power of 41.4-42.8 dBm with a peak drain efficiency of 66%-70% across the operational bandwidth. Notably, it maintains 52%-59% and 50%-53% drain efficiencies at 6-dB and 10-dB power back-off levels, validating its superior efficiency characteristics at high power back-off regimes.

title: Efficient Breast Cancer Detection Using a Dilated Convolutional Network with Walk-Spread-Based Optimization
authors: Natrayan L, S. Dhivya Devi, Indumathi Koppisetty, Swapnil M Parikh, Rajanish Kumar Kaushal, M. SivaramKrishnan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Successful breast cancer prognosis hinges upon timely and precise imaging diagnostics. Conventional methodologies, augmented by standard neural networks, frequently falter due to heterogeneous growth patterns across diverse tumor phenotypes and prohibitive energy expenditure required for iterative learning cycles. This study advances a streamlined detection architecture wherein a Dilated Convolutional Network (DCN) is synergistically coupled to Walk-Spread-Based Optimization (WSBO) to foreground pertinent histopathological features while concurrently calibrating learning parameters. Embedded dilated filters sample multiple receptive fields and merge pixelwise indicators with wide-ranging spatial context, thereby preserving pixel-level histological fidelity and subsampling apparent and latent tumor signs at heterogeneous scales. Simultaneously, the WSBO mechanism modulates the encroachment-versus-innovation ratio of search agents toward meta-parameter convergence, thereby avoiding overfitting through adaptive, cycled perturbation and resampling. Rigorous validation against publicly curated, multicentric breast cancer archives yields statistically significant gains in classification efficacy, diminished classification bias over undetected micro-calcifications, and acceleration of the convergence surface. This evidence substantiates the capacity of the composite DCN-WSBO pipeline to furnish clinicians with robust, computationally economical breast cancer stratification manifestly suited for integration within hospital-grade imaging and episodic, edge-based diagnostic modalities.

title: Evaluating Kernel-Based Similarity Measures for Demographic Face Clustering Using VGGFace2 Embeddings
authors: Nur Nadiah Asyikin Samruzlan, Raseeda Hamzah, Khyrina Airin Fariza Abu Samah, Gao YouSheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Face clustering is a fundamental problem in computer vision and biometric systems, where the choice of similarity measures critically influences performance. Traditional metrics such as Euclidean distance are simple and efficient, but often fail to capture the complex, non-linear relationships present in high-dimensional facial embeddings. This research investigates the effectiveness of Kernel-based Similarity Measures (KSM), specifically the Radial Basis Function (RBF) kernel and the Polynomial kernel, compared to the Euclidean baseline for demographic face clustering. Using a balanced subset of the VGGFace2 dataset categorized by age and gender, embeddings were extracted with a pre-trained ResNet50 model, and similarity matrices were computed for each measure. The results demonstrate that kernel functions significantly outperform Euclidean distance, with the RBF kernel achieving the highest accuracy (86.4%), followed by the Polynomial kernel (83.7%). These findings confirm that kernel functions provide more robust and discriminative similarity representations, making them well-suited for demographic face clustering tasks.

title: Novel Rod-of-Scoops Control to Lower Sediment Disturbance in Deep Sea Mining
authors: Jia Ming Lim, Patrick W. C. Ho, Chee Pin Tan
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Increasing demand for critical metals has renewed interest in deep-sea polymetallic nodules, but current hydraulic collectors risk ecological impact through direct benthic damage and sediment plumes. To bridge the gap between high-throughput but disruptive collectors and low-impact but slow AUVs, Rod-of-Scoops (RoS) is proposed: a synchronised dual-winch control mechanism to steer 2-DoF suspended scooping rods along trajectories designed to minimise sediment disturbance. This study aims to use 2D trajectory simulations, demonstrate precise time-accurate synchronised dual-winch control, and establish design envelope for potential commercial RoS model using a simplified scaling analysis benchmarked against the ~75 kg•s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> ore throughput typical of industrial collectors. The study was conducted in three phases: (1) mining throughput under ideal conditions was estimated with 2D visualisations, and 2D kinematic model was used to simulate rod suspension dynamics; (2) bench control was designed to test and demonstrate time-accurate rod control; and (3) proof-of-concept of RoS was consolidated using an aquarium in lab setting. A synchronised DC-motor drive with 1 kHz bang-bang quadrature encoder feedback maintained less than 0.1 cm mid-path error and 0.2 cm endpoint error in bench testing, while aquarium trials in MESH 30–100 sand produced qualitatively background-level plumes that settled within 24 hours. Automated count-based pickup yield was 0–1%; post-hoc analysis suggests off-plane sway and scoop incidence angle rather than X-Y tracking precision as the primary yield limiters. These results quantify controller performance, highlight the qualitative nature of the current plume assessment, and reveal key mechanical upgrades required to pursue lower-plume selective harvesting at scale, positioning RoS as a conceptual pathway toward lower-impact deep sea mining pending quantitative plume and yield validation.

title: ProxyPrints: From Database Breach to Spoof, A Plug-and-Play Defense for Biometric Systems
authors: Yaniv Hacmon, Keren Gorelik, Gilad Gressel, Yisroel Mirsky
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Fingerprint recognition systems are widely deployed for authentication and forensic applications, but the security of stored fingerprint data remains a critical vulnerability. While many systems avoid storing raw fingerprint images in favor of minutiae-based templates, recent research shows that these templates can be reverse-engineered to reconstruct realistic fingerprint images, enabling physical spoofing attacks that compromise user identities with no means of remediation. We present ProxyPrints, the first practical defense that brings cancellable biometrics to existing fingerprint recognition systems without requiring modifications to proprietary matching software. ProxyPrints acts as a transparent middleware layer between the fingerprint scanner and the matching algorithm, transforming each scanned fingerprint into a consistent, unlinkable alias. This transformation allows biometric identities to be revoked and replaced in the event of a breach, without affecting authentication accuracy. Additionally, ProxyPrints provides organizations with breach detection capabilities by enabling the identification of out-of-band spoofing attempts involving compromised aliases. We evaluate ProxyPrints on standard benchmark datasets and commercial fingerprint recognition systems, demonstrating that it preserves matching performance while offering strong security and revocability. Our open-source implementation includes tools for alias generation and deployment in real-world pipelines, making ProxyPrints a drop-in, scalable solution for fingerprint data protection.

title: BiFuzz: A Two-Stage Fuzzing Tool for Open-World Video Games
authors: Yusaku Kato, Norihiro Yoshida, Erina Makihara, Katsuro Inoue
abstract: Open-world video games present a broader search space than other video games, posing challenges for test automation. Fuzzing, which generates new inputs by mutating an initial input, is commonly used to uncover issues. In this study, we proposed BiFuzz, a two-stage fuzzer designed for automated testing of open-world video games, and investigated its effectiveness. The results revealed that BiFuzz mutated the overall strategy of gameplay and test cases, including actual movement paths, step by step. Consequently, BiFuzz can detect character stuck issues. The tool and its video are at https://github.com/ Yusaku-Kato/BiFuzz and https://www.youtube.com/watch? $\mathbf{v}=$ VOrHfnLJSbk. Index Terms-open-world video game, fuzzing

title: Explainable Machine Learning for Structural Materials: SHAP-Enhanced Ensemble Prediction of Concrete Compressive Strength
authors: Tianci Cui, Yixian Peng
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Accurate prediction of concrete compressive strength is critical for structural design and quality assurance, yet traditional methods struggle with complex non-linear relationships between mixture proportions and mechanical properties. This study proposes a two-layer ensemble learning framework integrated with SHAP-based interpretability for transparent strength forecasting. The architecture employs seven diverse base learners (Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors, Deep Neural Network, Stacking, CatBoost, Support Vector Machine, Gaussian Process Regression) in the first layer, followed by a LightGBM meta-learner that adaptively synthesizes predictions to achieve superior accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.948, RMSE=4.161 MPa). SHAP analysis quantifies global feature importance, identifying age (mean |SHAP|=1.5) and water content (mean |SHAP|=1.2) as dominant factors, while waterfall plots provide instance-level explanations for mixture optimization. Experimental validation on 1,030 concrete specimens demonstrates the framework's dual capability of delivering accurate predictions and actionable engineering insights, advancing interpretable AI applications in structural materials design.

title: Simulation-Experiment Correlation of Defect Evolution in FCVA and HCA Deposited ta-C Films for Hermetic Encapsulation in Advanced Electronics Packaging
authors: Li Ying, Tan Yik Kai, Lum Ya Woon, Jason Cheah Jun Wei, Jacob Lim Song Kiat, Zhao Sheng Fu, Chen Yutong, Liu Qing, Gan Chee Lip, Tay Beng Kang, Shi Xu, Eric Phua Jian Rong
abstract: The advancement of semiconductor, MEMS, and optoelectronic devices demands ultra-thin encapsulation layers that combine hermeticity with resilience under aggressive thermal and humidity conditions. Tetrahedral amorphous carbon (ta-C) coatings, owing to their high sp<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> bonding fraction and chemical stability, have emerged as promising candidates; however, their barrier performance is strongly determined by the deposition pathway. Here, ta-C films prepared by filtered cathodic vacuum arc (FCVA) and high current arc (HCA) methods are systematically compared to uncover how microstructural differences govern defect generation, moisture transport, and long-term durability. By integrating AFM mapping, diffusion simulations, salt immersion exposure, and mechanical evaluation, we demonstrate that FCVA deposition yields dense, pinhole-suppressing coatings with enhanced barrier integrity, whereas HCA growth introduces macroparticles and heterogeneity that compromise hermeticity. This process-structure-performance framework provides mechanistic insight into the reliability of carbon-based barriers and identifies FCVA derived ta-C as a benchmark route for robust device encapsulation.

title: In-Network Computing Based Distributed User Plane Service Deployment
authors: Zhikang Yang, Luhan Wang, Bingxuan Li, Haiwen Niu, Yao Li, Zhaoming Lu
publish: 2025 9th IEEE International Conference on Network Intelligence and Digital Content (IC-NIDC)
abstract: User Plane Function (UPF), which is responsible for data forwarding in the core network, faces significant challenges under traditional monolithic architectures due to their rigidity and limited performance scalability. Benefiting from the development of In-Network Computing (INC), the UPF can leverage INC's computational capabilities to enhance the data forwarding performance of the user plane service chain. However, how to efficiently deploy the UPF chain onto distributed INC devices remains to be explored. To address this gap, this study proposes a novel co-design framework that integrates UPF functional decomposition with INC, and introduces a Hybrid QuantumInspired Genetic Algorithm with Adaptive Resource Allocation (QIGA-ARA) to jointly optimize UPF deployment and INC resource allocation, aiming to enhance both system efficiency and user experience. This two-stage solution first employs a quantum-inspired genetic algorithm for deployment mapping, followed by a multi-priority resource allocation strategy to refine the assignment. Simulation results demonstrate that QIGA-ARA consistently achieves near-optimal performance with significantly faster convergence compared to traditional methods.

title: D-Trace: Diffusion-Based Fingerprinting for Indoor Tracking in Single-Gateway LoRa Networks
authors: Xirui Dong, Runqun Xiong, Chen Ouyang, Jing Cheng, Zhuqing Xu, Jiahui Jin, Junzhou Luo

title: Retraction Notice: Detection of retinal hemorrhage in fundus images using ANFIS classification and FCM with cuckoo search optimization algorithm
authors: L. Godlin Atlas, Kumar Parasuraman
publish: 2017 2nd International Conference on Communication and Electronics Systems (ICCES)
abstract: In the fundus image screening framework, reliable detection of retinal hemorrhage is required. Retinal hemorrhages are created by diabetic retinopathy and hypertension. In our study propose to build up the retinal discharge fundus images by the method for pre-processing, feature extraction, classification, and segmentation by different strategies and methodologies. By utilizing ANFIS the affected and non-affected images are accurately divided and the affected images are segmented by FCM-CS optimization methods. From that, the accuracy, specificity, sensitivity, FAR and FRR are broke down for that segmented images. The outcomes demonstrate that the proposed system achieves the greatest accuracy and performs optimal value contrasted with existing methodologies.

title: Beyond the Accelerator: A Full-Stack HW/SW Co-Design Analysis for Recommendation System Inference
authors: Zhanqiu Hu, Mark Zhao, Zhiru Zhang, Udit Gupta
abstract: Modern recommendation engines power most online interactions through multi-stage pipelines at massive scale. These pipelines combine user and item feature processing, approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) retrieval, and deep neural network (DNN) ranking. However, optimizing only the DNN ranking stage leaves substantial bottlenecks in data movement and non-DNN stages, leading to suboptimal performance. This work introduces ReCoOpt, a modular framework for exploring the joint hardware and software design space of end-to-end recommendation inference pipelines. By profiling and tuning retrieval, feature fetching, and ranking across heterogeneous hardware, we show that non-DNN stages dominate latency and require co-design. Using public MovieLens datasets, ReCoOpt identifies configurations that improve hit rate by 0.05 under a fixed latency budget, double throughput, or reduce latency by 40%. Our findings highlight that balanced co-design rather than accelerator-only optimization is key to future scalable recommendation systems.

title: Beyond Heat Dissipation: Optimizing Diffusion Models in Frequency Domain_supp1-3666860.pdf
authors: Yifan Zhao
abstract: The majority of standard diffusion models employ pixel-wise degradations while neglecting multi-scale characteristics of images. Recently, generalized diffusion models with Positive Semi-definite Degradations (PSD), such as heat dissipation and blurring, have been proposed to solve it, but suffering from problems of low generation quality due to incomplete optimization analysis and non-adaptiveness to the training process and different data distributions with hand-crafted and fixed inductive biases. In this paper, we present a comprehensive theoretical analysis of the optimization process in frequency domain for PSD-based generalized diffusion models, which implies the forward process of PSD frequency domain non-isotropic degradation implicitly acting on the inductive biases of the Variational Lower Bound non-isotropic weighting in the optimization reverse process. Based on this insight, we propose the Frequency Inductive Biases Bootstrapping Optimization (FIBBO) method, which parameterizes the forward process and learns distinct frequency degradation-generation trajectories iteratively. To tackle the problem of PSD hand-crafted and fixed inductive biases, FIBBO dynamically modifies the non-isotropic Gaussian kernel of the forward degradation process so that the inductive biases introduced can be adjusted adaptively during training. Experiments on public datasets show that FIBBO makes significant improvements in the generation quality of PSD-based generalized diffusion models. The code will be publicly available.

title: 3-D Electron-Beam Profiling and Modulation by Precharged BEOL Metal Layers in FinFET CMOS Technology
authors: Ho-Ting Chao, Wei Chang, Jiaw-Ren Shih, Chrong Jung Lin, Ya-Chin King, Yue-Der Chih, Jonathan Chang
abstract: This work presents a 3-D electron-beam (e-beam) detector array utilizing deep ultraviolet (DUV)-induced surface precharging to control electron injection behavior. The sensing nodes are implemented using back-end-of-line (BEOL) metal layers in advanced CMOS ICs, enabling passive operation and nondestructive readout. DUV illumination generates positive surface charges on the top metal layer, forming built-in electrostatic fields that modulate electron penetration depth and lateral trajectory. The experimental results show that precharged surface electrodes enhance electron penetration by several tens of a micrometers in depth. Selective DUV exposure further creates lateral electric fields, leading to controlled redistribution of injected electrons. In addition, a precharged metal ring achieves lateral beam focusing. This approach enables on-wafer spatial modulation of e-beams without external bias, offering a scalable and flexible solution for future lithography processes.

title: E2Diff: An Efficient Enhanced Diffusion Model for Blind Face Restoration
authors: Rong Chen, Yu Wang, Gencheng Wang, Daqin Li
abstract: Blind face restoration (BFR) is crucial to improve the quality of low-quality facial images. Although diffusion model-based methods achieve reliable performance in BFR tasks, particularly under unknown and complex degradation, they suffer from high computational complexity, which results in unrealistic details in the face images generated at high frequencies. To address these limitations, a new efficient enhanced diffusion network, known as E2Diff, is proposed. Firstly, a new denoising generator with a lightweight residual module is introduced, which considerably reduces the number of parameters by 40% compared to the standard U-Net model. Secondly, to improve high-frequency face features while maintaining facial structures, a diffusion framework is incorporated into the design of a high-frequency feature-generating module. Lastly, the diffusion probability model (DPM) solver is integrated into E2Diff, which significantly increases the model sampling frequency. Extensive evaluations on severely degraded face datasets have revealed that E2Diff outperforms current state-of-the-art approaches based on generative adversarial networks, Transformers, and diffusion models in both qualitative and quantitative terms. E2Diff achieves competitive PSNR and SSIM scores while obtaining the best perceptual metrics (LMD = 5.91, FID-G = 16.62, FID-F = 39.87). The results of this study revealed that E2Diff effectively combines visual realism, restoration integrity, and computing efficiency, thereby providing a viable solution for real-world BFR applications. The code is available at https://github.com/windcr/E2Diff.

title: MiniExtractor-PHyMiPS: A Parameter-Efficient Pipeline with Compact Feature Extractor, MI, and PCA for Breast Cancer Detection
authors: Mohd Saqib Ansari, Mohd Hasan, Omar Farooq, Syed Atiqur Rahman, Md Shahbaz Hussain, Yassine Himeur
abstract: Breast ultrasound (BUS) is an essential imaging technique for the early detection of breast cancer, particularly in women with dense breast tissue. However, the high interclass similarity and variability in tumor morphology pose significant challenges for automated classification. To address this, MiniExtractor-PHyMiPS, a hybrid pipeline that integrates deep learning for complex feature extraction and conventional machine learning for robust classification, is proposed. This hybrid pipeline consists of a compact convolutional neural network (CNN)-based MiniExtractor to identify complex features from BUS images. Subsequently, mutual information (MI) analysis is used to compute feature relevance scores, and the top-ranked features are selected. These selected features are then transformed using principal component analysis (PCA) to reduce the dimensionality of the feature space before classification. A support vector machine (SVM) classifier is used for the final decision-making. Extensive experiments were conducted on two publicly available datasets: the Breast Ultrasound Images (BUSI) dataset and the Ultrasound Breast Images for Breast Cancer (UBIBC) dataset. The proposed pipeline achieved 96.90% accuracy on BUSI and 95.81% on UBIBC, surpassing several state-of-the-art deep learning models, including ResNet50, DenseNet201, and XceptionNet, while using only 448 parameters in the MiniExtractor feature extractor. Furthermore, the proposed hybrid pipeline demonstrated consistent bidirectional cross-dataset generalization between BUSI and UBIBC, while maintaining a compact end-to-end deployment footprint. Although the MiniExtractor itself contains only 448 parameters, the complete deployed pipeline—including PCA transformation (1870 parameters) and the RBF-SVM classifier (17,256 parameters with 493 support vectors)—results in a total of approximately 20k parameters (19,574). In addition, the end-to-end inference complexity is approximately 0.884 MFLOPs per sample, with an average CPU inference time of 0.0478 ms per image, which is substantially lower than conventional end-to-end deep learning models. The findings of this study reveal the high efficacy and robustness of the MiniExtractor-PHyMiPS in achieving superior performance of automated breast cancer detection via ultrasound images, making it well-suited for real-world clinical applications, including deployment in low-resource settings and mobile diagnostic platforms.

title: A Hierarchical Framework for Large-Scale Generation of Multi-Dimensional Optimized Pulse Patterns for SHC-PWM
authors: Igor Araus, Cristobal Cortés, Javier Pereda, Felix Rojas
abstract: The practical adoption of multidimensional Optimized Pulse Patterns (OPP) is hindered by the massive computational cost of generating their offline Look-Up Tables (LUTs). This article introduces a hierarchical framework that systematically dismantles this computational bottleneck. The method leverages a data-driven sensitivity analysis to guide an adaptive solver, which strategically applies robust global solvers only to the few highly sensitive regions of the solution space while rapidly solving the vast majority of points with a fast local solver. This adaptive policy, which includes robust fallback mechanisms, is shown to be both fast and highly reliable. Comprehensive benchmarks, through experimental validation, show the framework can generate LUTs with over 11 million points, achieving a success rate greater than 99.5 %. For a baseline case, it is approximately 24 times faster and more robust than a state-of-the-art heuristic method. The proposed method provides an efficient and systematic pathway for the practical implementation of advanced programmed PWM techniques in high-power converters. The framework is presented and experimentally validated for the challenging case of multi-dimensional Selective Harmonic Control (SHC-PWM), which requires precise tracking of harmonic magnitude and phase references.

title: ARGfore: A multivariate framework for forecasting antibiotic resistance gene abundances using time-series metagenomic datasets_supp4-3667074.pdf
authors: Liqing Zhang

title: A generative model for spatial complex networks: applications to power grids and brain networks
authors: Alessandra Corso, Lucia Valentina Gambuzza, Ludovico Minati, Mattia Frasca
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: In this work, we propose a generative model for spatial networks. The central idea of the model is to use empirical data from real-world spatial networks as a foundation for the network structure that is constructed starting from a set of disconnected nodes and assigning the node positions and the links between them based on key topological and spatial features. In particular, for each node, the number of links to add is determined by sampling values from the degree distribution of real-world networks, while the nodes to connect with are randomly selected based on a probability that depends on the distance between the node positions. For the node positions, we propose either leaving them unconstrained or applying one of four physical constraints with varying levels of strictness. The model can be applied to both two- and three-dimensional spaces. As an example of the former, we consider power grids, while for the latter, we study brain networks. In both cases, we show that the model can be effectively applied to generate surrogate networks, providing a useful tool when spatial network data is limited.

title: A Hardware-Efficient QR Algorithm and Its VLSI Architecture for Eigenvalue Decomposition of Symmetric Matrices
authors: Vishnu P S, Jobin Francis, Subrahmanyam Mula
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: This article proposes a novel hardware-efficient QR algorithm and its VLSI architecture to compute the eigenvalue decomposition (EVD) of symmetric matrices. By replacing the computationally expensive matrix multiplication with matrix transposition, the proposed architecture achieves a significant reduction in latency as well as area compared to the conventional QR architecture. We prove the convergence of the proposed algorithm through mathematical analysis and study its convergence rate through numerical simulations. We implemented both conventional and proposed QR architectures on both field-programmable gate array (FPGA) and application specific integrated circuit (ASIC) platforms. Implementation results on the ZCU104 Ultrascale+ FPGA demonstrate a 51% reduction in computation time and a 50% decrease in DSP usage with the proposed algorithm. Furthermore, ASIC synthesis results also demonstrate a significant reduction in computation time compared to the conventional algorithm.

title: Attention-Aided Generative Semantic Coding for Privacy-Preserving Traffic Status Monitoring
authors: Qi Jiang, Zuxing Li, Nishan Wu, Chao Wang, Ming Xiao, Nguyen Huu Trung
abstract: Traffic status monitoring is essential to support intelligent transportation, and demands for highly efficient and secure processing and transmission of massive data. Inspired by recent information-theoretic study, the traffic status monitoring is formulated as a privacy-preserving semantic coding problem in this work to characterize the fundamental trade-off among data compression, traffic image reconstruction, segmentation of traffic participants, and preservation of pixel regions of traffic participants. Grounded on the theoretic results, a novel generative semantic coding scheme is further developed for data-driven semantic coding design with the aid of attention mechanisms to capture correlations between sparse information. Experiments on the RCooper dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed generative semantic coding scheme and its advantages over the benchmark compression methods.

title: Automated AI-Driven Report Generation for Chest X-Ray Interpretation in Teleradiology: Towards Enhanced Personalized Healthcare
authors: Gitarja Sandi, Suhono Harso Supangkat, Ermawati
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The rising global demand for radiological services, especially in the interpretation of chest X-rays (CXR), has imposed considerable strain on radiologists, resulting in heightened workloads and possible delays in diagnosis. Teleradiology has become an essential solution for mitigating geographical disparities and meeting the rising demand; however, it encounters challenges in sustaining reporting efficiency and accuracy, particularly with the escalating volume of CXR examinations. This study presents an innovative AIdriven method for the automated generation of reports in teleradiology, aimed at optimizing CXR interpretation and improving diagnostic precision. This research aims to develop and assess a deep learning system that combines advanced image analysis with natural language processing (NLP) techniques to autonomously produce detailed radiology reports from chest X-ray (CXR) images in a simulated teleradiology environment. The system utilizes a convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture to accurately identify and localize radiological findings in chest X-ray (CXR) images, in conjunction with a transformer-based natural language processing (NLP) model to generate coherent and clinically relevant text reports based on the identified findings. The CNN component underwent training and validation using a substantial and varied dataset of annotated chest X-ray images, which included numerous pathologies such as pneumonia, pneumothorax, consolidation, effusion, and cardiomegaly.

title: Mitigation of Cu Nodule Formation in High Open Area Products for Electroplated Cu RDL Applications
authors: Sheng-Wei. Lin, Chung-Hsiang Wang, Wei-Chun Lin
abstract: In the customer's 200 mm RDL applications, Cu nodules were observed by 2D wafer inspection after photoresist (PR) strip and led to an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>80 \%$</tex> yield loss in wafer. Our investigation revealed that the affected device has a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 5 X}$</tex> larger open area (OA) and higher current setting than the generic devices. A large OA implies that a sufficient supply of Cu ions must be provided within a unit time to facilitate the reduction reaction. However, we found that one cell's plating gap (PG), the distance between wafer and anode, was wider than another and without the compression made by a smaller plating gap, the flow within the gap was decreased. This reduction in ion exchange rate leads to the partial reduction of hydrogen ions into hydrogen gas, thereby affecting the electroplating process. In this study, by adjusting the plating cup' concentricity and conducting various splits with different ASD and PG values, it was confirmed that Cu nodule formation is more likely to happen under conditions of high OA/current output with insufficient mass transfer.

title: Towards Optimized Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Amar Wissam Houria, Azouaou Faiçal
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Theoretical and Applicative Aspects of Computer Science (ICTAACS)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as a transformative paradigm for enhancing Large Language Models (LLMs) by grounding responses with external, non-parametric knowledge to enhance outputs and reduce hallucinations; however, the naïve retrieve-then-read approach faces significant limitations, necessitating optimization across the entire pipeline. This comprehensive survey delves into the diverse landscape of these optimization methodologies, moving beyond traditional approaches to explore innovative strategies that address critical challenges. We propose a four-stage taxonomy (Indexing, Retrieval, Augmentation, Generation) to structure the landscape of RAG optimization techniques, providing a detailed examination of chunking strategies, embedding techniques, query expansion methods, context compression algorithms, and prompt engineering approaches. Furthermore, we investigate recent innovations such as adaptive retrieval, hybrid architectures, and joint optimization techniques that synergistically combine improvements across multiple stages for enhanced end-to-end performance. Based on our synthesis of the existing literature, significant challenges remain in the development of effective RAG systems. Specifically, further research is needed to establish robust evaluation metrics, address computational limitations, and adapt these systems for use in diverse domains.

title: Inclusive MOOCs: Surveying Accessibility Needs and Designing the RecMOOC4All Aggregator
authors: Salwa Mrayhi, Mohamed Koutheair Khribi, Mohamed Jemni
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on ICT &amp; Accessibility (ICTA) co-located with 2025 2nd International Conference and Forum on Assistive Technologies for Special Needs (ATSN)
abstract: Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs) enroll more than 220 million learners worldwide yet report median completion rates of about 12 %, with participation heavily skewed toward degree-holders. Accessibility deficits exacerbate this disparity: many platforms lack basic features such as captions, transcripts and keyboard navigation. Recent standards including WCAG 2.2 and UDL 3.0 call for perceivable, operable content and the dismantling of systemic barriers, but implementation remains uneven. To characterize learner needs and inform aggregator design (RecMOOC4All), a cross-sectional survey (item-level n≈287-527) was administered. The instrument was delivered online and focused on MOOC accessibility, discover ability, and personalization. Respondents, predominantly aged 25–44 and highly educated, rated accessibility as “very” or “somewhat” important in 87 % of cases; closed captioning (57 %), mobile-first interfaces (34 %), readable/plain-language content (33 %) and transcripts (30 %) were the most requested features. The most common barrier was difficulty finding accessible courses (52 %), followed by challenges completing courses to certificate level (35 %) and inaccessible assignments (~22 %). These patterns highlight a discovery-stage deficit and an implementation gap between guidelines and practice. Accordingly, the primary locus of intervention is the aggregator layer rather than any single provider. The paper proposes RecMOOC4A11, a multi-provider aggregator that would ingest and normalize course catalogues, expose WCAG-aligned accessibility scorecards and implement fairness-aware ranking and consent-based personalization. By foregrounding accessibility metadata and ethical AI practices, the design aims to support equitable discovery and participation in MOOCs.

title: Ransomware-as-a-Service and its Evolution: Lessons from the Babuk, GandCrab, and Sodinokibi Families
authors: Claude Fachkha, Salwa Razaulla, Christine Markarian, Amjad Gawanmeh, Benjamin C. M. Fung, Chadi Assi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Digitization of Systems and Services (IDSS)
abstract: Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) has transformed cybercriminal operations by enabling threat actors to launch ransomware attacks using ready-made tools provided by developers. This study analyzes the evolution of several RaaS samples from Babuk, GandCrab, and Sodinokibi collected over several years. We introduce a novel learning model that uses contrastive techniques to understand the underlying patterns in the malware's binary code, enabling better capture of the subtle changes they undergo as ransomware evolve. Our method, which focuses on concept drift, demonstrates strong capability in identifying different variants, achieving an accuracy of almost 80% in distinguishing their forms from other ransomware types. These findings shed light on the difficulties RaaS malware presents for standard defenses and emphasize the need for sophisticated analytical tools in cybersecurity to mitigate their threats.

title: SEED: Energy and Emission Estimation Dataset for Adaptive Video Streaming
authors: Samira Afzal, Narges Mehran, Farzad Tashtarian, Radu Prodan, Christian Timmerer
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: The environmental impact of video streaming is gaining more attention due to its growing share in global internet traffic and energy consumption. To support accurate and transparent sustainability assessments, we present SEED (Streaming Energy and Emission Dataset): an open dataset for estimating energy usage and CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions in adaptive video streaming. SEED comprises 500 video segments. It provides segment-level measurements of energy consumption and emissions for two primary stages: provisioning, which encompasses encoding and storage on cloud infrastructure; and end-user consumption, including network interface retrieval, video decoding, and display on end-user devices. The dataset covers multiple codecs (AVC, HEVC), resolutions, bitrates, cloud instance types, and geographic regions, reflecting real-world variations in computing efficiency and regional carbon intensity. By combining empirical benchmarks with component-level energy models, SEED enables detailed analysis and supports the development of energy- and emission-aware adaptive bitrate (ABR) algorithms. The dataset is publicly available at: https://github.com/cd-athena/SEED.

title: Facial Skincare Assessment and Personalized Ingredient-Based Recommendations
authors: Ashwin Karthik S M, Deepan S B, Priya S B
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The paper addresses the challenge of providing personalized, ingredient-level skincare guidance in a scalable and accessible manner. To overcome limitations of existing systems which rely on brand-specific products or static image analysis the authors propose a hybrid AI framework that integrates a CNN for detecting skin concerns from facial images, a text classifier for user-described symptoms, and a Large Language Model to generate structured skincare recommendations. The key contribution lies in combining multi-modal inputs (image text) with AI reasoning to deliver real-time, interpretable, and clinically relevant guidance, including active and supportive ingredients, as well as dietary and lifestyle suggestions, through a user-friendly interface.

title: FullHybridDepthNet: End-to-End Hybrid Depth Estimation with Contrastive Multi-Objective Training and Intelligent Computing
authors: Manan Gupta, Bali Devi, Venkatesh Gauri Shankar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Monocular depth estimation, the process of inferring 3D scene geometry from a single 2 D image, is a pivotal challenge in computer vision with applications spanning autonomous navigation, augmented reality, and 3D reconstruction. The task's inherent ill-posedness—stemming from the ambiguity where multiple 3D scenes can project onto the same 2D plane—demands sophisticated models capable of leveraging contextual cues and learned priors. We present FullHybridDepthNet, an innovative hybrid architecture that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for local feature extraction, Transformers for global context modeling, and implicit neural representations for continuous depth refinement. Enhanced by a multi-objective loss function, gradient-weighted attention mechanisms, and contrastive learning, our model achieves superior performance on the NYU Depth V2 dataset, with accuracy of 0.878, RMSE of 0.412 meters, and inference time of 42 ms on an NVIDIA RTX 3090 GPU.
