This work pushes the boundaries of real-time, high-accuracy depth estimation, offering a robust and scalable framework for practical deployment.

title: Research on Cross Species Disease Diagnosis Framework Based on Self Supervised Learning
authors: Liping Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: This article proposes a cross species disease diagnosis framework based on self supervised learning, which solves the problems of high data annotation cost and weak feature transfer ability in traditional methods for cross species diagnosis through dynamic feature decoupling and multimodal data fusion technology. The experiment showed that the framework improved the accuracy by 11.7 % compared to traditional methods in the mixed diagnosis task of bitter melon downy mildew and tomato late blight, and maintained a diagnostic accuracy of 90.3 % even with a 70 % reduction in data annotation. The research provides a lowcost and highly generalized technical path for agricultural disease prevention and control.

title: Design a Tri-Band Microstrip Patch Antenna and Optimization of Impedance Matching via Coaxial Feed Line Locations
authors: J Rangarajan, C Selvi, G Arunachalam, K Anand, N Sneka, K Dinakaran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: This work presents the design and optimization of a tri-band antenna with excellent return loss and VSWR characteristics. The coaxial feed line location for patch antenna is a critical factor in achieving optimal performance, as it directly influences impedance matching and resonance. Several feed line locations in the third quadrant were analyzed in this work, resulting in configurations resonating at the target frequencies of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.375 \text{GHz}, 3.025 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 4.735 GHz. Among these, the feed line locations <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.55 \times 0.55$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.57 \times 0.57$</tex> are recommended for broader tri-band applications due to their superior VSWR values (less than 2 dB) and low reflected power <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2.3\%-4.7\%)$</tex>. The findings highlight the importance of feed line optimization in antenna design, ensuring reliable performance with minimal power loss.

title: Reliability Study of a New PFAS-Free Passivation Material for WLCSP
authors: Yong Chen, David Gani, Jing-En Luan, Corrado Grienti, Yiyi Ma
abstract: The characteristics of passivation materials play a crucial role in the performance of Wafer Level Chip Scale Package (WLCSP). This study evaluates a new PFAS-free polymer (PSV-A) against a conventional PFAS-containing polymer (PSV-B) in WLCSP applications. Accelerated aging tests, including preconditioning and unbiased Highly Accelerated Stress Test (uHAST), revealed distinct failure modes: Ti-Cu interface delamination in PSV-A samples and Ti-PSV-B interface delamination in PSV-B samples, respectively. Finite element thermomechanical simulation identified high stress concentration at the via sidewall, with PSV-A exhibiting peak stress near the via bottom, which correlated well with the failure location. Differences in via sidewall angle result in thinner Ti deposition thereby increasing outgassing effects in PSV-A, which weakens Ti-Cu adhesion. A design of experiments (DOE) approach with thicker Ti and optimized curing temperature effectively mitigated outgassing and improved TiCu adhesion. Further process optimization is recommended to enhance PSV-A reliability in WLCSP.

title: A Novel Machine Learning Approach for Tuberculosis Detection using Volatile Organic Compounds
authors: Vishal BG, Jossy George, Chanti S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: For world health, better TB diagnosis is still absolutely necessary. Using VOC Atlas, this study assesses a few machine learning techniques for categorizing breath samples depending on volatile organic compound (VOC) profiles. We created a machine learning pipeline and tried out four different models: Random Forest, XGBoost, Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), and a 1D-Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN). There were 1,500 patient profiles in the dataset spanning three groups: healthy people, drug-sensitive TB cases, and multidrugresistant TB cases. Using VOC biomarker patterns found in VOC Atlas and prior TB research, these profiles were developed. While XGBoost stood out by reaching 100% accuracy, our studies revealed that most models performed rather well. This implies that gradient boosting-based ensemble models can adequately grasp the complex patterns found in breath data. One major caveat is that we have not tested these models on real clinical breath samples to validate them. Testing these models with actual patient samples in clinical settings would be the next reasonable step. All told, this research provides a strong basis for creating non-invasive ways to detect illnesses.

title: A Comprehensive Review on Integrating Blockchain with AI and IoT
authors: R.V. Kannadasan, P. Anusha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The convergence of Blockchain, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and the Internet of Things (IoT) is revolutionizing decentralized systems by enhancing security, scalability, and intelligence. This review paper explores the synergistic integration of these technologies, focusing on their collective impact across various domains. Blockchain provides a decentralized, immutable ledger that ensures data integrity and trustworthiness. AI enables intelligent data processing and decision-making, while IoT facilitates real-time data collection and interaction with the physical world. Together, they address critical challenges such as data security, system scalability, and real-time analytics in applications ranging from smart cities and healthcare to industrial automation and supply chain management. Through a comprehensive review of existing literature, this paper examines the benefits, challenges, and open research directions associated with the integration of Blockchain, AI, and IoT. By providing insights into current advancements and future opportunities.

title: How Parasocial Interaction Shapes Trust and Engagement in E-Commerce Platforms
authors: Anggraeni Woro Hapsari, Anggi Agusta Tiurina Harahap, Worry Mambusy Manoby, Dhita Widya Putri, Adrián Szilárd Nagy
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This study explores the impact of Parasocial Interaction (PSI)-the emotional, one-sided bonds formed between consumers and mediated figures-on Customer Trust and Customer Engagement within e-commerce platforms. Drawing from relational marketing theory and the social presence framework, this research further investigates the mediating role of trust in this relationship. A structural equation model, tested using WarpPLS, was applied to data collected from 480 Indonesian users who had recent experiences with AI-driven e- commerce interfaces. The results demonstrate that PSI significantly influences both trust and engagement. Additionally, trust is found to partially mediate the relationship between PSI and engagement, suggesting that while emotional resonance plays a key role, trust serves as a cognitive- emotional bridge that translates PSI into behavioral commitment. Notably, participants expressed high engagement levels with both human and AI hosts, highlighting the ability of well-designed virtual agents to foster a sense of intimacy and authenticity. These findings contribute to the extension of PSI theory into the AI-mediated commerce context and underscore the critical role of culturally relevant trust-building mechanisms, particularly in collectivist societies like Indonesia. The study advocates for trust-centered platform design and emphasizes the need for further cross-cultural PSI research to sustain relational commerce in the digital age.

title: Path Planning Algorithm for Mobile Robots Based on Improved TD3
authors: Tengfei Pu, Mengmeng Jiang, Ziyuan Zhang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering, Materials and Automation Technology (MMEAT)
abstract: Path planning plays a critical role in mobile robot navigation, where effective path planning techniques can significantly enhance operational efficiency. To address existing challenges in mobile robot path planning—including sparse reward functions, collisions in complex real-world scenarios, non-smooth trajectories, and suboptimal paths—this study proposes an improved deep reinforcement learning algorithm based on Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3). A dynamic composite reward function is designed to mitigate reward sparsity, aiming to simultaneously minimize collision probability and the number of turning points while achieving optimal paths.

title: Graph-based Reinforcement Learning for Hour-Level Regional Power Grid Dispatch
authors: Xinhang Xiao, Chenxi Dai, Jin Yang, Xiaohui Bi, Dingjun Yao, Xiaopu Zeng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Regional power grid dispatch faces increasing challenges from demand uncertainty and rapid load fluctuations. Traditional methods rely on separate forecasting and optimization stages, limiting adaptability to unforeseen conditions. This paper proposes a graph-based reinforcement learning (RL) framework that formulates hour-level dispatch as a Markov Decision Process and employs Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to learn adaptive allocation policies. By representing districts as graph nodes and modeling their spatial dependencies through edges, the framework jointly captures intra-district load dynamics and inter-district correlations. It directly optimizes operational objectives, including shortage prevention, oversupply reduction, and capacity utilization, through environmental feedback. Experiments on the Suzhou Industrial Park dataset show that the proposed graph-based PPO method achieves higher cumulative reward and lower variance than conventional baselines, demonstrating superior efficiency, stability, and coordination for intelligent grid operation under uncertainty.

title: Optimization Method for Carbon Energy Collaborative Operation of Integrated Energy System in Park Based on Internal Dynamic Carbon Price
authors: Yudi Ding, Kai Yuan, Zhijun Wu, Longjun Luo, Siqi Ma, Feng Zhao, Tiancheng Shi, Shibin Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Developing green and low-carbon industrial parks is essential for advancing the energy transition. To enhance the carbon–energy synergy of park-level integrated energy systems (PIES), this study proposes a novel low-carbon economic operation framework based on dynamic internal carbon pricing (DICP). First, a carbon-energy coupling model of a typical PIES is established, through which dynamic carbon emission factors on the load side are derived. By incorporating shadow carbon costs, a DICP model is then formulated and embedded into the operational optimization of PIES. The proposed framework provides carbon incentives for flexibility resources such as energy storage, demand response, and energy conversion units, thereby guiding their responses to variations in carbon emission factors. Case studies are conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Results show that DICP significantly improves the carbon reduction potential of PIES while maintaining economic efficiency.

title: Automated Metrology of Advanced Package Interconnections Using a High-Speed In-Line 3D X-Ray System
authors: Thomas Gregorich, Kamyar Majlan, Johannes Ruoff, Aksel Goehnermeier, Benedikt Imhof, Martin Dietzel
abstract: As Moore's Law slows, the electronics industry increasingly relies on advanced IC packages to deliver the necessary interconnect density required for continued performance improvements. This trend is particularly pronounced in AI data centers, where massive Large Language Models (LLMs) are driving memory and bandwidth demands to unprecedented levels. Over the next decade, peak package interconnect density is expected to grow significantly - rising from approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$400 \mathrm{I} / \mathrm{O}$</tex> per square millimeter (IO/sqmm) to nearly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0, 0 0 0 ~ I O} /$</tex> sqmm, representing a 25-fold increase. This paper introduces a novel 3D X-ray-based methodology for extracting metrology data from buried micro-connections. The proposed approach combines high resolution with fast throughput, making it suitable for real-time, inline applications. The theory of operation, along with metrology data from advanced IC packages, will be discussed in detail.

title: Empowering Disabled and Elderly Individuals with a Knowledge-Seeking Robot
authors: Farah Alotaibi, Mohammed Faisal Naji, Hajar Alsulaili, Reem Aldhafiri, Fayzah Alotaibi, Sarah Aladwani
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on ICT &amp; Accessibility (ICTA) co-located with 2025 2nd International Conference and Forum on Assistive Technologies for Special Needs (ATSN)
abstract: With rapid technological advancements and the increasing use of ChatGPT, technology has become an essential resource for information searches. However, elderly and disabled individuals may face challenges in accessing information. Approximately 761 million people are aged 65 or older, and around 1.3 billion people have significant impairments. Disabilities are more prevalent than commonly believed and often contribute to lower life satisfaction. The elderly, in particular, often encounter difficulties navigating search engine technologies such as Google and Yahoo. These platforms present numerous websites with varying levels of accuracy, forcing users to sift through a mix of correct and incorrect information. This process can be particularly daunting for those less familiar with digital interfaces. As a current solution, the advent of AI technology, exemplified by ChatGPT, provides a more accessible alternative. This AI-driven tool allows elderly individuals to obtain accurate information without the need to browse through numerous websites, thereby simplifying the search process and enhancing their overall experience. To further address these challenges and provide an even more user-friendly solution, we have developed ASKER, a low-cost assistance robot that leverages advanced AI technologies. ASKER uses natural language processing, voice recognition, and generative AI to facilitate engaging conversations, particularly for the elderly and disabled. ASKER utilizes ChatGPT's capabilities for reasoning and managing user query responses in a practical and efficient manner. The system comprises five models. the text preparation model (TPM), liquid crystal display (LCD), speech generation model (SGM), speech recognition model (SRM), and customized ChatGPT. The experimental results illustrated the success of the proposed robot.

title: Research on Wireless Propagation Characteristics and Channel Modeling for 6G
authors: Zhongqiu Xiang, Xueying Li, Xuecheng Liu, Lei Guo, Fan Chen, Xiaoxiao Sun, Xirui Dang, Jieying Qi
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Terahertz technology (THz), as one of the key technologies in 6G communication, has unique advantages and huge application potential, and is the key to achieving high-speed communication in 6G. But there are still some technical challenges at present. Due to the high frequency of terahertz waves, they are easily absorbed and scattered by molecules such as oxygen and water vapor when propagating in the atmosphere, resulting in severe signal attenuation. This paper studies the influence mechanism of meteorological factors on the propagation of terahertz electromagnetic waves and establishes a quantitative analysis model. Meanwhile, the deterministic propagation model is utilized to conduct a refined simulation of the propagation characteristics and multipath propagation of terahertz. This paper realizes the simulation of terahertz wireless propagation characteristics and channel modeling, providing an important quantitative basis for future 6G technology research and network construction.

title: NeuroQuant AI: A Hybrid AI Framework for Real-Time Adaptive Trading
authors: Venkatesh Bhat, Basavaraj S, Akhilesh H R, Dhanaraj S B, Akshay R B
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Financial markets are characterized by high volatility, rapid information flow, and complex interactions between technical and sentiment-driven factors. Traditional algorithmic trading systems, often based on single-model approaches or static rule sets, struggle to adapt to these conditions in real time. This paper introduces NeuroQuant AI, a hybrid framework that integrates Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for time-series price forecasting, transformer-based sentiment analysis for extracting market signals from news and social media, and reinforcement learning (RL) for adaptive strategy optimization.The framework is designed with built-in risk management protocols (stop-loss, take-profit, and trailing mechanisms) and deployed through APIs such as Binance, Alpha Vantage, and MetaTrader, enabling end-to-end real-world applicability. Extensive backtesting on forex and cryptocurrency datasets demonstrates that the hybrid approach consistently achieves superior risk-adjusted returns, with statistically significant improvements in Sharpe ratio, maximum drawdown, precision, and recall, validated using confidence intervals. Comparative benchmarking against baseline LSTM-only and sentiment-only models further highlights the novelty and effectiveness of the integrated design.In addition to empirical performance, the framework emphasizes scalability in low-latency, high-frequency trading environments, with latency benchmarks confirming suitability for real-time execution. Finally, initial efforts toward explainable AI (XAI) are incorporated, allowing users to interpret model-driven trading decisions and thereby increasing trust in high-risk financial applications. These contributions position NeuroQuant AI as a promising step toward robust, interpretable, and deployable AI-driven trading systems.

title: Digital Infrastructure and Indonesian Society's Readiness for Digital Publishing Transformation: A Case Study of the Publishing Industry in Indonesia
authors: Nur Azmi Karim, Dewa Ayu Adella Desvita Virginie, Sannylyn, Vianney Virgil Kusanggoro
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The digital transformation of Indonesia's publishing industry has significantly altered public literacy patterns. The purpose of this study is to examine how internet penetration and smartphone use affect the public's willingness to embrace AI-based learning systems and the reduction in interest in printed books. Multiple linear regression on secondary data from 2020–2024 reveals that smartphone use indirectly contributes to the fall in interest in printed books while having a large positive impact on the uptake of digital publishing. On the other hand, the output of printed books is significantly impacted negatively by internet penetration, suggesting that greater digital access results in less consumption of physical books. These results demonstrate that for the adoption of AI-driven education to be inclusive and long-lasting, there has to be a robust digital infrastructure and adequate digital literacy.

title: AI Powered SmartMirror: A Multimodal System for Personalized Outfit and Skincare Recommendations Using Real-Time Visual Context
authors: Gugapritha R., S Abisheik Raj, Madhumitha K., Suganeshwari G
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The use of artificial intelligence in personal grooming and aesthetic management is leading to exciting new software solutions in fashion technology and digital wellness. Given the increasing demand for personalized skincare and wardrobe management, adaptive applications that learn from people’s behavior, along with real-time real-world factors, are more in demand. Smartphones may provide disjointed applications such as virtual try-on software or basic skincare testers, but cohesive, integrated platforms that embrace real-time operational feedback for personalizing one’s wardrobe are still being developed. SmartMirror acts as a conventional smart assistant without the previously perceived limitations, through the mirror interface. SmartMirror in conjunction with a user’s secure, personalized dressing recommendations and self-skincare advice based on their real-time appearance using facial authentication, computer vision, and real-time contextual awareness. Unlike existing systems, SmartMirror combines multiple AI-enabled software modules, including YOLOv8 to identify clothing and skin issues, Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to identify skin types, and contextual awareness as determined by calendar intelligence and environmental data from weather APIs. It has additional features such as a wardrobe manager using Firebase to monitor user-owned clothing and prevent duplicate recommendations. Overall the proposed SmartMirror system has the ability to provide its recommendations in real-time, by sensing its user’s visible attributes via a camera stream, isolating visual and contextual environmental features from each camera stream, and making context-based suggestions such as suggesting wearing lighter clothing. It also includes Pyspark for analyzing usage trends and make data-driven decisions. This paper provides the architectural design, first-stage deployment, and future research directions of SmartMirror.

title: Automatic File Renaming Using Fine-Tuned BERT and Pattern-Based Entity Extraction
authors: Kenneth Lay, Bryan Sujoso, Andrew Tanuwijaya, Raymond Bahana, Feri Setiawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: In the current modern digital age, with the exception of a few handful of diligent students, the vast majority of students do not bother naming their files, regardless of what course or class it may be. Whether it be a Python file for a calculator project or an Excel spreadsheet containing some details for their final project, many students still name their files into something that may make sense to them in the moment, but be less recognizable in the end. The aim of this project is to attempt to create a website that contains a system that allows students to upload their files and allow it to rename itself in a more effective and organized manner. This is done through a fine-tuned BERT-based Named Entity Recognition (NER) model, which processes the document's content to identify key entities such as author, title, and unique IDs. Regular Expressions (Regex) were also used to attempt to increase the accuracy and handle specific patterns, to try and ensure the extraction of details from the compatible file formats are accurate. Another discovery made was that the fine-tuned model achieved an accuracy of 86% and a score of 0.87 F1, which outperforms the other pre-trained BERT models. The system is able to identify and extract author names, titles, and IDs from unstructured text to a consistent file name format.

title: High-Aspect-Ratio, Fine-Pitch Through-Mold Interconnect Fabrication for Heterogeneous Integration of High-Performance Computing Systems
authors: Lai Yee Chia, Tran Van Nhat Anh
abstract: This paper presents the development of key fabrication process steps of fine-pitch, high aspect ratio Cu pillars <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> width, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$150 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> height for TMIs formation applications. By addressing the key hallenges in photolithography, plasma treatment and electroplating processes, the Cu pillars were successfully fabricated on 300 mm wafer level, with a critical dimension of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$27.8 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> and a height of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$141.1 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>. The approach provides a promising alternative technique for the fine-pitch TMIs fabrication in high performance computing systems.

title: Hire Lens: AI Resume Builder and Scanner
authors: I. Kavya Shree, M. Thangamani, K. Malini, U. Janani, Rajasekhara Babu L, Rania Rabie Alshikhe
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Modern recruitment systems increasingly rely on automated screening tools to handle high applicant volumes. However, most existing Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) primarily depend on superficial keyword matching, which often overlooks contextual meaning and reinforces bias present in historical hiring patterns. As a result, qualified candidates may be filtered out, and hiring decisions can unintentionally reflect demographic or linguistic bias. This paper presents Hire Lens, an AI-driven resume scanner and builder designed to enhance fairness, clarity, and relevance in digital recruitment. The system integrates Natural Language Processing (NLP), Machine Learning (ML), and Human-Centered Design principles to evaluate resumes based on semantic understanding rather than keyword frequency. Key features include automatic anonymization to reduce bias, recruiter-style feedback generation, clarity scoring for resume statements, and a skill-to-job heatmap for visual competency comparison. Evaluations conducted with students and recruiters indicate improved resume quality, increased transparency in screening, and more equitable candidate consideration. The proposed approach demonstrates how AI, when combined with ethical design, can promote a more inclusive and effective recruitment experience.

title: High Coupling Efficiency Adhesive for Photonic Packaging
authors: S.C. Lim, A. Hartwig, S. Seethaler, T.X. Guo, H. Wang, O. Matyssek
abstract: The rapid rise of 5G and artificial intelligence (AI) has accelerated the demand for high-speed data communication and driven the evolution of advanced packaging technologies such as 2.5 D and 3 D architectures for AI applications. Within this field, silicon photonics packaging is becoming an attractive alternative to conventional copper-based electronics, offering faster data transfer with lower power consumption. Although electronic packaging has seen remarkable progress, photonic integrated circuit (PIC) packaging still faces major hurdles in achieving consistent performance and reliability. Key challenges include the need for highly accurate and efficient active alignment for optical coupling, ensuring durability during reflow and environmental testing, and managing heat dissipation effectively. Adhesives play a vital role throughout the photonic packaging process, providing solutions to these technical barriers. Their properties-such as bonding strength, optical transmission, and resistance to environmental impact are crucial factors determining the success of packaging applications. This paper examines the primary bonding tasks in photonic packaging and outlines the specific requirements that adhesives must meet. Emphasis is placed on both the optical and mechanical characteristics of adhesives, as well as their ability to retain stable performance under varying operating conditions. Recent advancements in adhesive technology are also highlighted, demonstrating how they improve efficiency and reliability in photonic devices, supported by experimental results on coupling efficiency. In addition, the paper presents a wafer-level photonic packaging method designed to preserve optical performance during key steps such as sawing and grinding. By selecting materials with specialized optical properties, this approach ensures that the packaging process maintains the integrity and functionality of photonic components.

title: Effect of Annealing Post-Treatment on Bonding Strength of Two Dissimilar Polymeric Materials Fabricated by Using Fused Filament Fabrication
authors: Rahmat Riza, Muslim Mahardika, Budi Arifvianto
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: The annealing process is commonly used to enhance material properties. However, this process is still questionable for application to polymer materials, especially its effect on dissimilar material bonding printed by the additive manufacturing process, where the bonding is one of the main factors determining the mechanical properties of the fused filament fabrication product, as one of the additive manufacturing methods. This study examined the effect of heat treatment after the specimens had been prepared or annealing post-treatment. Two selected temperatures, 60 °C and 80 °C, and two annealing durations, 10 minutes and 45 minutes, were performed in the research. Peel-off test results based on the ASTM 3163-01 standard showed that the annealing process tends to reduce the strength of the specimens. The duration factor significantly affects material properties more than annealing temperature.

title: Is there a relationship between Mean Opinion Score (MOS) and Just Noticeable Difference (JND)?
authors: Jingwen Zhu, Hadi Amirpour, Wei Zhou, Patrick Le Callet
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Evaluating perceived video quality is essential for ensuring high Quality of Experience (QoE) in modern streaming applications. While existing subjective datasets and Video Quality Metrics (VQMs) cover a broad quality range, many practical use cases—especially for premium users—focus on high-quality scenarios requiring finer granularity. Just Noticeable Difference (JND) has emerged as a key concept for modeling perceptual thresholds in these high-end regions and plays an important role in perceptual bitrate ladder construction. However, the relationship between JND and the more widely used Mean Opinion Score (MOS) remains unclear. In this paper, we conduct a Degradation Category Rating (DCR) subjective study based on an existing JND dataset to examine how MOS corresponds to the 75% Satisfied User Ratio (SUR) points of the 1<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">st</sup> and 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nd</sup> JNDs. We find that while MOS values at JND points generally align with theoretical expectations (e.g., 4.75 for the 75% SUR of the 1<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">st</sup> JND), the reverse mapping—from MOS to JND—is ambiguous due to overlapping confidence intervals across PVS indices. Statistical significance analysis further shows that DCR studies with limited participants may not detect meaningful differences between reference and JND videos.

title: RIS-Aided Cell-Free Massive MIMO Systems with Near-Field Communications
authors: Changchang Xu, Qi Zhang, Jun Zhang, Shu Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The integration of reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) with cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) system has emerged as a promising paradigm for enhancing the spectral efficiency and coverage of next-generation wireless networks. However, most existing studies rely on far-field assumptions. With the emergence of extra-large RIS, the near-field effect becomes inevitable, and in CF-mMIMO systems, the short distances between access points (APs) and users further increase the likelihood of near-field channels. Therefore, a full near-field channel model needs to be considered. In this paper, we investigate RIS-aided CF-mMIMO systems with full near-field communications and derive the downlink sum rate. Based on it, we formulate a non-convex optimization problem to jointly design power allocation and RIS phase shifts for maximizing the achievable sum rate. We efficiently introduce an alternating optimization scheme that leverages the lagrangian dual transform in conjunction with fractional programming, thereby decomposing the original problem into a series of tractable subproblems. Simulation results validate the accuracy of theoretical analysis and demonstrate that the proposed algorithm effectively improves the RIS-aided CF-mMIMO system downlink sum rate.

title: An Intrusion Detection Method Based on Improved Shrink Autoencoder
authors: ZiJian Wang, ShuWu Chen, Qiang Tu, HaiHui Xie, ZhenQuan Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: The increasing threat of cyber-attacks makes Intrusion Detection System (IDS) crucial in network security. To enhance detection speed, feature dimension reduction is a key approach to handling network activity features. Autoencoder is commonly used for dimension reduction. However, network traffic data has the characteristic of high-dimensional sparsity. When an autoencoder processes high-dimensional sparse features through dimension reduction, its limited model capacity cannot fully model the complex nonlinear relationships in high-dimensional sparse data, resulting in insufficient feature extraction. Therefore, this paper presents an attention encoder-based IDS to address the limitations of autoencoder in handling high-dimensional sparse network traffic data. By integrating an attention mechanism into a shrink autoencoder and combining it with a feature-weighted centroid classifier, the proposed method effectively distinguishes between normal and abnormal network activities. Experiments show that this approach achieves an F1 score of 0.9977 and an AUC of 0.9922, significantly outperforming existing methods.

title: Exploring Cost-Efficient and Resource-Optimized Kubernetes Deployments in Multi-Region Settings
authors: Lixinqian Yu, Richard O. Sinnott
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Smart Computing (BigComp)
abstract: Kubernetes is the predominant Cloud container orchestration platform. However, the default Kubernetes scheduler is cost-blind and region-agnostic. This can give rise to unnecessary financial costs when running clusters in public/commercial clouds. Existing research primarily focuses on cost optimization for single-region, homogeneous Clouds. Supporting heterogeneous clusters with cost and resource utilization under hybrid (on-demand and pre-emptible) pricing models remains under-explored. In this paper, we propose an end-to-end, cost-efficient, and resource-optimized scheduling framework designed specifically for distributed Cloud environments. Experimental evaluations on the Google Cloud Platform demonstrate that our approach substantially reduces costs (45% below baseline on-demand pricing and 62% below baseline hybrid pricing), whilst improving resource utilization.

title: Efficient Malicious Encrypted Traffic Detection via Multi-Scale Convolution-Augmented Transformer: The NetFlowClassifier Approach
authors: Zongbao Wang, Juan Fu, Zhili Ma, Zhiru Li, Mingming Xiang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of encryption technologies, an increasing proportion of Internet traffic is encrypted, which fundamentally weakens traditional traffic inspection mechanisms. Existing port-based and payload-based detection methods have become ineffective, while approaches relying on handcrafted features suffer from limited discriminative capability and poor generalization. To address the problem of malicious encrypted traffic identification, this paper proposes a lightweight hybrid model named NetFlowClassifier, together with a targeted data preprocessing pipeline. An encrypted traffic dataset is first constructed from CSE-CIC-IDS2018 for binary classification of benign and malicious flows. The proposed model integrates multi-scale depthwise separable convolution and an improved Transformer encoder to jointly capture local discriminative patterns and long-range dependencies. In addition, learnable feature position encoding and attention-weighted pooling are introduced to enhance feature representation. Experimental results show that NetFlowClassifier achieves an F1-score of 0.9661, outperforming baseline CNN and Transformer models by 1.8%–3.2%. With 11.2 million parameters and a throughput of 128 samples per second, the model effectively balances detection accuracy and computational efficiency. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach for malicious encrypted traffic detection in modern network environments.

title: Building Customer Loyalty in E-Commerce with User Experience and Interface Design While Adapting to AI Developments in FinTech
authors: Vanessa Abigael, Denanira Tafida Yumna, Masayu Kania Zahra Nafilah, Ferdianto
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The rapid development of e-commerce in Indonesia has required various platforms to increase customer satisfaction and loyalty in order to compete. UI/UX design is an aspect that affects these two things. This research includes testing how system quality, information quality, and service quality in UI/UX affect customer satisfaction, and how satisfaction and trust can influence customer loyalty on Indonesian e-commerce platforms. The DeLone & McLean model is the reference in this research. Data from 453 active e-commerce users in Indonesia were collected using a purposive sampling technique created using Google Forms. The analysis was performed through Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The outcome of this study shows that system quality, information quality, and service quality significantly enhance customer satisfaction. In addition, customer satisfaction and trust have a positive influence on customer loyalty. Hence, a well-designed, trustworthy, and easy-to-use interface plays an important role in loyalty. Focusing on UI/UX quality and building trust is an effective way for e-commerce platforms to increase customer satisfaction and loyalty in the competitive Indonesian market. This study also emphasizes the growing role of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in supporting FinTech services that are increasingly integrated into e-commerce platforms.

title: AndroByte: LLM-Driven Privacy Analysis through Bytecode Summarization and Dynamic Dataflow Call Graph Generation
authors: Mst Eshita Khatun, Lamine Noureddine, Zhiyong Sui, Aisha Ali-Gombe
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: With the exponential growth in mobile applications, protecting user privacy has become even more crucial. Android applications are often known for collecting, storing, and sharing sensitive user information such as contacts, location, camera, and microphone data, often without the user's clear consent or awareness, raising significant privacy risks and exposure. In the context of privacy assessment, dataflow analysis is particularly valuable for identifying data usage and potential leaks. Traditionally, this type of analysis has relied on formal methods, heuristics, and rule-based matching. However, these techniques are often complex to implement and prone to errors, such as taint explosion for large programs. Moreover, most existing Android dataflow analysis methods depend heavily on predefined list of sinks, limiting their flexibility and scalability. To address the limitations of these existing techniques, we propose AndroByte, an AI-driven privacy analysis tool that leverages the reasoning of a large language model (LLM) on bytecode summarization to dynamically generate accurate and explainable dataflow call graphs from static code analysis. AndroByte achieves a significant Fß-Score of 89% in generating dynamic dataflow call graphs on the fly, outperforming the effectiveness of traditional tools like FlowDroid and Amandroid in leak detection without relying on predefined propagation rules or sink lists. Moreover, AndroByte's iterative bytecode summarization provides comprehensive and explainable insights into dataflow and leak detection, achieving high, quantifiable scores based on the G-Eval metric.

title: Blockchain-Based PoC (Proof of Contribution Framework): Securing Research Deliverables and Intellectual Property with Web3 Technologies
authors: Sagnik Mazumdar, Umesh Pal, Soham Banerjee, Tanishq Bhattacharjee, Roni Das
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: This project applies the latest cutting-edge Web3 technologies to design a Blockchain-Based Proof of Contribution (PoC) Framework aimed at improving efficiency, security, and transparency in managing research outputs and safeguarding intellectual property in collaborative settings. This framework uses the features of smart contracts on Ethereum and blockchain technology to guarantee transparency and accountability by automating the process involved in awarding the funds raised, verification of donations, and resolving disputes. After hashing a contribution cryptographically, every one of them stays on the blockchain, generating an irreversible record of its veracity. The application of IPFS in this makes decentralized and safe offchain storage possible, scalable, and intact. It has an automated dispute resolution process, which guarantees that the outcomes will be fair and not sent out for arbitration from outside. The framework deals with issues of trust, data security, and intellectual property in order to provide a safe and effective setting in which researchers can focus on creativity, knowing their work is safely documented, appropriately acknowledged, and protected from misuse.

title: In Pursuit of Lean OS Kernels: Improving Configuration-Based Debloating
authors: Akshith Gunasekaran, Gabriel Ritter, Rakesh B. Bobba
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Modern OS kernels are bloated to support diverse hardware and features, yet most deployments use only a small subset. This paper advances configuration-based kernel debloating to reduce attack surface and code size in systems with known workloads. We present Tracie,a trace-based specialization tool that improves precision through better workload attribution and trace-to-config mapping, achieving a 21.67% reduction—outperforming prior work by 8%—and removing 1 additional CVEs. We also introduce Dice, a novel trace-free, dependency-aware debloating approach that iteratively prunes the configuration graph, achieving 35.59% reduction and eliminating 10 more CVEs. Finally, we empirically analyze the Linux kconfig system and show that structural limitations—such as coarse granularity and rigid dependencies—constrain the effectiveness of configuration-based debloating.

title: Revisiting Prime+Prune+Probe: Pitfalls and Remedies
authors: Moritz Peters, Florian Stolz, Jan Philipp Thoma, Tim Güneysu, Yuval Yarom
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Randomizing the mapping of memory addresses to cache locations is a promising approach for protecting computer systems against cache attacks. Multiple randomized caches have been proposed recently, with the aim of preventing adversaries from creating eviction sets - collections of addresses that compete with target memory addresses on cache space. However, Purnal et al. (IEEE SP 2021) demonstrated the Prime+ Prune+ Probeattack, which allows attackers to efficiently build generalized eviction sets, enabling target memory address eviction with a high probability. As the complexity of constructing eviction set is a key factor in randomized cache design, the Prime+prune+probe attack significantly reduces the security bounds of these randomizing designs. Since the Prime+prune+probe attack is probabilistic, generalized eviction sets often get stuck after repeated use, making them ineffective for typical cache attack settings. Prior works have noticed this behavior and proposed mitigation approaches. These approaches are based on evicting members of the eviction set from the cache, either probabilistically, using random memory accesses, or directly, using dedicated flush instructions. However, these proposals do not analyze the effectiveness of the techniques or evaluate their success. In this work we revisit Prime+prune+probe and analyze it in light of the possibility of eviction sets getting stuck. We observe that flushing does not behave as anticipated in realistic cache architectures, where invalid cache lines are filled first before evicting other lines. We further propose a new technique for allowing repeated attacks - combining random noise with flushing. We conduct an in-depth analysis of all discussed techniques and compare their complexity attacking an AES T-table implementation. We find that combining probabilistic eviction with flushing outperforms the traditional approaches by a factor of two, allowing attackers to increase the granularity and observe victim processes even better than in prior works.

title: Comparison of Mechanical, Thermal and Reliability Properties of Sintered Copper, Sintered Silver and Solder Paste
authors: Dongfang Dai, Kai Zheng, Yexiong Huang, Jiabin Yu, Xianping Chen
abstract: With the continual advancement of power devices, system integration density has increased significantly, leading to rising device junction temperatures. While silver sintering technology has been adopted for chip-level packaging, solder paste remains the standard solution for system-level thermal management. This study develops a pressurized sintered copper paste for system-level heat dissipation and conducts a comprehensive comparative analysis of the mechanical properties, thermal performance, and reliability between system-level pressurized sintered silver paste, copper paste, and commercial solder paste. The results demonstrate that devices interconnected with pressurized sintered silver/copper paste exhibit a 16.5% reduction in thermal resistance compared to those using solder paste. Following rigorous thermal shock (TS) testing of 1000 cycles between −55° C and 150° C, copper-sintered devices maintain structural integrity without delamination failure and retain lower thermal resistance. This performance characteristic demonstrates their superior reliability advantage over both silver-sintered devices and solder pasteinterconnected devices.

title: DDPG-Driven Multi-Layer Task Offloading: Edge–Fog Collaboration and Learning Stability
authors: Jingyun Hao, Can Wang, Huanzhi Wang, Youyun Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and the deployment of 5G/6G networks have made task offloading in multi-layer edge–fog–cloud systems increasingly challenging, especially under high device density. This paper proposes a Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG)-driven multi-layer task offloading scheme that jointly optimizes state representation and edge–fog collaboration. Specifically, a 13-dimensional continuous state vector is constructed to capture task features, device states, and global resources, while a fog-assisted interaction mechanism is introduced to reduce local bias and improve coordination efficiency. A dynamic exploration strategy is combined with Prioritized Experience Replay (PER) to enhance sampling efficiency and stabilize training. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves higher task success rates, lower energy consumption, and reduced waiting and wasted time compared with baseline algorithms, while ensuring stable learning under varying device densities.

title: Optimal Sizing and Operation of Shared Energy Storage in Microgrids: A Distributionally Robust Stackelberg Game
authors: Yumei Zhao, Mei Guan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: To address the operational risks caused by the high penetration of distributed renewable energy and the underutilization of demand-side flexibility in microgrids, this paper proposes an optimal sizing and operation framework for Shared Energy Storage Systems (SESS) based on a distributionally robust Stackelberg game. Specifically, a high-order Resistance-Capacitance (RC) network model is introduced to quantify building thermal inertia, enabling Integrated Buildings (IBs) to function as flexible thermal loads. A bilevel optimization model is formulated wherein the SESS acts as the leader maximizing profit, while IBs act as followers optimizing power dispatch and thermal comfort. Furthermore, a data-driven distributionally robust optimization (DRO) approach constructing an ambiguity set via historical moment information is employed to handle renewable uncertainty, and the bilevel problem is reformulated into a tractable Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) model via Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed method effectively coordinates source-load-storage resources, significantly reducing total costs and load shedding risks compared to deterministic and stochastic approaches.

title: End-to-End Joint Authentication Framework for Intelligent Communication Systems via Device Physical Fingerprints and Deep Temporal–Spatial Packet Learning
authors: Shuguang Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: With the widespread deployment of the Internet of Things and heterogeneous networks, traditional identity authentication based on public key infrastructure (PKI) has exposed serious flaws in energy consumption, latency, and certificate management complexity. This flaw makes it difficult to resist device cloning, traffic forgery, and side-channel attacks. To this end, this study proposes an end-to-end joint authentication model (JointModel). This model combines the device physical fingerprints with deep spatiotemporal packet learning under the framework of a lightweight identity-based cryptographic system. This combined model achieves continuous and adaptive identity authentication. At the feature level, the model introduces a physical fingerprint embedding network and a spatiotemporal convolution-bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) fusion structure. This breakthrough constructs a multi-dimensional joint feature space. At the same time, at the decision level, a weighted adaptive fusion strategy is used to combine the physical fingerprint confidence, traffic behavior confidence, and cryptographic verification results. Further dynamic adjustment of authentication weights achieves the continuous and reliable security verification. The systematic experimental part was verified based on a self-built physical fingerprint dataset of 40 devices and a public intrusion detection traffic library. The results showed that in five independent experiments, the average authentication accuracy of the model reached 96.76 %, the AUC increased to 0.983, and the equal error rate (EER) decreased to 2.14 %. At the same time, as an innovative evaluation metric, it significantly outperformed PKI-only and single-modality methods in terms of cloning attack detection rate. In order to measure noise robustness, under the conditions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 \%}, / \text{1 0 \%}$</tex> and 20 % packet loss, the model accuracy remained at 95.50 %, 93.80 % and 88.90 % respectively. In terms of computing resources, the model parameter scale is approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{4. 2 M}$</tex>, and the single inference delay is approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 6. 6 6} \pm \text{0. 1 8} \sim \text{m s}$</tex>.

title: Learned, Lagged, LLM-Splained: LLM Responses to End User Security Questions
authors: Vijay Prakash, Kevin Lee, Arkaprabha Bhattacharya, Danny Yuxing Huang, Jessica Staddon
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Answering end user security questions is challenging. While large language models (LLMs) like GPT, Llama, and Gemini are far from error-free, they have shown promise in answering a variety of questions outside of security. We qualitatively evaluated responses from three popular LLMs to 900 systematically collected security questions in the first such evaluation in the area of end user security. While LLMs demonstrate broad generalist “knowledge” of end user security information, there are patterns of errors and limitations across LLMs-including stale, inaccurate, and incomplete answers, as well as indirect or unresponsive communication styles-which negatively impact the user experience. Based on these patterns, we suggest directions for improved model development and recommend user strategies for interacting with LLMs when seeking assistance with security.

title: A Systematic Literature Review: IoT-Based Soil NPK Sensing Using Machine Learning
authors: Sitti Heliana, Suhono Harso Supangkat
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Efficient soil nutrient management is fundamental to achieving sustainable agriculture and ensuring long-term food security. Conventional laboratory-based methods for soil nutrient analysis, while highly accurate, are often time-consuming, costly, and unsuitable for real-time field applications. In recent years, Internet of Things (IoT)-based systems have emerged as a transformative solution, enabling real-time, in-situ monitoring of soil nutrients. These systems, particularly when integrated with machine learning (ML), have shown great promise for monitoring key macronutrients such as Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), and Potassium (K). This systematic literature review synthesizes and critically examines peer-reviewed studies published between 2018 and 2025 that focus on IoT-enabled NPK sensing in agricultural contexts. It classifies sensor technologies-ranging from optical and electrochemical to MEMS/NEMS, polymer-based, and nanobiosensors-used in conjunction with various IoT architectures and ML models. The review also evaluates the predictive performance of different ML algorithms applied for nutrient estimation and fertilizer recommendation. The findings reveal a growing trend toward the adoption of integrated, realtime, and data-driven monitoring systems in precision agriculture, offering enhanced scalability, adaptability, and accuracy in smart farming practices.

title: Improvement and Strengthening Method for Local Structure of UHV Converter Oil Tank for Defence Against High-Energy Arc Faults
authors: Zongliang Zhang, Yikun Zhao, Ke Wang, Jiantao Sun, Jiaxi Li, Shuqi Zhang, Peng Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: Ultra-high voltage transmission system development demands improved equipment safety standards. As converter station core components, UHV transformers critically impact grid stability through unique characteristics including elevated voltage capacity, complex insulation architecture, and expanded oil volume. High-energy arc faults induce exponentially greater short-circuit stresses in UHV transformers versus conventional units, with current tank strength specifications proving insufficient for fault energy dissipation. To address this, we developed a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1: 1$</tex> simulation model of a UHV converter transformer tank. Outgoing-line device area was selected as fault locations. Explicit dynamic simulations revealed pressure distribution patterns and structural deformation under arc discharge, identifying structural vulnerabilities during extreme faults. Targeted reinforcement strategies were proposed for local tank components. Comparative analyses demonstrated improved mechanical safety and anti-explosion performance in modified designs versus conventional configurations. Analysis reveals horizontal box welds and vertical wall welds as high-risk zones in UHV converter transformer tanks during arc faults. Comparative analysis with conventional tank structures reveals that four structural reinforcement strategies-double-box-edge connecting plate assemblies, vertically welded C -shaped external stiffeners, internally baffled angular flow guides, and circular transition butt welds-demonstrate significant mechanical improvements under 30 MJ arc fault conditions. These optimizations yield <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 1 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3. 3 \%}$</tex> strain reduction efficiencies at major- and minor-axis horizontal welds respectively, accompanied by 59.0 % vertical weld strain mitigation. These optimizations collectively enhanced arc fault resistance. This electronic document is a “live” template. The various components of your paper [title, text, heads, etc.] are already defined on the style sheet, as illustrated by the portions given in this document.

title: Research on Intelligent Supervision and Legal Adaptation of Construction Contract Performance Based on Blockchain and BIM
authors: Liu Jinkang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: The supervision of contract performance in the construction engineering field has long been plagued by core issues such as information opacity and frequent disputes, which seriously restrict the efficiency of project management and the sustainable development of the industry. Addressing this pain point, this paper proposes an intelligent supervision system for construction contract performance based on blockchain and Building Information Modeling (BIM), and constructs a three-tier architecture system of “data layer - rule layer - application layer”. Through core modules including BIM-contract association, blockchain evidence storage, and intelligent early warning, the system realizes the whole-process transparency of performance data, the automatic execution of performance behaviors, and the dynamic risk management and control. The results of system testing and case verification show that the system can significantly improve the transparency of contract performance and shorten the traditional project payment cycle from dozens of days to minutes. At the same time, through the design of legal adaptability, it effectively solves the problems of the legal effect of blockchain evidence storage and the legal characterization of smart contracts, providing dual guarantees of technological innovation and legal compliance for the supervision of construction contracts. In addition, the application of this system can reduce the consumption of ineffective resources in the engineering field, promote energy conservation, carbon reduction, and green and sustainable development of the construction industry, and has important practical value for promoting the digital transformation of construction project management.

title: A Robust Transmission Method for RIS-Assisted Low-altitude UAV Communication Systems
authors: Jianxin Sun, Jie Tang, Shuaijun Li, Junlin Zhang, Mingqian Liu, Yanlang Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Centered on low-altitude unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), the low-altitude economy (LAE) is fast becoming the decisive arena for advancing a nation's global technological and industrial competitiveness. However, LAE also faces the challenge of communication transmission reliability due to the complexity of low-altitude environment. Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) technology has been proved to improve the signal transmission ability of low-altitude UAV. Nevertheless, with the introduction of RIS, the need to estimate the channel state information (CSI) for a large number of reflecting elements, coupled with the high-speed mobility of UAVs at low altitude, poses significant challenges to achieving accurate channel estimation and robust system performance. To address this issue, we provide a valuable solution by leveraging the ability of RIS to provide virtual line-of-sight (LoS) paths in UAV communication systems. Specifically, from the matched filter characteristics of orthogonal linear frequency modulation (LFM) waveform and Rician channel properties, we construct a Doppler-robust real-domain linear model. Relying on linear channel estimation and minimum Euclidean distance algorithms, we enhance the reliability of low-altitude UAV communication. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively improves the robustness of UAV communication during high-speed flight.

title: The Knowledge of Nodular Defect Prevention in Tin (Sn)-Silver (Ag) Electroplating Process
authors: Guo-Shu Chen, Chung-Hsiang Wang, Wei-Chun Lin
abstract: Tin (Sn)-Silver (Ag) solder joints are widely utilized in wafer-level packaging (WLP) to establish reliable interconnections between chips and wafers. The various bump structures, including C4 bumps, Cu pillar bumps, micro-bumps and mega pillar bumps, which are essential components in heterogeneous integration technologies, especially 2.5D and 3D packaging structures. The advanced devices, characterized by larger plating area, high-density bumps arrays, high aspect ratios vias and mixed critical dimensions, significantly narrow the electroplating process window, thereby increasing the risk of nodular defect formation “Fig. 1”. Process variability in advanced semiconductor packaging significantly affects solder bump morphology and reliability. Deviations in chemical compositions, improper tool settings, and marginal process recipes contribute to electrochemical instability, often resulting in nodular defects. These defects are characterized by voltage spikes “Fig. 2”, including by sudden changes in the electric field gradient and concentration gradients. Under such conditions, dendritic nucleation initiates at the plating surface, followed by anisotropic growth that forms tree-likes structures. To suppress nodular defects in electroplating, conventional methods such as current density regulation, chemical composition adjustment, functional additives utilization and chemical convection enhancement are used to stabilize electrochemical conditions. Advanced systems like Lam SABRE® 3D enhance process control through precise parameter tuning and waveform optimization, effectively preventing defect formation. As advanced devices are sensitive to narrow plating windows, broader windows are preferred. Although multi-step plating enhanced control, it is negatively impacting throughput. Consequently, simplified plating strategies are preferred in high-volume production. For instance, implementing a single process with well-optimized plating parameters across multiple product types has effective in maintaining the electroplating performance and ensuring defect-free results.

title: Low Temperature Post Bond Anneal for Hybrid Bonding Enabled by Interfacial (IF) Metal Capping - An Assessment of Reliability
authors: Santosh Kumar Rath, Xiaodong Chen, Gilbert See, Eric. J. Bergman, Chang Bum Yong Andy, Prayudi Lianto
abstract: Hybrid bonding is a cutting-edge semiconductor technology that enables ultra-fine pitch interconnects between chips or wafers, typically in the micrometer range. A key step in this process is the post-bond annealing, which reinforces the dielectric bonds and ensures electrical continuity across copper (Cu) pads for both signal and power transmission. For temperature-sensitive devices and materials, a low-temperature anneal process is preferred to stay within the constraints of the overall thermal budget. This study investigates the impact of selectively applying an interfacial metal layer on Cu pads to reduce the post-bond anneal temperature to 250°C or lower. Results show that the presence of the interfacial metal does not significantly affect the electrical bonding yield, even after the wafers undergo rigorous reliability tests, including a 96-hour pressure cooker test and thermal cycling up to 1000 cycles.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Assisted Expert Offloading for Low-Latency MoE on Edge Computing
authors: Gaosheng Xu, Yuchen Shi, Jiangwei Liu, Kangqi Xiao, Guixin Pan, Xiaoning Zhang, Rongping Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: The Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture is a key technology for building ultra-large-scale language models, yet its substantial memory requirements pose significant challenges for deployment on resource-constrained terminal devices. To address this issue, this paper investigates a dynamic computation offloading strategy within a terminal-edge collaborative computing framework. We propose a dynamic expert offloading framework based on Reinforcement Learning (RL), which intelligently determines the execution location-either locally on the terminal or offloaded to an edge server-for each activated “expert” subnetwork in the MoE model. This decision is based on real-time system states, such as the computational load of the terminal and edge nodes and the network bandwidth between them. The primary objective is to minimize the end-to-end inference latency. We designed a decision-making agent based on a Deep QNetwork (DQN) and validated its effectiveness through simulation experiments. The results demonstrate that the framework can learn an efficient offloading policy. As the training converges, its performance surpasses that of baseline methods, and it can adapt to dynamically changing environmental loads. This work provides a feasible technical path for the low-latency, highefficiency inference of large MoE models on terminal devices.

title: Multi-Domain Medical Image Enhancement and Diagnosis: A Unified Deep Generative Framework with Transferable Learning and Attention Mechanisms
authors: Vennila R, Vijaya K
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Medical image diagnosis often suffers from low resolution, scarcity of annotated data, and domain heterogeneity, which adversely affect model accuracy and generalizability. To address these challenges, we propose a unified cross-modal deep generative framework that integrates Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), transfer learning, and attention mechanisms for medical image enhancement and diagnosis. The framework includes a GAN-based enhancement module to improve image clarity, a transfer learning-based feature extractor for robust representation, and a dual-attention module to emphasize clinically relevant regions. Experiments on brain MRI, fundus, and chromosome images demonstrate significant improvements over baseline methods in PSNR (up to 36.2 dB), SSIM (0.92), and diagnostic accuracy (98.3%). The attention mechanism enhances interpretability by localizing disease-specific features. Overall, our approach not only improves image quality but also boosts classification accuracy across modalities, offering a generalizable and clinically relevant solution.

title: Sum Rate Optimization in Element-Splitting-Based Self-Sustainable IRS-aided SWIPT Systems
authors: Xueyan Cao, Shuqi Li, Gerile Ge, Shubin Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Intelligent reflecting surface (IRS)-assisted wireless communication has emerged as a promising paradigm for enhancing system performance, albeit facing inherent challenges such as severe double-fading attenuation and dependency on external power sources, including grids or batteries. To address these limitations, this paper presents a novel self-sustainable IRS (S-IRS) that seamlessly integrates energy harvesting with data transmission, aiming to unlock its potential within simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. A specific protocol leveraging element-splitting techniques is proposed to facilitate S-IRS-enabled SWIPT, and the system's energy harvesting efficacy and communication efficiency are comprehensively investigated. We formulate and solve a problem to maximize the sum rate of wireless devices within an alternating optimization framework, taking into account algorithm complexity and performance constraints. The effectiveness and advantages of the proposed algorithm are validated through comparisons with other benchmark schemes. The simulation results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed framework, offering insights into its benefits and elucidating the role of key design parameters in shaping the system's performance.

title: Research on Dynamic Resource Allocation Strategies for Large-Scale Power Model Agents Based on GPU and Domestic Computing Chip Hybrid Scheduling
authors: Heyi Zhang, Feng Zhao, Shuo Zhang, Yana Zhu, Xinyu Cao, Jiaqi Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Research and analyze the differences in computing power and energy efficiency between graphics processing units (GPUs) and domestically produced computing chips (CNChips) at the microarchitecture level, and establish heterogeneous computing power models and hierarchical scheduling frameworks. Subsequently, a reinforcement learning driven dynamic scheduling algorithm is designed to achieve task transfer and resource reallocation through real-time state feedback. The results show that the dynamic scheduling strategy reduces the average execution time by about 27% compared to the static scheduling scheme, increases throughput by about 33%, and reduces energy consumption in the medium load interval by about 20%. Under the condition of multi node expansion, the system performance retention rate still exceeds 90%, and the load fluctuation amplitude is reduced by nearly 40%, demonstrating the stability and scalability of the algorithm in long-term operation and high concurrency tasks.

title: LGN-CAT: A Cross-Attention Deep Learning Framework for Identifying Diagnostic Biomarkers in Intrahepatic Cholangiocarcinoma
authors: Zhikui Feng, Maihemuti Maimaiti, Quan Zou, Guanggen Zhang, Hui Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
abstract: Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (ICC), a lethal liver cancer with limited diagnostic options, urgently requires reliable biomarkers for early detection. To address this, we integrated three ICC transcriptomic datasets (GSE26566, GSE45001, GSE107943) from GEO, identifying <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 2 4}$</tex> cross-dataset differentially expressed genes (DEGs). We developed the Label-Guided Network with Cross-Attention (LGN-CAT), a deep learning framework integrating self-attention and cross-attention mechanisms (for aligning gene features with diagnostic labels). During inference, the LGN-CAT framework employs a dual-channel reasoning mechanism: simultaneously matching positive/negative label embeddings with feature combinations and selecting the highest-scoring match as the final prediction, thereby enhancing robustness against label uncertainty. Through iterative feature selection with 10-fold cross-validation, we identified four pivotal biomarkers (GLYATL1, C8A, GLYAT, and C6), achieving diagnostic performance metrics of AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.953$</tex>, Accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.877$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F 1}$</tex>-score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.906$</tex>. Immune infiltration analysis further revealed significant correlations between these biomarkers and immune cell subsets (e.g., M1 macrophages, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{r}=0.40$</tex>; memory B cells, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{r}=-0.37$</tex>), suggesting their dual role in diagnosis and tumor microenvironment regulation. This study establishes LGN-CAT as an interpretable and robust tool for ICC biomarker discovery, highlighting the potential of attention-based deep learning for precision oncology applications.

title: 6G Network Deployment Scheme Based on Control and Service Separation
authors: Xin Sun, Zecai Shao, Shuyuan Zhang, Na Li, Guangyi Liu, Siqi Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: With the rapid development of mobile communication technology, the 6G network faces the challenge of explosive growth of data traffic and diversification of emerging service requirements. This paper proposes a 6G network deployment scheme based on control and service separation, aiming at the shortcomings of the existing radio access network in spectrum resource utilization, network cost and multi-dimensional capability integration. This deployment solution combines the differential characteristics of high and low frequency bands to achieve collaborative optimization of network coverage and capacity. The control base stations (BS) is responsible for capabilities such as signaling management and collaborative control of multi-dimensional capabilities, while the service BS focuses on data transmission and local resource scheduling. Based on this deployment scheme, a new air interface connection mode and radio resource control state are proposed, which can effectively reduce user equipment power consumption and handover delay.

title: Fault Injection and Tolerance Analysis of Battery Management Systems Using SystemC-AMS
authors: Hao-Yang Chi, Chih-Tsun Huang, Jing-Jia Liou, Harry Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Test Conference in Asia (ITC-Asia)
abstract: Battery management system (BMS) is a key component to keep battery packs operating in safe and enduring conditions. This paper presents a SystemC-AMS–based BMS simulation platform including both BMS control and cell modeling designed to support fault injection and modular protection analysis. Through single-bit fault injection experiments, only a small fraction (12.8%) of injected faults violate the safety conditions. And SOC estimation and ADC modules are identified as the most critical components with faults covering all severe conditions such as overcharge, overdischarge and overheating. After applying dual module redundancy (DMR), we can enhance the reliability and reduce safety violations from the original 92321 errors to 72020 and 7860 errors or 78.01% and 8.51%, respectively for ADC and SOC modules. With DMR protection for both the ADC and SOC modules, we can eliminate all safety violations.

title: Covert Communication Against Active-Passive Collaborative Attacks in Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Binbin Tian, Jianqiang Chen, Rui Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate covert communication against active and passive collaborative attacks initiated by multiple eavesdroppers (Eves) in cognitive radio networks (CRNs). Specifically, the optimal Eve is scheduled to transmit artificial noise (AN) to interfere with the secondary receiver (SR), while other Eves perform passive eavesdropping. Meanwhile, the joint design of noise uncertainty and power uncertainty has been proposed, and the detection performance of Eves as well as the covert performance of SR has been analyzed, such as the average minimum detection error probability (DEP) at Eves, covert rate and covert outage probability (COP) at SR. Moreover, the optimal transmission power can be obtained with the aid of the proposed simulated annealing algorithm. The results indicate that the proposed scheme can achieve covertness even in the face of powerful adversaries.

title: Energy Demand Forecasting in Building using Recural Neural Network
authors: Gemelfour Ardiatus Sudrajad, Riri Fitri Sari
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: The Most power plants in Indonesia still use fossil fuels to produce electricity, especially in the Java-Bali region which provides more than 70% of electricity needs [1]. Apart from that, from PLN's electricity load profile, the peak load occurs during prime time, which is when the biggest users are from the household sector. Building energy accounts for 33% of world energy consumption and 40% of world GHG emissions directly and indirectly. The target of the General National Energy Plan (RUEN) in 2025 is to achieve the target of 23% of the energy mix (renewable energy) in the electricity system. This paper proposes a deep learning method based on recurrent neural network (RNN) and some machine learning algorithm to energy modelling and energy demand forecasting for buildings. This method analyses factors including energy consumption patterns in buildings, and the non-linear relationships between these parameters on hourly, daily, weekly and monthly intervals. In this research, a building dataset was used which recorded energy usage (KWh) for 3 years from 2012-2015. The characteristics of this data set can be generalized as follows: average energy usage 1,105 kWh, median energy usage 884 kWh, mode energy usage 525 kWh, standard deviation energy usage 686.9 kWh, min energy usage 0 kWh, and max energy usage 12,372 kWh. The highest energy use trend is in the prime time range 19.00-22.00 and the lowest energy use is in the 04.00-07.00 range. The results demonstrate that the proposed method can forecast building energy demand and energy supply with a high level of accuracy, showing a RNN regressor has a MAPE value of 29.20% error range in hourly data prediction for one month ahead. This prediction model can also be applied to RE production to determine the characteristics and potential of RE in the building area.
