The resonant-frequency shift exhibits an approximately linear relationship with the applied force magnitude over the measurement range. These results demonstrate the feasibility and application potential of the proposed passive wireless 3D force sensor.

title: Air Quality Monitoring Sensor Architecture for Supporting Personalized Environmental Healthcare: A Comprehensive Approach
authors: Gitarja Sandi, Suhono Harso Supangkat, Ermawati
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Air pollution is still a big problem in cities, and it can seriously harm people's health and the environment. Traditional air quality monitoring systems are limited in how far they can see and how quickly they can respond since they rely on static and expensive infrastructure. This research introduces an innovative Air Quality Monitoring Sensor Architecture (AQMSA) that utilizes Internet of Things (IoT) and edge computing technologies to facilitate scalable, low-latency, and cost-effective monitoring. There are three layers in the architecture: sensor nodes, edge devices, and a cloud platform. Together, these layers collect, preprocess, and evaluate air quality data. Also, the architecture combines air quality data with healthcare analytics to offer individualized environmental healthcare through risk alarms depending on exposure. The results show that AQMSA could be a strong and flexible framework for long-lasting, health-focused smart cities.

title: Experimental Investigation on Thermal Performance of Prismatic Cell Li-ion Battery for different Charging and Discharging Profiles using ANN Model
authors: Deepa Palaniappan, Deepika Sridaran, Kanchan Saxena, Sutha Subbian, Pappa Natarajan
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Effective battery thermal management is very essential to enhance the safety, efficiency, and lifespan of battery systems in Electric Vehicles (EVs). Various factors influencing the thermal performance of battery system include fast charging, discharging, aging, manufacturing defect, environmental conditions, etc. To study the effect of thermal performance on battery, accurate heat generation prediction is very essential. The main issues in sensor-based temperature measurement and heat generation estimation are inaccuracy and reliability due to point-point measurement and physical contact with battery system. To address these issues, thermal image-based battery surface temperature monitoring and surface heat generation prediction based on Artificial Neural Network (ANN) is proposed to study the thermal performance of 24V,100Ah Li-ion prismatic battery cell under different charging and discharging cycles. The proposed methodology involves collecting real-time thermal images of the battery under different charging and discharging conditions using TIX580 infrared thermal imaging camera and analysing its performance. To automate heat generation prediction, Levenberg-Marquardt (LM) and Bayesian Regularization (BR) ANN models are developed. Finally, the performances of the prediction models are compared. The results show temperature uniformity and accuracy of heat generation prediction and addresses thermal runaway risks.

title: Comparative Analysis of Raspberry Pi Generations and Camera Modules for High-Quality Image Streaming in Driving Safety Monitoring
authors: Kinanti Rahayu Azzahra, Cetta Maulana Andika, M Tsani Faishal Azhar, Surya Michrandi Nasution, Anggunmeka Luhur Prasasti, Reza Rendian Septiawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Driver fatigue and distraction are major causes of traffic accidents. This study compares real-time driver monitoring systems (DMS) using different Raspberry Pi models (3, 4, and 5) and camera modules (Arducam Stereo USB, Raspberry Pi NOIR V2, and Raspberry Pi 3 Night Vision). We evaluated image streaming quality, processing efficiency, and detection accuracy in various lighting conditions. We tested the DMS with a customized image dataset to identify facial features and distractions, using a machine learning process with Mediapipe and XGBoost. The Raspberry Pi 5 paired with the Night Vision Camera performed the best, achieving 92% detection accuracy in different lighting with only 33.6% CPU usage and an average inference time of 71 ms. As one of the first studies to evaluate Raspberry Pi 5 for embedded DMS applications, this research provides valuable insights into thermal behavior, processing speed, and real-time effectiveness across different hardware setups. This cost-effective embedded system shows strong potential for use in real-time driver safety applications. Future work will focus on testing in real-world conditions, including a wider range of users and improvements to hardware for better performance and reliability in various driving situations.

title: Fabrication Process of Superconductive Through-Si Via for 2.5D Quantum Interposer
authors: Yugi Otake, Mai Thi Ngoc La, Fumihiro Inoue
abstract: High density qubit integration is a critical challenge in advancing quantum computing, for which chiplet architecture requires. This study investigated the fabrication of superconducting indium (In) filled through silicon via (TSV) as a key enabling technology for quantum chiplet architectures. We optimized an in-house electrochemical deposition (ECD) process to achieve conformal filling of high aspect ratio TSV. Subsequently, chemical mechanical polishing (CMP) was employed to remove the In overburden. CMP was conducted using calcined and non-calcined ceria slurries under acidic, neutral, and alkaline conditions. Electrochemical measurements revealed that In removal mechanism transitions from being chemically dominant in neutral and alkaline environments, where the catalytic non-calcined ceria excels, to being mechanically dominant in acidic conditions. In the acidic environment, the formation of a soft, protective <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{I n}(\mathbf{O H})_{3}$</tex> layer enabled the highest removal rates (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>120 ~\text{nm} / \text{min}$</tex>) and lowest surface roughness (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<50 ~\text{nm} \text{Ra}$</tex>).

title: Trace: Test Repair via Agent-based Context Extraction with LLMs
authors: Jingxiang Tu, Bo Lin, Yihao Qin, Shangwen Wang, Liqian Chen, Xiaoguang Mao
abstract: As software evolves, test code must be co-maintained to ensure quality, but it often becomes obsolete, leading to failures that mislead developers and increase maintenance overhead. While recent Large Language Model (LLM)-based approaches show promise for repairing obsolete tests, their effectiveness is constrained by a critical challenge: providing comprehensive, repository-level context without overwhelming the models’ input limits. Fixed retrieval strategies often fail to capture the diverse dependencies required for complex repairs. In this paper, we present Trace, a retrieve-agent-based repository-level test repair method. The Trace selectively retrieves crucial context by analyzing (1) class-level structures to understand internal changes, (2) caller methods to capture real-world usage patterns, and (3) related files along the call graph to trace transitive dependencies. This multi-faceted context provides the LLM with a precise and concise understanding of the necessary code environment. We evaluated Trace on a dataset of real-world Java test updates, where it demonstrated superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art baselines. Our results confirm that a structured, adaptive retrieval process is key to unlocking the full potential of LLMs for automated test maintenance.

title: Feature-based Transfer Learning in Cross-Project Defect Prediction: A Systematic Review
authors: Yunpeng Wang, Dongcheng Li, Man Zhao, W. Eric Wong
abstract: This review examines recent feature-based transfer learning techniques for Cross-Project Software Defect Prediction. We summarize representative approaches in five categories-feature selection, feature mapping/alignment, deep/adversarial learning, semantics-enhanced transfer, and hybrid/multi-source designs-and discuss their reported effectiveness across common benchmark datasets. The review highlights trade-offs between predictive accuracy, computational cost, and model interpretability, and concludes with open challenges and directions for future work.

title: A Wavelet-based Image Coding Framework for Data Storage on DNA
authors: Chen Qin, Yuanchao Bai, Wenbo Zhao, Xianming Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: In the face of the exponential growth of digital data, DNA is expected to become a new storage medium. Image data makes up a large proportion of digital data. However, existing DNA data storage models are mainly designed for general files. To address this issue, we propose a novel image encoding method for DNA data storage. We employ discrete wavelet transform to decompose the image and utilize an improved exponent-mantissa representation for numerical data. Subsequently, we achieve enhanced compression performance through context-adaptive arithmetic coding. Additionally, we construct a dictionary between ternary sequences and oligonucleotides to generate nucleotide sequences that meet the specified constraints. Experiments show that our method outperforms JPEG-DNA and BioCoder in compression performance and generates higher-quality nucleotide sequences.

title: HDR Video Composition using Differently Exposed Stereo LDR Videos
authors: Shashaank Aswatha Mattur, Mohamed-Chaker Larabi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: High Dynamic Range (HDR) videos provide a wider luminance range and more accurate real-world illumination compared to Low Dynamic Range (LDR) videos. Unlike images, HDR video generation requires additional attention to ensure flicker-free results while preserving temporal coherence. This paper introduces a deep learning-based approach to generate HDR videos from stereo LDR frames with different exposures, effectively reducing flickering and artifacts. It employs a two-stage pipeline with individual convolutional neural networks. At each time step, the HDR video frame is constructed by combining its corresponding stereo LDR frames with the previous HDR frame. This strategy enhances temporal coherence by ensuring smoother transitions between consecutive frames. The process begins by recovering high dynamic range radiance maps from the initial stereo LDR frames, which are subsequently replaced by the composed HDR frames in later iterations. Experiments on existing datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in generating high-quality HDR videos with improved temporal consistency.

title: MFG-CRT: Multi-Feature Guided Cross-Refinement Transformer for RGB-Guided Thermal Image Super-Resolution
authors: Dogun Kim, Jin Kim, Dongyeon Kang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: Thermal cameras are essential in applications such as surveillance, search, and rescue due to their ability to operate effectively in complete darkness and adverse weather conditions. However, the lower resolution of thermal images often limits their effectiveness. To address this, we propose multi-feature guided cross-refinement transformer (MFG-CRT), a novel RGB-guided super-resolution architecture that integrates RGB and thermal information through a Multi-Feature Guide framework, improving both resolution and detail retention in thermal images. Leveraging a Thermal Feature Extractor and Guided Upsampling Structure, MFG-CRT effectively balances high-frequency detail preservation and global context integration. Our lightweight model achieves notable performance in RGB-guided thermal super-resolution tasks, offering low inference time and low memory usage while maintaining a strong trade-off between efficiency and accuracy. Optimized for resource-constrained environments, it is suitable for on-device applications.

title: Power-on-Demand: A Self-Driving Mobile Powerbank with Autonomous Plugging
authors: Dhruvin Doshi, Mark Gotthardt, Simon Schmidt, Kevin Wettin, Daniel Schuetz, Annika Raatz
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: The accelerating adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) necessitates charging solutions that are both scalable and infrastructure-independent. This paper introduces an autonomous mobile powerbank that achieves end-to-end energy delivery without human intervention. The system integrates three core subsystems: a dual-Ackermann six-wheel platform for highload mobility and terrain adaptability, a 7-DOF KUKA LBR iiwa manipulator with vision-guided control for autonomous plugging, and a 55 kWh power management unit supporting bidirectional charging. Built on ROS2, the architecture leverages a multi-sensor perception suite, GDPR-compliant vision processing, and redundant safety mechanisms to ensure robust and secure operation. Preliminary evaluations indicate that the driving platform achieves a navigation accuracy of ±4.74 cm without GNSS, while the autonomous plugging module demonstrates an average plugging time of 23.62 s and a 95% success rate under daylight conditions. Unlike existing mobile chargers, the proposed system uniquely combines urban navigation, multiterrain capability, and fully automated connector engagement, establishing a scalable framework for on-demand EV charging in dynamic environments.

title: CE-Optimized Cross-Beam Combining for GEO Satellite in Space-Air-Ground Integrated Network
authors: Liangzi Wu, Yucong Zou, Shuai Wang, Buning Tian, Zhe Song, Xiaqing Miao
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Geostationary Earth orbit (GEO) satellites constitute critical components in space-air-ground integrated network (SAGIN). This paper presents a cross-beam signal combining framework that leverages beam-overlapping regions using direct sequence spread spectrum (DSSS) in multi-beam GEO satellite systems. By synchronously transmitting identical information across multiple beams and performing coherent combining at the receiver, the proposed scheme effectively enhances the system's low-probability-of-detection (LPD) and anti-jamming capabilities. To mitigate phase offsets and channel gain mismatches caused by transponder non-idealities and beam-edge effects, a cross-entropy (CE)-optimized joint estimation algorithm is proposed for simultaneous estimation of multiple parameters across signal branches. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves notable improvements in signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), estimation accuracy, and computational efficiency, confirming its robustness and scalability.

title: Integrating AI and IoT for Efficient Courier Logistics: A Literature Review on Delivery Time Prediction and Geofencing
authors: Yogi Putra Pratama, Suhono Harso Supangkat, Devi Willieam Anggara
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT) is reshaping courier logistics, enabling more intelligent and efficient delivery systems to meet the growing demands of the e-commerce industry. A systematic literature review (SLR) and bibliometric analysis of 80 Scopus records identified 16 high-quality studies. While AI and IoT have been widely studied separately, their combined application in innovative courier logistics remains underexplored. The main obstacles are scalability, field implementation, and the absence of a unified AI–IoT–Edge integration framework capable of real-time, adaptive operation in dynamic urban environments. This study proposes an end-to-end architecture integrating multi-sensor IoT geofencing (GPS, BLE, RFID), edge-based hybrid AI models (LSTM + Transformer) for dynamic ETA prediction, and a multi-objective route optimizer balancing SLA and carbon reduction. The framework ensures feature consistency between training and inference, supports adaptive re-routing, and promotes sustainable logistics operations. This study demonstrates that AI–IoT integration enables real-time adjustments, enhancing delivery accuracy, operational resilience, and environmental performance. Integration, scalability, and sustainability remain key challenges. This paper presents these challenges and a novel integration framework to guide AI–IoT adoption in courier logistics.

title: Automated Laser-Guided Targeting for FPV Drones
authors: Lubomyr Petryshyn, Mykhailo Petryshyn
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Methods and Systems of Navigation and Motion Control (MSNMC)
abstract: Automated targeting for FPV drones is critically important in modern warfare, where unmanned systems are integral to the effectiveness of combat operations. The increasing reliance on FPV drones to disrupt enemy logistics, evacuation routes, and supply chains underscores the need for robust automated targeting systems capable of overcoming electronic warfare (EW) countermeasures and improving precision in striking targets. Issues related to limited visibility, subjective operator errors, and vulnerability to EW interference highlight the urgency of developing innovative targeting methods designed to minimize drone losses while significantly enhancing strike accuracy. The proposed approach, based on laser scanning technology and automated trajectory adjustments, addresses these contemporary challenges by leveraging advanced automation and artificial intelligence capabilities, thereby offering a strategic advantage to the side that employs these technologies more effectively.The aim of this research is to develop a novel method for automated targeting of FPV drones, ensuring precise adherence to the intended attack vector through the integration of laser-based coordinate scanning and bit-oriented encoding. This approach aims to reduce operator subjectivity, minimize trajectory deviations caused by adverse weather conditions or EW interference, and decrease drone losses during the approach phase to the target.The scientific novelty of this research lies in the development of a new targeting method. A two-stage system is proposed, combining operator-driven visual targeting with automated trajectory correction using laser scanning. This approach minimizes the drone’s angular deviation from the designated strike vector through continuous feedback. A method for 2D scanning of the target area using laser illumination has been developed, enabling precise target positioning even under challenging conditions. Additionally, a modified centrally inverted unitary code and an extended discrete-phase code are introduced to enhance the efficiency of scan data processing while reducing the impact of EW interference.The practical significance of this research lies in enhancing strike effectiveness. The proposed method enables FPV drones to accurately engage targets while reducing losses caused by operator errors or adverse weather conditions. This is particularly critical for striking moving or camouflaged targets. Automated trajectory correction decreases the likelihood of drone losses during the approach phase, conserving resources and improving overall operational efficiency. Furthermore, the integration of laser scanning with bit-oriented encoding reduces dependence on radio signals, making the system more resilient to EW countermeasures.Scalability: The method can be adapted to various types of FPV drones and applications, including defense, reconnaissance, and targeting of logistical assets. The system can be integrated into existing FPV drones, allowing the operator to maintain visual control of the target while automation ensures precise guidance during the final approach phase.

title: An improved genetic algorithm based on reinforcement learning for solving the personnel scheduling problem of assembly lines
authors: Xiaoke Shi, Zhaozhao Su, Shiji Song, Tao Zheng
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering, Materials and Automation Technology (MMEAT)
abstract: This paper addresses the NP-hard combinatorial optimization problem of personnel scheduling on assembly lines, which involves the dual allocation of workers and tasks. It also tackles the issue that existing intelligent algorithms are insufficient in dynamically adjusting core parameters, thereby restricting the efficiency and quality of solutions in practical applications. To address these problems, an improved genetic algorithm based on reinforcement learning (RLGA) is proposed. This algorithm is built on the foundation of parallel genetic algorithms and innovatively incorporates a two-stage reinforcement learning strategy to achieve dynamic parameter optimization. Firstly, a hybrid learning model with an adaptive switching mechanism is constructed. In the early stage of the algorithm, the SARSA algorithm is used for exploratory parameter tuning. When the convergence trend becomes stable, the Q-learning algorithm is employed for refined adjustments. Subsequently, a state space representation method based on genetic iterative characteristics and a composite reward mechanism integrating convergence speed and population diversity are designed. Finally, multiple instances of different-scale datasets are utilized to compare the learning effect and performance of the RLGA algorithm in solving personnel scheduling on assembly lines. Experimental results demonstrate that this algorithm outperforms similar algorithms in solving medium-scale and large-scale problems.

title: Localization Using Doppler Measurements from LEO Constellation Based Signals of Opportunity
authors: Jiayi Guo, Hongwei Wang, Xiaofei Long
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Positioning by exploiting signals of opportunity (SOP) offers a promising alternative when GNSS signals are unreliable or unavailable. This paper investigates the use of low-Earth-orbit (LEO) satellite transmissions as SOP for positioning, formulating the problem within a nonlinear state-space framework. A cubature Kalman smoother jointly estimates the terminal position and the unknown Doppler frequency offsets of multiple LEO emitters, while an expectation maximization (EM) algorithm recursively learns the covariances of both process and measurement noise, ensuring asymptotically optimal performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms recent nonlinear least-squares approaches.

title: Android Espion: Integrated Remote Control for Android Device
authors: S Mahalakshmi, Nithiya Baskaran, R Rani Hemamalini, K Kowsalyadevi, Pachaivannan. Partheeban
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: The Android Espion is a highly useful application that allows users to control Android smartphones or tablets from a computer through Android Debug Bridge (ADB) connection. The purpose of writing this paper was to discuss how the Android Espion application makes remote access to Android devices easy and allows users to control their Android smartphones or tablets from a computer by enabling developer options, ensuring that the computer and Android smartphone or tablet are connected to the same network. The primary functionality of the Android Espion application consists of "remote audio streaming for a far better audio experience, real-time Android screen capture, and mirroring, which enables users to access their Android smartphone or tablet display from a computer and control Android smartphone or tablet operations" by mirroring the display, meaning users can access and control Android smartphone or tablet operations on a larger computer screen. Another major functionality of the Android Espion application is the ability "to enable users and keep track of various device parameter values when extracting device information." Another important functionality of the Android Espion application "is to enable users to manage apps and stay updated with different apps' operations from a computer, making use of the Android Debug Bridge connection." Furthermore, a fast and secure way for users to manage files from a computer to an Android smartphone or tablet, or vice versa, is provided through the fast file transfer process that takes place between the computer and Android smartphone or tablet. Additionally, Android smartphone or tablet users are able to access images directly on their Android smartphone or tablet from their computer, improving smartphone or tablet operations and functionality for users. This paper further makes readers understand the ever-increasing need for a device management application that works on a computer and the possible applications of Android Debug Bridge for providing users with comprehensive control of Android smartphone or tablet operations on a computer.

title: Dimensions of AI-Induced Fatigue Among Filipino Students: An Exploratory Study
authors: John Paul P. Miranda, Madilaine Claire B. Nacianceno, Rhiziel P. Manalese, Ivan G. Liwanag, Laharni S. Simpao, Marie Angeli F. Peñaflor, Vasco Vic G. Valdez, Marlon T. Acoba
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: The purpose of this study was to examine how students in the Philippines experience fatigue when using AI tools in their academic work. A total of 594 students from public and private higher education institutions in Pampanga participated during the first semester of academic year 2025–2026. Data were collected using a 29-item survey instrument rated on a five-point Likert scale and analyzed through exploratory factor analysis with principal axis factoring and varimax rotation. Results showed that 25 items were retained and grouped into three factors, which explained 46.5% of the variance. These factors were Cognitive-Emotional Fatigue, Tech-Clarity Fatigue, and Passive Fatigue. Reliability analysis indicated acceptable internal consistency. The study indicates that AI-induced fatigue is multidimensional which involves emotional, technical, and behavioral components. The study recommends the development of clearer classroom guidelines, orientation activities, and reflective practices to encourage critical and responsible use of AI in higher education.

title: Design and Implementation of a Real-Time Smart Door Lock with FaceNet Face Authentication and Blink Detection
authors: Justin Fairman Tan, Arif Abdul Aziz, Istiqomah
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a real-time smart door lock system that combines face recognition with blink-based liveness detection to counter spoofing attempts. The face recognition component utilizes a pretrained InceptionResNetV1 model through the FaceNet framework to generate 512-dimensional embeddings, compared using cosine similarity. Liveness detection relies on the Eye Aspect Ratio (EAR) computed from facial landmarks detected by MediaPipe FaceMesh, allowing real-time blink verification. The system operates locally on an Intel NUC with a standard webcam and communicates with an ESP32-based locking mechanism. Testing results show 100% recognition accuracy over 350 trials and 95% liveness accuracy across 20 spoofing attempts. Despite its simplicity, the system performs competitively with lower hardware and computational demands than existing solutions. Furthermore, by collecting training data directly at the deployment location, the system maintains robustness without requiring adaptation to environmental variations. These results support the practicality of the proposed approach for secure, contactless access control in fixed-location smart environments.

title: Clustering and Voltage Control Strategy for Remote Mountainous Distribution Networks Based on Comprehensive Indices
authors: Fangru Shi, Xiuhua Wang, Jianping Ye, Can Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: With the high-penetration integration of DPV systems into remote mountainous distribution networks, the combined uncertainties of load and stochastic PV output can easily trigger voltage violations. To address this issue, a voltage control strategy based on comprehensive indices and an improved Fast Unfolding clustering method is proposed. Firstly, a set of comprehensive indices-including electrical modularity, power balance, cluster accommodation capability, and coupling degree-are established to guide the partitioning of the remote mountainous distribution network. An improved Fast Unfolding algorithm is employed for cluster partitioning, and the core node within each cluster is selected based on the voltage-reactive power sensitivity matrix. Building upon the partitioning results, a voltage control model for the remote mountainous distribution network is formulated. This model takes the output power of the core node PV inverters as decision variables, aiming to minimize voltage deviation. An grey wolf optimizer is applied to solve the model. Finally, simulations are conducted using actual data from a remote mountainous area combined with the IEEE 33 bus distribution system.

title: Fault Module Localization and Type Identification in Multilevel Power Grid Simulator
authors: Zhidong Wang, Yuhan Wang, Weinan Chen, Mengfang Li, Jinyue Su, Bingchen Li, Jiehui Li, Ruiyu Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
abstract: To address the complexity of power grids caused by renewable energy integration and the connection of power electronic devices, as well as the impact of faults in power grid simulators, this paper proposes a method for fault module localization and type identification. Real-time data of capacitor voltage and three-phase current are collected, and characteristic quantities such as amplitude, frequency, angular velocity, and angular acceleration are extracted. By matching these quantities with a fault feature matrix library, fault localization and type identification are achieved. Case analysis demonstrates that the proposed method can accurately identify faults, providing strong support for the maintenance and stable operation of power grid simulators.

title: Development of an Automated Vehicle Protection System Against Flood Damage
authors: N. A. B. Ahmad, Tharan Kumar A-L K.Jegathes Kumar, Muhammad Amir Aziat Ishak, Samsul Haimi Dahlan
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: This study presents the development of a small-scale automated vehicle wrapping mechanism aimed at protecting stationary vehicles from flood damage. The system integrates an ESP32 microcontroller, a water level sensor (XKC-Y25-T12V), and a servo motor (MG90s) to automatically deploy a polyethylene (PE) wrap when rising floodwater is detected. Prototype testing was conducted in a controlled laboratory environment by simulating flood conditions using a water-filled container, with the sensor probe attached at a predefined height. Repeated testing demonstrated high reliability and responsiveness, with an average servo activation time of 0.97 s and a complete wrap deployment time of 1.77s. The wrapping mechanism exhibited consistent stability over multiple deployment cycles, with no mechanical jamming. Additionally, static structural simulation of the full-scale model under hydrostatic pressure indicated minimal deformation, with stress values remaining within safe limits. These findings suggest that, with minor design improvements such as increasing the PE layer thickness, the proposed system can serve as a rapid-response flood protection solution for urban residential and parking areas.

title: A Dynamic Shunting and On-Demand Filtering Model Based on Log Type Clustering
authors: Guanglin Zhu, Tao He, Chao Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: To address the issues of resource waste and latency accumulation caused by the "one-size-fits-all" filtering in traditional ELK Logstash, this paper proposes a dynamic log shunting model based on log type clustering. The model achieves real-time log type identification through lightweight feature extraction and an improved adaptive-parameter DBSCAN algorithm. Based on the identification results, it allocates customized filtering pipelines and dynamically adjusts processing concurrency according to the pipeline load to eliminate invalid computations. Experiments under loads ranging from 10k to 500k events per second (EPS) demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms traditional Logstash and advanced baselines represented by the Drain parser. It improves throughput by 35.2% to 48.7%, reduces average CPU utilization by 28.3%, and stabilizes latency within 50 ms. A 72-hour high-load test confirms the model's stability, providing an efficient log processing solution for the ELK stack.

title: Design Research on an Intelligent Orthotic Brace System for Idiopathic Scoliosis
authors: Chun Gan, Xiaomin Liu, Yu Zhao, Jiahao Li, Ruiqin Zhao, Haochen Duan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: To address the limitations of existing braces for idiopathic scoliosis—namely their inflexible structure, imprecise positioning, inability to adaptively adjust corrective forces, and lack of monitoring and feedback mechanisms—a novel structural innovation has been applied. This intelligent orthotic brace integrates sensor and drive technologies to provide patients with an optimized therapeutic device. Proposing a novel structural innovation in orthotic braces, this design adapts to patients' therapeutic progress and body shape changes throughout their entire treatment cycle. An incremental PID algorithm ensures corrective forces remain within preset optimal ranges at all times. By integrating this incremental PID with motor-driven systems, it transforms traditional reliance on subjective physician judgment into precise, quantifiable, and objective positioning.

title: Hybrid Solar Tracking System for Urban High-Rise Buildings
authors: Krithika Suresh, Srilashmii Veeraraghavan, Aakrit Vikash, Lekshmi S, M Nithya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: A dual-axis solar tracking system that is optimized for effective energy harvesting in urban settings is designed and developed in this paper. Two servo motors are used in the system to control the orientation of the solar panel along both the horizontal and vertical axes. Four LDR sensors are processed by an Arduino-based controller, which continuously tracks and aligns the panel in the direction of the strongest sunlight. An ultrasonic sensor for real-time obstacle detection is integrated to guarantee safe operation, allowing for intelligent path adjustment to avoid obstacles. Reliable light tracking, efficient collision avoidance, and enhanced solar energy harvesting are all demonstrated by the experimental results. In dynamic or limited environments, the suggested system provides a small, affordable way to maximize photovoltaic performance.

title: Adaptive Sliding Window Decoding Algorithm for Zipper Codes under BEC
authors: Mengyao Li, Lijun Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: In this paper, an adaptive sliding window scheme for zipper codes is proposed, which dynamically adjusts the window size according to the channel state as well as the error patterns. The extended Hamming code is selected as the component code, and the appropriate step is designed to reduce the number of adjustments as per the structure of zipper code and the error correction capability of the component code. Under the binary erasure channel (BEC), the simulation results show that the proposed algorithm achieves a significant reduction in average window size while maintaining equivalent bit error rate (BER) performance. Furthermore, under identical window size configurations, the algorithm remarkably enhances BER performance by effectively leveraging error correction capabilities.

title: EEG Signal-based Depression Diagnosis Using BiLSTM Network
authors: Aravindkumar Sekar, Angalaeswari S, Vasugi K
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Depression is a significant global mental health challenge, affecting approximately 5% of adults and severely impacting both social and economic spheres. Traditional diagnostic methods, such as clinical interviews and questionnaires, often lack objectivity and can lead to delays in accurate diagnosis. In this study, we propose a hybrid model combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks to analyze electroencephalography (EEG) signals as an objective biomarker for diagnosing depression. The CNN component captures local spatial features within EEG time-series data, while the BiLSTM component extracts long-range dependencies by analyzing temporal patterns in both forward and backward directions. Our approach addresses class imbalance and preprocessing challenges, ensuring robust performance and generalization. Through comprehensive testing, we demonstrate that this model outperforms traditional methods, offering a promising tool for precise and timely depression diagnosis, which could significantly improve clinical outcomes in mental health care.

title: CASA2SEI: Curriculum-Learned Adversarial Augmentation with Dual-Stream Network for Stable Specific Emitter Identification
authors: Zhengwu Liu, Jiexin Yin, Qi Yu, Xiyu Song
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: Specific Emitter Identification (SEI) is of critical importance in electronic warfare and cybersecurity. It discriminates device identities by extracting unique Radio Frequency Fingerprinting (RFF). However, existing selfsupervised methods employing data augmentation techniques based on feature-space adversarial perturbations suffer from inherent instability, largely stemming from a mismatch between the perturbation intensity and model learning capacity. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a novel selfsupervised adversarial augmentation method incorporating curriculum learning principles for SEI. Building upon the BYOL self-supervised framework, we design a dual-stream residual network that fuses features extracted from the time domain, frequency domain, and an adaptive wavelet domain, subsequently enhancing representation capabilities through cross-attention mechanisms. Moreover, we integrate curriculum learning into the adversarial augmentation process, in which the intensity of the applied perturbation is adaptively adjusted to balance sample diversity with the learning capacity of the model when generating adversarial samples in the feature space. Experiments are conducted on both an open-source Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast(ADS-B) dataset and an actual collected dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves performance compared to the existing SA2SEI approach.

title: Fuel Cell Thermal Management System Based on Model Predictive Control
authors: Jianguo Liu, Yupu Liu, Pan Zhao, Fuwu Yan, Hao Zheng, Youhua Wu, Chihua Lu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: To address issues of system oscillation, excessive temperature overshoot, and sluggish response in the thermal management system of proton exchange membrane fuel cells under dynamic load conditions, this study proposes a model predictive control (MPC)-based thermal management strategy, which leverages mathematical models to predict future dynamic behavior and employs rolling optimization to adjust control variables in realtime, thereby optimizing final output objectives. The study first validated the accuracy of the stack model based on experimental data from a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100-\text{kW}$</tex> fuel cell system; subsequently, it established discrete state-space equations through linearization of the thermal management system and optimized key parameters of the MPC controller. Under dynamic load conditions, comparative testing of different control strategies for temperature control. The MPC controller demonstrated significantly enhanced performance over both PID and fuzzy PID controllers in mitigating overshoot and steady-state error. Furthermore, during pump and fan speed regulation, the MPC controller achieved superior stability with minimal overshoot, validating the strategy's high robustness and adaptability.

title: Performance Analysis of Open-Source Diffusion Models for Artistic Image Generation
authors: Antony Seba P, Harsha Prada Chandrakumar, Janani C, Indhuja I
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Text-to-image generation has grown into an important application of generative AI. Among the many models, diffusion-based architectures such as Stable Diffusion and Dreamlike Diffusion have become popular. They can create high-quality images with realistic and stylistically rich visuals using natural language prompts. In this work, we assess and contrast several well-known diffusion-based models. We create images and evaluate their quality using perceptual and semantic evaluation metrics, such as CLIP score, LPIPS, FID, and Inception Score, using a uniform prompt describing a forest scene. Our analysis shows that while Dreamlike Diffusion excels in artistic coherence and perceptual detail, models like Stable Diffusion XL offer balanced trade-offs between realism and prompt alignment. For instance, cases involving the production of artistic images, the results provide valuable information regarding model selection. This interpretational study can act as a guide for researchers and practitioners who want to compare generative models with standard metrics highlighting fidelity.

title: Spatio-Temporal Data Augmentation Strategies for Improving Air Pollution Source Identification
authors: Huaijin Wang, Khalifa Djemal, Amir Ali Feiz
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Digitization of Systems and Services (IDSS)
abstract: Nowadays, urban pollution continuously impacts people's daily lives. The identification and geolocation of pollution sources have become increasingly important. However, the detected pollutant data suffers from the experimental environment and conditions, making it challenging for models to accurately extract meaningful information and generate reliable predictions. To address these issues, data augmentation emerges as a crucial technique for enhancing training datasets in machine learning, especially for the sparse and noisy spatio-temporal air pollution data. In this study, we propose a hybrid method combining Auto-Regressive Iterated Moving-Average (ARIMA) and Inverse Distance Weighting (IDW) to interpolate missing spatio-temporal data, and we implement another method, Iterated Stepwise Multiple Linear Regression (ISMLR), which enriches the dataset by adding more explanatory variables, to compare with the hybrid method. To evaluate the effectiveness of these augmentation strategies, we employ two machine learning models: Random Forest and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP). The experimental results indicate that both data augmentation methods slightly improve model performance, confirming their suitability for augmenting spatio-temporal contaminated data.

title: Machine Learning-Based Approaches for Malicious Insider Threat Detection: a Survey of Recent Advances and Open Issues
authors: Ravi Kumar Tenali, V Vijaya Chamundeeswari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Insider threat detection (ITD) is an important research area considering the growing number of data breaches caused by attack actors with legitimate system access. Capturing hidden, and context-based anomalies in a complex enterprise setting can be a difficult challenge for traditional signature- and rule-based mechanisms. This survey gives an overview of the latest developments for learning-based approaches for ITD focusing on the move of static heuristics to adaptive and data-driven intelligence. Machine Learning (ML), Deep Learning (DL) and hybrid frameworks have proven very useful in modeling user behavior, detecting anomalies, and mitigating privilege escalation attacks. The review focuses on the limitations of the current ML methods in terms of data imbalance, feature heterogeneity, and interpretability and considers hybrid anomaly detection methods such as the Local Outlier Factor (LoF)-based Hybrid Anomaly Checker for the sake of increased accuracy. Furthermore, it covers the usage of benchmark datasets (e.g., CERT r4.2) and performance metrics such as True Positive Rate (TPR), F1Score, Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC) and Time-to-Detection (TTD). By synthesis of findings from current studies, the present paper covers the critical research gaps and describes emerging trends such as graph-based modeling, multimodal fusion, and continuous learning frameworks that characterize the future of insider threat analytics.

title: Application of Remote Sensing and AI in Precision Agriculture: Monitoring Plant Health and Growth
authors: Priyanka Gupta, Kanta Malik, Rajan Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: The main food production and human sustenance activity is agriculture, which has greatly been improved through use of new methods. Among them, remote sensing (RS) and artificial intelligence (AI), are essential techniques in precision agriculture since they allow identifying the health of plants at an early stage, without errors, and in detail. In this study, the researcher is interested in the application of these technologies as a utilization tool to achieve precision and efficiency in agricultural activities, especially crop health, stress control, and the use of resources. The idea of remote sensing involves the use of satellites, drones, or ground sensors to take images on a large scale and in real-time to monitor the status of crops in large pieces of agricultural farming land. When used in conjunction with remote sensing, AI not only hastens the speed of monitoring plant health, but also enhances the chances of making accurate future decisions that, in case of remedial action, will help avoid crop losses and yield better outputs. The problems that come with these technologies are also discussed in this study, with the primary focus on high quality annotated datasets availability, environmental variability, model interpretation and explanations concerns. The aspect of ethicality, data privacy, and constant surveillance of agricultural activities, and their possibly adverse consequences of these practices are also addressed.

title: Image Cropping Guided by Vision-Language Understanding
authors: Li Yan, Huiying Zheng, Rui Yao, Yan Zhang, Jinyue Liu, Hancheng Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Image cropping aims to remove irrelevant content from an image to optimize composition and enhance aesthetic quality. Existing image cropping methods primarily rely on specifically designed modules or auxiliary datasets to capture aesthetic attributes or key regions within the image. However, these methods often struggle to fully understand and represent the overall visual aesthetics. Consequently, we propose a novel image cropping method guided by visual-language understanding. Specifically, we leverage a multimodal model to fully explore the feature representations between images and aesthetic descriptions and utilize these representations to model and predict the cropping results. Furthermore, a large language model is utilized to generate textual descriptions of aesthetic attributes from images, enhancing the semantic richness of multimodal inputs. Comprehensive experiments on public datasets validate that our method surpasses current state-of-the-art methods.

title: VMIGEN: Utilizing Virtual Machine Introspection for Fuzzing Complex Closed-Source Targets
authors: Florian Schweins, Moritz Schloegel, Moritz Bley, Nico Schiller, Thorsten Holz
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Modern fuzzing is a highly successful testing method, but it still struggles with stateful software that expects complex, context-rich inputs. Instead of further tuning the fuzzing process itself, we introduce VMIGEN, a new approach that captures interactions by implicitly recording both complex inputs and the corresponding system states needed for effective testing. By using Virtual Machine Introspection (VMI), a technique for observing the state and behavior of a VM from the outside, we can monitor actual runtime events for a given system. This way, we can extract concrete inputs and snapshot the whole system at relevant interactions to preserve the full system state, thereby enabling effective fuzzing. At the same time, our approach does not require access to source code, allowing us to test closed-source software on Windows. To demonstrate VMIGEN's effectiveness, we use it to test kernel drivers, including those of anti-virus engines, and Remote Procedure Call (RPC) interfaces. Our comprehensive evaluation shows that our VMI-based method enables an existing fuzzer to achieve up to 6.6x more code coverage. In total, VMIGEN allowed us to discover 33 previously unknown bugs, which we disclosed in a coordinated way to the affected vendors.

title: Photovoltaic Identification for a Large Scale with High-resolution Satellite data and Hybird CNN and Tranformer Model
authors: Yuanhao Chen, Fuyou Tian, Xin Zuo, Da Zhang, Fujia Tian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) energy plays a pivotal role in global decarbonization efforts, directly contributing to SDG 7 (Affordable and Clean Energy) and SDG 13 (Climate Action). As the world's largest PV producer and consumer, accurate mapping of China's solar infrastructure is critical for optimizing land use, assessing ecological impacts, and informing energy policy. However, rapid large-scale deployment necessitates high-resolution monitoring to overcome mixed-pixel limitations inherent in 10-meter resolution data. This study presents a large-scale photovoltaic (PV) power station identification framework using sub-meter resolution satellite imagery and a hybrid TransUnet model integrating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers. The TransUNet architecture synergistically employs CNNs for local texture extraction and Transformers for global geometric modeling, achieving 93<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">%</sup> training accuracy and 90% validation accuracy with 1,458 annotated samples <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(512\times 512$</tex> pixels). For Ningxia (2021 baseline), we estimate 220 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{km}^{2}$</tex> of PV installations-intermediate between prior estimate. Spatial analysis reveals concentrated deployment in Wuzhong City's desert regions, reflecting strategic land-use optimization. In Xinjiang's Tarim Basin, we identified 104.6 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{km}^{2}$</tex>, predominantly near Kashgar, Hotan, and Korla urban clusters with favorable infrastructure. ERAS-Land solar radiation data confirms abundant resources in southern Xinjiang, particularly around the Kunlun Mountains. These findings provide critical baseline data for sustainable PV planning, demonstrating the value of temporally-specific, high-resolution monitoring in dynamic renewable energy landscapes.

title: FlashGMM: Fast Gaussian Mixture Entropy Model for Learned Image Compression
authors: Shimon Murai, Fangzheng Lin, Jiro Katto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Visual Communications and Image Processing (VCIP)
abstract: High-performance learned image compression codecs require flexible probability models to fit latent representations. Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs) were proposed to satisfy this demand, but suffer from a significant runtime performance bottleneck due to the large Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF) tables that must be built for rANS coding. This paper introduces a fast coding algorithm that entirely eliminates this bottleneck. By leveraging the CDF’s monotonic property, our decoder performs a dynamic binary search to find the correct symbol, eliminating the need for costly table construction and lookup. Aided by SIMD optimizations and numerical approximations, our approach accelerates the GMM entropy coding process by up to approximately 90x without compromising rate-distortion performance, significantly improving the practicality of GMM-based codecs. The implementation will be made publicly available at https://github.com/tokkiwa/FlashGMM.

title: Performance Analysis of FSO Deployment in the North-East India
authors: S Kirubakaran, Aswani Ashok, Hari Krishna Atluri, Naveen Choudhary, Kishor Lala, A. Paventhan, Gajanan Pimparkar
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Reliable Internet access is still challenging in India’s rural and remote areas, where fiber deployment becomes impractical due to the complex geographical environment. It can be very difficult to dig trenches for fiber ducts because of steep slopes and uneven terrain. Drilling or tunneling involves additional costs due to heavy machinery and high manpower. Free Space Optical Communication (FSOC) is an alternative which provides high data rates without the need for physical cabling. In this paper, the deployment and evaluation of a 5.57 km FSOC link between the Kohima Secretariat and the Kohima Science College in Nagaland has been analysed. In addition to atmospheric characteristics such as rainfall and wind speed, link performance metrics, including throughput, latency, and availability, were monitored over a five month period (March–July 2021). Link failures were frequently observed during periods of wind speed above 40 mph and rainfall rates exceeding 0.27 in/hr. During the heavy monsoon period, the effective throughput dropped to 0.24 Gbps and the link loss exceeded 52%. Power and network failures had little impact on availability, but weather-induced outages were the main cause. During FSOC downtime, service continuity was guaranteed by adding an radio frequency failover connection. This work highlights the necessity of FSO deployments with failover mechanisms and displays the potential benefits and limitations of FSOC in difficult terrains. The study also offers constructive contributions to expand FSOC based rural connectivity in India and other relevant locations around the world.

title: Key Insights into Design for Reliability of 3D NAND Packages in Solid-State Drive
authors: Ling Pan, Faxing Che, Yeow Chon Ong, Wei Yu, Hong wan Ng
abstract: The complexity and resource demands of AI applications are driving the need for high-performance storage solutions like SSDs. To address increasing performance demands, SSD manufacturers have adopted advanced packaging methods, including high-density components, additional copper layers in the integrated circuit boards, and the use of epoxy material in corner fills. These innovations provide improved speed and capacity; however, they also introduce important reliability concerns. Additionally, the use of highdensity 3D NAND die stacking technology presents challenges related to reliability under temperature cycling conditions. As a result, implementing Design for Reliability (DfR) practices to ensure the reliability of 3D NAND in SSDs, particularly focusing on thermal reliability, has become crucial. Finite Element Analysis (FEA) was utilized to determine the failure location and calculate the plastic work density per cycle to evaluate the fatigue life under temperature loading conditions. [1] –[4] This study explored design prospects to assess their impact on NAND solder joint reliability (SJR) in SSDs. Advancements in SSDs can be made by analyzing FEA modeling and testing data for more durable development and manufacturing.

title: Automated Recognition and Explainable Interpretation of Hindu God Statues and Sculptures Using Computer Vision
authors: Naresh Kumar M, Lathaselvi G
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: Hindu iconography, enshrined in a colossal pantheon of statues and sculptures of gods, forms an enormous cultural and historical heritage. But there lies an immense barrier of accessibility, keeping scholars, tourists, and enthusiasts away from easily recognizing and interpreting these elaborate artifacts. This knowledge barrier is generally supplemented with linguistic challenges and paucity of descriptive information in-situ. This paper presents ’Statueist,’ a web application developed with the vision to bridge the chasm through the use of mainstream computer vision and machine learning approaches. The system employs deep learning-based models to effect real-time recognition of statues through images submitted by an end-user. It identifies major iconographic elements—hand gestures (mudras), postures (asanas), weaponry (ayudhas), and crowns (kiritas)—to generate accurate, context-rich, and interpretable explanations. Notable features are multilingual facility with textual explanations, geoservices to discover statues near users, and an integrated educational platform. We explain the architecture of the system, dataset creation and learning methodology of the model, the performance of the underlying object detection model, and briefly touch upon the broader potential of the latter in preserving cultural heritage, education, and sustainable tourism.

title: Hierarchical Alternating Optimization for Enhanced 6DMA-Assisted Over-the-Air Computation
authors: Junjie Gao, Bin Yan, Rui Ma, Zheng Wang, Yongming Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: In this paper, an enhanced six-dimensional movable antenna (6DMA) assisted over-the-air computation (AirComp) system is proposed to minimize the computation mean square error (CMSE) in internet of things (IoT) networks. Unlike conventional 6DMA systems, our design not only enables each surface to adjust its 3D position and rotation, but also allows antennas to move within each surface. Specifically, we formulate a joint optimization problem for both enhanced 6DMA configurations and transceiver design. To solve this complicated non-convex problem, we propose a hierarchical alternating optimization (HAO) algorithm which decouples the problem as the subproblems of transceiver design and antennas configuration optimization subproblems. Then, alternating optimization and moth-flame optimization (MFO) are applied to solve them, respectively. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed enhanced 6DMA scheme significantly outperforms existing approaches.

title: Personalized Kidney Stone Prediction: Review and Analysis
authors: V. Dhivya, D. Sivaganesan, S.Senthil Prabhu, B. Suganya, C. Devipriya, T. Kanagasabapathy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: Kidney stone disease is a widespread urological condition that can lead to severe pain, hospitalization, and longterm health complications. Traditional diagnostic methods, such as ultrasound and CT scans, are effective but often expensive and inaccessible to certain populations. This study proposes a review of various Machine Learning learning-based predictive models that utilizes datasets of structured patient data, including demographic details, biochemical markers, and lifestyle factors, to enable early prediction for kidney stones. Compared to traditional machine learning and neural network models, deep learning techniques exhibit higher accuracy and superior generalization. The proposed system offers a costeffective alternative to imaging-based diagnostics, non-invasive potentially improving clinical decision-making and patient outcomes.

title: An Intelligent Algorithm Based on GNN and SAC for Service Function Chain Deployment
authors: Daoren Zhu, Jinqiu Lin, Rui Wang, Xiushuang Yi, Haobing Guo, Kungui Zeng
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: With the rapid development of internet technology, traditional network architectures relying on dedicated hardware face challenges of poor flexibility and high costs in meeting diverse service demands. While Network Function Virtualization (NFV) and Service Function Chaining (SFC) improve network efficiency through hardware-function decoupling and flexible orchestration, SFC deployment still suffers from low success rates under Quality of Service constraints, insufficient physical network information utilization, and unstable performance. To address these issues, this paper proposes GatSacDeployModel, a service function chain deployment algorithm that combines Graph Attention Networks (GAT) for physical network feature extraction with Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) for intelligent Virtual Network Function placement, utilizing shortest K-nearest neighbor path algorithm for efficient SFC routing. Extensive experiments on a custom simulation platform demonstrate that GatSacDeployModel significantly outperforms mainstream algorithms in deployment success rate, long-term revenue-to-cost ratio, and network resource utilization efficiency.

title: Research on Anti-Interference Grounding Technology for Communication Systems in Wind Turbines
authors: Yang Gao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on New Energy and Power Engineering (ICNEPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of wind power technology, the capacity and height of individual wind turbines have been continuously increasing, leading to increasingly complex electromagnetic environments. This paper focuses on the issue of electromagnetic interference (EMI) faced by wind turbine communication systems and studies technical solutions to enhance the anti-interference capability of communication signals through optimized grounding system design. By analyzing the interference characteristics and grounding requirements of wind turbine communication systems, the paper elaborates on specialized grounding measures for communication systems, including optimization of cable shield grounding, improvement of equipotential connections, and the synergistic design with shielding and filtering technologies. In conjunction with the latest technological developments in recent years, the paper describes the application of new independent grounding systems and advanced grounding materials. Practical cases show that reasonable grounding system design can significantly reduce communication error rates and decrease equipment damage caused by lightning strikes by more than 90 %, providing technical support for the safe and stable operation of wind farms. Finally, the paper provides a prospect on the future development trends of grounding and anti-interference technologies for wind turbines.

title: Frequency Domain Adaptive Modal Alignment for Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification
authors: Xuehu Chen, Qi Ge, Liang Xiao, Tao Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Consumer Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICCECE)
abstract: Visible–Infrared Person Re-Identification (VI-ReID) is a critical task in intelligent surveillance systems.Existing methods typically rely on complex feature mapping networks to learn shared representations that mitigate modality discrepancies, yet often overlook the intrinsic modality gap between visible and infrared images.Moreover, these methods primarily exploit spatial-domain information and largely underutilize the discriminative potential of the frequency domain. To address these limitations, we propose a novel Frequency Domain Adaptive Modality Alignment (FDAMA) framework, which adopts a hierarchical alignment strategy starting from the frequency domain, performing alignment of representations across different modalities simultaneously at both the image level and the feature level, so as to enforce cross-modal consistency constraints. Specifically, we first introduce the Adaptive Modality Mapping (AMM) module at the image level: this module adaptively filters the amplitude spectra in the frequency domain to effectively reduce the modality gap between visible and infrared images, thereby laying the foundation for subsequent feature-level alignment. Second, we propose an Amplitude Prior-Guided Phase Modulation (APGM) module that incorporates amplitude-derived priors into the modulation of phase features, enhancing their ability to encode semantically consistent cross-modal representations and thereby reducing modality discrepancies at the feature level. Extensive experiments conducted on three widely adopted public benchmarks—SYSU-MM01, RegDB, and LLCM—demonstrate that our proposed FDAMA significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods.

title: A Cooperative Computation Offloading Strategy for Reusable Tasks in the Multi-UAV-Assisted MEC System
authors: Wenqing Wu, Shie Wu, Hanchao Mu, Jiahui Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 25th International Conference on Communication Technology (ICCT)
abstract: By integrating unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) with mobile edge computing (MEC) servers, the multi-UAV-assisted MEC system has become a potential paradigm for task offloading due to its flexibility and mobility. However, the performance of the system is constrained by the limited energy of the UAVs and the dynamic environment brings challenges for task offloading among multiple UAVs. In this paper, taking into consideration the diversity and similarity of the tasks, we investigate the energy consumption minimization problem under the multi-UAV-assisted MEC system with reusable tasks. To address the issue, a joint optimization of cooperative task offloading and UAV flight trajectories (JOCOT) algorithm is proposed. Firstly, UEs are clustered based on the graph coloring method. Then, based on the multi-agent deep deterministic policy gradient (MADDPG) method, the cooperative task offloading and UAV flight trajectories are jointly optimized. Simulation results show that the proposed JOCOT scheme outperforms other benchmark methods, consuming the least energy under different scenarios.

title: DP-Mix: Differentially Private Routing in Mix Networks
authors: Mahdi Rahimi
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Mixnets, as overlay networks, ensure anonymity for messages by forwarding them through intermediary nodes that obscure their traffic patterns from network-level adversaries. Nonetheless, the selection of intermediaries is traditionally performed uniformly at random, resulting in optimal routes being chosen no more frequently than suboptimal ones. This often causes messages to traverse inefficient paths that degrade performance or weaken security. While there have been proposals to improve route selection in mixnets, they are limited to latency reduction and rely on heuristic strategies that lack formal anonymity guarantees. To bridge this gap, we develop a framework for differ-entially private routing in mixnets aimed at general-purpose optimization. In this framework, each candidate route is as-signed an optimality score, and routes are then selected to favor high-scoring paths while preserving anonymity under a pure-ε differential privacy guarantee. We instantiate this model for optimizing path security, enhancing reliability, and minimizing communication latency. Additionally, we introduce a gradient-based algorithm applied post hoc to the route selection process-without weakening privacy guarantees-to prevent the over-selection of particular nodes, which could otherwise lead to security vulnerabilities or network congestion. Through analytical evaluation and simulation over data from deployed Nym mixnets, we demonstrate that DP-Mix consistently achieves high optimality scores across all instantiations while preserving a strong level of anonymity. In particular, for latency optimization, our method outperforms state-of-the-art solutions, achieving up to a 8× improvement in the latency-anonymity trade-off.

title: Integrating Digital Marketing Tools as Strategic Approach to Customer Retention in FnB Industry
authors: Angela Seraphine Theresia, Ono Supriadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: This research investigates the combined effect of four interrelated drivers-omnichannel marketing, ethical corporate social responsibility, personalized outreach, and robust customer relationship management-on retention rates in the food and beverage industry. Facing intensifying rivalry, firms now view loyalty not simply as a perk but as the foundation of enduring viability. When a company weaves together its sales, service, and promotion channels into one transparent structure, customers experience smoother journeys and report stronger attachment to the brand. Similarly, transparent practices like fair pricing, consistent quality, and public accountability build the trust upon which loyalty rests. At the same time, artificial intelligence pushes personalization well beyond inserting a name in an email, enabling companies to detect subtle preferences and respond with genuinely relevant offers. Blend these forces, and shoppers begin to form a deeper emotional connection, a bond that past studies link directly to repeat purchases. The findings thus equip food-and-beverage managers to design practical retention strategies while opening new avenues for scholars eager to explore the topic.

title: NeuroViT: Transformer-Enhanced U-Net for Pituitary Tumor Detection
authors: Ashwini Anil, Gopika Chandana, Anjana Krishnan P, Safad Ismail
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology (CICT)
abstract: As pituitary tumors vary in size, shape, and anatomical position, detecting them with brain MRI is a crucial and difficult task in neuro-oncology. In this work, we introduce Neuro VIT, a hybrid Deep Learning (DL) technique that improves tumor localization and classification by combining U-Net with Vision Transformer (ViT). While the ViT component uses global attention to acquire more comprehensive contextual information, U-Net offers detailed spatial feature extraction for segmentation. The model is evaluated on a pituitary MRI dataset and compared with State of the Art( SOA) convolutional and transformer-based techniques using standard classification and segmentation metrics. The proposed Neuro ViT performed well across classes, with a classification accuracy of 97%. The model obtained a Precision of 1.00, Recall of 0.95, and F1-score of 0.97 with a support of 1160 samples for the Pituitary class. For the No Tumor class, it achieved a Precision of 0.70, Recall of 0.97, and F1-score of 0.81 based on 140 samples. Furthermore, with a Mean Dice Score of 0.7462, the model showed excellent segmentation performance. The hybrid model continuously showed greater sensitivity to small and subtle tumors and provide more dependable overall performance as compared to traditional CNN architectures and other cutting-edge deep learning techniques.

title: Meta-Learning Framework with MS-1D-CNN and ArcFace for Few-Shot Machinery Fault Diagnosis
authors: Mehedy Hasan Rony, Harikrishnan Ramiah, Jeevan Kanesan, Shiran Madhuranga
publish: 2025 IEEE 23rd Student Conference on Research and Development (SCOReD)
abstract: Machine health monitoring systems (MHMS) are essential for predictive maintenance and operational reliability in industrial environments. Conventional deep learning methods depend on large, labeled fault datasets, which are rarely available in practice. This study proposes a lightweight multi-skip one-dimensional convolutional neural network (MS-1D-CNN) with separable convolutions, combined with Few-Shot Learning (FSL) and an Additive Angular Margin Loss (ArcFace) classification head to enhance intra-class compactness and inter-class separability. The framework is validated on the Case Western Reserve University (CWRU) bearing dataset under K-shot evaluation. Results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves more than 99.90% accuracy with significantly fewer labeled samples, highlighting its suitability for real-time fault diagnosis in industrial applications with limited data and constrained computational resources.

title: Explainable Deep Learning Multi-Class Skin Cancer Detection Using Hybrid CNN and Enhanced Grad-CAM Visualization
authors: T. Jaya, Pavithra K, Aishwarya K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Information and Control Systems (ICOIICS)
abstract: One of the most common cancers diagnosed globally is skin cancer, and improving treatment outcomes and survival rates requires early detection. Although deep learning has demonstrated great promise in recent years for the analysis of dermoscopic images, the “black- box” nature of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) frequently hinders its use in clinical settings. Using the HAM10000 dataset, we created a hybrid CNN method for multi-class classification of skin lesions. To improve interpretability, we also incorporated an Enhanced Grad-CAM method. To improve feature representation, our model adds more convolutional layers to an already-optimized Xception backbone. With balanced precision and recall across lesion categories, the experiments produced an overall accuracy of 89%. Crucially, the improved Grad-CAM output highlights the regions which are most for predictions making the system more transparent and trustworthy.

title: Prediction of Solar Panel Requirements Based on Solar Irradiance and Energy Consumption Data in Jakarta
authors: Marcel Suandi Tambing, Suhono Harso Supangkat, Devi Willeam Anggara
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: Increase of electricity demand in Indonesia encouraged to development of renewable energy as a sustainable solution. Solar panel are one of the main choices, but planning to build the solar panel system is often not optimal due to significant variations in solar irradiation in various regions. Therefore, to plan a solar panel system, it needed a prediction of solar panel requirement based on historical data on sunshine duration and solar irradiance. The approach taken using the Long Term Short Memory (LSTM) as one of the deep learning method that can handle time series data. Research case using dataset from Jakarta region with meteorological parameters: temperature, humidity, rainfall, duration of sunshine and solar irradiation obtained throughout 2024. LSTM model is trained using hyperparameter tuning techniques to obtain optimal prediction results. The result show that the model can predict sunshine duration and solar irradiance with a high level of accuracy (minimum loss reaches 0.0757), which can be used to calculate the requirement of solar panel and battery systems, such as in public streetlamp scenarios. This research gives contributes to planning adaptive PV systems based on local data.
